# 3.30 UL Scheduler

## 3.30.0-3 UL Scheduler Document Owner Deputy Jim Brausch (ID: 11194198)

## 3.30.1 Scheduling Overview

### 3.30.1.0-4 Scheduling Overview K2 validation on FDD carrier shared with LTE (ID: 11194232)

**3.30.1.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `12639235`)

Scheduling Overview On an FDD carrier shared with LTE [CB007372-AA] and when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-AA], in every TTI, the UL scheduler shall check if current slot n has a k2 value in the max sharing pattern (see 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for k2 calculation). Note that a PDCCH with an UL grant can only be sent if the current slot n has a k2 value.

  1. If the current slot n has a k2 value:



The UL scheduler shall serve the pending UL resource requests (5G_UP_ALG_10506_replaced([11194320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). PUSCH transmissions shall be scheduled for slot n+k2. This applies also to RA Msg3.

  1. Otherwise when NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actDssMsg3Enh=true **the UL scheduler shall only serve pending RA Msg3 resource requests (5G_UP_ALG_10506_replaced([11194320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if any. A RA Msg3 shall be scheduled for slot n+k2, assuming a k2 value in the range [1, …, k2Max] such that the RA Msg3 is scheduled in the slot selected by the DL scheduler. If multiple k2 values are possible, the UL scheduler shall select the smallest one. Note the RA Msg3 considered in this condition are only the ones that could not be scheduled in condition 1 above.**



[CB010439-C]

Note: for UL Tx switching with DSS PCell + TDD SCell, entries with k2min+1 may be removed due to DCI 0_0 and DCI 0_1 size conflict (refer to [11171658: DCI Format 0_0 and 0_1 conflict detection and TDRA list modification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuF96_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), then the UL scheduler shall not serve such UL Tx switching UE in n+k2min+1 slot.

[End CB010439-C]

### 3.30.1.0-5 Scheduling Overview RF calibration support for UL (ID: 11194247)

**3.30.1.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11194258`)

Scheduling Overview [Before CNI-86060]

For digital RF beamforming, due to UL calibration requirements from RF, the UL scheduler shall consider an algorithm to reserve rdUlCalSlotNum slots of a minimum of rdUlCalMinSymbolNum consecutive symbols for UL calibration purpose within period of rdCalDuration seconds.

A minimum timing, rdCalMinTime (in ms), is needed between two consecutive slots carrying UL calibration symbols.

This RF calibration dedicated radio resource shall not be used for any data transmissions. The 5G-L2-PS shall send DlData_PatternConfigReq message to 5G-L1-DL with parameter of calibrationBitmap to indicate which symbols shall be reserved for calibration in each 5GNR-slot, which shall be all symbols of a slot.

The TD scheduler shall use 5GNR UL data slots with data symbols for RF calibration.

5G-L2-PS shall maintain a counter slotCountForUlCalibration to trigger UL calibration actions. Counter shall be updated every scheduling period. At startup and every rdCalDuration seconds counter is reinitialized to rdUlCalSlotNum.

At first UL calibration opportunity, TD scheduler checks if a UL calibration is needed. A calibration is needed when slotCountForUlCalibration counter is not null and when previous UL calibration was triggered more than rdCalMinTime ms before. If a slot is used for UL calibration, counter slotCountForUlCalibration is decreased by 1.

rdCalDuration : see 5G_L2_3732_replaced([11183498](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27j-afvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdDUCalMinSymbolNum : see 5G_L2_1048_replaced([11191387](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28dWSPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdUlCalSlotNum : see 5G_L2_11403_replaced([11182869](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF5vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdCalMinTime : see 5G_L2_11402_replaced([11183349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iwSfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For data symbols used as calibration symbols: TD scheduler could manage those calibration opportunities when no users are scheduled or enforce them if needed. 5G-PS-L2 shall set data symbols (Ud in frame format) for calibration in DlData_PatternConfigReq message (in calibrationBitmap set bits associated to RF calibration symbols to 1).5G-L2-PS shall transmit the message DlData_PdcchSendReq only in case it contains a non-zero RB assignment and omit the message otherwise.

NOTE:

  * UL and DL calibrations are independent of each other (see 5G_UP_ALG_4098_replaced([11173686](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuVObfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for DL).

  * Any data scheduled together with calibration will be lost.




If no beamforming is applied, see 5G_UP_ALG_2183_replaced([11112736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZEBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_907_replaced([11353384](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub-6UvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), there is no need for RF calibration even for TDD.

[End CNI-86060]

[PR710475] [23R2 Only]

NOTE: Below is the same content as above, which is needed since obsoleting the UL requirement via the CNI-86060 was shifted to 23R3 via the PR710475.

For digital RF beamforming, due to UL calibration requirements from RF, the UL scheduler shall consider an algorithm to reserve rdUlCalSlotNum slots of a minimum of rdUlCalMinSymbolNum consecutive symbols for UL calibration purpose within period of rdCalDuration seconds.

A minimum timing, rdCalMinTime (in ms), is needed between two consecutive slots carrying UL calibration symbols.

This RF calibration dedicated radio resource shall not be used for any data transmissions. The 5G-L2-PS shall send DlData_PatternConfigReq message to 5G-L1-DL with parameter of calibrationBitmap to indicate which symbols shall be reserved for calibration in each 5GNR-slot, which shall be all symbols of a slot.

The TD scheduler shall use 5GNR UL data slots with data symbols for RF calibration.

5G-L2-PS shall maintain a counter slotCountForUlCalibration to trigger UL calibration actions. Counter shall be updated every scheduling period. At startup and every rdCalDuration seconds counter is reinitialized to rdUlCalSlotNum.

At first UL calibration opportunity, TD scheduler checks if a UL calibration is needed. A calibration is needed when slotCountForUlCalibration counter is not null and when previous UL calibration was triggered more than rdCalMinTime ms before. If a slot is used for UL calibration, counter slotCountForUlCalibration is decreased by 1.

rdCalDuration : see 5G_L2_3732_replaced([11183498](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27j-afvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdDUCalMinSymbolNum : see 5G_L2_1048_replaced([11191387](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28dWSPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdUlCalSlotNum : see 5G_L2_11403_replaced([11182869](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF5vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdCalMinTime : see 5G_L2_11402_replaced([11183349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iwSfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For data symbols used as calibration symbols: TD scheduler could manage those calibration opportunities when no users are scheduled or enforce them if needed. 5G-PS-L2 shall set data symbols (Ud in frame format) for calibration in DlData_PatternConfigReq message (in calibrationBitmap set bits associated to RF calibration symbols to 1).5G-L2-PS shall transmit the message DlData_PdcchSendReq only in case it contains a non-zero RB assignment and omit the message otherwise.

NOTE:

  * UL and DL calibrations are independent of each other (see 5G_UP_ALG_4098_replaced([11173686](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuVObfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for DL).

  * Any data scheduled together with calibration will be lost.




If no beamforming is applied, see 5G_UP_ALG_2183_replaced([11112736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZEBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_907_replaced([11353384](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub-6UvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), there is no need for RF calibration even for TDD.

[End PR710475]

### 3.30.1.0-6 Scheduling Overview UL Scheduling of 5G UEs (ID: 12772430)

**3.30.1.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `12641953`)

Scheduling Overview 12641953: Scheduling Overview The scheduling of the 5G UEs in UL shall be processed in several steps on 5GTF-subframe/NR-Slot basis. An overview of the procedural steps is given generically by the subsequent figure.

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart depicting a process. The flowchart begins with a rounded rectangle labeled "Start". This is followed by a rectangular box labeled "UL Candidate Set 1". Next, another rectangular box contains two lines of text: "UL Time Domain Scheduling" and "-> UL Candidate Set 2". Following this is a rectangular box labeled "ULxPDCCH Scheduling". The next rectangular box is labeled "UL Frequency Domain Scheduling". The flowchart concludes with a rounded rectangle labeled "End". The background of the image is black, and the flowchart elements are white with black text.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15010.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

The following procedure applies for the UL scheduling of 5G UEs.

UL Candidate Set 1:

The set of 5G UEs selected by the UL Pre-Scheduling as candidate to be scheduled in UL (UL Candidate Set 1, see 5G_UP_ALG_4765_replaced([11194278](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvz1GPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is the input to the UL Time Domain Scheduling.

UL Time Domain Scheduling:

The time domain scheduler shall evaluate the scheduling metric of all 5G UEs which are eligible for the scheduling in UL (Candidate Set 1).

For analog beamforming, a scheduling metric per x-pol beam is defined from all the 5G UEs that are served by the corresponding beam based on the scheduling metrics of the 5G UEs. The x-pol beam with the highest metric is then selected for scheduling. With the selection of the x-pol beam the set of 5G UEs, which are available for the resource assignment on xPUSCH are determined. These are the 5G UEs which are accessible via the selected x-pol beam in the order of the scheduling metric.

For digital beamforming, all 5G UEs from candidate set 1 are available for the resource assignment on PUSCH.

The 5G UE with the highest scheduling metric shall be taken into account first in FD scheduling continued by the 5G UEs in the order of descending scheduling metric. The 5G UEs which are in the position for the scheduling in FD scheduler form the UL Candidate Set 2. For any details refer to the dedicated chapter: UL Time Domain Scheduling (5G_UP_ALG_4847_replaced([11195780](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-NLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE [CB007372-AA] when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-AA], 5G-L2-PS shall not put any UE except the UE with msg3 scheduled in DL to CS2 list, in the slots which are reserved for LTE in DL according to the current DL sharing pattern (5G_UP_ALG_2304_replaced([11171916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaFvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB007372-AA] Note: For NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true the Initial BWP is within the NR dedicated area (see [12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and msg2, msg3, msg4 scheduling doesn't depend on DL sharing pattern. [End CB007372-AA]

UL PDCCH Scheduling:

The PDCCH scheduler allocates the resources (CCEs) on the physical DL control channel for carrying the grants dedicated to the 5G UEs, which are finally selected for FD scheduling. A pre-defined xPDCCH format shall be used for carrying the grants to the 5G UEs. For any details refer to the dedicated chapter: UL xPDCCH Scheduling (5G_UP_ALG_4919_replaced([11198742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling:

The scheduling of the 5G UEs of Candidate Set 2 in FD scheduler includes details about the resource allocation in terms of number of allocated PRBs/RBGs as well as the location of the physical resources within the carrier, the MCS and hence the transport block size. Only a predefined number of 5G UEs in Candidate Set 2 can be scheduled in FD scheduler. The frequency domain scheduler is responsible for the allocation of the physical resources over all carriers and provides the allocation details per carrier.

In opposite to the conditions in DL, the time relation of PDCCH and PUSCH are different. While the resources on PDCCH are allocated at NR slot n the corresponding UL transmission allocates resources on PUSCH at NR slot n+k2. For any details refer to the dedicated chapter: UL Frequency Domain Scheduling (5G_UP_ALG_4959_replaced([11199240](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: This description is meant to provide an overview on the overall 5G UEs UL Scheduling flow. The detailed requirements in the dedicated chapters specify what has to be supported in each subfeature. The validity TAG at the top of each requirement indicates whether it is valid for a certain subfeature or not.

---

### 3.30.1.0-8 Scheduling Overview UL Scheduling of 5G UEs per L2 PS instance with traffic prioritization (ID: 11194284)

**3.30.1.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11194294`)

Scheduling Overview Required for the subsequent scheduler tasks is that the UE context for each UE contains the serving X-Pol beam index, see beam index definition in the beam management chapter (see 5G_UP_ALG_2183_replaced([11112736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZEBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and beam index initialization in 5G_L2_134_replaced([11143879](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_232jHvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Furthermore, the pre-requisite is that the UL pre-scheduler has determined the set of active UEs (Candidate set 1) as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_4804_replaced([11194813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv3fdvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Candidate set 1 according to 5G_UP_ALG_4825_replaced([11195013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4tnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is input for the TD scheduler.

The overall processing of the UL scheduler (in each L2 PS instance) shall be according to the following steps.

Step 1: Beam selection for analog beamforming by the TD scheduler  


For analog beamforming, the TD scheduler determines the X-Pol beam (best beam) to be used in the next scheduling NR slot taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource request classes into account, see 5G_UP_ALG_4769_replaced([11194310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvz1JPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The high level prioritization concept defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4770_replaced([11194316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the request class definition details are specified in 5G_UP_ALG_4773_replaced([11194371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that step 1 is skipped for digital beamforming since no beam selection is required.

[CB012734-E] If NRBTS/NRCELLGRP.actHighNumberOfUes = TRUE and rdActEnhBeamSelection = TRUE, the step1 shall be skipped as beam selection for analog beamforming is already performed in Pre-scheduler as described in 5G_UP_ALG_27614_replaced([11194972](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4tl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB012734-E]

Step 2: UE selection and carrier assignment by the TD scheduler

For analog beamforming, the TD scheduler determines the set of UEs served by the best beam (rrmUlCandidateSet1Selected). [CB012734-E] If NRBTS/NRCELLGRP.actHighNumberOfUes = TRUE and rdActEnhBeamSelection = TRUE, all UEs from candidate set 1 are considered. [End CB012734-E]

For digital beamforming, all UEs from candidate set 1 are considered.

Out of those, for each carrier the set of UEs which are candidate to be scheduled in that carrier (CS2 subcell specific lists) is determined taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource request classes into account, see 5G_UP_ALG_4769_replaced([11194310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvz1JPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The high level prioritization concept defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4770_replaced([11194316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the request class definition details are specified in 5G_UP_ALG_4773_replaced([11194371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Overload Control may limit UE selection and carrier assignment. See 5G_L2_1820_replaced([11212319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-k2IvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

if NRBTS/NRDU.flexibleCAMode is set to NumberCellAmount (value 3) and there are two cells setup in the same L2-PS core:

 For CA FDD Pcell with distributed PUCCH allocation (5G_UP_ALG_2885_replaced([11123447](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqjhoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), in DPA process slot of Pcell (Measurement Processing cycle in 5G_UP_ALG_2936_replaced([11123865](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqmk8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) may limit UE selection. detail to be decided in CP3 level design.

 serving cell may trigger overload control eariler compare with one cell per L2-PS core, since one cell per L2-PS core have more free time to handle other activity besides schedule (like different cell L2-PS message exchange , L2-PS interface message with other component handling), which will additional cut down available schedule time. detail to be decided in CP3 level design.

Step 3: HARQ handling per subcell

HARQ handling per carrier may be an integral part of the TD scheduler UE selection procedure as the UE selection procedure directly determines the UE that will have to perform retransmission on a carrier. UL HARQ handling is specified in 5G_UP_ALG_5070_replaced([11202307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcHPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 4: DCI creation and PDCCH scheduling

According to the outcome of the TD scheduler the following scheduling tasks shall be executed:  


For each carrier the DCI for each transmission on PUSCH shall be created and scheduled for transmission on the PDCCH.

---

### 3.30.1.0-10 Scheduling Overview UL Scheduling for the artificial load generation If related RD parameter has been set (rdUlDummyUserConfiguration, see 5G_L2_1814_replaced( 11212167 )), and there is no UE scheduled in any cell of the cell group in given slot, artificial load needs to be scheduled. Beam is selected according to RD parameter. The artificial PUSCH load needs to be generated on the cell which subcellId = 0,, in the FD scheduling, PUSCH receive request need to sent to L1, which should be based on the following table: RNTI decide by RD parameter MCS decide by RD parameter ncsid hardcode to 0 ulPTRS hardcode to 0 ulPtrsTimeDensity hardcode to 0 ulPtrsFrequencyDensity hardcode to 0 ulPtrsNumofPorts hardcode to 0 ulPtrsResElemoffset hardcode to 0 harqprocessIndex Fill as the slot number for the current TTI absoluteharqprocessIndex Fill as the slot number for the current TTI freshHarqTrans hardcode to 1 selfContainedFlag Always set to NON_SELF_CONTAINED ulDmrsConfigType Always set to DMRS_CONFIG_1 numOfPuschSymbols get from slot type ulDmrsLen fix to 1 tbSizeInBits decided by MCS, PRB and DRMS length startPrb set to 0 numOfPrbs the MAX PRB number in default BWP numOfLayers RD parameter modulationOrder decide by MCS ulDmrsMappingType Always set to DMRS_MAPPING_TYPE_B startSymbol set to 0 mcsTable set to 64QAM If the long PUCCH is selected (IE pucchModeSelect in PsCell_CellSetupReq is set to longPucch), the parameters startPrb and numOfPrbs are different. The parameter startPrb shall be set to longPucchStartPuschPrb (IE longPucchStartPuschPrb is provided in PsCell_CellSetupReq) and numOfPrbs shall be set to longPucchNumPuschPrb (IE longPucchNumPuschPrb is provided in PsCell_CellSetupReq). (ID: 11194304)

## 3.30.2 UL Traffic prioritization concept

### 3.30.2.1 Definition of resource request classes for different traffic types

#### 3.30.2.1.0-1 Definition of resource request classes for different traffic types TD scheduler request classes and prioritization concept (ID: 11194320)

**3.30.2.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11194327`)

Definition of resource request classes for different traffic types The UL TD scheduler decisions shall be based on a prioritization concept using (resource) request classes for different traffic types:

The concept is based on the following principles:

• The resource request classes shall represent sets of UEs with different scheduling priorities. The scheduling priorities depend on the type of traffic that has to be scheduled.

• The request classes are strictly ordered according to their request class priority, i.e. UEs in request classes with higher priority are prioritized over UEs in request classes with lower priority.

• UEs in higher request classes are prioritized over UEs in lower request classes independent of their request class subpriorities.

• UEs within the same request class are ordered according to different request class sub-priorities. The concept for this ordering depends again on the different traffic request classes.

• UEs with higher request class sub-priority within a request class are prioritized over UEs with lower request class sub-priority.

A resource request class is 'Component Carrier specific', if its sub-priority metric is Component Carrier specific.

A resource request class is 'not Component Carrier specific', if its sub-priority metric is not component carrier specific.

Note that for non CA UE the differentation of 'component carrier specific' or 'not component carrier' specific is not required.

A 5GUE can be a member of zero, one or more request classes per slot. Depending on the dynamic scheduling decisions and traffic properties the membership of a 5GUE may change in every slot.  


The packet scheduler derives the information of required retransmissions from 5G-L1-UL based on the received CRC or DTX information for the PUSCH transmission.

Please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_10322_replaced([11194333](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) the list of UL resource request class priorities.

Note:   
For future releases additional request classes will be added e.g. to support extended QoS concepts and related different priorities will be assigned to the extended set. This might change the defined priorities from above.

---

#### 3.30.2.1.0-2 Definition of resource request classes for different traffic types Full list of UL TD resource request class priorities (ID: 11194333)

**3.30.2.1.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `13360982`)

Definition of resource request classes for different traffic types Please find following request classes, priorities and sub-prioritization concepts are used:

|  |  |  |  |   
---|---|---|---|---|---  
**Request class** | **Request class priority** | **NIDD parameter for request class priority** | **Request description** | **Component Carrier specific / Not Component Carrier specific** | **Remark**  
rachMsg3 | 90 | prioUlRachMsg3 | RACH msg3 (from DL grant) refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4775_replaced ([11194381](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Component Carrier specific |   
contentResGrant | 85 | prioUlContentResGrant | UL grant for contention resolution refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4777_replaced ([11194390](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Component Carrier specific |   
harqRetransRach | 80 | prioUlHarqRetransRach | HARQ retransmission for msg3 refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4779_replaced ([11194401](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv1DMPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Component Carrier specific |   
criticalHarqRetransSrb | 78 | prioUlCriticalHarqRetransSrb | Critical HARQ retransmissions for SRB1/2 or 5QI5/69 SIP signaling, refer to [13361400](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_JPBmMPP_EfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). | Component Carrier specific | [CB014699-B] Added for re-transmission of critical SRB1/2 or 5QI-5/69 [End CB014699-B].  
criticalHarqRetransVoip | 76 | prioUlCriticalHarqRetransVoip | HARQ retransmissions for critical 5QI-1/65/66 VoIP, refer to[13361403](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_nHkt0fQAEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). | Component Carrier specific | [CB014699-B] Added for critical 5QI-1/65/66 GBR re-transmission [End CB014699-B].  
criticalHarqRetransGbr | 74 | prioUlCriticalHarqRetransGbr | HARQ retransmissions for critical non-5QI-1/65/66 GBR, refer to [13376663](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_qbi_KPxHEfCPDo8cGBoqIw) | Component Carrier specific | [CB014699-C] Added for critical non-5QI-1/65/66 GBR retransmissions. [End CB014699-C].

**NIDD parameter for request class priority are hidden parameters; the values are restricted to the value defined in** Request class priority column.****

[CB014699-B] Note: SRB3 is not prioritized for UE with critical traffic [End CB014699-B].

Note: values in implementation are simply relative priority from enumeration. 

Note: AnyTrafficBlockedInPDCCH is removed as not present in implementation.

---

### 3.30.2.2 Determination of the request classes

#### 3.30.2.2.0-1 Determination of the request classes For all request classes: (ID: 11194371)

**3.30.2.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11194376`)

Determination of the request classes UEs are ordered according to their request class sub-priorities. In case the request class sub-priorities are equal those UEs with equal request class sub-priorities are ordered in random order.

NOTE: Different LCGs for SRB and DRB are introduced by 5GC000578-J. For example, SRB with LCG0 and non-GBR DRB with LCG1. 5QI1 GBR DRBs must be put into a different LCG than non-GBR DRBs. 5QI5 DRBs should be treated as signaling and put in LCG0.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-2 Determination of the request classes Request class rachMsg3 (ID: 11194381)

**3.30.2.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11194386`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall take the UE that requires the transmission of RACH msg3 in the carrier according to the grant created in a previous DL TD scheduling step. Msg3 transmission is prioritized over all other request classes as the Msg3 transmission was already selected in a previous downlink TD scheduler step and shall not be made void.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-3 Determination of the request classes Request class contentResGrant (ID: 11194390)

**3.30.2.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11194396`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require an UL grant for contention resolution after Msg 3 reception (5G_UP_ALG_3437_replaced([11152927](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjFs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This set of UEs creates the request class contentResGrant. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since the UE was added to this class. The UE with the most time passed shall have the highest sub-priority.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-4 Determination of the request classes Request class harqRetransRach (Msg3) (ID: 11194401)

**3.30.2.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11194405`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending Msg3 retransmissions in the carrier. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransRach for Msg3 retransmission UEs. Within this set the reached number of Msg3 HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities within the set.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-5 Determination of the request classes Request class criticalHarqRetransSrb (ID: 13361400)

**3.30.2.2.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `13361402`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending retransmissions for logical channels containing RRC complete messages meant for RRC re-establishment, critical SRB1/2 or critical 5QI-5/69**in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalHarqRetransSrb for critical SRB1/2 retransmission UEs.**

Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities within the set.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-6 Determination of the request classes Request class criticalHarqRetransVoip (ID: 13361403)

**3.30.2.2.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `13361404`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending critical VoNR(5QI1/65/66) traffic retransmissions in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalHarqRetransVoip for critical 5QI1/65/66 retransmission UEs.

Within this set, gNB-DU shall determine, for each UE, the HARQ process with highest sub-priority, then order critical UEs by their HARQ sub-priority. HARQ process sub-priority is determined by the highest performed Tx number then by the longest time since first transmission such that HARQ critical retransmissions are performed as fast as possible.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-7 Determination of the request classes Request class criticalHarqRetransGbr (ID: 13376663)

**3.30.2.2.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `13376688`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending critical non-VoIP(excluding 5QI1/65/66) GBR retransmissions in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class critical harqRetransGbr for non-5QI-1/65/66 GBR retransmission UEs.   
Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities, i.e. the sub-priority is higher when more retransmissions have already been performed.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-8 Determination of the request classes Request class criticalHarqRetransDrb (ID: 13399476)

**3.30.2.2.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `13399477`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending critical non-GBR retransmission in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalHarqRetransDrb for critical non-GBR. Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities, i.e. the sub-priority is higher when more retransmissions have already been performed.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-9 Determination of the request classes Request class harqRetransSrb (ID: 11194410)

**3.30.2.2.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11194423`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending retransmissions for logical channels containing RRC complete messages meant for RRC re-establishment, SRB or 5QI-5 [CB008118-A] or 5QI69 [End CB008118-A] in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransSrb for SRB retransmission UEs.

Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities within the set.

NOTE: Currently it is assumed that SRB1, SRB2, SRB3,5QI5 [CB008118-A] and 5QI69 [End CB008118-A] are configured within the same LCG(LCG0). Therefore, single 'SRB' resource request class is defined and it can be applied to NSA SRB3 introduced by 5GC000578, SA SRB2 introduced by 5GC000752, SA SRB1 introduced by 5GC000723, 5QI5 SIP Signaling introduced by 5GC000786-A [CB008118-A] and 5QI69 SIP signalling introduced by CB008118-A. [End CB008118-A]

Note: design is deviating from spec and HARQ prioritization is not correctly handled as observed in PR803239

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-10 Determination of the request classes Request class harqReTransDcGbr (ID: 11194427)

**3.30.2.2.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11194430`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending retransmissions [PR834641] of the initial transmissions scheduled via dynamic grants intended [End PR834641] for the prioritized DC-GBR traffic (the corresponding initial transmission was intended for prioritized DC-GBR data, see 5G_UP_ALG_30945_replaced([11196041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [PR834641], including srResp and dcGbrTraffic priority class grants with prioritised DC-GBR bearer[End PR834641]) in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransDcGbr for DC-GBR 5QI traffic retransmission UEs. Within this set, the subpriorities are defined by the delay term, calculated according to the head of line packet delay of the MAC-PDU waiting for re-transmission. 

The head-of-line (HOL) packet delay of the MAC-PDU waiting for re-transmission shall be the sum of the HOL delay for the DC-GBR DRB at the time of scheduling the initial transmission (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_30952_replaced([11196063](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) plus the delay accumulated since then until the time of scheduling the PUSCH retransmission slot

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-11 Determination of the request classes Request class harqRetransVoip (ID: 11194436)

**3.30.2.2.0-11.0-3**  (ID: `11194450`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending [Before CB008118-A]5QI-1[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] voice retransmissions in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransVoip for [Before CB008118-A]5QI-1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] retransmission UEs.

Within this set, gNB-DU shall determine, for each UE, the HARQ process with highest subpriority, then order UEs by their HARQ subpriority. HARQ process subpriority is determined by the highest performed tx number then by the longest time since first transmission such that HARQ retransmissions are performed as fast as possible.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-12 Determination of the request classes Request class harqRetransGbr (ID: 11194456)

**3.30.2.2.0-12.0-2**  (ID: `11194466`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending [Before CB008118-A] non-5QI-1[CB008118-A] non-VoIP(excluding 5QI1/65/66) [End CB008118-A] GBR retransmissions in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransGbr for non-5QI-1 GBR retransmission UEs.

Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities, i.e. the sub-priority is higher when more retransmissions have already been performed.

Note: design is deviating from spec and HARQ prioritization is not correctly handled as observed in PR803239

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-13 Determination of the request classes Request class anticipatedUlGrantSrb (ID: 11194471)

**3.30.2.2.0-13.0-2**  (ID: `11194480`)

Determination of the request classes When priority boost for AUG is enabled (NRCELL.actAugSrbPrioBoost is set to enable), gNB-DU shall determine the set of UEs that require transmission for anticipated UL-DCCH (SRB) following DL SRB messages (DL-xCCH) under the following conditions

1\. Until first DRB configuration for UE **[CNI-127390-D] or**

2\. When NRCELL.actRrcReconfProcessingDelay set to true, the SRB buffer status change impacts UE-Context at gNB-DU indicated by higher layer [PR837690] or

3\. When UE context modification is indicated with UE dedicated config state as "disable" or "in progress" by higher layer [End PR837690]

**[End CNI-127390-D]**

[PR841915] gNB-DU shall ensure that at least one uplink grant with priority boost is granted when any of the conditions mentioned above are met. [End PR841915]

Once gNB-DU applies this priority to UE, it shall continue to maintain this priority under the following conditions

[Before PR833408-25R3]

1\. Until it receives data [Before PR830327-25R3] and a null BSR for the UE’s LCG to which SRB is mapped, to avoid one RLC segment being stalled in a lower priority class [PR830327-25R3] irrespective of BSR existence and its value [End PR830327-25R3].

2\. SRB buffer status change following a PsUser_User[Setup,**[CNI-127390-D]Modify**[End CNI-127390-D]]Req, until RRC Response data is received on the LCGs to which SRB is mapped [Before PR830327-25R3] with BSR = 0 [PR829206-25R3] or with no BSR [End PR829206-25R3] [PR830327-25R3] irrespective of BSR existence and its value [End PR830327-25R3].****

[PR833408-25R3]

  1. Until it receives a MAC data PDU with the SRB1 logical channel identity or a MAC data PDU with a logical channel identity value higher than the SRB1 logical channel identity. Rationale: In an uplink CA scenario, the PCell can stop the AUG after receiving a MAC data PDU with a logical channel identity higher than SRB1, indicating that the SCell has received the RRC response. [PR841915] Or
  2. Until Max uplink Harq transmission is reached (NRBTS/NRCELLGRP.maxUlHarqTx) [End PR841915]



[End PR833408-25R3]

[Before PR833408-25R3]

_Rationale: In this case, gNB-DU knows that an RRC Response is expected from UE. In case a grant is sent early to UE, UE can respond only with RLC status PDU with NULL BSR. gNB-DU shall continue to maintain this priority for the RRC response. Note: This is a special case addressed to ensure the priority boost is applied until the response is received._

[End PR833408-25R3]

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-14 Determination of the request classes Request class cgModification (ID: 11194486)

**3.30.2.2.0-14.0-1**  (ID: `11194492`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall create this request class locally. TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs requesting for CG modification.

The higher the age of the UE in CG-Modification state, the higher sub-priority in the class the UE shall have.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-15 Determination of the request classes Request class cgHarqRetrans (ID: 11194497)

**3.30.2.2.0-15.0-1**  (ID: `11194500`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall create this request class locally. TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending CG retransmission. This set of UEs creates the request class cgHarqRetrans for CG retransmission UEs.

[Before CB008124-C]Note: sub-priorities could be defined with future enhancements.

[CB008124-C] The reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities within the set. [End CB008124-C]

Note: design is deviating from spec and HARQ prioritization is not correctly handled as observed in PR803239

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-16 Determination of the request classes Request class cgDeactivation (ID: 11194506)

**3.30.2.2.0-16.0-1**  (ID: `11194512`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall create this request class locally. TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs requesting configured grant deactivation. This set of UEs creates the request class cgDeactivation for CG UEs that request CG deactivation.

[Before CB008124-C]There are no sub-priorities to be defined.[CB008124-C] A UE in CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling state shall have higher sub-priority than a UE in CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder state. The higher the age of the UE in is corresponding CG Deactivation state, i.e. CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder or CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling, the higher sub-priority in the class the UE shall have. [End CB008124-C]

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-17 Determination of the request classes Request class harqRetransDrb (ID: 11194518)

**3.30.2.2.0-17.0-4**  (ID: `11194538`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs with pending retransmissions in the cell for logical channels containing non-GBR data(including NBR category, [CB008118-A] MC-nGBR (5QI70) [End CB008118-A] and normal non-GBR category), [CB008600-B] de-prioritized DC-GBR data, [End CB008600-B] deprioritized [Before CB008118-A]VoIP 5QI1 [CB008118-A] VoIP 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] data, deprioritized [Before CB008118-A] non-5QI-1 [CB008118-A] non-VoIP [End CB008118-A] GBR, and TCP Boost proactive grants. This set of UEs creates the request class harqRetransDrb for retransmission UEs.

Within this set the reached number of HARQ retransmissions determines the sub-priorities within the set.

Although the TCP boost proactive grants are lower priority than DRB traffic, the uplink transmission associated with the proactive grant may contain time sensitive data such as TCP ACKs. Because of this the retransmissions should not be delayed and should be treated with equal priority as DRB traffic retransmissions. 

Note: design is deviating from spec and HARQ prioritization is not correctly handled as observed in PR803239

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-18 Determination of the request classes Request class srResp (ID: 11194545)

**3.30.2.2.0-18.0-3**  (ID: `11194635`)

Determination of the request classes 11194635: Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the allocation of PUSCH resources as response to a received positive SR. This set of UEs creates the request class srResp.Within this class the sub-priorities are based [CB013961-A], if NRCELL.actServiceAwareSRPrioritization is set to False, [End CB013961-A] on the residual SR response time which is calculated from current scheduling time [image-0][image_desc]The image is entirely black. srPrio = srPrioHigh * 64 + srPrioLow calculated as below (the smaller value shall have the highest priority):

srPrioHigh = [image-4][image_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15064.005.png[/image_path][/image-4] , where [image-5][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15064.006.png[/image_path][/image-5]

srPrioLow = floor([image-6][image_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15064.007.png[/image_path][/image-6])

for sr-TransMax refer to 5G_CP_IF_RRC_4868_replaced([11358091](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wZAdFfvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note: in he formula above, 64 represents the smallest integer power of two that is higher than or equal to the maximum possible value of sr-TransMax. [End CB013961-A]

([image-7][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15064.008.png[/image_path][/image-7] refer to the time that scheduling request was received; [image-8][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15064.009.png[/image_path][/image-8] refer to SR periodicity of this UE 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_7611_replaced([11312715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Trksc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

In addition to UEs for which a positive SR was received, this class contains UEs with no DRX configuration for which isUlGrantNeededForRlcStatusPdu (5G_UP_ALG_5134_replaced([11203852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) set to TRUE was received in LoCtrl_UlMacPduReceiveInd message as in 5G_UP_ALG_5134_replaced([11203852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), i.e., UEs with no DRX configuration that should be scheduled in the uplink to allow the transmission of a RLC status PDU. Rationale for the gNB initiated UL grant is to avoid RLC window stalling when many PDUs were sent but UE couldn't send a SR due to limited SR occassions.

[CB013961-A] If NRCELL.actServiceAwareSRPrioritization is set to True, before adding UE to srResp class, UE is evaluated for scheduling with DRB priority in accordance with SR response scheduling flow (see 5G_UP_ALG_31474_replaced([11203903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB013961-A].

While a UE is in request class srResp its sub-priority shall not be updated even if 5G-L2-PS receives another SR or another LoCtrl_UlMacPduReceiveInd with isUlGrantNeededForRlcStatusPdu = 1 (see 5G_UP_ALG_5134_replaced([11203852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the UE.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-19 Determination of the request classes Request class criticalSrbTraffic (ID: 13361405)

**3.30.2.2.0-19.0-1**  (ID: `13361408`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the first transmission of logical channels which may carry critical SRB1/2 or critical 5QI5/69 data in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalSrbTraffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since UL BSR indicated critical SRB1/2 or critical 5QI5/69 data presence in the buffer or since last time housekeeping function estimated an empty transmission buffer for SRB LCG ([11203641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE: Currently it is assumed that SRB1/2 and 5QI5/69 are configured within the same LCG(LCG0). Therefore, single 'SRB' resource request class is defined.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-20 Determination of the request classes Request class criticalVoipTraffic (ID: 13361409)

**3.30.2.2.0-20.0-1**  (ID: `13361410`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require scheduling opportunities for critical 5QI1/65/66 traffic. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalVoipTraffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since UL BSR indicated critical**5QI1/65/66 data presence in the buffer.**

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-21 Determination of the request classes Request class criticalGbrTraffic (ID: 13376672)

**3.30.2.2.0-21.0-1**  (ID: `13376673`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of critical GBR data (excluding 5QI1/65/66) data in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalGbrTraffic. Within this class the sub-priority is based on the GBR scheduling criteria (refer to [11196133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_9nyioPvPEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For the UE with multi critical GBR bearers, the sub-priority is based on the scheduling criteria of the critical GBR bearer with the highest GBR Delay metric.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-22 Determination of the request classes Request class criticalDrbTraffic (ID: 13399502)

**3.30.2.2.0-22.0-1**  (ID: `13399503`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the first transmission of critical non-GBR data in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class criticalDrbTraffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the proportional fair metric calculated for the UE based on the channel quality of the cell and the total critical DRB traffic sent in the past on any cell. The proportional fair metric is calculated according to [11195815](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[CB014699-D] Nominal bit rate configuration is ignored (if configured) for critical non-GBR bearers.

If a critical GBR bearer (positive token) is de-prioritized due to GBR congestion, it is treated as critical non-GBR traffic and moved to the criticalDrbTraffic request class [End CB014699-D].

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-23 Determination of the request classes Request class srbTraffic (ID: 11194641)

**3.30.2.2.0-23.0-3**  (ID: `11194655`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the first transmission of logical channels which may carry SRB or [Before CB008118-A]5QI5 [CB008118-A] 5QI5/69 [End CB008118-A] data in the cell. This set of UEs creates the request class srbTraffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since UL BSR indicated SRB or [Before CB008118-A]5QI5 [CB008118-A] 5QI5/69 [End CB008118-A] data presence in the buffer or since last time housekeeping function estimated an empty transmission buffer for SRB LCG (see 5G_UP_ALG_5115_replaced([11203641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[Before CB008118-A]

NOTE: Currently it is assumed that SRB1, SRB2, SRB3 and 5QI5 are configured within the same LCG(LCG0). Therefore, single 'SRB' resource request class is defined and it can be applied to NSA SRB3 introduced by 5GC000578, SA SRB2 introduced by 5GC000752, SA SRB1 introduced by 5GC000723, and 5QI5 SIP Signaling introduced by 5GC000786-A

[CB008118-A]

NOTE: Currently it is assumed that SRB1, SRB2, SRB3 and 5QI5/5QI69 are configured within the same LCG(LCG0). Therefore, single 'SRB' resource request class is defined and it can be applied to NSA SRB3 introduced by 5GC000578, SA SRB2 introduced by 5GC000752, SA SRB1 introduced by 5GC000723, 5QI5 SIP Signaling introduced by 5GC000786-A, and 5QI69 SIP Signaling introduced by CB008118-A [End CB008118-A].

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-24 Determination of the request classes Request class dcGbrTraffic (ID: 11194661)

**3.30.2.2.0-24.0-1**  (ID: `11194665`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of prioritized DC-GBR data in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class dcGbrTraffic. Within this class the sub-priority is based on the DC-GBR scheduling criteria. See 5G_UP_ALG_30945_replaced([11196041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-25 Determination of the request classes Request class voipTraffic (ID: 11194670)

**3.30.2.2.0-25.0-2**  (ID: `11194682`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require scheduling opportunities for [Before CB008118-A]5QI-1[CB008118-A]5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] logical channel. This set of UEs creates the request class voipTraffic. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since UL BSR indicated [Before CB008118-A]5QI1[CB008118-A]5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] data presence in the buffer.

[Before CB008118-A]

Note: The 5QI-1 DRB shall be mapped to an LCG that contains 5QI-1 only.

[CB008118-A]

Note: The 5QI1/65/66 DRB shall be mapped to an LCG that contains 5QI1/65/66 only.

[End CB008118-A]

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-26 Determination of the request classes Request class gbrTraffic (ID: 11194687)

**3.30.2.2.0-26.0-3**  (ID: `11194703`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of GBR data [Before CB008118-A] (excluding 5QI-1)[CB008118-A] (excluding 5QI1/65/66) [End CB008118-A] in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class gbrTraffic. Within this class the sub-priority is based on the GBR scheduling criteria. See 5G_UP_ALG_8045_replaced([11196133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. For the UE with multi GBR bearers, the sub-priority is based on the scheduling criteria of the GBR bearer with the highest GBR Delay metric.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-27 Determination of the request classes Request class drbTraffic (ID: 11194709)

**3.30.2.2.0-27.0-7**  (ID: `13416023`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the first transmission of DRB data in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class drbTraffic.

Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the proportional fair metric calculated for the UE based on the channel quality of the cell and the total DRB traffic sent in the past on any cell. The proportional fair metric is calculated according to 5G_UP_ALG_4851_replaced([11195815](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This class shall be divided into three categories and with order**:**

1- NBR Category: **If NRCELL.actNbrForNonGbrBearers = TRUE, the UE having at least one non-GBR bearers with UL nominal bitrate configured which belonging to an LCG that with positive token bucket and not de-prioritized according to NBR congestion control algorithm defined in 5G_UP_ALG_22901_replaced([11201474](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwWnpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for which sub-priorities are based scheduling metric, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_22260_replaced([11196307](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwB3iPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB014699-D] Critical non-GBR bearers shall be excluded even if UL nominal bitrate is configured [End CB014699-D].**

Note: When NRCELL.actNbrForNonGbrBearers = TRUE and NRBTS.NRCELL.actTdHighBufUEPrio=UL_Only or DLandUL, buffer length alone can be used as the selection criterion for high-buffer UEs, regardless of NBR prioritization or token availability. As such, a high-buffer UE could be an NBR bearer or a non-GBR bearer, depending solely on buffer status.

2- Large buffer non-GBR Category:

  * If NRCELL.actLargeBufferUe is equal to UL or ULAndDL, the UEs which satisfy the following conditions shall be added into this Category:

  * Total amount of buffered non-GBR data is larger than NRCELL.dataBufferThresholdHighUl, and

  * Proposed MCS rrmUlProposedMcs calculated with full BW allocation shall be NOT smaller than rdMcsThresholdUl (5G_UP_ALG_33231_replaced([11209793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw1v2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

  * The gNB shall remove the UE from this Category on the following condition:

  * Total amount of buffered non-GBR data is smaller than or equal to NRCELL.dataBufferThresholdLowUl.

  * When NRCELL.dataBufferThresholdLowUl is set to 0, it means UE will be kept as "Large buffer non-GBR Category" until all the data buffer have been drained. After UE is removed from "Large buffer non-GBR Category", the PF-metric is re-initialized. The sub-priority scheduling criteria for large buffer non-GBR category, please refer to [12687283](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_9OlzAFV1EfC45IKMrv0u7Q).

  * The deprioritized NBR due to congestion is not qualified for Large buffer non-GBR category.
  * The deprioritized GBR due to congestion is not qualified for Large buffer non-GBR category.
  * **Note: Radio link quality is not checked for the exit of "Large buffer non-GBR Category" status to avoid the risk of ping-pong by radio link quality fluctuation.**



3- Normal non-GBR Category: All other UEs with non-GBR bearers, which sub-priorities are based on the proportional fair metric calculated for the UE based on the channel quality of the cell and the total DRB traffic sent in the past on any cell. The proportional fair metric is calculated according to 5G_UP_ALG_4851_replaced([11195815](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Congestion deprioritized GBR shall be treated in the normal non-GBR category or Large-Buffer non-GBR category if all the conditions are satisfied and use the specific scheduling weight rdCongDepriorGbrSchedulWeight for SchedWeightFactor defined in 5G_UP_ALG_22269_replaced([11195869](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0OfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For rdCongDepriorGbrSchedulWeight refer to (5G_UP_ALG_31697_replaced([11193461](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

**If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 0, HwVariant is not ABIL, and the real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G_UP_ALG_31301_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value provided by the gNB-DU overload control handler, is greater than or equal to the activation threshold of rdNominalBitrateOlcUlPerCent (See 5G_UP_ALG_31841_replaced([11216990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxcMwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then, the NBR category bearers are deprioritised in bulk to normal non-GBR category, irrespective of the NBR congestion control algorithm defined in 5G_UP_ALG_22592_replaced([11183359](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvJtzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When the real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G_UP_ALG_31301_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value provided by the gNB-DU overload control handler, is below the value set for **deactivation threshold of rdNominalBitrateOlcUlPerCent (See 5G_UP_ALG_31841_replaced([11216990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxcMwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then all those bearers that were NBR category previously, or are setup newly during the overloaded duration when the countermeasureare is active, are eligible for NBR categorisation and are repriorised as per individual scheduling metric and availability of data in buffer.****

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-28 Determination of the request classes Request class tcpBoostTraffic (ID: 11194737)

**3.30.2.2.0-28.0-1**  (ID: `11194741`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that shall get TCP boost proactive grant scheduling opportunities. This set of UEs creates the request class tcpBoostTraffic. Within this class the sub-priority is based on the time passed since the last UL PUSCH scheduling for the UE.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-29 Determination of the request classes Request class deprioritizedVoip (ID: 11194746)

**3.30.2.2.0-29.0-3**  (ID: `13374647`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that do not meet the packet aggregation criteria (see 5G_UP_ALG_4909_replaced ([11198468](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_4912_replaced ([11198620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and require the first transmission of [CB014699-B] Critical 5QI-1/65/66 or [End CB014699-B] 5QI1/65/66 **radio bearer in any of the allocated cells. This set of UEs creates the request class deprioritizedVoip. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the length of time that the head of line VoIP packet has been in the UE's buffer. The priority is highest for packets that have been in buffer longest.**

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-30 Determination of the request classes Request class deprioritizedGbr (ID: 11194763)

**3.30.2.2.0-30.0-5**  (ID: `13377472`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that require the initial transmission of deprioritized GBR data (excluding 5QI1/65/66) [CB014699-C] and deprioritized critical GBR ((excluding critical 5QI1/65/66) due to negative token bucket. [End CB014699-C] in any of the allocated cells. Deprioritized GBR [CB014699-C] Or critical deprioritized GBR [End CB014699-C] is data for GBR bearer(s) that has/have a negative token bucket, i.e. the bearer(s)’s data transmission rate has/have exceeded the bearer(s)’s GBR value. This set of UEs creates the request class deprioritizedGbr. This criteria for GBR de-prioritization are caused by excessive data but is not applicable for GBR congestion deprioritize handling. 

Deprioritized DC-GBR traffic is data for DC-GBR bearer(s) that have exceeded either the average guaranteed flow bitrate over the averaging window (GFBR over AW) or maximum burst data volume over shorter PDB (MDBV over PDB) has been exceeded. See 5G_UP_ALG_30945_replaced ([11196041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. This set of UEs shall also be included in the request class deprioritizedGbr.

[CB014699-C] The sub-priority metric for the deprioritized critical GBRs (excluding critical 5QI-1/65/66) is computed according to their GBR scheduling criteria, see [11196133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) [End CB014699-C] 

The sub-priority metric for the deprioritised GBR, deprioritised DC-GBR bearers are computed according to their GBR and DC-GBR scheduling criteria respectively. See 5G_UP_ALG_8045_replaced ([11196133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_30945_replaced([11196041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

For the UE which belongs to deprioritizedGbr due to multiple DRBs ([CB014699-C] critical GBR/[End CB014699-C] DC-GBR/GBR), the sub-priority is based on the scheduling criteria of the bearer ([CB014699-C] critical GBR/[End CB014699-C] GBR/DC-GBR) with the highest [CB014699-C] critical GBR/[End CB014699-C]GBR/DC-GBR delay metric.

Note: Deprioritized GBR (non 5QI1/65/66) bearer [CB014699-C] and deprioritized critical GBR (critical non 5QI-1/65/66)) bearer [End CB014699-C] that has a negative token bucket, i.e. the bearer’s transmission rate has exceeded the bearer’s GBR value that was provided by the AMF. Deprioritized GBR traffic being HARQ retransmitted will have harqRetransDrb priority.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-31 Determination of the request classes Request class ulProactSched (ID: 11194788)

**3.30.2.2.0-31.0-1**  (ID: `11194793`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that shall get proactive scheduling opportunities. This set of UEs creates the request class ulProactSched. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since the last UL PUSCH scheduling for the UE on any cell.

---

#### 3.30.2.2.0-32 Determination of the request classes Request class ulTaGrant (ID: 11194799)

**3.30.2.2.0-32.0-1**  (ID: `11194804`)

Determination of the request classes The TD scheduler shall determine the set of UEs that shall get Time Alignment scheduling opportunities. This set of UEs creates the request class ulTaGrant. Within this class the sub-priorities are based on the time passed since the last UL PUSCH scheduling for the UE on any cell.

---

### 3.30.2.3 Sub priority Metric of a Resource Request Class

## 3.30.3 UL Pre-Scheduling

### 3.30.3.0-1 UL Pre-Scheduling Measurement Gap (MG) handling for SA (ID: 11194818)

**3.30.3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11194830`)

UL Pre-Scheduling UE is not eligible to UL scheduling between "starting time of MG (subframe) - K2 for UL PUSCH - mgta" and "starting time of MG (subframe) + MGL-mgta" to control the UL PUSCH transmission during MG period (refer Table 9.1.2-4: in 3GPP 38.133 to calculate number of interrupted slots).

Whenever UE receives new MeasGapConfig which is different from current one, UE will setup the new received measurement gap and perform the measurement accordingly.

5G-L2-PS will not send UlData_PucchReceiveReq to 5G-L1-UL to avoid DTX of corresponding SR/HARQ/CSI during the Measurement Gap performing period.

[CB008124-D] 5G-L2-PS shall not send UL PUSCH decode request to 5G-L1-UL for users who are configured and have active Configured Grant Type2 allocation in the UL, in the configured CG occasions during the measurement gap performing period.

After the interrupted slots due to MG is over, the UE is eligible for a Dynamic grant either during a CG slot or during a non-CG slot depending on the UE UL buffer occupancy. If due to the interruption in UL data transmission in the MG period, there is accumulation of data in the UL, such that the fixed FD resource allocation designated for CG-Type2 is insufficient, then the gNB scheduler can take a decision to overwrite the next immediate CG-Type2 for the UE, post the MG period, with a dynamic UL PUSCH grant sufficient to grant enough resources to clear the backlog of data accumulated only upto a remaining portion of data corresponding the granted packet size with CG that could be transmitted by following CG-Type2 in the upcoming CG occasion. This is done to ensure that the resources for CG are utilised diligently. Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_15617_replaced([11198720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for using dynamic grant to overwrite a CG if conditions apply. [End CB008124-D]

5G-L2-PS sends PsUser_UserModifyResp to CP-RT to ACK/NACK corresponding measurement gap setup.

When 5G-L2-PS receives a PsUser_UserModifyReq with measurement gap released configured (MeasGapConfig presents, MeasGapConfigStatus = 0), measurement gap indicated by the previously received measGapConfig should be released.

L2-PS shall take this configuration in to effect when the relevant RRC Reconfiguration Complete (PsUser_RrcReconfCompletedReq) is received to avoid scheduling complexity between L2-PS and UE due to MG, refer to 5G_CP_IF_RRC_800_replaced([11316765](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wTec1fvjEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

  * once UE is able to receive RRC reconfiguration - UE is not in MGL period(while the time of receiving MeasCapConfigStatus = 0 at L2-PS shall be no later than the time of receiving RRC reconfiguration with MeasGapConfig release at UE), MG configuration will be released.



Since feature interaction with Carrier Aggregation (CA) feature is considered for future sub-features, K2 value shall be decided from PSCell configuration in this sub-feature. This requires further tuning when CA is considered.

---

### 3.30.3.0-3 UL Pre-Scheduling UL pre-scheduler candidate set (ID: 11194840)

**3.30.3.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `12802574`)

UL Pre-Scheduling The UL Pre-scheduler shall include a 5GUE in

-UL pre-scheduler candidate set:

 if **the UE has pending transmission of RACH Msg3 (initial transmission** or HARQ retransmission).****

  * or if there is data to be sent, i.e., the buffer utilization is greater than 0 (5G_UP_ALG_5115_replaced([11203641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**.**



Note to clarify a previously unclear requirement and to align with implementation (no functional change, no CuDO impact):

for intra-L2RT UL CA as well as for inter-L2RT UL CA buffer split calculated according 5G_UP_ALG_9496_replaced([11122831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqgeXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be used for computing the buffer utilization on each carrier.

 or the UE has pending HARQ retransmission; **this applies also to an FR1 UL CA UE with nill buffer split for this carrier.**

 or the UE is eligible for PUSCH proactive scheduling (5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and its UE dedicated configuration is activated.

 or the UE is eligible for PUSCH skippable proactive grant scheduling **(5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and its UE dedicated configuration is activated.**

 or the UE is eligible for a TCP Boost proactive grant **(5G_UP_ALG_16873_replaced([11195408](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) AND there are no higher priority entries for the UE in the pre-scheduler candidate set **AND its UE dedicated configuration is activated.****

 or the UE has successfully sent Msg3 and an UL grant is needed for contention resolution **(5G_UP_ALG_3437_replaced([11152927](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjFs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **and the timer for contention resolution is running (5G_UP_ALG_12681_replaced([11152993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjFvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).****

 or a positive SR has been received for the UE.

 or if the UE has pending transmission of RA-Msg5.

 or if isUlGrantNeededForRlcStatusPdu (5G_UP_ALG_5134_replaced([11203852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) set to TRUE was received for this UE in LoCtrl_UlMacPduReceiveInd and DRX is not configured **(configured with DRX, refer 5G_UP_ALG_2263_replaced([11114367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_2265_replaced([11114390](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the UE (this UE is put in request class SrResp, see 5G_UP_ALG_5134_replaced([11203852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

 or if the UE has pending TA grant.

 A-CSI scheduling in UCI only mode is enabled and UE is eligible for A-CSI scheduling in “UCI only” mode, see 5G_UP_ALG_4812_replaced([11194863](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4GgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 [Before CB007372-AA] If NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssFastAlloc=true and retransmission would be scheduled after the end of DSS UL allocation transient phase(s) (as defined in [11172453](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuMrgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and if the number of PRBs needed for the retransmission is not greater than number of PRBs available for PUSCH calculated based on the new DSS UL allocation message(s) 

Note: This part of the requirement is moved by CB007372-AA to [11196526](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDFrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) because it isn't done by UL Pre-scheduler.

[End CB007372-AA]

---

### 3.30.3.0-4 UL Pre-Scheduling Building the list of UEs schedulable for A-CSI in "UCI only" mode (ID: 11194863)

**3.30.3.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `13136347`)

UL Pre-Scheduling [Before CNI-137616-C] If scheduling in “UCI only” mode is enabled, i.e. _rdUciOnly is set to “True” (5G_L2_3766_replaced([11183028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27gUBvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CNI-137616-C] If single K2 mode is being used ([11116305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), gNB shall determine the list of the UEs eligible for A-CSI triggering, that could be scheduled in "UCI only" mode as follows:_

 UE is eligible for A-CSI triggering according to ([11159392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktJiofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), except UEs in low A-CSI priority (i.e. those UE exclusively added due to condition 7.c in ([11159392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktJiofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The size of the UCI only list shall be limited to MIN(rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl, rrmMaxNumACsiUEs _) (see[11196426](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCeoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for RRM variables definition)_

[Before CNI-137616-C] _rdUciOnly shall be defined as R &D parameter during the CP3 phase. Range: True, False. Default value shall be set to True (5G_L2_3766_replaced([11183028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27gUBvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End CNI-137616-C]_

---

### 3.30.3.0-5 UL Pre-Scheduling Suspend Scheduling after TCI state indication (ID: 12478833)

**3.30.3.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12478835`)

UL Pre-Scheduling [Before CNI-137616-B] If _rdActCr3574 == 1 (5G_L2_1340_replaced([11187360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28AqU_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): [End CNI-137616-B]_

After a beam failure recovery, if a MAC CE PDCCH TCI State Indication has to be scheduled as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_3444_replaced([11153203](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksk64_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , L2-PS shall suspend any UL scheduling by CORESET ID ≠ 0 and suspend UL data scheduling for SCell as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4807_replaced([11194840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv3fe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) between the receiving time of Msg3 with CRNTI (5G_UP_ALG_3436_replaced([11152921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjFsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and until the updated beam ID can be applied (5G_UP_ALG_2032_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to the CORESET ID = value defined in (5G_L2_837_replaced([11173906](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26fnYfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). 

Scheduling shall be conducted by CORESET ID = 0 during this duration. L2-PS shall flush UL data in all HARQ buffers at the point in time when the target beam can be used for scheduling by CORESET ID ≠ 0, see 5G_UP_ALG_2032_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.30.3.0-7 UL Pre-Scheduling Reception of PsUser_UserModifyReq without UE dedicated configuration update indication (ID: 11194889)

**3.30.3.0-7.0-9**  (ID: `13391894`)

UL Pre-Scheduling Upon receiving the configurations from PsUser_UserModifyReq without the UE dedicated configuration status (meaning UE dedicated configuration activated), 5G-L2-PS needs to compare the following configuration in PsUser_UserModifyReq with those that are stored,

  * nrofSrsPorts in srsConfig, as in 5G_ARC_ALG_2815, and /or **maxNumOfUlLayers**
  * dmrsAdditionalPosition in puschConfig, as in TS 38.211, clause 6.4.1.1.3
  * actAperCsi, since the size of the CSI Request IE (as computed with ([11159474](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktKJsfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the DCI changes.
  * power saving BWP in the list of configured dedicated bandwidth parts
  * SCell configuration (SCell release).
  * For FDD PCell (including DSS PCell) when TDD UL Tx switching SCell or TDD UL SCell is added or released and causes a different dedicate pusch-TimeDomainAllocationList or pusch-TimeDomainAllocationListR16 in PUSCH-Config.

  * For:  
\- [CB014067-A] 2CC: [End CB014067-A] TDD FR1 + TDD FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE, [CB014067-A] or  
\- 3CC: one FDD and two intra-band contiguous TDD FR1 CCs with TDD PCell UL CA UE, [End CB014067-A]  
if the dedicate pusch-TimeDomainAllocationList is changed (see ([11115928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpzTt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) on PCell due to SCell addition [CB014067-A] (2CC) [End CB014067-A] or SCell change [CB014067-A] (3CC) [End CB014067-A] or SCell swap [CB014067-A] (3CC) [End CB014067-A] or SCell release [CB014067-A] (2CC) [End CB014067-A].

  * If 'adaptive number of SRS ports' status is changed from enable to disable due to UL Tx Switching SCell is added via SCell addition/change/swap or if 'adaptive number of SRS ports' status is changed from disable to enable due to UL Tx Switching SCell is released via SCell addition/change/swap and nrofSrsPorts with codebook usage is changed (refer to ([11157717](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks9VafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

  * For:  
\- TDD FR1 + FDD inter-band 2CCs UL CA with 3Tx or  
\- TDD FR1 + TDD FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA, [CB014067-A] or  
\- one FDD and two intra-band contiguous TDD FR1 CCs UL CA UE, [End CB014067-A]  
on TDD PCell, if full power transmission mode is changed to OFF from ON and ‘adaptive number of SRS ports' status is changed to enabled from disabled, or full power transmission mode is changed to ON from OFF and ‘adaptive number of SRS ports' status is changed to disabled from enabled due to SCell addition/change/release.
  * If any of the abovementioned L1 reconfiguration in the PCell or retained SCell(s) is required during step-wise SCell addition / upgrade for UE, which is already configured with SCell(s)
  * If UE is already configured with FDD PCell and TDD DL+UL SCell, and step-wise SCell Addition procedure for another SCell is ongoing, and if this SCell Addition is accompanied by recalculation of dedicated PUSCH TDRA list for FDD PCell (for details see ([11115928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpzTt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
  * If UE is already configured with FDD PCell and TDD DL-only SCell, and SCell Upgrade procedure of existing SCell to DL&UL SCell is ongoing, and if this SCell Upgrade is accompanied by recalculation of dedicated PUSCH TDRA list for FDD PCell (for details see ([11115928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpzTt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).



If any of the above configurations are different or if the UL scheduling is triggered due to RRC Reconfiguration done immediately after successful RRC Reestablishment, 

when NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to disable,

5G-L2-PS shall conduct UL scheduling using Type3-PDCCH DCI format 0_0, in the active BWP, for the transient phase, which is corresponding to the period between receiving PsUser_UserModifyReq and receiving PsUser_UserReconfCompletedReq. Otherwise the UL DCI will be transmitted in the USS.

Otherwise, when NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to "Enable"

once the indication from the higher layer for "RRCReconfiguration" is received, gNB DU shall suspend UL scheduling and DL data transmission once the indication from the higher layer for "RRCReconfiguration" is received until UE is capable to process the UL DCI with the new configuration. Hence, when RRC configuration is expected to be activated in the UE, gNB DU uses DCI Format 0_0 if no UE_Specific search space is configured in the UE, otherwise it uses the DCI Format 0_1. This procedure is specified in ([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The transient phase handling of SCell release shall start after N DPA scheduling cycle after the receiption of start notification for SCell release (refer to ([11120617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqSb7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In case L1 reconfiguration in PCell or retained SCell(s) accompanying step-wise SCell Addition / Upgrade requires transient phase, PCell’s gNB-DU shall start handling of the transient phase N DPA scheduling cycle after the reception of start notification for step-wise SCell Addition / Upgrade (refer to ([11120331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqP_pPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Upon receiving the configurations from PsUser_UserModifyReq, with the UE dedicated configuration status set to "inProgress", the U-Plane applies the procedure as described in DL-DCCH management section (see ([11153730](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-PvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

### 3.30.3.0-8 UL Pre-Scheduling Reception of an UE context modification activating or modifying the UE dedicated configuration (ID: 11194925)

**3.30.3.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11194930`)

UL Pre-Scheduling The 5G-L2-PS applies the procedure as detailed in the requirement 5G_UP_ALG_17992_replaced([11153671](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.30.3.0-9 UL Pre-Scheduling Check UE DRX status (ID: 11194935)

**3.30.3.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11194945`)

UL Pre-Scheduling The UE DRX Status check shall verify that the UE stays in the DRX active state of the DRX cycle, i.e. the UE is able to receive messages via the PDCCH for the subframe the evaluation relates to. For this purpose the UL scheduler shall check UE DRX status from DRX module.

In case the UE stays in DRX active state the scheduler checks of the further conditions decides whether the UE is a candidate of Scheduling Set 1. If the UE stays in the ‘DRX sleep’ state of the DRX cycle and no data transmission via PDCCH is possible the evaluation of the further conditions which relates to the evaluation of the Candidate Set 1 shall be skipped and the evaluation shall be resumed for this UE in the following TTI.
