### 3.30.3.0-20 UL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from UL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set due to betaOffset not configured in initial access to avoid UCI on PUSCH when UE has no dedicated configuration (ID: 11195145)

**3.30.3.0-20.0-1**  (ID: `11195150`)

UL Pre-Scheduling [PR718658]

UL Pre-Scheduler shall exclude a UE from pre-scheduler candidate set on an UL serving cell (P(S)cell)

during the whole RRC setup/reconfiguration transient phase in initial access, i.e, between the reception of PsUser_UserSetupReq and PsUser_UserReconfCompletedReq, if NRCELL.actSmallestRRCSetup==TRUE [PR734429] OR if ueDedicatedConfigState==disabled [End PR734429],

  * if any of betaOffsetAck indexes(betaOffsetAck-Index1, betaOffsetAck-Index2, betaOffsetAck-Index3) (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2595_replaced([11301063](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqXynvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not configured and if there is Harq-Ack feedback in the same slot
  * or if any of betaOffsetCsi indexes(betaOffsetCSI-Part1-Index1, betaOffsetCSI-Part1-Index2, betaOffsetCSI-Part2-Index1, betaOffsetCSI-Part2-Index2) (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2595_replaced([11301063](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqXynvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not configured and if there is p-CSI in the same slot.
  * or if alpha (configured by higher layer and provided by IE uciOnPuschScaling, see 5G_UP_ALG_3661_replaced([11160631](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPCPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not configured and (if there is Harq-Ack feedback in the same slot or if there is p-CSI in the same slot)



Rationale is that there may be an ambiguity in 3GPP what is the default value of betaOffset or alpha for UCI on PUSCH if UCI-OnPUSCH is not signaled/configured explicitly as UE behavior is unpredictable in this case, hence, for simplicity the Harq-Ack/p-CSI multiplexed with into PUSCH is prevented to avoid the ambiguous betaOffset during the transient phase.

[End PR718658]

---

### 3.30.3.0-21 UL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from UL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set due to betaOffset Update to avoid UCI on PUSCH during the transient phase (ID: 11195156)

**3.30.3.0-21.0-3**  (ID: `11195173`)

UL Pre-Scheduling The gNB-DU shall exclude a UE from the UL pre-scheduler candidate set on an UL serving cell (P(S)cell) during a User Reconfiguration Transient Phase, if all the following are true:

  * semiStatic betaOffset configuration for UE
  * Any of betaOffsetAck indexes (betaOffsetAckIndex1, betaOffsetAckIndex2, betaOffsetAckIndex3) is updated [Before PR771227] together with any of the following:
  * UE's SAg Mode change
  * or MCS table update in UL involving 64QAMLowSE MCS table, i.e, 64QAMLowSE MCS table<->any other MCS table) [End PR771227]
  * and if one of the following:
  * There is DL Harq-Ack feedback in the same slot, or
  * There is still DPA allocated resources in the same slot due to resource pre-allocation in previous DPA cycle before DPA is stopped.



The gNB-DU shall exclude a UE from the UL pre-scheduler candidate set on an UL serving cell (P(S)cell) during a User Reconfiguration Transient Phase, if all the following are true:

  * semiStatic betaOffset configuration for UE
  * Any of betaOffsetCsi indexes (betaOffsetCSI-Part1-Index1, betaOffsetCSI-Part1-Index2, betaOffsetCSI-Part2-Index1, betaOffsetCSI-Part2-Index2) is updated [Before PR771227] together with MCS table update in UL involving 64QAMLowSE MCS table, i.e, 64QAMLowSE MCS table<->any other MCS table [End PR771227]
  * and there is p-CSI expected in the same slot.



Rationale is that gNB doesn't know when exactly the UE will apply the new configuration for betaOffset and hence, for simplicity the DL Harq-Ack/p-CSI multiplexed with into PUSCH is prevented to avoid the ambiguous betaOffset during the short transient phase.

Notes:

  * 5G_UP_ALG_3512_replaced([11159392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktJiofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) specifies that A-CSI will not be triggered if UE is in transient phase.
  * Per 5G_UP_ALG_23680_replaced([11124016](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqnMCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)): during the transient phase DPA will be stopped.
  * Definitions for betaOffset indexes specified in 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2595_replaced([11301063](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqXynvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * User Reconfiguration Transient Phase defined as period between the reception of a user modify request message and reception of a user reconfiguration complete request message.
  * dynamicBetaOffsetInd reflects UE Capability. The enableDynamicBetaOffsets value reflects cell level configuration. Both are specified by 5G_UP_ALG_3500_replaced([11159329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * Two ways for semiStatic configuration: [1] UE does not support dynamic (dynamicBetaOffsetInd = false); [2] UE supports dynamic (dynamicBetaOffsetInd = true), but cell does not (enableDynamicBetaOffsets = 'disable')
  * betaOffsetAck indexes are updated for SAg per 5G_UP_ALG_21585_replaced([11205427](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwpikvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * Additional intent in limiting UL scheduling during transient phase:
  * Avoid UCI on PUSCH during the transient phase, which can lead to high code rate due to combination of the following: number of AckNack bits, small PUSCH PRB allocations, low MCS, and high betaOffsets needed for Slot Aggregation with 4x repetition.

---

### 3.30.3.0-22 UL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from UL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set due to betaOffset Update to avoid UCI on PUSCH during the transient phase (ID: 11195180)

**3.30.3.0-22.0-1**  (ID: `11195185`)

UL Pre-Scheduling In the case of an FDD Supercell, the gNB-DU shall exclude a UE from the UL pre-scheduler candidate set on all UL serving cell (P(S)cell) if there is p-CSI expected in the same slot. The intend it to avoid UCI report on PUSCH as reports on PUCCH are required to make supercell related measurement at descirbed in 5G_UP_ALG_28468_replaced([11113912](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpfKrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.30.3.0-23 UL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from UL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set in 1st UL slot due to RIM UL mitigation extension (ID: 11195190)

**3.30.3.0-23.0-1**  (ID: `11195195`)

UL Pre-Scheduling The gNB-DU shall exclude a UE from the UL pre-scheduler candidate set on an UL serving cell (P(S)cell or Scell) related to the 1st UL slot after S slot, if all the following conditions are fulfilled:

 The NIDD parameter NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE.actRimMitigationExtend is set to TRUE

 RIM UL mitigation extension is triggered (see 5G_UP_ALG_27175_replaced([11163444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktZaQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 The gNB-DU schedules the UE either

o for an initial transmission and the UE is classified as a cell edge UE

o or for a HARQ retransmission

o or for a Msg5

o [CB012535-AA-UPD001] or for a Msg3 retransmission [End CB012535-AA-UPD001]

o [04305757] or for a UL grant for contention resolution [End 04305757]

All UEs, which are excluded by the scheduling during the 1st UL slot after the S slot are eligible to be scheduled in the 2nd UL slot after S slot.

A UE is classified as a cell edge UE if the DL pathloss of the UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_2746_replaced([11121446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqYikPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is greater than the threshold /NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE.cellEdgeUeDlPathlossThr.

UEs, which are scheduled by a configure grant type 2, shall keep the initially assigned physical resources when the RIM UL mitigation extension is triggered, and the gNB-DU shall not perform a reallocation of the UE’s configured grant resources. Since the HARQ retransmission is scheduled by a dynamic grant, the HARQ retransmissions of a configured grant transmission shall be scheduled like for all other UEs in the 2nd UL slot after the S slot.

---

### 3.30.3.0-24 UL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from UL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set for UE on Scell (4:6 semistatic frame format) (ID: 11195200)

**3.30.3.0-24.0-1**  (ID: `11195206`)

UL Pre-Scheduling UL Pre-Scheduler shall exclude a UE from pre-scheduler candidate set for the 8th and 18th UL slot in a frame, if

The cell is TDD with NRCELL.tddFrameStructure/ulDlDataSlotRatio set to 4:6 (Refer 5G_UP_4446_replaced([11346967](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIlvPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for slot definition) AND

NRCELL.increasePUSCHPrepTimeULCA of the cell is set to TRUE AND

the cell is UE's Scell AND

UE’s PCell is FDD AND

[PR795300]K1min is 3 for FDD PCELL [End PR795300]

---

### 3.30.3.0-25 UL Pre-Scheduling Excluding UE from Scheduling for P-CSI Report with TP On and PI-2 BPSK (ID: 13481602)

**3.30.3.0-25.0-1**  (ID: `13481603`)

UL Pre-Scheduling UL Pre-Scheduler shall exclude a UE from being scheduled on PCell during a User Reconfiguration Transient Phase where FORCED_DCI_00 is used, if all the following are true:

  1. TP On for Msg3/DCI 0_0 is enabled
  2. PUSCH Pi-2 BPSK is configured for specific UE
  3. PUSCH grant is for P-CSI



Rationale:

  * Issue: 
  * Decoding of UCI on PUSCH with TP On and Pi-2 BPSK doubles the number of needed PRBs due to modulation order Qm=1 (compared to TP On or Off with QPSK)
  * If PI-2 BPSK is applied to FORCED_DCI_00 (User Reconfiguration Transient Phase), the number of PRBs needed for a P-CSI report will likely exceed the number of available PUSCH PRBs
  * TP On for Msg3/all DCI 0_0 related PUSCH transmissions is controlled together for all UEs by NRCELL.actMsg3TP= true
  * PUSCH PI-2 BPSK is meant for DCI 0_1 granted PUSCH transmissions in a TP On state in a dedicated BWP. However, since FORCED_DCI_00 granted transmissions are also in a dedicated BWP, UE will apply Pi-2-BPSK content within PUSCH-Config IE at RRC layer to both DCI 0_0 and DCI 0_1 transmissions
  * Correction is for L2-PS to block FORCED_DCI_00 grants for P_CSI in a TP On (PUSCH DFT-s-OFDM) state when Pi-2 BPSK is configured. Blocking can occur at UL Pre-Scheduling list or at CS1 list.

---

### 3.30.3.0-26 UL Pre-Scheduling Reception of PsUser_UserSuspendSchedulingReq (ID: 11195211)

**3.30.3.0-26.0-3**  (ID: `11195225`)

UL Pre-Scheduling Upon reception of PsUser_UserSuspendSchedulingReq:

UL Prescheduler shall, for the designated UE:

\- Shall be declared as suspended for DRB scheduling

\- Shall not allow the UE to be added into the Candidate Set 1 when any of the following are true:

\- there is only DRB data available

\- UE is eligible for PUSCH periodic proactive scheduling (5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) but is not included in any other priority class

\- UE is eligible for TCP boost proactive scheduling (5G_UP_ALG_16872_replaced([11195403](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- UE is only present in CG associated priority classes: cgHarqRetrans, cgDeactivation, cgModification

\- [CB009409-C] TCP boost is enabled and the UE is eligible for low priority "RLC Status PDU" proactive grant (5G_UP_ALG_5134_replaced([11203852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB009409-C]

\- Shall stop all HARQ re-transmissions for DRB data

Note:

Retransmissions of UL HARQ processes should not be further scheduled, as during SgNB release procedure PDCP status forwarding might be initiated in parallel, so that after this stage no further UL packets shall be forwarded. After the Suspend Request, the PS user context will be deleted (inclusive allocated HARQ buffers) with a second procedure(PsUser_UserDeleteReq/Resp) - no resume functionality might follow.

---

### 3.30.3.1 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling

#### 3.30.3.1.0-1 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling Low Priority Proactive Grants: Overview (ID: 11195235)

**3.30.3.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11195245`)

PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling [Before CNI-88099 - AB]

When 5G-L2-PS receives PsUser_UserSetupReq message with a RApreamble not set to 0 (i.e. with a UE setup configuration corresponding to a Contention Free RACH (CFRA) for an Incoming Handover/PsCell Change):

Proactive scheduler shall not be running until the reception of a Msg3 with CRC OK or until the scheduling of all the Msg3 HARQ retransmissions. The trigger is defined in [5G_UP_ALG_17999_replaced([11153112](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kskT0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]

[CNI-88099 - AB]

Low Priority Proactive UL Grants (LP PUG for short) are characterized by the following:

• Anticipatory in nature (i.e. not triggered by no- null UL buffer status reports (BSR) ​ nor positive SR indication) and only triggered by L2 PS

• Scheduled with ulProactSched request class (lowest priority class)

• NIDD timing control

 Minimum gap between grants (NIDD parameter has "ulSchedTimeInterval" in acronym)

 Window of time over which grants are sent, for DRX configured UE (NIDD parameter has "ulProGrantTimer" in acronym)

• UE eligibility and feature interworking restrictions

• Additional behavior for UEs configured at RRC for skippable grants

 If UE is configured for skippable grants, it will skip (not reply to grant with a PUSCH transmission) if it has no data to send (or no PHR report or UCI to send)

 Else, UE is required to respond to grant with PUSCH transmission (which will have padding bits if UE has nothing to send)

Notes:

 Control for LP PUG is defined at 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 UE eligibility for LP PUG is defined at 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Timing/scheduling of LP PUG is defined at 5G_UP_ALG_16388_replaced([11195341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_16389_replaced([11195357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Handling of PUSCH decodes associated with LP PUG is defined at 5G_UP_ALG_16390_replaced([11195370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Overlap of PUSCH and PUCCH slots for a UE is defined at 5G_UP_ALG_9232_replaced([11195320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CNI-88099 - AB]

---

#### 3.30.3.1.0-2 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling Low Priority Proactive Grants: Control (ID: 11195251)

**3.30.3.1.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `11195280`)

PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling L2 PS shall define UE eligibility and grant control parameters for Low Priority UL Proactive Grants, as defined below.

Control Parameters and variables:

 Grant Types

 Proactive Grant Type for UE is defined per 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Timing

 rrmProGrantGap (Gap timer):

 For Proactive Grant type = **Not Skippable: rrmProGrantGap = NRCELL.ulSchedTimeInterval**

 For Proactive Grant type = **Skippable-Dynamic or** Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced : rrmProGrantGap = NRCELL.ulSchedTimeIntervalTxSkip****

 rrmProGrantsPeriod (Prolong timer):

 For Proactive Grant type = **Not Skippable: rrmProGrantsPeriod = NRCELL.ulProGrantTimer**

 For Proactive Grant type = **Skippable-Dynamic or** Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced : rrmProGrantsPeriod = is NRCELL.ulProGrantTimerTxSkip****

 Payload Size

 For Proactive Grant type = **Not Skippable:**

 If UE not configured for DRX or (if UE Configured for DRX and rrmProGrantsPeriod =0): Payload Size = NRCELL.payloadSizeProactiveNonDrx

 If UE is configured for DRX and when checking the possibility of MCS reduction (as described in 5G_UP_ALG_5102_replaced([11203503](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): Payload Size = NRCELL.payloadSizeProactiveDrx

 For Proactive Grant type = **Skippable-Dynamic or =** Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced : Payload Size = NRCELL.payloadSizeProactiveTxSkip****

UE Eligibility:

 States

 Ineligible: In this state, a UE will not be scheduled with LP PUG. All UEs are ineligible for LP PUG until Context Setup is completed and the UE dedicated configuration is activated

 Eligible: In this state, a UE will be considered for scheduling with timing and scheduling considerations defined by:

 5G_UP_ALG_16388_replaced([11195341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Timing and 5G_UP_ALG_16389_replaced([11195357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Scheduling

 Transition triggers:

 Ineligible-to-Eligible: Trigger occurs if all of the following are true:

 Cell is Pcell

 UE low priority PUG status is True( refer to 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16425_replaced([11288139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp4qavvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 If ulProGrantType field provided at user setup or user modify met criteria for Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced and ulSchedTimeIntervalTxSkip is not to be set to zero in cell setup/reconfiguration message to L2 PS

 If ulProGrantType field provided at user setup or user modify met criteria for Not Skippable and ulSchedTimeInterval is not to be set to zero in cell setup/reconfiguration message to L2 PS

 If UE is configured with Configured Grant but UE enters a non-firing state( see 11172972).

 For DRX Configured:

 User Context is setup and successful reception of MSG3 and MSG5 and the UE dedicated configuration is activated

 For DRX Not Configured:

 For CBRA connection setup:

 If successful reception of MSG3 with SRB0, then successful reception of MSG5 and the UE dedicated configuration is activated

 Else: User Context is setup and successful reception of MSG3 and the UE dedicated configuration is activated

 For CFRA setup:

 For MSG3: Received with CRC OK or all of its HARQ transmissions completed

 VoNR **[Before CB008118-CB] (5QI-1)**[CB008118-CB](5QI-1/65/66) **[End CB008118-CB] DRB not present** ****

 If NRCELL.actServiceAwareConfig=true **and NRCELL.pucchModeSelect= 'longPucch', then UE SR Periodicity >= rrmProGrantGap (refer to 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_7611_replaced([11312715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Trksc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for UE SR Periodicity)**

 UE is not receiving, or stops receiving TCP Boost proactive uplink grants (see 5G_UP_ALG_16873_replaced([11195408](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 If UE is configured with DRB priority PUG, that is, one of DRB of UE is associated with DRB priority PUGs, and UE is not elgible for DRB priroity PUG. Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_30337_replaced([11195585](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8_DvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 Eligible-to-Ineligible: Trigger occurs if any of the following are true

 If ulProGrantType field provided at user setup or user modify met criteria for Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced and ulSchedTimeIntervalTxSkip is to be set to zero in cell setup/reconfiguration message to L2 PS

 If ulProGrantType field provided at user setup or user modify met criteria for Not Skippable and ulSchedTimeInterval is to be set to zero in cell setup/reconfiguration message to L2 PS

 If UE is configured with Configured Grant and UE enters a firing state( see 11172972).

 UE low priority PUG status is False( refer to 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16425_replaced([11288139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp4qavvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 VoNR **[Before CB008118-CB] (5QI-1)**[CB008118-CB](5QI-1/65/66) **[End CB008118-CB] DRB is added** ****

 If NRCELL.actServiceAwareConfig=true **and NRCELL.pucchModeSelect= 'longPucch', then if UE SR Periodicity < rrmProGrantGap**

 UE transitions into the TCP Boost Ramp-up state and begins to receive TCP Boost proactive uplink grants (see 5G_UP_ALG_16873_replaced([11195408](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 If UE is configured with DRB priority PUG, that is, one of DRB of UE is associated with DRB priority PUGs, and UE is elgible for DRB priroity PUG. Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_30337_replaced([11195585](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8_DvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Notes:

 Cell Activation

 LP PUG is enabled at PCell if **NRCELL.actProactUlSchedulingEnh is set to 'allUes' or** onlySkippableGrantCapableUes’.****

 Exceptions:

 LP PUG is disabled at PCell if NRCELL.ulSchedTimeInterval (for non-skippable) or NRCELL.ulSchedTimeIntervalTxSkip (for skippable) is set to 0 or not configured

 A UE with a **[Before CB008118-CB] 5QI-1**[CB008118-CB] 5QI-1/65/66 **[End CB008118-CB] bearer will not be configurable for skippable grants at RRC layer, therefore it will not have Proactive Grants of type =** Skippable-Dynamic or **Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced**** _*_** *

 If rrmProGrantsPeriod =0, then UE is treated as non-DRX for the purpose of LP PUG control

 The term "Proactive Grant payload size" refers to amount of user data requested by grant. The min. number PRBs allocated on PUSCH is selected such that TBS is greater than or equal to "Proactive Grant payload size" (see 5G_UP_ALG_5010_replaced([11201008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 Use of NRCELL.payloadSizeProactiveDrx or NRCELL.payloadSizeProactiveNonDrx does not require NRCELL.actUlTxSkip = true.

  * Proactive Grant Type could change between Not Skippable and one of the skippable types (see 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * For CA case, Proactive Grant scheduling is only applied on PCell, it is NOT applied on any SCell **even through SCell has the same Proactive Grant type as PCell.**

---

#### 3.30.3.1.0-3 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling Low Priority Proactive Grants: Type Definition (ID: 11195286)

**3.30.3.1.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `13409450`)

PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling The L2 PS shall classify anticipatory-proactive grants for a UE identified in the list below with a Proactive Grant Type as defined by the ulProGrantType field provided by CP at user setup or user modify.

Proactive grants for which this requirement applies:

 Low Priority Proactive UL Grants (LP PUG , see 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 DRB priority PUG([11195558](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_6eMUcPvPEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). 

**Notes:**

 Definitions of Proactive Grant Type:

 Proactive Grant Type = Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced or Not Skippable indicated from Cplane according to NRCELL.actUlTxSkip and UE capability. Refer to [11288227](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_tiCtcfvXEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

 Other Notes

 CP SFS level requirement may refer to “Definitions of Proactive Grant Type” above for setting ulProGrantType in user setup or user modify, so do not remove.

 NRCELL.actUlTxSkip is only used to control enable/disable UL TX skip in UE side, NRCELL.actProactUlSchedulingEnh is used to enable/disable low priorirty PUG.

 A UE with a 5QI-1/65/66 (VoNR) [CB014699-E] and critical VoNR(5QI-1/65/66) [End CB014699-E] bearer will not be configured by CP for skippable grants. In addition, LP PUGs are disabled (so no need for Proactive Grant Type identification). There is no explicit RRC Reconfiguration just to maintain this. If presence of VoNR bearer changes, CP will change skippable grants configuration as part of an already triggered reconfiguration.

 The term "skippable proactive grant" may be used in other requirements to refer to Proactive Grant Type = Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced, also for DRB priority PUG.

 A UE configured for skippable grants may skip other grants besides LP PUG , but L2 PS behavior only needs Proactive Grant Type indication for UL grants identified above.

 See migration rules in Operability SFS for handling of NRCELL.ulProGrantTimerTx when this parameter is not configured by operator

  * FR1 UL SCell has the same Proactive Grant Type as PCell because this IE is configured in UE level.
  * Proactive Grant Type is used by other PUG(s), it is not recommended to over-written. The implementation can be decided in CP3.

---

#### 3.30.3.1.0-4 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling Low Priority Proactive Grants: PUSCH vs PUCCH UCI Ambiguity (ID: 11195320)

**3.30.3.1.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11195335`)

PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling An UL grant of a type shown below shall not be scheduled in the same slot with overlapping CSI/HARQ-ACK on PUCCH when a UE has Proactive Grant type = Skippable-Dynamic (see 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) :

 Low Priority Proactive UL Grants (LP PUG , see 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 TA Grant

 RLC Status PDU Grant (also referred to as “fake SR” grant, see 5G_UP_ALG_5134_replaced([11203852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

TCP Boost Proactive Uplink Grant (see 5G_UP_ALG_16873_replaced([11195408](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 [CB012810-D]DRB priority Proactive Uplink Grant (see 5G_UP_ALG_30332_replaced([11195558](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8_CfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB012810-D]

Notes:

 PUSCH transmission skipping in a slot with overlapping CSI/HARQ-ACK on PUCCH for the same UE presents a scenario for skippable proactive grants where UE behavior is undefined by 3GPP Rel-15.

 This requirement for Proactive Grant type = Skippable-Dynamic applies to all HARQ transmissions.

 A skippable proactive grant can be scheduled in the same slot with overlapping CSI/HARQ-ACK on PUCCH for the same UE when Proactive Grant type = Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced

 In this case, the UE behavior per 3GPP Rel 16 Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced is to send UCI bits on PUSCH (instead of PUCCH) and not skip the grant. No UE transmission on PUCCH expected.

 If UE has DL CCs > 1, DL scheduling across PCell and SCell is dynamic enough to make it difficult for UL scheduler to always obtain information about UCI PUCCH slot prior to making an UL grant decision. There may some instances where collision between granted PUSCH and PUCCH is not avoided for a UE with Proactive Grant type = Skippable-Dynamic

---

#### 3.30.3.1.0-5 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling Low Priority Proactive Grants: Timing (ID: 11195341)

**3.30.3.1.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11195351`)

PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling L2 PS shall control the timing of Low Priority Proactive UL Grants (LP PUG) as shown below, when the UE is in a LP PUG Eligible state as per 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

  * UE is configured for DRX:



• If UE is in DRX Active state:

A. When UL buffer becomes empty (i.e. BSR from non-zero to zero):e

  1. Start prolong timer defined by rrmProGrantsPeriod (see 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  2. Start gap timer defined by rrmProGrantGap (see 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) )

  3. At rrmProGrantGap expiration:




• If rrmProGrantsPeriod is running:

• If UE is not in a LTE-only split work mode (see notes):

• Consider scheduling of a LP PUG per 5G_UP_ALG_16389_replaced([11195357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

• For Proactive Grant type = Not Skippable:

• If any UL grant was scheduled for UE per 5G_UP_ALG_16389_replaced([11195357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)): Go to step A.2 above (restart rrmProGrantGap)

• Note: This gives an opportunity to restart rrmProGrantGap if scheduling of grant with ulProactSched request class did not occur for any reason. Important for UL grant request for ping delay reduction

• Else: Do not send any further LP PUGs and let rrmProGrantsPeriod expire (or cancel it)

• For Proactive Grant type = Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced :

• Go to step A.2 above (restart rrmProGrantGap)

• If rrmProGrantsPeriod has expired:

• Go to step A above

• UE is not configured for DRX:

1\. Start gap timer defined by rrmProGrantGap (see 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) )

2\. At rrmProGrantGap expiration:

• Consider scheduling of a LP PUG per 5G_UP_ALG_16389_replaced([11195357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

• Go to step 1 above 

Notes:

• Proactive Grant Type for UE is defined per 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

• LTE-only split work mode defined as: UE in UlSplitWorkMode equal to LteOnly and [Before CB013728-A]rdUlProactiveEnableForLTEOnlyMode (5G_UP_ALG_8547_replaced([11208948](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kww3V_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[CB013728-A] NRCELL.actProactGrantsUlLteOnlyMode[End CB013728-A] is set as [Before CB013728-A]0[CB013728-A]False[End CB013728-A]. In this mode, rrmProGrantGap is restarted without scheduling of a LP PUG . The UlSplit_workmode check is valid only when UL dynamic switch feature is enabled, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_3354_replaced([11151714](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksbJ6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The rmProGrantGap time interval need not restart again when UE switches from LteOnly to NR state

---

#### 3.30.3.1.0-6 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling Low Priority Proactive Grants: Scheduling (ID: 11195357)

**3.30.3.1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11195364`)

PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling L2 PS shall consider scheduling a new Low Priority Proactive UL Grants (LP PUG ) as defined below, when the UE is in a LP PUG Eligible state as per 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Pre-Scheduling:

 For Grant Type = Not Skippable (see 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) :

 UE already has an entry in Pre Scheduler candidate set for any reason:

 Do not add a LP PUG entry in Pre Scheduler candidate set

 Else (no entry)

 Add a LP PUG entry in Pre Scheduler candidate set (according to 5G_UP_ALG_4807_replaced([11194840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv3fe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , i.e. as a member of the ulProactSched request class)

 For Grant Type = Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced :

 UE already has a PUCCH opportunity (CSI/HARQ-ACK) in slot:

 Determine whether to add a LP PUG entry in Pre Scheduler candidate set per 5G_UP_ALG_9232_replaced([11195320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Else, "Total Buffer Check" procedure:

 If total buffered bytes (sum of pending and scheduled) for UE is not zero (Not Null):

 Do not add a LP PUG entry in Pre Scheduler candidate set

 Else if total buffered bytes is zero (Null)

 Add a LP PUG entry in Pre Scheduler candidate set (according to 5G_UP_ALG_4807_replaced([11194840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv3fe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , i.e. as a member of the ulProactSched request class)

[CNI-88099-AB] 

Note: This requirement is added as part of splitting 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) into several requirements.

This note shall be removed from the next requirement version, in a future release ! 

[End CNI-88099-AB]

---

#### 3.30.3.1.0-7 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling Low Priority Proactive Grants: PUSCH Response (ID: 11195370)

**3.30.3.1.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11195374`)

PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling L2 PS shall process a PUSCH decode corresponding to a Low Priority Proactive UL Grant (LP PUG) as defined below.

For HARQ Tx number = 1:

 For PUSCH decode = DTX:

 For Grant Type = Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced (see 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) :

 Process DTX per 5G_UP_ALG_16415_replaced([11195380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Remove any LP PUG entry in Pre Scheduler candidate set

 For Proactive Grant type = Not Skippable:

 Normal HARQ processing per 5G_UP_ALG_5073_replaced([11202321](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 For No DTX and CRC Not Ok

 Normal HARQ processing per 5G_UP_ALG_5073_replaced([11202321](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Notes:

 For Grant Type = Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced:

 Assumption for CRC Not OK is that UE did not skip LP PUG and bad CRC is due to RF

 Assumption for DTX is that UE skipped the LP PUG and therefore DTX should be ignored by all functions which make use of it. This is described in 5G_UP_ALG_ rather than here because there are other anticipatory/dummy UL grants which require the same behavior

 LP PUG entry in Pre Scheduler candidate set could have been removed upon granting of initial HARQ transmission without waiting for PUSCH decode result (up to implementation)

[CNI-88099-AB] 

Note: This requirement is added as part of splitting 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) into several requirements.

This note shall be removed from the next requirement version, in a future release ! 

[End CNI-88099-AB]

---

#### 3.30.3.1.0-8 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling DTX Handling for Skippable Grants (ID: 11195380)

**3.30.3.1.0-8.0-5**  (ID: `13074348`)

PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling When a UE is configured by RRC for skippable UL grants (see 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2 PS shall ignore the impact of PUSCH DTX upon the following functions as described below for the list of anticipatory grants described herein.

Functions for special PUSCH DTX handling:

  * UL HARQ processing:
  * UL HARQ Retransmission:
  * Stop the HARQ Process upon DTX for Init Tx or reTx (see 5G_UP_ALG_5074_replaced([11202363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * UL OLLA
  * Skip delta SINR evaluation (see 5G_UP_ALG_11054_replaced([11137899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcSd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Skip the delta SINR evaluation
  * Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA (see 5G_UP_ALG_3158_replaced([11138143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdglfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Do not count for CRC Nack flood identification
  * UL BLER Target
  * UL recent BLER calculation for High Reliability (see 5G_UP_ALG_17217_replaced([11138412](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreuuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Skip UL recent BLER calculation for High Reliability
  * Beam fallback for UL:
  * UL Beam probing and fallback (see 5G_UP_ALG_2041_replaced([11112416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZr_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Procedures related to DTX are not applicable
  * UL Radio link problem detection:
  * UL Radio link problem detection for PUSCH (see 5G_UP_ALG_8227_replaced([11151822](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksbw__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Both numOfPuschDtx and numOfPuschSample do not include DTX
  * Rank and PMI selection on PUSCH:
  * Long period without SRS measurements (see 5G_UP_ALG_3232_replaced([11140029](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krrjAvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * DTX ignored in determination of " last valid PMI"(not lead to storing a "not ack" decision)
  * Housekeeping:
  * Cumulative TB Sizes Granted But Not Received Yet (see 5G_UP_ALG_5125_replaced([11203756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwg_uPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * DTX also leads to rrmScheduledUlData reduction
  * PDCCH Outer Loop Control and CQI management:
  * DCI size offset computation
  * CQI offset computation from DL and UL HARQ feedbacks (see 5G_UP_ALG_4074_replaced([11170531](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_QJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Do not count for PDCCH CQI compensation offset
  * Downlink and Uplink feedbacks filtering (see 5G_UP_ALG_4079_replaced([11170587](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_QLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Ignore DTX indication
  * PM counters:
  * PUSCH initial transmissions in MCS table histogram counters for CP OFDM and DFT-s-OFDM (M55302C00000-28, M55302C06000-27,M55302C11000-27
  * See PM counter definitions, starting with 22R4
  * Ignore both attempt and resulting DTX



The L1 measurement management for the Uplink PUSCH power control is specified in the requirement [11119357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_dA-3QPvJEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Applicable Grant Types for special PUSCH DTX handling, only if UE has total buffered bytes = 0 or fake data buffer raised by DRB priority Proactive Uplink Grant triggered([11204049](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_rRmZAPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or in case that the**UL Grant (DG) is in result to anticipative DC-GBR buffer increase with CG (see[11173454: Anticipative buffer correction in case that the opportunity of CG initial transmission is missed](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)):**

  * LP PUG (see 5G_UP_ALG_16385_replaced([11195235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6iyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * UL Grant for RLC Status PDU (also referred to as “fake SR” grant) grants triggered initially by L2-Hi; see 5G_UP_ALG_5134_replaced([11203852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * TA Grants (see 5G_UP_ALG_4844_replaced([11195652](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv9mHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * UL grant after a zero BSR was received
  * TCP Boost Proactive Uplink Grants (see 5G_UP_ALG_16873_replaced([11195408](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * DRB priority Proactive Uplink Grant (see 5G_UP_ALG_30332_replaced([11195558](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8_CfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
  * UL Grant (DG) after proactive DC-GBR buffer increase with CG (see [11173454: Anticipative buffer correction in case that the opportunity of CG initial transmission is missed](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)



Applicablicability for

  * Configured Grant and ignoring DTX when UE is configured for skippable grants:
  * UL HARQ Retransmission
  * UL Radio link problem detection (initial transmission) (see 5G_UP_ALG_8227_replaced([11151822](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksbw__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))



**Notes:**

  * This requirement applies to other dummy grants besides LP PUG , but it is placed in the requirement section of LP PUG for convenience
  * UL grant after a zero BSR was received refers to case when UL grant is sent just prior to total buffered bytes = 0 due to delay in getting buffer status from L2-LO. The grant is sent but subsequent DTX due to grant being skipped by UE is ignored (see PR624410). PM impact from DTX is not included
  * UE is configured by RRC for skippable UL grants if Proactive Grant type = Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced(see 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * By definition, LP PUGs are sent only in a BSR=0 scenario**.**



**[PR854447]**

  * Due to the processing chain in UL scheduling and the RTT from scheduling a UE until reception of the granted transport block, a UE with a positive housekeeping status may be scheduled even if the UE’s transmission buffer is already exhausted. The gNB-DU stops the scheduling of such a UE after reception and processing of the BSR=0, which is added to the last transport block with valid user data. All granted but not sent PUSCH transmissions when the grants are skipped in the UE force DTX indications and hence have negative impact on the BLER statistic until the UE isn’t any longer scheduled for non-zero BSR.



**[End PR854447]**

[PR859752]

  * When the transmission buffer of a UE with Configured Grant Type 2 exhausts, the UE skips the UL transmission and the granted physical resources are unallocated. Such skipped PUSCH transmissions force DTX indications in gNB-DU and hence have negative impact on the BLER statistic until either the Configured Grant Type 2 is deactivated for the UE or new data is available in UE’s transmission buffer.



[End PR859752]

---

### 3.30.3.2 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling

#### 3.30.3.2.0-1 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling TCP Boost Proactive Uplink Grant Overview (ID: 11195408)

**3.30.3.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11195425`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling 11195425: TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling To achieve optimal downlink TCP throughput requires that the UE send TCP ACKs with low delay in response to downlink TCP transmissions. The quicker the TCP ACKs are sent by the UE the sooner the TCP slow start procedure on the TCP server will react and send data at a higher rate.

To ensure the TCP ACKs are received quickly from the UE the gNB detects when a downlink data flow has started and sends TCP boost proactive uplink grants (PUGs) to the UE. Retreiving the TCP ACKs quickly at the start of the TCP data flow is key. Once the data flow has been established and the data rate has increased significantly the speed at which the TCP ACKs are retrieved is not as critical. To take advantage of this the TCP boost PUGs will be sent more frequently during the first part of the data flow called the Ramp-up period. The PUGs will then continue at a slower rate during the Stable period. 

Detection of the TCP data flow is performed by L2-HI RLC on a per DRB basis. RLC monitors the amount of data that is received from the network. If the received data arrives after a configurable period of no downlink data (e.g. 100ms) and the received data exceeds a configurable data size threshold (e.g. 3 Kbytes) in a configurable period of time (e.g. 5ms) then it is considered the start of a TCP data flow. When a new data flow is detected L2-HI RLC will send a notification to the UE's PCell. All TCP boost PUGs will be sent to the UE from the PCell, i.e. the UE will not receive TCP boost PUGs from an SCell.

The following diagram provides an example of the TCP boost PUGs being sent to a UE. 

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the TCPBoost mechanism in a cellular network. It depicts a timeline with various events and durations related to data transmission. **Key elements and their descriptions:** * **Timeline (t):** A horizontal axis representing time. * **TCPBoost Inactive Time:** Two segments labeled "TCPBoost Inactive Time" indicate periods when the system is not actively boosting TCP performance. The asterisk (_) next to the first instance suggests a special condition. *__Initial PUG Delay:__A short duration after the start of the first "TCPBoost Inactive Time" before the first PUG (Physical Uplink Grant) event. *__Ramp-up PUG Interval:__A period characterized by a series of PUGs (indicated by gray downward arrows) and downlink transmissions (indicated by green upward arrows). This phase appears to be for increasing the data rate. *__Stable PUG Interval:__A period following the ramp-up phase, also with PUGs and downlink transmissions, but potentially at a sustained higher rate. *__(TCPBoost) Ramp-up Duration:__A bracket encompassing the "Initial PUG Delay" and "Ramp-up PUG Interval," representing the total time for the ramp-up phase. *__(TCPBoost) Stable Duration:__A bracket encompassing the "Stable PUG Interval," representing the duration of the stable high-rate transmission. *__TCPBoost Duration:__A bracket that covers both the ramp-up and stable durations, representing the overall period of TCPBoost. *__Downlink transmission (green upward arrows):__These arrows indicate data being sent from the base station to the user equipment (UE). The legend specifies that the first three green arrows represent the data flow minimum data rate threshold. *__TCPBoost PUG (gray downward arrows):__These arrows represent Physical Uplink Grants, which are signals from the base station to the UE, likely indicating permission to transmit data. *__UL transmission (blue upward arrows):__These arrows represent uplink transmissions from the UE to the base station. The legend clarifies that "Skippable grant UEs will skip PUSCH transmission if UE has no data to send." *__White dashed boxes:__These boxes appear to represent periods of data transmission or data flow. *__Purple circle:__A purple circle marks a specific point in time during the "TCPBoost Duration." The legend explains that "During the TCP boost duration if the time since the last DL data transmission is greater than the TCPBoost Inactive Time, exit the TCP boost cycle and start monitoring for the start of the next TCP dataflow." *__Explanatory Text:__* A note states: "_ Inactive time is not applied when DRB is initially established. A data flow can be detected immediately." * A bullet point explains: "TCPBoost PUGs are triggered when a data flow is detected after the DL has been idle for greater than TCPBoost Inactive Time. In the example above 3 green arrows represent the data flow minimum data rate threshold." The diagram illustrates a cycle where after a period of inactivity, a data flow triggers a TCPBoost process. This process involves an initial delay, followed by a ramp-up phase with increasing grants and transmissions, and then a stable phase. The system exits the boost cycle if a certain inactivity threshold is met during the boost duration.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15210.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

To support the TCP boost functionality parameters are used to configure the TCP boost durations, PUG intervals, and data flow detection thresholds. These parameters are contained in a TCP boost profile that can have multiple instances. For each 5QI (SA) / QCI (NSA) DRB that needs to use the TCP boost functionality the 5QI / QCI NRDRB profile must reference an instance of the TCP boost profile. If the 5QI / QCI NRDRB profile does not reference a TCP boost profile then TCP boost functionality will not be applied to data flows that occur on that 5QI / QCI DRB. When the UL scheduler is applying TCP boost functionality to a TCP data flow it must use the parameters in the TCP boost profile that is associated with the 5QI / QCI DRB that the TCP data flow occurs on.

Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_16874_replaced([11195442](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8X8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the details of the TCP Boost PUG functionality [CB013709-B] and for the interaction with overload handling refer to 5G_UP_ALG_32422_replaced([11195430](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB013709-B]

Diagram located at: [CFAM Diagrams](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB009000%20-%20CB009499/CFAM/CB009409%20UL%20Proactive%20Grants%20for%20TCP%20Speed-Up/CFAM%20Diagrams?csf=1&web=1&e=LngXZ2)

#### 3.30.3.2.0-2 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling Interaction Between TCP Boost Proactive Grant and Overload Handling (ID: 11195430)

**3.30.3.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11195436`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling **If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 0** , HwVariant is not ABIL, and the RT processing load severity metric of the UL scheduler (5G_UP_ALG_31301_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) reaches the load level for performing overload mitigation actions, PUGs for boosting the TCP throughput shall be suspended only for new [Before PR842015] UEs [PR842015] TCP flows [End PR842015]. This implies that when overload is detected, then, for new TCP flows, PUGs shall not be issued for both ramp-up and stable states. If there are ongoing TCP Boost Cycles (stable or ramp-up) then those cycles will not be interrupted even if overload is detected. The gNB-DU shall apply the overload mitigation actions only for new TCP flows and not existing ones.****

The details of the overload mitigation actions with respect to corresponding load severity levels are specified in 5G_UP_ALG_16875_replaced([11195456](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8X8_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.30.3.2.0-3 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling TCP Boost Proactive Uplink Grant States (ID: 11195442)

**3.30.3.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11195451`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling 11195451: TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling When NRCELL.actTcpBoostPug does not equal ‘disabled’ TCP boost proactive grants will be sent to UEs that have a TCP data flow. The following state transition diagram describes the TCP boost processing. Refer to the referenced requirements for additional details.

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating the behavior of a TCP Boost algorithm. The diagram consists of three main states: "TCP Boost Ramp-Up (5G_UP_ALG_16877)", "TCP Boost Stable (5G_UP_ALG_16878)", and "TCP Boost Idle (5G_UP_ALG_16876)". The "TCP Boost Ramp-Up" state has a self-loop labeled "Put PUG entry in scheduling queue every Ramp-up interval". An outgoing transition from "TCP Boost Ramp-Up" to "TCP Boost Stable" is triggered by "Ramp-up Duration elapsed". The "TCP Boost Stable" state also has a self-loop labeled "Put PUG entry in scheduling queue every Stable interval". An outgoing transition from "TCP Boost Stable" to "TCP Boost Idle" is triggered by "Stable Duration elapsed OR TCP data flow ended". There are transitions leading to the "TCP Boost Idle" state. One transition comes from "TCP Boost Ramp-Up" labeled "TCP data flow ended". Another transition comes from "TCP Boost Stable" as described above. There are also transitions leading back to the "TCP Boost Ramp-Up" state. One transition comes from "TCP Boost Idle" labeled "TCP data flow detected". Another transition comes from "TCP Boost Ramp-Up" itself, indicating a potential re-entry into the ramp-up phase. The "TCP Boost Idle" state appears to be a terminal state or a state from which other states can be reached. There is an upward arrow originating from a circular icon below "TCP Boost Idle", suggesting an entry point or a reset mechanism. The text within the diagram is as follows: \- "Put PUG entry in scheduling queue every Ramp-up interval" \- "Ramp-up Duration elapsed" \- "TCP Boost Ramp-Up (5G_UP_ALG_16877)" \- "TCP data flow detected" \- "TCP data flow ended" \- "TCP Boost Idle (5G_UP_ALG_16876)" \- "Put PUG entry in scheduling queue every Stable interval" \- "TCP Boost Stable (5G_UP_ALG_16878)" \- "Stable Duration elapsed OR TCP data flow ended"[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15214.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

Note that the state machine is at the UE level. The UL scheduler will only start a new TCP boost cycle for a UE if a data flow is detected when the UE is in the TCP Boost Idle state. If a UE is in an active TCP boost cycle (i.e. in the Ramp-up or Stable state) and a second data flow is detected for the UE on another DRB that data flow detection will be ignored. 

Diagram located at: [CFAM Diagrams](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB009000%20-%20CB009499/CFAM/CB009409%20UL%20Proactive%20Grants%20for%20TCP%20Speed-Up/CFAM%20Diagrams?csf=1&web=1&e=LngXZ2)

#### 3.30.3.2.0-4 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling TCP Boost UE Eligibility Checks (ID: 11195456)

**3.30.3.2.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11195471`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling A UE must meet the following prerequisites to be a candidate for TCP boost proactive uplink grants (PUGs):

 If NRCELL.actTcpBoostPug equals ‘onlySkippableGrantCapableUes’ then only UEs with Proactive Grant Type = Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced will be eligible for TCP boost PUGs.

o This check will be performed by CP-RT as part of the bearer addition procedure (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_10057_replaced([11256266](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TnpOvPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 UE's DRB must be an RLC AM DRB

o This will be enforced via NIDD parameter rules that only allow RLC AM DRBs to reference a TCP boost profile

o RLC UM DRBs are used for low latency data flows that typically do not use TCP

 UE’s SR periodicity must be greater than or equal to NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugMinSrPeriodicity.

o This will be enforced by the UL scheduler

o If the UE’s SR periodicity is shorter than the minimum SR periodicity the UE will be able to send an SR sufficiently soon after the UE generates a TCP ACK , i.e. there is less need for TCP PUGs

o If Scheduling Request Handling is enabled (NRCELL.actAdaptiveSr = true) the UE’s SR periodicity can change while the UE is connected. The UE’s TCP boost PUG eligibility will be determined based on the UE’s newly configured SR periodicity.

 UE with a 5QI-1 bearer (VoNR UE) is not receiving VoNR proactive grants

o This will be enforced by the UL scheduler

o VoNR UEs that have an SR periodicity greater than 20ms receive VoNR proactive grants which are sufficient to also retrieve TCP ACKs with low latency.

 The UE cannot have an active Uplink Configured Grant Type 2 as defined by 5G_UP_ALG_14572_replaced([11172720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuN5oPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with NRBTS/NRULCG_PROFILE.periodicity less than NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugMinSrPeriodicity.

o This will be enforced by the UL scheduler

 UE is not configured with DRB priority PUG, that is, none of DRB of UE is associated to DRB priority PUG profile.

The gNB-DU shall perform the eligibility checks when all the following conditions are satsified

  * the UE is in the TCP Boost Idle state
  * a TCP data flow start indication is received.
  * **[CB013709-B] If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 0 then the real-time (RT) scheduling processing load severity metric (specified in 5G_UP_ALG_31301_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for UL scheduler shall satisfy either one of the following two conditions**
  * **less than the value set for the activation threshold of rdPugTcpBoostOlcUlPerCent (See 5G_UP_ALG_31840_replaced([11216976](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxblv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if overload mitigation actions are not ongoing for TCP PUG**
  * less than the value set for the de-activation threshold of rdPugTcpBoostOlcUlPerCent (See 5G_UP_ALG_31840_replaced([11216976](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxblv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if overload mitigation actions are ongoing for TCP PUG **[End CB013709-B]**



**[CB013709-B]**

Note: If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 0 and the real-time (RT) scheduling processing load severity metric for UL scheduler lies between the activation and deactivation thresholds of rdPugTcpBoostOlcUlPerCent, then as part of the overload mitigation actions, the gNB-DU shall perform/skip the eligibility checks for issuing TCP boost related PUGs to UEs based on the principle specified in 5G_UP_ALG_31314_replaced([11216293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxYiY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[End CB013709-B]**

---

#### 3.30.3.2.0-5 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling TCP Boost Idle State Processing (ID: 11195477)

**3.30.3.2.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11195486`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling When a UE is in the TCP Boost Idle state L2-HI RLC will monitor the UE’s RLC AM DRBs for the start of a downlink TCP data flow. The UL scheduler will be notified when the start of a data flow is detected and which DRB contains the data flow. Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_17102_replaced([11221931](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kx4RrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the details of the L2-HI RLC data flow monitoring requirements. 

Upon being notified that a TCP data flow has been detected for a DRB the UL scheduler shall do the following:

 Verify that the UE is eligible for TCP boost PUGs (see 5G_UP_ALG_16875_replaced([11195456](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8X8_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If the UE is TCP boost eligible proceed to the next step. If the UE is not TCP boost eligible the UL scheduler shall ignore the TCP data flow start notification and the UE shall stay in the TCP Boost Idle state. [CB013709-B] As specified in 5G_UP_ALG_16875_replaced([11195456](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8X8_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), if overload is detected then the UL scheduler in gNB-DU skips the eligibility checks. In that case, the UEs in TCP Boost Idle state shall continue to remain in idle state even if a new TCP data flow is detected. [End CB013709-B]

 If the DRB belongs to a UE whose Proactive Grant type equals Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced (see Note 1) the UL scheduler will immediately transition the UE to the TCP Boost Ramp-up state.

 If the UE’s Proactive Grant Type equals Not Skippable or Disabled (see Note 1) then the UE’s RF condition as specified by rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr (see 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) must be greater than NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugUlMinSinrThresh to allow the UL scheduler to transition the UE to the TCP Boost Ramp-up state.

o If the UE’s RF conditions are below the threshold the UE will stay in the Idle state until L2-HI RLC detects the start of a subsequent data flow and the UE’s RF conditions are above the RF threshold when the detection is made.

Note 1 - The UE's Proactive Grant Type is sent to L2-PS at user setup / user modify via the ulProGrantType field in the PsUser_UserSetupReq / PsUser_UserModifyReq message.

Note 2 - UEs that cannot skip uplink grants and that are in poor RF conditions (edge of cell) will generate too much unneeded inter-cell interference when sending uplink transmissions that are all padding bytes, i.e. TCP ACK was not present at the UE. Therefore it was decided that preventing inter-cell interference by not sending TCP boost PUGs to UEs in poor RF is more important than trying to increase the UE’s TCP data rate which is already limited by the poor RF.

---

#### 3.30.3.2.0-6 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling TCP Boost Ramp-up State Processing (ID: 11195491)

**3.30.3.2.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `11195501`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling When a UE enters the TCP Boost Ramp-up state the UL scheduler shall perform the following actions: 

1\. Wait for the UE to become DRX active. Once the UE is in the DRX active state the UL scheduler shall start the TCP Boost Ramp-up duration.

2\. When the TCP Boost Ramp-up duration has begun the UL scheduler shall wait for an initial delay of rdTcpBoostPugInitialDelay (5G_UP_ALG_17067_replaced([11209069](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwxeZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Once the delay time has been met the uplink scheduler shall perform the below two checks. If both checks pass then the uplink scheduler shall put a TCP boost PUG entry into the pre-scheduler list with tcpBoostTraffic request class priority and a requested data size equal to NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugTbs (see Note 1).

o There are no entries for the UE in any of the uplink request classes that have higher priority than TCP Boost

o If the UE’s Proactive Grant Type equals Not Skippable or Disabled (see Note 2) the UE's rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr (see 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) must be greater than NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugUlMinSinrThresh

3\. At time intervals equal to NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugRampUpInterval the uplink scheduler shall perform the below two checks. If both checks pass then the uplink scheduler shall add a TCP boost PUG entry into the pre-scheduler list with tcpBoostTraffic request class priority and a requested data size equal to NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugTbs.

o There are no entries for the UE in any of the uplink request classes that have higher priority than TCP Boost

o If the UE’s Proactive Grant Type equals Not Skippable or Disabled (see Note 2) the UE's rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr (see 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) must be greater than NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugUlMinSinrThresh

4\. The uplink scheduler shall perform step 3 repeatedly until one of the following conditions occur:

o The time the UE has spent in the Ramp-up state has exceeded NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugRampUpDuration

o L2-HI RLC has determined that the TCP data flow has stopped.

If the UE’s time in the Ramp-up state has exceeded NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugRampUpDuration, the uplink scheduler shall transition the UE to the TCP Boost Stable state.

If the uplink scheduler has received the TCP data flow stop notification, the uplink scheduler shall transition the UE to the TCP Boost Idle state.

If the UE has multiple bearers and L2-HI RLC detects the start of an additional data flow(s) for the UE while the UE is in the Ramp-up state the UL scheduler should ignore the detection(s).

Note 1 - For a TCP Boost entry in the pre-scheduler list if there are sufficient FD resources in the current slot the UE will be sent a grant with a TBS equal to NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugTbs. If there were insufficient FD resources in the current slot to fulfill NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugTbs the PUG will not be sent and will stay in the pre-scheduler list. Per existing functionality, when the grant is sent the PUG entry will be removed from the pre-scheduler list. 

Note 2 - The UE's Proactive Grant Type is sent to L2-PS at user setup / user modify via the ulProGrantType field in the PsUser_UserSetupReq / PsUser_UserModifyReq message.

[CNI-124939]

Note 3 - Interpretation of NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugRampUpInterval:

  * This parameter is in units of ms.
  * For FDD, the parameter value is used directly
  * For TDD FR1, the value used at L2-PS is the closest multiple of either 5ms or 2.5ms (depending on configured TDD frame structure). This will involve a rounddown or roundup as needed for a suitable value



[End CNI-124939]

---

#### 3.30.3.2.0-7 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling TCP Boost Stable State Processing (ID: 11195506)

**3.30.3.2.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11195511`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling When a UE enters the TCP Boost Stable state the UL scheduler shall perform the following actions: 

1\. At time intervals equal to NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugStableInterval the uplink scheduler shall perform the below two checks. If both checks pass then the uplink scheduler shall add a TCP boost PUG entry into the pre-scheduler list with tcpBoostTraffic request class priority and a requested data size equal to NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugTbs (see Note 1).

o There are no entries for the UE in any of the uplink request classes that have higher priority than TCP Boost

o If the UE’s Proactive Grant Type equals Not Skippable or Disabled (see Note 2) the UE's rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr (see 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) must be greater than NRBTS.NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugUlMinSinrThresh

Note - If NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugStableInterval=0 the UL scheduler shall not add TCP boost PUGs to the pre-scheduler list during the Stable duration even if the above checks pass.

2\. The uplink scheduler shall perform step 1 repeatedly until one of the following conditions occur:

o The time the UE has spent in the Stable state has exceeded rrmTcpBoostStableDuration which is equal to NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugDuration minus NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugRampUpDuration. 

o L2-HI RLC has determined that the TCP data flow has stopped.

Note - If NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugDuration is equal to 0, the TCP boost duration is infinite, i.e. rrmTcpBoostStableDuration is infinite. In this case the Stable state can only be exited when L2-HI RLC has determined that the TCP data flow has stopped.

If the UE’s time in the Stable state has exceeded rrmTcpBoostStableDuration then the uplink scheduler shall transition the UE to the TCP Boost Idle state.

If the uplink scheduler has received the TCP data flow stop notification, the uplink scheduler shall transition the UE to the TCP Boost Idle state.

If the UE has multiple bearers and L2-HI RLC detects the start of an additional data flow(s) for the UE while the UE is in the Stable state the UL scheduler should ignore the detection(s).

Note 1 - For a TCP Boost entry in the pre-scheduler list if there are sufficient FD resources in the current slot the UE will be sent a grant with a TBS equal to NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugTbs. If there were insufficient FD resources in the current slot to fulfill NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugTbs the PUG will not be sent and will stay in the pre-scheduler list. Per existing functionality, when the grant is sent the PUG entry will be removed from the pre-scheduler list.

Note 2 - The UE's Proactive Grant Type is sent to L2-PS at user setup / user modify via the ulProGrantType field in the PsUser_UserSetupReq / PsUser_UserModifyReq message.

---

#### 3.30.3.2.0-8 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling TCP Boost Processing For Skippable Grant Capable UEs (ID: 11195517)

**3.30.3.2.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11195522`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling When TCP boost proactive grants are sent to UEs with skippable grant capability the following special handling shall be performed:

 DTX handling: When a UE’s Proactive Grant type = Skippable-Dynamic or Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced (see Note 1), and DTX is detected for an initial transmission associated with a TCP boost proactive grant special handling is required. Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_16415_replaced([11195380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

 Overlap with CSI/HARQ-ACK slot: When a UE’s Proactive Grant type = Skippable-Dynamic (see Note 1), TCP boost proactive grants shall not be scheduled in the same slot with overlapping CSI/HARQ-ACK transmissions on PUCCH. Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_9232_replaced([11195320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

Note 1 - The UE's Proactive Grant Type is sent to L2-PS at user setup / user modify via the ulProGrantType field in the PsUser_UserSetupReq / PsUser_UserModifyReq message.

---

#### 3.30.3.2.0-9 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling TCP Boost Interaction With Other Types Of Proactive Grants (ID: 11195526)

**3.30.3.2.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11195532`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling To ensure that proactive grants are used in an efficient manner when multiple types of proactive uplink grants (PUGs) are active the UL scheduler shall do the following:

 VoNR PUGs - If a UE has a 5QI-1 bearer and the UE’s SR period is greater than 20ms VoNR PUGs will be sent to the UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_4901_replaced([11198250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwIlOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). In this case the TCP boost PUG procedure will not be performed. The VoNR PUGs will be used exclusively. If the UE releases the 5QI-1 bearer the TCP boost PUG procedure should be started.

 Low Priority Periodic PUGs - These PUGs will be stopped for a UE while the UE is in a TCP boost cycle. The TCP boost PUGs will be sent often enough to meet the objective of the Low Priority PUGs (see 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). 

 RLC Status PDU, SRB, and Time alignment PUGs - RLC Status PDU PUGs (see 5G_UP_ALG_6188_replaced([11225322](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kyMavPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), SRB PUGs (see 5G_UP_ALG_26555_replaced([11153913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and Time Alignment PUGs (see 5G_UP_ALG_4844_replaced([11195652](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv9mHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should continue independent of the TCP boost PUGs. These PUGs occur infrequently particularly when there is an active TCP data flow. It is not worth the code complexity to stop these PUGs during a TCP boost cycle

 Ping PUGs - L2-HI RLC will stop the Ping PUG requests (not the UL scheduler) when the UE has an active TCP boost cycle. The TCP boost PUGs will be sent often enough to meet the objective of the Ping PUGs (see 5G_UP_ALG_6191_replaced([11225342](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kyMawPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 3.30.3.2.0-10 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling TCP Boost error handling when parameters are mis-aligned between L2-HI and L2-PS (ID: 11195537)

**3.30.3.2.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11195543`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling Because two different mechanisms are used to deliver the TCP boost parameters to L2-HI and L2-PS there is a possibility of parameter mis-alignment between L2-HI and L2-PS. CP-RT provides the parameter values to L2-HI at bearer setup. L2-HI does not apply online modifications to existing bearers. L2-PS uses the latest values for new TCP boost cycles of all bearers which can cause a mis-alignment for existing bearers. Fortunately NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugDuration is the only parameter that is used by both L2-HI and L2-PS, and can be mis-aligned. The second mis-alignment case is when a TCP boost profile is added or deleted. L2-HI has no knowledge of the profile change for existing bearers whereas L2-PS applies the profile change to all bearers (see Note 1). The third mis-alignment case can occur during UE intra-gNB handover or re-establishment if neighboring cells have different TCP boost configurations. The following describes the handling for each case.

Parameter tcpBoostPugDuration mis-alignment processing:

tcpBoostPugDuration is decreased: 

 Data flow continues beyond the old and new tcpBoostPugDuration: L2-PS will stop the TCP boost PUGs based on new shorter value. L2-HI will stop monitoring the data flow after the old longer tcpBoostPugDuration. There is no negative side effect.

 Data flow stops between the new shorter tcpBoostPugDuration and old longer tcpBoostPugDuration: L2-PS will stop the TCP boost PUGs based on new shorter value. L2-HI will send a data flow stop notification to L2-PS when the data flow stops prior to the longer tcpBoostPugDuration expiry. L2-PS will ignore data flow stop notification because it is received after the TCP boost cycle was stopped and the UE was returned to the Idle state. There is no negative side effect.

 Data flow stops before the old and new tcpBoostPugDuration: L2-HI will send a data flow stop notification to L2-PS when the data flow stops prior to the longer tcpBoostPugDuration expiry. L2-PS will stop the TCP boost cycle when the stop notification is received, i.e. the shorter tcpBoostPugDuration has not yet expired. There is no negative side effect.

tcpBoostPugDuration is increased:

 Data flow continues beyond the old and new tcpBoostPugDuration: L2-HI will stop monitoring the data flow when the old shorter tcpBoostPugDuration expires. L2-PS will continue to send TCP boost PUGs to the UE until the new longer tcpBoostPugDuration expires. There is no negative side effect.

 Data flow stops between the old shorter tcpBoostPugDuration and new longer tcpBoostPugDuration: L2-HI will stop monitoring the data flow when the old shorter tcpBoostPugDuration expires. L2-PS will continue to send TCP boost PUGs to the UE until the new longer tcpBoostPugDuration expires. Note that there is a possible negative side effect. L2-HI will not detect the end of the data flow due to inactivity. L2-PS will continue to send TCP boost PUGs until the tcpBoostPugDuration expires which is beyond when the data flow stopped. If the new tcpBoostPugDuration is set to infinity L2-PS will continue to send TCP boost PUGs to the UE until the bearer is released regardless of when the data flow stopped. This will be an inefficient use of resources but will not break the software. Nor should it impact the performance of the cell given that TCP boost PUGs are lower priority than non-GBR data.

 Data flow stops before the old and new tcpBoostPugDuration: L2-HI will send a data flow stop notification to L2-PS when the data flow stops prior to the old shorter tcpBoostPugDuration expiry. L2-PS will stop the TCP boost cycle when the stop notification is received, i.e. the new longer tcpBoostPugDuration has not yet expired. There is no negative side effect.

TCP Boost Profile addition or deletion mis-alignment processing:

Profile is deleted:

 L2-HI will not be notified of the profile deletion for existing bearers. L2-HI will continue to monitor the legacy bearers and send data flow start and stop indications to L2-PS according to the now deleted profile.

 L2-PS will be notified that the profile was deleted. When L2-PS receives a start notification for a bearer that no longer has a TCP boost profile, the start notification should be ignored. If a stop notification is received after the profile is deleted L2-PS should stop the TCP boost PUGs. 

Profile is added:

 L2-HI will not be notified of the profile addition for existing bearers and will not monitor the existing bearers.

 L2-PS will be notified that the profile was added. However for existing bearers associated with the new profile, L2-PS will not update the existing bearer with the new profile ID. TCP boost PUGs will not be sent to these bearers. 

Note 1: Profile addition / deletion is most often due to configuration changes made by the operator. However cell reconfiguration failure rollback can also result in the profile deletion scenario.

---

#### 3.30.3.2.0-11 TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling TCP Boost behavior during intra-gNB handover or re-establishment (ID: 11195548)

**3.30.3.2.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `11195552`)

TCP Boost Proactive Grant Scheduling The following text describes the TCP boost behavior in handover scenarios.

TCP Boost configuration (parameter or profile) is different between source and target cells:

 L2-HI will continue to use the TCP boost values that were in effect when the bearer was established. L2-PS will switch to the target cell's TCP boost configuration. The results will be as described in the proceeding sections for parameter mis-alignment and profile addition / deletion.

TCP Boost is enabled in one cell and disabled in the other cell:

 If TCP boost is disabled in the cell in which the bearer was established, L2-HI will treat the bearer as not eligible for TCP boost PUGs for the life of the bearer, even if the UE hands over or re-establishes to a cell in which TCP boost is enabled.

 If TCP boost is enabled in the cell in which the bearer was established, L2-HI will treat the bearer as eligible for TCP boost PUGs and will monitor the bearer for the life of the bearer. When the UE hands over or re-establishes to a cell in which TCP boost is disabled, L2-PS will ignore the data flow start and stop notifications sent from L2-HI.

TCP Boost PUGs are stopped when the UE hands over or re-establishes to a new cell:

 The UE's TCP boost state is not transferred to the target cell. If prior to handover or re-establishment a bearer is in an active TCP boost cycle, i.e. TCP boost PUGs are being sent to the UE, the PUGs will stop once the UE hands over or re-establishes to the target cell. However, L2-HI will continue to monitor the bearer. If a new TCP data flow starts for that bearer a new TCP boost cycle will be initiated. This assumes that both cells have TCP boost enabled.

---

### 3.30.3.3 DRB priority Proactive Grant Scheduling

#### 3.30.3.3.0-1 DRB priority Proactive Grant Scheduling DRB priority Proactive Uplink Grant Overview (ID: 11195564)

**3.30.3.3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13407778`)

DRB priority Proactive Grant Scheduling gNB-DU shall consider a UE is configured with DRB priority Proactive UL grant while any of DRB is associated with any of DRB priority PUG profile NRDRB_PRIO_PUG.

gNB-DU shall select the DRB priority PUG profile according to DRB priorityLevel if more than one DRB of the UE are associated with DRB priroity PUG profiles, that is, the DRB priority PUG profile associated to highest DRB priority(i.e. the lowest NRBTS/NRDRB.priorityLevel value) shall be applied. If more than one DRB have same highest DRB priority which associated to DRB priority PUG profiles, gNB-DU shall select one of them according to corresponding DRB type (i.e.[CB014699-E] criticalGbrTraffic>criticalDrbTraffic > [End CB014699-E] DC-GBR DRB>nonVoNR GBR DRB>non-GBR DRB). If both of them are same (i.e. same value of highest priority and same DRB type), gNB-DU can selet any one of them, detail can be decided at EFS. If gNB-DU select another DRB profile or corresponding parameters updated in select DRB profile when DRB add/remove, gNB-DU shall stop the duration timer and interval timer for DRB priority PUG if it is still running, and remove the pending DRB Priority PUG which has not be sent out( that is, gNB-DU shall update the UL buffer housekeeping variables follow 5G_UP_ALG_32052_replaced([11204049](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwi05PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB014699-E] If a DRB is configured as both critical (by setting NRDRB.criticalDrbSchedEnabled =True) and for DRB Priority PUG, the gNB-DU prioritizes its resource requests and sends proactive grants when the uplink (UL) buffer is empty [End CB014699-E].

 DRB priority PUG control, see 5G_UP_ALG_31175_replaced([11195572](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8_DPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 DRB priority PUG Timing, see 5G_UP_ALG_30973_replaced([11195595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv9mEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 DRB priority PUG scheduling, see 5G_UP_ALG_30970_replaced([11195605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv9mFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.30.3.3.0-2 DRB priority Proactive Grant Scheduling DRB Priority Proactive Grants: Control (ID: 11195572)

**3.30.3.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11195578`)

DRB priority Proactive Grant Scheduling L2 PS shall define UE eligibility and grant control parameters for DRB Priority UL Proactive Grants, as defined below.

Control Parameters and variables:

 Grant Types

 Proactive Grant Type for UE DRB priority PUG

L2PS shall decide the Grant type according to ulProGrantType field received and the DRB priroity PUG profile selected.

 For DRB priority PUG Proactive Grant Type = Skippable-Dynamic, Skippable-Dynamic-Enhanced, Not Skippable is same as Proactive Grant Type for LP PUG, please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 For DRB priority PUG Proactive Grant Type = disabled if any of the following is true:
