Refer to **[Before CB014044-UPD002] 5G_L2_9328_replaced([11183534](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27j-ePvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **[CB014044-UPD002] [12737015: rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3lQpQndyEfCdZt6bzaG3fw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) **[End CB014044-UPD002]****_* [PR863926] If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =(UL or DL_UL): rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst refers to R &D parameter rdPfMetricReinitFilterConstLongHistory(refer to [12737015](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3lQpQndyEfCdZt6bzaG3fw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)). otherwise, rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst refers to legacy R&D parameter rdPfMetricReinitFilterConst (see 5G_L2_9328_replaced([11183534](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27j-ePvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End PR863926] , where i: Index of the 5G UE j: Index of carrier N: number of UEs i in candidate set 1 _*[CB014044-UPD002]** rrmUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrierExclVarFactors[i,j,t-1]: Same as rrmUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier of UE i in carrier j in slot t-1(see [11195815](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) with the exception that the rrmRGWeight of the RG associated with its bearers shall be excluded in the calculation. S: All UEs scheduled of DRB priority class (excluding NBR sub-category) in slot t-1 [End CB014044-UPD002] WUL, FR1_SCell_Activation = 2 for FR1 SCell and 1 for other case. **Note: After SCell activation, 5G-L2-PS will not know the SCell UL channel quality so boost the initial PF metric to get the UL channel quality ASAP.** In case there are no UEs scheduled in the previous slot (t-1) of DRB priority class **[CB014044-UPD002](If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =\(UL or DL_UL\): excluding NBR sub-category)**[End CB014044-UPD002] and there are no UEs of DRB priority class**[CB014044-UPD002](If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =\(UL or DL_UL\): excluding NBR sub-category)**[End CB014044-UPD002] in CS1 list, then the value of 0 is input to the filter in the current slot,**[Before CB014044-UPD002] i.e., we set**** _*_**_[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is a fraction set equal to zero. The numerator is a summation of "rrmUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i,j,t-1]" over a set "i∈I". The denominator is "Number of Scheduled UL UEs of DRB priority class in slot (t-1)".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15301.002.png[/image_path][/image-2]__[End CB014044-UPD002] i.e., filter is decayed.__In case there are no UEs scheduled in the previous slot (t-1) of DRB priority class_ _[CB014044-UPD002](If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =\(UL or DL_UL\): excluding NBR sub-category)_ _[End CB014044-UPD002] and there were UEs of DRB priority class_ _[CB014044-UPD002](If NRBTS/NRCELL/actTdPfMetricOpt =\(UL or DL_UL\): excluding NBR sub-category)_ _[End CB014044-UPD002] in CS1 list but just didn’t get scheduled, then the current value of_** _*_** initUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j,t-1] is input to the filter in the current slot,**[Before CB014044-UPD002] i.e., we set** [image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is presented as a fraction on the left side, which is equal to a term on the right side. The numerator of the fraction is: $\sum_{i \in I} \text{rrmUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier}[i,j,t-1]$ The denominator of the fraction is: Number of Scheduled UL UEs of DRB priority class in slot $(t-1)$ The right side of the equation is: $\text{initUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier}[j,t-1]$ Therefore, the full equation is: $$ \frac{\sum_{i \in I} \text{rrmUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier}[i,j,t-1]}{\text{Number of Scheduled UL UEs of DRB priority class in slot } (t-1)} = \text{initUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier}[j,t-1] $$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15301.003.png[/image_path][/image-3] **[End CB014044-UPD002] i.e., filter is frozen.** } Then the following operation shall give the initial average UL data rate for t=0 of 5G UE i depending on the number of configured component carriers #CCUL for UE i: [Before CB014044-M] [image-4][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03212205.png[/image_path][/image-4] [CB014044-M] If NRCELL.actTdPfMetricOpt is set neither to UL_Only nor DL_UL [Before PR866791] [image-5][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $$rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i, t_0] = \frac{1}{#CC_{UL}} \times \sum_{\text{all carrier } j \text{ of } UE_i} \frac{rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i, j, t_0]}{iniUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j, t_0]} \times \max\left{1, \sum_{k} W[i, k, t_0]\right}$$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-05041728.png[/image_path][/image-5] [End CB014044-M] [PR866791] [image-6][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i, t_0] = \frac{1}{#CC_{UL}} \times \sum_{all \ carrier \ j \ of \ UE_i} \frac{rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i, j, t_0]}{iniUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j, t_0]} \times ueWeight[i]$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-01300758.png[/image_path][/image-6] [End PR866791] [CB014044-M] If NRCELL.actTdPfMetricOpt is set to UL_Only or DL_UL [Before PR866791] [image-7][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i, t₀] = (1 / #CCUL) × Σall carrier j of UEi (rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i, j, t₀] / (rdInitUlUeSchedMetricScalingFactor × iniUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j, t₀])) × max{1, Σk W[i, k, t₀]}[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-05041729.png[/image_path][/image-7] [End CB014044-M] [PR866791] [image-8][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula is: $rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i, t_0] = \frac{1}{#CC_{UL}} \times \sum_{all \ carrier \ j \ of \ UE_i} \frac{rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i, j, t_0]}{rdInitUlUeSchedMetricScalingFactor \times iniUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[j, t_0]} \times ueWeight[i]$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-01300800.png[/image_path][/image-8] [End PR866791] rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i,t0] = max(rdMinExpectedBwShare _rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i,t0] * MaxPRBs, rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i,t0]), with: i: Index of the 5G UE j: Index of the carrier k: Index of the non-GBR bearer [PR866791] ueWeight[i]: the weight of UE i.[If enhanced slice aware scheduler is deactivated for the cell (](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwr-0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)[11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)[)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwr-0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [image-9][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in black text on a white background. The equation is: `ueWeight[i] = max{1, ∑k W[i, k, to]};`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-01300805.png[/image_path][/image-9] Else [image-10][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: ueWeight[i] = max { (rdRGMinLowShareUL / rdRGTinyWeightReciUL), Σk(W[i, k, t₀] × rrmRGWeightk(t₀)) }.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-01300805.png[/image_path][/image-10] End If For references, rdRGMinLowShareUL ([11209263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyshfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), rdRGTinyWeightReciUL ([11209353](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwzTlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and rrmRGWeight ([11205528](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). [End PR866791] W[i,k,t]: _ _[CB014044-UPD002] If NRCELL.actTdPfMetricOpt is set either to UL_Only or DL_UL: It shall be calculated as the sum of SchedWeightFactor(see[11195869](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0OfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) of nonGBR bearer(s) k for UE i at slot t0with data in buffer in corresponding LCG. For UEs belonging to NBR category within drbTraffic Class ([1194709](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv24YfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), NBR configured nonGBR bearer containing LCG(s) with rrmNbrTokenBucket[i,k,t0] > 0 shall only be considered. Else (NRCELL.actTdPfMetricOpt is set neither to UL_Only nor DL_UL): _ _[End CB014044-UPD002] schedulWeight of non-GBR bearer k for 5G UE i at slot t with data in buffer of corresponding LCG._ *** [CB014044-M] rdInitUlUeSchedMetricScalingFactor: The scaling factor for the initial average scheduler metric defined in [12552787](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_PAGecCTyEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)[End CB014044-M] rdMinExpectedBwShare: the minimum fraction of the bandwidth that assume any UE can used for receive, set as R&D parameter with range between [0,1], default value is 1/200, which corresponding to 0.5% of the channel bandwidth in the worst case. MaxPRBs: Maximum number of PRBs of bandwidth on carrier i, refer to requirement 5G_UP_ALG_1795_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) . There is a limit on the maximum value of initial average data rate, this limit is enforced with the following operation: in FR1, rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,0] = min ( rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,0] , rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i,j,0] for j= index of current carrier ) in FR2, rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,0] = min ( rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,0] , sum[j, for all active carriers of UE i]{rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,j,0]} ) Note that only a single carrier is configured per UE. For the first UE which is configured in L2-PS the average data rate is initialized based on ullaIniMcs (NIDD): [image-11][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15301.005.png[/image_path][/image-11] where MaxPRBs is defined in the requirement 5G_UP_ALG_1795_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (known as Maximum number of PRBs).

##### 3.30.4.2.0-12 Definition of per UE and per subcell / carrier scheduling metric for UL Definition of the PF numerator in case of Low Buffer Optimization (ID: 12549822)

**3.30.4.2.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `12549831`)

Definition of per UE and per subcell / carrier scheduling metric for UL [CB014044-L]

Rationale: The Proportional Fair (PF) metric is biased to prefer UEs with low data-rate. This includes Non-GBR UEs with a low data rate due to its traffic model and not due to its radio conditions if they have some data to send. This results in high PF metric for these UEs and might yield in reduced MCS if the MCS downgrade is activated and lower PRB utilization to take its low buffer fillings into account. Additionally, these low TP UEs stay with high PF metric (they got scheduled but actually did not send much) while other UEs with high buffer load are neglected. In case of NRCELL.actUlSchLowBufferOptimization is set to true this requirement changes the calculation of the current 5G UE UL Data Rate which is the numerator of the PF-metric to take a low utilization and other constraints into account. Please note that this is meant only for FDD and TDD FR1 and a PDL rule is in place to avoid usage of NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlSchLowBufferOptimization for TDD FR2 which is using different concepts compared to FR1 FDD and TDD.

if NRBTS/NRCELL/actUlSchLowBufferOptimization is set to true

the rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate in [11195815](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) is replaced by rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRate which is at maximum the same as rmCurrentUlUeDataRate in case of high buffer occupation but might be downgraded in case of low buffer utilization. This correction is achieved by an additional term which consist of the maximum of three parts. At first too high latency should be avoided by the rrmLatencyUlAvoidVirtualBuffer term. Then the main idea is put into place by taking the Non-GBR buffer occupation of the currently evaluated UE into account. Additionally, we assure a minimum of the PF-metric numerator by defining a fraction of rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate as a minimum as defined by the following calculation rule.

rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] =

min (

max (

rrmLatencyUlAvoidVirtualBuffer_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t],

sum[k: logical Channel Group Id (LCG) of non-GBR DRBs of UE i]{rrmBufferedDataLcg_bytes[k, i]} *

min(rdUlSchLowBufferFdmAssumption, rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl),

(1 - rdUlSchLowBufferMaxPenalty/100.0) * rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t]

),

rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t]

)

with:

i: Index of the 5G UE

j: Index of the carrier

k: logical Channel group Id of UE’s non-GBR DRBs

t: NR slot the calculation relates to

The R&D parameter rdUlSchLowBufferMaxPenalty is defined in [12548625](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3ACiICQJEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and rdUlSchLowBufferFdmAssumption is defined in [12548629](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_LWKtxCQKEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). The parameter rrmBufferedDataLcg_bytes is defined in [11203638](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). The current 5G UE UL data rate rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] is defined in [11195916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). Finally, rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl is defined in [11199273](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

The rrmUlLatencyAvoidVirtualBuffer is attempting to mitigate too high additional latency for the three different use cases. These three cases are:

1) Initial Tx shall not be degraded/delayed, especially to avoid Ping and TCP ramp-up delay problems.

2) Trailing bits of a data burst should be finished rather fast, so if you have transmitted bigger data packets the final transmission should not be delayed ideally.

3) A general aging approach so that the UEs in general do not suffer for too long.

rrmLatencyUlAvoidVirtualBuffer_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] is defined as follows:

If rrmAvgUlUeDataRate has been reset (according to [11195975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)): (First TB of a traffic burst case):

rrmLatencyUlAvoidVirtualBuffer_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] =

(1 - rdUlSchLowBufferReinitPenalty/100.0) * rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t]

otherwise (Aging)

rrmLatencyUlAvoidVirtualBuffer_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] =

rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRatePost_BytesPerSlot[t - rrmNumSlotSinceLastUlSched] /

((1.0 - 1.0/rdUlSchLowBufferFilterConst) ^ rrmNumSlotSinceLastUlSched)

end

rrmNumSlotSinceLastUlSched is defined in [11195975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) e.g. it is the number of slots since the last time the UE was in CS1 list, evaluated for scheduling. rdUlSchLowBufferReinitPenalty is defined in [12548642](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_b-BQ0CQKEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and rdUlSchLowBufferFilterConst is defined in [12548655](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rBRNkCQKEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

The gNB-DU shall calculate the a-posteriori downgraded UL UE data rate after the scheduling decision and it is dependent if there was a transmission in the current UL slot or not as follows:

If the UE i has been granted over the air for the current slot on the carrier j:

rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRatePost_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] =

(1 - rdUlSchLowBufferTbTxPenalty/100.0) * tbSize_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] * min(rdUlSchLowBufferFdmAssumption, rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl)

otherwise

rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRatePost_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] = rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t]

end

Please note that it is up to implementation to calculate rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRatePost either directly after the scheduling (like defined here) so at time [t] but then used in later slots when UE is again in CS1 list or prior to the scheduling but then with respect to the last time the UE was in CS1 list [t - rrmNumSlotSinceLastDlSched].

The R&D parameter rdUlSchLowBufferTbTxPenalty is defined in [12548675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_4TLIgiQKEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). tbSize_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] is the tbSize for this UE's aggregated traffic volume for all initial transmissions in this UL slot (t). The maximum number of UEs to be scheduled (FDMed) in UL (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl) is defined in [11199273](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[End CB014044-L]

---

##### 3.30.4.2.0-13 Definition of per UE and per subcell / carrier scheduling metric for UL UL PF low buffer optimization additional explanation (ID: 12550393)

**3.30.4.2.0-13.0-1**  (ID: `12550400`)

Definition of per UE and per subcell / carrier scheduling metric for UL 12550400: Definition of per UE and per subcell / carrier scheduling metric for UL [CB014044-L]

Rationale: The time flow in the low buffer optimized PF algorithm and additional comments are explained within this informational requirement. For the functional description please refer to [12549822](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_xSpfgCQfEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

If NRCELL.actUlSchLowBufferOptimization is set to true the current workflow is depicted below for the time slot t.

The light blue squares are basically new behavior and the yellow ones are either modified behavior or at least used once within the scheduler.

The rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate in [11195815](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) is replaced by rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRate which is at maximum the same as rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate in case of high buffer occupation but might be downgraded in case of low buffer utilization and for that the buffer occupancy is needed.

These values, rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate (calculated as before) and the buffer occupancy are two of the main inputs of this algorithm. A third part tries to mitigate negative effects additionally expected in form of latency for these low buffer UEs.

In order to do so, this rrmLatencyUlAvoidVirtualBuffer is calculated and it consists also of three parts. We try to avoid downgrading for UEs just arriving or having a new burst coming after some time of being without data. This is done by checking if the average data rate (legacy behavior) was reset. So that for these UEs are not downgraded . A second term checks the actually used transport-block size from the last time. This can be directly derived from the last time the UE was scheduled so basically at the end of the depicted graph after scheduling is done, defined for t and used in the next slot the UE is again in CS1 list. In case the UE is not scheduled the already calculated rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRate is used for rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRatePost instead. This after scheduling (a-posterori) updated rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRatePost is then used the next time the UE enters the CS1 list after aging is applied which rather fast increases the numerator of the PF term, too (left hand side of the figure).

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to User Equipment (UE) data rate calculation and management. The flowchart begins with a "Start" oval and ends with an "End" oval. The process involves several steps represented by rectangular boxes, decision points by diamond shapes (though none are explicitly diamond-shaped, the question boxes function as such), and connectors by arrows. Here's a breakdown of the flowchart's elements: **Start:** * An oval labeled "Start". **Initial Calculations:** * A yellow rectangle labeled "Calculate rrmCurrentUIUeDataRate". * A yellow rectangle labeled "Calculate Buffer occupancy". * A green rectangle labeled "Aging as a general means to avoid too high latency". **Decision Point and Conditional Paths:** * A yellow rectangle labeled "rrmAvgUlUeDataRate reset?". * **No Path:** Leads to a light blue rectangle labeled "Aging: rrmLatencyUlAvoid dVirtualBuffer". This path also receives input from the "rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRatePost from past calculation (last time UE was in CS1 list)" text. * **Yes Path:** Leads to a light blue rectangle labeled "New calculation: rrmLatencyUlAvoid VirtualBuffer". A dark green speech bubble points to this path, stating "Used to avoid latency for TCP slow start and ping". **Further Calculations and Decisions:** * Both the "No" and "Yes" paths from the "rrmAvgUlUeDataRate reset?" decision converge and lead to a light blue rectangle labeled "Calculate rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRate". * A dark green speech bubble points to the "Calculate rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRate" box, stating "Only one path used". * A yellow rectangle labeled "PF-Metric calculation". * A dark green speech bubble points to the "PF-Metric calculation" box, stating "Used to avoid latency of last trailing data block". * A yellow rectangle labeled "UE got scheduled". * **No Path:** Leads to a light blue rectangle labeled "rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRatePost(t) = rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRate(t)". A dark green speech bubble points to this path, stating "If not scheduled update a-posteriori value with Eq1 calculated one". * **Yes Path:** Leads to a light blue rectangle labeled "Calculate rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRatePost from used TBS". **Update and End:** * Both the "No" and "Yes" paths from the "UE got scheduled" decision converge to a light blue rectangle labeled "Update of rrmDowngradedUlUeDataRatePost". * An oval labeled "End". The flowchart uses different colored boxes to represent different types of operations: * Ovals: Start/End points. * Yellow Rectangles: Calculations or decision points. * Light Blue Rectangles: Data storage or specific calculation results. * Dark Green Rectangles/Speech Bubbles: Explanations or contextual information.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-04281312.png[/image_path][/image-0]

[End CB014044-L]

---

### 3.30.4.3 Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria

#### 3.30.4.3.0-1 Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria DC-GBR TD scheduling criteria calculation (ID: 11196041)

**3.30.4.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11196045`)

Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria Calculation of the time domain (TD) scheduling criteria for uplink DC-GBR has the following characteristics:

  * The data rate of the DC-GBR bearer is monitored as below:
  * The long term average DC-GBR data rate (GFBR over AW) is monitored via a sliding window (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_30953_replaced([11196083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If the DC-GBR data rate exceeds the GFBR target rate, that is ,guaranteedFlowBitRateUplink, the DC-GBR bearer will be depriotised to dePrioritizedGbr traffic class (see 5G_UP_ALG_8042_replaced([11194763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv24a_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
  * The short term max burst data rate (MDBV over PDB) is monitored via token bucket (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_30954_replaced([11196094](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If the DC-GBR data exceeds the token bucket size (i.e. token bucket becomes negative) the DC-GBR bearer will be depriotised to dePrioritizedGbr traffic class (see 5G_UP_ALG_8042_replaced([11194763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv24a_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
  * DC-GBR bearer initial traffic shall only be prioritized and be included in dcGbrTraffic request class (see 5G_UP_ALG_31011_replaced([11194661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv2RXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) only if both the short term max burst data rate (MDBV over PDB) and the long term average data rate (GFBR over AW) have not exceeded their respective target rates.



 DC-GBR bearers shall be considered in the calculation of the TD criteria only if there is data waiting for initial transmission.

The calculation of the UL DC-GBR TD scheduling criteria shall be as follows:

C UL,i (t) = PF UL, i, DC-GBR (t) * C2 UL,i,m(t)

where

PF UL, i, DC-GBR (t) DC-GBR time domain Proportional Fair term as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_8047_replaced([11196154](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

C2 UL, i, m (t) DC-GBR specific scheduling criteria as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_30949_replaced([11196051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 

with

i index of the UE

m index of the LCG serving the DC-GBR bearer of UE i

t current slot

Note: There shall be only one DC-GBR bearer supported per UE and it shall be mapped to a separate LCG. Hence, DC-GBR bearer/LCG will be used interchangeably. If in future features, multiple DC-GBR bearers are supported, then the DC-GBR related requirements need to be revisited again.

---

#### 3.30.4.3.0-2 Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria DC-GBR delay metric calculation (ID: 11196051)

**3.30.4.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11196056`)

Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria Calculation of the delay metric for DC-GBR bearers shall be done as follows.

C2 UL,i,m = delayTermUL,i,, m (t) for the LCG serving DC-GBR bearer if the UE is eligible for initial transmission, the corresponding LCG serving the DC-GBR bearer has a non-zero buffer size and it has priorized DC-GBR data traffic (see 5G_UP_ALG_30945_replaced([11196041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[Before CB008600-F] C2 UL,i,m = 1 [CB008600-F] if enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) C2 UL,i,m = rrmRGWeighti, m Else C2 UL,i,m = 1 [End CB008600-F] for the LCG serving DC-GBR bearer if the UE is eligible for initial transmission, the corresponding LCG serving the DC-GBR bearer has a non-zero buffer size but it has been de-prioritized (see 5G_UP_ALG_30945_replaced([11196041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). In this case the bearer will be put into the deprioritizedGbr traffic request class.

C2 UL,i,m = 0 otherwise, i.e. LCG serving the DC-GBR bearer has no data or the UE is ineligible for initial transmission. 

where

delayTermUL,i,, m (t) Delay Metric Value according to 5G_UP_ALG_30952_replaced([11196063](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB008600-F]

rrmRGWeighti, m RG weight of the RG to which the LCG m serving the DC-GBR bearer of UE i belongs, as per 5G_UP_ALG_23167_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB008600-F]

with

i index of the UE

m index of the LCG serving the DC-GBR bearer of UE i

t current slot

---

#### 3.30.4.3.0-3 Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria Delay metric value (DMV) calculation (ID: 11196063)

**3.30.4.3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11196067`)

Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria DC-GBR Delay Term (delayTerm) is based on a unique linear curve for each DC-GBR 5QI. The slope and the maximum value of the curve mainly depends on packet delay budget (NRBTS/NRDRB.delayCriticalGbrDrbParam.ulRanPdb), priority(NRBTS/NRDRB.priorityLevel) and the UL discard timer (NRBTS/NRDRB.discardTimerUl) for that bearer among other parameters.

If HOL_delay <= confDT5QI, m:

delayTermi, m (t) = maxDW5QI, m * (HOL_delaym (t) / confDT5QI, m )

Else (i.e. HOL_delay > confDT5QI, m)

If discardTimer5QI, m <= confDT5QI, m

delayTermi, m (t) = maxDW5QI, m

Else if discardTimer > confDT5QI, m

if HOL_delay <= discardTimer5QI, m

delayTermi, m (t) = maxDW5QI, m + rdSlopeFactorForDelayTerm * maxDW5QI, m * ((HOL_delaym (t) - confDT5QI, m )/ (discardTimer5QI, m- confDT5QI, m )) 

Else (i.e. HOL_delay > discardTimer5QI, m)

delayTermi, m (t) = (1 + rdSlopeFactorForDelayTerm)*maxDW5QI, m

with

t currrent slot

i index of the considered UE

m index of the LCG serving the DC-GBR bearer of UE i

where

confDT5QI, m 5QI-dependent configured delay target of DC-GBR DRB contained in LCG m, given by NRBTS/NRDRB.delayCriticalGbrDrbParam/ulRanPdb

discardTimer5QI, m UL Discard timer for the DC-GBR bearer contained in LCG m given by NRBTS/NRDRB.discardTimerUl. If NRBTS/NRDRB.discardTimerUl is not configured for the DRB, then NRBTS/NRDRB.discardTimer shall be used.

HOL_delaym (t) Head-of-line packet delay of DC-GBR DRB contained in LCG m at time t as described in 5G_UP_ALG_30956_replaced([11196073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdSlopeFactorForDelayTerm RD parameter to adjust the rate of increase of the delayTerm when the HOL_delay increases above confDT (see 5G_UP_ALG_30419_replaced([11194081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvym8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

maxDW5QI, m 5QI-dependent delay term/weight value reached for DC-GBR DRB contained in LCG m when its HOL packet delay becomes equal to confDT which shall be computed as below

maxDW5QI, m = 100/(priority5QI,m * 0.1)

with

priority5QI,m Priority of the DC-GBR DRB contained in LCG m given by NRBTS/NRDRB.priorityLevel

---

#### 3.30.4.3.0-4 Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria Head of Line Packet Delay determination (ID: 11196073)

**3.30.4.3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11196077`)

Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria Head of line (HOL) packet delay estimation for DC-GBR bearers. HOL packet represents the oldest packet that has arrived in the UE’s transmission buffer. To estimate the HOL packet delay accurately the HOL packet arrival time shall be determined. The HOL packet arrival time is calculated every time one of the following events occur: 1) UE sends a BSR with a non-zero value for an LCG containing the DC-GBR bearer, 2) UE transmits DC-GBR data on PUSCH, and 3) UE sends an SR. Note that the gNB must determine the slot in which the UE transmitted the initial BSR or PUSCH transmission if HARQ retransmissions were required. 

The following defines the HOL calculations that are required when one of the three events occur:

  1. BSR is received and contains a non-zero value for the LCG which contains the DC-GBR bearer:



bufferSizecurrent, LCG = 8 * BSRLCG

tHOL = t – bufferSizecurrent, LCG / GFBRRLC

where

bufferSizecurrent, LCG current buffer size in bits for LCG

BSR LCG data size in bytes reported for LCG

t current time 

tHOL time instance that the HOL packet arrived in the UE transmission buffer

GFBRRLC GFBR including the addtional overhead bytes for the DC-GBR bearer as described in 5G_UP_ALG_30958_replaced([11196117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

NOTE: The BSR size level index for the LCG represents a range of buffer size (BS) values in bytes. In this calculation BSRLCG shall use the upper boundary of the BS Value. The BS Value for the highest BS Index shall be defined as 1.4 times the BS Value of previous Index.

2\. PUSCH grant contains data for DC-GBR bearer:

tHOL = tHOL + TBSDC-GBR / GFBRRLC

where

TBSDC-GBR estimate of DC-GBR portion of the UL grant.

tHOL and GFBRRLC as defined in item 1.

When the transmitted TB is received at the gNB, the estimate is corrected as follows:

bufferSizeold, LCG = bufferSizecurrent, LCG

bufferSizecurrent, LCG = max (0, bufferSizeold, LCG – BitsRxcurrent, LCG + GFBRRLC * tsinceLastBSRorULTx)

tHOL = t - bufferSizecurrent, LCG / GFBRRLC

where

tsinceLastBSRorULTx time between the current UL scheduling slot and the UL slot when the most recent BSR or PUSCH transmission was transmitted by the UE.

BitsRxcurrent, LCG The number of bits sucessfully received from UE in the PUSCH TB for the LCG containing DC-GBR bearer

tHOL and GFBRRLC as defined in item 1.

NOTE: If the PUSCH transmission and BSR corresponding to DC-GBR bearer becomes available in the same time instance, the BSR is used to estimate the instance of the HOL packet arrival.

3\. SR is received:

If an SR is received and bufferSizecurrent = 0, determine the HOL packet arrival (tHOL)

tHOL = tSR – rdULDataArrivalEstBasedOnSr * srPeriodicity 

where

tSR slot that SR was received by gNB

srPeriodicity SR periodicity of the UE that sent the SR

rdULDataArrivalEstBasedOnSr R&D parameter to predict the data arrival time in UE after the reception of SR (see 5G_UP_ALG_31811_replaced([11209632](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw1IwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Disregard the SR for HOL delay estimation in the following scenarios:

  * If SR is transmitted after a BSR is received by the gNB and before an UL grant was sent to the UE, and bufferSizecurrent > 0, i.e. tHOL is determined based on the BSR information
  * If SR is transmitted after an UL grant was sent to the UE and bufferSizecurrent > 0
  * If SR is transmitted again without an intermediate BSR or PUSCH transmission



NOTE: SRs are triggered by regular BSRs which relate to the logical channel with the highest priority for which new data becomes available for transmission. Given that the priority of the DC-GBR bearers is higher than the priority of the non-GBR bearers and below the priority of SRBs which are characterised by sporadic data transfer it can be assumed that the SRs are triggered primarily by the DC-GBR bearer. 

The resultant HOL packet delay (HOL_delay) for the LCG m containing the DC-GBR bearer at time tdelta after the slot when a SR, BSR, PUSCH transmission is made is defined as:

If an SR is transmitted after a PUSCH transmission is received and bufferSizecurrent = 0 then packet delay is:

HOL_delaym (t) = tsr + tdelta - tHOL

Else if a BSR or a PUSCH transmission is received then the packet delay is defined as:

HOL_delaym (t) = tBSRorPUSCH + tdelta - tHOL

where

tsr slot that last SR was received by gNB

tBSRorPUSCH slot that last BSR or PUSCH transmission was received by gNB

tHOL time that head of line packet was created at UE. See above for tHOL calculations.

tdelta time (ms) between current slot and the slot that SR, BSR, PUSCH transmission was received by gNB

---

#### 3.30.4.3.0-5 Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria Monitoring of GFBR over AW (ID: 11196083)

**3.30.4.3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11196088`)

Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria For each LCG containing DC-GBR bearer, gNB-DU shall maintain a sliding window to monitor its long term guaranteed flow bitrate over the average window (GFBR over AW). For this purpose, the sum of the successfully received data from the DC-GBR bearer over the length of the sliding window shall always be maintained. The length of the sliding window shall be given by the averaging window duration for the monitoring of guaranteed flow bitrate of the DC-GBR bearer. The length of this averaging window shall be decided in CPlane according to NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB.delayCriticalGbrDrbParam.avgWindow of corresponding DC-GBR bearer or from NGAP message.

At any point of time, if the sum of the successfully received data over the sliding window is greater than GFBRMAC (see 5G_UP_ALG_30958_replaced([11196117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) multiplied by the length of the sliding window, then it shall be considered that the DC-GBR has exceeded its GFBR rate and the bearer shall be de-prioritised in scheduling as described in 5G_UP_ALG_30949_replaced([11196051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note: It shall be decided in EFS level on the more granular details on the maintenance of sliding window according to CPU load usage and memory usage.

---

#### 3.30.4.3.0-6 Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria Monitoring of MDBV over PDB (ID: 11196094)

**3.30.4.3.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11196098`)

Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria Overview

---

**3.30.4.3.0-6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11196103`)

Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria gNB-DU shall monitor the the short term maximum burst data rate of all LCGs containing the DC-GBR bearer via a token bucket as described in 5G_UP_ALG_30976_replaced([11196108](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Eventually, while scheduling the DC-GBR bearer, if the resultant token bucket value is positive >= 0, it shall consider that the DC-GBR bearer has not exceeded the maximum short term burst data rate (MDBV over PDB) and shall prioritise it provided that the long term guranteed flow bit rate is also not exceeded (see 5G_UP_ALG_30953_replaced([11196083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). However, if the token bucket value is negative, then the DC-GBR bearer shall be de-prioritised in scheduling as described in 5G_UP_ALG_8042_replaced([11194763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv24a_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.30.4.3.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `11196108`)

Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria DC-GBR Rate Monitoring with Token Bucket

---

**3.30.4.3.0-6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11196112`)

Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria gNB-DU shall maintain a token bucket variable rrmDcGbrTokenBucket(i,j) [bits] for each LCG i containing DC-GBR bearer for UE j. It is updated as below

  * when LCG i with DC-GBR bearer is established, rrmDcGbrTokenBucket shall be initialized to dcGbrTokenBucketUpperLimit.

  * Ideally, after rrmDcGbrTokenBucket is initialized, it shall be incremented every slot by the product of dcGbrPrioritisedBitRate x slotDuration_msec and reduced whenever the data is successfully received for the UE. However, this can be optimized by incrementing rrmDcGbrTokenBucket only at the beginning of the slot in which the UE configured with DC-GBR bearer is being considered for scheduling.

  * rrmDcGbrTokenBucket (i,j,t) = rrmDcGbrTokenBucket (i,j,t-1) + dcGbrPrioritisedBitRate (i,j) * elapsedTime_msec



where, elapsedTime_msec represents the time difference in msecs between the current and the last increment of rrmDcGbrTokenBucket as per this condition.

  * The token bucket variable rrmDcGbrTokenBucket(i,j) shall later be updated when the uplink grant[CB012810-E](include a DRB priority PUG)[End CB012810-E] is given to the UE with an allocation for the DC-GBR bearer i.e. rrmDcGbrTokenBucket(i,j) = rrmDcGbrTokenBucket (i,j) - numAllocDcGbrBearer_bits, where numAllocDcGbrBearer_bits is the number of bits that were allocated to the DC-GBR bearer as part of the UL grant. Later, the rrmDcGbrTokenBucket (i,j) shall be corrected by adjusting it with actual successfully received data for the DC-GBR bearer when the corresponding PUSCH transmission is successfully received or when the HARQ transmission was a failure and maxRe-Tx reached (in this case, receivedData_bits = 0) i.e. rrmDcGbrTokenBucket(i,j) = rrmDcGbrTokenBucket (i,j) - receivedData_bits + numAllocDcGbrBearer_bits. Note, DC-GBR bearer data can be transmitted by the UE also in UL PUSCH triggered by SR response UL grant. Note, it shall also be possible that SR grants/UL proactive grants can also be used by the UE to schedule the data for the DC_GBR bearer. In those cases, when scheduling the UL grant, numAllocDcGbrBearer_bits will be considered 0 as gNB-DU is unaware that the UL grant might be used for transmitting the data of DC-GBR bearer. However, even for those UL grants, on PUSCH reception, the rrmDcGbrTokenBucket (i,j) shall be reduced with the amount of sucessfully received DC-GBR data, if present.

  * The upper and lower limits of rrmDcGbrTokenBucket, namely dcGbrTokenBucketUpperLimit and dcGbrTokenBucketLowerLimit shall always be observed while updating the DC GBR token bucket variable. In addition, in case the dcGbrTokenBucketLowerLimit or dcGbrTokenBucketUpperLimit would change, and the current level of rrmDcGbrTokenBucket is beyond the allowed range, rrmDcGbrTokenBucket shall be clipped accordingly such that it is within the new allowed range.




where,

  * dcGbrPrioritisedBitRate [kbps] represents the short term max burst bit rate for the DC GBR bearer contained in the LCG configured of the UE and is given by 8*MDBVMAC[byte] / NRBTS/NRDRB.delayCriticalGbrDrbParam.ulRanPdb [msec]. Pls refer to 5G_UP_ALG_30958_replaced([11196117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for MDBVMAC calculation.
  * dcGbrTokenBucketUpperLimit shall be set to 8*MDBVMAC of the DC-GBR bearer contained in the LCG. Please refer to Pls refer to 5G_UP_ALG_30958_replaced([11196117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for MDBVMAC calculation.
  * dcGbrTokenBucketLowerLimit shall be set to -(8*MDBVMAC - dcGbrPrioritisedBitRate [kbps] * slotDuration_msec) of the DC-GBR bearer contained in the LCG. Pls refer to 5G_UP_ALG_30958_replaced([11196117](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for MDBVMAC calculation.



NOTE: There is only one DC-GBR bearer supported per LCG and per UE. In future releases, if multiple DC-GBR bearers supported per LCG and per UE, this requirement need to be revisited.

---

#### 3.30.4.3.0-7 Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria Additional overhead considerations for GFBR and MDBV (ID: 11196117)

**3.30.4.3.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11196123`)

Definition of DC-GBR Scheduling Criteria gNB-DU shall calculate the internal values of GFBR (namely GFBRMAC) and MDBV (namely MDBVMAC) to be used for the data monitoring on the DC-GBR bearers on top of the GFBR and MDBV values received for the DC-GBR QoS flows from the AMF as below:

GFBRRLC = GFBR * (100+NRBTS/NRDRB.l2OHFactorUl * 2/3)/100

GFBRMAC = GFBR * (100+NRBTS/NRDRB.l2OHFactorUl)/100

MDBVMAC = MDBV * (100+NRBTS/NRDRB.l2OHFactorUl)/100

where

GFBR is the average guaranteed flow bitrate of the DC-GBR bearer received from core network

MDBV is the maximum burst data volume of the DC-GBR bearer given by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB.delayCriticalGbrDrbParam.ulMdbv, if a valid value is not configured from core network, else, the value from the core network is taken.

---

### 3.30.4.4 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria

#### 3.30.4.4.0-1 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria Overview (ID: 13383081)

**3.30.4.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13377618`)

Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria The GBR scheduling criteria shall be applied for both Critical and non-Critical GBR in TD scheduling.

---

#### 3.30.4.4.0-2 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria GBR TD scheduling criteria calculation (ID: 11196133)

**3.30.4.4.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11196149`)

Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria Calculation of the time domain (TD) scheduling criteria for uplink GBR has the following characteristics:

 Does not apply to 5QI 1/65/66 which is prioritized higher than other GBR bearers. In this requirement GBR refers to non-5QI 1/65/66 GBR bearers.

 The data rate for GBR bearers is monitored via a token bucket. If the GBR data exceeds the token bucket size (i.e. token bucket becomes negative) the GBR bearer will be depriotized below non-GBR bearers. [CB008118-B] This criteria for GBR deprioritization is caused by excessive data but is not applicable for GBR congestion deprioritize handling [End CB008118-B].

 GBR bearers shall be considered in the calculation of the TD criteria only if there is data waiting for initial transmission.

The calculation of the UL GBR TD scheduling criteria shall be as follows:

C UL, i (t) = PF UL, i, GBR (t) * C2 UL, i, m (t)

where

PF UL, i, GBR (t) GBR time domain Proportional Fair term as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_8047_replaced([11196154](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

C2 UL, i, m (t) GBR specific scheduling criteria as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_8051_replaced([11196186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwBQcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 

with

i index of the UE

m index of GBR bearer of UE i

t current slot

---

#### 3.30.4.4.0-3 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria GBR proportional fair calculation (ID: 11196154)

**3.30.4.4.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11196166`)

Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria The uplink GBR proportional fair (PF) metric provides an indication of the GBR UE’s current channel conditions compared to its average channel conditions. Unlike the non-GBR PF metric the GBR PF metric does not measure the UE’s actual data rate. For GBR the data rate is independent of the channel conditions and is dictated by the GFBR value provided by the AMF. The GBR PF metric instead quantifies the UE’s channel conditions and is used to increase the GBR UE’s priority when its channel conditions are better than average and lower the UE’s priority when its channel conditions are worse than average. The GBR PF metric value shall be calculated as follows:

PFUL,i,GBR (t) = rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot (i, t) / rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot (i, t-1)

where

rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot (i, t) current wideband UL data rate of UE i, see 5G_UP_ALG_4856_replaced([11195916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot (i, t-1) average wideband UL data rate of UE i, see 5G_UP_ALG_8049_replaced([11196172](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwApbPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

with

i index of the UE

t current slot

[Before CB008600-B]

Note that the GBR PF value is only calculated for the UE's PCell carrier. GBR traffic is not transmitted over CA SCells.

[CB008600-B]

Notes:

  * This requirement is also applicable for DC-GBR functionality and UL DC-GBR shall use the GBR PF value also as DC-GBR PF. i.e. PF UL, i, DC-GBR (t) = PFUL,i,GBR (t)
  * The GBR/DC-GBR PF value is only calculated for the UE's PCell carrier. Both GBR and DC-GBR traffic are not transmitted over CA SCells.



[End CB008600-B]

---

#### 3.30.4.4.0-4 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria GBR average data rate calculation (ID: 11196172)

**3.30.4.4.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `12557470`)

Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria The GBR PF average wideband throughput in UL shall be calculated as follows:

rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot (i, t) =

(1 – (1 / rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConst) ) * rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot (i, t-1) +

(1 / rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConst) * rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot (i, t)

Where

rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot Current UL wideband throughput, see 5G_UP_ALG_4856_replaced([11195916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConst [Before CB014044-M] Filter constant given by R&D parameter, see 5G_L2_1024_replaced([11182943](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fs9fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [CB014044-M] The filter constant rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConst is defined by  
\- For an FDD cell, it shall be rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConstFdd, see [12552792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_cBJS4iTyEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)  
\- For a TDD FR1 cell, it shall be rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConstTddFr1, see [12555241](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_B0Tn8CW1EfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)  
\- For a TDD FR2 cell, it shall be rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConst, see 5G_L2_1024_replaced([11182943](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fs9fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))   
[End CB014044-M]

with

i index of the UE

t current slot

rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot shall be updated in every slot that the GBR and/or DC-GBR UE is included in the CS1 list.

For initialization of the average data rate filter, rrmAvgDlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot (i, t-1) shall be set equal to rrmCurrentDlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot (i, t). This will result in an initial PF value of 1.

**Notes:**

  * This requirement is also applicable for DC-GBR functionality and UL DC-GBR shall also use the rrmAvgDlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot.
  * The GBR/DC-GBR GBR average data rate is only calculated for the carrier that contains the GBR/DC-GBR bearer, i.e. the Pcell carrier.

---

#### 3.30.4.4.0-5 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria GBR delay metric calculation (ID: 11196186)

**3.30.4.4.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `11196204`)

Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria Calculation of the GBR delay metric for [Before CB008118-A] non-5QI-1 [CB008118-A] non-5QI 1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] GBR bearers shall be done as follows.

C2 UL,i,m = DMV UL,i,m (t) for GBR bearers if the UE is eligible for initial transmission, the LCG serving the GBR bearer has a non-zero buffer size and the GBR rate has not been exceeded, i.e. the token bucket filling level is positive then calculate the GBR bearer’s Delay Metric Value (DMV). See 5G_UP_ALG_5118_replaced([11203660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of the LCG buffer size and 5G_UP_ALG_8055_replaced([11196250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwBQevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of the token bucket.

If enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) C2 UL,i,m = rrmRGWeighti, m Else C2 UL,i,m = 1.

for GBR bearers if the UE is eligible for initial transmission, the LCG serving the GBR bearer has a non-zero buffer size, and the GBR data transmissions have exceeded the GBR data rate, i.e. token bucket filling level is negative (see 5G_UP_ALG_8055_replaced([11196250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwBQevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). In this case the bearer will be put into the deprioritizedGbr traffic request class.

C2 UL,i,m = 0 otherwise, i.e. LCG buffer size (rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes) is zero 

where

DMV UL,i,m (t) Delay Metric Value according to 5G_UP_ALG_8053_replaced([11196209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwBQdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmRGWeighti, m RG weight of the RG to which the GBR bearer m of UE i belongs, as per 5G_UP_ALG_23167_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

with

i index of the UE

m index of GBR bearer of UE i

t current slot

---

#### 3.30.4.4.0-6 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria GBR delay metric value (DMV) calculation (ID: 11196209)

**3.30.4.4.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11196218`)

Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria 11196218: Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria The Delay Metric Value (DMV) is based on a unique linear curve for each GBR 5QI. The slope and the maximum value of the curve are determined by the NIDD parameter delayTarget (DT) for that 5QI. The shorter the DT the steeper the curve and the higher the maximum DMV. All curves increase until the maximum packet delay budget define by 3GPP (i.e. 500ms for 5QI-76) is reached. At that point all curves flatten out and have zero slope for delay times exceeding 500ms. See below diagram for an example set of DMV curves. Each GBR 5QI has a maximum DMV that is calculated as follows: maxDMV5QI = maxDMV * (1 – (confDT5QI – minDT) / maxDT) where maxDMV 200: limit of the DMV that any 5QI may reach when ConfDT5QI > minDT minDT 50: minimum recommended packet delay budget specified by 3GPP 23.501 for non-Delay Critical GBR 5QIs maxDT 500: maximum recommended packet delay budget specified by 3GPP 23.501 confDT5QI min (configured delayTarget for the GBR 5QI, maxDT) The maxDMV5QI value should be calculated during cell start-up and whenever the value of the configured 5QI delayTarget is modified. Note that delayTarget refers to NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB_MAC.delayTarget which is defined for each GBR 5QI profile. Finally, the DMV based on the time that the head of line packet has been in the buffer is calculated as follows: DMV UL,i,m (t) = max ( 1, maxDMV5QI * (tdelay (t) / maxDT) ) for 0 <= tdelay (t) <= maxDT DMV UL,i,m (t) = maxDMV5QI for tdelay (t) > maxDT where i index of the considered UE m index of the LCG containing the GBR bearer tdelay (t) estimated Head of Line delay for the GBR bearer as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_8077_replaced([11196224](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwBQdvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [image-0][image_desc]The image is a line graph titled "Delay Metric Value versus 5QI Configured Delay Target (ConfDT ms)". The x-axis represents "Packet Delay (ms)" and ranges from 0 to 510 in increments of 10. The y-axis represents "Delay Metric Value" and ranges from 0.00 to 250.00 in increments of 50.00. There are six lines plotted on the graph, each representing a different "ConfDT" value: \- ConfDT 50 (blue line) \- ConfDT 100 (orange line) \- ConfDT 150 (gray line) \- ConfDT 200 (yellow line) \- ConfDT 300 (light blue line) \- ConfDT 400 (green line) \- ConfDT 500 (dark blue line) All lines originate from (0,0) and show an increasing trend as the packet delay increases. The lines are ordered from top to bottom as ConfDT 50, ConfDT 100, ConfDT 150, ConfDT 200, ConfDT 300, ConfDT 400, and ConfDT 500. The ConfDT 50 line reaches the highest delay metric value at the end of the x-axis, approximately 200. The ConfDT 500 line reaches the lowest delay metric value, approximately 20.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15336.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

---

#### 3.30.4.4.0-7 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria GBR Packet Head of Line Determination (ID: 11196224)

**3.30.4.4.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `11196243`)

Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria Head of line (HOL) packet delay estimation for [Before CB008118-A] non-5QI-1 [CB008118-A] non-5QI 1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] GBR bearers. HOL packet represents the oldest packet that has arrived in the UE’s transmission buffer. To estimate the HOL packet delay accurately the HOL packet arrival time shall be determined. The HOL packet arrival time is calculated every time one of the following events occur: 1) UE sends a BSR with a non-zero value for an LCG containing GBR bearer(s), 2) UE transmits GBR data on PUSCH, and 3) UE sends an SR. Note that the gNB must determine the slot in which the UE transmitted the initial BSR or PUSCH transmission if HARQ retransmissions were required. 

The following defines the HOL calculations that are required when one of the three events occur:

  1. BSR is received and contains a non-zero value for the LCG which contains the GBR bearer:



bufferSizecurrent, LCG = 8 * BSRLCG

tHOL = t – bufferSizecurrent, LCG /∑j GBRRLC,j )

where

bufferSizecurrent, LCG current buffer size in bits for LCG

BSR LCG data size in bytes reported for LCG

t current time 

tHOL time instance that the HOL packet arrived in the UE transmission buffer

GBRRLC,j GBR at RLC as calculated in 5G_UL_ALG_8059

j index of GBR bearer

NOTE: The BSR size level index for the LCG represents a range of buffer size (BS) values in bytes. In this calculation BSRLCG shall use the upper boundary of the BS Value. The BS Value for the highest BS Index shall be defined as 1.4 times the BS Value of previous Index.

2\. PUSCH grant contains data for GBR bearer(s):

tHOL = tHOL + TBSGBR / ∑j GBRRLC,j

where

TBSGBR estimate of GBR portion of the UL grant. 

tHOL and GBRRLC,j as defined in item 1.

j index of GBR bearer

When the transmitted TB is received at the gNB, the estimate is corrected as follows:

bufferSizeold, LCG = bufferSizecurrent, LCG

bufferSizecurrent, LCG = max (0, bufferSizeold, LCG – BitsRxcurrent, LCG + ∑j GBRRLC,j * tsinceLastBSRorULTx)

tHOL = t - bufferSizecurrent, LCG / ∑j GBRRLC,j

where

tsinceLastBSRorULTx time between the current UL scheduling slot and the UL slot when the most recent BSR or PUSCH transmission was transmitted by the UE.

tHOL and GBRRLC,j as defined in item 1.

NOTE: If the PUSCH transmission and BSR corresponding to GBR bearer(s) becomes available in the same time instance, the BSR is used to estimate the instance of the HOL packet arrival.

3\. SR is received:

If an SR is received and bufferSizecurrent = 0, determine the HOL packet arrival (tHOL)

tHOL = tSR – 0.5 * srPeriodicity 

where

tSR slot that SR was received by gNB

srPeriodicity SR periodicity of the UE that sent the SR

Disregard the SR for HOL delay estimation in the following scenarios:

 If the UE has a [Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1 [CB008118-A] 5QI 1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bearer. In this case the SR is most likely for voice data that is scheduled without the use of the GBR delay metric.

  * If SR is transmitted after a BSR is received by the gNB and before an UL grant was sent to the UE, and bufferSizecurrent > 0, i.e. tHOL is determined based on the BSR information
  * If SR is transmitted after an UL grant was sent to the UE and bufferSizecurrent > 0
  * If SR is transmitted again without an intermediate BSR or PUSCH transmission



NOTE: SRs are triggered by regular BSRs which relate to the logical channel with the highest priority for which new data becomes available for transmission. Given that the priority of the GBR bearers is higher than the priority of the non-GBR bearers and below the priority of SRBs which are characterised by sporadic data transfer it can be assumed that the SRs are triggered primarily by the GBR bearer. 

The resultant HOL packet delay (tdelay) at time tdelta after the slot when a SR, BSR, PUSCH transmission is made is defined as:

If an SR is transmitted after a PUSCH transmission is received and bufferSizecurrent = 0 then packet delay is:

tdelay = tsr + tdelta - tHOL

Else if a BSR or a PUSCH transmission is received then the packet delay is defined as:

tdelay = tBSRorPUSCH + tdelta - tHOL

where

tsr slot that last SR was received by gNB

tBSRorPUSCH slot that last BSR or PUSCH transmission was received by gNB

tHOL time that head of line packet was created at UE. See above for tHOL calculations.

tdelta time (ms) between current slot and the slot that SR, BSR, PUSCH transmission was received by gNB

---

#### 3.30.4.4.0-8 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria GBR data rate monitoring with a token bucket (ID: 11196250)

**3.30.4.4.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11196266`)

Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria gNB shall maintain a token bucket variable Bi,LCG_j for each established logical channel group LCG_j containing one or multi [Before CB008118-A] non-5QI-1 [CB008118-A]non- 5QI 1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] GBR bearer(s)of UE i. Bi,LCG_j shall be initialized to zero when the logical channel(s) (i.e. GBR bearer(s)) is/are established in the LCG and incremented by the product PBRi,LCG_j x 1 ms for each ms that elapses, where PBRi,LCG_j is the prioritized bit rate equal to the sum of GBRMAC (see 5G_UP_ALG_8059_replaced([11196286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwB3g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of the GBR bearer(s) mapped to LCG j.

However, the value of Bi,LCG_j can never exceed the bucket size. If Bi,LCG_j is larger than the bucket size of LCG j, it shall be set to the bucket size. The bucket size of LCG j is equal to PBRi,LCG_j x BSDi,LCG_j where BSD is the maximum of the bucket size duration of GBR bearers mapped in the LCG_j which is specified for each GBR 5QI profile by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB_MAC.schedulBSD. Logically the token bucket variable (Bi,LCG_j) shall be updated for the LCG j every slot after the Bi,LCG_j is initialized. This can instead be done at the beginning of every slot in which the bearer is considered for scheduling:

Bi,LCG_j(Tcurrent) = min ( Bi,LCG_j(Tprevious) + PBRi,LCG_j * diff(t), PBRi,LCG_j * BSDi,LCG_j )

where

diff(t) = (tcurrent – tprevious) represents the time duration since the last update of Bi,LCG_j.

The Bi,LCG_j variable is then updated when an uplink grant[CB012810-D](include a DRB priority PUG)[End CB012810-D] with an allocation for the GBR bearer in LCG_j is sent to the UE. Specifically, the token bucket for LCG_j is updated according to the amount of resources allocated to the GBR bearer:

Bi,LCG_j = Bi,LCG_j – ∑m allocationToGbrBearerm

where

allocationToGbrBearerm number of bits that were allocated to GBR bearer. See 5G_UP_ALG_8064_replaced([11199798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwP5_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

m index of GBR bearer of LCG j of UE i

Bi,LCG_j is then corrected when the PUSCH transmission is received. This is required since the actual multiplexing of data from multiple bearers in the UE is implementation specific and it cannot be ensured apriori how much data from each bearer will be multiplexed in the transport block (TB). The token bucket variable is updated as follows:

Bi,LCG_j = Bi,LCG_j + ∑m (allocationToGbrBearerm –dataReceivedForGbrBearerm )

where

dataReceivedForGbrBearerm number of bits that were received for GBR bearer

m index of GBR bearer of LCG j of UE i

NOTE: Since multi GBR bearers are mapped to a LCG, PBRi,LCG_j and BSDi,LCG_j shall be for multi GBR bearers.

---

#### 3.30.4.4.0-9 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria GBR token bucket lower limit (ID: 11196271)

**3.30.4.4.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11196282`)

Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria [Before CB008963-A]

A lower limit is defined for the token bucket used to monitor uplink GBR bearers. The lower limit shall prevent the token bucket algorithm from continuously decrementing the filling level if the GBR bearer which is assigned to that LCG is scheduled for an extended time duration above the allowed GBR which could happen in an empty cell. Having a lower limit of the token bucket filling level will shorten the time the GBR bearer is not scheduled when the number of UEs in the cell increases and the negative filling level of the token bucket would prevent the scheduling of the GBR bearer.

The time the GBR bearer comes back into the regular scheduling shall be a multiple of the target delay and the GBR value for the GBR bearer in the LCG. The following equation defines the filling level limit for the lower bound of the token bucket.

[CB008963-A]

A lower limit is defined for the token bucket used to monitor uplink GBR bearers. The lower limit shall prevent the token bucket algorithm from continuously decrementing the filling level if the GBR bearer(s) which is/are assigned to that LCG is/are scheduled for an extended time duration above the allowed GBR which could happen in an empty cell. Having a lower limit of the token bucket filling level will shorten the time the GBR bearer(s) is/are not scheduled when the number of UEs in the cell increases and the negative filling level of the token bucket would prevent the scheduling of the GBR bearer(s).

The time the GBR bearer(s) comes/come back into the regular scheduling shall be a multiple of the target delay and the GBR value for the GBR bearer(s) in the LCG. The following equation defines the filling level limit for the lower bound of the token bucket.

[End CB008963-A]

Bi,LCG_j,min = -5 * delayTargeti,LCG_j * GBRMAC,i,LCG_j

where

delayTarget i,LCG_j [Before CB008963-A] delay target of the GBR bearer which is specified for each GBR 5QI by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB_MAC.delayTarget

[CB008963-A] the maximun of delay target of the GBR bearer(s) which is/are specified for each GBR 5QI by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB_MAC.delayTarget[End CB008963-A]

GBR MAC,i,LCG_j [Before CB008963-A] GBRMAC (see 5G_UP_ALG_8059_replaced([11196286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwB3g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of the GBR bearer mapped to the LCG

[CB008963-A] the sum of GBRMAC (see 5G_UP_ALG_8059_replaced([11196286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwB3g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of the GBR bearer(s) mapped to the LCG [End CB008963-A]

with

i index of the UE

j index of the LCG

When the token bucket filling level is updated, it shall be limited to this lower bound:

Bi,LCG_j = max (Bi,LCG_j,min , Bi,LCG_j)

[Before CB008963-A]

NOTE: Since only one GBR bearer is mapped in a LCG in the current release delayTarget i,LCG_j and GBRi,LCG_j shall be specific to one GBR bearer.

[CB008963-A]

NOTE: Since multi GBR bearers are mapped in a LCG, delayTarget i,LCG_j and GBRi,LCG_j shall be for multi GBR bearers. [End CB008963-A]

---

#### 3.30.4.4.0-10 Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria GBR MAC and RLC data rate calculations (ID: 11196286)

**3.30.4.4.0-10.0-2**  (ID: `11196297`)

Definition of GBR TD Scheduling Criteria GBRMAC and GBRRLC values for non-RoHC compressed GBR bearers need to be calculated to support the GBR token bucket filling rate. For the calculation of GBRRLC and GBRMAC the gNB shall apply a minimum value to the GFBR value received from the AMF:

GBRinternal,xL = max (GFBRxL , rdMinGfbr), xL = [DL, UL]

where 

rdMinGfbr R&D parameter (default = 5 kbps);

GFBRxL GFBR value received from AMF or source cell (in case of handover)

GBRRLC and GBRMAC (UL and DL) shall be calculated as:

GBRRLC,xL = GBRinternal,xL * (100 + l2OHFactorxL * 2/3) / 100 xL = [DL, UL]

GBRMAC,xL = GBRinternal,xL * (100 + l2OHFactorxL) / 100 xL = [DL, UL]

where

l2OHFactorDL and l2OHFactorUL refer respectively to NIDD parameters NRBTS/NRDRB.l2OHFactorDL and NRBTS/NRDRB.l2OHFactorUL of each GBR 5QI profile

NOTE: [Before CB008118-A]5QI-1 [CB008118-A] 5QI-1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] is the only GBR bearer that uses RoHC compression. However GBR rate monitoring is not applied to [Before CB008118-A]5QI-1[CB008118-A] 5QI-1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bearers and therefore GBRMAC and GBRRLC values are not required for RoHC compressed GBR bearers.

---

### 3.30.4.5 Definition of NBR TD Scheduling Criteria

#### 3.30.4.5.0-1 Definition of NBR TD Scheduling Criteria NBR TD scheduling criteria (ID: 11196307)

**3.30.4.5.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12480352`)

Definition of NBR TD Scheduling Criteria **[CB013709-B] If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 0, HwVariant is not ABIL, and the real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G_UP_ALG_31301_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value provided by the gNB-DU overload control handler, is greater than or equal to the value set for **activation threshold of rdNominalBitrateOlcUlPerCent (See 5G_UP_ALG_31841_replaced([11216990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxcMwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then, gNB-DU shall skip the following actions for each NBR bearer i of UE j, since all the NBR bearers are deprioritised in bulk to normal nonGBR category. When the real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G_UP_ALG_31301_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value provided by the gNB-DU overload control handler, is below the value set for **deactivation threshold of rdNominalBitrateOlcUlPerCent (See 5G_UP_ALG_31841_replaced([11216990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxcMwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then gNB-DU shall resume the below steps for each NBR bearer i of UE j. **[End CB013709-B]**_*_** *

UL scheduler shall monitor the data rate of each of LCG i configured with the NBR bearer(s) for a given UE j via a token bucket , namely rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j). The details are described in 5G_UP_ALG_22262_replaced([11196329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwB3jPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The resulting rrmNbrTokenBucket is eventually used for the categorization of UEs within drbTraffic class as explained in 5G_UP_ALG_4794_replaced([11194709](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv24YfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

TD sub-prioritization of UEs in drbTraffic class shall follow below criteria:

  * The sub-prioritization metric for both categories of drbTraffic class (namely NBR category, large buffer non-GBR category **and normal non-GBR category, see 5G_UP_ALG_4794_replaced([11194709](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv24YfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be calculated as per 5G_UP_ALG_4851_replaced([11195815](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with a difference in the calculation of ueSchedWeightFactor as described in 5G_UP_ALG_22269_replaced([11195869](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0OfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**
  * Within drbTraffic class, UEs belonging to NBR category shall always be prioritized over UEs belonging to large buffer non-GBR category, and UEs belonging to large buffer non-GBR category shall always be prioritized over UEs belonging to the normal Non-GBR category independent of their calculated sub-prioritization metric in the determination of candidate set 2 (see 5G_UP_ALG_4848_replaced([11195784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-NLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).



The calculated sub-prioritization metric shall only be used to prioritize the UEs within the given category of the drbTraffic class.

---

#### 3.30.4.5.0-2 Definition of NBR TD Scheduling Criteria Maintenance of NBR Token Bucket (ID: 11196329)

**3.30.4.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11196334`)

Definition of NBR TD Scheduling Criteria NBR Rate Monitoring with Token Bucket

---

**3.30.4.5.0-2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11196340`)

Definition of NBR TD Scheduling Criteria The TD scheduler shall maintain a token bucket variable rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j) [bits] for each LCG i contianing NBR configured non-GBR bearer(s) for UE j. It is updated as below

  * when first UL NBR bearer (NRBTS/NRDRB_MAC.nbrUl is non-zero) is established within LCG i, rrmNbrTokenBucket shall be initialized to zero.

  * Ideally, after rrmNbrTokenBucket is initialized, rrmNbrTokenBucket shall be incremented every slot by the product of rrmNbrPbr_kbps(i,j) x slotDuration_msec and reduced whenever the data is successfully received for the UE. However, this can be optimized by updating rrmNbrTokenBucket only when one of the conditions below are satisfied:

  * At the beginning of every slot, the UE configured with NBR bearer(s) is being considered for scheduling (i.e. added to CS1 list)
  * Whenever the UL transmission is correctly received in L2PS for the bearers (NBR and nonNBR) established in LCG i



rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j,t) = rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j,t-1) + rrmNbrPbr_kbps(i,j) * elapsedTime_msec - dataReceivedForLcg_bits(i,j,t)

  * The upper and lower limits of rrmNbrTokenBucket, namely nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit and nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit shall always be observed while updating the NBR token bucket variable. In addition, whenever the nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit or nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit is changed, and the current level of rrmNbrTokenBucket is beyond the allowed range, rrmNbrTokenBucket shall be clipped accordingly such that it is within the new allowed range.



where,

  * rrmNbrPbr_kbps(i,j) is the sum over the prioritised bit rate [kbps] of configured NBR bearers within LCG i of UE j
  * The prioritised bit rate of each NBR bearer in kbps shall be derived from NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB_MAC.nbrUl [Mbps]. Please note that this value can change dynamically as the number of NBR bearers established within LCG may change over time.
  * nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit is described in 5G_UP_ALG_22265_replaced([11196344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwB3j_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit is described in 5G_UP_ALG_22267_replaced([11196354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCekPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * elapsedTime_msec represents the time elapsed in msec since the last update of rrmNbrTokenBucket.
  * dataReceivedForLcg_bits is the total data volume in bits correctly received for all bearers(NBR and nonNBR) established within LCG i for UE j in slot t.

---

**3.30.4.5.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11196344`)

Definition of NBR TD Scheduling Criteria NBR Token Bucket Upper Limit

---

**3.30.4.5.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11196350`)

Definition of NBR TD Scheduling Criteria For a given LCG i contianing NBR bearer of UE j, the upper limit of the NBR bucket variable, namely nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit [bits], is given by

nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit = rrmNbrPbr_kbps(i,j) * schedulBSD_msec(i)

where,

rrmNbrPbr_kbps(i,j) is the sum over the prioritised bit rate [kbps] of configured NBR bearers within LCG i of UE j. The prioritised bit rate of each NBR bearer in kbps shall be derived from NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB_MAC.nbrUl [Mbps].

schedulBSD_msec is the max of bucket size duration of all NBR bearers configured within LCG i. The bucket size duration of each DRB bearer is specified within the DRB profile given by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB_MAC.schedulBSD [msec]

Please note that nbrTokenBucketUpperLimit can change dynamically as the number of NBR bearers established within LCG may change over time.

---

**3.30.4.5.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11196354`)

Definition of NBR TD Scheduling Criteria NBR Token Bucket Lower Limit

---

**3.30.4.5.0-2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11196358`)

Definition of NBR TD Scheduling Criteria For a given LCG i contianing NBR bearer of UE j, the lower limit of the NBR bucket variable, namely nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit [bits], is given by

nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit = - rrmNbrPbr_kbps(i,j) * rdUlNbrTokenBucketLowerLimit

where,

rrmNbrPbr_kbps(i,j) is the sum over the prioritised bit rate [kbps] of configured NBR bearers within LCG i of UE j. The prioritised bit rate of each NBR bearer in kbps shall be derived from NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRDRB_MAC.nbrUl [Mbps].

rdUlNbrTokenBucketLowerLimit is an R&D parameter defined in 5G_UP_ALG_22406_replaced([11209235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwysgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Please note that nbrTokenBucketLowerLimit can change dynamically as the number of NBR bearers established within LCG may change over time.

---

### 3.30.4.6 Definition of Large Buffer TD Scheduling Criteria

#### 3.30.4.6.0-1 Definition of Large Buffer TD Scheduling Criteria (ID: 13337176)

**3.30.4.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12687284`)

Definition of Large Buffer TD Scheduling Criteria The TD sub-prioritization metric for large buffer UEs shall follow the criteria below:

  * If the number of large buffer candidate UEs in CS1, as calculated in [11195013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_3ZLERvvPEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (maxNumUeToAddLargeBuffer), exceeds the configured rdMaxNumLargeBufferUesPerSlotUl(see [11209808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_EAIIQPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), a round-robin mechanism shall be used to select rdMaxNumLargeBufferUesPerSlotUl UEs as large buffer UEs. These selected UEs shall be assigned the large buffer sub-priority as their TD sub-prioritization metric. The remaining unselected large buffer candidate UEs from the CS1 list shall be treated as normal non-GBR UEs.
  * Within the drbTraffic class:
  * UEs in the NBR category, if present, shall always be prioritized over UEs in the large buffer non-GBR category.
  * UEs in the large buffer non-GBR category shall always be prioritized over UEs in the normal non-GBR category, regardless of their calculated sub-prioritization metrics when determining candidate set 2 (see [11195784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-NLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.30.4.7 X-Pol Beam selection taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account

#### 3.30.4.7.0-1 X-Pol Beam selection taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account Beam Selection for Analog Beamforming (ID: 11196368)

**3.30.4.7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11196401`)

X-Pol Beam selection taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account 11196401: X-Pol Beam selection taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account [CB012734-E] If NRBTS/NRCELLGRP.actHighNumberOfUes = TRUE and rdActEnhBeamSelection = TRUE, beam selection is not required in TD scheduler as it is already performed in Pre-scheduler as described in 5G_UP_ALG_27614_replaced([11194972](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4tl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB012734-E]

For analog beamforming, the TD scheduler determines the X-Pol beam to be used in the next scheduling NR slots as follows:

For each UE in CS1 according to 5G_UP_ALG_4825_replaced([11195013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4tnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), L2-PS shall determine its highest priority resource request over all its serving cells. The priority of a resource request is defined by the priority of its resource request class (5G_UP_ALG_10506_replaced([11194320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and by a sub-priority which is the relative priority of a resource request within its resource request class (5G_UP_ALG_4773_replaced([11194371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If the class of the highest priority resource request among all the candidates is other than drbTraffic, then L2-PS selects the Beam of the UE with highest priority resource request among all the candidates. The index of this beam is denoted with kselectedBeam.

If the class of the highest priority resource request among all the candidates is drbTraffic, then the Beam is chosen as a result of the below algorithm:

Step 2.1:

For each UE i of CS1 belonging to the drbTraffic request class and each carrier j configured to the UE, L2-PS shall calculate the PF metric (rrmUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i, j]) as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_4851_replaced([11195815](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 2.2:

The TD scheduler shall calculate for each beam k in the set of beams of the L2 PS instance and each carrier j in the set of all subcells the maximum of the metrics calculated in step 2.1 among all the UEs in the drbTraffic request class assigned to the same beam k. The resulting metrics are denoted by

rrmUlUeSchedMetricPerBeamPerCarrier[k,j] = [image-0][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15366.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]{ rrmUlUeSchedMetricPerCarrier[i,j]}  


k = beam index

CS1(k, drbTraffic,j) = set of UEs assigned to beam k and member of the drbTraffic request class for carrier j

i = UE index

j = carrier index

Step 2.3:
