The TD scheduler shall determine a metric per beam k by the following operation:

rrmUlUeSumSchedMetricPerBeam[k] = [image-1][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15366.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]{ rrmUlUeSchedMetricPerBeamPerCarrier[k,j] }

k = beam index

j = carrier index, j = 1 … Nc

Nc is the number of carriers handled by the L2 PS instance

Step 2.4:   


The X-Pol beam to be used in the next UL scheduling NR slot is equal to the X-Pol beam that has the highest value of the metrics calculated in step 2.3.

kselectedBeam = [image-2][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15366.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]{ rrmUlUeSumSchedMetricPerBeam[k] }

kselectedBeam is the beam index of the beam that is used in the next UL scheduling slot.

k = beam index

B = set of beams handled by the L2 PS instance

---

#### 3.30.4.8 UE selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account

##### 3.30.4.8.0-1 UE selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account UL CS1 list truncation considering the PDCCH capacity (ID: 13347070)

**3.30.4.8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13347072`)

UE selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account When NRCELL.actPdcchLeastBlockingAlg is enabled and rdActCs1ListTruncationAndQuotaAllocation is True, gNB-DU shall truncate the n-GBR UEs prioritized in the TD metric order in the CS1 list to a smaller list based on the available CCEs to UL as mentioned in [13346612: CS1 list truncation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_VjDU4uVfEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

Note: rdActCs1ListTruncationAndQuotaAllocation enables or disables the CS1 list truncation algorithm and it is and R&D parameter ([13347077: rdActCs1ListTruncationAndQuotaAllocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6q93UeWOEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

---

##### 3.30.4.8.0-2 UE selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account Composing CS2 list (ID: 11196411)

**3.30.4.8.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11196422`)

UE selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account On carrier basis, L2-PS shall select the set of UE candidates to be scheduled on the carrier (CS2 List). The FD scheduler shall allocate resources on that carrier for selected UEs out of those in CS2 list. The selection is done as in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For analog beamforming, the input is the set of candidate UEs located in the selected Beam kselectedBeam (calculated in 5G_UP_ALG_22375_replaced([11196368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCemfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For digital beamforming, the input is the CS1 list.

More in detail: 

  * For analog beamforming, the TD scheduler determines all the UEs assigned to the beam kbestBeam. The set is denoted by rrmUlCandidateSet1Selected.

  * For digital beamforming, the set rrmUlCandidateSet1Selected is equal to the set of UEs from the CS1 list.

  * For each UE i in rrmUlCandidateSet1Selected, if it has at least a resource request which can be scheduled on carrier j:




1\. The TD scheduler shall determine the highest priority resource request of all UEs in rrmUlCandidateSet1Selected, according to 5G_UP_ALG_10506_replaced([11194320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_4773_replaced([11194371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

2\. The TD scheduler shall select the UE i with highest priority and shall assign the UE i to the CS2. If several UEs have the same priority, one out of the UEs with same priority shall be selected randomly.

3\. If the priority of UE i refers to an HARQ retransmission request priority class, the UE i shall be assigned to the carrier j where the initial transmission is scheduled. [CB012149-AB] The HARQ retransmission request priority class is skipped if condition in 5G_UP_ALG_25635_replaced([11197468](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is met, and the TD scheduler shall check next resource request classes if the UE i is applicable. [End CB012149-AB]

4\. The TD scheduler shall update rrmUlCandidateSet1Selected by removal the priority resource request corresponding to carrier j of all UEs in rrmUlCandidateSet1Selected and rrmUlCandidateSet1Selected shall be updated for the UEs with resource requests on other carriers.

5\. The assignment procedure shall be repeated at step 1. until all carriers are evaluated or rrmUlCandidateSet1Selected is exhaust.

  * If the CS2 lists of the carriers are not empty, the TD scheduler sends the CS2 lists to the FD scheduler for the resource allocation.

---

##### 3.30.4.8.0-3 UE selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 for A-CSI scheduling with different k2 timing. (ID: 11196426)

**3.30.4.8.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `12555674`)

UE selection and subcell / carrier assignment taking the prioritization and sub-prioritization concept of resource requests into account If the aperiodic CSI is activated at cell level (actAperCsiCell set in the cell setup message)[CB014093-C-UPD001][CB014093-C]by NRCELL**.actAperCsi = true**[End CB014093-C][End CB014093-C-UPD001], L2-PS evaluates the A-CSI scheduling timing and trigger the corresponding actions as follows:

If for the current slot N, k2aCsi is not defined in the slot’s context (see 5G_UP_ALG_2500_replaced([11116648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp5aX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

  * execution of the current requirement is skipped and 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is executed.



Else:

  * if the slot is marked as eligible for A-CSI scheduling, PS shall understand that A-CSI multiplexed with PUSCH data is scheduled with PUSCH at PUSCH timing N + k2aCsi, where k2aCsi > k2 (see 5G_UP_ALG_3503_replaced([11159340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).



If for the current slot N, k2aCsi is defined in the slot’s context (see 5G_UP_ALG_2500_replaced([11116648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp5aX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS shall evaluate the list of the UE eligible for A-CSI triggering (see 5G_UP_ALG_3512_replaced([11159392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktJiofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- L2-PS shall send to FD scheduler the list of the UEs eligible for A-CSI scheduling (i.e. those satisfying the conditions in 5G_UP_ALG_3506_replaced([11159352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7mPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at PUSCH time N + k2aCsi (note that for these UEs, FD scheduler shall be executed with PUSCH time N + k2aCsi; though, PDCCH scheduling shall still run for the current PDCCH)

  * All the conditions requested in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for scheduling with N+k2 timing are also requested when PUSCH timing is N + k2aCsi, with following restrictions:



o The scheduler shall select within the UEs with a scheduling priority class lower than or equal to _prioUlDrbTraffic_

Ul * rdMaxNumACsiUEsRatio)~~,~~_, where:_

 rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl has the same meaning as in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

  _rdMaxNumACsiUEsRatio shall be defined as R &D parameter and updated per release, with default value = _0.5, range 0.1 .. 0.9, step 0.05 (5G_L2_3753_replaced([11187496](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28BRbfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

o The following PUSCH mapping shall be considered as input for FD scheduler:

 Considering the PUSCH subareas in 5G_UP_ALG_4879_replaced([11196950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGwCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the following shall apply in addition to current specifications:

 NBWP0 PRBs corresponding to Initial BWP is always reserved.

 If a CFRA Msg3 is ongoing, first NBWP0 PRB in the dedicated BWP are reserved

 In a PUSCH subarea, A-CSI users are scheduled in contiguous PUSCH area from the lowest PRB index available to avoid PUSCH fragmentation

 Maximum maxAcsiAllocationWithK2AcsiTiming = MIN (rdMaxAnticipatedACsiSchedBw (see 5G_UP_ALG_33207_replaced([11209716](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw1I0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) converted in PRB, uplinkBwp.bandwidth - maxPendingReTxAllocation) PRB allocation shall be considered for cumulated A-CSI scheduling (for all the UEs) when scheduling is performed with k2aCsi timing

  * rdMaxAnticipatedACsiSchedBw (see 5G_UP_ALG_33207_replaced([11209716](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw1I0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be defined during the implementation phase with default value 1/4 (applicable to PUSCH schedulable area of the UL dedicated BWP)
  * maxPendingReTxAllocation represents the maximum reTx allocation a UE in current CS2 list has.
  * uplinkBwp.bandwidth represent the UL BWP size of the dedicated UL BWP as configured in the cell setup request message.



Note: when the product shall allow multiple dedicated BWP per UE or per L2-PS instance, this condition shall be revisited.

 PUSCH allocation shall not overlap the CG PRB pool area (see 5G_UP_ALG_14663_replaced([11172788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgsPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * Note that the above conditions only apply when PUSCH is scheduled with N+k2aCsi timing



For the UEs not sent to FD scheduler for PUSCH time N + k2aCsi or for the UEs not eligible for A-CSI tiggering, L2-PS shall continue with the scheduling decision for the PUSCH time N+k2, as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 3.30.4.8.1 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

##### 3.30.4.8.1-1 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

###### 3.30.4.8.1-1.0-6 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 The remaining available PUSCH PRBs shall be initialized as the number of PRBs in the BW as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_1795_replaced( 11110609 ). PUSCH subareas (see 5G_UP_ALG_4879_replaced( 11196950 )) are initialized after adding the first UE/RNTI to the to-be-scheduled list. If it belongs to the request class "contentResGrant", PUSCH subareas shall be re-initialized if a later UE that is added to the list belongs to the request class "harqRetransRach" (msg3 reTx) and if the retransmission is expected in the initial uplink bandwidth part area (i.e. when UE's active UL bandwidth part is the initial uplink bandwidth part ). In FDD cell shared with LTE (DSS) [CB007372-AA] when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-AA], calculate the initial available resources as the FD resources in the PRB range indicated by the UL SC Allocation Info IE allocated to NR by CRM within the UL SUBCARRIER RESOURCE ALLOCATION message according and 5G_UP_ALG_5180_replaced( 11172453 ). Note, this also applies if long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch'). [CB007372-AA] For a FDD cell shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true the available PUSCH resources are in NR dedicated area and more PRBs may be allocated in the LTE shared area (see 12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS , 11172453: Handling of UL SUBCARRIER RESOURCE ALLOCATION ). Note, for asymmetric DSS only long PUCCH may be configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch'). [End CB007372-AA] If PRB blanking function is activated, the remaining resource shall be initialized by the available PUSCH PRBs(number of PRBs in BW- nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL-nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeUL), which remained for the UL after PRB blanking. If specific BWP size function is activated (NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true), the remaining available PUSCH resource shall be initialized as rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4976_replaced( 11199501 )) If the long PUCCH is selected (IE pucchModeSelect in PsCell_CellSetupReq is set to longPucch), the remaining resources shall be initialized by if slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )) or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )), availableUlPrb ( defined below) If nrLteDssMode is &gt; 2 then availableUlPrb = min(availableUlPrb, rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp( 11199486 ) ) else rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 11199486 )) end minus the number of PRBs not overlapping with dedicated PUCCH PRBs and required for PUCCH (if any) carrying A/N of the common PUCCH (Msg4/SRB0,....) (5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 11199486 ))). [CB007372-AA] Note: it is also applicable for asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) [End CB007372-AA] minus the size of the PRACH, if any, including guard PRBs (details in 5G_UP_ALG_4950_replaced( 11199072 )). Note: for FDD, any blanked PRBs are already considered by rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp. Note when FR1 TDD and FDD cell has Dynamic UL resources allocation is active (NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true' ), the remaining resources shall be initialized at every UL slot. availableUlPrb is determined by the total number of usable PRB (numAllocCellPrb) granted to the cell every bbPoolEvalPeriod (refer 5G_UP_ALG_6781_replaced( 11211947 )) as per (5G_UP_ALG_6500_replaced( 11212285 )), and computed as If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE If n &gt; 1, the number of BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced( 11110833 )), for the jth BWP (5G_UP_ALG_1813_replaced( 11110813 )), where j ϵ {1,2.. n} availableUlPrb(i,j) =min{max{0, numAllocCellPrb - numPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i)}, rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i,j)}. Else availableUlPrb(i) =min{max{0, numAllocCellPrb - numPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i)}, rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i)} End where: available PRB for PUSCH is computed for each UL slot i excluding PRB needed for dedicated PUCCH. numPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i): corresponds to the amount of PRB required for PUCCH in UL slot i excluding the PUCCH PRBs (if any) to be used for carrying A/N of the common PUCCH (Msg4/SRB0,....), if PRBs for A/N of the common PUCCH (Msg4/SRB0,....) are not overlapping with the dedicated PUCCH PRBs. [Before CB013152-A-UPD004] when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true' [CB013152-A] in TDD or when NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt is 'true' in FDD [END CB013152-A] , updated value of rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) in slot i (if any) shall be used, otherwise  rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) value remains constant (5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 11199486 )). [CB013152-A-UPD004] when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true', updated value of rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) in slot i (if any) shall be used, otherwise  rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) value is constant (5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 1119 9486 )). [END CB013152-A-UPD004] * * Else If nrLteDssMode is &gt; 2 [CB007372-AA] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-AA] // FDD cell is shared with [CB007372-AA] symmetric [End CB007372-AA] LTE (DSS) availableUlPrb= max{0, numAllocCellPrb - PUCCH PRBs allocated by CRM ( 11172453 ) } [CB007372-AA] else if NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true // asymmetric DSS cell availableUlPrb= max{0, numAllocCellPrb -(uplinkBwp.bandwidth of largest UL BWP  [CB007372-D] - nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL-nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeUL [End CB007372-D] - rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp)} [End CB007372-AA] else //non-DSS cell availableUlPrb= max{0, numAllocCellPrb -(uplinkBwp.bandwidth of largest UL BWP - nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL-nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeUL - rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp)} where: uplinkBwp.bandwidth of largest UL BWP (PsCell_CellSetupReq) and numAllocCellPrb corresponds to the UL PRB grant from BBRM. If PRACH multiplexing is enabled (NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexing is 'TRUE'), in UL slots which have PRACH configuration, the remaining resource shall be initialized by the available PUSCH PRBs which are not used for PRACH (see 5G_UP_ALG_4950_replaced( 11199072 ) about PRBs for PRACH). Case 1: When NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexing is 'TRUE' or long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') and (NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexingLevel = 0), for PRACH long or short preamble format, according to 5GMax simulation, guard PRBS shall be reserved above and below PRACH signal to avoid scheduling PUSCH signals in these guard PRBs. Above PRACH signal, 2 PRBs shall be reserved. Below PRACH signal, 2 PRBs shall be reserved as well. There is no need to have any reserved guard PRB to an adjacent PUCCH of format 1 or 3 (Long PUCCH). Case 2: When long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') and NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexingLevel = 1, for PRACH long or short preamble format, due to FDAGC algorithm, no guard PRBs shall be reserved above and below PRACH signal. (ID: 11196913)

##### 3.30.4.8.1-2 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

###### 3.30.4.8.1-2.0-4 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 The parameter for the definition of the available PUSCH PRBs shall be initialized as the number of PRBs in the BW as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_1795_replaced( 11110609 ). PUSCH subareas (see 5G_UP_ALG_4879_replaced( 11196950 )) are initialized after adding the first UE/RNTI to the to-be-scheduled list. If it belongs to the request class "contentResGrant", PUSCH subareas shall be re-initialized if a later UE that is added to the list belongs to the request class "harqRetransRach" (msg3 reTx) and if the retransmission is expected in the initial uplink bandwidth part area (i.e. when UE's active UL bandwidth part is the initial uplink bandwidth part ). In FDD cell shared with LTE (DSS), calculate the initial available resources as the FD resources in the PRB range indicated by the UL SC Allocation Info IE allocated to NR by CRM within the UL SUBCARRIER RESOURCE ALLOCATION message according to 5G_UP_ALG_5180_replaced( 11172453 ). Note, this also applies if long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch'). [CB007372-AA] For a FDD cell shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true the available PUSCH resources are in NR dedicated area and more PRBs may be allocated in the LTE shared area (see 12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS , 11172453: Handling of UL SUBCARRIER RESOURCE ALLOCATION )  [End CB007372-AA] If PRB blanking function is activated, the remaining resource shall be initialized by the available PUSCH PRBs (number of PRBs in BW - nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL - nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeUL), which remained for the UL after PRB blanking. If specific BWP size function is activated (NRCELL.actSpecificBwpSize = true), the remaining available PUSCH resource shall be initialized as rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4976_replaced( 11199501 )). If the long PUCCH is selected (IE pucchModeSelect in PsCell_CellSetupReq is set to longPucch), the remaining resources shall be initialized by the following rules: 1. availableUlPrb, if any of the following parameters is set to TRUE: a. slowPrbPoolingAllowed (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )), b. slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )) c. slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )) availableUlPrb system calculated parameter corresponds to the PUSCH RB processing resources and is only relevant if pooling is activated. 2. rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 11199486 )): a. This system calculated parameter corresponds to the physical PUSCH PRBs and it is relevant if pooling is deactivated (i.e. all parameters mentioned in above step “1)” are set to FALSE). b. The number of PRBs of the common PUCCH (Msg4/SRB0,..) not overlapping with dedicated PUCCH PRBs (5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 11199486 ))) shall be subtracted from rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp In the following we describe the computing of availableUlPrb, the Radio Block resources allocable to PUSCH defined under above item 1. When considering availableUlPrb, in certain cases, as described further below a further computation is needed, to keep in consideration the allocations needed for PRACH. Note: 1) For FDD, any blanked PRBs are already considered by rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp. 2) When a FR1 TDD or FDD cell has Dynamic UL resources allocation active, (NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true'), the computation of the remaining PUSCH resources shall be initialized and repeated at every UL slot. If slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )) or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )), availableUlPrb is determined by the total number of usable PRB (numAllocCellPrb) granted to the cell every bbPoolEvalPeriod (refer 5G_UP_ALG_6781_replaced( 11211947 )) as per (5G_UP_ALG_6500_replaced( 11212285 )), and computed as If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE If n &gt; 1, the number of BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced( 11110833 )), for the jth BWP (5G_UP_ALG_1813_replaced( 11110813 )), where j ϵ {1,2.. n}  availableUlPrb(i,j) =min{max{0, numAllocCellPrb - totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i)}, rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i,j)}. Else  availableUlPrb(i) =min{max{0, numAllocCellPrb - totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i)}, rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i)} End where:  available PRB for PUSCH is computed for each UL slot i excluding PRB needed for dedicated PUCCH. available PRB for PUSCH is computed for each UL slot i by excluding PRB allocated to PUCCH.  totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i): this parameter is calculated in the 5G_UP_ALG_31229_replaced( 11196938 ) ”,  when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true', updated value of rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) in slot i (if any) shall be used, otherwise  rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) value remains is constant (5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 11199486 )). Else // (slowPrbPoolingAllowed = TRUE) If nrLteDssMode is &gt; 2 [CB007372-AA] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-AA] // FDD cell is shared with [CB007372-AA] symmetric [End CB007372-AA] LTE (DSS) availableUlPrb= max{0, numAllocCellPrb - PUCCH PRBs allocated by CRM ( 11172453 ) } [CB007372-AA] else if NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true // asymmetric DSS cell availableUlPrb= max{0, numAllocCellPrb -(uplinkBwp.bandwidth of largest UL BWP  [CB007372-D] - nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL-nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeUL [End CB007372-D] - rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp)} [End CB007372-AA] else //non-DSS cell availableUlPrb= max{0, numAllocCellPrb -(uplinkBwp.bandwidth of largest UL BWP - nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL-nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeUL - rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp)} End End End where: uplinkBwp.bandwidth of largest UL BWP (PsCell_CellSetupReq) and numAllocCellPrb corresponds to the UL PRB grant from BBRM. If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )), availableUlPrb is determined by the calculating the total number of usable PRBs from the number of layer PRBs granted to the cell every bbPoolEvalPeriod (refer 5G_UP_ALG_6781_replaced( 11211947 )) as per 5G_UP_ALG_25712_replaced( 11214266 ). The available number of usable PRBs " rrmMaxNumUsablePrb" is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_26582_replaced( 11214591 ). availableUlPrb is then computed as If n &gt; 1, the number of BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced( 11110833 )), for the jth BWP (5G_UP_ALG_1813_replaced( 11110813 )), where j ϵ {1,2.. n}  availableUlPrb(i,j) =min{max{0, min{(ulBandwidth - totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i) - numPrbForPrach(i)), (rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i,j) - numPrbForPrach(i) - numNonOverlappingCommonPucchPrb(i))}}, rrmMaxNumUsablePrb} Else  availableUlPrb(i) =min{max{0, min{(ulBandwidth - totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i) - numPrbForPrach(i)), (rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) - numPrbForPrach(i) - numNonOverlappingCommonPucchPrb(i))}}, rrmMaxNumUsablePrb} End End where:  available PRB for PUSCH is computed for each UL slot i by excluding PRB allocated to PUCCH.  totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i): this parameter is calculated in the 5G_UP_ALG_31229_replaced( 11196938 ) ”,  numPrbForPrach(i): If PRACH multiplexing is enabled (NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexing is 'TRUE' or long PUCCH is configured i.e. NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch'), then, in UL slots which have PRACH configuration the value shall be set to the number of PRBs used for PRACH, including any guard PRBs (refer 5G_UP_ALG_4950_replaced( 11199072 )), else 0. Also consider the cases regarding guard PRBs mentioned at the end of this requirement. Note: With Long PUCCH configured, PRACH multiplexing is always enabled.  numNonOverlappingCommonPucchPrb(i): In case the slot has some PRBs used as common PUCCH resources (e.g. for HARQ A/N of msg4, SRB0 etc.), the number of PRBs of the common PUCCH (Msg4/SRB0,..) not overlapping with dedicated PUCCH PRBs (5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 11199486 ))) else 0. [CB007372-AA] Note: it is also applicable for asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) [End CB007372-AA]  when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true', updated value of rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) in slot i (if any) shall be used, otherwise rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) value remains is constant (5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 11199486 )). If PRACH multiplexing is enabled (NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexing is 'TRUE') and pooling is disabled i.e. (slowPrbPoolingAllowed is FALSE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )) and slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )) is FALSE and slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed is FALSE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 ))), in UL slots which have PRACH configuration, the remaining resource shall be initialized by the available PUSCH PRBs which are not used for PRACH (see 5G_UP_ALG_4950_replaced( 11199072 ) about PRBs for PRACH). If slowPrbPoolingAllowed is TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )) or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )) is TRUE, in UL slots which have PRACH configuration, the remaining resource shall be initialized by the available PUSCH PRBs which are not used for PRACH (see 5G_UP_ALG_4950_replaced( 11199072 ) about PRBs for PRACH). Note : For eCPRI 7-2e based layer PRB Pooling, the PRACH PRBs are considered already in the availableUlPrb parameter above. Case 1: When NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexing is 'TRUE' or long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') and (NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexingLevel = 0), for PRACH long or short preamble format, according to 5GMax simulation, guard PRBS shall be reserved above and below PRACH signal to avoid scheduling PUSCH signals in these guard PRBs. Above PRACH signal, 2 PRBs shall be reserved. Below PRACH signal, 2 PRBs shall be reserved as well. There is no need to have any reserved guard PRB to an adjacent PUCCH of format 1 or 3 (Long PUCCH). The guard PRBs shall also be considered and added when determining numPrbForPrach(i) mentioned above. Case 2: When long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') and NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexingLevel = 1, for PRACH long or short preamble format, due to FDAGC algorithm, no guard PRBs shall be reserved above and below PRACH signal. END of requirement. (ID: 12637893)

##### 3.30.4.8.1-3 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

###### 3.30.4.8.1-3.0-1 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 The here defined correction/update of the parameter totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch is only for the case that pooling is activated and the RBs are restricted. Initialize totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch: by the amount of RB needed for dedicated PUCCH in slot I (note: the computation is based on the number of RB effectively allocated by the UEs in the slot). The computation of the RB allocated to PUCCH is done only once when the PUCCH is statically allocated and recalculated every time common PUCCH RB are added to the slot, while it must be recalculated at every slot when dynamic PUCCH option (NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true') has been activated (independently from common PUCCH presence in the slot). When the dynamic PUCCH option is active (NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true'), part of the resources of the dedicated PUCCH is allocated only on need purpose (so called dynamicPucch), while another part is still defined in statical manner (so called staticPUCCH), when the dynamic option is not active then only staticPUCCH exists for the dedicated PUCCH. Together with the dedicated PUCCH, the common PUCCH need to be considered too in our calculation. We may identify the following cases, (common PUCCH is as well dynamic because it is added in a slot only on purpose): 1. The common PUCCH fully overlap the static dedicated PUCCH area: a. in this case no RB belonging to the common PUCCH shall be added to totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i): no further RB shall be added to totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i) to those already considered for the dedicated staticPUCCH. b. Same statement is valid for several common PUCCH independent of the number of RBs allocated to the commonPucch itself (the overlapping is the only relevant aspect in such a case). 2. The common PUCCH partially overlap the static dedicated PUCCH area: a. in this case only the RB belonging to the common PUCCH non overlapping with the dedicated staticPUCCH area shall be added to totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i). b. Depending on the number of common PUCCHs and the assignment to RBs, the number of RBs may be 1 RB or more RBs. The maximum number of RBs depends on the common PUCCH configuration as defined bz 3GPP 38.213 chapter 9.2.1. 3. The common PUCCH is outside the static dedicated PUCCH area: a. The non overlapping RB blonging to the common PUCCH must be added to totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i). b. Depending on the number of common PUCCHs and the assignment to RBs the number of RBs may be 1 RB or more RBs. The maximum number of RBs depends on the common PUCCH configuration as defined by 3GPP 38.213 chapter 9.2.1. 4. For above case 2 and 3, the dynamicPUCCH resources allocated to UE shall be added at every slot (the allocated dynamicPUCCH resource may change on slot basis). If allocated, every dynamicPUCCH resource allocated to a UE adds one RB to totalNumPrbToBeUsedForPucch(i). Note again: When FR1 TDD and FDD cell has Dynamic UL resources allocation is active (NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true'), the remaining resources shall be initialized at every UL slot. (ID: 11196944)

##### 3.30.4.8.1-4 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

###### 3.30.4.8.1-4.0-7 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 The available PUSCH bandwidth is divided into smaller subareas by following transmissions present in a slot, if n &gt; 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced( 11110833 )), where the "largest BWP" defines the available PUSCH bandwidth : ****  Initial BWP transmission (determined in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced( 11196450 )). That applies only to initial BWP transmissions of UEs using DCI 0_0 scheduled from CSS (5G_UP_ALG_3937_replaced( 11168480 )). That does NOT apply to initial BWP transmissions of UEs using DCI 0_1 scheduled from USS (5G_UP_ALG_3937_replaced( 11168480 )), if the UE RRC connection is established in the initial BWP using USS, that is if condition in 5G_UP_ALG_17477_replaced( 11110942 ) is equal true.  PRACH including guard PRBs (details in 5G_UP_ALG_4950_replaced( 11199072 )) if actPrachMultiplexing set to TRUE (NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexing is 'TRUE') or if pucchModeSelect set to longPucch (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch').  PUCCH transmission carrying A/N of RA-Msg4/SRB0 in the common PUCCH resource parts defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 11199486 ) or A/N of any transmission until the UE dedicated configuration is activated including the UE in the initial BWP without dedicated configuration, if any, whereby single PUCCH is always transmitted in both parts due to intra-slot frequency hopping.  Any contiguous area semi-persistently allocated to a UE or group of UEs with configured grant (CG) type2 activated and for which the initial transmission of the corresponding CG Harq process is not blocked in the current slot (i.e. configuredGrantTimer is not running and UE not in gap(MG).      any contiguous long PUCCH area in case when placement of PUCCH splits UL BWP (large) into two parts (see 11199501 ). These two contiguous regions, separated by PUCCH area shall be considered as separated PUSCH subareas available for PUSCH scheduling. Note that for a UE with configured grant activated, its PRBs are reserved in time, with the periodicity of the configured grant (see 5G_UP_5893_replaced( 11348488 )), and frequency, as allocated at activation time (se5G_UP_ALG_15123_replaced( 11173014 )), as long as the UE is in a CG “firing” state (see 5G_UP_ALG_15099_replaced( 11172972 )). PUSCH subareas are areas of the contiguous PRBs left after considering the transmissions mentioned above Please note that, For FR1 TDD and FR1 FDD [CB007372-M] and asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) [End CB007372-M] cell, when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true', the starting point and the number of contiguous PRBs in the PUSCH subareas are changing at each slot. The sum of PRBs in all subareas should be equal to the number of available PRBs in the whole PUSCH BW as initially calculated in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced( 11196450 ). If slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )) or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )) or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced( 11278751 )), the sum of PRBs in all subareas should not exceed the number of available PRBs in the whole PUSCH BW as initially calculated in 5G_UP_ALG_4876_replaced( 11196884 ). If n &gt; 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced( 11110833 )): The available resources shall be calculated in each PUSCH subarea separately for each BWP including the initial BWP. In a PUSCH subarea, the number of available resources shall be initialized by the number of overlapping PUSCH PRBs of a given BWP and the PUSCH subarea. As an example PUSCH PRBs of the "smallest BWP" are defined according to rrmStartPuschPrbPowerSavingBwp and rrmNumPuschPrbPowerSavingBwp as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced( 11199486 ). For FDD: and only if Long PUCCH is not configured: PRB blanking shall result in reduction of the size of the border subareas. The number of blanked PRBs for the lower and upper subareas is nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL &amp; nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeUL respectively. If the aperiodic CSI is activated at cell level ( actAperCsiCell set in the cell setup message , L2-PS evaluates whether UL scheduling was already performed in the current PUSCH slot due to A-CSI scheduling with k2aCsi timing (see 5G_UP_ALG_4869_replaced( 11196426 )). If so, during the initialization of the PUSCH subareas, L2-PS shall take into account the already allocated PUSCH resources. The management and the maintenance of the scheduling availability per PUSCH subareas in result of UE allocation and with respect for UE active BWP is done as described in 5G_UP_ALG_15096_replaced( 11196984 ). (ID: 13471224)

##### 3.30.4.8.1-5 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

###### 3.30.4.8.1-5.0-2 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 UEs shall be allocated in a PUSCH subareas where they get the needed PRBs for their transmission. For retransmissions, msg5 (&amp; in some cases also for initialTx), this is done during UE selection (see 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced( 11196450 )). For other initialTx UEs, this is done later during resource distribution (see 5G_UP_ALG_4994_replaced( 11199854 )). [CB014965-A] gNB shall follow the rules defined in this requirement when gNB performs PUSCH subarea resource booking for a UE in exhaustive UL scheduling [Before CB014965-A-UPD001] round 2 [End CB014965-A-UPD001] ( 12533660 ). [End CB014965-A] For a given UE the number of available resources in a PUSCH subarea is defined by the minimum available resources of the active BWP and all larger BWPs. For a ith BWP UE, the available resources are the lowest value of the available resources in the ith BWP and all kth BWPs, where k &lt; i, i ϵ {1 , …, n}. For a UE allocated to an initial BWP transmission, the available resources are the lowest value of all BWPs. NOTE: Above approach assumes that the smaller BWP is included in a larger BWP (see 5G_UP_ALG_1811_replaced( 11110797 )) and the initial BWP is included all BWPs. Single UE is allocated to only 1 PUSCH subarea. Available resources in a PUSCH subarea shall be updated whenever a UE is allocated in it. No more UEs shall be allocated to a PUSCH subarea with no available resources. If n &gt; 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced( 11110833 )): The available resources shall be updated for each PUSCH region separately per BWP in the following manner: If ith BWP UE is allocated to a PUSCH subarea, available resources of the ith and all kth BWPs shall be updated, where k &lt; i. If a UE is allocated to an initial BWP transmission, available resources of all BWPs shall be updated. In the example for n = 3 dedicated BWP per cell: If 1st BWP UE is allocated to a PUSCH subarea, available resources of the 1st BWP shall be updated If 2nd BWP UE is allocated to a PUSCH subarea, available resources of both 1st and 2nd shall be updated If 3rd BWP UE is allocated to a PUSCH subarea, available resources of 1st, 2nd, and 3rd BWP shall be updated If a UE is allocated initial BWP transmission, available resources of 1st, 2nd, and 3rd BWP and the initial BWP shall be updated. [CNI-127997] The gNB-DU shall always track the exclusive resources within a BWP(i) in a PUSCH subarea. While reserving PRBs say 'N', in BWP(i) such that i ϵ {1, ..., n}, if the amount of PRBs to be reserved is greater than the remaining number of exclusive resources for the BWP(i), then corresponding number of resources given by (N - number of remaining exclusive resources of BWP(i)) shall also be deducted from the immediate smaller nested BWP(j) such that j ϵ {i+1, ..., n} or initial BWP. Rationale: This ascertains that the reservation is made from the respective exclusive PRBs first in each BWP(i) when the PUSCH subarea chosen contains the exclusive resources for the BWP(i). [End CNI-127997] [Before CNI-127997] A UE shall not be allocated to a PUSCH subarea without available resources: For a ith BWP UE, if no available resources are in this PUSCH subarea for the ith or all kth BWPs, where k &lt; i. For a UE allocated to an initial BWP transmission, if no available resources are in this PUSCH subarea for any BWPs In the example for n = 3 dedicated BWP per cell: A UE shall not be allocated to a PUSCH subarea without available resources: For a 1st BWP UE, if no available resources are in this PUSCH subarea for the 1st BWP For a 2nd BWP UE, if no available resources are in this PUSCH subarea for the 1st or 2nd BWP. For a 3rd BWP UE, if no available resources are in this PUSCH subarea for the 1st, 2nd , or 3rd BWP. For a UE allocated to an initial BWP transmission, if no available resources are in this PUSCH subarea for the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, or initial BWP. [CNI-127997] For a ith BWP UE, the UE shall not be allocated to a PUSCH subarea without available resources for the ith BWP. [End CNI-127997] NOTE: assumption of above requirement is that smaller BWP are completely included in larger BWPs. The UEs above shall also contain virtual UEs. (ID: 11196994)

##### 3.30.4.8.1-6 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

###### 3.30.4.8.1-6.0-6 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 L2PS shall evaluate the availability of UL subcell to schedule a given UE as follows: Note: which UL subcell can be used for receiving PRACH is determined by the parameter of ulSubcellCaps in (see 5G_ 5G_UP_2554_replaced( 11390916 )). Note: for TDD FR1, when subcell pooling is enabled (spatialStreamPoolingAllowed is equal to TRUE, (5G_RAN_OP_1339_replaced( 11689946 ))), L1 UL subcell shall be "available for usage" based on subcell pooling (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_5230_replaced( 11210126 )). For TDD if FD beamforming is enabled (ulEcpriFdBeamforming = True, (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced( 11297475 ))) { - PUSCH can be scheduled on any available L1 UL subcell. The total number of beams on UL per symbol shall not exceed the value of MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolUl (see 5G_UP_ALG_10697_replaced( 11199387 )). However, on the UL subcell which is used for receiving PRACH, n beam(s)  among rrmMaxNumBeamsUlSubcell shall be reserved for PRACH signal whatever single RO or multiple ROs are configured for the PRACH. Where:  n: if nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion is 1 or 2 and short PRACH, then n = nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion, else n = 1. Note: Here nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion is not direct value of NIDD parameter NRCELL.nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion. See 5G_UP_ALG_1959_replaced( 11111903 ) for nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion and short PRACH. ****  Rationale: RU MADE ES1 shares the number of beams handling capability for short PRACH and PUxCH and support up to 2 for nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion. No limitation for other RUs or MADE ES1 with long PRACH. Method here uses a generic way to hide the RU hardware category from L2. "else n = 1" is to keep the default legacy behaviour. It can be improved to "else n = 0" in future with parameter control.**** -     NOTE: If (NRCELL.actNrRim != 0 &amp;&amp; (RI_state=1 or RI_state=2) &amp;&amp; NRBTS/NRCELL.actIncreaseMaxULScheduleUE = False, in the slot where RIM RI/RS is scheduled, 5G-L2-PS shall not schedule more than min(rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl (see 5G_UP_ALG_10654_replaced( 11199273 )), 15) for PUSCH. Rationale: This restriction is coming from MADE1 radio. MADE1 radio has 32 regions per symbol, out of which 16 can be used for PUSCH allocations on SRS/other refined beam combinations. When RIM is activated, it consumes one region for it to work, so only maximum of 15 UEs can be scheduled instead of 16. When NRBTS/NRCELL.actIncreaseMaxULScheduleUE = true, maximum number of UEs which can be scheduled on UL per symbol per slot per cell is increased from 16 to 24. In this case, When RIM is activated, gNB can receive data of maximum 15 UEs using SRS/refined beam combination, for all the remaining UEs, gNB shall receive data using SSB beam combination (refer 12493196 for more details). - NOTE: When subcell pooling is enabled (spatialStreamPoolingAllowed is equal to True, (5G_RAN_OP_1339_replaced( 11689946 ))), even the floating UL subcell is PUCCH capable and/or PUSCH capable, it can’t be scheduled for PUCCH or PUSCH multiplexing on PRACH slot, it means that PUCCH or PUSCH multiplexing on PRACH slot can only be scheduled on primary subcell. } For TDD if FD beamforming is disabled (ulEcpriFdBeamforming = False, (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced( 11297475 ))) { - NOTE: In PRACH slots, execute below procedures to find available UL subcell for scheduling UEs with the exception of the UL subcell which is used for receiving PRACH. This one shall be used for scheduling UEs if they have the same beam for receiving PRACH and less than rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell UEs are assigned to it, but this is only valid in case of single RO and not in case of multi ROs (in case of multi ROs no UEs can be attached to the PRACH subcell). - If supercell mode is enabled, the mapping table (refer to 5G_UP_1851_replaced( 11375846 )) is used to check UE beam specific L1 subcell for each UE allocation. - If supercell mode not enabled (refer to 5G_UP_5471_replaced( 11353418 )): a) There is an L1 UL subcell with less than rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell UEs assigned to it, all having the same Beam as the UE in question, or b) There is an L1 UL subcell with less than rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell UEs assigned to it having less than or equal to rrmMaxNumBeamsUlSubcell different beams and one of the beams is the same as that of the UE in question, or c) There is an L1 UL subcell with no UEs assigned to it, or d) There is an L1 UL subcell with less than maxNumUesFdmUlSubcell UEs assigned to it having less than rrmMaxNumBeamsUlSubcell different beams and none of the beams is the same as that of the UE in question. } For FDD: - The to-be-scheduled list of UEs has less than rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell UEs having the same L1 UL subcell as the current UE. - For a FDD cell shared with LTE and NRCELL.lteNrDssMode &gt; 2, if the UE has a pending initial msg3Tx, then the UE can be scheduled in this subcell. (ID: 13133991)

##### 3.30.4.8.1-7 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

##### 3.30.4.8.1-8 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

###### 3.30.4.8.1-8.0-5 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 11197361: Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci is derived from the following tables according to the number HARQ-AN bits, number of CSI bits: Table 0/Table 1/Table 2 shall be used when the UE is not scheduled with 64qamLowSE-Index table in UL. If the NRCELL.actDisableMinPuschUciPrbsUpd is set to False (default value) then following below tables ( Table 1 and Table 2) are used, otherwise ( Table 0) is used. * Table 3 shall be used when the UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE-Index table in UL. Note Table 3 is not dependent on either NRCELL.tmpActFeat2 Bit8 or NRCELL.actDisableMinPuschUciPrbsUpd. * For UL Tx switching UE in FDD PCell, Table 1 or Table 2 or Table 3 shall be used independent on either NRCELL.tmpActFeat2 Bit8 or NRCELL.actDisableMinPuschUciPrbsUpd. * * Table 0 [docx_desc] N_AN minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci N_AN N_CSI = 0 0 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 16 16 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 32 32 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 48 0 N/A 4 8 15 1 1 4 12 15 2 1 5 12 16 3 1 5 12 16 4 1 5 12 16 5 1 6 15 18 6 2 6 15 18 7 2 8 15 18 8 2 8 15 18 9 3 8 15 18 10 3 8 15 18 11 3 8 15 18 12 4 10 16 18 13 4 10 16 20 14 4 10 16 20 15 4 10 16 20 16 4 10 16 20 17 4 12 16 20 18 4 12 18 20 19 5 12 18 24 20 6 15 18 24 21 6 15 18 24[/docx_desc][docx_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/attachments/m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file15488_0.docx[/docx_path] Table 1 shall be used when the UE is scheduled with the QPSK modulation independent if TP is activated or deactivated. Table 1 - minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci - QPSK [docx_desc] N_AN minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci N_AN N_CSI = 0 0 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 16 16 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 32 32 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 48 0 N/A 5 13 18 1 1 6 14 19 2 1 7 14 20 3 1 7 14 20 4 2 7 14 20 5 2 7 15 20 6 2 7 16 20 7 3 8 16 21 8 3 8 17 21 9 3 8 17 21 10 4 12 17 22 11 4 12 18 22 12 4 12 18 22 13 4 12 18 22 14 4 13 18 22 15 5 13 18 22 16 5 13 18 22 17 5 13 20 22 18 5 13 20 22 19 6 14 20 27 20 7 16 21 32 21 7 16 22 32 22 8 17 22 32 23 8 17 22 32[/docx_desc][docx_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/attachments/m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file15488_1.docx[/docx_path] Table 2 shall be used when the UE is scheduled with the pi/2-BPSK modulation. Table 2 - minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci - pi/2-BPSK [docx_desc] N_AN minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci N_AN N_CSI = 0 0 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 16 16 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 32 32 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 48 0 N/A 8 24 36 1 2 12 25 36 2 3 12 25 36 3 3 12 25 36 4 3 15 25 36 5 3 15 25 40 6 4 15 27 40 7 4 16 30 45 8 5 18 32 45 9 6 18 32 45 10 6 18 36 45 11 8 18 36 45 12 8 18 36 45 13 8 20 36 45 14 8 20 36 45 15 8 20 36 45 16 8 24 36 45 17 12 24 36 45 18 12 24 40 45 19 12 25 40 48 20 15 27 45 50 21 15 27 45 54 22 16 32 45 54 23 16 32 45 54[/docx_desc][docx_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/attachments/m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file15488_2.docx[/docx_path] Table 3 - 64qamLowSE-Index - QPSK [docx_desc] N_AN minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci N_AN N_CSI = 0 0&lt; N_CSI ≤ 6 6 &lt; N_CSI ≤11 6 &lt; N_CSI ≤11 11&lt; N_CSI ≤ 16 16 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 32 32 &lt; N_CSI ≤ 48 0 N/A 6 8 8 9 17 24 1 6 8 11 11 12 20 26 2 6 9 12 12 14 22 29 3 6 9 12 12 14 22 29 4 6 9 12 12 14 22 29 5 6 10 13 13 15 22 29 6 7 11 15 15 16 24 30 7 8 12 16 16 16 25 31 8 9 13 16 16 18 26 31 9 10 14 18 18 18 26 32 10 12 16 18 18 20 29 45 11 12 16 20 20 21 29 45 12 10 14 18 18 18 27 45 13 11 15 18 18 20 27 45 14 12 16 18 18 20 29 45 15 12 16 20 20 20 29 45 16 12 16 20 20 21 30 46 17 13 17 20 20 22 30 50 18 13 17 21 21 22 30 50 19 14 18 22 22 22 31 50 20 17 21 25 25 26 45 56 21 18 22 26 26 26 45 56 22 18 22 26 26 26 45 56 23 18 22 26 26 27 46 56[/docx_desc][docx_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/attachments/m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file15488_3.docx[/docx_path] For the case only CSI bit(s) are multiplexed on PUSCH, minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci can be found in the first row of each above table. Where: N_AN: number of HARQ-ACK/NACK bits to be multiplexed on PUSCH; N_CSI: number of CSI bits to be multiplexed on PUSCH; Notes: 1. Value of minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci is computed according to the criteria that after multiplexing UCI bits, coding rate of UL transport block shall not exceed 0.95. The computation of the minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci values in Table 1 and Table 2 bases on a coding rate below 0.45. Some assumptions for the computation: * If the NRCELL.actDisableMinPuschUciPrbsUpd is set to False then betaOffsetAck is set to 20 (corresponding index is 11) for HARQ-AN bits &lt;=2, betaOffsetAck = 10 (corresponding index is 8) for 2 &gt; HARQ-AN bits &lt;=11 and betaOffsetAck = 6.25 (index 6) for HARQ-AN bits &gt; 11 otherwise betaOffsetAck is set to 10 (corresponding index is 8) for HARQ-AN bits &lt;=11 and betaOffsetAck = 6.25 (index 6) for HARQ-AN bits &gt; 11; * betaOffsetCSI-Part1 is set to 6.25 (corresponding index is 13); * Number of PUSCH OFDM symbols = 10, number of CDM groups without data = 2 (i.e., REs in DMRS OFDM symbols not occupied by DMRS are not used for UL-SCH mapping), number of OFDM symbols for DMRS = 2; * Number of PUSCH layers = 1; * MCS index of PUSCH is assumed to be MCS0; * PTRS overhead is 8 REs every 2 PRBs, corresponding to K_PT-RS = 2, L_PT-RS = 1, PTRS is disabled for the computations of Table 1 and Table 2; The computation of the minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci values in Table 3 bases on a coding rate below 0.07. Some assumptions for the computation: * betaOffsetIndexHarqAck = [9, 6, 3]; * betaOffsetIndexCsi1 = [11, 6]; * Number of PUSCH OFDM symbols = 10, number of CDM groups without data = 2 (i.e., REs in DMRS OFDM symbols not occupied by DMRS are not used for UL-SCH mapping), number of OFDM symbols for DMRS = 2; * Number of PUSCH layers = 1; * MCS index of PUSCH is assumed to be MCS0 2. For CA UE with PUCCH allocated by DPA, HARQ-AN bits number is derived from the PUCCH pre-allocation result of DPA, not the actual PDSCH scheduling result in the corresponding bundle window. [CB007491-F] 3. When UL two additional DMRS is used, minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci defined in above tables may not enough in case number of PUSCH OFDM symbols is 10 (the number of data symbols becomes 7 due to 3 DMRS),  there's no change for this special case to avoid the logic over complex due to below reasons: (1) It could be happened only when CA UL TX switch is triggered under UL:DL = 4:6 frame structure and on a high-speed cell (NRCELL.actHighSpeedCell is true). (2) Only when MCS is very low (typically 0) the PRB may not enough, it is very rare case to have so low MCS when CA UL TX switch is triggered. (3) Even it's really happened (PRB is not enough hence code rate for UL-SCH is higher than 0.95), the UL-SCH is still possible to be successfully decoded in re-transmission. [End CB007491-F] ** (ID: 11197361)

##### 3.30.4.8.1-9 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

###### 3.30.4.8.1-9.0-2 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 minAllowedPuschPrbUlPtrs is the minimal allowed PUSCH PRB when UL PTRS is configured [Before CB013204-A] If all below conditions meet: 1. UE is in SA mode. 2. DCI 0_1 is used in UL scheduling. 3. DFT-s-OFDM is not used in UL scheduling. then minAllowedPuschPrbUlPtrs = max((min(4, NRB0), minSupportedNumPrbsUl (see 5G_L2_1136_replaced( 11183158 ))) . Where NRB0 is obtained from frequencyDensity, if frequencyDensity is not present, NRB0=1 is used in above formula. else minAllowedPuschPrbUlPtrs = minSupportedNumPrbsUl (see 5G_L2_1136_replaced( 11183158 )). endif [CB013204-A] If all below conditions meet: 1. UE is in SA mode. 2. DCI 0_1 is used in UL scheduling. 3. DFT-s-OFDM is not used in UL scheduling. then minAllowedPuschPrbUlPtrs = max((min(4, NRB0), rrmMinPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31765_replaced( 11199466 ))) . Where NRB0 is obtained from frequencyDensity, if frequencyDensity is not present, NRB0=1 is used in above formula. else if DFT-s-OFDM is used in UL scheduling, and with configured sample density PTRS is present with 1 PRB minAllowedPuschPrbUlPtrs = max (2, rrmMinPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31765_replaced( 11199466 ))) else minAllowedPuschPrbUlPtrs = rrmMinPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31765_replaced( 11199466 )) endif [End CB013204-A] Rationale: During transition phase PTRS is disabled (even if generally switched on) to avoid ambiguity. This is done by 5G-L2-PS due to limiting the PRB allocation to values less than NRB0 (see 38.214 section 6.2.3.1). In transition phase, the lack of PTRS is no problem (even in high speed scenarios) because due to DCI 0_0 grants,  3 DMRS are transmitted and PTRS is actually not needed. After transition phase using regular UL DCI 0_1, PTRS needs to be provided all the time (in case operator configured NRB0 as such, e.g. equals 1). Therefore PRB allocation need to be equal or larger than minAllowedPuschPrbUlPtrs in order to assure PTRS transmission. Note1: In initial SA RRC setup (i.e. non-Handover case), CP-RT modified operator configured NRB0 to be no less than 4 (equals to rdMinPrbPtrsSAUL+1). In handover case, CP-RT use operator configured NRB0 directly. Note2: DFT-s-OFDM has its own PTRS configuration in RRC, while DCI 0-0 always use CP-OFDM,  therefore no need to restrict PRB allocation due to [Before CB013204-A] UL PTRS when DFT-s-OFDM is used.[CB013204-A] PTRS transient phase issue. When PTRS is configured for DFT-s-OFDM, if PTRS is present with 1 PRB allocation, gNB-DU shall schedule minimal 2 PRBs due to too high PTRS overhead with 1 PRB. PTRS presence with 1 PRB could be deduced by NRB0 = 1, where NRB0 is derived from C-Plane sample density for the UE [End CB013204-A] (ID: 11197441)

##### 3.30.4.8.1-10 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

###### 3.30.4.8.1-10.0-1 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 Overview (ID: 11197488)

**3.30.4.8.1-10.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11197544`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 For certain conditions, UEs with retransmission request classes (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4867_replaced([11196407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCenPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) can be skipped.

Such conditions are mentioned below:

  * Number of symbols available for a retransmission. (refer 5G_UP_ALG_31718_replaced([11197630](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Transport block size threshold for retransmissions in case TD-LTE frame structure with tdLteConfig0 is enabled. (refer 5G_UP_ALG_31720_replaced([11197665](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwH-IfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

###### 3.30.4.8.1-10.0-2 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 Number of symbols check for retransmission request classes (ID: 11197630)

**3.30.4.8.1-10.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11197637`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 For all retransmission request classes, the gNB-DU shall skip such request classes (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4867_replaced([11196407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCenPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if retransmission slot has fewer number of symbols of PUSCH than initial transmission slot except below cases:

  * [CB012149-DA] Activate adaptive retransmission is activated and retransmission happens for UL tx switching UE in FDD cell (either as active UL SCell or as PCell with an active TDD UL SCell) and retransmission slot does not expect A-SRS report (Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2536_replaced([11117398](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp-57vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for slots with expected A-SRS report in FDD). [End CB012149-DA]

---

###### 3.30.4.8.1-10.0-3 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 TB size threshold for retransmission request class (ID: 11197665)

**3.30.4.8.1-10.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11197693`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 If TD-LTE frame structure with tdLteConfig0 is enabled, then, in the first 4 consecutive UL slots following a DL slot (i.e. slot offsets 4, 5, 6, 7, 14, 15, 16 & 17 in every SFN of the rrmSlotPattern(refer 5G_UP_ALG_2313_replaced([11114697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnGefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))):

For all retransmission request classes, the gNB-DU shall skip such request classes (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4867_replaced([11196407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCenPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for a UE in a slot if the sum total of bits allocated to the UEs with retransmission that have been selected until the current UE in that slot is greater than the threshold provided by rrmUlReTxLimitTdLteConfig0

End

where :

  * rrmUlReTxLimitTdLteConfig0 = rdUlReTxLimitTdLteConfig0Loki if L1 HW variant = "Loki", else rdUlReTxLimitTdLteConfig0

  * rdUlReTxLimitTdLteConfig0Loki is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_31783_replaced([11209665](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw1IxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

  * rdUlReTxLimitTdLteConfig0 is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_31785_replaced([11209676](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw1IyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 3.30.4.8.1-11 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2

###### 3.30.4.8.1.0-1 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 Overview (ID: 11196450)

**3.30.4.8.1.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `12491428`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 5G-L2-PS shall determine first the list of UEs with a CG occasion in current slot (i.e. UEs in a CG ‘firing’ state, as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15099_replaced([11172972](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for which the configuredGrantTimer corresponding to the HARQ ID associated to the CG occasion (see 5G_UP_ALG_14922_replaced([11202350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not running and for which the 5G-L2-PS does not receive a PsUser_UserStopReq yet.

If NRBTS.actScheUePooling is set to 'true' or slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the list is evaluated in accordance to 5G_UP_ALG_20531_replaced([11173131](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQ89_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If condition 3) below is passed, i.e an UL subcell to schedule this UE is available as per 5G_UP_ALG_4886_replaced([11197000](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGwGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), UE shall be directly added to “To-be-scheduled” list in accordance to the scheduling parameters associated to the CG grant. Total number of consumed FDM tokens is updated accordingly.

 If the HARQ ID corresponding to CG occasion requires a retransmission, in case the decision to send the UE to “To-be-scheduled” is not later canceled by a CG HARQ retransmission or by a CG overwriting by DG then HARQ process is flushed.

Note that if UE is in any priority class, so that UE shall be processed from CS2 list below, scheduling parameters may be updated**.**

L2-PS shall determine the list of to-be-scheduled UEs from CS2 to be sent to the FD scheduler for resource allocation. Starting from UEs having the highest TD metric, a UE is added to the list if the following criteria is satisfied:

1\. Initial BWP usage criteria is met (see 5G_UP_ALG_11393_replaced([11196492](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDFpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

2\. Dedicated BWP usage criteria is met (see 5G_UP_ALG_11402_replaced([11196505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDFqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

3\. An UL subcell to schedule this UE is available as per 5G_UP_ALG_4886_replaced([11197000](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGwGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

4\. Sufficient BWP FD resources are available (see 5G_UP_ALG_11420_replaced([11196526](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDFrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

5\. Sufficient PDCCH resources are available (see 5G_UP_ALG_11429_replaced([11196565](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDstfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

6\. UE is not in overheating status (see 5G_UP_ALG_11438_replaced([11196580](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDsuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

7\. Sufficient FD resources are available to multiplex UCI on PUSCH transmission (see 5G_UP_ALG_11447_replaced([11196591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDsuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * Multiplexing of UCI on PUSCH – Basic concept (see 5G_UP_ALG_23283_replaced([11196594](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDsvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * Restriction of UCI multiplexing on PUSCH if the ATB restricts the PRB allocation (see 5G_UP_ALG_23288_replaced([11196619](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwETwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * Multiplexing of UCI on PUSCH retransmissions (see 5G_UP_ALG_23292_replaced([11196634](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwETxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * UCI Multiplexing on PUSCH in SCell for UEs configured with UL CA (see 5G_UP_ALG_23297_replaced([11196661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwETzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * Multiplexing of UCI on PUSCH for UEs with voice service (see 5G_UP_ALG_23301_replaced([11196682](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwET0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * Multiplexing of UCI on PUSCH for High Reliablity DRB (see 5G_UP_ALG_23304_replaced([11196703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwE60vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))




Note: If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall ensure that the constraint related to layer PRBs mentioned in 5G_UP_ALG_26661_replaced([11214618](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOxYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is maintained while checking for the FD resources required to multiplex UCI on PUSCH. In case allocating the required resources to a UE breaks the constraint, the UE shall be skipped and the next UE in the list shall be checked for allocation as long as there are still layer PRB resources available.

8\. PUSCH and long PUCCH transmission coexistence criteria must be met (see 5G_UP_ALG_11456_replaced([11196713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwE61fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

9\. A-CSI resources must be available for A-CSI eligible UEs (see 5G_UP_ALG_11465_replaced([11196729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwE62PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

10\. PDCCH beam to be used for UE is not muted (see 5G_UP_ALG_11474_replaced([11196742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwE62vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

11\. Initial transmission number of PRBs must meet minimum PRB requirements (see 5G_UP_ALG_11483_replaced([11196751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwE63PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

12\. PUSCH transmission after RRC setup must not multiplex HARQ A/N (see 5G_UP_ALG_11492_replaced([11196779](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwFh4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if the UE RRC connection is established in the initial BWP using USS, that is if condition in 5G_UP_ALG_17477_replaced([11110942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is equal true.

13\. PUSCH transmission after re-establishment must not multiplex HARQ A/N (see 5G_UP_ALG_11492_replaced([11196779](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwFh4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

14\. Scheduling of a UE with configured grant type2 configured, must be evaluated in accordance to its CG state (see 5G_UP_ALG_15166_replaced([11196787](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwFh4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB013895-A] 15. Scheduling of UE considering RU limitation (see [12493196: Scheduling decision considering RU limitation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NYOjURN7EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB013895-A]

When a UE is added to the to-be-scheduled list the following functions must be performed:

1\. Assign the UE to a L1 UL subcell (see 5G_UP_ALG_11501_replaced([11196808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwFh5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

2\. Assign the UE to a PUSCH subarea (see 5G_UP_ALG_11510_replaced([11196812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwFh6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

3\. Allocate a PDCCH candidate to the UE (5G_UP_ALG_11528_replaced([11196844](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwFh7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

4\. Update initial BWP usage (5G_UP_ALG_11661_replaced([11196859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGI8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

5\. Determine MU MIMO resources remaining (see 5G_UP_ALG_11537_replaced([11196874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGI9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

6\. L2RT shall execute MU paring algorithm from rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl UEs in to-be-scheduled list. After paring algorithm, L2RT shall re-update to-be-scheduled list (see 5G_UP_ALG_11510_replaced([11196812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwFh6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

###### 3.30.4.8.1.0-2 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 Initial BWP usage criteria (ID: 11196492)

**3.30.4.8.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11196501`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 [CB009550-A] If NRCELL.actFdmInitialDedicatedFdd is set to FALSE [End CB009550-A] [CB009550-B] and NRCELL.actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is set to FALSE [End CB009550-B]

{

The criteria is fulfilled for a UE, using DCI 0_0 scheduled from CSS (5G_UP_ALG_3937_replaced([11168480](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktv_nfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if the number of UEs to-be-scheduled with Initial BWP (rrmNumUesFdmUlInitialBWP) has not reached rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlInitialBWP, and if no UE with class contentResGrant in the to-be scheduled list.

5G-L2-PS shall apply dedicated BWP usage criteria (see 5G_UP_ALG_11402_replaced([11196505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDFqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to UEs using DCI 0_1 scheduled from USS (5G_UP_ALG_3937_replaced([11168480](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktv_nfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) irrespective of the active BWP.

For FDD , if a UE in dedicated BWP and rrmNumUesFdmDlInitialBWP >0, stop adding UEs to the list of to-be-scheduled UEs.

}[PR694285]Else If NRCELL.actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is set to TRUE, maximum rrmMaxRaMsg3FdmUlInitialBWP RA Msg3 shall be scheduled in a slot.[End PR694285]

---

###### 3.30.4.8.1.0-3 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 Dedicated BWP usage criteria (ID: 11196505)

**3.30.4.8.1.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `12557600`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 [Before CB014044-M]

The number of UEs to-be-scheduled has not reached rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl and max(available resources in a PUSCH subarea) is no less than **rrmMinSupportedPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31776_replaced([11199457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**.

[CB014044-M]

If NRCELL.actTdHighBufUePrio is set to disabled or NRCELL.actTdHighBufUePrio is set to UL or DL_UL but the conditions defined by [12517603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e_1yEBiQEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) are not satisfied 

The number of UEs to-be-scheduled has not reached rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl and max(available resources in a PUSCH subarea) is not less than rrmMinSupportedPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31776_replaced([11199457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Else

  * The gNB-DU shall calculate numOfLargeBufferUeThreshold = rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl * ([Before PR869874]NRCELL. ulHighBufferPrioUeRatio [PR869874]NRCELL. ulHighBufPrioUeRatio[End PR869874]/100) 
  * The number of UEs to-be-scheduled has not reached rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl– numOfLargeBufferUeThreshold and max(available resources in a PUSCH subarea) is not less than rrmMinSupportedPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31776). 
  * The gNB-DU shall sort the next candidates according to their PF metric and add them in this order from this list to the UEs to-be-scheduled list of the corresponding carrier by evaluating the steps from [11196444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCeo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). 
  * After the above step, as per High buffer UE TD algorithm (see [12634190](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rBwE0D7cEfCqvdo9YkxBnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) choose next candidate UE.



[End CB014044-M] 

  * End process if this condition is not fulfilled (no more UEs to be added to the list)
  * If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and if rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl > 1:
  * If rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl == 2, and if the UE is defined as nth BWP UE(see 5G_UP_ALG_1813_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): the UE is not added to the list if the number of UEs to-be-scheduled has reached (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl -1) and if in this list no UE is defined as ith BWP UE, where _i shall be not be equal_ n.**
  * If rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl > 2, and if the UE is defined as ith BWP UE, where i ϵ {1, .., n} (see 5G_UP_ALG_1813_replaced([11110813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): the UE is not added to the list if the number of UEs to-be-scheduled has reached (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl -1) and if in this list no UE is defined as 1st BWP UE or if a kth BWP UE can be added, where k < i.



NOTE: See examples of similar candidate set 2 creation in downlink 5G_UP_ALG_4261_replaced([11175776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kultGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 3.30.4.8.1.0-4 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 Sufficient BWP FD resources are available (ID: 11196526)

**3.30.4.8.1.0-4.0-8**  (ID: `13439351`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 If the UE in question is the 1st UE having an initial BWP transmission (SRB0) or a dedicated BWP retransmission UE or needs msg5 in dedicated BWP, the needed resources are not greater than the remaining resources with consideration for the active BWP of the UE as expressed in 5G_UP_ALG_4879_replaced([11196950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGwCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 

**\- If** slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall keep track of the used and remaining layer PRB resources available in each slot (rrmNumUsedPuschLayerPrbPerSlot, rrmRemainingPuschLayerPrbPerSlot; refer 5G_UP_ALG_26582_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and update them while allocating FD resources **to each UE.gNB-DU shall check that the layer PRB constraint mentioned in 5G_UP_ALG_26661_replaced([11214618](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOxYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is always satisfied. The number of layer PRBs used for a given PRB allocation can be calculated as **numReqLayerPrb mentioned in 5G_UP_ALG_26582_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case allocating the required resources to a UE breaks the constraint, the UE shall be allocated the maximum possible resources (only in case of initial BWP transmission). The next UE in the list shall be checked for allocation as long as there are still layer PRB resources available. Further UE allocation and scheduling in all steps shall be stopped if there are no more layer PRB resources available for scheduling in the current slot.**_*_** *

  * For initial BWP transmission UEs:

  * The location and bandwidth of the UL initial BWP are configured as defined in to 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2143_replaced([11308391](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrDvIvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2140_replaced([11308375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrDvHvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).




**\- In FDD cell shared with LTE (DSS), for initial BWP transmission, the number PRBs as described in 5G_UP_ALG_3424_replaced([11152773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksh3mPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

**\- Note: The resources for UE transmitting in initial BWP are limited by the size of PUSCH area in the initial BWP.**

  * If slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the number of PRBs to be used for the initial BWP transmission shall not exceed the total amount of usable PRBs available for PUSCH in UL that takes into account PRBs allocated by BBRM.

  * If NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssFastAlloc=true and if the number of PRB needed for the retransmission is greater than number of PRB available for PUSCH [CB007372-E] in given BWP [End CB007372-E] calculated based on the new DSS UL allocation message(s) and the next retransmission would be scheduled after the end of DSS UL allocation transient phase(s) (as defined in ([11172453](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuMrgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true and if PUSCH in NR dedicated part is larger than number of PRB available for PUSCH [CB007372-E] in given BWP [End CB007372-E] calculated based on the new DSS UL allocation message(s) then the maximum number of PUSCH PRBs is set to number of PUSCH PRBs in NR dedicated part. If NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actUlAdaptReTxEnhDss=TRUE and (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs=TRUE or NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcsEnh=TRUE), then the PRBs needed for retransmission are adapted as described in ([11202887](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwd8aPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), otherwise it is the same as in initial transmission.




The max harq retrans shall be considered as reached for such candidate. 5G-L2-PS shall stop the retransmission and free the HARQ process.

  * For dedicated BWP retransmissions UEs: If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) or the conditions defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are not fulfilled, **the same amount of resources as their initial transmission and if the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, the same amount of resources or a different amount of resources as their initial transmission is possible.**

  * If slowPrbPoolingAllowed is set to 'true' (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and if the same amount of usable PRBs are not available in UL for the retransmission (5G_UP_ALG_4876_replaced([11196884](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGI9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), **re-transmitting UE(s) shall not be added to the list and the retransmission shall be skipped and HARQ process is freed.**




**\- If** slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), for a UE with retransmission, the number of layer PRBs used shall not exceed the remaining available layer PRBs in a slot (**rrmRemainingPuschLayerPrbPerSlot; refer 5G_UP_ALG_26582_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The number of layer PRBs used for a given PRB allocation can be calculated as **numReqLayerPrb mentioned in 5G_UP_ALG_26582_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case the required number of layer PRBs are not available in the current slot, the retransmission shall be skipped and retried in a later slot.**_*_** *

However, if the required number of layer PRBs for the retransmission are greater than the maximum number of available layer PRBs per slot during a pooling period (refer rrmMaxNumLayerPrbPerSlot in 5G_UP_ALG_26582_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the retransmission shall be skipped and the HARQ process is freed
