###### 3.30.4.8.1.0-21 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 High buffer UE prioritization in TD algorithm (ID: 12557471)

**3.30.4.8.1.0-21.0-1**  (ID: `12557472`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 **Overview**

---

**3.30.4.8.1.0-21.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12557475`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 [CB014044-M]

The gNB-DU shall enhance the RB utilization while also providing special handling to high buffer UEs, e.g., FWA (Fixed Wireless Access). For this purpose, the gNB-DU shall prioritize few high buffer UEs to improve the RB utilization. This scheme is trying to reserve resources in scheduling (defined by parameter [Before PR869874]NRCELL. ulHighBufferPrioUeRatio [PR869874]NRCELL. ulHighBufPrioUeRatio[End PR869874]) out of rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl for UEs with high buffer in case high TD metric UEs are not in the position to occupy all available resources. Such an approach helps to fully utilize the PRB resources to boost the cell throughput.

[End CB014044-M]

---

**3.30.4.8.1.0-21.0-2**  (ID: `12557473`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 **Condition to apply the algorithm**

---

**3.30.4.8.1.0-21.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12557476`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 12557476: Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 [CB014044-M]

If NRBTS/NRCELL.actTdHighBufUEPrio is set to UL or DL_UL the gNB-DU shall apply the following algorithm.

The algorithm is applicable only if the total number of UEs (S) in scheduling candidate set is larger than max number of UEs that the gNB-DU can select for scheduling (Nsched), i.e., S>Nsched. In that case, the gNB-DU shall sort the non-GBR UEs [Before CB014044-UPD002] in descending order of PRB needs RB_estu and **[CB014044-UPD002] in S list by DRB buffer and then it shall check below condition for the top of numOfLargeBufferUeThreshold UEs**[End CB014044-UPD002]****

[Before PR861041] [Before CB014044-UPD002]  
[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $$ \sum_{u=1}^{N_{sched}} N_{k2UlDci} - N_{sched_highPrio} \quad RB_{est_u} > n \times N_{BW} \times N_{k2UlDci} - \sum_{v=1}^{N_{sched_highPrio}} RB_{est_v} $$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-08281549.png[/image_path][/image-0]

[CB014044-UPD002]

[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $$ \sum_{u=1}^{\text{numOfLargeBufferUeThreshold}} N_{k2UlDci} \cdot RB_est_u > n \times N_{BW} \times N_{k2UlDci} - \sum_{v=1}^{N_{\text{sched_highPrio}}} RB_est_v $$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-08281549.png[/image_path][/image-1]

[End CB014044-UPD002] [PR861041]

[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a single line and uses standard mathematical notation. The equation reads: `numOfLargeBufferUeThreshold` $\sum_{u=1}$ `RB_est_u > N_BW -` `N_sched_highprio` $\sum_{v=1}$ `RB_est_v`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-01220643.png[/image_path][/image-2]

[End PR861041]

with

[Before CB014044-UPD002]  
Nsched: Max number of UEs in scheduling set (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl see [11199273](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))  
[End CB014044-UPD002]  
Nsched_highPrio: Number of higher priority UEs (GBR, reTx)  
RB_estu: Estimated PRB number of non-GBR UE u [PR873563] Instead of using rrmUlProposedMcs (defined in [11201008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) ), rrmAvgSpectralEffUlSlot (defined in [11212124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw_g0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) will be used to determine RB_est. [End PR873563]  
RB_estv: Estimated PRB number of higher priority UE v  
NBW: Number of PRB available in bandwidth  
[Before PR861041]  
NMuMimo: Number of PRB allocated by MU-MIMO paired UEs  
n: Channel bandwidth multiplier  
[Before PR861041]

[Before PR861041]The channel bandwidth multiplier n is equal to 1 [CB014044-Q] if NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is set to FALSE. If NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is set to TRUE the channel bandwidth multiplier n is equal to [Before CB014044-M-UPD004] rdChannelBwMultiplierUl (see [12657768](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WNRmAEpxEfC0Ye3M8ARUWQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).[CB014044-M-UPD004] NRCELL.channelBwMultiplierUl [End CB014044-M-UPD004][End CB014044-Q][End PR861041]

If the above condition is fulfilled, the gNB-DU shall apply the corresponding algorithm defined by [12634190](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rBwE0D7cEfCqvdo9YkxBnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). If the condition is not fulfilled, the gNB-DU shall not apply the algorithm.

Note 1: The algorithm is applicable only for non-GBR UEs.

[Before CB014044-M-UPD004][CB014044-R]

Note 2: When NRBTS/NRCELL.actNbrForNonGbrBearers is set to TRUE and NRBTS/NRCELL.actTdHighBufUEPrio is set to UL or DL_UL, the gNB-DU shall take only the buffer length as the selection criterion for high-buffer UEs, regardless of NBR prioritization or token availability. As such, a high-buffer UE can be an NBR bearer or a non-GBR bearer, depending solely on the buffer status.

[End CB014044-R][End CB014044-M-UPD004]

[End CB014044-M]

---

**3.30.4.8.1.0-21.0-3**  (ID: `12634190`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 Algorithm of the high buffer UE prioritization in TD

---

**3.30.4.8.1.0-21.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12634197`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 12634197: Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 [CB014044-M] When NRBTS/NRCELL.actTdHighBufUEPrio is UL or DL_UL, the gNB-DU shall apply the following algorithm: Definitions: * Let S be the list of not evaluated UEs (with priority class less than or equal to drbTraffic) in CS2 list: [Before CB014044-M-UPD004][CB014044-R] Note: When NRBTS/NRCELL.actNbrForNonGbrBearers is set to TRUE and NRBTS/NRCELL.actTdHighBufUEPrio is set to UL or DL_UL, the gNB-DU shall take only the buffer length as the selection criterion for high-buffer UEs, regardless of NBR prioritization or token availability. As such, a high-buffer UE can be an NBR bearer or a non-GBR bearer, depending solely on the buffer status. [End CB014044-R][End CB014044-M-UPD004] * Let L be the list of UEs selected for scheduling (to-be-scheduled list) during the UL slot under evaluation. * Let N_sched be the maximum number of UEs that can be scheduled in FDM UL during the UL slot under evaluation, defined as: N_sched = rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl (see [11199273](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) * Let N_PRB be the total estimated PRBs allocated for UEs in list L. [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as: $N_{PRB} = \sum_{u=1}^{|L|} RB_{est_u}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-06011512.png[/image_path][/image-0] * Let N_BW be the total available PRBs. * Let n be the channel bandwidth multiplier. The channel bandwidth multiplier n is equal to 1 [CB014044-Q] if NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is set to FALSE. If NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is set to TRUE the channel bandwidth multiplier is equal to [Before CB014044-M-UPD004] rdChannelBwMultiplierUl (see [12657768](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WNRmAEpxEfC0Ye3M8ARUWQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).[CB014044-M-UPD004] NRCELL.channelBwMultiplierUl [End CB014044-M-UPD004] [End CB014044-Q] * Let the threshold be defined as: threshold = N_sched × ([Before PR869874]NRCELL. ulHighBufferPrioUeRatio [PR869874]NRCELL. ulHighBufPrioUeRatio[End PR869874] / 100) Algorithm: * Sort the candidate UE list S in descending order based on their TD metric. * Iterate through each UE u in S: * If the current number of UEs in L satisfies |L| ≥ N_sched - threshold and N_PRB < n x N_BW: Check if UE u is eligible for scheduling based on predefined criteria. [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical inequality. The inequality is written as: $RB_{est_u} \geq \frac{n \times N_{BW} - N_{PRB}}{N_{sched} - |L|}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-01210843.png[/image_path][/image-1] If eligible, schedule the UE: Update: * N_PRB = min(N_PRB + RBest_u, n x N_BW) * L = L ∪ {u} * S = S \ {u} * Else, skip u and continue to the next UE. * If any of the following conditions are met, terminate the loop: \+ No more PRBs are available N_PRB >= n x N_BW \+ All UEs in S have been visited * If |L| < N_sched: * Iterate through Candidate Set 2 again and select additional non-GBR UEs based on TD metric priority. \+ Continue this process until: |L| = N_sched Note: The value RBest_u (estimated PRBs required by UE u)[Before PR873563] is defined in spec see [11201008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). [PR873563] Instead of using rrmUlProposedMcs (defined in [11201008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) ), rrmAvgSpectralEffUlSlot (defined in [11212124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw_g0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) will be used to determine RB_est. [End PR873563] [End CB014044-M]

---

###### 3.30.4.8.1.0-22 Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 Friend User prioritization in TD scheduler during the to-be-scheduled list creation (ID: 13343526)

**3.30.4.8.1.0-22.0-1**  (ID: `13343527`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 TD metric modification for friends

---

**3.30.4.8.1.0-22.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13343528`)

Decision on which UEs are to be forwarded to FD scheduler from Candidate set 2 When NRCELL.actBundlePreviousPairedUes is set to 'disabled' or 'DL' the following procedure shall be skipped.

gNB-DU shall perform the below steps for each of the friends added from the Friend Lists (FLs) in the CS1 list, such that they can be selected together in the to-be-scheduled list of users for the current slot.

  * Sum the individual TD metrics of each initial transmission user belonging to the same traffic priority class in a FL that is selected to be included in the CS1 list. This gives the TD metric of the FL.
  * For each initial transmission UE in a FL, belonging to the same traffic priority class, their TD metric is updated to the TD metric of the FL it belongs, from the above step. The aggregated TD metric shall make it possible for the TD-scheduler to pick the friend users together in to-be-scheduled list of users (tbsList), thus allowing to be “pooled” together.
  * When one UE belongs to more than one “FL”, it’s TD metric shall be updated to the max of the TD metrics of each FL it belongs to.



For the TD metric of the UEs please refer to [11195815:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). For the Friend List description and how friends are added and removed from the lists refer to [13340154](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_E5zl4dv3EfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

Note: The TD metric modification/improvement for the friend users described above shall be performed solely for the purpose of increasing the possibility to select the friends (if already present in the CS1 list) together in the CS2/to-be-scheduled list. This improved TD metric thus shall be maintained temporarily during the process of constructing the CS2/to-be-scheduled list. If found that any of these friend user which were MU eligible based on the recent pairing history is deemed MU ineligible (based on the conditions defined in [11146147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksCvavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for MU pairing in the current slot, then that UEs TD metric shall fall back to its original value.

---

#### 3.30.4.9 Restriction of other scheduling on 5GTF-subframes/NR-Slots with Msg3 transmission

### 3.30.4.10 Pause or skip in time domain scheduling

#### 3.30.4.10.0-1 Pause or skip in time domain scheduling Pause in scheduling due to CRC NACK flood (ID: 11197752)

**3.30.4.10.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11197821`)

Pause or skip in time domain scheduling The UL time domain scheduler shall exclude the carrier of the UE from scheduling for that the UL link adaptation provides the request for pausing in scheduling by means of the parameter rrmPauseUeInUlScheduling (see 5G_UP_ALG_3158_replaced([11138143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdglfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The carrier of the UE for that the UL link adaptation requests the pause in scheduling shall come back periodically into scheduling set for one initial UL transmission to allow the evaluation of the current radio link quality with the aid of the CRC feedback. The period the certain carrier of the UE shall be scheduled for one initial UL transmission shall be determined by the R&D parameter rdPeriodUlScheduling (see 5G_L2_1176_replaced([11182848](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF4vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [PR811432] and for traffic with request class anticipatedUlAugSrb shall be determined by rdPeriodUlSchedulingAugSrb (5G_L2_23684_replaced([11183300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iJRfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the PCell. R&D parameter [End PR811432]rdPeriodUlSchedulingSCell (see 5G_L2_1177_replaced([11182853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF4_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [PR811432]shall be considered[End PR811432] for the SCell. HARQ retransmissions shall be scheduled similarly to UEs with regular air link conditions if the UL scheduler receive a CRC NACK feedback as response to the periodically scheduled initial UL transmission.

---

#### 3.30.4.10.0-2 Pause or skip in time domain scheduling Pause in scheduling for FR1 UL CA UE on PCell in Ul overlapping slots due to too low split TX power (ID: 11197837)

**3.30.4.10.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11197842`)

Pause or skip in time domain scheduling In case the split TX power is too low on Pcell for a UE configured with FDD + FR1 TDD UL CA according to 5G_UP_ALG_10921_replaced([11119951](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the UL time domain scheduler shall exclude the UE on PCell in Ul overlapping slots until Ul scheduling is resumed on Pcell in Ul overlapping slots according to 5G_UP_ALG_10923_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 5G_UP_ALG_10919_replaced([11119940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Ul overlapping slot definition refers to 5G_UP_ALG_10871_replaced([11119486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.30.4.10.0-3 Pause or skip in time domain scheduling Skip in scheduling for FR1 UL CA UE on FDD serving cell in Ul overlapping slots (ID: 11197899)

**3.30.4.10.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `13464048`)

Pause or skip in time domain scheduling For FR1 R15 UL CA UE configured with TDD PCell and FDD SCell which is not in the allow-list (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_25188_replaced([11208767](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwwQQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and is not configured with ~~simultaneousPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17~~ parallelTxPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17 , the UL time domain scheduler shall exclude the UE on FDD SCell in every Ul overlapping slots. UL CA 2CCs with 3Tx shall also follow this requirement.

For FR1 UL Tx switching option 1 (switchedUL) UE with TDD as PCell and FDD as UL SCell **[CB014067-C] or TDD PCell + TDD SCell + FDD SCell**[End CB014067-C], the UL time domain scheduler shall exclude the UE on FDD SCell in every Ul overlapping slots.****

For FR1 UL Tx switching option 1 (switchedUL) UE with FDD as PCell and TDD as UL SCell, the UL time domain scheduler shall exclude the UE on FDD PCell in every Ul overlapping slots if UL buffer split status started (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_9496_replaced([11122831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqgeXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Ul overlapping slot definition refers to 5G_UP_ALG_10871_replaced([11119486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.30.4.11 Search space (Type1-PDCCH) availability check

#### 3.30.4.11.0-1 Search space (Type1-PDCCH) availability check Type1-PDCCH availability check (ID: 11198055)

**3.30.4.11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11198108`)

Search space (Type1-PDCCH) availability check For the retransmission(s) of the RA-Msg3, the L2-PS checks the uplink availability of the Type1-PDCCH for **[Before PR878503] the current slot**[PR878503] all the slots pdcchGrantSlots (as defined in [11199201](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Qmu44PvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) **[End PR878503] in the active PDCCH slot pattern:** ****

 If the Type1-PDCCH is not available [PR878503] in any of the slots [End PR878503], the RNTI (TC-RNTI) associated to the Random Access procedure is not included in the CS2 list.

 If the Type1-PDCCH is available, the RNTI is added in the CS2 list.

With or without DSS, the 5G-L2-PS must not add the TC-RNTI in the CS2 list if the Type1-PDCCH is not allowed in the slot according to its configuration parameters (periodicity, slot offset and duration) as defined in the 5G_UP_ALG_4022_replaced([11168667](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktx0wfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: as this check is relevant for TC-RNTI only, it is relevant for CBRA only. The check for Type1-PDCCH availability is not to be executed in case of CFRA, because there is no TC-RNTI in this procedure (it is the C-RNTI).

---

### 3.30.4.12 VoNR Scheduling

#### 3.30.4.12.0-1 VoNR Scheduling VoNR Scheduling Overview (ID: 13392088)

**3.30.4.12.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13383869`)

VoNR Scheduling gNB shall schedule [CB014699-B] critical and [End CB014699-B] non-critical VoNR as described in this chapter.

---

#### 3.30.4.12.1 Scheduling Voice Packets

##### 3.30.4.12.1.0-1 Scheduling Voice Packets Scheduling uplink VoIP when SR period is 20ms or less (ID: 11198145)

**3.30.4.12.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11198236`)

Scheduling Voice Packets This requirement specifies how the scheduler will update the UE's [Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bufferSize when the SR period is 20ms or less.

If the UE's SR periodicity is 20ms or less then:

[Before CB008118-A]

If (NRCELL.actUlAudioGapReduction == True) AND (time since last SR received is greater than 200ms) AND (UE’s 5QI1 bufferSize == 0) then

set the UE’s 5QI1 bufferSize to rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) 

Else

When an SR is received for a UE with a 5QI1 bearer

If the UE’s 5QI1 bufferSize == 0 then

set the UE’s 5QI1 bufferSize to rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Else // UE’s 5QI1 bufferSize != 0

[CB008118-A]

If (NRCELL.actUlAudioGapReduction == True) AND (time since last SR received is greater than 200ms) AND (UE’s 5QI1/65/66 bufferSize == 0) then

set the UE’s 5QI1/65/66 bufferSize to rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) 

Else

When an SR is received for a UE with a 5QI1/65/66 bearer

If the UE’s 5QI1/65/66 bufferSize == 0 then

set the UE’s 5QI1/65/66 bufferSize to rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Else // UE’s 5QI1/65/66 bufferSize != 0

[End CB008118-A]

ignore the SR. 

Endif

Endif

Note 1 – An SR should not be received when there is data in the [Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] buffer. However if the SR periodicity is very short and a [Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] UL grant was not sent since the last SR was sent the UE will send another SR.

Note 2 - In case SR transmissions are being lost a VoNR proactive grant will be triggered if the time since the last SR reception was greater than 200ms. If the UE is in the silent period the UE should send an SR every 160ms. Therefore the time before triggering the proactive grant should be longer than the typical SR transmission interval during the VoIP silence period.

---

##### 3.30.4.12.1.0-2 Scheduling Voice Packets Scheduling uplink VoIP when SR period is greater than 20ms (ID: 11198250)

**3.30.4.12.1.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11198348`)

Scheduling Voice Packets This requirement specifies how the scheduler will update the UE's **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize when the SR period is greater than 20ms.** ****

Scheduling VoIP when the UE’s SR period is greater than 20ms relies on the scheduler periodically increasing the uplink **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize to ensure that UL grants are scheduled and the VoIP packets are transmitted by the UE in a timely manner. The second key aspect of this algorithm is being able to detect the transition between talk periods when VoIP packets are sent approximately every 20ms and silent periods when SID packets are sent approximately every 160ms. During talk periods the scheduler will update the**[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1 **[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66**[End CB008118-A] buffer every 20ms and will use proactive grants to retrieve the voice packets. During silent periods the scheduler will send uplink grants every 160ms in response to SRs. The algorithm is as follows:**_*_** *****

If **the UE’s SR period is greater than 20ms then:**

When the first SR is received from the UE after the **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bearer is established do:** ****

**[CB009849-D]**

If NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to FALSE, **[End CB009849-D]**

set the UE’s **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize to rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

set the first VoIP Count Down (VCD) timer length to (20 - 6)ms and start timer, this is equivalent to starting the timer 6ms prior to the the slot that L2-PS would receive the SR (see Note 1)

**[CB009849-D]**

Else

set the UE’s **[Before CB008118-C] 5QI-1**[CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-C] bufferSize to 2 * rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

set the first VoIP Count Down (VCD) timer length to (40 - 2)ms and start timer, this is equivalent to starting the timer 2ms prior to the the slot that L2-PS would receive the SR (see Note 3) **[End CB009849-D]**

End

Do while the **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bearer is present:** ****

When the VCD timer expires

**[ Before CB008118-A]**

set VCD timer to the value returned by voipTimerValue algorithm and restart timer

If ((rrmVoipState == 'silent') and (UE does not have a 5QI-2 bearer) and ((NRCELL.actUlAudioGapReduction == False) OR ((NRCELL.actUlAudioGapReduction == True) and (time since last SR received is less than 200ms))) then

do nothing

Else If (5QI-1 bufferSize == 0) OR ((5QI-1 bufferSize <= 2000) and (NRCELL.actVoipPacketAggr == False))

**[CB008118-A]**

set VCD timer to the value returned by voipTimerValue algorithm and restart timer

If ((rrmVoipState == 'silent') and (UE does not have a 5QI-2/67 bearer) and ((NRCELL.actUlAudioGapReduction == False) OR ((NRCELL.actUlAudioGapReduction == True) and (time since last SR received is less than 200ms))) then

do nothing

Else If (5QI-1/65/66 bufferSize == 0) OR ((5QI-1/65/66 bufferSize <= 2000) and (NRCELL.actVoipPacketAggr == False [PR840550] **[CB009849-D]or NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrantis set to TRUE**[End CB009849-D] [End PR840550]))****

**[End CB008118-A]**

then

**[CB009849-D]**

If NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrantis set to FALSE,**[End CB009849-D]**

increase the**[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize by rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

**[CB009849-D]**

Else

increase the **[Before CB008118-C] 5QI-1**[CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-C] bufferSize by 2 * rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**[End CB009849-D]**_*_** *

Else do nothing

End // timer expires

When an SR is received (see Note 2)

If (UE’s **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize == 0) AND** ****

**((NRCELL.actUlAudioGapReduction == False [PR834843] AND NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to FALSE [End PR834843])**

OR ((NRCELL.actUlAudioGapReduction == True [PR834843] OR NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to TRUE [End PR834843])

AND (time since last UL grant sent is > 2ms))) then

**[CB009849-D]**

If NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to FALSE **[End CB009849-D]**

increase the UE’s **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize by rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

**[CB009849-D]**

Else

increase the UE’s **[Before CB008118-C] 5QI-1**[CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-C] bufferSize by 2 * rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**[End CB009849-D]**_*_** *

Else the scheduler should discard the SR and act as if the SR was not received

End If

If the UE’s **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize != 0 then ignore the SR.** ****

[Before PR834843]**[CB009849-D]**

If (NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to TRUE,

Ignore the SRs that are received less than 3 slots after a VoNR proactive grant is sent to the UE.**[End CB009849-D] [End PR834843]**

End // SR received

End // Do while **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bearer is present** ****

Endif // SR period > 20ms 

voipTimerValue ():

if rrmVoipState (see 5G_UP_ALG_4907_replaced([11198450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) == 'silent' then

if UE has **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-2**[CB008118-A] 5QI2/67 **[End CB008118-A] bearer return 40ms // 40ms proactive grants needed to retrieve video packets while voice is in silent period** ****

Else return 160ms

Endif

Else

**[CB009849-D]**

If NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to FALSE **[End CB009849-D]**

return 20ms // talk period

**_*[CB009849-D]_** *

Else

return 40ms // talk period

Endif **[EndCB009849-D]**

Endif

Note 1: By starting the VCD timer 6ms prior to the SR slot ensures that the VCD timer will expire just prior to the start of the DRX onDuration given that the SR slot is located within the onDuration which typically has a length of 6ms for VoNR DRX . This is important to ensure that the **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize is updated prior to the start of the onDuration so that first grant in the onDuration is based on an updated bufferSize.** ****

Note 2: When the UE’s bufferSize is being updated by the scheduler every 20ms during a talk period the UE should not have to send an SR.**However if the UE's SR slot is in the first half of the DRX onDuration a race condition can occur in which the VoNR proactive grant is sent at the same time the UE sends an SR. When this occurs a redundant PUSCH transmission can occur. The first transmission, due to the VoNR proactive grant, contains the voice data. The second transmission, due to the SR grant, will contain all padding bytes which is a waste of resources. To prevent this, a check is made to see if a proactive grant was sent to the UE just prior to the gNB receiving the SR. If yes, the SR will be ignored. The key time that an SR is required from a UE is when the UE is in a silence period. During silence periods proactive grants are not sent. Uplink grants are sent in response to SRs. One exception is when an SR has not been received for greater than 200ms in the silence period. In this case it is assumed that the SR detection was missed by the gNB and a proactive grant will be sent to the UE.**

**[CB009849-D]**

Note 3: When NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to TRUE, Proactive grant timer should expire 2ms before the SR opportunity, not 6ms prior to SR opportunity. This is to reduce the occurrence of Voice packet generation at the UE between the proactive grant slot and the UE’s SR opportunity, causing an additional grant to be sent to retrieve the voice data.

**[End CB009849-D]**

---

##### 3.30.4.12.1.0-3 Scheduling Voice Packets Scheduling uplink VoIP when UE is configured with a shared SR resource (ID: 11198358)

**3.30.4.12.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11198384`)

Scheduling Voice Packets This requirement specifies how the scheduler will update the UE's **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize when the UE is configured with shared SR resource. For the definition of shared resource for SR, please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4602_replaced([11187378](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvazhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_7088_replaced([11278984](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpTbkfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).******

Similar to case of scheduling VoIP when the UE’s SR period is greater than 20ms, scheduling an UE here relies on the scheduler periodically increasing the uplink **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize to ensure that UL grants are scheduled and the VoIP packets are transmitted by the UE in a timely manner. This is due to the case that for an UE configured with a shared SR, its SR will only be detected if it is multiplexed with pCSI.** ****

The algorithm also enables the detection of the transition between talk periods when VoIP packets are sent approximately every 20ms and silent periods when SID packets are sent approximately every 160ms. During talk periods the scheduler will update the **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] buffer every 20ms and will use proactive grants to retrieve the voice packets. During silent periods the scheduler will update the**[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1 **[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66**[End CB008118-A] buffer every 40ms (see Note 3). The algorithm is as follows:**_*_** *****

Algorithm for updating bufferSize

If the UE is configured with a shared SR resource, then:

When **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bearer is established:** ****

{

**_*[CB009849-D]_** *

If NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to FALSE, **[End CB009849-D]**

Set the UE’s **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize to rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

Set the first VoIP Count Down (VCD) timer length to 20ms and start timer

**_*[CB009849-D]_** *

Else

Set the UE’s **[Before CB008118-C] 5QI-1**[CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-C] bufferSize to 2 * rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

Set the first VoIP Count Down (VCD) timer length to 40ms and start timer **[End CB009849-D]**

}

When DRX profile is configured for the UE with **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bearer:** ****

{

Stop VCD timer

When the UE enters the DRX On Duration do: (see Note 1)

{

**_*[CB009849-D]_** *

If NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to FALSE, **[End CB009849-D]**

set the UE’s **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A]bufferSize to rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

set VoIP Count Down (VCD) timer length to (20 - 2)ms and start timer, this is same as starting the timer 2ms prior to the start of the DRX On Duration (see Note 1)

**_*[CB009849-D]_** *

Else

set the UE’s **[Before CB008118-C] 5QI-1**[CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-C] bufferSize to 2 _rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))_*****

set VoIP Count Down (VCD) timer length to (40 - 2)ms and start timer, ~~Rationale: this is same as starting the timer 2ms prior to the slot that L2-PS would receive the SR.~~ **[End CB009849-D]**

}

}

Do while the **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bearer is present:** ****

When the VCD timer expires

set VCD timer to the value returned by voipTimerValue algorithm and restart timer

**[Before CB008118-A]**

If (5QI-1 bufferSize == 0) OR ((5QI-1 bufferSize <= 2000) and (NRCELL.actVoipPacketAggr==False))

**_*[CB008118-A]_** *

If (5QI1/65/66 bufferSize == 0) OR ((5QI1/65/66 bufferSize <= 2000) and (NRCELL.actVoipPacketAggr==False [PR840550] **[CB009849-D]or NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to TRUE**[End CB009849-D][End PR840550]))****

**[End CB008118-A]**

**_*[CB009849-D]_** *

If NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to FALSE,**[End CB009849-D]**

increase the **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A]bufferSize by rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

**_*[CB009849-D]_** *

Else

increase the **[Before CB008118-C] 5QI-1**[CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-C] bufferSize by 2 * rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

**[End CB009849-D]**

Else

do nothing

End // _timer expires_

When an SR is received (see note 2)

If the UE’s **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize == 0 [PR834843]AND** ****

((NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant == False) OR ((NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant == True) AND (time since last UL grant sent is > 2ms))) [End PR834843]

**_*[CB009849-D]_** *

If NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to FALSE,**[End CB009849-D]**

increase the **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize by rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

**_*[CB009849-D]_** *

Else

increase the **[Before CB008118-C] 5QI-1**[CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-C] bufferSize by 2 * rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))******

then ignore the SR

End // _SR received_

If (NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to TRUE,

Ignore the SRs that are received less than 3 slots after a VoNR proactive grant is sent to the UE.

**[End CB009849-D] [End PR834843**]****

End // _Do while 5QI1 bearer is present_

End If //_UE is configured with a shared SR resource_

Algorithm for calculating VCD Timer when UE is configured with a shared SR Resource

voipTimerValue ()

if rrmVoipState (see 5G_UP_ALG_4907_replaced([11198450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) == 'silent'

return 40ms **//40ms proactive grants needed to retrieve SID frames when UE does not have a dedicated SR resource (see Note 3)**

Else

**[CB009849-D]**

If NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to FALSE, OR (NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is set to TRUE AND **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] DRB is not configured for the UE)**[End CB009849-D]**_*_** *

return 20ms // talk period

**_*[CB009849-D]_** *

Else

return 40ms // talk period

Endif **[End CB009849-D]**

Endif

**Additional Notes**

Note 1: The proactive grants are aligned with the DRX On Duration. If the **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] buffer size is not updated just prior to the start of the DRX On Duration, the grant size may not be sufficient to empty the UE's buffer and a second grant may be required. This is important to ensure that the**[Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1 **[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66**[End CB008118-A] bufferSize is updated prior to the start of the On Duration so that first grant in the On Duration is based on an updated bufferSize.**_*_** *****

Note 2: When the UE’s bufferSize is being updated by the scheduler every 20ms during a talk period via proactive grants it is unlikely that the UE will need to send an SR. The key time that an SR will be sent is when the UE is in a silence period. During silence periods proactive grants are not sent. 

Note 3: Even though a proactive grant every 160ms is sufficient to retrieve SID frames during a silence period, the proactive grants need to be sent every 40ms to quickly detect the UE's transition from silence to talk. Sending proactive grants this often during the silence period is not efficient but sending proactive grants every 160ms could result in audio gaps of 160ms when the UE transitions from silence to talk.

---

##### 3.30.4.12.1.0-4 Scheduling Voice Packets Voice Packet Size Determination (ID: 11198413)

**3.30.4.12.1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11198430`)

Scheduling Voice Packets This requirement specifies how to determine the VoIP packet size the scheduler will use when updating uplink [Before CB008118-A] 5QI-1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bufferSize.

The calculation of the uplink voice packet size depends on the VoIP state (see 5G_UP_ALG_4907_replaced([11198450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The following algorithm shall be used:

If NRCELL.actAdaptVoipTbs == True then

If rrmVoipState == 'talk' or 'unknown' (see 5G_UP_ALG_4907_replaced([11198450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmVoipPacketSize = rrmAdaptVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4917_replaced([11198680](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJzWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Else // rrmVoipState == ‘silence’ then 

If NRBTS.actRohc == True

rrmVoipPacketSize = 18 bytes (see Note 1)

Else // NRBTS.actRohc == False

rrmVoipPacketSize = 75 bytes (see Note 2)

Endif

Else // NRCELL.actAdaptVoipTbs == False

rrmVoipPacketSize = NRBTS.ulsVoipPacketSize (configurable parameter)

Endif

Note 1: 18 bytes is the size of a RoHC compressed SID frame over IPv6. The size includes 8 bytes for RTP/UDP/IP/PDCP/RLC/MAC headers, 5 bytes for the SID payload, and 5 bytes for MAC PHR and BSR control elements.

Note 2: 75 bytes is the size of a SID frame over IPv6. The size includes 65 bytes for AMR/RTP/UDP/IP/PDCP/RLC/MAC headers, 5 bytes for the SID payload, and 5 bytes for MAC PHR and BSR control elements.

---

##### 3.30.4.12.1.0-5 Scheduling Voice Packets VoIP State Determination (ID: 11198450)

**3.30.4.12.1.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `12802765`)

Scheduling Voice Packets This requirement specifies how to determine whether a VoNR UE is in a talk or silent state.

The VoIP state (talk, silent, unknown) is an input to the VoIP packet size calculation (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and VoIP proactive grant timing (see 5G_UP_ALG_4901_replaced([11198250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwIlOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The following algorithm shall be used to determine the VoIP state.

When the 5QI1/65/66 bearer is added rrmVoipState shall be set to 'unknown'. The state will remain 'unknown' until conditions are met to change the state to 'talk' or 'silent'. Once changed to 'talk' or 'silent' the state shall not return to 'unknown'.

**[Before 05288264]**

If the interval between receiving two VoIP packets is greater than 100ms or **[PR848873] if UE having 5QI-65/66 (not having 5QI-1) received zero BSR greater than 100ms**[End PR848873],then****

**[05288264]**

If (NRBTS/NRCELL.actInternalImprovements1 (Bit0) == TRUE and If no UL activity for 5QI-1/65/66 bearer for greater than 100ms)  
or (if NRBTS/NRCELL.actInternalImprovements1 (Bit0) == FALSE and (If the interval between receiving two VoIP packets is greater  
than 100ms or if UE having 5QI-65/66 (not having 5QI-1) received zero BSR greater  
than 100ms )), then

**[End 05288264]**

rrmVoipState = 'silent'

Else // interval is less than or equal to 100ms

rrmVoipState = 'talk'

Endif

Note - The time between VoIP packets means the time between 5QI1/65/66 RLC SDUs being received at the gNB. If an RLC SDU is segmented the receive time of the first segment received should be used. The times associated with reception of subsequent segments should be ignored.

**[05288264]**

Note – no UL activity for for 5QI-1/65/66 bearer means no RLC SDUs (or RLC SDU segments)  
received at the gNB

**[End 05288264]**

---

#### 3.30.4.12.2 Packet Aggregation

##### 3.30.4.12.2.0-1 Packet Aggregation UL VoIP Packet Aggregation when SR period is 20ms or Less (ID: 11198498)

**3.30.4.12.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11198614`)

Packet Aggregation This requirement specifies how the scheduler will aggregate voice packets when the SR period is 20ms or less.

Note that this requirement is not applicable for a UE that is configured with a shared SR, irrespective of the UE’s SR period. For UE’s configured with a shared SR, please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4912_replaced([11198620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The purpose of uplink VoIP packet aggregation is to schedule two uplink packets at one time when the cell is highly loaded to conserve PDCCH resources. Note that the UE must be in good RF to support packet aggregation. The following is the uplink packet aggregation algorithm when the Scheduling Request (SR) periodicity is 20ms or less.

While (NRCELL.actVoipPacketAggr == True) AND (UE’s SR periodicity <= 20ms) AND (UE’s rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr > NRBTS.ulVoipPacketAggrSinrThreshold) AND (rrmVoipState == 'talk' OR 'unknown') AND (UE has one [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bearer) AND (UE is not in Slot Aggregation On State), Do: 

where:

rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmVoipState is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4907_replaced([11198450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Slot Aggregation On State is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_19242_replaced([11204811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

actVoipPacketAggr, ulVoipPacketAggrSinrThreshold are NIDD parameters and received by PsCell_CellSetupReq/ PsCell_CellReconfigurationReq message. 

1) When an SR is received and the [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bufferSize == 0 do the following in the same slot that the SR was received:

\- start the packet aggregation (PA) timer with a time of 20ms

\- increase the UE’s [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bufferSize by rrmVoipPacketSize (see 5G_UP_ALG_4904_replaced([11198413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- do not add an entry to the UE's SR queue. The SR is only used to trigger the next iteration of packet aggregation scheduling.

2) While the PA timer is running the [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] data shall be classified as deprioritizedVoIP which has lower priority than non-GBR data. This will ensure that the VoIP data will not be scheduled if there is data pending for other UEs allowing packet aggregation to occur.

3) If [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] data is scheduled when the priority is deprioritizedVoip and a zero BSR is received (see Note 1) stop the PA timer and return to step 1.

4) If an SR is received when the PA timer is running do not increase the [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bufferSize or add an entry to the UE’s SR queue. 

5) When the PA timer expires, increase the UE’s priority to voipTraffic priority and increase the UE’s [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bufferSize by rrmVoipPacketSize. The priority shall remain as voipTraffic until a zero BSR is received. 

6) When a zero BSR is received do:

If all of the conditions in the While statement are True then return to step 1

Else process uplink voice packets without applying packet aggregation until all of While conditions are True at which time packet aggregation processing will begin again with Step 1.

End While

Note 1: A zero BSR is a BSR that indicates the LCG that contains [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] has zero data. To ensure that the UE’s 5QI1 buffer is emptied and that the UE will send an SR the next time [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] data arrives in its buffer the gNB must receive a [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] zero BSR from the UE before returning to step 1 of the algorithm. This includes the case in which the UE empties its [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] buffer with the PUSCH transmission but there is no room in the transmission for a BSR. In this case the scheduler must send another uplink grant to the UE to receive the zero BSR. 

Note 2: The above algorithm is independent of the UE’s DRX configuration. If DRX is configured, when the UE is in the DRX active state the priority of the VoIP data will be as defined in this requirement.

---

##### 3.30.4.12.2.0-2 Packet Aggregation UL VoIP Packet Aggregation when SR period is greater than 20ms (ID: 11198620)

**3.30.4.12.2.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11198641`)

Packet Aggregation This requirement specifies how the scheduler will aggregate voice packets when the UE's SR period is greater than 20ms or for a UE that is configured with a shared SR, irrespective of the UE's SR period.

The purpose of uplink VoIP packet aggregation is to schedule two uplink packets at one time when the cell is highly loaded to conserve PDCCH resources. Note that the UE must be in good RF to support packet aggregation. The following is the uplink packet aggregation algorithm when the Scheduling Request (SR) periodicity is greater than 20ms or when it is configured with a shared SR resource. In both these cases, the scheduler relies on proactive grants to ensure that the VoIP transmissions are made in a timely manner (see 5G_UP_ALG_4901_replaced([11198250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwIlOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_18130_replaced([11198358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwIlPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). As such this algorithm will be based on proactive grants, not SRs received from the UE.

While (NRCELL.actVoipPacketAggr equal to TRUE [PR840550]AND NRCELL.actVonr40msProGrant is equal to FALSE[End PR840550]) AND**((UE's SR periodicity > 20ms) OR (UE is configured with a shared SR)) AND (UE’s rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr > NRBTS.ulVoipPacketAggrSinrThreshold) AND (rrmVoipState = 'talk' OR 'unknown') AND (UE has one **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 **[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66**[End CB008118-A] bearer) AND (UE is not in Slot Aggregation On State) **do:****_*_** *

where:

rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmVoipState is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4907_replaced([11198450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**Slot Aggregation On State is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_19242_replaced([11204811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**

actVoipPacketAggr, ulVoipPacketAggrSinrThreshold are NIDD parameters and received by PsCell_CellSetupReq/ PsCell_CellReconfigurationReq message.

1) When the next **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] proactive grant is made (see Note 1) start the packet aggregation (PA) timer with a time of 20ms in the same slot that the proactive grant was made.** ****

2) While the PA timer is running the **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] data shall be classified as deprioritizedVoIP which has lower priority than non-GBR data. This will ensure that the VoIP data will not be scheduled if there is data pending for other UEs allowing packet aggregation to occur.** ****

3) If **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] data is scheduled when the priority is deprioritizedVoip and a zero BSR is received (see Note 2) stop the PA timer and return to step 1.** ****

4) If an SR is received when the PA timer is running do not increase the **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize or add an entry to UE’s SR queue.** ****

5) When the PA timer expires, increase the UE’s priority to voipTraffic priority and increase the UE’s **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] bufferSize by rrmVoipPacketSize. The priority shall remain as voipTraffic until a zero BSR is received.** ****

6) When a zero BSR is received do:

If all of the conditions in the While statement are True then return to step 1

Else process uplink voice packets without applying packet aggregation until all of While conditions are True at which time packet aggregation processing will begin again with Step 1.

End While

Note 1: A **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] proactive grant is when the scheduler updates the 5QI1 bufferSize by by rrmVoipPacketSize. (see 5G_UP_ALG_4901_replaced([11198250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwIlOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_18130_replaced([11198358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwIlPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).******

Note 2: A zero BSR is a BSR that indicates the LCG that contains **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] has zero data. To ensure that the UE’s**[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 **[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66**[End CB008118-A] buffer is emptied and that the UE will send an SR the next time **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] data arrives in its buffer the gNB must receive a**[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 **[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66**[End CB008118-A] zero BSR from the UE before returning to step 1 of the algorithm. This includes the case in which the UE empties its **[Before CB008118-A] 5QI1**[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 **[End CB008118-A] buffer with the PUSCH transmission but there is no room in the transmission for a BSR. In this case the scheduler must send another uplink grant to the UE to receive the zero BSR.****_*_** **_*_** **_*_** **_*_**
