#### 3.30.4.12.3 Adaptive Voice TBS Determination

##### 3.30.4.12.3.0-1 Adaptive Voice TBS Determination VoIP Effective Bit Rate Determination (ID: 11198652)

**3.30.4.12.3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11198673`)

Adaptive Voice TBS Determination 11198673: Adaptive Voice TBS Determination This requirement specifies how the scheduler detemines a UE's estimated voice data rate.

Adaptive VoIP TBS adapts the voice packet size to the source data rate of the audio codec rather than the configured value for NRBTS.ulsVoipPacketSize. The configured value needs to be large enough for the worst-case codec that may be used for VoIP. This may be too large for some codecs. In addition, it is possible for codecs to lower or increase the codec rate during a call. Adapting the uplink voice packet size used for uplink grants to the data rate in use makes a more efficient use of radio resources.

When NRCELL.actAdaptVoipTbs = true L2-PS will derive the voice packet size using the measured [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] data rate. The measured data rate is called [Before CB008118-A] Effective Bit Rate (EBR5qi1) [CB008118-A] Effective Bit Rate (EBR5qi1/65/66) [End CB008118-A]. Using the Effective Bit Rate will reduce padding bytes and improve call quality. The following defines the [Before CB008118-A] EBR5qi1 [CB008118-A] EBR5qi1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] algorithm. 5G_UP_ALG_4917_replaced([11198680](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJzWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) defines the algorithm to convert EBR5qi1 to a voice packet size. [CB008118-A] Note: The same algorithm under 5G_UP_ALG_4917_replaced([11198680](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJzWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is equally applied to convert EBR5qi65/66 to a voice packet size [End CB008118-A].

[Before CB008118-A]

The “Effective Bit Rate (EBR5qi1)” of 5QI-1 bearers is calculated as follows:

[CB008118-A]

The “Effective Bit Rate (EBR5qi1/65/66)” of 5QI-1/65/66 bearers is calculated as follows:

[End CB008118-A]

For every 40 msec period, p, L2-PS will calculate the number of received bits of [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 traffic, B5QI1(p) [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 traffic, B5QI1/65/66(p) [End CB008118-A].

[Before CB008118-A]

[image-0][image_desc]B_{5Q11}(p) = \sum_{s} B_{ul,i,j}(s)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15548.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

[CB008118-A]

B5QI1/65/66(p) = [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. It shows a summation symbol ($\sum$) with the index 's' below it. To the right of the summation symbol, there is a term denoted as $B_{ul,i,j}(s)$.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15548.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

[End CB008118-A]

EBRMAC(p) = Alpha * B5QI1(p) * 25 + (1 - Alpha) * EBRMAC(p-1)

[Before CB008118-A]

EBR5QI1(p) = (1 + rdUlVoipPktLossRate) * EBRMAC(p)

[CB008118-A]

EBR5QI1/65/66(p) = (1 + rdUlVoipPktLossRate) * EBRMAC(p)

[End CB008118-A]

Where

p: the 40 msec period

i: index of the UE

[Before CB008118-A]

j: index of the 5QI1 LC

[CB008118-A]

j: index of the 5QI1/65/66 LC

[End CB008118-A]

s: index of the slot in the 40 msec period

[Before CB008118-A]

Bul,i,j(s): number of received bits in LC j, for UE i, slot s. (the 5QI1 traffic)

[CB008118-A]

Bul,i,j(s): number of received bits in LC j, for UE i, slot s. (the 5QI1/65/66 traffic)

[End CB008118-A]

rdUlVoipPktLossRate: R&D parameter that specifies uplink RTP target packet loss rate (see 5G_UP_ALG_33209_replaced([11209727](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw1v0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Alpha = rdAdaptVoipTbsAvgConst / 1000: is used as a filter, 1000 is the maximum value of rdAdaptVoipTbsAvgConst (see 5G_UP_ALG_33217_replaced([11209777](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw1v2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note: Multiplying by 25 converts the bits received in a 40 msec period to the bits received in a second.

The calculation must consider

  * During talk periods, the measured B5QI1(p) is updated for each 40 msec period. It is set to the number of successfully received VoNR bits without MAC layer overhead (MAC subheaders and MAC-C elements). If no [Before CB008118-A]5QI1[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] data is received in a slot, nothing is added to B5QI1(p).
  * [Before CB008118-A] EBR5QI1 [CB008118-A] EBR5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] is calculated during talk periods only. During this time, the [Before CB008118-A]5QI1[CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] data carries voice payload, but no silence (SID) frames. When the voice state equals "silence" or "unknown" the [Before CB008118-A] EBR5QI1 [CB008118-A] EBR5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] is not updated. During a talk period if [Before CB008118-A] B5QI1(p) [CB008118-A] B5QI1/65/66(p) [End CB008118-A]equals zero at the end of the 40ms period that interval shall be skipped, i.e. EBR5QI1 shall not be updated for that interval.
  * After a silence period the initial value of [Before CB008118-A] EBR5QI1 [CB008118-A] EBR5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] for the subsequent talk period is the calculated value from the previous active talk period.
  * Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4907_replaced([11198450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJMRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of talk and silent periods.
  * The initial value of [Before CB008118-A] EBR5QI1 after 5QI1 bearer establishment or modification is set to the uplink GBR value from the NGAP signaling message that established or modified the 5QI1 flow [CB008118-A] EBR5QI1/65/66 after 5QI1/65/66 bearer establishment or modification is set to the uplink GBR value from the NGAP signaling message that established or modified the 5QI/65/66 flow [End CB008118-A]. The NGAP GBR values refer to 5G_CP_IF_NTW_1854_replaced([11355178](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__A7BVPvmEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (GBR QoS Flow Information).



With emergency call support it is possible for one UE to have two 5QI1 bearers (normal call and emergency call). L2-PS shall use the following rules to initialize EBR5QI1 when two 5QI1 bearers are present:

 When a second 5QI1 bearer is added reset EBR5QI1 to the GBR value associated with the newly added bearer. 

 When two 5QI1 bearers are added or modified at the same time reset EBR5QI1 to the highest GBR value of the added / modified 5QI1 bearers.

 When two 5QI1 bearers are present and there is a GBR modify request for one of the bearers reset EBR5QI1 to the modified GBR value.

 When a 5QI1 bearer is released, reset EBR5QI1 to the GBR value of the remaining 5QI1 bearer

Note that the GBR values refer to 5G_CP_IF_NTW_1854_replaced([11355178](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__A7BVPvmEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (GBR QoS Flow Information).

[CB008118-A] With MC-Voice (5QI 65/66), It is possible for one UE to have one 5QI1 bearer(normal or emergency call) and one 5QI65 or 5QI66 bearers. L2-PS shall use the following rules to initialize EBR5QI1/65/66 voice bearer, when two bearers are present: 

  * When a 5QI1/65/66 bearer is added as a second bearer, reset EBR5QI1/65/66 to the GBR value associated with the newly added bearer.
  * When 5QI1 and 5QI65 or 5QI66 bearers are added or modified at the same time reset EBR5QI1/65/66 to the highest GBR value of the added / modified 5QI1/65/66 bearers.
  * When one 5QI1 and one 5QI65 or 5QI66 bearers are present and there is a GBR modify request for one of the bearers reset EBR5QI1/65/66 to the modified GBR value.
  * When a 5QI1/65/66 bearer is released, reset EBR5QI1/65/66 to the GBR value of the remaining 5QI1/65/66 bearer



[End CB008118-A]

---

##### 3.30.4.12.3.0-2 Adaptive Voice TBS Determination Calculation of Adaptive Voice Packet Size Based on Voice Effective Bit Rate (ID: 11198680)

**3.30.4.12.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11198690`)

Adaptive Voice TBS Determination This requirement converts the voice Effective Bit Rate (EBR5QI1) determined in 5G_UP_ALG_4915_replaced([11198652](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJzVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to a voice packet size.

The [Before CB008118-A] 5QI1 [CB008118-A] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] voice packet size will be calculated as:

[Before CB008118-A] 

rrmAdaptVoipPacketSize = (EBR5QI1 / 8) / 50 + 6

[CB008118-A]

rrmAdaptVoipPacketSize = (EBR5QI1/65/66 / 8) / 50 + 6

[End CB008118-A]

Notes

 EBR5QI1 is in units of bits/second. Dividing by 8 converts to bytes, dividing by 50 gives the needed number of bytes per uplink voice packet, including PDCP overhead.

 An additional 6 bytes is added as overhead to include MAC, PHR and BSR and RLC-UM overhead bytes

---

### 3.30.4.13 Configured Grant Type2 Scheduling Procedure

#### 3.30.4.13.0-1 Configured Grant Type2 Scheduling Procedure Process CG TD scheduling in CG slot procedure (ID: 11198700)

**3.30.4.13.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11198705`)

Configured Grant Type2 Scheduling Procedure The algorithm below is built around the following principle:

\- when this procedure is called for a UE being processed from CS2 list, UE is already in the to-be-scheduled list (see 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Execution of the procedure below shall update the scheduling parameters of the UE accordingly.

\- For a UE in CG firing state (se5G_UP_ALG_15099_replaced([11172972](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), this procedure is evaluated irrespective of the request class with the highest priority (that drive the UE behavior in the UL TD scheduler)

\- try first, irrespective of UE CG state, to schedule a pending CG retransmission, if any.

\- if no retransmission is to be scheduled, schedule the UE in accordance to its current CG state.

Algorithm: 

Denote ‘UE request class’ = highest priority class of the UE that drives UE priority in CS1 list.

If the UE request class is different from cgHarqRetrans or cgDeactivation [CB008124-CA]or cgModification [End CB008124-CA]

\- [Before CB008124-CB]Exit procedure (UE remains in to-be scheduled list w/o any scheduling parameters modifications) [CB008124-CB] UE is evaluated for CG overwriting in accordance to the procedure specified in 5G_UP_ALG_15617_replaced([11198720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008124-CB]

[CB008124-CA]Else if the UE request class is cgModification:

follow the procedure associated to TD-SCH event in 5G_UP_ALG_15622_replaced([11173051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB008124-CA]

Else if the UE request class is cgHarqRetrans:

if any harqId corresponding to a CG that requires a retransmission exists

o from the list of the CG Harq processes which requires a retransmission for this UE, select the oldest harqId among the Harq processes with the highest number of retransmissions performed

\- if a harqId was selected for retransmission:

o if there is a free PDCCH candidate for transmitting the DCI as determined by 5G_UP_ALG_4433_replaced([11170857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuAeTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

 if UE can be scheduled with DCI 0_1, i.e. not in transient phase (with DCI 0_1 size modification) :

 mark UE eligible for PDCCH resources with UL DCI 0_1 addressed to CS-RNTI and with CG reTx format (see 5G_UP_ALG_14814_replaced([11172959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

 consider NDI not toggled for this UE (revert assumption in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 Exit procedure.

 endif

o endif

\- endif

Else:

EndIf

if either UE request class is cgDeactivation or UE request class is cgHarqRetrans and UE is part of cgDeactivation request class

o if UE state is CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder:

 if there is a free PDCCH candidate for transmitting the DCI as determined by 5G_UP_ALG_4433_replaced([11170857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuAeTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

 mark UE eligible for PDCCH resources with UL DCI 0_0 addressed to CS-RNTI and with CG deactivation order format (see 5G_UP_ALG_14812_replaced([11172948](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPHzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 endif

o else if UE state is CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling:

 no action (UE already has the UL transmission occasion corresponding to current CG occasion at N+k2)

o endif

EndIf

---

#### 3.30.4.13.0-2 Configured Grant Type2 Scheduling Procedure Process CG TD scheduling in non CG slot procedure (ID: 11198709)

**3.30.4.13.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11198715`)

Configured Grant Type2 Scheduling Procedure The algorithm below is built around the following principle:

\- For a UE in CG firing state (se5G_UP_ALG_15099_replaced([11172972](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), this procedure is evaluated only if the request class with the highest priority (that drive the UE behavior in the UL TD scheduler) is cgHarqRetrans or cgDeactivaiton

\- try first, irrespective of UE CG state, to schedule a pending CG retransmission, if any.

\- if no CG retransmission is to be scheduled, schedule the UE in accordance to its current CG state.

Algorithm:

\- Denote ‘UE request class’ = highest priority class of the UE that drives UE priority in CS1 list.

\- if UE request class is cgHarqRetrans

o from the list of the CG Harq processes which requires a retransmission for this UE, select the oldest harqId among the Harq processes with the highest number of retransmissions performed

\- endif.

\- if a harqId was selected for retransmission:

o if there is a free PDCCH candidate for transmitting the DCI as determined by 5G_UP_ALG_4433_replaced([11170857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuAeTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

 if UE can be scheduled with DCI 0_1, i.e. not in transient phase (with DCI 0_1 size modification) :

 If same number of PRBs as for CG grant with same or more OFDM symbols as for the CG grant can be reserved:

o mark UE eligible for PDCCH resources with UL DCI 0_1 addressed to CS-RNTI and with CG reTx format (see 5G_UP_ALG_14814_replaced([11172959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

o Exit procedure.

 endif

 endif

o endif.

\- endif.

\- Else

\- if either UE request class is cgDeactivation or UE request class is cgHarqRetrans and UE is part of cgDeactivation request class

o if there is a free PDCCH candidate for transmitting the DCI as determined by 5G_UP_ALG_4433_replaced([11170857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuAeTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

 if UE state is CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder

 mark UE eligible for PDCCH resources with UL DCI 0_0 addressed to CS-RNTI and with CG deactivation order format (see 5G_UP_ALG_14812_replaced([11172948](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPHzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 else if UE is in CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling

 if UE is not in any other priority class (non CG specific),

o decide PUSCH allocation size to be minimum number of PRBs scheduleable in UL (see see 5G_UP_ALG_11483_replaced([11196751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwE63PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This quantity must be sufficient irrespective of the MCS for the MAC-CE subheader.

 Else

o Consider the number of PRBs as requested by the next higher priority class the UE is member of

 Endif.

 Note: such grant shall be a regular DG, i.e. addressed to C-RNTI.

 endif

o Endif.

\- Endif.

\- Endif.

If the UE is not scheduled in one of the cases above but it’s member of other priority class (i.e. non CG specific), evaluation of the UE scheduling continues by following the legacy DG scheduling procedure (note that this could only happen when UE is member of cgHarqRetrans but CG retransmission was not possible).

---

#### 3.30.4.13.0-3 Configured Grant Type2 Scheduling Procedure Decision on which UEs will have Configured grant (CG) overwritten in a CG occasion (ID: 11198720)

**3.30.4.13.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11198737`)

Configured Grant Type2 Scheduling Procedure A UE with CG occasion in the current slot that was sent to FD scheduler (see 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is analyzed in order to decide if the UE:

\- shall keep the CG unchanged

\- have configured grant (CG) overwritten with dynamic grant (DG) but on the CG resources

\- shall have CG overwritten with DG by potentially receiving more PRBs than allocated to CG

If UE is pending CG deconfiguration (see 5G_UP_ALG_14584_replaced([11172905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPHxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) procedure (until the reception of the RRC Reconfiguration Complete after RRC Reconfiguration carrying CG release)

OR otherwise all the conditions below are accomplished:

o in accordance to UE request class, ~~the required TBS as computed with 5G_UP_ALG_5010_replaced([11201008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),~~([CB008600-F] dcGbrProactiveIncrease + [End CB008600-F] rmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes + max size of all possible MAC CEs)*8 for a new transmission is larger than CG TBS as computed for CG (see 5G_UP_ALG_14664_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 5G_UP_ALG_15651_replaced([11172853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) , or in case that a retransmission was selected OR, if target PUSCH requires UCI multiplexing, the minimum number of PRBs for UCI multiplexing with PUSCH as requested by 5G_UP_ALG_11447_replaced([11196591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDsuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is larger than the number of PRBs of the CG grant AND UE is not in transient phase requiring scheduling via DCI 0_0. l l

 In case that CG is multiplexed with UCI, if the number of PRBs of the CG grant is less than the minimum number of PUSCH PRBs needed for multiplexing with UCI, then CG overwriting attempt is requested irrespective of the presence of the UE in CS2 list. For the UE not having a request class associated, implementation shall decide whether the UE shall be considered from prescheduling phase or a later injection into CS2 list directly shall be considered, by evaluating the CG UEs with CG occasion in the current slot already sent to to-be-scheduled list. UE shall be considered for scheduling with high scheduling equivalated priority, immediately smaller than harqRetransVoip (for UEs with a request class associated, so already present into the CS2 list, the priority shall be promoted). For direct injection into CS2 list solution, DRX active time shall be evaluated.

When the number of RBs is increased to mitigate the degradation of the coding rate due to the rate matching of data around the UCI, the expected MCS reduction shall not lead to lower TBS than the one configured for CG (see 5G_UP_ALG_5102_replaced([11203503](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB008600-F] dcGbrProactiveIncrease = grantedPacketSize if, for a DC-GBR configured UE and having the CG profile mapped to DC-GBR DRB, the conditions to increase the DC GBR buffer specified in 5G_UP_ALG_31937_replaced([11173463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, or zero otherwise [End CB008600-F]

OR otherwise UE is eligible for A-CSI scheduling

AND:

o there is PDCCH candidate available for this UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_11429_replaced([11196565](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDstfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

then:

o [CB008600-F] if dcGbrProactiveIncrease evaluated above is not zero, execute the DC-GBR buffer update as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_31937_replaced([11173463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB008600-F].

o If **[PR815362] UE is pending CG deconfiguration procedure (see above) or if**[End PR815362] the maximum required number of PRBs (as given by 5G_UP_ALG_5010_replaced([11201008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for an initial transmission or in accordance to the required number of PRBs for a retransmission) is less than or equal to the number of PRBs of the CG and, if target PUSCH requires UCI multiplexing, not less than the minimum number of PRBs for UCI multiplexing with PUSCH as requested by 5G_UP_ALG_11447_replaced([11196591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDsuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [PR837907] AND, in case that the UE is undergoing CG deconfiguration requiring scheduling with DCI 0_0, if the CG RBs of the UE are fully included in the first N RBs of the active UL BWP, where N is the size of the initial UL BWP [End PR837907]:****

 Consider the dynamic grant (DG) over the (whole) configured grant (CG) allocation.

 Stop further processing (relative to PRB allocation) of the UE in FD scheduler.

 Allocate the PDCCH candidate to the UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_11528_replaced([11196844](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwFh7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

o Else if considering UE's CG PRBs potentially available, a minimum number of PRBs could be reserved as requested by 5G_UP_ALG_11420_replaced([11196526](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDFrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 release the CG PRBs . Updating the existing subareas in accordance to 5G_UP_ALG_16821_replaced([11173200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuRkCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be performed.

 UE shall be further considered as input for FD resources allocation.

 Allocate the PDCCH candidate to the UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_11528_replaced([11196844](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwFh7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

If CG overwritting can't be done as per procedure above, stop further processing of the UE in FD scheduler (relative to PRB allocation) , i.e. CG is kept.

---

## 3.30.5 UL PDCCH Scheduling

### 3.30.5.1 Basic requirements

#### 3.30.5.1.1 Multiplicity

##### 3.30.5.1.1.0-1 Multiplicity There is one xPDCCH scheduler per subcell for UL DCI handling. (ID: 11198757)

### 3.30.5.2 UL DCI Creation and Transmission Request to L1-DL

#### 3.30.5.2.1 3GPP NR DCI Format

##### 3.30.5.2.1-1 DCI Format 0_1

##### 3.30.5.2.1-2 DCI Format 0_1

###### 3.30.5.2.1-2.0-1 DCI Format 0_1 The DCI field, antenna port. See ( 11103106 ) and ( 11103186 ) for all supported antenna port values. (ID: 11198910)

##### 3.30.5.2.1-3 DCI Format 0_1

###### 3.30.5.2.1-3.0-4 DCI Format 0_1 If UL DCI corresponds to an UL DCI 0_0 granted over the coresetId set 0, as received in commonControlResourceSet[0] of NRBTS/BWP_PROFILE/PDCCH/PDCCH_CONFIG_COMMON.pdcchConfigCommon, the Time Domain Resource assignment field in the UL DCI is set to pusch-symbolAllocation index defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2424_replaced( 11115802 ), considering -  the pusch-symbolAllocation table reported to CP-RT in the   PsCell_CellSetupResp message, if the table reported to CR-RT was not empty, In other case the default A table. If UL DCI corresponds to an UL DCI 0_1 or the CoresetId is not set to 0, the Time Domain Resource assignment field in the UL DCI is set to pusch-symbolAllocation index defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2424_replaced( 11115802 ), considering : first the pusch-symbolAllocation table reported to CP-RT in the  PsUser_UserSetupResp message, if the table reported to CR-RT was not empty, else secondly the pusch-symbolAllocation table reported to CP-RT in the   PsCell_CellSetupResp message, if the table reported to CR-RT was not empty, In other case the default A table. pusch-symbolAllocation index is determined using - K2 derived from PUSCH configuration of current slot as per 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced( 11116264 ) - SLIV derived from the PUSCH slot format of current slot as per 5G_UP_ALG_2406_replaced( 11115717 ). For UL CA UE, SLIV(41) for puschMappingType Type A or SLIV(55) for puschMappingType Type B is scheduled at FDD UL Slot which is overlapped with TDD slot with periodic SRS configuration according to 5G_UP_ALG_5062_replaced( 11202027 ). [CB012149-AB] For UL Tx Switching UE, SLIV for the slot with uplink Tx Switching period located is set according to 5G_UP_ALG_5062_replaced( 11202027 ). [End CB012149-AB] notes :  "If UL DCI corresponds to an UL DCI 0_0 granted over the coresetId set 0"  assumes the UL DCI 0_0 is only configured over the common search space &amp; never over the Ue specific Search space. (ID: 11198939)

##### 3.30.5.2.1-4 DCI Format 0_1

###### 3.30.5.2.1-4.0-1 DCI Format 0_1 11198980: DCI Format 0_1 Setting of parameter Frequency Domain Resource Assignment with resource allocation Type 1 The PUSCH PRBs allocated via the UL DCI format 0_0 are based on a resource allocation type 1 only. With such scheme, the Frequency Resource Assignment consists of a Resource Indication Value (RIV). RIV value is a function of the [image-0][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] number of contiguous PRB and the [image-1][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] starting PRB inside the Bandwidth Part. UL DCI format 0_1 are also  based on a resource allocation type 1 . The resource Allocation type of the DCI 0_1 is received in IE resourceAllocation in PsUser_UserSetupReq. RIV field has a size of [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in square brackets. The expression is: $$ \left[ \log_2 \left( \frac{N_{\text{BWP}}^{\text{size}} (N_{\text{BWP}}^{\text{size}} + 1)}{2} \right) \right] $$ The text within the image is: [log2(NsizeBWP(NsizeBWP + 1)/2)][/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] bits , where : If the DCI corresponds to a DCI Format 0_0 in a common search space, [image-3][image_desc]The image contains the text "N_bwp^nied". The text is blurry and has a chromatic aberration effect, giving it a rainbow-like halo. The "N" is large and bold, while the "bwp" is smaller and positioned below and to the right of the "N". The "nied" is even smaller and positioned above and to the right of the "N".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] is the size of the initial uplink bandwidth part. If the DCI 0_1 is not used to switch of active bandwidth part [image-4][image_desc]The image contains the text "N_bwp^nied". The text is blurry and has a chromatic aberration effect, giving it a rainbow-like halo. The "N" is large and bold, while the "bwp" is smaller and positioned below and to the right of the "N". The "nied" is even smaller and positioned above and to the right of the "N".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.004.png[/image_path][/image-4] is the size of the active uplink bandwidth part. If the DCI 0_1 is used to switch of active bandwidth part [image-5][image_desc]The image contains the text "N_bwp^nied". The text is blurry and has a chromatic aberration effect, giving it a rainbow-like halo. The "N" is large and bold, while the "bwp" is smaller and positioned below and to the right of the "N". The "nied" is even smaller and positioned above and to the right of the "N".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.004.png[/image_path][/image-5] is the size of the source uplink bandwidth part. The Resource Indication Value is defined in 38.214 section 6.1.2.2.2 as follows: if [image-6][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.005.png[/image_path][/image-6]then [image-7][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.006.png[/image_path][/image-7] else [image-8][image_desc]The image is completely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.007.png[/image_path][/image-8] where[image-9][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.008.png[/image_path][/image-9] 1 and shall not exceed[image-10][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15585.009.png[/image_path][/image-10]. The setting of parameters that 5G-L2-PS use to compute the RIV refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4948_replaced( 11198986 ) (ID: 11198980)

[image-19][image_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the allocation of Physical Uplink Shared Channel (PUSCH) and Physical Uplink Control Channel (PUCCH) resources within a Carrier Resource Block (CRB) for a dedicated Bandwidth Part (BWP) 1, denoted as (N PRB). The diagram is structured vertically, with the CRB and its associated resource allocation shown on the left. The right side provides labels for the different resource regions. On the far left, a column labeled "CRB" is divided into segments. The top segment is labeled "N-1", and subsequent segments are indicated by ellipses (...), suggesting a range of resource units from N-1 down to 0. To the right of the CRB segments, there are two main vertical colored regions representing resource allocations: 1. Upper Region (Green): This region is labeled "PUSCH regionA" and is associated with the text "rrnNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp" on its left. This indicates a block of PRBs allocated for PUSCH in region A. 2. Middle Region (Blue): This region is labeled "PUCCH". It is positioned below the "PUSCH regionA" and is associated with the text "rrnNumPuschPrbBelowDedicatedBwp" on its left. This signifies a block of PRBs allocated for PUCCH. 3. Lower Region (Light Green): This region is labeled "PUSCH regionB" and is associated with the text "rrnStartPuschBelowPrbDedicatedBwp" on its left. This denotes a block of PRBs allocated for PUSCH in region B. There are also horizontal labels that define the boundaries or starting points of these regions: * "rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp" is positioned between "PUSCH regionA" and "PUCCH", indicating the start of the PUCCH allocation or the end of the PUSCH region A. * "rrmStartPuschBelowPrbDedicatedBwp" is positioned below "PUSCH regionB", likely indicating the start of the PUSCH region B or the end of the PUCCH allocation. The numerical labels "3", "2", "1", and "0" are shown at the bottom of the CRB column, possibly representing specific resource unit indices or a count within a particular allocation. The overall diagram visually represents how PRBs are partitioned and assigned to different uplink channels (PUSCH and PUCCH) within a specific BWP configuration.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03281603.png[/image_path][/image-19] [End CB012299-A] - Note, any blanked PRBs are already considered by rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp. if the DCI corresponds to a DCI 0-0 [CB012299-A] and is not used to ack/complete the change of active bandwidth via RRC[End CB012299-A][image-20][image_desc]The image contains the text "N_bwp" with a superscript "n_ind". The text is blurry and has a chromatic aberration effect, with red and blue fringes around the letters. The background is white, and the text is black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15587.008.png[/image_path][/image-27]is the size of the target uplink bandwidth part. [End CB012299-A] if long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch'), then [image-28][image_desc]The image is entirely black. There is no discernible content, text, or patterns within the image.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15587.011.png[/image_path][/image-28] of Msg3 (by DCI 0_0/ Msg2) is set as defined in case of RAR in 5G_UP_ALG_3419_replaced( 11152630 ). Note: The RIV formula interleaves the large grants, grant larger than half of the bandwidth, with the small grants, grant smaller than half of the bandwidth. In such way that: Small grants are numbered in ascendant order according the grant size (i.e. number of allocated PRBs): a smaller RIV indicated a smaller grant size Large grants are numbered in descendant order according the Grant size: a smaller RIV indicated a larger grant size [CB012299-A] Note: Clarification about RIV calculations DCI 0_0 (Common Search Space): For the RIV the VRB0 is the first CRB of the UE active BWP (PRB0) if the DCI is scrambled with C-RNTI For the RIV the VRB0 (RB in the UE active BWP) is computed as follows: first RB of the initial uplink BWP minus the first RB of the UE active BWP, if  the the DCI is scrambled with TC-RNTI and if the initial Uplink BWP is fully included by the UE active BWP (always the case) So the area where it is possible to allocate RBs with DCI 0_0 depends on the UE active BWP and the type of RNTI use by the UE (TC-RNTI (until the contention resolution for CBRA) or C-RNTI) DCI 1_0 (Common Search Space) For the RIV, the VRB0 is the first RB of the CORESET used for the common search space. DCI [0,1]_1: For the RIV, the VRB0 is the first RB of the UE active BWP (PRB0) For the DCI BWP switch, the FDRA size is linked to the current UE active BWP (so before the switch), and the RIV is computed for the target BWP (ie according to the source and target BWP, the FDRA size is truncated (restricting the RB allocation) or contains padding bit). In the case of RRC Setup complete (for which the RRC setup indicates first active: BWP-1 so it is a RRC BWP switch)  when USS is defined in the RRC Setup, the UE receives a DCI 0_1 in its UE active BWP (BWP-1) the RIV should be calculated by active BWP (BWP-1) size, otherwise it is scheduled by a DCI 0_0. [End CB012299-A] (ID: 12440944)

##### 3.30.5.2.1-6 DCI Format 0_1

###### 3.30.5.2.1-6.0-3 DCI Format 0_1 12462267: DCI Format 0_1 This requirement defines how to calculate the maximum number of PRBs can be allocated for PRACH and PUSCH in UL slots with PRACH configuration. The numbers of PRBs which are above and below PRACH signal can be calculated with below equations: [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula enclosed in a rounded rectangle. The formula is: $L_{RBs}^{above} = ul Bandwidth - prach PrbOf f set - nbrOf PrbPer Rach Occassion * msg1Fdm$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03271200.png[/image_path][/image-0] [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a rounded rectangle with a light gray background. Inside the rectangle, there is a mathematical equation written in white text. The equation is: $L_{RBs}^{below} = prachPrbOffset$. The text is in a serif font.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03271203.png[/image_path][/image-1] where [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a light gray rounded rectangle with a dark gray border. Inside the rectangle, there is text. The text is "L" with a subscript "RBs" and a superscript "above". The "L" is large and in a serif font. The "RBs" is smaller and in a serif font. The "above" is smaller and in a serif font, positioned above and to the right of the "L".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03271209.png[/image_path][/image-2] is number of PRBs above PRACH signal; [image-3][image_desc]The image contains a light gray rounded rectangle with a dark gray border. Inside the rectangle, there is text. The text is "L" with a subscript "RBs" and a superscript "below". The "L" is large and bold, while the subscript and superscript are smaller and in a lighter font.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03271210.png[/image_path][/image-3] is number of PRBs below PRACH signal; The ulBandwidth is maximum number PRBs on UL which is defined in 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2029_replaced( 11277080 ); The msg1Fdm is parameter in rachConfig structure (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2005_replaced( 11275328 )). The prachPrbOffset is parameter in rachConfig structure (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2005_replaced( 11275328 )). The nbrOfPrbPerRachOccasion can be calculated based on the table 6.3.3.2-1 in 3GPP 38.211. [CB012299-A] When two PUSCH regions (A,B) are configured in the dedicated BWP separated by PUCCH region ( 11199501 ), in slots with prach configuration   additional PRBs are available: [image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation enclosed in a rounded rectangle. The equation is: $L_{RBs}^{below PUCCH} = rrmN um PuschPrbBDedicatedBwp$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03271212.png[/image_path][/image-4] [End CB012299-A] The maximum number of continuous PRBs can be allocated for PUSCH in PRACH slots is : [Before CB012299-A] [image-5][image_desc]The image contains a rounded rectangle with a light gray background. Inside the rectangle, there is a mathematical expression written in a dark gray color. The expression is: $max(L_{RBs}^{above}, L_{RBs}^{below})$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03271215.png[/image_path][/image-5] [CB012299-A] [image-6][image_desc]The image contains a rounded rectangle with a light gray background. Inside the rectangle, there is a mathematical expression written in black text. The expression is: $max(L_{RBs}^{above}, L_{RBs}^{below}, L_{RBs}^{belowPUCCH})$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03271215.png[/image_path][/image-6] [End CB012299-A] When NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexingLevel set to 0 or (NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexingLevel set to 1 and NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is set to shortPucch) If PRACH is with long or short format, according to 5GMax simulation, guard PRBs shall be reserved above and below PRACH signal to avoid scheduling PUSCH signals in these guard PRBs. Above PRACH signal, 2 PRBs shall be reserved. Below PRACH signal, 2 PRBs shall be reserved as well. Therefore, the maximum number of continuous PRBs can be allocated for PUSCH in PRACH slots is [Before CB012299-A] [image-7][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in a rounded rectangle. The expression is: $max(L_{RBs}^{above} - 2, L_{RBs}^{below} - 2)$. The text is in a light gray color against a slightly darker gray background.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03281130.png[/image_path][/image-7]  [CB012299-A][image-8][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in a rounded rectangle. The expression is: $max(L_{RBs}^{above} - 2, L_{RBs}^{below} - 2, L_{RBs}^{belowPUCCH})$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-03281134.png[/image_path][/image-8][End CB012299-A] Else, when NRCELL.actPrachMultiplexingLevel is set to 1 and when NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is set to longPucch No guard PRBs shall be reserved for PRACH long or short preamble format. (ID: 12462267)

##### 3.30.5.2.1-7 DCI Format 0_1

###### 3.30.5.2.1-7.0-1 DCI Format 0_1 11199171: DCI Format 0_1 If "resourceAllocation" received (38.331, section 6.3.2) RRC setup message  is set to resourceAllocationType1, "frequency domain resource assignment" IE is computed using 5G_UP_ALG_4947_replaced( 11198980 ) with : - If the active bandwidth part is switched with this DCI, the IE is filled assuming the size of the source UL BWP (BWP switch via UL DCI is not supported). - Else the size of the IE and the RIV value is computed using the active bandwith part If "resourceAllocation" received (38.331, section 6.3.2) in RRC setup message is set to resourceAllocationType0,  the "frequency domain resource assignment" IE is a bitmap indicating the resource block groups ( RBGs) that are allocated to the UE. An RBG is a set of P consecutive PRBs. P is provided in 3GPP 38.214 Table 6.1.2.2.1-1, using IE rbgSizePusch (see 38.214, section 6.1.2.2.1). if n &gt; 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced( 11110833 )), the rbgSizePusch is defined by the active BWP of the UE. The total number of RBGs ([image-0][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15592.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]) for a uplink bandwidth part i of size [image-1][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15592.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]PRBs is given by [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $N_{RBG} = \lfloor \frac{N_{BWP,i}^{size} + (N_{BWP,i}^{start} \mod P)}{P} \rfloor$ The equation defines $N_{RBG}$ in terms of other variables: $N_{BWP,i}^{size}$, $N_{BWP,i}^{start}$, and $P$. The floor function is indicated by the vertical bars surrounding the fraction.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15592.003.png[/image_path][/image-2], where - the size of the first RBG is [image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $RBG_{0}^{size} = P - (\frac{N_{BW}P_{i,l}^{start}}{P} mod P)$.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15592.004.png[/image_path][/image-3], - the size of last RBG is[image-4][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15592.005.png[/image_path][/image-4] if  [image-5][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15592.006.png[/image_path][/image-5] and P otherwise, - the size of all other RBGs is P. -   [image-6][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15592.007.png[/image_path][/image-6] the size of the Ue active bandwidth part (5G_UP_ALG_1823_replaced( 11110957 )). -  [image-7][image_desc]The image contains a blurry mathematical expression. The expression appears to be a fraction with "N_start" in the numerator and "BWP_i" in the denominator.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15592.008.png[/image_path][/image-7] the start of the Ue active bandwidth part (5G_UP_ALG_1823_replaced( 11110957 )) When the active bandwidth part is switched with this DCI, the bitmap size is computed assuming the size of the source UL BWP (BWP switch via UL DCI is not supported). In other case the bitmap is of size [image-8][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15592.009.png[/image_path][/image-8]bits with one bitmap bit per RBG such that each RBG is addressable. The RBGs shall be indexed in the order of increasing frequency and starting at the lowest frequency of the bandwidth part Using the frequency assignment of the uplink scheduler in 5G_UP_ALG_4970_replaced( 11199433 ), 5G-L2-PS shall set the xth bit of the frequency domain resource bitmap to 1 if the RBG x was allocated to the UE and to 0 otherwise. Bit x=0 is the MSB of the bitmap. That means, if the whole bandwidth part is allocated to an UE then all bits of frequency domain resource assignment are set to 1. (ID: 11199171)

##### 3.30.5.2.1-8 DCI Format 0_1

###### 3.30.5.2.1-8.0-1 DCI Format 0_1 11199187: DCI Format 0_1 If UL-PTRS is enabled and maxNumOfUlLayers of UE is higher than 1 (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2398_replaced( 11285744 )), the ‘PTRS-DMRS association’ field in DCI format 0_1 has 2 bits, otherwise the ‘PTRS-DMRS association’ field has 0 bits.  The flag enableUlPtrs (refer to 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2249_replaced( 11282562 )) shall be used to determine if UL-PTRS is enabled or not. shall be used to determine if UL-PTRS is enabled or not. The detailed definition of bit width of the field 'PTRS-DMRS association' can be found at below table: [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a table with a green header and yellow and white rows. The table has five columns: "Bit width", "nrofSrsPorts", "maxNumOfUILayers", "UL PTRS", and "Notes". Here is the table content: Bit width nrofSrsPorts maxNumOfUILayers UL PTRS Notes 0 any any OFF maxRank in PUSCH- Config = 1 0 any 1 ON maxRank in PUSCH- Config = 1 0 1 any ON maxRank in PUSCH- Config = 1 2 2 2 ON maxRank in PUSCH- Config &gt; 1 If ‘PTRS-DMRS association’ field in DCI format 0_1 has 2 bits, the meaning of its values is defined in the table 7.3.1.1.2-25 at 3GPP 38.212 because only PTRS port 0 is supported. The L2-PS shall always use the value 0 on this field which means the PTRS port 0 shall always associate with the first scheduled DMRS port. For example, for a UE with assigned DMRS antenna ports 0 &amp; 1, the UL PTRS signals shall associate with antenna port 0. For a UE with assigned DMRS antenna ports 2 &amp; 3, the UL PTRS signals shall associate with antenna port 2. For Transform Precoding enabled, max Rank is 1. Therefore the ‘PTRS-DMRS association’ field has 0 bits. (ID: 11199187)

##### 3.30.5.2.1-9 DCI Format 0_1

###### 3.30.5.2.1-9.0-1 DCI Format 0_1 5G-L2-PS shall align the Payload size of DCI 0_0 to the payload size of the DCI 1_0, using the padding IEs of the DCI 0_0. 5G-L2-PS shall compute the payload size of the DCI 0_0 and DCI 1_0 considering the active uplink bandwidth part for DCI 0_0 and the active downlink bandwidth part for DCI 1_0. Note : In 38.212 the padding is only applicable when DCI 0_0 / DCI 1_0 is scheduled in the Common search Space. 5G-L2-PS shall assume DCI 0_0 / DCI 1_0 is only configured by C-Plane in Common search space and not in Ue specific search space. (ID: 11199190)

##### 3.30.5.2.1.1 DCI Format 0_0

###### 3.30.5.2.1.1.0-1 DCI Format 0_0 DCI format 0_0 applicability (ID: 11198778)

**3.30.5.2.1.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12478849`)

DCI Format 0_0 DCI format 0_0 is applicable when UE is granted in Common Search space (?)

It corresponds to:

 RA-Msg3 retransmission granted by a TC-RNTI.

 HARQ retransmission of the PUSCH granted by the RAR after a Contention Free (CFRA) Handover/PSCell Change granted by C-RNTI.

 [Before CNI-137616-B] If _rdActCr3574 == 1 (5G_L2_1340_replaced([11187360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28AqU_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), [End CNI-137616-B] UL data granted with CRC scrambled by C-RNTI just after a beam failure recovery which is corresponding to the period from the receiving time of Msg3 with CRNTI until the updated beam ID can be applied [5G_UP_ALG_2032_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))], as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4814_replaced([11194878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4Gg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))._

 A UE having an "adaptive number of SRS ports" state (see 5G_UP_ALG_6925_replaced([11157717](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks9VafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and whose state is either "Reconfiguration 1-to-2" or "Reconfiguration 2-to-1" (see 5G_L2_656_replaced([11164313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25sWIvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 If 'adaptive number of SRS ports' status is changed from enable to disable due to UL Tx Switching SCell is added via SCell addition/change/swap or if 'adaptive number of SRS ports' status is changed from disable to enable due to UL Tx Switching SCell is released via SCell addition/change/swap and nrofSrsPorts with codebook usage is changed (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_6925_replaced([11157717](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks9VafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 When NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to disable, UE User Reconfiguration procedure Transition Phase due to transition of Transform Precoder state

 When NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to disable, an RRC Reconfiguration procedure Transition Phase due to configuring 256QAM, please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_5097_replaced([11203479](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G_UP_ALG_5097_replaced([11203479](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 When NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to disable, an RRC Reconfiguration procedure Transition Phase due to pusch-TimeDomainAllocationList or pusch-TimeDomainAllocationListR16 in PUSCH-Config changed.

 Any transmissions when the UE-Specifc search space are not configured yet in the UE (UE-Specific search space not present in the UE context creation or UE dedicated configuration not activated yet (see requirement 5G_UP_ALG_17986_replaced([11153416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))).

For more details on transient phase requiring UE’s scheduling with DCI format 0_0 see also 5G_UP_ALG_4816_replaced([11194889](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4GhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Note:**

 DCI format 0_0 in a common search space only applies to the Primary Cell (DCI Format 0_0 not configured in the Secondary cell)**.**

 The "adaptive number of SRS ports" only applies to the Primary Cell**.**

 UE is not scheduled during reconfiguration transient phase due to transition of Transform Precoder state on Scell, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_9767_replaced([11195124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv57tPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DCI format 0_0 element definition refer to 5G_UP_ALG_14012_replaced([11171394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuEIoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 3.30.5.2.1.2 DCI Format 0_1

###### 3.30.5.2.1.2.0-2 DCI Format 0_1 DMRS sequence initialization is 1bit Notes : There is a bug in 3GPP standard that introduce a uncertanity for the 2018 june baseline. In 2018 september baseline, 3GPP reverts to the its previous behavior. PR391213 allows to control the 5G-L2-PS behavior with June baseline only. (ID: 11198822)

### 3.30.5.3 Selection of UL PDCCH resource among multiple slots

#### 3.30.5.3.0-1 Selection of UL PDCCH resource among multiple slots (ID: 11199201)

**3.30.5.3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13424578`)

Selection of UL PDCCH resource among multiple slots When NRCELL.actIncUlPdcchCapacity is set to TRUE and if gNB is using multiple K2 mode (5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_30394_replaced([11116305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_13617_replaced([11348263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk3vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB can allocate UL PDCCH resource for a given UL slot from multiple DL slots.

To avoid the risk of DTX, gNB shall choose a slot for PDCCH grant depending upon a given UEs inactivity timer and starting of DRX cycle as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_30878_replaced([11199212](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwLoj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[PR875131-26R2]

In a similar fashion, gNB shall ensure that the slot for PDCCH grant selected for given UE is configured in the UE active SSSG (see [11168730: Search Space Set Group management](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktx0yvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

[PR875131-26R2]

Also for a given UL slot gNB shall choose a DL slot for UL PDCCH grant among available slots based on priority order as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_30880_replaced([11199230](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.30.5.3.0-2 Selection of UL PDCCH resource among multiple slots Candidates UE selection with consideration of DRX (ID: 11199212)

**3.30.5.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11199224`)

Selection of UL PDCCH resource among multiple slots 11199224: Selection of UL PDCCH resource among multiple slots [image-0][image_desc]The image is a dark, gradient background. The top portion of the image is a deep teal color, which gradually transitions into a very dark blue or black at the bottom. There are no discernible objects, text, or patterns within the image.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15600.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

The UE will not be decoding the PDCCH when gNB gives UL PDCCH grant in a slot which is after the UEs DRX inactivity timer ends or when an UL PDCCH grant is given in a slot which is before the UEs DRX cycle starts. This will lead to DTX.

To avoid the risk of DTX gNB shall consider the following solution while scheduling:

 During pre-selection of candidates in UL scheduling gNB shall consider union of UEs which are ending their DRX cycle or starting the DRX cycle in the two slots which will be used to send the UL grants. For example:

[CB013305-D-UPD001]

o while scheduling for first UL slot N, gNB shall consider union of UEs whose DRX inactivity timer is ending or DRX cycle beginning in slots N-K2max and or DRX ON cycle and the local (excluding other CCs) UL DRX inactivity timer ending in N-K2max+1 or beginning in slots N-K2max. Same way for second UL slot N+1, gNB shall consider the union of UEs whose DRX inactivity timer is ending or beginning in slots (N+1) - K2max + 1 or DRX ON cycle and the local (excluding other CCs) UL DRX inactivity timer ending in (N+1)-K2max+2 and (N+1) - K2max +3 or beginning in slots (N+1)- K2max +2.

o the UEs who are scheduled in first UL slot N and whose drxOnPeriod is have DRX inactivity timer ending by slot N- k2max need to be strictly scheduled with k2=k2max in slot N-k2max.

o the UEs who are scheduled in first UL slot N and are not in drxOnPeriod in N- K2max (this means that they are added to presched thanks to other drx timers, inactivityTimer, retxTimer, raContentionResolutionTmr), the K2max need to be strictly scheduled with k2=k2max (since we are not sure if in N- K2max+1 UEs are still active)

o the UEs who are scheduled in first UL slot N and have DRX cycle starting on N-k2max+1 need to be strictly cannot be scheduled with k2=k2max-1 in slot N-k2max+1 Such UEs will be scheduled in next UL slot.

o the UEs who are scheduled in second UL slot N+1 and whose drxOnPeriod is have DRX inactivity timer ending by slot (N+1)- K2max+1 need to be strictly scheduled cannot be scheduled with k2= K2max-1 in slot (N+1)- K2max+1. Such UEs will be scheduled in next UL slot.

o the UEs who are scheduled in second UL slot N+1 and are not in drxOnPeriod in (N+1)- K2max+1 (this means that they are added to presched thanks to other drx timers, inactivityTimer, retxTimer, raContentionResolutionTmr), the K2max need to be strictly scheduled with k2=k2max-1 (since we are not sure if in N- K2max+1 UEs are still active)

Note: CB013305-D-UPD001 insertion is marked with underline. CB013305-D-UPD001 deletion is marked with strikethrough. The formatting and this note need be removed in new version.

[End CB013305-D-UPD001]

o if k2min=2, the UEs who are scheduled in second UL slot N+1 and have DRX cycle starting on slot (N+1)-k2max+2 cannot be scheduled with k2= K2max-2 in slot (N+1)-K2max+2. Such UEs will be scheduled in next UL slot.

[image-1][image_desc]The image displays a timeline illustrating the behavior of User Equipment (UEs) in a cellular network, specifically focusing on their Discontinuous Reception (DRX) cycles and interactions with the uplink (UL) scheduler and UL PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel). The top portion of the image shows a timeline divided into slots, labeled from N-6 to N+1. Each slot is depicted as a colored block, with "D" indicating a Downlink slot and "U" indicating an Uplink slot. There's also a slot labeled "S" which likely represents a special slot. Annotations above the timeline indicate events: \- "UL scheduler run 1" and "UL scheduler run 2" are shown with green and red arrows pointing to specific slots. \- "UL PDCCH with K2 = 3" and "UL PDCCH with K2 = 2" are indicated with red dotted arrows pointing to slots N-2 and N-1 respectively. \- "UL PDCCH with K4" and "UL PDCCH with K2 = 3" are shown with green dotted arrows pointing to slots N-4 and N respectively. The bottom portion of the image illustrates the DRX cycles of UEs. Two categories of DRX cycles are shown: "UEs DRX cycle ending in:" (represented by purple lines) and "UEs DRX cycle starting from:" (represented by orange lines). Each category has four sub-categories, each with a label indicating the ending or starting slot relative to 'N' and 'k2max'. The colored lines represent the duration of these DRX cycles across the timeline. Vertical dashed lines align with the slot boundaries in the timeline above, helping to correlate the DRX cycles with specific slots.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15600.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

Figure for reference with 5GC001116 frame structure without NR frame shift

Where:

 k2min is the min k2 value for PUSCH when multiple K2 mode is being used.

 K2max is the max k2 value for PUSCH when multiple K2 mode is being used.

 Refer 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_30394_replaced([11116305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_13617_replaced([11348263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk3vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note: Due to complexity in implementation, gNB cannot check if the DRX cycle of a UE is starting in next slot in pre-scheduling phase. Hence, for the cases when UEs DRX cycle starting on N-k2max+1 or (N+1)-k2max+2, gNB consider scheduling the user in next UL slot. This will have slight increase in latency with multiK2Mode as compared to singleK2Mode.

[CB013305-D-UPD001]

In addition, the current implementation is such that gNB cannot know about UEs DRX timer state in next slot. Hence, gNB cannot scheduled such UEs in the PDCCH of next slot. gNB can only use the PDCCH of next slot for UEs that are active with DRX On duration timer in the next slot. Since the DRX on cycle is small, the number of UEs for whom gNB can use PDCCH from additional slots will be smaller. Hence the gains of CB013305-C/D/E/F (NRCELL.actIncUlPdcchCapacity) may be limited due to these restrictions.

[End CB013305-D-UPD001]

---

#### 3.30.5.3.0-3 Selection of UL PDCCH resource among multiple slots Prioritization among available slots during allocation of PUSCH UEs in the UL slots (ID: 11199230)

**3.30.5.3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12798838`)

Selection of UL PDCCH resource among multiple slots Since gNB have the flexibility to use the DL slot in which gNB sends DCI for UL slot, gNB shall follow the priority order as below:

  * > When the TD scheduler is run for a UL slot, a given UE context can potentially use a PDCCH from 2 DL slots depending on the “Start CCE index” (5G_UP_ALG_4433_replaced([11170857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuAeTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or the DRX status constraints. If a given UE has the choice between 2 PDCCH tokens, then

  * > > the PDCCH tokens corresponding to smaller K2 shall be consumed first and then the PDCCH token corresponding to larger K2 should be consumed if available.

  * [PR850661-26R1] >> For the ith UL PDCCH slot, the number of UL PDCCH can be allocated shall not exceed rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl_i specified in [11199273](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). Where i = 1, 2, corresponding to the first and second UL PDCCH slot for the to-be scheduled PUSCH slot. [End PR850661-26R1]



Note: The reason for using PDCCH corresponding to smaller k2 first is due to limitations in DRX implementation. Please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_30874_replaced([11199201](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwLojfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

---

## 3.30.6 UL Frequency Domain Scheduling

### 3.30.6.0-1 UL Frequency Domain Scheduling UL FDM specific information and parameters (ID: 11199244)

**3.30.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11199248`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling Overview

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11199253`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling UL FDM allows the scheduling of multiple UEs in a single slot by giving each UE different frequency resources. This requirement summarizes the information and parameters that affect UL FDM

· UL FDM Information (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_10651_replaced([11199259](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPlvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

· UL FDM Parameters (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_10652_replaced([11199268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11199259`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling UL FDM Information

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11199263`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling UL FDM information:

  * FDM is currently supported in FR1 TDD and FDD only

  * UL FD resources for are counted with granularity of PRBs

  * In FR2, the behavior of FD scheduling is not changed by 5GC000679. Only a single UE is scheduled with full BW allocation as before.

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11199268`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling UL FDM Parameter

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11199273`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling The maximum number of UEs to be FDMed in UL - rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-3.0-1.0-11**  (ID: `13469661`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling The maximum number of UEs to be FDMed in UL:

  * If FDM is disabled (NRBTS.actFdmScheduling = False)



Then

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = 1

  * Else:



rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = rrmNumUlSubcells * rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell

**TDD FR1:**

[Before CB013712-A] 

If (NRBTS.actFdmScheduling == True && NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && NRCELL.pucchModeSelect == longPucch && **L1 Variant == Loner)**

Then

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl, NRCELL.maxNumPuschAllocationPerSlot, 16)

[End CB013712-A]

If (NRBTS.NRCELL.actSuperCell==TRUE && actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True)

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl , NRCELL/maxNumPuschAllocationPerSlot) 

Else If (L1 Variant == Loki or Thor or GPU) && NRBTS.actFdmScheduling == True && NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == False && NRCELL.pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed (5G_RAN_OP_1938_replaced([11692648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Y_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) == False )

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min (rmMaxNumUesFdmUl, 4)

Else If (L1 Variant == Loki or Thor && NRBTS.actFdmScheduling == True && NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && NRCELL.pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed (5G_RAN_OP_1938_replaced([11692648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Y_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) == False )

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl, NRCELL.maxNumPuschAllocationPerSlot, 8)

Else If (L1 Variant == Thor && NRBTS.actFdmScheduling == True && NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && NRCELL.pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed (5G_RAN_OP_1938_replaced([11692648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Y_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) == False && rdTriggerEcpri72eInterfaceSplit == False)

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl, NRCELL.maxNumPuschAllocationPerSlot, 8) 

Else If (L1 Variant == Thor && NRBTS.actFdmScheduling == True && NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && NRCELL.pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed (5G_RAN_OP_1938_replaced([11692648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Y_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) == False && rdTriggerEcpri72eInterfaceSplit == True)

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl, NRCELL.maxNumPuschAllocationPerSlot, 9) 

Note: rdTriggerEcpri72eInterfaceSplit is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_25991_replaced([11193867](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvwKu_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**Else If (L1 Variant == Loki or Thor or GPU) && NRBTS.actFdmScheduling == True && scheUePoolingAllowed (5G_RAN_OP_1938_replaced([11692648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Y_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) == True)**

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM

Note: maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5264_replaced([11210371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw4zLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before CB013712-A]Note: 16 is the maximum number of L1 capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell per ABIL in21A.[End CB013712-A]

Note: the parameter maxNumPuschAllocationPerSlot is received from CP-RT at cell setup or reconfiguration and a new value is applied at next scheduling period.

[Before CB013712-A]In some special deployments, for example, TDD deployment with four cells on the ABIL (5GC002509) and TDD deployment with 3 cells on the ABIL (5GC002411), when L2 instances for UL and DL are deployed on the same core, the UP overload control might react and restrict the CS1. Instead of up to 16 UEs, the maximum number of UEs in this scenario is 9 per slot.[End CB013712-A]

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl shall be further limited by rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlCceSplit if applicable (5G_UP_ALG_17533_replaced([11199329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwM2pPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or maximal amount of UL FDM UEs in symbol 1 (5G_UP_ALG_17073_replaced([11178239](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku1ksPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

If NRCELL.actDynPdcchTdMode == perBWP

{

> If gNB_DU is using single K2 mode or multiple K2 mode with only 1 UL PDCCH slot (See [11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), [11116305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), 5G System Level [11348263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk3vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)),
>
>> rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min(rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlCceSplit, rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl, rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSymbol1)
> 
> If gNB_DU is using multiple K2 mode with 2 UL PDCCH slots (See [11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), [11116305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), 5G System Level [11348263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk3vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)),
>
>> for each UL PDCCH slots,
>>
>>> rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl_i = min(rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlCceSplit, rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl , rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSymbol1). where i = 1, 2, corresponding to the first and second UL PDCCH slot for the to-be scheduled PUSCH slot.
>> 
>> rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min(rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl, rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl_1+ rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl_2)

}

Note: The maximum number of UEs to be FDMed in UL cannot reach 16 in the UL slot with RIM RI /RS detection (see 5G_UP_ALG_4886_replaced([11197000](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGwGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

**FDD:**

[Before CB013712-A]

If (L1 Variant == Loner && NRBTS.actFdmScheduling == True && NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling == True && NRCELL.pucchModeSelect == longPucch)

Then

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl, NRCELL.maxNumPuschAllocationPerSlot, 10)

Else [End CB013712-A]

If (L1 Variant == Loki or Thor[CB016062-AD]or GPU [CB016062-AD]&& NRBTS.actFdmScheduling == True && **NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == False && **NRCELL.pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed (5G_RAN_OP_1938_replaced([11692648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Y_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) == False)****

Then

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl, NRCELL.maxNumPuschAllocationPerSlot, 4)

Note: 4 is the maximum number of PUSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIO. For L1 ABIO capacity see 5G_UP_3212_replaced([11397284](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf1fi_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else If (L1 Variant == Loki or Thor [CB016062-AD]or GPU [CB016062-AD]&& NRBTS.actFdmScheduling == True && NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot == True && NRCELL.pucchModeSelect == longPucch && scheUePoolingAllowed (5G_RAN_OP_1938_replaced([11692648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Y_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) == False)

Then

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = min (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl, NRCELL.maxNumPuschAllocationPerSlot, 10)

Else If (L1 Variant == Loki or Thor [CB016062-F] or GPU [End CB016062-F]&& NRBTS.actFdmScheduling == True && scheUePoolingAllowed (5G_RAN_OP_1938_replaced([11692648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D3n1Y_9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) == True)

{

rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = maxNumPdschPerSlotByBBRM

if (NRBTS/NRDU.flexibleCaMode is set to NumberCellAmount (value 3) && dual cells setup in the L2-PS core && sum of active CA UE number of the two cells (counted only in Pcell) is greater than rdFDMReducedThresholdActiveCAUEnumber && the slot is CA DPA process slot)

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = max (1, maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM * DPASlotFDMReducedFactor)

Note:

DPASlotFDMReducedFactor is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_7981_replaced([11211886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9ruPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

DPA process slot stand for measurement processing cycle in 5G_UP_ALG_2936_replaced([11123865](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqmk8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rdFDMReducedThresholdActiveCAUEnumber is defined in 5G_L2_4749_replaced([11188204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28Gw-PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

}

Note: maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5264_replaced([11210371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw4zLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before CB013712-A]Note: 10 is the maximum number of L1 capacity per cell on ABIL in 21A.[End CB013712-A]

Note: the parameter maxNumPuschAllocationPerSlot is received from CP-RT at cell setup or reconfiguration and a new value is applied at next scheduling period.

**3.30.6.0-1.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11199329`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling The maximum number of UEs to be FDMed in UL per slot in a CCE split enabled slot for multiple dedicated BWPs in TDD FR1

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11199334`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling if (dlEcpriFdBeamforming == True (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) && ulEcpriFdBeamforming == True (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) && NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling == True )

When NRCELL.actDynPdcchTdMode is set to perBWP, then if CCE split is enabled in a slot, the maximum number of UEs FDMed in that slot (rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlCceSplit) is limited by the number of supported beams per symbol in DL (MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolDl from 5G_UP_ALG_10617_replaced([11179139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4A9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlCceSplit = MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolDl - rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlCceSplit // rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlCceSplit is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17531_replaced([11179128](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4A9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11199338`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling The number of L1 UL subcells available for PUSCH - rrmNumUlSubcells

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `13455320`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling The number of L1 UL subcells available for PUSCH **rrmNumUlSubcells depends on the parameter ulSubcellCaps (as defined in 5G_UP_2554_replaced([11390916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufWXWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and [Before CB013712-A]whether UL subcell is available for usage based on subcell pooling (ref to 5G_UP_ALG_5230_replaced([11210126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw3lB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB013712-A] with a maximum value of _ulSubcellIdMax+1 (*ulSubcellIdMax is defined in 5G_UP_1859_replaced([11375909](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueQLKvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))_***
