**3.30.6.0-1.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `11199348`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling The maximum number of UEs that can be multiplexed in an UL subcell- rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-3.0-5.0-7**  (ID: `13469663`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling The maximum number of UEs that can be multiplexed in an UL subcell:

**TDD FR1** rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell****

|  |  |  |  |   
---|---|---|---|---|---  
**L1HW** | **Parameter** |  |  |  |   
_actFdmScheduling_ | True |  |  | False |   
_actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot_ | True | False | False | False |   
_pucchModeSelect_ | longPucch | longPucch | ShortPucch | ShortPucch |   
Loner |  | 16 Note1 | 4 Note2 | 4 Note2 | 4 Note2  
Loki |  | 8 Note6 | 4 | 2 | 2  
Thor |  | 9 Note8 | 4 | 2 | 2  
GPU |  |  | 4 |  |   
  
**[Before CB013712-A]Note1: maximum number of PUSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIL in 22R2, refer to 5G_UP_3203_replaced([11397198](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf1fgPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **[End CB013712-A]****

Note2: rdMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcellTdd refer to 5G_UP_ALG_10753_replaced([11208974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kww3WvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note6: maximum number of PUSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIO in 22R2, refer to 5G_UP_3203_replaced([11397198](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf1fgPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note8: Since BB pooling is mandatory to be enabled on Thor BB HW, PUSCH capacity per subcell is not useful and not applicable anymore. In case of UE pooling is disabled on Thor BB HW, 9 is the maximum number of PUSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIP, refer to 5G_UP_3203_replaced([11397198](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf1fgPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell for a FR1 TDD Supercell**

If NRBTS.NRCELL.actSuperCell=TRUE

  * For half-board deployment,
  * If NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot=TRUE
    * rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell=16
  * Else,
    * rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell=4
  * Else, if the deployment is full-board and HwVariant=ABIO/ABIQ/ASOE/ASOF
  * If NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot=TRUE
    * rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell=10
  * Else,
    * rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell=4
  * Else, if the deployment is full-board and HwVariant=ABIP/ASOG/ASOH/RINLINE2
  * If NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot=TRUE
    * rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell=12
  * Else,
    * rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell=4



**FDD rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell**

|  |  |  |  |   
---|---|---|---|---|---  
**L1HW** | _actFdmScheduling_ | True |  |  | False  
_actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot_ | True | False | False | False |   
_pucchModeSelect_ | longPucch | longPucch | ShortPucch | ShortPucch |   
Loner |  | 10 Note3 | 2 Note4 | 2 Note4 | 2 Note4  
Loki |  | 10 Note5 | 4 | 2 | 2  
Thor |  | 10 Note9 | 4 | 2 | 2  
GPU |  | [CB016062-F,CB016062-AD]10 [End CB016062-D, CB016062-F] | [CB016062-AD]4[End CB016062-AD |  |   
  
**[Before CB013712-A]Note3: maximum number of PUSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIL, refer to 5G_UP_3212_replaced([11397284](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf1fi_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**[End CB013712-A]****

Note4: rdMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcellFdd, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_10755_replaced([11208989](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kww3XPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note5: maximum number of PUSCH capacity with 1 subcell per cell and single cell on ABIO, refer to 5G_UP_3212_replaced([11397284](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf1fi_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note9: Since BB pooling is mandatory to be enabled on Thor BB HW, PUSCH capacity per subcell is not useful and not applicable anymore. In case of UE pooling is disabled on Thor BB HW, the default value of "rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell" should use same value as Loki BB HW, refer to 5G_UP_3212_replaced([11397284](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf1fi_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This number can be further restricted by the number of DCI start positions allocated for UL according 5G_UP_ALG_4430_replaced([11170844](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuAeS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

List of relevant NIDD parameters

\- NRBTS.actFdmScheduling

\- NRCELL.actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot

\- NRCELL.pucchModeSelect

\- NRBTS.actDynOverloadControl

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `11199387`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling The maximum number of Beams to be supported per L1 UL subcell- rrmMaxNumBeamsUlSubcell

---

**3.30.6.0-1.0-3.0-6.0-5**  (ID: `12784801`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling The maximum number of Beams to be supported per L1 UL subcell:

**For TDD FR1:**

If (ulEcpriFdBeamforming == True (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) && NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling== True)

Then

rrmMaxNumBeamsUlStream is assigned the "Number of supported sections per symbol" value, where:

For eCPRI FR1 TDD Radio Units that support beamforming, the Radio Unit provides 2 parameters:

Number of supported beams per symbol in UL //MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolUl

Number of supported sections per symbol in UL //MaxNumSectionsPerSymbolUl

**Note. In UL, MADE ES1 region limitations are in place (see 5GC002328, CB006466, CB007731). However, in UL, PUSCH and PUCCH never use 2RX receiver on a C4 subcells, so the understanding of 'region' and rrmMaxNumBeamsUlStream is the same. Introduction of UL MU MIMO will need to reconsider this assumption closely. Even if the RU number of beams per symbol is the same as rrmMaxNumBeamsUlStream, it is the number of sections per symbol that represents the regions properly.**

rrmMaxNumBeamsUlSubcell = rrmMaxNumBeamsUlStream

When NRBTS/NRCELL.actIncreaseMaxUlScheduleUE is set to false,

IF (MaxNumSectionsPerSymbolUl < (MaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell + 1 + nActivatedSsPbch (see 5G_UP_82_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) OR

MaxNumBeamsPerSymbolUl < (MaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell + 1 + nActivatedSsPbch (see 5G_UP_82_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) )), where if NRBTS.actEcpri72e==0, MaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell = rrmMaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell; else if NRBTS.actEcpri72e !=0, MaxNumUesFdmUlSubcell =0;

THEN

L2PS shall print out a warning AND

IF NRCELL.actLongPucchF1F3BfOnOranExtT6 is False THEN

apply sector beam to PUCCH Format 3 (not enable the PUCCH Format 3 beamforming).

IF NRCELL.actLongPucchF1F3BfOnOranExtT6 is True THEN

apply SSB beam(s) to PUCCH Format 3 and Format 1, see UP_ALG_2216.

Note. Such a situation is unlikely to happen. All Nokia RUs, as part of required eCPRI functionalities, shall support a minimum number of beams/sections to ensure proper operation. Additionally, a similar check is done in CPRT to protect the cell against such situations. See also 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and R&D parameters defined in: 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4034_replaced([11326839](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Ts0CoPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4038_replaced([11326899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Ts0po_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that in the case of DCM configuration using extension type 6 (NRCELL.actLongPucchF1F3BfOnOranExtT6 is True), it is assumed that this check is not triggered when the rdParameter, as given by CPRT, is set appropriately, as specified in the DoCoMo interface specification.

Else  
rrmMaxNumBeamsUlSubcell = 1

When supercell mode is enabled, refer to 5G_UP_5471_replaced([11353418](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub_hVfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, the L1 subcell used for UE allocation is found by evaluating the mapping table L1 subcell <-> beam ID.**

**For FDD:**

[CB008577-A] If the cell has FDD beamforming enabled ([11111439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) : 

\- rrmMaxNumBeamsUlSubcell shall be: "Number of supported sections per symbol" (refer to cplane [12783847: RU capability based configuration for NetConfigY (ORAN) fronthaul with BF WMP radio](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qCq88o7qEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

else: [End CB008577-A]

  * rrmMaxNumBeamsUlSubcell = 1

---

### 3.30.6.0-2 UL Frequency Domain Scheduling Cross-slot scheduling due to A-CSI scheduling with large A-CSI k2 timing (ID: 11199408)

**3.30.6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11199412`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling If current PUSCH slot was already scheduled for A-CSI users in a previous UL scheduling (see 5G_UP_ALG_4869_replaced([11196426](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCeoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), FD scheduling shall be done cumulatively.

Note that one or more UL schedulers occasions started before could have already scheduled users in current slot for A-CSI transmission. The already consumed resources should be taken into acocunt by the FD scheduler

Note: A-CSI on PUSCH may sometime need to be scheduled with more than k2 distance, due to the restrictions in UE CSI computation time as specified in 38.214 $5.4

---

### 3.30.6.0-3 UL Frequency Domain Scheduling Assignment per priority class (ID: 11199418)

**3.30.6.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11199429`)

UL Frequency Domain Scheduling FD scheduler shall start assigning xPUSCH resources to virtual UEs or UEs with highest priority class. When no xPUSCH resources can be allocated to any UE within a priority class, FD scheduler shall start assigning xPUSCH resources to the next priority class.

Sub-priority defined within a priority class shall be respected whenever allocating resources between different users of the same priority class.

For the virtual UE which may comprise several UEs or only one UE, the priority and sub-priority for the virtual UE see 5G_UP_ALG_9548_replaced([11147793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHn8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

FD scheduler shall be only executed in the primary UL subcell when NRCELL.nrCellType is configured to xDL8UL [CB009363-F] and NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming is equal to 4 [End CB009363-F] and distribute xPUSCH resources to virtual UEs.

---

### 3.30.6.1 Assignment of the number of PRBs

#### 3.30.6.1.0-1 Assignment of the number of PRBs Max PRB restriction with UL PTRS (ID: 13352007)

**3.30.6.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11199441`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs In SA Mode, when UL PT-RS is enabled (refer to 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2249_replaced([11282562](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tpfo3PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), for PUSCH scheduled with dedicated BWP, the following restrictions on PRB allocation for PUSCH shall be applied:

 In UE's transient phase:

o The maximum number of allocated PRBs for PUSCH shall be less than NRB0.

Notes:

  * NRB0 obtained from frequencyDensity when Transform Precoding is disabled for specific UE

  * This requirement is applicable only for initial Connection Setup (not for reconfiguration during setup due for HO)

  * CP OFDM (Transform Precoding disabled) with DCI 0_0 is used for User Reconfiguration Transient Phase.




\- Only the specific scenario for Transient Phase described above is applicable for this max PUSCH PRB restriction. It does not apply for other Transient Phase occurrences, such as CP OFDM / DFT-S-OFDM PUSCH waveform switching (when DFT-S-OFDM is active at cell level, all DCI 0_0 related transmissions use CP OFDM, including for Transient Phase).

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-3 Assignment of the number of PRBs Number of PRBs during C[B,F]RA procedure (ID: 13420174)

**3.30.6.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11199446`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs During both types of RA procedure (Contention Based or Contention Free), the initial transmission of the RA-Msg3 is scheduled by a Random Access Response (RAR). For the RA-Msg3 retransmissions, the DCI Format 0_0 is used, nevertheless, for the CBRA, the DCI Format 0_0 is still scrambled with a TC-RNTI (the RA procedure is not completed and the real UE active BWP and it s state is still not known), whereas for the CFRA, the RA procedure is already completed since the reception of the RA-Msg2 and the DCI Format 0_0 is scrambled for the UE by a C-RNTI in the UE active BWP (in this case, the UE does not decode the FDRA field from the DCI as it is done from the RAR).

For CBRA procedure, after the successful reception of the RA-Msg3, the UE state and its active BWP are known. The contention resolution is a regular uplink grant scrambled with its C-RNTI in the UE active BWP. The rules to allocate the PUSCH resources are the regular ones according to the DCI Format used. 

The number of PRBs to allocate to Msg3 is 3 or 4 RBs according to the number of PUSCH symbols available in the target uplink slot ([13349365: Transport Block size for RA-Msg3 and PRB constraints](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_KVGddOo7EfCnwLcpJWh_Zw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

For the localization of the RA-Msg3 (initial and retransmissions), the U-Plane applies the rules defined in the requirement ([13442631: For UEs scheduled by a RAR (C[B,F]RA: RA-Msg3) or by a DCI Format 0_0 scrambled with a TC-RNTI (CBRA: RA-Msg3 reTx)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3JCzkSYXEfGxldjOZQLezQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-4 Assignment of the number of PRBs Minimal PRBs and TBS for PUSCH transmission (ID: 11199457)

**3.30.6.1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12741227`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs The minimal PRB allowed by PUSCH transmission (for all MCS, both initial Tx and reTx) is defined as:

If NRCELL.actOptPuschPrbAlloc is true

rrmMinSupportedPuschPrb = NRCELL.minPuschPrb

Else

rrmMinSupportedPuschPrb = minSupportedNumPrbsUl (5G_L2_1136_replaced([11183158](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27hiIfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Endif

[PR846547]

The minimal TBS allowed by PUSCH transmission (for all MCS, both initial Tx and reTx) is defined as:

If NRCELL.actOptPuschPrbAlloc is true

[Before PR864990-26R1] 

rrmMinSupportedPuschTbs = NRCELL.ulMinTbs

[PR864990-26R1]

If PUSCH is scheduled by DCI 0-0 during RRC transient phase, and max PRB is not restricted due to UL PTRS in SA mode as per [13352007: Max PRB restriction with UL PTRS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_4dGk4O0eEfC3ocOh_n7w7A)

rrmMinSupportedPuschTbs = max (NRCELL.ulMinTbs, rdUlMinTbsTransientPhase ([13352004: rdUlMinTbsTransientPhase](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_isVSIO0cEfC3ocOh_n7w7A)))

Else

rrmMinSupportedPuschTbs = NRCELL.ulMinTbs

Endif

[End PR864990-26R1] 

Else

rrmMinSupportedPuschTbs = rdUlMinTbs

Endif

[End PR846547]

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-5 Assignment of the number of PRBs Minimal PRBs for PUSCH initial transmission with given MCS (ID: 11199466)

**3.30.6.1.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `13352037`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs With a given MCS, the minimal PRB allowed by PUSCH initial transmission is defined as:

If NRCELL.actOptPuschPrbAlloc is true

If PUSCH is to be scheduled by gNB-DU without knowledge of UE buffer status,

rrmMinPuschPrb is the minimal PRB which is not smaller than max(NRCELL.minPuschPrb, rdMinPrbNoBSR ([13238121: rdMinPrbNoBSR](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_ikh2kr-hEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ))) and the TBS calculated from the MCS/PRB is not smaller than [Before PR864990-26R1]NRCELL.ulMinTbs [ PR864990-26R1] rrmMinSupportedPuschTbs ([11199457: Minimal PRBs and TBS for PUSCH transmission](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_SEJ5IPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End PR864990-26R1].

Else

rrmMinPuschPrb is the minimal PRB which is not smaller than NRCELL.minPuschPrb and the TBS calculated from the MCS/PRB is not smaller than [Before PR864990-26R1]NRCELL.ulMinTbs [ PR864990-26R1] rrmMinSupportedPuschTbs ([11199457: Minimal PRBs and TBS for PUSCH transmission](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_SEJ5IPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End PR864990-26R1].

Endif

If rrmMinPuschPrb is greater than rdMinPuschPrbUpperLimit ([12692112: rdMinPuschPrbUpperLimit (In Module: 5G UP Algorithms)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WvtPEVfiEfCMqIX_zEVxAQ)), then it shall be set to rdMinPuschPrbUpperLimit.

Else

rrmMinPuschPrb = minSupportedNumPrbsUl (5G_L2_1136_replaced([11183158](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27hiIfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Endif

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-6 Assignment of the number of PRBs Minimal PRBs for DRB priority Proactive Uplink Grant transmission in high reliablity mode (ID: 11199477)

**3.30.6.1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11199481`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs The minimal PRB allowed by DRB priority Proactive Uplink Ggrant (PUG) (for all MCS, initial Tx and non adaptive reTx) while UE works in high reliablity is defined as rdMinPRBsAllocForDrbPriorityPugHR( refer to 5G_UP_ALG_32369_replaced([11209690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw1IyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For adaptive retx, the minimal PRB allowed by DRB priority PUG shall follow 5G_UP_ALG_31776_replaced([11199457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-7 Assignment of the number of PRBs PRBs available for PUSCH allocation in dedicated and initial BWP (ID: 11199486)

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11199491`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **Overview**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11199494`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs If long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') and NRCELL.lteNrDssMode is set to none (no DSS) in dedicated BWP, that can be the nth BWPs (5G_UP_ALG_1811_replaced([11110797](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpHXP_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4976_replaced([11199501](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOExvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB006137-BA] This is also apply to FR1 CA UE which is scheduled in SCell uplink. [End CB006137-BA]

If long PUCCH is configured and NRCELL.lteNrDssMode is set to none (no DSS) in initial BWP, please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4979_replaced([11199553](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOEzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If long PUCCH is configured and in an UL slot L2-PS expects A/N of Msg4 or SRB0 message, please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4981_replaced([11199575](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOr0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11199501`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **PRBs for PUSCH in dedicated UL BWP in Long PUCCH case**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `13447756`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs 13447756: Assignment of the number of PRBs If long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') and (NRCELL.lteNrDssMode is set to none (no DSS) or NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true), the allowed PRB area when a PUSCH shall be scheduled in the dedicated Bandwidth Part (see also 5G_UP_ALG_17927_replaced([11110863](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-TPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is defined by:

 If NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='false ':

rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp = longPucchStartPuschPrb + uplinkBwp.location

rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp = longPucchNumPuschPrb

where:

 longPucchStartPuschPrb, longPucchNumPuschPrb and uplinkBwp.location are received in cell setup message for the corresponding Bandwidth Part

 If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), **separately for each dedicated UL Bandwidth Part (BWP), the PRB area available for PUSCH scheduling shall be defined by BWP specific parameters received at cell setup. That are longPucchStartPuschPrb and longPucchNumPuschPrb.**

 If NRCELL.lteNrDssMode > 0 (DSS active) and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false then, as defined by 5GC0001586, the startPrb and the size of the PUSCH area is defined by EN DSS UL SUBCARRIER RESOURCE ALLOCATION REQUEST message (5G_UP_ALG_5180_replaced([11172453](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuMrgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  
For a FDD cell shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true the available PUSCH resources are in NR dedicated area and more resources may be allocated in the LTE shared area (see [12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), [11172453: Handling of UL SUBCARRIER RESOURCE ALLOCATION](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuMrgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)). PUCCH is in NR dedicated area, longPucchStartPuschPrb and longPucchNumPuschPrb are used to determine which PRBs from NR dedicated area ([12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) are available for PUSCH.

 In FR1 TDD and FR1 FDD cell, when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true', startingPrb and the numPrb for PUSCH in each of the dedicated bandwidth parts shall be calculated per slot i as below: 

[PR879881]   
For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) with shared area at bottom of NR carrier ([12576071: DSS alignment](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__6WyYC_pEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))   
rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) = longPucchStartDynPuschPrb + uplinkBwp.location  
else  
[End PR879881]

rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) = longPucchStartDynPuschPrb + numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i) + uplinkBwp.location

If n>1, where n is the number of dedicated bandwidth part per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),  
[PR879881] or for asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) [End PR879881]

rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) = longPucchNumDynPuschPrb - numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i)

else

rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) = longPucchNumDynPuschPrb - 2 * numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i)

where:

 longPucchStartDynPuschPrb and longPucchNumDynPuschPrb and uplinkBwp.location are received in cell setup message.

 numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i) shall be calculated as per 5G_UP_ALG_11637_replaced([11199608](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOr1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 In FR1 TDD and FR1 FDD cell, when NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 ='true', startingPrb and the numPrb for PUSCH in each of the dedicated bandwidth parts shall be calculated per slot i as below:

rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) = longPucchStartDynPuschPrb + numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i) + uplinkBwp.location

If n>1, where n is the number of dedicated bandwidth part per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) = longPucchNumDynPuschPrb - numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i)

else

rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp(i) = longPucchNumDynPuschPrb - 2 * numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i)

where:

 longPucchStartDynPuschPrb and longPucchNumDynPuschPrb and uplinkBwp.location are received in cell setup message.

 numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) shall be calculated as per 5G_UP_ALG_32892_replaced([11199677](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When dedicated BWPs are non-aligned (CB012299) without PRB blanking   
two contiguos PUSCH regions, separated by PUCCH can be defined in the large BWP .

The allowed PRB area when a PUSCH shall be scheduled in the large dedicated Bandwidth Part (see also 5G_UP_ALG_17927_replaced([11110863](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-TPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be recalculated for every such region.

Top area (regionA) :

as defined above by rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp and rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp

Bottom area below long PUCCH (regionB):

rrmStartPuschPrbBelowDedicatedBwp = longPucchStartPuschPrb2 + uplinkBwp.location

rrmNumPuschPrbBelowDedicatedBwp = longPucchNumPuschPrb2

where “longPucchStartPuschPrb2” and “longPucchNumPuschPrb2” parameters are defined by the C-Plane in the cell setup message.

Example 2 regions configuration (CB012299):

[image-0][image_desc]The image displays a table with four columns: "CRB", "Large BWP", "Small BWP", and "PS BWP". The "Initial Bwp" column is partially visible at the top. The "CRB" column lists numbers from 105 down to 0, with some gaps indicated by "...". The other columns contain numerical values, with some cells colored yellow, green, and blue. There are also several text boxes and labels associated with different regions of the table: \- **PUSCH TOP (regionA), size= longPucchNumPuschPrb** : This text box is positioned to the right of the upper section of the table, spanning rows from approximately 105 down to 83 in the "CRB" column. \- **PUCCH (common)** : This text box is located to the right of the "PS BWP" column, roughly aligned with CRB values from 79 down to 45. \- **longPucchStartPuschPrb** : This label is placed horizontally across the "PS BWP" column, between CRB values 79 and 78. \- **Long PUCCH** : This text box is to the right of the "PS BWP" column, aligned with CRB values from 41 down to 35. \- **longPucchStartPuschPrb** : This label is placed horizontally across the "PS BWP" column, between CRB values 41 and 40. \- **CRB=28** : This label is positioned below the table, to the right of the "CRB" column. \- **PUSCH BOTTOM (regionB, below Long PUCCH), size= longPucchNumPushPrb2** : This text box is located below the "Long PUCCH" label, spanning rows from approximately 27 down to 25 in the "CRB" column. \- **longPucchStartPuschPrb2** : This label is placed horizontally across the "PS BWP" column, between CRB values 1 and 0. The table appears to represent resource allocation or mapping for different bandwidth parts (BWPs) and physical resource blocks (PRBs) in a communication system. The colors likely indicate different types of resource assignments or configurations. Here is the table data in markdown format:

CRB | Large BWP | Small BWP | PS BWP

... | ... |  |

... | ... |  |

Notes:

 In FR1 TDD and FR1 FDD cell, when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true', in dedicated Bandwidth part, PUSCH region may expand into the pCSI area of PUCCH by re-using the unused pCSI PRBs in any given slot. Therefore, every UL slot i, rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp and rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp for each of the bandwidth part shall be re-calculated considering the unused PRBs in the pCSI area which can be used for PUSCH in the slot. However, an unused P-CSI PUCCH PRB is only considered if it doesn’t result PUSCH fragmentation i.e., PUSCH PRB region need to be contiguous.

If NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 ='true',**PUSCH region may expand into the whole area of PUCCH by re-using the unused PUCCH PRBs** adjacent to the PUSCH region**** in any given slot. Therefore, every UL slot i, rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp and rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp for each of the bandwidth part shall be re-calculated considering the unused PRBs in the PUCCH area which can be used for PUSCH in the slot. However, an unused PUCCH PRB is only considered if it doesn’t result PUSCH fragmentation i.e., all PRB in the PUSCH region need to be contiguous.****

 Intra-slot frequency hopping is always enabled for pCSI resource and pCSI PRBs in the first and second hop within the slot are allocated symmetrical to the PUSCH region (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2978_replaced([11311435](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrZGQvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), when the cell has a single dedicated bandwidth part. In case, there is a change in this pCSI resource allocation configuration, this requirement need to be modified accordingly too.

 Following picture gives a visualization of how the PUCCH regions are allocated when the cell has a single dedicated BWP. The orange parts are the two pCSI areas at the BW edges. If NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true', The two orange areas can be shared with PUSCH (the green region), in case in the slot some of the pCSI PRB are not allocated (please remember that L2 PS will use only the PRB adjacent to the green region and which are contiguous to it; any unused PRB that however are separated by the green region by PRB allocated to pCSI cannot be used for PUSCH). If NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 ='true', **the whole PUCCH areas (orange, red and blue regions) can be shared with PUSCH (the green region), in case in a given slot some the PUCCH PRB are not allocated (please, remember that L2 PS will use only the PRB adjacent to the green region and which are contiguous to it; any unused PRB that are separated from the green region by PRB allocated to PUCCH, cannot be used by PUSCH).**

[image-1][image_desc]The image is a stacked bar chart showing the distribution of "#PRB" (Physical Resource Blocks) across different categories of "F1/F3 resources". The y-axis is labeled "#PRB" and ranges from 0 to 250, with tick marks at 50, 100, 150, 200, and 250. The x-axis is not explicitly labeled but represents the different resource categories. The stacked bar is composed of four colored segments, from bottom to top: 1\. **Blue:** Labeled "PUCCH F1 for SR" (Scheduling Request). This segment is very small, starting at 0 and ending slightly above 0. 2\. **Red:** Labeled "PUCCH F1/F3 for HARQ AN" (Hybrid Automatic Repeat-reQuest Acknowledgement/Negative Acknowledgement). This segment starts where the blue segment ends and extends to approximately 10. 3\. **Orange:** Labeled "PUCCH F3 for PCSI" (Physical Channel State Information). This segment starts at approximately 10 and extends to approximately 250. 4\. **Green:** Labeled "PUSCH" (Physical Uplink Shared Channel). This segment starts at approximately 250 and extends to the top of the bar, which is slightly above 250. The legend at the bottom of the chart clearly associates each color with its corresponding resource category. The text "F1/F3 resources" is oriented vertically along the right side of the chart.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15657.001.png[/image_path][/image-1]

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `11199553`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **PRBs for PUSCH in initial UL BWP in Long PUCCH case**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `12705105`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs If long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') and (NRCELL.lteNrDssMode is set to none (no DSS) or NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true), the allowed PRB area when a PUSCH shall be scheduled in the initial Bandwith Part (see also 5G_UP_ALG_17927_replaced([11110863](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-TPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is defined by

rrmStartPuschPrbInitialBwp = longPucchStartPuschPrb + uplinkBwp.location

rrmNumPuschPrbInitialBwp = longPucchNumPuschPrb

where

  * longPucchStartPuschPrb, longPucchNumPuschPrb and uplinkBwp.location are received in cell setup message.



Note, if NRCELL.lteNrDssMode > 0 (DSS active) and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false then, as defined by 5GC0001586, the startPrb and the size of the PUSCH area is defined by EN DSS UL SUBCARRIER RESOURCE ALLOCATION REQUEST message (5G_UP_ALG_5180_replaced([11172453](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuMrgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For a FDD cell shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true the available PUSCH resources for Initial BWP are in NR dedicated area (see [12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). PUCCH is in NR dedicated area, longPucchStartPuschPrb and longPucchNumPuschPrb are used to determine which PRBs from NR dedicated area longPucchStartPuschPrb and longPucchNumPuschPrb are used to determine which PRBs from NR dedicated area ([12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) are available for PUSCH. 

Note: For FR1 FDD cell , NRCELL.lteNrDssMode shall be set to none (no DSS) when if NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc is set to enable [CB007372-M] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-M]**.**

If long PUCCH is configured, if NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc is set to enable, and if the UE RRC connection is established in the initial BWP using USS, that is if condition in 5G_UP_ALG_17477_replaced([11110942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is equal true, 5G-L2-PS shall calculate the starting PRB and the number of PRB of the initial bandwidth part as described per slot i as below:

[PR879881, CB007372-M]  
For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) with shared area at bottom of NR carrier ([12576071: DSS alignment](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__6WyYC_pEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg))   
rrmStartPuschPrbInitialBwp(i) = longPucchStartDynPuschPrb + uplinkBwp.location  
else  
[End PR879881, CB007372-M]  
rrmStartPuschPrbInitialBwp(i) = longPucchStartDynPuschPrb + numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i)+ uplinkBwp.location

If n>1, where n is the number of dedicated bandwidth part per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),  
[PR879881, CB007372-M] or for asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) [End PR879881, CB007372-M]

  * rrmNumPuschPrbInitialBwp = longPucchNumDynPuschPrb - numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i)



else

  * rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp = longPucchNumDynPuschPrb - 2 * numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i)



where,

  * longPucchStartDynPuschPrb, longPucchNumDynPuschPrb and uplinkBwp.location are received in cell setup message.
  * numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i) shall be calculated as per 5G_UP_ALG_11637_replaced([11199608](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOr1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))



If long PUCCH is configured, if NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 is set to enable, and if the UE RRC connection is established in the initial BWP using DSS, that is if condition in 5G_UP_ALG_17477_replaced([11110942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is equal true, 5G-L2-PS shall calculate the starting PRB and the number of PRB of the initial bandwidth part as described per slot i as below:

rrmStartPuschPrbInitialBwp(i) = longPucchStartDynPuschPrb + numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i)+ uplinkBwp.location

If n>1, where n is the number of dedicated bandwidth part per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

  * rrmNumPuschPrbInitialBwp = longPucchNumDynPuschPrb - numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i)



else

  * rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp = longPucchNumDynPuschPrb - 2 * numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i)



where,

  * longPucchStartDynPuschPrb, longPucchNumDynPuschPrb and uplinkBwp.location are received in cell setup message.



numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) shall be calculated as per 5G_UP_ALG_32892_replaced([11199677](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-4**  (ID: `11199575`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **PRBs excluded for PUSCH due to Common PUCCH in Long PUCCH case**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `12578024`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs 12578024: Assignment of the number of PRBs If long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') and in an UL slot L2-PS expects A/N of Msg4 or SRB0 message (see 5G_UP_ALG_18087_replaced([11187133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvY-UvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then following CRB areas shall not be allocated for PUSCH as (some of) these CRBs are occupied by the corresponding PUCCH:

Low common PUCCH resource part:

  * starts at rrmStartCrbCommonPucchLowPart = RB_BWP_offset + Initial UL BWP start
  * ends after rrmNumPrbCommonPucch = 8 / N_CS



High common PUCCH resource part:

  * starts at rrmStartCrbCommonPucchHighPart = Initial UL BWP start + Initial UL BWP size - RB_BWP_offset - rrmNumPrbCommonPucch
  * ends after rrmNumPrbCommonPucch



Whereby,

  * RB_BWP_offset and N_CS are defined in 5G_UP_ALG_18087_replaced([11187133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvY-UvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

  * Initial UL BWP size corresponds to bandwidth of uplink initial BWP, see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_1215_replaced([11253792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TnjvIfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

  * Initial UL BWP start corresponds to location of uplink initial BWP, see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_1215_replaced([11253792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TnjvIfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).




The start PRBs relative in a specific BWP are derived by subtracting the start CRB of the BWP, e.g. start PRB of lower common PUCCH resource part within the UL Initial BWP is defined by (rrmStartCrbCommonPucchLowPart – Initial UL BWP start).

Low dedicated PUCCH resource part:

startPrbDedicatedLower = startPrbUlDedicatedBwp

numOfPrbDedicatedLower = rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp

High dedicated PUCCH resource part:

startPrbDedicatedUpper = startPrbUlDedicatedBwp + rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp + rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp

numOfPrbDedicatedUpper = rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp

startPrbUlDedicatedBwp is derived from CellSetup::cellBwp::location, 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2143_replaced([11308391](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrDvIvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The following figure illustrates the different parameters for three exemplary cases:

[image-0][image_desc]The image displays three vertical bars, each representing a different configuration of resources. The bars are labeled with text indicating the type of resources they contain. The leftmost bar is the tallest and is divided into three sections. The top and bottom sections are labeled "Dedicated Long PUCCH Resources". The middle section is labeled "PUSCH". The middle bar is shorter than the leftmost bar and is divided into four sections. The top section is not explicitly labeled but appears to be a resource block. Below this is a section labeled "Common Long PUCCH Resources". Below that is another section labeled "Common Long PUCCH Resources". The bottom section is also not explicitly labeled but appears to be a resource block. A dashed line separates the top section from the "Common Long PUCCH Resources" section, and another dashed line separates the two "Common Long PUCCH Resources" sections. The rightmost bar is the tallest and is divided into three sections. The top section is labeled "Common Long PUCCH Resources". Below this is a section that appears to be a resource block, separated by a dashed line. The bottom section is labeled "Common Long PUCCH Resources". The overall image seems to be a diagram illustrating different resource allocation schemes, possibly in a telecommunications context, differentiating between dedicated and common resources for PUCCH (Physical Uplink Control Channel) and PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel).[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15665.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

Note, if C-Plane configures pucchResourceCommon index 15 then it is guaranteed that Common PUCCH resources don't overlap with Dedicated PUCCH resources but fragment PUSCH area instead.

Note:  
For FR1 TDD and FR1 FDD cell , when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc is ‘True’, rrmStartPuschPrbDedicatedBwp and rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp do represent respectively the minimum number of PRB available to PUSCH and the PUSCH starting PRB when all PUCCH resources are allocated.

[Before CB013152-A-UPD004] Therefore, in slot where initial BWP is expected, all the above statements are valid and are still ruling when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc=True [CB013152-A] or when NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = 'true' in FDD, [END CB013152-A].**Above note is still valid even when NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 is 'True' in a TDD cell.**

[CB013152-A-UPD004]  
Therefore, in slot where initial BWP is expected, all the above statements are valid and are still ruling when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc=True.  
Above note is still valid even when NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 is 'True' in a TDD cell

[End CB013152-A-UPD004]

[CB007372-A-UPD001][CB007372-A] Note: this requirement is applicable to asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.lteNrDssMode > 0 and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) [End CB007372-A][End CB007372-A-UPD001]

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-5**  (ID: `11199608`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Computation of Number of pCSI PRBs unavailable for PUSCH Allocation

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-5.0-6**  (ID: `13447534`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs In FR1 TDD cell and FR1 FDD, when NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true', for a given dedicated bandwidth part, the number of used pCSI PRBs which are unavailable for PUSCH in lower side of the spectrum in any given UL slot i, numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge, is calculated as below

[PR879881]   
For asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) with shared area at bottom of NR carrier ([12576071: DSS alignment](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__6WyYC_pEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))   
**numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = longPucchStartDynPuschPrb +** longPucchNumDynPuschPrb - lowestPCsiPrbUsedInHighEdge(i)  
else  
[End PR879881] 

**numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = highestPCsiPrbUsedInLowEdge(i) - longPucchStartDynPuschPrb + 1**

The highest PRB used for pCSI resource in the lower side of spectrum in the slot i, highestPCsiPrbUsedInLowEdge is given as

highestPCsiPrbUsedInLowEdge(i) = max(usedEndPCsiPrbInLowEdge(i,j) among all UEs j configured with pCSI resource in slot i )

where,

  * If n>1, where n is the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  
[PR879881] or for asymmetric DSS (NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true) with shared area at top of NR carrier ([12576071: DSS alignment](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__6WyYC_pEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End PR879881]
  * usedEndPCsiPrbInLowEdge(i,j) = max(startingPrb(i,j), secondHopPrb(i,j)) + nrofPRBs(i,j) - 1
  * Else
  * usedEndPCsiPrbInLowEdge(i,j) = min(startingPrb(i,j), secondHopPrb(i,j)) + nrofPRBs(i,j) - 1



[PR879881]

The lowest PRB used for pCSI resource in the higher side of spectrum in the slot i, lowestPCsiPrbUsedInHighEdge is given as

lowestPCsiPrbUsedInHighEdge(i) = min(usedStartPCsiPrbInHighEdge(i,j) among all UEs j configured with pCSI resource in slot i )

where,  
usedStartPCsiPrbInHighEdge = min(startingPrb(i,j), secondHopPrb(i,j)) 

[End PR879881]

The longPucchStartDynPuschPrb is described in 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2876_replaced([11310329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrPVSPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The startingPrb, secondHopPrb and nrofPRBs are the IEs provided in the configured pCSI resource in slot i for the UE j sent via the PUCCH-config structure in RRC.

If a UE is configured with multiple pCSI resources in the same slot, all these resources shall be considered in highestPCsiPrbUsedInLowEdge calculation.

If there are no UEs configured with pCSI resource in a slot i, then the complete pCSI PUCCH region shall be considered as available for PUSCH in the slot. So, numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i) for the slot i shall be set to 0.

If NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 = true and

if SRCX_STATE = SRCX_IDLE then above computations stays unchanged

else if SRCX_STATE = SRCX_CONFIGURED then

if usedEndPCsiPrbInLowEdge(i,j)> offsetForSRCX + #PRB(SRCX) + longPucchStartDynPuschPrb then usedEndPCsiPrbInLowEdge(i,j) is unchanged and computed as described above

else if usedEndPCsiPrbInLowEdge(i,j< offsetForSRCX + longPucchStartDynPuschPrb

then if SRCXisEmpty is true then usedEndPCsiPrbInLowEdge(i,j) is unchanged and computed as described above

else usedEndPCsiPrbInLowEdge(i,j) = usedEndSRCXPrb(i)

Where usedEndSRCXPrb(i) is calculated in requirement 5G_UP_ALG_32888_replaced([11199655](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOr3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

And where:

**SRCX_STATE: see algo 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16374_replaced([11312800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrlTifvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

**offsetForSRCX: Offset in PRBs for the start of the SRCX area above the start of the pCSI area (and, in case PUCCH PRBs are at both edges, same offset is used at the upper edge) see algo 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16389_replaced([11312833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrlTkPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

**#PRB(SRCX): see algo 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16359_replaced([11310157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrOHJvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

**SRCXisEmpty(i): see algo 5G_UP_ALG_32888_replaced([11199655](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOr3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-6**  (ID: `11199641`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **Computation of Number of SR PRB allocated in SR regions**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12578145`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Computation of number of F1 PRB for SR and HARQ allocated in FDD

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12578160`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs This requirement applies only to FDD cells, and it is required when:

[Before CB013152-A-UDP4]

NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE in FDD cell and feature CB012149 is not active in the cell (for CB012149 activation in the cell and its impact on the PUCCH configuration, please check [12657228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fLJmsUhSEfC0Ye3M8ARUWQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

[CB013152-A-UDP4]

NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 = TRUE in FDD cell and NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE in the cell (for CB012149 activation in the cell and its impact on the PUCCH configuration please check [12657228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fLJmsUhSEfC0Ye3M8ARUWQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

[End CB013152-A-UDP4]

Under this configuration, it shall be considered that SR and Format 1 HARQ share the PRBs of PUCCH configured to support Format 1 resources.

At every UL slot, the U_plane already keeps track of the SR resources allocated and expected in the slot, and at every UL slot, the L2-PS already keeps track of the F1 resources HARQ allocated in the slot.

If HarqF3isEmpty = FALSE then SRandHARQareEmpty is set to FALSE

else if HARQ or SR resources are used in the slot then (SRandHARQareEmpty is set to FALSE and

if n>1, where n is the number of dedicated BWP per cell (see [11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

then (usedendSRandHARQPrb shall be set either to 2 or, if minNumPucchPrb=0, to 1),

else (in case of single dedicated BW part) usedendSRandHARQPrb shall be set to 1),

else no SR and HARQ F1 resources are allocated in the region in slot (i), and U_plane shall set SRandHARQareEmpty to TRUE.

In case NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE in FDD and in the parameter list NRBTS.NRDU.ulTxSwitchingSupportList is present the value "220", the computation shall be done as described in requirement: [12584210](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_bzXS4DTHEfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `11199645`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Computation of PRB allocated in SRBR region

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11199649`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **Determination of SRBRisEmpty**

At every UL slot (i), the U_plane already keeps track of the SR resources allocated and expected in the slot (i).

If HARQisEmpty = TRUE (5G_UP_ALG_32887_replaced([11199667](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then L2-PS shall determine, among all the SR resources expected in slot(i), if any SR resource is allocated in the SRBR region (this SRBR region is ALWAYS made of TWO PRBs only) and, if yes, shall set SRBRisEmpty to FALSE.

If n>1, where n is the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), usedEndSRBRPrb shall be set to the index value of the PRB in SRBR region which is occupying the PRB nearer to the BW center of the cell, increased by 1, otherwise (in case of single dedicated BW part) usedEndSRBRPrb shall be set to 2.

In case no SR resource is allocated in the SRBR region in slot (i), then U_plane shall set SRBRisEmpty to TRUE.

If HARQisEmpty = FALSE (5G_UP_ALG_32887_replaced([11199667](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then U_plane shall force SRBRisEmpty to FALSE.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `11199655`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Computation of PRB allocated in SRCX region

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-6.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11199662`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **Determination of usedEndSRCXPrb(i)** and SRCXisEmpty****

This requirement applies only to FDD.

When NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = FALSE in FDD, this computation is not required.

When NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE in FDD, L2-PS is aware if F3 HARQ PRBs of PUCCH are configured to support Carrier Aggregation in the cells.

At every UL slot (i), the L2-PS already keeps track of the F3 HARQ resources allocated and expected in the slot (i).

If SRCX_STATE = SRCX_IDLE then SRCXisEmpty is always TRUE.

If SRCX_STATE = SRCX_CONFIGURED then L2-PS shall determine, among all the SR resources expected in slot(i), which one is occupying the PRB nearer to the BW center of the cell, then deliver this value in usedEndSRCXPrb(i) and set SRCXisEmpty to FALSE.

The value usedEndSRCXPrb(i) shall be absolute, i.e it should be calculated from longPucchStartDynPuschPrb.

In case no SR resource is allocated in the SRCX region in slot (i), then L2-PS shall set SRCXisEmpty to TRUE.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7**  (ID: `12658055`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **Computation of HARQ PRB allocated in HARQ regions (FDD and TDD)**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11199667`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **Computation of Number of HARQ PRB allocated in HARQ region**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12584213`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **Determination of usedEndHARQPrb(i) and HARQisEmpty in FR1 TDD**

At every UL slot (i), the L2-PS already keeps track of the F1 and F3 HARQ resources allocated in the slot (i).

If pCSIisEmpty is FALSE then HARQisEmpty shall be forced to FALSE.

If pCSIisEmpty is TRUE, then L2-PS shall determine, among all the HARQ resources expected in slot (i), which one is occupying the PRB more near to the BW center of the cell, and deliver this value in usedEndHARQPrb(i), if n>1, where n is the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), otherwise it should twice the value and deliver it in in usedEndHARQPrb(i),(this because in single dedicated BW mode the PUCCH is at the two edges of the BW).

The value usedEndHARQPrb(i) shall be absolute i.e it should be calculated from longPucchStartDynPuschPrb.

In case no HARQ resource is allocated in the HARQ region in slot (i), then L2-PS shall set HARQisEmpty to TRUE, otherwise HARQisEmpty is set to FALSE.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `12584210`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Determination of Format 1 SR and HARQ utilization in a FDD cell when only one feature activation flag of CB013152 is active

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12658082`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs This requirement applies only to FDD cells, and it is required when:

[Before CB013152-A-UPD004]

NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE in FDD cell, and feature CB012149 is active in the cell (for CB012149 activation in the cell and its impact on the PUCCH configuration, please check [12657228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fLJmsUhSEfC0Ye3M8ARUWQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

[CB013152-A-UPD004]

NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 =TRUE and NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = FALSE in FDD cell.

[End CB013152-A-UPD004]

[Before CB013152-A-UPD004]

The interworking with feature CB012149 requires a specific PUCCH layout, which is identical to the one defined when: 

NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = FALSE.

[End CB013152-A-UPD004]

Under this ~~dedicated~~PUCCH layout, only a single OCC, (OCC0) per Format 1 PRB is supported, and therefore, PUCCH Format 1 HARQ and Format1 SR do not share the same PRBs (too few resources per PRB are available under this configuration for having a meaningful sharing).

Consequently, the calculations related to used SR and Format 1 HARQ PRBs are different and are defined in this dedicated requirement.

Please, remind as well that the setting NRCELL/reqMinNumPuschPrb = 0 does not have effects on this specific configuration (there is no specific reduced PUCCH configuration for this PUCCH layout and in case of setting NRCELL/reqMinNumPuschPrb = 0 the PUCCH layout will not support CB012149).

[Before CB013152-A-UPD004]

When the FDD cell supports the feature CB012149, distinct dedicated Format 1 PRBs are configured in PUCCH region for the HARQ feedback and for SR separately.

[CB013152-A-UPD004]

When the FDD cell supports this configuration (i.e. NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 =TRUE and NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = FALSE), distinct dedicated Format 1 PRBs are configured in PUCCH region for the HARQ feedback and for SR separately.

[End CB013152-A-UPD004]

At every UL slot, the L2-PS already keeps track of the Format 1 HARQ resources allocated and expected in the slot, then the following shall happen for Format 1 HARQ:

  * If HarqF3isEmpty is FALSE, then HarqF1isEmpty shall be forced to FALSE and SRisEmpty shall be forced to FALSE.
  * If HarqF3isEmpty is TRUE, then L2-PS shall verify if Format1 HARQ resources are expected in the slot (i) and if yes then HarqF1isEmpty shall be set to FALSE.
  * If HarqF3isEmpty is TRUE, and L2-PS has verified that Format 1 HARQ resources are expected in slot (i) then:



L2-PS shall set value usedEndF1HARQPrb(i) = 4, if n>1 where n is the number of dedicated BWP per cell, (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). else if n=1, L2-PS shall set usedEndF1HARQPrb(i) = 3, (this because in single dedicated BW mode the PUCCH is at the two edges of the BW).

  * If HarqF3isEmpty is TRUE, and L2-PS has verified that no Format 1 HARQ resources are expected in slot (i) then HarqF1isEmpty shall be set to TRUE.



At every UL slot, the U_plane already keeps as well track of the SR Format 1 resources allocated and expected in the slot, then the following shall happen for SR:

  * If HarqF3isEmpty is FALSE, then SRisEmpty shall be forced to FALSE.
  * If HarqF3isEmpty is TRUE and If HarqF1isEmpty = FALSE then SRisEmpty is set to "FALSE".

  * If HarqF3isEmpty is TRUE and If HARQF1isEmpty is TRUE and if SR resources are used in the slot, then SRisEmpty is set to FALSE and, when n (where n is the number of dedicated BWP per cell, see 5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))) is equal to:

  * n = 1, then usedEndSRPrb shall be set to 2.
  * n > 1, then used SR resources in the slot are computed as:
    * if allocated SR resources are only in the 2 outermost PRBs, then usedEndSRPrb shall be set to 2, else (i.e. when the SR resources are placed as well on the PRBs above the HARQ Format 1 region), then the usedEndSRPrb shall be set to 6 and HARQF1isEmpty shall be forced to FALSE and usedEndF1HARQPrb(i) = 4.
  * If no SR Format 1 resources are allocated in the SR region in that slot, then U_plane shall set SRisEmpty to TRUE and set usedEndSRPrb to 0.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `13333316`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs FDD cell: how to determine F3 HARQ utilization

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13333232`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs This requirement applies only in FDD.

Under this configuration, it shall be considered that SR and Format 1 HARQ share the PRBs of PUCCH configured to support Format 1 resources.

When the FDD cell supports the carrier aggregation, F3 resources are configured in PUCCH region for the HARQ feedback of the carrier aggregated cells.

At every UL slot, the L2-PS already keeps track of the F3 HARQ resources allocated and expected in the slot.

If no carrier aggregation is supported in the FDD cell, the F3 HARQ PRB are not configured in the PUCCH and in this case the following shall be forced:

  * HARQF3isEmpty = TRUE
  * usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 0



If carrier aggregation is supported in the FDD cell, then the following shall happen:

  * If pCSIisEmpty is FALSE, then HARQF3isEmpty shall be forced to FALSE.
  * If pCSIisEmpty is TRUE, and L2-PS has verified that F3 HARQ resources are expected in slot (i) then HarqF3isEmpty shall be set to FALSE and if n (where n is the number of dedicated BWP per cell, see 5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))):
  * when NRCELL.actdynUlResAllocPhase2 = TRUE and NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE in the FDD cell.
  * n = 1, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 2 or if NRCELL.reqMinNumPuschPrb = 0, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 1.
  * n > 1, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 4 or if NRCELL.reqMinNumPuschPrb = 0, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 2.
  * Computation when NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 = TRUE and NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = FALSE in the FDD cell.
  * n = 1, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 4.
  * n > 1, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 8.

  * If pCSIisEmpty is TRUE, and L2-PS has verified that no F3 HARQ resources are expected in slot (i) then HarqF3isEmpty shall be forced to TRUE.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-4**  (ID: `12578250`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Determination of F3 HARQ utilization in a FDD cell

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12578260`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Computation when NRCELL.actdynUlResAllocPhase2 = TRUE and NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE in the FDD cell.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12657234`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs [Before CB013152-A-UPD004]

This requirement applies only to FDD cells, and it is required when:

NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE in FDD cell and feature CB012149 is not active in the cell (for CB012149 activation in the cell and its impact on the PUCCH configuration, please check [12657228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_fLJmsUhSEfC0Ye3M8ARUWQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Under this configuration, it shall be considered that SR and Format 1 HARQ share the PRBs of PUCCH configured to support Format 1 resources.

When the FDD cell supports the carrier aggregation, F3 resources are configured in PUCCH region for the HARQ feedback of the carrier aggregated cells.

At every UL slot, the L2-PS already keeps track of the F3 HARQ resources allocated and expected in the slot.

If no carrier aggregation is supported in the FDD cell, the F3 HARQ PRB are not configured in the PUCCH and in this case the following shall be forced:

  * HARQF3isEmpty = TRUE
  * usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 0



If carrier aggregation is supported in the FDD cell, then the following shall happen:

  * If pCSIisEmpty is FALSE, then HARQF3isEmpty shall be forced to FALSE.
  * If pCSIisEmpty is TRUE, and L2-PS has verified that F3 HARQ resources are expected in slot (i) then HarqF3isEmpty shall be set to FALSE and if n (where n is the number of dedicated BWP per cell, see 5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))):
  * n = 1, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 2 or if NRCELL.reqMinNumPuschPrb = 0, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 1.
  * n > 1, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 4 or if NRCELL.reqMinNumPuschPrb = 0, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 2.

  * If pCSIisEmpty is TRUE, and L2-PS has verified that no F3 HARQ resources are expected in slot (i) then HarqF3isEmpty shall be forced to TRUE.




In case instead NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE in FDD and activated CB012149 the computation shall be done as described in requirement: [12686968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_MLadYFNiEfCnPp04H8v-AQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[END CB013152-A-UPD004]

Replaced by [13333316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ZWDxcNaCEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12657228`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Computation when NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 = TRUE and NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = FALSE in the FDD cell.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-7.0-4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12657238`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs [Before CB013152-A-UPD004]

This requirement applies only to FDD, and it is applied only when:

NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE in FDD and

in the parameter list NRBTS.NRDU.ulTxSwitchingSupportList is present the value "220" and 

the parameter NRCELL.longPucchConfigType is equal to 0 (equal to "duActCAPucchConfig")

and if NRBTS.NRDU.actInterNumerologyCA is set to to TRUE or 

the parameter NRBTS.NRDU.flexibleCAMode is not equal to 0 (i.e. not equal to "basicCaMode").

Explicative Note:

The feature CB012149 does not have a boolean feature activation flag for its activation, but this enumeration parameter is used instead: 

NRBTS.NRDU.ulTxSwitchingSupportList

the above-mentioned value "220" implies that the feature is active in a BTS, but it is necessary to determine if the feature is active in the cell too (this depends from the CA configuration defined in the cell).

For this reason only when in the list NRBTS.NRDU.ulTxSwitchingSupportList the value "220" is included, and CA is configured in the cell (i.e. when NRCELL.longPucchConfigType is set to 0 and NRBTS.NRDU.actInterNumerologyCA is set to TRUE or NRBTS.NRDU.flexibleCAMode is not equal to "basicCAMode") then CB012149 feature is enabled in the FDD cell.

The interworking with feature CB012149 when is activated in a cell requires a specific PUCCH layout, which is identical to the one defined when: 

NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = FALSE.

Only a single OCC, (OCC0) per F1 PRB is supported under this PUCCH layout, and therefore PUCCH Format 1 HARQ and Format1 SR do not share the same PRBs, because the PUCCH Format 1 resources per PRB are too few for having a meaningful sharing.  
Consequently, the calculations are different and are defined as a dedicated requirement.

The setting NRCELL/reqMinNumPuschPrb = 0 does not have an effect on this specific configuration (there is no specific reduced PUCCH configuration for this PUCCH layout).

When the FDD cell supports the carrier aggregation as Pcell, F3 resources are configured in PUCCH region for the HARQ feedback of the carrier aggregated cells.

At every UL slot (i), the L2-PS already keeps track of the F3 HARQ resources allocated and expected in the slot (i).

If no carrier aggregation is supported in the FDD cell, the F3 HARQ PRB are not configured in the PUCCH and in this case the following shall be forced:

HarqF3isEmpty = TRUE

usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 0

If carrier aggregation is supported in the FDD cell, then the following shall happen:

  * If pCSIisEmpty is FALSE, then HARQF3isEmpty shall be forced to FALSE.
  * If pCSIisEmpty is TRUE, and L2-PS has verified that F3 HARQ resources are expected in slot (i) then HarqF3isEmpty shall be set to FALSE and if n (where n is the number of dedicated BWP per cell, see 5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))):
  * n = 1, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 4.
  * n > 1, then usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) = 8.

  * If pCSIisEmpty is TRUE, and L2-PS has verified that no F3 HARQ resources are expected in slot (i) then HarqF3isEmpty shall be forced to TRUE.  
[END CB013152-A-UPD004]




Replaced by [13333316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ZWDxcNaCEfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-8**  (ID: `11199677`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Computation of Number of PUCCH PRBs unavailable for PUSCH Allocation

---

**3.30.6.1.0-7.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `12580978`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs In FR1 TDD cell, when NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 =TRUE, for a given dedicated bandwidth part, the number of used PUCCH PRB PRBs which are unavailable for PUSCH in lower side of the spectrum in any given UL slot i, numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge, is calculated as in the following.

If SRBRisEmpty is TRUE

then numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = 0

else if HARQisEmpty is TRUE (5G_UP_ALG_32887_replaced([11199667](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

then numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = usedEndSRBRPrb,

else If pCSIisEmpty is TRUE

then numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = usedEndHARQPrb(i)

else numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i)

Where numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i) has been calculated as described in 5G_UP_ALG_11637_replaced([11199608](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOr1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB013152-A]

[Before CB013152-A-UPD004]

In FDD FR1 cell, when NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = True, the computation depends on the activation of CB012149 (activated by NRBTS.NRDU.ulTxSwitchingSupportList) and on the effective activation of Carrier Aggregation feature in the cell.

[CB013152-A-UPD004]

In FDD FR1 cell, when NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 = True, the computation depends on configuration of NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt and on the effective activation of Carrier Aggregation feature in the cell.

[End CB013152-A-UPD004]

[Before CB013152-A-UPD004]

Note:

CB012149 feature is enabled in a FDD cell.only when in the list NRBTS.NRDU.ulTxSwitchingSupportList the value "220" is included and CA is configured in the cell (i.e. when NRCELL.longPucchConfigtype is set to 0 and NRBTS.NRDU.actInterNumerologyCA is set to to TRUE or NRBTS.NRDU.flexibleCAMode is not equal to 0).

Therefore, for FDD cell, when NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = True, for a given dedicated bandwidth part, the number of used PUCCH PRBs which are unavailable for PUSCH in lower side of the spectrum in any given UL slot, numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge, is calculated as in the following:

[End CB013152-A-UPD004]

[CB013152-A-UPD004]

Therefore, for FDD cell, when NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 = True for a given dedicated bandwidth part, the number of used PUCCH PRBs which are unavailable for PUSCH in lower side of the spectrum in any given UL slot, numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge, is calculated as in the following:

[End CB013152-A-UPD004]

[Before CB013152-A-UPD004]

1) If CB012149 is not activated in the cell (please check if conditions in above note are not satisfied):

[CB013152-A-UPD004]

1) NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE:

[End CB013152-A-UPD004]

For a given dedicated bandwidth part, the number of used PUCCH PRBs which are unavailable for PUSCH in lower side of the spectrum in any given UL slot i, numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge, is calculated as in the following.

If SRandHARQareEmpty is TRUE (see [12578145](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_cxVNoTDCEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) then numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = 0

else

if HarqF3isEmpty is TRUE (see [12578250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_9ZoFsDDTEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg)) then numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = usedEndSRandHARQPrb , else If pCSIisEmpty is TRUE

then 

if usedEndF3HARQPrb(i)=0 then numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = usedendSRandHARQPrb else numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = usedEndF3HARQPrb(i) (see [12578250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_9ZoFsDDTEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg))

else numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i)

Where numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i) has been calculated as described in 5G_UP_ALG_11637_replaced([11199608](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOr1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before CB013152-A-UPD004]

2) If CB012149 is activated in the cell (please check if conditions in above note are satisfied)

[CB013152-A-UPD004]

2) NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = FALSE:

[End CB013152-A-UPD004]

[Before CB013152-A-UPD004]The interworking with feature CB012149 requires a specific PUCCH layout, which is identical to the one defined when: 

NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = FALSE. [End CB013152-A-UPD004]

Under this ~~dedicated~~ legacy PUCCH layout, only a single OCC, (OCC0) per F1 PRB is supported, and therefore PUCCH Format 1 HARQ and Format1 SR do not share the same PRBs (too few resources per PRB are available under this configuration for having a meaningful sharing).

If SRisEmpty is TRUE (see [12584210](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_bzXS4DTHEfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) then numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = 0

else if HARQF1isEmpty is TRUE (see [12584210](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_bzXS4DTHEfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) then

numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = usedEndSRPrb

else if HARQF3isEmpty is TRUE (see [12578250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_9ZoFsDDTEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg)) then 

numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = max(usedEndSRPrb,usedEndF1HARQPrb)

else If pCSIisEmpty is TRUE then 

numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = usedEndF3HARQPrb(i)(see [12578250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_9ZoFsDDTEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg))

else numUsedPrbsInPucchEdge(i) = numUsedPCsiPrbsInPucchEdge(i)

[End CB013152-A]

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-8 Assignment of the number of PRBs PRB limitation for PUSCH allocation in dedicated and inital BWP (ID: 11199687)

**3.30.6.1.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11199700`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs PRB limitation for PUSCH allocation in dedicated and inital BWP

[Before PR755530]

Only for TDD configuration, i.e. duplexMode=TDD, and when FR1, i.e. frequencyRange=FR1, is used,

then if UE allocation on PRBs on PUSCH with MSC0 exceeds rdMaxNumPrbAtMcs0Rank1(5G_L2_1347_replaced([11182903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF7fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for rank1 or rdMaxNumPrbAtMcs0Rank2(5G_L2_1348_replaced([11182919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fs8PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for rank2 transmission the number of PRBs for allocation shall be forced to rdMaxNumPrbAtMcs0Rank1 or rdMaxNumPrbAtMcs0Rank2 respectively.

[PR755530]

Note that this limitation for the introduction of R&D parameter rdMaxNumPrbAtMcs0Rank1 and rdMaxNumPrbAtMcs0Rank2 was originally intended to contain size of Sub-block deinterleaving buffering in decoder but this L1 limitation has been removed in 5G21A/B when sub-block de-interleaver has been deeply enhanced to optimize buffer size.

[End PR755530]

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-9 Assignment of the number of PRBs PRB limitation for PUSCH retransmission (ID: 11199705)

**3.30.6.1.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11199710`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Possible HARQ retransmissions shall allocate the same number of PRBs as allocated by the initial UL transmission [CB008662-A] if the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) or the conditions defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are not fulfilled. If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, the PRB assignment follows the rules defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11970_replaced([11202476](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and reserved MCS is used as defined by 5G_UP_ALG_12913_replaced([11202887](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwd8aPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008662-A]

The start PRB of the HARQ retransmission can be different from that allocated by the initial transmission

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-10 Assignment of the number of PRBs PRB Assignment after the BWP Switch (ID: 11199715)

**3.30.6.1.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11199721`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Once the or any switch of bandwidth part is terminated, PUSCH is granted onto the active UE bandwidth part (5G_UP_ALG_1823_replaced([11110957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJMY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the number of UL PRBs is selected as described below.

The UE bandwidth part is defined such that the full carrier bandwidth (see 5G_UP_ALG_1795_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is contained in it.

If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(, the UE dedicated uplink bandwidth part (5G_UP_ALG_1795_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equal or smaller than the full carrier bandwidth (see 5G_UP_ALG_1795_replaced([11110609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If the long PUCCH is selected (IE pucchModeSelect in PsCell_CellSetupReq is set to longPucch) and NRCELL.lteNrDssMode = 0 (no DSS), the max. PUSCH bandwidth is restricted to the UL PRBs after PUCCH configuration and the size of the initial BWP when a Common Search Space (Type1 or Type3 PDCCH) is used to send the grant with a DCI Format 0_0 scrambled with a C-RNTI. The available PUSCH PRBs are defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced([11199486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOEw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The UL FD scheduler allocates the frequency resources as determined in 5G_UP_ALG_4988_replaced([11199729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The frequency domain scheduler shall check if the number of PRBs that are assigned to the UE can be further reduced as described in 5G_UP_ALG_5102_replaced([11203503](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In PRACH slots, the allocation of frequency resources is determined in 5G_UP_ALG_4988_replaced([11199729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-11 Assignment of the number of PRBs UL FD resource allocation for multiple UEs using FDM (ID: 11199729)

**3.30.6.1.0-11.0-6**  (ID: `13361572`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs For a list of virtual UEs or UEs if it satisfies the requirement in 5G_UP_ALG_8852_replaced ([11146317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksD9g_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), or to be scheduled using FDM do the following:

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), after allocating FD resources to each UE in every step mentioned below, gNB-DU shall determine the number of layer PRBs consumed (**rrmNumLayerPrbUsedPerAllocation described in 5G_UP_ALG_26582_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and use it to keep track of the used, as well as remaining layer PRB resources (**rrmNumUsedPuschLayerPrbPerSlot, **rrmRemainingPuschLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G_UP_ALG_26582_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). gNB-DU shall also ensure that the constraint related to layer PRBs mentioned in 5G_UP_ALG_26661_replaced([11214618](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOxYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is maintained. In case allocating the required resources to a UE breaks the constraint, the UE shall be allocated the maximum possible resources. The next UE in the list shall be checked for allocation as long as there are still layer PRB resources available. Further UE allocation and scheduling in all steps shall be stopped if there are no more layer PRB resources available for scheduling in the current slot.******

1-If there are FD resources available, give UEs which are candidate of srResp request class and the UE has a prioritized DC-GBR LCG: **needed resources to allocate SR payload, namely PayloadSr, see 5G_UP_ALG_12751_replaced ([11203952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

[CB014699-B]

2-If there are FD resources available, give UEs with critical SRB or critical 5QI5/69 transmission, the amount of FD resources as indicated in [11199753](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwP59PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). Note: This existing requirement of FD resource allocation for SRB is equally applied for critical SRB (which are candidate of critical srbTraffic).

3-If there are FD resources available, give UEs with critical 5QI1/65/66 transmission, the amount of FD resources as indicated in [11199776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwP5-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) . Note: This existing requirement of FD resource allocation for VoNR(5QI-1/65/66) is equally applied for critical VoNR (which are candidate of critical voipTraffic) [End CB014699-B].

[CB014699-C]

4-If there are FD resources available, give UEs with criticalGbrTraffic data the amount of FD resources as indicated in [11199798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwP5_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Note: This existing requirement of FD resource allocation for GBR(excluding 5QI-1/65/66) is equally applied for critical GBR (which are candidate of criticalGbrTraffic) [End CB014699-C].

[CB014699-F]

5-If there are FD resources available, give UEs with criticalDrbTraffic data the prioritized amount of FD resources as indicated in [13404784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kltlhgygEfGu_4itz1o3RA). [End CB014699-F].

6-Give UEs with SRB or 5QI5/69 transmission the amount of FD resources as indicated in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced ([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

7-If there are FD resources available, give UEs with dcGbrTraffic data the amount of FD resources as indicated in 5G_UP_ALG_30802_replaced ([11199765](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwP59vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

8-If there are FD resources available, give UEs with 5QI1/65/66 voipTraffic data the amount of FD resources as indicated in 5G_UP_ALG_15400_replaced ([11199776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwP5-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
