9-If there are FD resources available, give UEs with 5QI1/65/66 deprioritizedVoip data the amount of FD resources as indicated in 5G_UP_ALG_15400_replaced ([11199776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwP5-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

10-If there are FD resources available, give UEs with gbrTraffic data the amount of FD resources as indicated in 5G_UP_ALG_8064_replaced ([11199798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwP5_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). 

11-If there are FD resources available give UEs with non-GBR NBR bearers which have positive token bucket (NBR Category), the amount of resources as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_22159_replaced ([11199843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

12-Calculate available FD resources = the FD resources in the BW (5G_UP_ALG_4947_replaced([11198980](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwLBfvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) - FD resources allocated in previous steps, and distribute remaining resource to below initial transmission UEs for the non-GBR which includes:

  * [CB014699-F] critical non-GBR bearers after distributed in step 5, [End CB014699-F]
  * MC-nGBR(5QI70),
  * normal non-GBR,
  * NBR excessive data after distributed in step 11,
  * [CB014699-C] deprioritized critical GBR bearers, [End CB014699-C]
  * deprioritized MC-GBR(5QI67) **bearer, GBR bearers,**
  * DC-GBR excessive data after distributed in step 7 and deprioritized DC-GBR bearer.



13-If there are FD resources available, distribute these resources to initial transmission UEs as in 5G_UP_ALG_4994_replaced([11199854](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If NRCELL.actDemoteProactUlSched is TRUE, UEs from ulProactSched class shall be excluded from this distribution round.

14-If NRCELL.actDemoteProactUlSched is TRUE, distribute remaining resources, if any, to UEs from ulProactSched class as per 5G_UP_ALG_15683_replaced ([11201000](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwS9TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

15-Allocate resources to all UEs in the list using the random allocation algorithm 5G_UP_ALG_5026_replaced([11201742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If UCI is expected to be multiplexed in an initial PUSCH transmission, then the minimum amount of FD resources assigned to a UE shall be as determined in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) condition (7).

If link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency for UL is enabled ([12472235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_OeKtoA1aEfCuhewgqkUVKQ "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_tauaapvfee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf")) and if rdActUlPostWrrAllocOpt ([12575755](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_ReVSYS_dEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2ftx_revsys_defcrmdp0nuo3hg&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf")) is True, gNB-DU shall perform the local PRB optimization (described in [12495965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_8LAUMBQ8EfCph-OiSyk16Q "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2ftx_8laumbq8efcph-oisyk16q&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf")) for a power limited UE ([11130171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq-_ffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_tauaapvfee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf")). This procedure can be triggered at either one or the other point of the FD resources distribution, as indicated below:

  * If the UL Low Complexity WRR scheduler is used, and the UE is considered by the WRR-sch, the local PRB optimization is done just after FD resource are distributed to that UE by the WRR-sch considering total PRBs (from WRR and priority PRBs) allocated to the UE.

  * If gNB-DU chooses exhaustive UL scheduling (E-ULS) as [11199874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), and the UE is considered by E-ULS, the local PRB optimization is performed during E-ULS, see [12543746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__-uPACGFEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9): SubareaAssignment and Redistribution for details.




In case of A-CSI scheduling in mode UCI only, the exact allocation size shall be computed with 5G_UP_ALG_3559_replaced([11159589](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktKww_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **FD scheduler shall not increase this allocation above the minimum supported (see 5G_UP_ALG_4999_replaced ([11199901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**.****

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-12 Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution to SRBs of initial transmission UEs (ID: 11199753)

**3.30.6.1.0-12.0-2**  (ID: `12436237`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to empty the SRBs' buffered data a UE has, [PR817840] if there is enough PUSCH RBs available in the slot.

If during the PUSCH RB allocation process, the U-Plane detects that the number of available RB is lower than this value but at least equal or greater than smallest PUSCH allocation defined by minSupportedNumPrbsUl (5G_L2_1136_replaced([11183158](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27hiIfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then the initial transmission can be reduced to the available PUSCH RBs. If the condition is not met, the UE is not scheduled in the slot.

To estimate the minimum number of RBs to flush the LCG Id handling the SRBs of the UE (before any reduction), the U-Plane has to find the first TBS (5G_UP_ALG_5085_replaced([11203349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) , with the proposed MCS rrmUlProposedMcs (calculated while assuming all PUSCH RBs in the UE active BWP are available), the number of PUSCH symbols expected in the slot, greater than sum( rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[i] ) * 8 , where i is the LCG ID of configured SRBs as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_10514_replaced([11203665](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [EndPR817840]

[Before PR817840] ; This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS (5G_UP_ALG_5085_replaced([11203349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the proposed MCS rrmUlProposedMcs (calculated while assuming full BW allocation) to be greater than sum( rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[i] ) * 8 , where i is the LCG ID of configured SRBs as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_10514_replaced([11203665](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End PR817840]

Note: msg3 is considered as initial BWP transmission (whereas it is in in the current UE active BWP which is not necessarily the initial BWP), while for msg5, FD resources needed are indicated in 5G_UP_ALG_3455_replaced([11153560](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksnXIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Thus, the calculation indicated in previous line is not needed in their case

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-13 Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution to DC-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs (ID: 11199765)

**3.30.6.1.0-13.0-1**  (ID: `11199769`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to meet the desired allocation for DC-GBR bearers that have pending data. This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS with the proposed MCS rrmUlProposedMcs _to be greater than dcGbrDesiredBytesi,LCG_j * 8._

where

**_*[Before CB008600-B-UPD001]_** *

dcGbrDesiredBytesi,LCG_j = min{(bufferHousekeeping(i,LCG_j), MDBV} + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize_bytes

bufferHousekeeping(i,LCG_j) buffer housekeeping bytes for DC-GBR LCG j of UE i (5G_UP_ALG_5114_replaced([11203638](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize_bytes MAC plus RLC header size as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_30511_replaced([11179689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**_*[CB008600-B-UPD001]_** *

dcGbrDesiredBytesi,LCG_j = min{(bufferHousekeeping(i,LCG_j), MDBV} + rdL2Overhead

bufferHousekeeping(i,LCG_j) buffer housekeeping bytes for DC-GBR LCG j of UE i (5G_UP_ALG_5114_replaced([11203638](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdL2Overhead R&D parameter defined in 5G_UP_ALG_33084_replaced([11194130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvym_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**_*[End CB008600-B-UPD001]_** *

i index of UE with DC-GBR bearer

j index of LCG that contains DC-GBR bearer

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-14 Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution to 5QI1 bearers of initial transmission UEs (ID: 11199776)

**3.30.6.1.0-14.0-3**  (ID: `11199794`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to empty the 5QI1 bearer buffered data a UE has.

This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS (5G_UP_ALG_5085_replaced([11203349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the proposed MCS rrmUlProposedMcs to be greater than rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[i] * 8 , where i is the LCG ID of configured [Before CB008118-A]5QI-1[CB008118-A] 5QI-1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] bearer as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_10514_replaced([11203665](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For VoNR users with active [Before CB008118-C]5QI-1[CB008118-C] 5QI-1/65/66 [End CB008118-C] bearer for voice service when NRCELL.actUlMinTbsForVoNR is set to TRUE, if the corresponding TBS based on the final selected MCS as MCS0 is less than or equal to the configured value given by NRCELL.ulMinTbsForVoNR then L2-PS shall set the amount of FD resources for the UE as per the rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15554_replaced([11203613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note 1: MAC and RLC overhead bytes are not added to the TBS because the overhead bytes have already been included in the voice packet size calculation that is used to update the [Before CB008118-A]5QI-1[CB008118-A] 5QI-1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[i]. See 5G_UP_ALG_4917_replaced([11198680](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJzWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for voice packet size calculations.

Note 2: Each slot’s PUSCH symbol number is configured at cell setup phase, but that number can be changed dynamically due to functions such as aperiodic SRS. To calculate the amount of FD resources required by 5QI1 in one slot, the UL scheduler must use the slot’s current PUSCH symbol number to make sure that 5QI1 buffered data can be emptied.

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-15 Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution to non-5QI-1 GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs (ID: 11199798)

**3.30.6.1.0-15.0-2**  (ID: `11199809`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to meet the desired allocation for [Before CB008118-A] non-5QI-1[CB008118-A] non-5QI 1/65/66 [End CB008118-A] GBR bearers that have pending data. This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS with the proposed MCS rrmUlProposedMcs to be greater than gbrDesiredBytesi,LCG_j * 8.

where

gbrDesiredBytesi,LCG_j = rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytesi,LCG_j + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize_bytes

with

rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytesi,LCG_j bytes in LCG buffer as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4561_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize_bytes MAC plus RLC header size as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_8033_replaced([11179713](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

i index of UE with GBR bearer

j index of LCG that contains GBR bearer

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-16 Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution to Deprioritized GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs (ID: 11199814)

**3.30.6.1.0-16.0-4**  (ID: `13387310`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Deprioritized GBR/[CB014699-C] deprioritized critical GBR [End CB014699-C] bearers (refer to [11196186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwBQcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) **and Deprioritized MC-GBR bearer(deprioritized MC-GBR(5QI67) bearer together with deprioritized GBR bearers in same scheduler request class deprioritizedGbr))** shall be treated as non-GBR bearers for FD resource allocation with a scheduling weight (see 5G_UP_ALG_23866_replaced([11199933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ) = rdDepriorGbrSchedulWeight (see 5G_UP_ALG_10990_replaced([11193445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For the uplink weighted round-robin (WRR) requirements, the term non-GBR shall be interpreted as “non-GBR or deprioritized GBR [CB014699-C] or deprioritized critical GBR [End CB014699-C]”.****

If enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated (refer to [11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the deprioritizedGbr DRBs are treated the same way as non-GBR DRBs, that is the above statement is applied also.

**Note: For tracking purpose, the WRR algorithm interacts with enhanced slice aware scheduler with following requirements:**

 5G_UP_ALG_23866_replaced ([11199933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) UE Weight computation

 5G_UP_ALG_23867_replaced ([11199953](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Round robin weight computation

 5G_UP_ALG_23868_replaced ([11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Resource assignment

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-17 Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution to Deprioritized DC-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs (ID: 11199834)

**3.30.6.1.0-17.0-1**  (ID: `11199840`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs * Deprioritized GBR bearers (see 5G_UP_ALG_8051_replaced([11196186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwBQcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and De-prioritized DC-GBR bearers (deprioritized DC-GBR bearers together with deprioritized GBR bearers in same scheduler request class deprioritizedGbr)shall be treated as non-GBR bearers for FD resource allocation with a scheduling weight ( 5G_UP_ALG_23866_replaced([11199933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ) = rdDepriorGbrSchedulWeight(see 5G_UP_ALG_10990_replaced([11193445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For the uplink weighted round-robin (WRR) requirements, the term non-GBR shall be interpreted as “non-GBR or deprioritized GBR or deprioritized DC-GBR”.

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-18 Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution to critical non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs for prioritized resource (ID: 13404784)

**3.30.6.1.0-18.0-1**  (ID: `13404785`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs The gNB-DU scheduler shall allocate prioritized resources to critical non-GBR traffic with upper boundary limited by NRCELL.prioritiPrbForCriticalNonGbrUl, which is configured as a maximum percentage of the total available resources in the cell or BWP. Resources are distributed fairly among the competing critical non-GBR UEs.

If the remaining available PRBs >= the UE's minimum required PRBs which is determined as below:

  * For SU-MIMO UE, the minimum PRB is determined as [11199466](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_SHs8YPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), and [11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX__ZyrwPvPEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) if UCI is expected to be multiplexed, and [11197403](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_FHPWwPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) if PTRS is configured.
  * [CB0146699-E] For virtual UE containing critical nonGBR UE, the required minimum PRB is determined as [11147997](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_4kkdMPvLEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)[End CB0146699-E]



Then the UE is eligible to be allocated as:

min{ PRB Demand, PRBWeightedPrioritized };

where,

PRB Demand = PRBs required to completely drain the buffer of UE LCGs containing critical non-GBR bearers(refer to [11203638](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_pLV3IPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [CB0146699-E], if it's virtual UE containing critical nonGBR UE, the required PRB is determined as [11147797](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHn8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA) [End CB0146699-E].

PRBWeightedPrioritized = This represents the UEs fair share of the prioritized resource pool. It is calculated as:

round ((Wu / Σ Wj) * max{0, (min{PRB remaining, PRBBWP Limit} - Σ minimumPRB_j) } ) + minimumPRB_u;​​​​​

where,

minimumPRB_u is the [Before CB014699-E-UPD002] minimum PRB required by UE u determined as above [CB014699-E-UPD002] actual minimum PRB requirement for the UE u currently under consideration [End CB014699-E-UPD002]. 

Σ minimumPRB_j [Before CB014699-E-UPD002] is the sum of minimum PRBs required by all critical non-GBR UEs [CB014699-E-UPD002] = minimumPRB_u + minimumPuschPrb (See [11199457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) * Total number of remaining critical non-GBR UEs [End CB014699-E-UPD002].

PRBBWP Limit: [Before CB014699-E-UPD002]This is the total number of PRBs available in the BWP multiplied by the NRCELL.prioritiPrbForCriticalNonGbrUl percentage. [CB014699-E-UPD002] It is determined by multiplying the configured priority setting (NRCELL.prioritiPrbForCriticalNonGbrUl) by the size of the current Bandwidth Part and then subtracting any prioritized resources that have already been assigned to critical non-GBR traffic within that BW. [End CB014699-E-UPD002]

PRB remaining: This is the PRBs available for allocation in the UE's active BWP and all nested smaller BWP among all PUSCH subareas.

Wu/ΣWj: [CB014699-E-UPD002] The weight ratio used to calculate the proportional share of remaining resources for each critical non-GBR UE [End CB014699-E-UPD002].

  * Wu is the scheduling weight of the specific UEs critical non-GBR bearers,
  * Σ Wj is the sum of the weights of all [CB014699-E-UPD002] remaining [End CB014699-E-UPD002] critical non-GBR bearers competing for resources in that BWP, please refer to [11199933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) to determine the scheduling weight for critical non-GBR bearers.
  * [CB0146699-E] If NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is enabled, the above u, j are the indexes of critical nonGBR UE or virtual UE containing critical nonGBR UE(s). For such a virtual UE, its weight should be determined as [11147872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHn9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [End CB0146699-E]



Note:

[Before CB014699-E-UPD002]

  * There is tradeoff to complexity for the weight ratio based allocation, now the fair shared PRB can be larger than UE demand but the unused proportional share is not re-distributed to other UE. Left to CP3 decision if further enhance to always exclude the allocated critical nonGBR resource from the sum() operation(i.e. ΣWj, PRBBWP Limit, Σ minimumPRB_j) to fully utilize the remaining resource from the configured resource pool.



[CB014699-E-UPD002]

  * The total PRB allocated to critical nonGBR can exceed the resource derived from NRCELL.prioritiPrbForCriticalNonGbrUl if the last critical nonGBR UE requires min PRB of amount greater than PRBBWP Limit.



\- PRBBWP Limit a dynamic value that is updated iteratively within a single scheduling process. After the scheduler allocates a certain number of PRBs to the first UE, that number is recorded and when calculating the PRBBWP Limit for the second UE, the number of PRBs assigned to the first UE is subtracted from the initial prioritized pool.

[End CB014699-E-UPD002]

\- Following the initial prioritized allocation, any remaining data for critical non-GBR traffic competes for remaining resources with non-critical non-GBR traffic under [11199854](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_UQemwPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-19 Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution NBR configured Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs (ID: 11199843)

**3.30.6.1.0-19.0-3**  (ID: `13423898`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs L2-PS shall calculate the amount of FD resources needed to meet the desired allocation for Non-GBR NBR LCGs that have positive token bucket (NBR Category) and pending data. This is determined by finding the smallest number of FD resources that would allow the corresponding TBS with the proposed MCS rrmDlProposedMcs _to be greater than the nbrDesiredBytes(i,LCG_j) * 8._

_Where:_

nbrDesiredBytes(i,LCG_j) = min{(bufferHousekeeping(i,LCG_j), bytesToReachNbrRate(i,LCG_j)} + rrmMaxMacPlusRlcHeaderSize_bytes

bufferHousekeeping(i,LCG_j) buffer housekeeping bytes for NBR LCG j of UE i (5G_UP_ALG_5114_replaced([11203638](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

bytesToReachNbrRate(i,LCG_j) = max{rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j), x*slotDuration_msec * rrmNbrPbr_kbps(i,j)}/8

bytesToReachNbrRate Tx bytes required for the NBR LCG j of UE i to reach its target NBR rate

i index of UE with NBR configured non-GBR LCG

j index of NBR configured non-GBR LCG

rrmNbrTokenBucket(i,j) and rrmNbrPbr_kbps(i,j) _are defined in 5G_UP_ALG_22263_replaced([11196334](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwB3jfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and x is defined by an R&D parameter rdNbrOvershootFactor (5G_UP_ALG_22424_replaced([11193605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvuVg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))_

The number of PRBs allocated to the NBR LCG_j is denoted by rrmNbrDesiredPrbs(j). The number of PRBs allocated to UEs with NBR LCGs is calculated by adding the number of PRBs allocated to all NBR LCGs driving scheduling for the UE.

The overall number of PRBs allocated to NBR LCGs for the purpose of meeting UL NBR bitrate for a UE is restricted by NIDD parameter _NRCELL.maxPrbsPerNbrUe and number of PRBs usable for PUSCH for any LCG. If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), _NRCELL.maxPrbsPerNbrUe represents the maximum percentage of FD (PUSCH) resources within a given dedicated BWP that can be allocated for prioritized NBR bearer(s) of a given UE. In that case, this limit shall be computed corresponding to the number of PRBs usable for PUSCH for any bearer/LCG within the UE's active dedicated BWP.**

For a virtual UE, [CB014699-E]if critical nonGBR UE(s) is present in it, the prioritized PRB of the virtual UE allocated before [11199854](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_UQemwPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) is determined as [13404784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_miiekgygEfGu_4itz1o3RA), otherwise the prioritized PRB is determined as hereafter. [End CB014699-E] the amount of needed NBR FD resources shall be determined according to the NBR UE(s) present in it. If multiple NBR UEs are part of candidate UE group which are then paired, then the needed NBR FD resources for the NBR candidate UE group shall be the sum of needed NBR resources for all NBR UEs present within the candidate UE group. Eventually, if more than one NBR UE (either normal NBR UE or NBR candidate UE group) are paired in virtual UE, the NBR FD resources needed for virtual UE shall be the maximum value of needed NBR resources among its paired NBR UEs.**The PRB compensation factor([11148009](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2APvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall not be considered in the calculation. Rationale: The inclusion of PRB compensation factor leads to higher number of PRB reservation for NBR UE than initially assumed leading to unavailability of PRBs for subsequent selected UEs in the slot. Hence, though, the exclusion of PRB compensation factor might lead to lower possible TBS for the NBR UE than initially required, NBR is not expected to have guaranteed rate and also the same NBR UE is also included in later WRR distribution too. Hence, the impact would be minor and considered acceptable.**

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-20 Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution to non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs (ID: 11199854)

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-1**  (ID: `11199860`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Overall

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11199869`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs L2-PS shall distribute the available resources calculated in 5G_UP_ALG_4988_replaced([11199729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to initial transmission virtual UEs or UEs with non-GBR transmission

If the UE has already acquired some FD resources for SRB transmission, the amount of FD resources it will get from this distribution is added to it.

The resources are distributed using a weighted round robin algorithm as follows:

For an initial transmission UE, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4999_replaced([11199901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For multi PUSCH areas, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_5004_replaced([11200368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwSWM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For algorithm stop and the output of the algorithm, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_5007_replaced([11200389](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwSWN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**[CB007113-I] subcell PRBs and physical PRBs availability shall be checked in the case of two subcells, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_9132_replaced([11200876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwS9RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **[End CB007113-I]****

For a virtual UE that only contains initial transmission non-GBR traffic UEs, L2RT can treat it as a regular UE carrying initial transmission non-GBR traffic for PRB allocation in the FD scheduler.

After the PRB allocation for the virtual UE is completed, the PRB distribution among the UEs within the virtual UE shall then be determined based on 5G_UP_ALG_9064_replaced([11200416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwSWPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-2**  (ID: `11199874`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Overview of the PUSCH scheduling for multiple Non-GBR UEs with initial Transmission 

EDITORIAL NOTE: This Requirement is to be moved to Chapter 2

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `13322815`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs 13322815: Assignment of the number of PRBs If a UE or a virtual UE with Non-GBR traffic is chosen to be scheduled by the TD scheduler (see 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for initial transmission, there is a check about the minimum needed resources by this UE. This is either dependent of a pre-defined minimum value for all UEs (minSupportedNumPrbsUl) or dependent if UCI information is expected and therefore the minimum allocation might need to be higher. If (NRCELL-actExhaustiveScheduling is "UL" or 'DLAndUL") and the number of UEs in CS1 list from corrective overload control is less than or equal to rdCs1ListMaxSizeToTriggerExhaustiveUlScheduling (see [12493184](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_eI-rERN4EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) and decision to apply exhaustive UL scheduling as[12703976](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_V8q5IF70EfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), then * gNB shall choose exhaustive UL scheduling as [11199907](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), [12493290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_eXnAIBN8EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). If **NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg != LegacyRange_lowComplexityWrr and NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg !=** ExtendedRange_lowComplexityWrr** _* { The list of to be scheduled UEs is then further processed. The overall scheduling algorithm consists of two loops. The inner loop which is a Weighted Round Robin (WRR) algorithm (see 5G_UP_ALG_5002_replaced([11200325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRvLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and an outer-loop to assure that all the resources are allocated. First the scheduling weight is normalized (5G_UP_ALG_4999_replaced([11199901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which then is taken as an input of the WRR algorithm. Another input value is the number of free resources to be allocated. Mainly from the free available resources and the sum of all scheduling weights and some predefined limits, a common step-size (rrmFdResPerRoundUl, see 5G_UP_ALG_4999_replaced([11199901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is calculated. This step-size is re-calculated for each round of the outer loop and is also used as an input for the WRR. After the WRR is finished (see 5G_UP_ALG_5002_replaced([11200325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRvLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for one outer loop iteration, there is a special check in case of multiple PUSCH sub-areas (5G_UP_ALG_5004_replaced([11200368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwSWM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This is done to avoid that one UE is getting more resources than the maximum of free resources of all sub-areas because scheduling over more than 1 sub-area is not possible with RAT1 (PRB assignment need to be consecutive). If this check is successful for a UE, this UE is pre-allocated [Before PR863105-26R2] to the sub-area [End PR863105-26R2] with the highest number of free resources (filling it up completely) and the resource higher than this maximum allocation are given back to the residual to be scheduled UEs. These free resources are to be allocated in the next run of the outer-loop then. [PR863105-26R2] If rdCgOwPrioAlloc = False, the UE shall be pre-assigned to the subarea; otherwise, the UE shall not be pre-assigned to the subarea. This guarantees that CG overwriting UEs of small allocation shall be prioritized in sub-area assignment and all CG overwriting UEs shall be assigned to subareas which still have enough free resources for the required minimum allocation, as CG UEs must be scheduled in the current slot ([11199874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End PR863105-26R2] If all the resources are (theoretically) allocated the actual distribution to the PUSCH is done. In case of multiple PUSCH sub-areas, it is first decided in which sub-area a UE is allocated to and then the starting positions and grants are calculated. Figure 5G_UP_ALG_4997_replaced([11199874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2 gives an overview of the several steps. [image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to 5G network resource allocation. The flowchart starts with a "Start" oval. The main process is enclosed within a large, light orange rounded rectangle, suggesting an outer loop or a major phase. Inside this, a light blue rounded rectangle encloses a series of steps, representing a round of allocation. A green rounded rectangle at the bottom contains the final assignment step. Here's a breakdown of the steps: 1\. _*5G_UP_ALG_4871 choses (v)UEs to be scheduled and might be pre-allocation**: This is the initial step where virtual UEs (vUEs) are selected for scheduling and potential pre-allocation. 2\. **rrmFdResPerRoundUI calculation(5G_UP_ALG_4999)** : This step involves calculating some resource-related metric per round for the selected vUEs. 3\. **PRB Allocation Loop over (v)UEs with WRR calculation(5G_UP_ALG_5002)** : This is an inner loop where Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) are allocated to vUEs using a Weighted Round Robin (WRR) algorithm. 4\. **Check for biggest assignment and do pre-allocation if true(5G_UP_ALG_5004)** : This step checks for the largest assignment and performs pre-allocation if a certain condition is met. 5\. **Decides to stop allocation or next round** : Based on some criteria, the process decides whether to stop the allocation or proceed to the next round. 6\. **PUSCH area assignments** : This is the final step within the main process, where PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) area assignments are made. There are also several annotations and external connections: * **Outerloop** : An arrow points from the left side to the "rrmFdResPerRoundUI calculation" step, labeled "Outerloop". This suggests that the entire blue section might be part of an outer loop. * **PRB allocation for virtual UEs or UEs according to whether MU pairing algorithm is executed in current slot(5G_UP_ALG_XXX)** : A text box on the top right, connected by an arrow to the overall process, describes the PRB allocation logic, mentioning MU pairing. * **Innerloop: PRB allocation wrt to their need but at max rrmFdResPerRoundUl till all free PRBs are allocated or all (v)UEs are happy** : A text box on the right, connected by an arrow to the "PRB Allocation Loop over (v)UEs with WRR" step, explains the logic of the inner loop. * **One Round** : A small orange box on the right, connected by an arrow to the "PRB Allocation Loop over (v)UEs with WRR" step, labels this part of the process as "One Round". * **PRB allocation for all UEs which are included in virtual UE. (5G_UP_ALG_9065 )** : A text box at the bottom, below the green rectangle, describes the final PRB allocation for all UEs included in virtual UEs.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15718.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] Figure 5G_UP_ALG_4997_replaced([11199874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))-2: Overview of the PUSCH scheduling for Non-GBR UEs with initial Tx in MU MIMO The PUSCH subarea assignment is done in the order of the sorted UEs, denoted by L. The sorted UE list L is prepared as follows. If rdCgOwPrioAlloc = True (see [12807678](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HxhEcaLAEfCkxIKMlo4L6w?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), we first process CG overwriting UEs with small allocation, so that these UEs get at least the required minimum allocation. These UEs cannot be skipped during the resource allocation and should always be scheduled. If rdCgOwPrioAlloc = False, UEs are sorted by the decreasing order of allocated PRBs. If (rdCgOwPrioAlloc = True) and (there is at least one CG overwriting UE in the UE list) and (#PUSCH subareas > 1 (see [11196950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGwCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA))) 1\. Find the maximum value of the minimum allocation (in number of PRBs, see [11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) of all UEs selected for CG overwriting, denoted by M_cg. 2\. Identify all UEs that are selected for CG overwriting and have been allocated less than or equal to ((M_cg - 1) * #PUSCH subareas) PRBs (see [11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)), denoted by L_cg. The list of all remaining UEs is denoted by L_r. 3\. Sort L_cg by the increasing order of allocated PRBs for each UE. 4\. Sort L_r by the decreasing order of allocated PRBs for each UE. 5\. Create the sorted UE list L by concatenating L_cg and L_r, i.e. L = L_cg || L_r. Else Sort all UEs by the decreasing order of allocated PRBs for each UE, which creates the sorted UE list L. End If In general, the PUSCH sub-area (if there are more than one) is chosen with the smallest amount of free resources that would still take the current allocation of the UE. Unfortunately (except the check for in 5G_UP_ALG_5004_replaced([11200368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwSWM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) only the available resources were considered in the main loops, but not the actual PUSCH sub-area themselves. Therefore, there might be quantization effects. E.g. the last UE(s) would not get scheduled after the algorithm with X PRBs like originally assigned, but there might get only Y PRBs. Because the Y PRBs are left in one PUSCH sub-area (or X-Y PRBs and Y > X-Y left in the other one). In that case the UE is scheduled (if min allocation can be upheld) to the PUSCH sub-area with the highest left-over resources and the resources in the other PUSCH area might not be scheduled. File: Overview of the PUSCH scheduling for multiple Non-GBR UEs with initial Transmission.pptx Folder: else { The list of to be scheduled UEs is then further processed. The scheduling weight is normalized (5G_UP_ALG_23867_replaced([11199953](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which is taken as an input of the WRR algorithm. Other input values are the number of free resources to be allocated and some predefined limits. [PR867699-26R2] If rdCgOwPrioAlloc is true (see [12807678](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HxhEcaLAEfCkxIKMlo4L6w?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), CG overwriting UEs are put to the front of the UE list, to avoid the situation that these UEs cannot get the required minimum allocation. [End PR867699-26R2] During WRR algorithm (5G_UP_ALG_32596_replaced([11200136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRvJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the number of PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of UEs' initial transmission is calculated, then allocating PRBs to the UE in a PUSCH subarea is performed. In general, the PUSCH sub-area (if there are more than one) is chosen with the smallest amount of free resources that would still take the current allocation of the UE. There is also a special check in case of multiple PUSCH sub-areas (5G_UP_ALG_5004_replaced([11200368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwSWM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This is done to avoid that one UE is getting more resources than the maximum of free resources of all sub-areas because scheduling over more than 1 sub-area is not possible with RAT1 (PRB assignment need to be consecutive). If this check is successful for a UE, this UE is pre-allocated to the sub-area with the highest number of free resources (filling it up completely) and the resource higher than this maximum allocation are given back to the residual to be scheduled UEs. If all the resources (theoretically) are allocated, the actual distribution to the PUSCH is done. In case of multiple PUSCH sub-areas, it is first decided in which sub-area a UE is allocated to and then the starting positions and grants are calculated. While calculating the number of PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of UEs' initial transmission, unfortunately (except the check for in 5G_UP_ALG_5004_replaced([11200368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwSWM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) only the available resources were considered, but not the actual PUSCH sub-area themselves. Therefore, there might be quantization effects. E.g. the last UE(s) would not get scheduled after the algorithm with X PRBs like originally assigned, but there might get only Y PRBs. Because the Y PRBs are left in one PUSCH sub-area (or X-Y PRBs and Y > X-Y left in the other one). In that case the UE is scheduled (if min allocation can be upheld) to the PUSCH sub-area with the highest left-over resources and the resources in the other PUSCH area might not be scheduled. After the WRR stops, if some PRBs are unused, allocating unused PRBs to the most deserving candidates is performed (see 5G_UP_ALG_5002_replaced([11200325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRvLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). } End if If NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is enabled, the above flow is the PRB distribution for the virtual UE. The scheduling information for the virtual UE see 5G_UP_ALG_9548_replaced([11147793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHn8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). After the PRB distribution for the virtual UE, L2RT should allocate PRB to UEs which are included in virtual UE, please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_9064_replaced([11200416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwSWPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3**  (ID: `11199901`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs For an initial transmission UE

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11199907`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs General Steps

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12504455`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs The resource distribution to non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs is done with the weighted round robin (WRR) algorithm. The WRR algorithm is consisted of the following steps:

  1. Preparation for WRR execution

  2. UE weight computation 

  3. Round robin weight computation

  4. Resource assignment




[CB014965-A]

If gNB chooses exhaustive UL scheduling as [11199874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), step 3 is skipped.

[End CB014965-A]

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11199918`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Preparation for WRR execution

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `13404937`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs If NRCELL.actDemoteProactUlSched is False, UEs from ulProactSched request class, are included in the WRR resource distribution.

The UEs that are included in the WRR algorithm are UEs of below request classes:

  * [CB014699-F]criticalDrbTraffic after distributed in [13404784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_miiekgygEfGu_4itz1o3RA) or [End CB014699-F]
  * srResp or
  * drbTraffic which includes normal non-GBR and NBR excessive data after distributed in [11199843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_UMookPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), or
  * deprioritizedGBR



If enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), each UE that is a candidate for srResp request class is given an initial allocation that can accommodate the SR response (PayloadSr, see 5G_UP_ALG_12751_replaced([11203952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and is no smaller than the minimum allocation according to 5G_UP_ALG_23868_replaced([11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Remove every UE that is a candidate for srResp request class but is not a candidate for drbTraffic or deprioritizedGbr request class from the list of UEs input to WRR algorithm (see 5G_UP_ALG_23913_replaced([11195051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv5UpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for checking whether a UE is a candidate for drbTraffic or deprioritizedGbr). Note, the initial allocation given to a UE that is a candidate for srResp request class, shall be counted in the following steps of WRR. Once the initial allocation is given to a UE, the UE will not be given minimum allocation by later steps of the WRR algorithm. 

If NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg != LegacyRange_lowComplexityWrr and NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg != ExtendedRange_lowComplexityWrr and gNB does not choose exhaustive UL scheduling ([11199874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

Before the resource distribution, the list of UEs input to the weighted round robin algorithm is shuffled (random order of UEs).

End if

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11199933`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs UE Weight computation

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `12556787`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs If NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is enabled, L2RT shall distribute resource for the virtual UE with weighted round robin algorithm. The weight of a virtual UE is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_9548_replaced([11147793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHn8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

If enhanced slice aware scheduler is deactivated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))) and If NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is disabled, for a UE , the weight is obtained by calculating the sum of NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight of the non-GBR bearers that are mapped to LCGs that have non-zero buffer size. [CB014044-N] If NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdAlgoEnh= UL_Only or DL_UL, the weight is further multiplied by the FD weighted metric of the UE, namely fdWeightedMetric[i,j,t]. For FD weighted metric description, please refer to [12556782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EoDCQCgjEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [End CB014044-N]

If enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))) and if NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is disabled, for a UE, the weight is obtained by calculating the sum of combined weight (the product of NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight and RG weight ~~of the UE~~ (5G_UP_ALG_23167_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)))) of the non-GBR bearers that are mapped to LCGs that have non-zero buffer size (see 5G_UP_ALG_5118_replaced([11203660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for the mapping between DRB and LCG, and the determination of data in buffer for an LCG). [CB014044-N] If NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdAlgoEnh= UL_Only or DL_UL, the weight is further multiplied by the FD weighted metric of the UE, namely fdWeightedMetric[i,j,t]. For FD weighted metric description, please refer to [12556782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EoDCQCgjEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [End CB014044-N]

The above statement is applied to bearers mapped to deprioritizedGbr request class, with the scheduling weight refer to 5G_UP_ALG_10992_replaced([11199814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwP6APvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), but not from NIDD NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `12556782`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **Calculation of FD Weight Metric**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12556783`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs 12556783: Assignment of the number of PRBs [CB014044-N]

Let S be initialized as the set of all UEs that join the WRR in carrier j in slot t

For each UE i in S, FD weighted metric of the UE i, namely fdWeightedMetric[i,j, t] is given by:

[Before PR872202]

If fdSchedMetric[i,j,t] > avgFdSchedMetric[j,t]:

fdWeightedMetric[i,j,t] = 0.1* rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConst * fdSchedMetric[i,j,t]

Else [End PR872202]

fdWeightedMetric[i,j,t] = fdSchedMetric[i,j,t]

with

fdSchedMetric[i,j,t] is the FD scheduling metric of UE i in carrier j in slot t calculated as

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $fdSchedMetric[i, j, t] = \frac{rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i, j, t]}{rrmAvgUlUeDataRate [i, j,t-1]} \times rrmUeSchedWeight[i,t]$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-05191138.png[/image_path][/image-0]

rrmUeSchedWeight[i,t] is the UE i scheduling weight [CB014044-O] if the slice aware scheduler for uplink is not activated for the cell (see [11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB014044-O]. It shall be calculated as the sum of NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight of its non-GBR bearers in UL that have data in the buffer in slot t. [CB014044-O] If the slice aware scheduler for uplink is activated for the cell (see [11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), it shall be calculated as the sum of combined weight (i.e., the product of NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight and rrmRGWeight) of its non-GBR bearers that have data in the buffer during slot t, where rrmRGWeight is the RG weight of the resource group associated to the non-GBR bearer (see [11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). [End CB014044-O]

rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i,j,t] is the current UL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in slot t in bytes per slot as described in [11195916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

rrmAvgUlUeDataRate is the UL average data rate of the UE as described in [Before PR855011] [11195975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [PR855011] [12823784: Calculation of the average 5G UE UL Data Rate for FD Weighted Metric](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks3Qcap2EfCrANRYxAlR5A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [End PR855011]

avgFdSchedMetric[j,t] is the average value of FD scheduling metric among all UEs that join the WRR in carrier j in slot t calculated as below:

[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in an italicized font. The formula is: $avgFdSchedMetric[i, j, t] = \frac{\sum_{i \in S} fdSchedMetric[i, j, t]}{sizeof(S)}$ This formula defines the average of a metric called `fdSchedMetric` for indices `i`, `j`, and `t`, over a set `S`. The average is calculated by summing the `fdSchedMetric` for all elements `i` in the set `S` and then dividing by the size of the set `S`.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-05121556.png[/image_path][/image-1]

The filter constant rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConst defined by the formulas above shall correspond to

  * For an FDD cell, it shall be rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConstFdd, see [12552792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_cBJS4iTyEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

  * For a TDD FR1 cell, it shall be rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConstTddFr1, see [12555241](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_B0Tn8CW1EfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)




[End CB014044-N]

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `12823784`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs **Calculation of the average 5G UE UL Data Rate for FD Weighted Metric**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `12823785`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs 12823785: Assignment of the number of PRBs The average data rate of the 5G UE in UL shall be calculated as below:

rrmAvgUlUeDataRate computation on a per carrier basis:

The calculation rule of the average 5G UE date rate depends on the setting of the NIDD parameter NRBTS.NRCELL.actFdAlgoEnh. If NRBTS.NRCELL.actFdAlgoEnh is neither set to UL_Only nor DL_UL:

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation defines `rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i, j, t]` as a function of previous values of itself and `rrmUlSumOfTransportBlockSize[i, j, t]`. The equation is: `rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i, j, t] = (1 - 1/β)^n × rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i, j, t-n] + 1/β × rrmUlSumOfTransportBlockSize[i, j, t]`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-05041714.png[/image_path][/image-0]

and if NRCELL.actUlTdPfMetricOpt is set to UL_Only or DL_UL:  
[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is: $rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i, j, t] = (1 - \frac{1}{\beta})^n \times rrmAvgUlUeDataRate[i, j, t - n] + \frac{1}{\beta} instUlUeDataRate[i, j, t]$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-05041713.png[/image_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is: `rrm AvgUlUe Data Rate[i, j,t] = max (avgUlUe Data Rate Lower Threshold[i,j,t], rrmAvgUlUe Data Rate[i, j, t])`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/1e244f7f-edc3-4ad4-9833-57855e766e57.png[/image_path][/image-2]

with:

i: Index of the 5G UE

t: NR slot the calculation relates to (note t is the time elapsed from the initialization which means initialization at the UE setup or reinitialization)

n: rrmNumSlotSinceLastUlSched

β: rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConst

The filter constant rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConst defined by the formulas above shall correspond to  
\- For an FDD cell, it shall be rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConstFdd, see [12552792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_cBJS4iTyEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)  
\- For a TDD FR1 cell, it shall be rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConstTddFr1, see [12555241](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_B0Tn8CW1EfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)  
\- For a TDD FR2 cell, it shall be rdAvgUlUeDataRateFilterConst see 5G_L2_1024_replaced([11182943](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fs9fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

rrmUlSumOfTransportBlockSize(i,j,k): represents the aggregated traffic volume in bytes for all the 1st transmission transport blocks that are scheduled for the 5G UE with index i on carrier j during the NR slot t as long as the 5G UE is in Candidate Set 1.

avgUlUeDataRateLowerThreshold[i,j,t]: The lower bound on the avgDataRate in UL for the UE i in carrier j in slot t. It shall be calculated as rdMinExpectedBwSharePerUe * rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i,j,t]. Please refer to [12811470: rdMinExpectedBwSharePerUe](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_E6Q9oKQ4EfCBvZf8dodgmQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for rdMinExpectedBwSharePerUe and [11195916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha definition.

instUlUeDataRate[i,j,t]: The instantaneous UL data rate of a 5G UE i, in bytes per slot, which is valid in NR slot t for the carrier j, shall be evaluated as below:

[Before PR864769]   
[image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is: `instUlUe Data Rate[i, j,t] = currentUlUeData RateWithFdCost[i, j, t]`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/1d26be1a-85cd-4b6a-9ef3-513375795ead.png[/image_path][/image-3]

[PR864769]

[image-4][image_desc]The image contains mathematical notation. The text reads: instUlUeDataRate [i, j, t] = max(currentUlUeDataRateWithFdCost [i, j, t], rrmUlSumOfTransportBlockSize[i, j, t])[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-01140823.png[/image_path][/image-4]

[End PR864769]

currentUlUeDataRateWithFdCost[i,j,t] is the cost of scheduling the UE i in FD scheduler on carrier j in NR slot t calculated as:[Before PR864769]

[image-5][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula is: currentUlUeDataRateWithFdCost[i, j, t] = (rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot_alpha[i, j, t] * allocFdResources[i, j, t]) / useableFdResources[j, t][/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-05041704.png[/image_path][/image-5]

[PR864769]

[image-6][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is: `currentUlUeDataRateWithFdCost[i, j,t] = rrmCurrentUlUeDataRateBytesPerSlot[i, j, t] * allocFdResources[i, j,t]/usableFdResources[j,t]`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/4025365a-f2bb-47f9-a299-e75134457d7b.png[/image_path][/image-6]

rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i,j,t] is the current UL data rate of the 5G UE i related to carrier j in slot t in bytes per slot as described in [11195916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

[End PR864769]

allocFdResources[i,j,t] is the number of allocated PUSCH FD resources for the UE i in the carrier j in NR slot t

usableFdResources[i,j,t] is the maximum number of available PUSCH FD resources in the carrier j in NR slot t

For the initialization of the exponential filter, the average data rate shall be derived from the scheduling conditions in the previous NR slot in carrier j as per ([11196008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The exponential filter shall be initialized the 1st time that the PF metric has to be computed after the UE setup.

The exponential filter shall be re-initialized in the following cases:

  * when the UE re-enters the pre-scheduler candidate set with new data in DRB buffer (coming back from nullBuffer state) after staying out for longer than NRCELL.avgDataRateReinitProhibitTimer; Reinitialization is done at most only once after the UE returns to nonNullBuffer state from nullBuffer state.

  * when the UE is scheduled from SR priority class while it had non null DRB buffer and was not scheduled for longer than rdAvgDataRateReinitProhibitTimer ms (5G_L2_1205_replaced([11183368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iwTfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

  * with each change in the set of the non-GBR bearers and hence with each change of the overall schedulWeight.

  * after each FR1 SCell activation for SCell with UL configured according to 5G_UP_ ALG_2698.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `11199953`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Round robin weight computation

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-6.0-6**  (ID: `12556788`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs 12556788: Assignment of the number of PRBs If gNB chooses exhaustive UL scheduling as [11199874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), gNB shall skip action specified in this requirement.

If **NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = LegacyRange_lowComplexityWrr** or NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange_lowComplexityWrr****

Let _S be initialized as the set of all UEs that join the WRR and and this list may change after each iteration._

Let _R be available PUSCH resources (in number of PRBs) for non-GBR initial transmission._

Compute the round robin weight for each UE _u in_ S as follows:**

In the first iteration:

[image-0][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

Where:

[image-2][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]The PRB share based on normalized scheduling weight of UE _u._

[Before CB014044-N]

[image-3][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]UE weight of UE _u for non-GBR bearers (see 5G_UP_ALG_23866_replaced([11199933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Note: If UE weight for UE u is equal to 0, [image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression written in a blurry, dark font against a white background. The expression is "w_u^U".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-04231728.png[/image_path][/image-4]shall be set to a positive value far less than 1._

[CB014044-N]

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdAlgoEnh = UL_Only or DL_UL

[image-5][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.004.png[/image_path][/image-5]UE scheduling weight of UE u multiplied by the FD weighted metric of the UE u, namely FdWeightedMetric[u,j,t]. For FdWeightedMetric description, see [12556782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EoDCQCgjEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Else

[image-6][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.004.png[/image_path][/image-6]UE weight of UE u for non-GBR bearers (see 5G_UP_ALG_23866_replaced([11199933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))). Note: If UE weight for UE u is equal to 0, [image-7][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.004.png[/image_path][/image-7]shall be set to a positive value far less than 1.

[End CB014044-N]

N is the remaining available PRBs in the largest BWP across all **PUSCH sub-area(s).**

In the second iteration:

For each UE _u in_ S**

set [image-8][image_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.005.png[/image_path][/image-8]to the minimum allocation for UE u (note: the minimum allocation is same as in 5G_UP_ALG_ 32767).

Distribute resources to UE _u in_ S as specified in enhanced resource assignment (5G_UP_ALG_ 32771), with[image-9][image_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or patterns.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.006.png[/image_path][/image-9]and available PUSCH resources as inputs.**

Remove from _S the UEs that have gotten nonzero PRBs in this iteration._

Remove from _S the UEs that have gotten zero PRB in this iteration due to that the UEs have gotten the maximum resources it can get (5G_UP_ALG_5010_replaced([11201008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))._

Remove from _S the UEs that have got zero PRB in this iteration due to that the UEs' active BWP have no available PUSCH PRBs._

**Note, the WRR resource allocation specified 5G_UP_ALG_ 32771 is done in one or two iterations, depending on UE weights, their buffer status and available PUSCH resources.**

The rationale of the above WRR algorithm is as follows:

  1. The WRR algorithm specified in 5G_UP_ALG_ 32771 keeps UEs in WRR list if the minimum allocation can't be met in the first iteration and wait for unused FD resources from other UEs, because it is possible that some FD resources are unused by UEs with high schedule weight due to limited buffer which can be distributed to the most deserving candidate which have either no reserved resources or not been fully satisfied with the reserved FD resources to drain their total buffered data.

  2. The resource allocation is done in one or two iterations. This makes sure that UEs with high UE weight are considered in the first iteration. If some resources are still available after the first iteration, other UEs with small UE weight (for these UEs, the minimum allocation can't be met in the first iteration) will be considered in the second iteration for resource allocation.

  3. If UEs with high UE weight have a high traffic demand, it is possible that UEs with small UE weight will not be given any resources for the current slot. This is to make sure that resources given to UEs shall be more proportional to their UE weights.

  4. Normalized round robin weights for UEs are computed in single iteration.




else if enhanced slice aware scheduler is deactivated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = disabled OR NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange_LegacyWrr

The round robin weights should be normalized with respect to the UE with the smallest weight. Specifically the weights should be normalized via dividing the weights by the smallest weight then rounding the result to the nearest integer. This will result in a set of normalized weights where the smallest normalized weight equals 1.

[CB014044-N]

The round robin weights are calculated based on the UE scheduling weights and depend on the setting of the NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdAlgoEnh.

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdAlgoEnh = UL_Only or DL_UL, the UE scheduling weight of UE_u is multiplied by the FD weighted metric of the UE u, namely FdWeightedMetric[u,j,t]. The parameter FdWeightedMetric is defined in [12556782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EoDCQCgjEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[End CB014044-N]

Then distribute resources to all UEs joining WRR as specified in the step of resource assignment (5G_UP_ALG_23868_replaced([11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Example: 3 UEs with round robin weights 100, 20, & 60 should be normalized such that the weights are 5, 1, & 3 respectively.

Example: 3 UEs with round robin weights 100, 30, & 80 should be normalized such that the weights are 3, 1, & **3** respectively.****

Else

Let _S be initialized as the set of all UEs that join the WRR and and this list may change after each iteration_ _._ **

Let _R be available PUSCH resources (in number of PRBs) for non-GBR initial transmission._**R is reduced only when WRR finishes allocation for a UE.** **

For each UE _u in_ S that is selected for CG overwriting (see 5G_UP_ALG_15617_replaced([11198720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

Reserve minimum allocation for UE _u (see 5G_UP_ALG_23868_replaced([11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))_

End For

While (_R > 0) and (_S is not empty)**

For each UE _u in_ S**

Compute the normalized round robin weight for UE _u as follows:_

[image-10][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $w_{u}^{R} = round\left(\frac{w_{u}^{U}}{\sum_{i \in I} w_{i}^{U}} \cdot R_{u}\right)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.007.png[/image_path][/image-10]

where

_w_u^R: normalized round robin weight of UE_ u**

[Before CB014044-N]

[image-11][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.004.png[/image_path][/image-11]UE weight of UE _u_

[CB014044-N]

If NRBTS/NRCELL/actFdAlgoEnh = UL or DL_UL

[image-12][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.004.png[/image_path][/image-12]UE scheduling weight of UE u multiplied by the FD weighted metric of the UE u, namely FdWeightedMetric[u,j,t]. For FdWeightedMetric description, see [12556782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EoDCQCgjEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Else

[image-13][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.004.png[/image_path][/image-13]UE weight of UE u

[End CB014044-N]

If (UE u is selected for CG overwriting) and ([image-14][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as $w_{u}^{R} = 0$. The letter 'w' is in lowercase, followed by a subscript 'u' and a superscript 'R'. This is followed by an equals sign and the number '0'.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.008.png[/image_path][/image-14])

set[image-15][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as $w_u^R = 1$. The text is in a dark color against a light background.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.009.png[/image_path][/image-15]

End If

End For

If [image-16][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as $w_{u}^{R} = 0$. The letter 'w' is in lowercase, followed by a subscript 'u' and a superscript 'R'. This is followed by an equals sign and the number '0'.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.008.png[/image_path][/image-16] for every UE _u in_ S**

For every UE _u in_ S, set[image-17][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written as $w_u^R = 1$. The text is in a dark color against a light background.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.009.png[/image_path][/image-17].**

End If

Assign 0 PRB to all UEs _u in_ S with _w_u^R = 0._ **

Distribute resources to all UEs _u in_ S that have nonzero _w_u^R, as specified in the step of resource assignment (5G_UP_ALG_23868_replaced([11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), with normalized round robin weights _w_u^R and available PUSCH resources _R as inputs._ ****

Remove from _S the UEs that have got nonzero PRBs in this iteration._

Remove from _S the UEs that have got zero PRB in this iteration due to the UEs active BWP have no available PUSCH PRBs._

_R is reduced by the number of PRBs that have been distributed in this iteration._

End While

**Note: the step of resource assignment (5G_UP_ALG_23868_replaced([11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) can be executed for multiple times, depending on UE weights, their buffer status and available PUSCH resources. In each execution of resource assignment (5G_UP_ALG_23868_replaced([11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), either all UEs with nonzero round robin weights are given the maximum number of resources or the available PUSCH resources are exhausted.**

The rationale of the above WRR algorithm is as follows:

  1. The above WRR algorithm makes sure that, If [image-18][image_desc]The image is blurry and contains a logo with the letters "W" and "R" in a stylized font. The letters are multicolored and appear to be overlapping. There is also a smaller letter "u" below the "W" and "R". The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.010.png[/image_path][/image-18]= 0,then keep UEs in WRR list(i.e. to be schedule list) and wait for unused FD resources from other UEs with [image-19][image_desc]The image is blurry and contains text that is difficult to read. The text appears to be "W, R, U" with some smaller characters below the "W" and "R". The colors are muted and the overall image quality is poor.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.011.png[/image_path][/image-19]is not equal to zero, because it is possible that some FD resources are unused by UEs with high schedule weight due to limited buffer which can be distributed to the most deserving candidate which have either no reserved resources or not been fully satisfied with the reserved FD resources to drain their total buffered data.



2\. The resource allocation is done in one or more iterations until all UEs are assigned maximum resources or all PUSCH resources are given out. This makes sure that UEs with high UE weight are considered in the first iteration. If some resources are still available after the first iteration, other UEs that have small UE weight (i.e. normalized round robin weight of these UEs is equal to 0 in the first iteration) will be considered in later iterations for resource allocation.

3\. If UEs with high UE weight have a high traffic demand, it is possible that UEs with small UE weight will not be given any resources for the current slot. This is to make sure that resources given to UEs shall be more proportional to their UE weights.

4\. The above WRR algorithm makes sure that either a UE is given 0 resource or it is given at least its minimum resources.

5\. Normalized round robin weights for UEs are computed in each iteration. They can take any nonnegative integer values. In one iteration, the resource assignment step may be executed on a set of UEs that have normalized round robin weights higher than one (e.g. second iteration of the example below).

6\. If a UE is selected for CG overwriting (CG is applied to the UE and the current slot is a CG PUSCH slot for the  
UE), then minimum allocation is reserved for the CG UE as described in 5G_UP_ALG_23868_replaced([11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

7\. If a UE is selected for CG overwriting, then the UE gets a normalized round robin weight of at least 1. This ensures that resources are distributed to the UE in the first iteration when we still have enough resources to provide the minimum allocation for CG overwriting UE. We need to guarantee that CG OW UEs are always scheduled and their minimum allocation are always granted.

Example: Suppose the available resources are 67 PRBs, 3 UEs with UE weights 500, 2, 3. UE1 has buffered non-GBR data only for 5 PRBs, the other two UEs both have full buffer non-GBR data. UE1, UE2 and UE3 have min allocation equal to 6, 3, 9 respectively.

Then the WRR algorithm will give out resources as follows. Each iteration runs according to resource assignment at 5G_UP_ALG_23868_replaced([11200101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |   
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---  
| R (before iteration) | S (before iteration) | Normalized round robin weight of UE1 [image-20][image_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.012.png[/image_path][/image-20] | Normalized round robin weight of UE2 [image-21][image_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.013.png[/image_path][/image-21] | Normalized round robin weight of UE3 [image-22][image_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail or extract any text.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15733.014.png[/image_path][/image-22] | PRBs assigned to UE1 in the iteration | PRBs assigned to UE2 in the iteration | PRBs assigned to UE3 in the iteration  
Iteration 1 | 67 | {UE1, UE2, UE3} | 66 | 0 | 0 | 6 | 0 | 0  
Iteration 2 | 61 | {UE2, UE3} | NA | 24 | 37 | NA | 24 | 37  
  
End If

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-7**  (ID: `11200101`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource assignment

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-3.0-7.0-7**  (ID: `13453552`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs If gNB chooses exhaustive UL scheduling as [11199874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), please refer to [12493290: Exhaustive UL scheduling](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_eXnAIBN8EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

Else If **NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = LegacyRange_lowComplexityWrr** **or NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg = ExtendedRange_lowComplexityWrr**

Please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_32596_replaced([11200136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRvJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

else

The resources are distributed rrmFdResPerRoundUl at a time, with rrmFdResPerRoundUl = max (1, floor { (Available PUSCH for nonGBR)/( sum{normalizedSumWeights})}).

If (NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg is equal to ExtendedRange_EnhancedWrr) or (enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))))

Each UE with zero normalized weight should not be allocated any PRBs in the resource assignment step.

The allocation to UEs with nonzero normalized weight should proceed at follows.

Else

The allocation to all UEs should proceed at follows.

End If
