**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-9**  (ID: `11200876`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution to non-GBR bearers of two subcells

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-9.0-3**  (ID: `11200914`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs 11200914: Assignment of the number of PRBs If NRCELL.nrCellType is configured to xDL8UL and NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is set to true and NRCELL.numberOfRxBeamforming is equal to 4 and slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and numAllocCellPrb granted by BBRM is larger than maxNumPrbLimitToUsePerUlBw (5G_UP_ALG_14781_replaced([11146479](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksEkkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2PS shall first caculate available subcell PRBs and physical PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs as below:

available subcell PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs = min{maxNumSubcelPrbAvailableForPusch(5G_UP_ALG_10770_replaced([11147657](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHn4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) - FD resources allocated in previous steps of 5G_UP_ALG_4988_replaced([11199729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))}, 2*rrmAvailablePrbForInitTxNonGbr}, where;

  * available cell physical PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs, rrmAvailablePrbForInitTxNonGbr = availableUlPrb - FD resources allocated in previous steps of 5G_UP_ALG_4988_replaced([11199729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).



Then

  * L2PS shall allocate PRBs for initial transmission to UEs with non-GBR bearers with round robin method (refer 5G_UP_ALG_5002_replaced([11200325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRvLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for WRR basic rules).
  * **[CB014965-A]**
  * If gNB is using exhaustive scheduling in uplink as ([11199874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [12533660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_96eogRxlEfCQJ5daVHVkgg)), 
  * gNB-DU shall perform the check for subcell PRB resources during resource assignment which is specified in [12533660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_96eogRxlEfCQJ5daVHVkgg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).
  * else
  * **[End CB014965-A] L2PS shall allocate PRBs for initial transmission to UEs with non-GBR bearers with round robin method (refer 5G_UP_ALG_5002_replaced([11200325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRvLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for WRR basic rules).**



**[CB014965-A] Hereafter is applied when gNB is NOT using exhaustive scheduling in uplink as ([11199874](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [12533660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_96eogRxlEfCQJ5daVHVkgg)) **[End CB014965-A]****

**[CNI-127997]**

If NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg != LegacyRange_lowComplexityWrr and NRBTS.actExtSchedWeightAndPrefWrrAlg != **ExtendedRange_lowComplexityWrr**

**[End CNI-127997]**

The step-size rrmFdResPerRoundUl (5G_UP_ALG_4999_replaced([11199901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) PRBs will be allocated in each round based on token and scheduling weights. And in each round, L2PS should check whether (available BBRM PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs - [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a double summation. The outer summation is from n=1 to N, and the inner summation is from m=1 to N. The term being summed is K * alpha * rrmFdResPerRoundUI. The expression is enclosed in parentheses.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15773.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] is larger than 0, if yes, continue to allocate, otherwise, stop.

Where,

  * m: index of UEs

  * n: index of round

  * N: number of total UEs (including virtual UEs and SU UEs)

  * K: number of total rounds

  * alpha: alpha is equal to two if the virtual UEs are scheduled on two sub-cells, otherwise, alpha should be equal to one.




If (available subcell PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs - [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a double summation. The outer summation is from n=1 to N, and the inner summation is from m=1 to N. The term being summed is K * alpha * rrmFdResPerRoundUI. The expression is enclosed in parentheses.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15773.001.png[/image_path][/image-1] is equal to rrmFdResPerRoundUl, and virtual UE is scheduled in two subcells, L2PS should skip this round and go to the next round. If possible, the last rrmFdResPerRoundUl PRBs can be allocated to SU UEs or virtual UE which is located in one subcell or floor(rrmFdResPerRoundUl/2) of the additional PRBs can be allocated to a virtual UE located on two subcell ( if any)

Note: L2PS should also synchronously count the number of total used PRBs **at each round robin. If the number of total used PRBs reaches the number of PRBs in the UL bandwidth or available subcell PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs are all allocated to UEs, L2PS should stop assign PRBs to UEs.**

**[CNI-127997]**

else 

gNB-DU shall performed the check for subcell PRB resources during resource assignment which is specified in 5G_UP_ALG_32596_replaced([11200136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRvJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

gNB-DU shall also performed the check during unused PRB allocation after WRR algorithm stops (see 5G_UP_ALG_5007_replaced([11200389](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwSWN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), in the case that while assigning unused PRBs to a UE, the additional required subcell PRB resources are greater than the remaining subcell PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs (available BBRM PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs - [image-2][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features, text, or objects.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15773.002.png[/image_path][/image-2]), gNB-DU shall reduce the number of FD resources allocated to the UE such that the subcell PRB restriction is satisfied. If the remaining subcell PRBs for the Non-GBR bearers of initial transmission UEs is equal to 0, the assignment shall be stopped for any further remaining UEs.

Where:

  * alpha: see above.

  * [image-3][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15773.003.png[/image_path][/image-3]: The number of PRBs allocated for non-GBR bearers of initial transmission of UE i.




end if

**[End CNI-127997]**

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10**  (ID: `11200918`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs PRB allocation for beam groups / UE groups virtual UEs in dedicated MU-MIMO layers

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11200922`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Default Requirement for PRB Allocation for the UEs in the dedicated MU-MIMO layer

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13133209`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs 13133209: Assignment of the number of PRBs **Step1: Calculation of total Minimum distributed PRBs in MU-MIMO layer i** L2RT shall calculate the total minimum distributed PRBs required by all UEs in MU-MIMO layer i, denoted as [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a subscript format. The main term is "N". The subscript consists of "vUE(A),i,min". The "v" and "i" are in lowercase, while "U", "E", and "A" are in uppercase. The "min" is also in lowercase.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/a152a0dc-940f-478f-9009-82e5c4c6ba36.png[/image_path][/image-0] where \- i ranges from 1 to the maximum number of MU-MIMO layers for the given virtual UE (vUE(A)), \- minimum number of distributed PRBs for non-GBR UE refer to [12549048](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_jCe0QSQZEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) Note: The compensation factor α, defined in 5G_UP_ALG_9571_replaced([11148009](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2APvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), is not considered in this step. Step 2: PRB Allocation for each UE in MU-MIMO layer i step2.1 Remaining PRBs after Step 1 After Step 1, the number of remaining available PRBs in MU-MIMO layer i is denoted as: [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is: $rrm Remaining AllocPrb_i = N_{vUE(A), FdAlloc} - N_{vUE(A),i,min}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/36137d9c-241f-4fe5-a6f6-d910ec08ab74.png[/image_path][/image-1] where, \- i denotes the MU-MIMO layer index, ranging from 1 to the maximum number of MU-MIMO layers for this vUE(A); \- NvUE(A),FdAlloc: Number of PRBs allocated to vUE(A) by the FD scheduler. \- NvUE(A),i,min: Total minimum PRBs required by all UEs in layer i (from Step 1) **Step2.2 PRB Distribution among UEs in each MU-MIMO layer i** L2RT shall determine the PRB allocation Nj for each UE(j) in MU-MIMO layer i, where UE(j) belongs to the set Ui, and Ui represents the set of UEs scheduled in MU-MIMO layer i. [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $N_j = \min(\text{RoundUp}(rrmRemainingAllocPrb_i) * \frac{\alpha_j * ReqPrb_j - minPrb_j}{\sum_{u \in U_i}(\alpha_u * ReqPrb_u - minPrb_u)}, ReqPrb_j)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-09230916.png[/image_path][/image-2] Formula document link: File Name: MU PRB allocation formular where, \- j is the UE index, i is the MU-MIMO layer index ; \- Ui represents the set of UEs scheduled in MU-MIMO layer i ; \- u denotes an individual UE in layer i, i.e., an element of the set Ui ; \- rrmRemaingAllocPrbi is the available number of PRBs in MU-MIMO layer i . \- αjis the PRB compensation factor for UE j, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_9571_replaced([11148009](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2APvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)); \- ReqPrbj is the number of required PRB for UE j; \- minPrbj is the number of smallest required PRB for UE j; **Step3: The total number of Prbs allocated to UE j is the sum of PRBs in step1 and step2.** Step 4: After determining the total PRBs for all UEs in Step3, L2RT shall allocate the final PRBs to UE j as: Nj= min⁡(UE(j)'s PRB from Step 3,available PRBs for PUSCH) where, the available PRBs for PUSCH shall be updated by subtracting the final PRBs allocated to the previous UEs (1 to j-1). Note: This step serves as a protection to prevent the case where the number of distributed PRBs exceeds the number of PRBs required for an NBR UE, see the example in the following link. [PRB allocation example.xlsx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB008000%20-%20CB008499/CFAM/CB008360%20UL%20MU%20MIMO%20-%204%20layers%20for%20FR1%20TDD/Formular%20edition/PRB%20allocation%20example.xlsx?d=w6b9621d653734a3998788aeba23baa07&csf=1&web=1&e=VxHc2f) [CB008148-C] Step5: If UE j is DFT-s-OFDM UE, the adjusted PRB number for UE j shall be N''j = max {n | n <= Nj,12n = 2a3b5c}, where Nj is decided in step4. [End CB008148-C] The start PRB position for each UE refer to [13133306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wxpb5rvYEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ)

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-2**  (ID: `12794577`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Handling of Non-GBR UEs without NBR category under distributed PRB shortage

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12794578`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs When all UEs on layer i are non-GBR UEs without the NBR category and if available resources cannot satisfy the total minimum PRB allocation, L2RT shall:

  1. Sort UEs in each MU-MIMO layer descending order of their minimum PRB requirements.
  2. Select UEs starting from those with the smallest minimum PRB requirement, until the total PRBs assigned in the MU-MIMO layer fit within the available resources in the sub-area.
  3. Update the interface transmitted to L1 if some UEs are excluded from the vUE. For detailed procedures, refer to CP3.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-3**  (ID: `12746952`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs PRB allocation to the UEs in candidate UE group if at least one NBR UE is existed in this candidate UE group

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12749013`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs 12749013: Assignment of the number of PRBs [PR846088] This requirement is not applicable anymore. Please refer to [12553426](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_EXqIACUREfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the PRB allocation to UEs in candidate UE group with NBR UE(s) [End PR846088]

[Before PR846088]

If NBR category UEs are present in candidate group, each of those NBR UE i are allocated PRBs first as per their NBR requirement i.e. αi * reqNbrPrbs(i) where reqNbrPrbs is the NBR PRB requirement for UE(i) determined as per 5G_UP_ALG_22159_replaced([11199843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **Only the remaining PRBs from virtual UE are distributed as below. The NBR UE is also additionally considered in the below nonGBR resource distribution. Hence, the scheduled PRBs of the NBR UE will be sum of NBR PRB allocation and nonGBR resource distribution.**

all the UEs in that candidate UE group can get PRB number as roundDown [image-0][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15768.006.png[/image_path][/image-0],

where**,**

[image-1][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15768.007.png[/image_path][/image-1] = [image-2][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15768.008.png[/image_path][/image-2] **\- sum of the PRB allocation for NBR UEs within candidate group in earlier step.**

rrmRemainingReqPrbNum = rrmTotalReqPrbNum - sum of the PRB allocation for NBR UEs within candidate group in earlier step.

[End PR846088]

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-4**  (ID: `12553426`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs PRB allocation to the UEs in beam group if at least one NBR UE is existed in this beam group or candidate UE group

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `13133325`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs If the total sum of `numberOfDistributedResources` ([12549048](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_jCe0QSQZEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) across UEs in beam group(j) ([11149207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksMgcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) or candidate UE group([11148139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) exceeds the available PRBs (which is defined as the maximum PUSCH PRB across all subareas), the following two-step process must be executed, Note: This requirement only work when at least one NBR UE existed in beam group(j)([11149207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksMgcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) or candidate UE group([11148139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step1:

  * L2RT shall prioritize the removal of non-GBR UEs without NBR category one by one until the sum of the remaining UEs' `numberOfDistributedResources` is less than or equal to the available PRB (max PUSCH PRB across all subareas)



Step 2:

  * If all non-GBR UEs without NBR category have been removed, but the total `numberOfDistributedResources` still exceeds the available PRB, L2RT shall proceed with the following sub-steps (step2.1 & step2.2)



step2.1 If the sum of minimum required PRBs for all remaining UEs is less than or equal to the available PRB:

 **Step A: Allocate the** minimum required PRB to all NBR UEs first. The minimum required PRB for an NBR UE is determined as:****

o min required PRB for NBR = MAX{rrmMinPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31765_replaced([11199466](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci (5G_UP_ALG_6391_replaced([11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))};

 **Step B: After minimum allocations, for UEs that still require additional PRBs, L2RT shall allocate these extra PRBs to the remaining UEs based on the following priority -- allocate them** proportionally to PRB number of NBR UEs.****

o distribute the remaining PRBs **proportionally to NBR UEs.**

o The detailed equation to allocate the remaing PRBs to UEs is as follows:

rrmRemainingAllocPrb * (NBRUE(i)- minNumOfPrbUE(i))/ (sum prbs over NBR UEs in beam group or candidate UE group(j) - sum min prbs over NBR UEs in this beam group or candidate UE group(j)), where minNumOfPrbUE(i) see the stepA above and NBRUE(i) is the number of PRBs for NBR UE(i), refer to [12549048](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_jCe0QSQZEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_jCe0QSQZEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9").

step2.2 If the sum of minimum required PRBs exceeds the available PRB:

 **Step A: Reduce the allocation for all NBR UEs, ensuring they receive only their** minimum required PRB.****

 **Step B: If resources are still insufficient, L2RT shall remove some NBR UEs to meet the PRB constraint. Note: No specific removal priority is required — UEs can be randomly removed among the same type.**

[CB008148-C]

Step3. If UE i is DFT-s-OFDM UE, the adjusted PRB number for UE i shall be

NUE(i),new = max {n | n <= NUE(i),12n = 2a3b5c}, where NUE(i), the number of PRB decided in above steps in this requirement

[End CB008148-C]

The start PRB position for each UE refer to [13133306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wxpb5rvYEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ)

Note: If UE drop from virtual UE regardless of any reason, the interface shall be updated accordingly. The details shall be determined by CP3

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-5**  (ID: `13424191`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs PRB allocation to UEs in beam group or candidate UE group where at least one critical nonGBR UE exists

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `13424193`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs If there is at least one critical nonGBR UE existing in beam group(j)([11149207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksMgcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) or candidate UE group([11148139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), however, the total sum of required PRB ([11200416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_XQ7gAPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from critical nonGBR UEs in beam group(j) or candidate UE group exceeds the available PRBs (11200416), the following process is executed.

Step1:

  * L2RT shall remove all non-critical non-GBR UEs (including UE with NBR category) from the beam group or candidate UE group



Step 2:

  * If the total minimum required PRBs <= the available PRB:

  * Allocate the **minimum required PRB first. The minimum required PRB for an critical nonGBR UE is determined as**

    * MAX{rrmMinPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31765_replaced([11199466](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci (5G_UP_ALG_6391_replaced([11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))};
    * After minimum allocations, for UEs that still require additional PRBs, L2RT shall allocate them **proportionally to PRB number as follows:**

    * rrmRemainingAllocPrb * (requiredPRBUE(i)- minPRBUE(i))/ (Σ requiredPRBUE - Σ minPRBUE), where:

    * rrmRemainingAllocPrb: remaining available PRBs

    * requiredPRBUE(i) : number of required resources (refer to [11200416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_XQ7gAPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for UE i
    * minPRBUE(i) : minimum required PRB of UE i obtained as above
    * Σ requiredPRBUE / Σ minPRBUE:sum of required and minimum required prbs over critical nonGBR UEs in this beam group or candidate UE group.
    * ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​Else, i.e. the total minimum required PRBs > the available PRB: L2RT shall remove some UEs to meet the PRB constraint so that remaining UE can be allocated with minimum required PRBs. Note: No specific removal priority is required — UEs can be randomly removed among the same type.



Step3. If UE i is DFT-s-OFDM UE, the adjusted PRB number for UE i shall be

NUE(i),new = max {n | n <= NUE(i),12n = 2a3b5c}, where NUE(i), the number of PRB decided in above steps in this requirement

The start PRB position for each UE refer to [13133306](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wxpb5rvYEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ)

Note: If UE drop from virtual UE regardless of any reason, the interface shall be updated accordingly. The details shall be determined by CP3

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-6**  (ID: `12549048`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Definition of `numberOfDistributedResources`

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12549052`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs The numberOfDistributedResources for UE(i) is defined as follows:

For non-GBR UEs without NBR category and initial traffic, numberOfDistributedResources for this UE is equal to the minimum required PRB determined as follow:

  * For non-GBR UEs without NBR category and UCI multiplexing with PUSCH, the min number of required PRB is minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci (5G_UP_ALG_6391_replaced([11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[CB013204-A] MAX{rrmMinPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31765_replaced([11199466](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci (5G_UP_ALG_6391_replaced([11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))}**[End CB013204-A];****
  * For non-GBR UEs without NBR category and UCI is NOT multiplexing with PUSCH, the min number of required PRB is minSupportedNumPrbsUl (see 5G_L2_1136_replaced([11183158](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27hiIfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[CB013204-A] rrmMinPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31765_replaced([11199466](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[End CB013204-A].****



For non-GBR UEs with NBR category and initial traffic, numberOfDistributedResources for this UE is defined in[11199843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-7**  (ID: `13133306`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs Start PRB Requirement for the UEs in the dedicated MU-MIMO layer

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-10.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `13133309`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs * The starting PRB position of the first UE in MU-MIMO layer i shall be immediately adjacent to the ending PRB position of the previous virtual UE or previous SU-MIMO UE. * For the second UE and all subsequent UEs in the MU-MIMO layer i, each UE’s starting PRB position shall be immediately adjacent to the ending PRB position of the preceding UE within the same MU-MIMO layer, without any gap in between. If a gap can't be avoided, the issue shall be addressed through interface level handling to meet L1 implementation requirements, and the detailed solution shall be defined by CP3.

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-11**  (ID: `13317259`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs DFT-s-OFDM maximum PRB alignment for MU-MIMO UEs

---

**3.30.6.1.0-20.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `13317460`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs After the PRB allocation in the above procedure is done for each UE, L2RT shall first identify all MU-MIMO layers  
that contain only one UE, and this UE uses the DFT-s-OFDM waveform. These UEs shall be collected into a  
dedicated set. Next, L2RT shall select from this set the DFT-s-OFDM UE with the largest PRB allocation.Then,  
L2RT shall apply this maximum PRB value to all other DFT-s-OFDM UEs in the set during scheduling. L2RT shall  
fill it in the message between gNB and UE and gNB L2 and gNB L1. The details refer to CP3 interface

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-21 Assignment of the number of PRBs Resource distribution to proactive scheduled UEs (ID: 11201000)

**3.30.6.1.0-21.0-1**  (ID: `11201003`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs If NRCELL.actDemoteProactUlSched is TRUE, 5G-L2-PS shall distribute the remaining available FD resources to the UEs from ulProactSched in a dedicated resource distribution round as follows:

Starting with the UEs to be allocated in the smallest BWP

  * For the UE with the highest priority to be allocated in the considered BWP:

  * if there is one PUSCH subarea for the considered BWP with as many resources available as the maximum resources the UE can get as per 5G_UP_ALG_5010_replaced([11201008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), allocate the UE to the PUSCH subarea:

  * if there are not enough resources in any PUSCH subarea for the considered BWP, UE shall be skipped and shall remain in the ulProactSched priority class

  * Update the maximum available resources in a PUSCH subarea for any BWP and find the next UE with highest priority in the considered BWP. If no more UEs are to be allocated in the considered BWP, check the UEs to be allocated in the next smallest BWP

  * Repeat until all UEs were checked or no more resources are available in the BW.

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-22 Assignment of the number of PRBs Maximum number of resources for initial transmission to UE (ID: 11201008)

**3.30.6.1.0-22.0-8**  (ID: `11201047`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs For all initial transmission UEs in the to-be-scheduled list, the maximum number of resources a UE can get is

  * Minimum number of non-proactive grant resources for initial transmission to UE according to 5G_UP_ALG_26565_replaced([11201071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

  * Minimum number of proactive grant resources for initial transmission to UE according to 5G_UP_ALG_26550_replaced([11201054](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).




If UCI is expected to be multiplexed in an initial PUSCH transmission then the minimum amount of FD resources assigned to a UE shall be as determined in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) condition (7).

Note: for UE in transient phase, the above check is also required 

If an UE is scheduled for A-CSI reporting UCI only mode:

\- maximum allocation size shall be computed as max (minNumOfPrbsForEntireUciInUciOnlyMode, X) where:

o see 5G_UP_ALG_3559_replaced([11159589](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktKww_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for minNumOfPrbsForEntireUciInUciOnlyMode

o X is the minimum number of PRB allowed with 5G_UP_ALG_4999_replaced([11199901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

\- MCS that shall be considered is MIN(rrmUlProposedMcs , maxMcsQpsk), where maxMcsQpsk is the maximum MCS for modulation order Qm = 2 supported for the current MCS table

\- If ATB procedure reduces the allocation below minNumOfPrbsForEntireUciInUciOnlyMode, A-CSI scheduling is canceled.

For slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the maximum number of PUSCH PRB allocation to a UE shall not exceed rrmMaxNumPuschPrbPoolingLimit which is defined as the amount of PRB allocated by the BBRM excluding the amount of PRBs reserved for the dedicated long PUCCH:

if NRCELL.actUlMuMimo=TRUE AND NRCELL.nrCellType = xDL8UL

rrmMaxNumPuschPrbPoolingLimit= min{rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp, max{0, numAllocCellPrb -(ulBandwidth - rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp) + nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL + nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeUL}}.

else

rrmMaxNumPuschPrbPoolingLimit= max{0, numAllocCellPrb -(ulBandwidth - rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp) + nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL + nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeUL}.

end

where:

  * rrmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp (5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced([11199486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOEw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), ulBandwidth (PsCell_CellSetupReq, Ref 5G_ARC_ALG_2029). In the case of multiple BWP support, rmNumPuschPrbDedicatedBwp correpondings to the value given for the largest BWP.



-numAllocCellPrb (5G_UP_ALG_6500_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): amount of PRB allocation from BBRM being used in the current pooling window. However, during BBRM allocation transient phase (5G_UP_ALG_6839_replaced([11213362](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxG1mvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if the new numAllocCellPrb value received for the next pooling period is lower than what is being used in the current window, the new value is used.

  * nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUL, nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeUL are provided by higher layer in prbBlanking element of PsCell_CellSetupReq message



If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

  * the total number of usable PUSCH PRB limit in the slot shall not break the layer PRB capacity constraint defined in 5G_UP_ALG_26661_replaced([11214618](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOxYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The number of used/required layer PRBs for a given number of PRBs can be determined using the function numReqLayerPrb defined in 5G_UP_ALG_26582_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Provide also the rank of the UE as input to this function.



[PR810984]

  * The maximum number of available PRBs for PUSCH shall also not exceed rrmMaxNumPuschPrbPoolingLimit defined separately for layer PRB based eCPRI 7-2e pooling as below :

  * rrmMaxNumPuschPrbPoolingLimit = availableUlPrb (described in 5G_UP_ALG_26664_replaced([11196919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGI_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as the maximum number of PRBs available for PUSCH allocation per slot)




End If

[End PR810984]

For an FR1 UL CA UE which is in power split mode according to 5G_UP_ALG_10871_replaced([11119486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the maximum number of PUSCH PRB allocation to the UE shall not exceed maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower defined in 5G_UP_ALG_10883_replaced([11119896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) on Pcell in Ul overlapping slots.

  * Ul overlapping slot definition refers to 5G_UP_ALG_10871_replaced([11119486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).



Rationale: UE prioritizes to distribute TX power to PUSCH on Pcell, without the limitation, Ul parallel transmission on Scell will possibly fail due to low UE TX power.

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-23 Assignment of the number of PRBs Maximum number of proactive grant resources for initial transmission to UE (ID: 11201054)

**3.30.6.1.0-23.0-3**  (ID: `12741599`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs For all initial transmission UEs in the to-be-scheduled list, which is scheduled proactively, the maximum number of resources a UE can get is

**the minimum of the following values:**

**\- For non-skippable low priority proactive grants (see 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) _rrmMinFdResProactive = the minimum number of resources (PRBs) that would allow the corresponding TBS to be greater than NRCELL.payloadSizeProactiveNonDrx**8 *(see 5G_L2_1180_replaced([11191335](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28cvP_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for UEs not configured with DRX and NRCELL.payloadSizeProactiveDrx **8 _(see 5G_L2_1181_replaced([11182827](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27ee3vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for UEs configured with DRX if UE is not scheduled with 64qamLowSE-Index table __or max(_ correspnding TBS depending on UE confgiured with DRX or not, rdMinTbsLowSE) (rdMinTbsLowSE defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17009_replaced([11209054](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwxeZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE-Index table *_or ulUeAmbr (5G_UP_ALG_19973_replaced([11205469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwpimPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if avgAmbrPerUlSlot (5G_UP_ALG_19975_replaced([11205514](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not exceeded or accumulatedAmbr (5G_UP_ALG_20154_replaced([11205502](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if average ambr is exceeded when ulUeAmbr is configured. TBS is computed with the proposed MCS _rrmUlProposedMcs._****_*_** ***

  * For skippable low priority proactive grants (see 5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), _rrmMinFdResProactive = the minimum number of resources (PRBs) that would allow the corresponding TBS to be greater than_ rrmPayloadSzProGrantTxSkip * 8 for both UEs not configured with DRX and for UEs configured with DRX if UE is not scheduled with 64qamLowSE-Index table **or max(_rrmPayloadSzProGrantTxSkip * 8 _, [Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547] ) ([Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547] defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17009_replaced([11209054](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwxeZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE-Index table _*or ulUeAmbr (5G_UP_ALG_19973_replaced([11205469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwpimPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if avgAmbrPerUlSlot (5G_UP_ALG_19975_replaced([11205514](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not exceeded or accumulatedAmbr (5G_UP_ALG_20154_replaced([11205502](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if average ambr is exceeded when ulUeAmbr is configured. TBS is computed with the proposed MCS rrmUlProposedMcs._*******



Note: _rrmPayloadSzProGrantTxSkip is defined at 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))_

\- For TCP boost proactive grants (see 5G_UP_ALG_16873_replaced([11195408](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7w8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), _rrmMinFdResProactive = the minimum number of resources (PRBs) that would allow the corresponding TBS to be greater than_ NRBTS/NRDRB_TCP_BOOST.tcpBoostPugTbs **_*or ulUeAmbr (5G_UP_ALG_19973_replaced([11205469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwpimPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if avgAmbrPerUlSlot (5G_UP_ALG_19975_replaced([11205514](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not exceeded or accumulatedAmbr (5G_UP_ALG_20154_replaced([11205502](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if average ambr is exceeded when ulUeAmbr is configured _*. TBS is computed with the proposed MCS rrmUlProposedMcs.******* *

  * For DRB proactive grants (see 5G_UP_ALG_30332_replaced([11195558](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8_CfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), _rrmMinFdResProactive shall be the Minimum number of resources for initial transmission to UE according to 5G_UP_ALG_26565_replaced([11201071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))_.**



~~1.~~~~the minimum number of resources (PRBs) that would allow the corresponding TBS to be greater than~~ ~~NRBTS.NRDRB_PRIO_PUG/drbPriorityPugPayloadSize~~~~*8 if UE is scheduled with MCS table other than 64qamLowSE-Index table, or~~

~~2.~~~~max(~~~~NRBTS.NRDRB_PRIO_PUG/drbPriorityPugPayloadSize~~~~*8~~~~,~~ ~~rdMinTbsLowSE) (rdMinTbsLowSE defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17009_replaced([11209054](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwxeZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE-Index table or~~

~~3.~~~~ulUeAmbr (5G_UP_ALG_19973_replaced([11205469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwpimPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if avgAmbrPerUlSlot (5G_UP_ALG_19975_replaced([11205514](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not exceeded or accumulatedAmbr (5G_UP_ALG_20154_replaced([11205502](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if average ambr is exceeded when ulUeAmbr is configured.~~

~~TBS is computed with the proposed MCS rrmUlProposedMcs.~~

_rrmMinFdResProactive shall be max(rdMinPRBsAllocForDrbPriorityPugHR(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_32369_replaced([11209690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw1IyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), _rrmMinFdResProactive) if UE works in high reliablity mode.**

\- The maximum number of PRBs (_rrmMaxNumPrbUl) provided by ATB procedure in 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))._

**Note: requirement created due to editorial split of 5G_UP_ALG_5010q**

#### 3.30.6.1.0-24 Assignment of the number of PRBs Maximum number of non-proactive grant resources for initial transmission to UE (ID: 11201071)

**3.30.6.1.0-24.0-4**  (ID: `12741617`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs For all initial transmission UEs in the to-be-scheduled list, which is not scheduled proactively, the maximum number of resources a UE can get is

  * the minimum of the following values:

  * The minimum number of resources (PRBs) that can contain the amount of buffered data _rrmMinNumPrbULBO, where_ rrmMinNumPrbULBO _determined by finding the smallest number of PRBs that would allow the corresponding TBS (5G_UP_ALG_5085_replaced([11203349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to be greater than the UEs ((_rmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes or rrmBufferedDataUlTotalWithAmbrLimit_bytes if ulUeAmbr is configured - cgAdjustment _\+ max size of all possible MAC CEs_)_8) if UE is not scheduled with 64qamLowSE-Index table_ _or max((_ rmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes **_*or rrmBufferedDataUlTotalWithAmbrLimit_bytes if ulUeAmbr is configured_ \- cgAdjustment _\+ max size of all possible MAC CEs_)*8, [Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547] ) ([Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547] defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17009_replaced([11209054](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwxeZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE-Index table. (see 5G_UP_ALG_10514_replaced([11203665](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) & 5G_UP_ALG_5107_replaced([11203559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).****_*_** **_*_**




(see 5G_L2_2009_replaced([11215729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-6NU_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), assuming maximum MAC CE sizes). Requirement 5G_UP_ALG_5107_replaced([11203559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) describes how many bytes are considered for the MAC CEs.

Where cgAdjustment is defined as follows:

cgAdjustment = the transport block size for Configured Grant (CG) as determined at CG activation time (see 5G_UP_ALG_14664_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or at CG modification time (see 5G_UP_ALG_15651_replaced([11172853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if the UE is configured with CG and it’s in a firing CG state (see 5G_UP_ALG_15099_replaced([11172972](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ) and current slot is not CG occasion and the substraction of the cgAdjustment is allowed as per 5G_UP_ALG_22926_replaced([11173208](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuRkC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and UE is not in the scope of the requirement 5G_UP_ALG_31906_replaced([11173454](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 

cgAdjustment = 0 otherwise.

TBS is computed with the proposed MCS _rrmUlProposedMcs (calculated while assuming full BW allocation at last sinr reporting for UE in UL._

For VoNR users with active 5QI-1/65/66 bearer for voice service and NRCELL.actUlMinTbsForVoNR is set to TRUE and if either (the UE is not SAg capable) or (NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg is set to FALSE), then the number of resources (PRBs) is given by max{NRCELL.numPuschPrbForMinTbsVoNR, _rrmMinNumPrbULBO} where numPuschPrbForMinTbsVoNR is the number of PRBs that is required to realize the minimum TBS configured by NRCELL.ulMinTbsForVoNR as per the rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15554_replaced([11203613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))._

For VoNR users with active 5QI-1 **5QI-1/65/66 bearer for voice service, if the UE is SAg capable and NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg is set to Enabled_VoNr, then:**

 If this is a 1x grant, the MCS/PRB combination is determined as if this was non-VoNR(5QI-1/65/66) data. I.e., there are no VoNR specific PRB limits. The determination of 1x, 2, or 4x SAg is defined by by 5G_UP_ALG_19882_replaced([11205071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 If a 2x or 4x SAg grant is used, then the number of PRBs used will be set as defined in the requirements referenced above.

  * rrmMaxNumPrbULATB. Only considered if ATB procedure is enabled and rrmUlProposedMcs is equal to MCS0 if UE is not scheduled with qam64LowSE table **or using a corresponding MCS depending on rrmMaxNumPrbULATB if UE is scheduled with qam64LowSE-Index table) (see 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

  * rrmMaxNumPrbUl. Only considered if rdMprActive = 1 (or 2 for SA, see definition in 5G_L2_1371_replaced([11185224](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27wysPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))




**\- when NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to disable** , (see 5G_UP_ALG_4816_replaced([11194889](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4GhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are not scheduled more than the size of the initial BW****

  * For UE eligible for A-CSI scheduling with k2aCsi timing and not in uciOnly mode, the procedure concerning rmMinNumPrbULBO calculation and A-CSI scheduling validation specified in 5G_L2_13606_replaced([11172031](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26SzFPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is followed.



\- For UEs in DSS UL allocation transient phases (5G_UP_ALG_5180_replaced([11172453](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuMrgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) the maximum number of PRBs is limited to the value calculated based on the new and current resource allocation. If NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true and if PUSCH in NR dedicated part is larger than calculated minimum of the new and current resource allocation in the LTE shared area [CB007372-E] in given BWP [End CB007372-E] then the maximum number of PRBs is limited to number of PUSCH PRBs in NR dedicated part. If NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actUlAdaptReTxEnhDss=TRUE and (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs=TRUE or NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcsEnh=TRUE), then the maximum number of PRBs is limited, such that a retransmission with NRCELL.targetPctResourceReservedMcsUl is possible. I.e. if NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false initial transmission of one UE is limited to rounddown(min(currentAlloc, newAlloc)_100/NRCELL.targetPctResourceReservedMcsUl ). If NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true the initial transmission of one UE is limited to rounddown( max(PUSCH in NR dedicated area, min(CRM currentAlloc [CB007372-E] in given BWP [End CB007372-E], CRM newAlloc [CB007372-E] in given BWP [End CB007372-E]) )_ 100/NRCELL.targetPctResourceReservedMcsUl ).

Note, UL reTx due to HARQ NACK may use fewer PRBs with adaptive reTx, UL reTx due to DTX use same number of PRBs as initial Tx. In DTX case RLC retransmission may be needed. **Considering adaptive reTx allows more PRBs in initial Tx.**

\- UEs which active BWP is not the largest BWP (see 5G_UP_ALG_1811_replaced([11110797](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpHXP_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall not be scheduled more resources than the size of their active BWP.

**Note: requirement created due to editorial split of 5G_UP_ALG_5010_replaced([11201008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-25 Assignment of the number of PRBs MCS correction for power limited UEs with adapted maximum number of resources (ID: 11201086)

**3.30.6.1.0-25.0-3**  (ID: `11201145`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs 11201145: Assignment of the number of PRBs For all UEs with initial transmission, which are power limited related to full allocation, the MCS related to full allocation shall be corrected if the maximum number of resources doesn’t correspond to full allocation and the finally determined allocation provides a gain in transmission power with reduced number of PRBs. For this purpose, L2-RT shall evaluate the scaled PHR rrmPhrScaled[i,j,tPHR] (see 5G_UP_ALG_9755_replaced([11130171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq-_ffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and if the value of the scaled PHR is negative, a gain in transmission power is expected with smaller PRB allocation. The possible gain shall be calculated by the following equation.

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula defines `rrmPowerGain[i,j, t]` as the maximum of 0 and the minimum of `-rrmPhrScaled[i,j, t_PHR]` and 10, multiplied by the base-10 logarithm of the ratio of `rrmMaxNumPrbULATB` to `rrmNumPuschPrb[i,j,t]`.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15799.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

For an FR1 Ul inter-band CA UE with initial transmission, which is in power split mode according to 5G_UP_ALG_10871_replaced([11119486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the possible gain shall be calculated based on split TX power on SCell in Ul overlapping slots as the below:

[image-1][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or objects within the image.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15799.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

with

i: Index of the UE

j: Index of the carrier

t: Time (UL slot) the UE is scheduled

tPHR: Time (UL slot) the PHR is received last

rrmNumPuschPrb[i,j,t]: Number of PRBs of the UL transmission

rrmMaxNumPrbULATB: Maximum number of PRBs corresponding to the wideband MCS

rrmPhrScaled[i,j,tPHR]: Power Headroom Report scaled to an allocation of rrmMaxNumPrbPusch

rrmNumPuschPrb[i,SCell,tPHR]: Number of PUSCH PRBs contributing to PHR report on SCell

rrmPhr[i,SCell,tPHR]: Received Power Headroom Report for SCell

rrmMaxNumPuschPrb[SCell]: Number of PRBs available for PUSCH in the particular bandwidth system on SCell

splitTxPower[i,SCell,t]: split TX power given to SCell, shall be converted to dBm from mW for the calculation and rerfers to 5G_UP_ALG_10879_replaced([11119799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_10881_replaced([11119868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[image-2][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15799.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]: [image-3][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15799.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]in the PHR report for SCell

If the ATB shrinks the UE’s PRB allocation a gain in transmission power can be achieved if the UE is with rrmMaxNumPrbUlATB PRB in power limitation. Otherwise, the reduction of the PRB allocation won’t provide any gain in transmission power.

The wideband MCS of the UE’s last UL transmission is the basis for the MCS correction and shall be considered for the calculation of the delta MCS, which corresponds to the gain in transmission power. The following equation defines the calculation rule of the delta MCS parameter.

[image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula is: rrmDeltaMcs[i,j,t] = floor(rdSinrToMcsFactor × rrmPowerGain [i, j, t])[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15799.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]

The R&D parameter rdSinrToMcsFactor is defined in 5G_L2_1435_replaced([11182811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27ee2_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The corrected MCS is calculated by the sum of the wideband MCS and the delta MCS rrmDeltaMcs. If the corrected MCS corresponds to MCS10 or MCS17 [CB008246-E-UPD001] which comes from 64QAM table only( i.e., according to Table 5.1.3.1-1 in TS38.214) [End CB008246-E-UPD001] the next higher MCS (MCS index + 1) shall be finally selected from the set of supported MCSes. Furthermore, the corrected MCS shall be clipped at the upper range of the MCS index, so that the finally selected MCS doesn’t exceed the highest supported MCS.

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-26 Assignment of the number of PRBs Best MCS-PRB combination for power limited UE (ID: 13424606)

**3.30.6.1.0-26.0-1**  (ID: `13424616`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs If Link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency for UL is enabled ([12472235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_OeKtoA1aEfCuhewgqkUVKQ)) ,

  * gNB-DU shall perform the evaluation of the best (PRB, MCS) combination to drain the UE buffer for a power limited UE as [12493068](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Q8WaEBNqEfCph-OiSyk16Q)for the traffic excluding nonGBR with the following inputs:
  * PRB_start: rrmMaxNumPrbUl from [12472327](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_RTT0sw3cEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)
  * MCS_start: rrmUlProposedMcs from [12472327](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_RTT0sw3cEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)
  * SINR_start: rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr from [1247232](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_RTT0sw3cEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)
  * UE buffer: buffer size containing all the bearers excluding non-GBR.
  * The output of [12493068](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Q8WaEBNqEfCph-OiSyk16Q) includes 

    * PRB_N: the max number of PRBs to drain the considered buffer from algorithm [12493068](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Q8WaEBNqEfCph-OiSyk16Q)
    * MCS_N: the MCS associated with PRB_N
    * SINR_N: the rescaled corrected average SINR associated with PRB_N
  * If the power limited UE has nonGBR traffic then gNB-DU shall perform the procedure defined in [13406690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-W9DBQ1xEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) to calculate the maximum number of PRBs needed to drain the non-GBR buffer of the UE.

  * The output of [13406690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-W9DBQ1xEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) includes PRB_MaxFeasibleAllocation (the max number of PRBs to drain the non-GBR buffer)

  * The MCS_N has to be adapted if there is less PRBs allocated than demanded (PRB_N) due to not enough resources by recalculating the rescaled corrected average SINR for power limited UE based on the actual allocated number of PRBs ([11131543](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_kEmOgPvKEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and comparing it with required SINR according to [12492829](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX__yo8ARL5EfCph-OiSyk16Q)




[CB014045-A-UPD001]

It has been agreed that, in order to reduce the effort of refactoring of the legacy SW, the implementation will deviate from the above requirement and follow the below description instead:

  * For a certain UE, for each pending traffic x with priority higher than non-GBR, in order of descending priority and as long as there are available resources to distribute, gNB-DU shall run [12493068](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Q8WaEBNqEfCph-OiSyk16Q "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2ftx_q8waebnqefcph-oisyk16q&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf") (with below input configuration) to estimate the number of required PRBs to serve only buffer x:
  * (PRB_start, MCS_start) = (rrmMaxNumPrbUl, rrmUlProposedMcs) from [12472327](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_RTT0sw3cEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_tauaapvfee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf");
  * SINR_start = rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr from [1247232](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_RTT0sw3cEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_tauaapvfee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf");
  * ueTargetBuffer = size of buffer x;



getting: (PRB_Nx, MCS_Nx).

Then gNB-DU gives the UE prb(x) = MIN{PRB_Nx, "available PRBs"} and updates the counting of the remaining available PRBs.

  * After that, and also if the available resources have been exhausted, gNB-DU shall run [13406690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-W9DBQ1xEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) to estimate the number of required PRBs to serve the entire pending (nonGBR) data:
  * getting: (PRB_MaxFeasibleAllocation).
  * prbs(nonGBR) = MIN{PRB_MaxFeasibleAllocation, "available PRBs"}
  * prbs(nonGBR) is maximum number of PRBs that the WRR/Exhaustive scheduler can give to this UE.

  * If finally less PRBs are allocated than demanded (PRB_N) due to lack of available resources, MCS_N has to be adapted by recalculating the rescaled corrected average SINR for power limited UE based on the actual allocated number of PRBs ([11131543](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_kEmOgPvKEe-AqvopbP1qhQ "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2ftx_kemogpvkee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf")) and comparing it with required SINR according to [12492829](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX__yo8ARL5EfCph-OiSyk16Q "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2ftx__yo8arl5efcph-oisyk16q&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf");




[End CB014045-A-UPD001]

If Link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency for UL is disabled ([12472235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_OeKtoA1aEfCuhewgqkUVKQ)), gNB shall follow the procedure defined in [11201086](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwULYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-27 Assignment of the number of PRBs FD Resource allocation for TA grant (ID: 11201150)

**3.30.6.1.0-27.0-3**  (ID: `13464663`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs [CB010737-C]

If NRCELL/actAdaptiveDmrsList does not contain "Mixed PUSCH" or UE is not identified as a static UE for adaptive DMRS ([13396700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_IVoJUAflEfGozuGzRUXHpg "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fbi_ivojuaflefgozugzruxhpg&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf")):

[End CB010737-C]

Whenever a TA grant is requested and UCI is expected with the PUSCH transmission (long PUCCH is configured - NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') the FD scheduler shall assign a relatively small allocation of max (16, minAllowedPushPrbWithUci([11197037: Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_CveOIfvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) PRB to the UE, if the ATB (see [11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) doesn’t restrict the PRB allocation to a smaller value and if the UE is not part of any other UL requestClass traffic (see [11194320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If UCI is not expected with the PUSCH the allocation of the TA grant shall be 16 PRB.

[CB010737-C]

Note: The TA grant for timing advance measurement is typically scheduled when there’s no other UL grant scheduled for a certain time, then only small PRB allocation for TA grant. 

else if NRCELL/actAdaptiveDmrsList contains "Mixed PUSCH" and UE is identified as a static UE for adaptive DMRS ([13396700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_IVoJUAflEfGozuGzRUXHpg "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fbi_ivojuaflefgozugzruxhpg&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconf")):

Whenever a TA grant is requested and UCI is expected with the PUSCH transmission (long PUCCH is configured - NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') the FD scheduler shall assign the allocation as for the normal data transmission, but with a minimal value equals to max (16, minAllowedPushPrbWithUci([11197037: Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_CveOIfvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) PRB to the UE, if the ATB (see [11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) doesn’t restrict the PRB allocation to a smaller value and if the UE is not part of any other UL requestClass traffic (see [11194320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv0cIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If UCI is not expected with the PUSCH the minimal value of the allocation of the TA grant shall be 16 PRB.

Note: The TA grant for FOE (frequency offset estimation) measurement could be scheduled also when there’s continuous UL transmission. Then the TA grant could be also used for the normal data transmission hence the PRB allocation should still follow the UE buffer status, just to set a minimal value to ensure the accuracy of the measurement. 

[End CB010737-C] 

If the ATB shrinks the UL allocation of the UE to a smaller value the TA grant shall request an allocation, which fits to the allocation defined by the ATB to allow the reception of a non-DTXed UL transmission. In addition, for an additional PRB reduction based on the buffer utilization (see [11203503](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the FD scheduler shall not reduce the PRBs to a value smaller than 16 PRBs.

Editor's note: Further PRB restrictions like defined in [11203573](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) shall be considered for the scheduling of the TA grant.

---

#### 3.30.6.1.0-28 Assignment of the number of PRBs FD Resource allocation for DSS (ID: 11201166)

**3.30.6.1.0-28.0-2**  (ID: `12680303`)

Assignment of the number of PRBs [CB007372-AA] For a FDD cell shared with LTE and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true, PUSCH transmissions after the RA procedure shall be scheduled the same way as on FDD non DSS cells ([11201742: FD Resource allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). This requirement is applicable only for FDD cell shared with LTE and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false. [End CB007372-AA]

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE, PUSCH transmissions after the RA procedure shall be scheduled on the PRBs from **the min to the max of the NR PUSCH area including prachPrbOffset received in EN DSS UL SUBCARRIER RESOURCE ALLOCATION REQUEST (5G_UP_ALG_5180_replaced([11172453](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuMrgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))****.**

Msg3 transmissions are scheduled with the number of PRBs given in the requirement [13349365: Transport Block size for RA-Msg3 and PRB constraints](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_KVGddOo7EfCnwLcpJWh_Zw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), starting from prachPrbOffset.

If PRB blanking function is activated:

{

DSS FD resource allocation is specified in 5G_UP_ALG_4988_replaced([11199729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

}

The PRBs indicated in this requirements are given with respect to the start of the full UL bandwidth. The start relative to the PRB 0 of the initial and dedicated bandwidth parts shall be computed by 5G-L2-PS.
