  * nCCE_used(i, t) = number of CCEs within nth(highest) dedicated BWP allocated for PDCCH (UL grant) for UE(s) with highest eligible priority class = [CB014699-D] criticalVoipTraffic, criticalGbrTraffic and [End CB014699-D] VoipTraffic & gbrTraffic and scheduling PUSCH transport block containing data from their prioritized (GBR positive token bucket) GBR bearer irrespective of their active BWP, otherwise, it shall be 0.

  * nCCE_usable(i, t) = number of CCEs available for UL NR scheduling within the ith dedicated BWP.

  * Where: t refers to scheduled slot.




ELSE

  * GbrUlPdcchLoad(t) = nCCE_used(t) / nCCE_usable(t), where:

  * nCCE_used(t) = number of CCEs allocated for PDCCH (UL grant) for UE(s) with highest eligible priority class = [CB014699-D] criticalVoipTraffic, criticalGbrTraffic and [End CB014699-D] VoipTraffic & gbrTraffic and scheduling PUSCH transport block containing data from its prioritized (GBR positive token bucket) GBR bearer, otherwise, it shall be 0.

  * nCCE_usable(t) = number of CCEs available for UL NR scheduling.

  * Where: t refers to scheduled slot.




END

  * Notes for nCCE_usable calculation:

  * nCCE_usable shall calculate the number of CCEs available for UL scheduling in any given slot.

  * The construction of coreset and PDCCH slot pattern for a given cell is explained in 5G_UP_ALG_11211_replaced([11168405](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktv_kfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) & 5G_UP_ALG_3958_replaced([11168909](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktzp8fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and split of CCEs, if any, between UL/DL is explained in 5G_UP_ALG_3955_replaced([11169860](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt6Xp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) which can be used to calculate nCCE_usable.
  * The pre-empted CCEs (5G_UP_ALG_4032_replaced([11169907](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt6-svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the available CCEs, if any, shall be considered in nCCE_usable.
  * With PRB pooling (if slowPrbPoolingAllowed (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to 'true'), the nCCE_usable shall consider the number of CCEs that can be used which is limited by the total amount of usable PRB allocated for PDCCH by the BBRM and this grant may change every BB pooling period (5G_UP_ALG_6960_replaced([11169929](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt6-t_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

  * Average:

  * avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(t) = (1/Tprb) * GbrUlPdcchLoad(t) + (1−(1/Tprb)) * avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(t−1)

  * If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

  * avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(i, t) = (1/filterCoefficient) * GbrUlPdcchLoad(i, t) + (1−(1/filterCoefficient)) * avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(i, t−1)

  * Where: i ϵ {1, 2, …, n} and n refers to the number of dedicated BWP per cell (see 5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * Where: filterCoefficient could be a R&D parameter, with the following suggested properties:

  * Range= 40...400; Step size= 10; Default= 200 (see R&D parameter rdGbrCongestFilterCoeff in 5G_L2_4366_replaced([11188276](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28HYBvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * Periodic Load:




At the end of every GBR congestion detection period T (NRCELL.congDetectPeriod): 

\- avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(T) = the latest value of avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(t) prior to T

\- If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

\- avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(i, T) = the latest value of avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(i, t) prior to T

\- Where: i ϵ {1, 2, …, n} and n refers to the number of dedicated BWP per cell (see 5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

which is used as GBR UL PDCCH congestion control mechanism described in 5G_UP_ALG_31356_replaced([11201693](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Exceptions:

  * For gbrPdcchCongHandlingUl = FALSE or GBR congestion detection period NRCELL.congDetectPeriod = 0: L2-PS does not need to perform this GBR UL PDCCH Load monitoring

  * If there are no GBR and [CB014699-D] critical GBR [End CB014699-D] configured bearers, the avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(T) is set to 0%

  * Slots without any CCEs available for UL NR PDCCH (PDCCH for UL grant) shall be skipped.




Note that when the PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell ([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) _GBR congestion limit takes the value of_ maxGBRTrafficLimit_L2PS (**[12704521S](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_oy0TEGCREfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) ).

---

###### 3.30.6.1.1.1.0-2 UL PDCCH GBR Congestion Control Monitoring of UL PDCCH resources per GBR UE (ID: 12496156)

**3.30.6.1.1.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `13399834`)

UL PDCCH GBR Congestion Control gNB-DU shall monitor the PDCCH resource (CCEs) used by UEs for scheduling the prioritized (i.e. having positive GBR token bucket) LCGs containing GBR bearers which is eventually used to de-prioritize those GBR UEs during PDCCH congestion control based on their GBR PDCCH resource usage.

This requirement is applicable only for FR1(both TDD and FDD). The rationale is that there is no benefit in executing PDCCH congestion control algorithm in FR2 without FDM & dynamic PDCCH.

Activation: GBR feature, and UL PDCCH congestion control is activated:   
(NRBTS/NRCELL.actNonVoNRGBRService = TRUE [CB014699-D] and/or NRCELL.actCriticalTraffic=TRUE [End CB014699-D] & NRBTS/NRCELL.gbrPdcchCongHandlingUl = TRUE) in the cell.

Metrics:

  * Scope: per UE

  * Unit: CCEs




Base:

\- usedCCEsGbrUl(i, t) = number of CCEs allocated for PDCCH (UL grant) for UE i with highest priority class = VoIP traffic and GBR traffic and scheduling PUSCH data for LCG containing positive GBR token bucket, otherwise, it shall be 0.

\- Where:

i refers index of the UE

t refers to the scheduled slot

  * Average:



The average PDCCH CCE utilization be the UE i to schedule GBR data is then calculated via an exponential filter every UL scheduling slot t

\- avgUsedCCEsGbrUl(i,t) = (1/filterCoefficient) * usedCCEsGbrUl(i,t) + (1−(1/filterCoefficient)) * avgUsedCCEsGbrUl(i,t−1)

filterCoefficient could be a R&D parameter constant for the averaging function, with the following suggested properties:

\- Range= 40...400; Step size= 10; Default= 200 (see R&D parameter rdGbrCongestFilterCoeff in 5G_L2_4366_replaced([11188276](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28HYBvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * Periodic Load:



At the end of every GBR congestion detection period T (NRCELL.congDetectPeriod): 

\- avgUsedCCEsGbrUl(i, T) = the latest value of avgUsedCCEsGbrUl(i) prior to T

which is used as GBR UL PDCCH congestion control mechanism described in 5G_UP_ALG_22725_replaced([11183487](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvK76_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Exceptions:

  * For NRCELL.gbrPdcchCongHandlingUl = FALSE or GBR congestion detection period NRCELL.congDetectPeriod = 0: L2-PS does not need to perform this GBR UL PDCCH Load monitoring

  * Slots without any CCEs available for UL NR PDCCH (PDCCH for UL grant) shall be skipped.

  * The exponential filter is updated UL slot (see above) or only when UE is scheduled. In later case, the filter update shall also be done in that slot for all missing UL NR slots (considering zero load in those slots) since the filter was last updated so that the UE GBR load is not artificially seen higher. It is left to entity level/implementation to define which manner has the least complexity.

---

###### 3.30.6.1.1.1.0-3 UL PDCCH GBR Congestion Control UL PDCCH Congestion Detection (ID: 11201693)

**3.30.6.1.1.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13400013`)

UL PDCCH GBR Congestion Control gNB-DU shall support the GBR UL PDCCH congestion detection mechanism.

This requirement is applicable only for FR1(both TDD and FDD). The rationale is that there is no benefit in executing PDCCH congestion control algorithm in FR2 without FDM & dynamic PDCCH.

**Activation: GBR feature and PDCCH congestion control is activated in the cell**

(NRCELL.actNonVoNRGBRService = TRUE & NRCELL.gbrPdcchCongHandlingUl = TRUE)

Scope: GBR PDCCH congestion shall be detected

IF n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

  * Per dedicated BWP i.e. 'n' independent PUCCH congestion detection mechanism per cell.



Note, there can be more than one dedicated BWP configured per cell. power saving BWP is considered a dedicated BWP and GBR PDCCH congestion detection shall also be performed for power saving BWP.

ELSE

  * per cell.



GBR UL PDCCH congestion detection requires the following inputs:

  * NRCELL.actGBRAdmissionControl is set to 2(Corresponding to GBR services).
  * GBR congestion limit: NRCELL.maxGBRTrafficLimit (I.e. ,marks a certain maximum percentage of resource allocation for GBR periodic average UL PDCCH CCE utilization for UEs scheduling its GBR bearers in the cell).
  * For UL PDCCH GBR CCE load, namely avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(T) refer to 5G_UP_ALG_31354_replaced([11201675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * For UL PDCCH DC-GBR CCE load, namely avgDcGbrUlPdcchLoad(T) refer to 5G_UP_ALG_32410_replaced([11201685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * UL PDCCH GBR congestion hysteresis factor (R&D parameter rdPdcchGbrCceCongHyst, see 5G_UP_ALG_31290_replaced([11193635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvuVifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
  * UL PDCCH GBR hysteresis limit = (UL PDCCH GBR congestion limit) * (UL PDCCH GBR congestion hysteresis factor)



Once every GBR congestion detection period (NRCELL.congDetectPeriod):

  * UL PDCCH GBR congestion is detected, if the periodic average UL PDCCH GBR CCE load exceeds the NRCELL.maxGbrTrafficLimit, otherwise congestion is not considered detected.



Note that when the PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell ([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) _GBR congestion limit takes the value of_ maxGBRTrafficLimit_L2PS (**[12704521S](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_oy0TEGCREfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) ).

For UL PDCCH GBR load, following conditions is defined:

If NRCELL.actDcGbrService = TRUE,

  * UL PDCCH GBR load = Sum of periodic average UL PDCCH GBR CCE load avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(i, T) and UL PDCCH DC-GBR CCE load avgDcGbrUlPdcchLoad(T)



Else

  * UL PDCCH GBR load = Periodic average UL PDCCH GBR CCE load avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(i, T)



If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

  * For i_th dedicated BWP, where i ϵ { 2, …, n}, UL PDCCH GBR load= Periodic average GBR UL PDCCH CCE load avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(i, T);
  * For i_th BWP, where i = 1 (largest dedicated BWP), UL PDCCH GBR load= DC-GBR PDCCH usage avgDcGbrUlPdcchLoad(T)+ UL PDCCH GBR CCE load avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(i, T)



Rationale: To restrict the sum of CCE usage for all kind of GBR traffic (including GBR, MC-GBR, DC-GBR) not exceed NRCELL.maxGBRTrafficLimit, as DC-GBR PDCCH usage avgDcGbrUlPdcchLoad(T) shall only report for largest BWP, so it shall be count together with UL GBR PDCCH usages of avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(i, T) for largest BWP GBR UL PDCCH congestion; and for other BWP(except largest BWP) the UL GBR PDCCH usages of avgGbrUlPdcchLoad(i, T) shall be used for GBR UL PDCCH congestion as it counts all GBR(also including MC-GBR, DC-GBR) CCE usage .

As soon as GBR PDCCH congestion is detected, the following handling shall apply:

  * UEs configured with GBR bearers (having **positive token buckets) shall be selected for de-prioritization in scheduling. GBR PDCCH congestion control shall not be applicable to [CB014699-D] critical 5QI-1/65/66 and [End CB014699-D] 5QI-1/65/66 and DC-GBR.**
  * Criteria for GBR UE selection is same as PUSCH defined in 5G_UP_ALG_31361_replaced([11201715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * As many GBR UEs [CB014699-D] and Critical GBR UEs [End CB014699-D] shall be selected such that sum of the UL-PDCCH CCE utilization of the selected UEs for scheduling its GBR bearers is larger or equal to the excess of the PDCCH GBRload hysteresis limit.
  * All GBR bearers of the de-prioritized GBR UEs shall be considered as normal non-GBR traffic and shall be treated as normal non-GBR category in scheduling as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4794_replaced ([11194709](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv24YfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * [CB014699-D] All critical GBR bearers of the de-prioritized critical GBR UEs shall be considered as critical non-GBR traffic and shall be move to criticalDrbTraffic in scheduling as defined in ([13399502](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rOoVAAsFEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB014699-D].



GBR PDCCH congestion is considered to be resolved, if the average PDCCH GBR load drops below the PDCCH GBR load hysteresis limit, otherwise congestion is not considered resolved.

When GBR congestion is resolved, all de-prioritized GBR UEs [CB014699-D] and critical GBR UEs [End CB014699-D] will return to normal GBR scheduling. In other words, the set of de-prioritized GBR UEs is emptied.

Note:

  * If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), as the GBR congestion control mechanism is per dedicated BWP, it is possible that congestion might be detected in one BWP but not in the other and so on.

---

##### 3.30.6.1.1.2 PUSCH GBR Congestion Control

###### 3.30.6.1.1.2.0-1 PUSCH GBR Congestion Control GBR PUSCH PRB load congestion detection (ID: 11201708)

**3.30.6.1.1.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13399625`)

PUSCH GBR Congestion Control gNB-DU shall support the GBR PUSCH congestion detection mechanism on the cell in FR1 TDD and FDD.

  * Activation: GBR feature and PDSCH congestion control is activated in the cell



(NRCELL.actNonVoNRGBRService = TRUE & NRCELL.gbrPuschCongHandling = TRUE)

  * Scope: GBR PUSCH congestion shall be detected

  * IF n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):




\- Per dedicated BWP i.e. 'n' independent PUSCH congestion detection mechanism per cell.

Note, there can be more than one dedicated BWP configured per cell. GBR PUSCH congestion detection shall also be performed for power saving BWP similar to dedicated BWP.

ELSE

  * per cell.

  * Input:

  * GBR PUSCH congestion detection per cell or per dedicated BWP requires the following inputs:

  * Per cell, periodic average PUSCH GBR PRB load avgGbrPrbLoad_n(T) refers to 5G_UP_ALG_8172_replaced([11201209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwUycfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and periodic average PUSCH DC-GBR PRB load avgDcGbrPrbLoad_n(t) (refer ro 5G_UP_ALG_31516_replaced([11201392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwWAlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Rationale : Monitoring and counting of DC-GBR PUSCH usages shall be added into the GBR PUSCH usages as total PUSCH GBR load. Note- DC-GBR load monitoring is applied only to largest dedicated BWP. 




\- per dedicated BWP , periodic average PUSCH GBR PRB load avgGbrPrbLoadDL(i, T) refer to 5G_UP_ALG_8172_replaced([11201209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwUycfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Power saving BWP shall additionally support the PRB load monitoring and its periodic average PUSCH GBR PRB load avgGbrPrbLoadDL(i, T) shall be used for GBR congestion detection.

\- T refers to end of GBR congestion detection period provided at cell setup/reconfiguration

(NRCELL.congDetectPeriod). Any change in value provided is immediately taken into use at end of current congestion detection interval.

  * PUSCH GBR congestion limit: Marks a certain maximum percentage of PUSCH resources to be utilized for GBR as defined by NIDD parameter NRCELL.maxGbrTrafficLimit.



\- PUSCH GBR congestion hysteresis factor: R&D parameter rdUlsGbrPrbCongHyst, see 5G_UP_ALG_31565_replaced([11209415](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwzTn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

  * PUSCH GBR load hysteresis limit: As product of the PUSCH GBR limit and the PUSCH GBR congestion hysteresis factor.

  * Congestion Detection:

  * If any following conditions are fulfilled, the GBR PUSCH congestion is detected, otherwise congestion is not considered detected :

  * GBR PUSCH congestion is detected per Cell / Per Dedicated BWP , if the periodic average PUSCH GBR load exceeds the GBR Congestion limit ( I.e. , NRCELL.maxGBRTrafficLimit). Note: If GBR PUSCH Congestion detection is performed per BWP, GBR PUSCH congestion control shall also performed per dedicated BWP. Because, it is possible that some BWP is congested but still while other is not congested, or have been resolved.




Note that when the PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell ([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) _GBR congestion limit takes the value of_ maxGBRTrafficLimit_L2PS (**[12704521S](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_oy0TEGCREfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) ).

As soon as GBR PUSCH congestion is detected on cell or any dedicated BWP in the cell, the congestion deprioritize handling shall be performed according to 5G_UP_ALG_31361_replaced([11201715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). GBR PUSCH congestion control shall not be applicable to [CB014699-D] critical 5QI-1/65/66 and [End CB014699-D] 5QI-1/65/66 and DC-GBR. 

  * Congestion Resolved:

  * If following conditions are fulfilled, the GBR PUSCH congestion is resolved, otherwise congestion is not considered resolved:

  * Largest dedicated BWP periodic average PUSCH GBR PRB load avgGbrPrbLoad(T) drops below the PUSCH GBR load hysteresis limit,Otherwise congestion is not considered to be resolved. The rationale of deprioritize GBR load to the load hysteresis limit is to avoid ping-ping congestion happen.




[CB014699-D] When GBR congestion is resolved, the re-prioritized critical GBR LCGs return to normal GBR scheduling according to ([11201724](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB014699-D].

When GBR congestion is resolved, the re-prioritized GBR LCGs return to normal GBR scheduling according to 5G_UP_ALG_31366_replaced ([11201724](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 3.30.6.1.1.2.0-2 PUSCH GBR Congestion Control GBR LCG/UE de-prioritization in UL congestion (ID: 11201715)

**3.30.6.1.1.2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `13399635`)

PUSCH GBR Congestion Control When GBR PUSCH congestion is detected on a cell or any dedicated BWP in the cell, gNB shall deprioritize GBR bearer(s) [CB014699-D] deprioritize critical GBR bearer(s) [End CB014699-D] or UE(s) as following:

  * The LCGs with GBR bearers shall be selected to de-prioritize from Cell or from each dedicated BWP in the cell which are detected congestion (5G_UP_ALG_31359_replaced ([11201708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

  * If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced ([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):




As the GBR congestion control mechanism is performed per dedicated BWP, it is possible that congestion might be detected in one BWP but not in the other and so on. If GBR congestion is detected in multiple BWPs (including PS-BWP), GBR [CB014699-D] and critical GBR [End CB014699-D] LCG selection for de-prioritization shall be performed starting from smallest to the largest BWP, so that the predicted GBR load reduction resulting from LCG de-prioritization in smaller BWP can already be considered in the larger BWP as the smaller BWPs are always nested within the larger BWP.

Else

The GBR LCGs/UEs to be de-prioritized shall be selected per cell.

  * As many LCGs with GBR bearers [CB014699-D] and critical GBR bearers [End CB014699-D] shall be selected such that sum of the GBR PUSCH resource utilization of the selected bearers (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_31378_replaced ([11201731](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is larger than or equal to the excess of the PUSCH GBR load hysteresis limit per Cell or dedicated BWP in the cell. The rationale of deprioritize GBR load to the load hysteresis limit is to avoid ping-ping congestion happen. GBR PUSCH congestion control shall not be applicable to [CB014699-D] critical 5QI-1/65/66 and [End CB014699-D] 5QI1/65/66 and DC-GBR.

  * The UL GBR LCGs or [CB014699-D] or critical GBR LCGs [End CB014699-D] or UEs to be de-prioritized and excluded from scheduling are determined in order of the following criteria and step (criteria and tie break are identical for PUSCH and PDCCH congestion detection):




Step1: LCGs of UEs in power limitation.

Step2: Then from higher to lower LCG/UE priority value based on the NRCELL.congWeightAlg selection:

  * If the NRCELL.congWeightAlg = 'priorityLevel', the bearer congestion priority is based on the NRBTS/NRDRB.priorityLevel configured value for the bearer, the lower priority values indicate higher priority.

  * If the NRCELL.congWeightAlg = 'arpPrioPerVul', the bearer congestion priority is based on the ARP priority configured for the bearers (refer to 5G_CP_IF_NTW_1869_replaced ([11355256](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__A7oaPvmEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_CP_IF_NTW_8457_replaced ([11355289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__A7ocPvmEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). First the bearers with ARP vulnerability "preemptable" are prioritized in order of the ARP priority, then the bearers with ARP vulnerability "not preemptable" also in order of ARP priority.

  * LCG priority value is equal to the lowest priority value among all GBR bearers within the LCG.

  * UE priority value is equal to the lowest priority value among all GBR bearers within the UE.




Step3: Then from higher to lower PUSCH GBR resource utilization of an LCG for PUSCH Congestion Control (see 5G_UP_ALG_31378_replaced ([11201731](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), OR higher to lower PDCCH GBR resource utilization of a UE for PDCCH Congestion Control (see artifact [12496156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-fhWtBRLEfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Step4: Random order.

  * In case of GBR LCG de-prioritization due to PUSCH congestion, gNB-DU shall additionally trigger CG suspension/deactivation for the corresponding UE, if required, as described in 5G_UP_ALG_31617_replaced([11173272](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note, both dynamic grant de-prioritization and CG deactivation for the UE shall be done in parallel independently and dynamic grant de-prioritization shall not wait for the trigger from CG deactivation procedure.



GBR LCGs/UEs (with positive token bucket) having been selected to be de-prioritized shall be handled in UL scheduling as follows until congestion is resolved:

  * Delay based prioritization values (See 5G_UP_ALG_8053_replaced ([11196209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwBQdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are no longer applied for the [CB014699-D] deprioritized critical GBRLCGs/UEs [End CB014699-D], deprioritized GBR LCGs/UEs due to GBR congestion; instead, a priority value and fix schedule weight: rdCongDepriorGbrSchedulWeight (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_31697_replaced ([11193461](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value of standardized 5QIs with resource type: non-GBR shall be used. It is to handle in "drbTraffic" request class (see 5G_UP_ALG_4794_replaced ([11194709](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv24YfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as same as non-GBR but using specific scheduling weight. [CB014699-D] When a critical GBR bearer is de-prioritized, it is handled as critical non-GBR traffic and moved to the criticalDrbTraffic request class (refer to [13399502](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rOoVAAsFEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and using the fix schedule weight: rdCongDepriorCriticalGbrSchedulWeight ([13399502](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rOoVAAsFEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB014699-D].



This selection only needs be performed for congested cell or dedicated BWP(s) in the cell. When GBR PUSCH/PDCCH congestion is detected or when there are any load level changes following this detection, additional LCGs/UEs need to be de-prioritized or some de-prioritized LCGs/UEs need to be removed from the de-prioritized list. Priority level of LCGs/UEs de-prioritization shall be considered within its active BWP which is going under congestion control.

The de-prioritization shall be performed on LCG(s) basis for PUSCH congestion and be performed on UE basis in case of PDCCH congestion.

UE is not considered for de-prioritization if the UE is in the "inactive BWP" like during the UL BWP scanning procedure (see [11111313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpMPyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

---

###### 3.30.6.1.1.2.0-3 PUSCH GBR Congestion Control PUSCH GBR congestion reprioritization (ID: 11201724)

**3.30.6.1.1.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11201727`)

PUSCH GBR Congestion Control gNB shall select as many deprioritized GBR bearer(s) [CB014699-D] or deprioritized critical GBR bearer(s) [End CB014699-D] or UE(s) as possible back to GBR scheduling when GBR PUSCH congestion is resolved (refer 5G_UP_ALG_31358_replaced ([11201703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The re-prioritization shall be performed with following conditions met and on LCG basis which containing GBR [CB014699-D] and critical GBR [End CB014699-D] bearers:

  * For a cell or each dedicated BWP in the cell, the average PUSCH GBR PRB load plus estimated load of reprioritize bearer(s) (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_31378_replaced ([11201731](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which belongs to them would not exceed PUSCH GBR congestion limit.

  * After reprioritization, the gNB shall update the average PUSCH GBR PRB load of a cell or each dedicated BWP in the cell with the estimated load of the reprioritized LCG(s).




The priority of GBR [CB014699-D] and critical GBR [End CB014699-D] LCG to be reprioritized with the following steps:

Step1: The congestion priority value based on the NRCELL.congWeightAlg selection (same as de-prioritization step1 defined in 5G_UP_ALG_31361_replaced([11201715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Step2: If value is same in step 1, GBR PUSCH PRB load (5G_UP_ALG_31378_replaced([11201731](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in increasing order.

**[CB008118-CB] When a GBR LCG is re-prioritized, gNB-DU shall also resume the CG activation procedures for the corresponding UE as described in 5G_UP_ALG_31617_replaced([11173272](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). ****[End CB008118-CB]** ****

---

###### 3.30.6.1.1.2.0-4 PUSCH GBR Congestion Control Estimation PUSCH PRB load per GBR bearer for deprioritization and reprioritization (ID: 11201731)

**3.30.6.1.1.2.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `13399754`)

PUSCH GBR Congestion Control gNB shall estimate average PUSCH PRB load of GBR UE [CB014699-D] and Critical GBR UE [End CB014699-D] at LCG level, that will be used for PUSCH congestion de-prioritization and re-prioritization on FR1 TDD and FDD cell.

The monitoring function shall be applied to all LCGs containing [CB014699-D] critical GBR and [CB014699-D] GBR bearers (referred to as an GBR LCG).

Activation: GBR feature and PUSCH congestion control are activated (NRCELL.actNonVoNRGBRService=true & NRCELL.gbrPuschCongHandling = TRUE)

Metrics:

  * Scope: per GBR LCG (refer to LCGs containing GBR bearers)

  * The instantaneous UL GBR UE spectrum efficiency is calculated at every slot t:




InstUeUlSE(i,t) =TbsUl(i,t) / PrbUl(i,t),

where:

i: index of the UE with an GBR bearer/CB014699-D] critical GBR bearer [End CB014699-D].

t: slot of UE is scheduled for PUSCH.

PrbUl(i,t): The overall number of PUSCH PRBs used for UE i in slot t for all bearers

TbsUl(i,t): UL TBS of UE i at slot t which is equal to the sum of the TBS of the transmitted codeword(s)

If the UE is not scheduled in a slot with UL transmission, the calculation shall not update.

  * The average UL GBR UE spectrum efficiency is then calculated via an exponential filter on slot basis.



avgUeUlSE(i,t) = (1/Tprb) * InstUeUlSE(i,t) + (1−(1/Tprb)) * avgUeUlSE(i, t−1),

where

i: index of the UE with an GBR bearer/CB014699-D] critical GBR bearer [End CB014699-D].

t: slot of UE is scheduled for PUSCH.

Tprb: R&D parameter constant for the averaging function, with the following suggested properties:

\- Range= 40...400; Step size= 10; Default= 200 (see R&D parameter rdGbrPrbLoadFilterCoeff in 5G_L2_4364_replaced([11188264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28HYBPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

InstUeUlSE(i,t): as calculated above

\- The PUSCH PRB load per GBR LCG k is calculated when PUSCH GBR congestion is detected or resolved (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_31359_replaced([11201708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

\- GbrUlPrbLoad(i,m)= ∑k GFBRUl(i,k) /(avgUeUlSE(i,T)* normalization_factor), where:

i: index of the UE with an GBR bearer/CB014699-D] critical GBR bearer [End CB014699-D].

k: index of the GBR bearer belong to UE i

T: slot of congestion is detected or resolved.

∑k GFBR Ul(i,k): derive from GFBR value received from AMF or source cell (in case of Handover) of the GBR bearers k for UE i belongs to LCG m with unit [Kbps]

avgUeUlSE(i, T): the latest value of avgUeUlSE(t) when congestion is detected or resolved.

normalization_factor: Normalization based on the total number of UL slot per second (see 5G_UP_54_replaced([11346921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIls_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TDD and 5G_UP_77_replaced([11347090](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz0_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for DSS).

Exceptions:

  * For gbrPuschCongHandling = FALSE or GBR congestion detection period NRCELL.congDetectPeriod = 0, gNB no need to perform this GBR Load calculation.



**Note: The PUSCH PRB load per GBR LCG calculation needs only be performed when GBR PUSCH congestion is detected or resolved OR when any cell GBR load changes that trigger additional UEs need to be de-prioritized/re-prioritized. It is left to entity level/implementation to define which exact procedure has the least complexity (e.g., triggered search, managed sorted lists, etc).**

---

### 3.30.6.2 FD Resource allocation

#### 3.30.6.2.0-1 FD Resource allocation Overview (ID: 11201746)

**3.30.6.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12698430`)

FD Resource allocation After calculating the amount of resources each UE in the to-be-scheduled list shall get, L2PS shall decide which FD resources shall be allocated to each UE

The method at which the resources are allocated for each PUSCH subarea (or initial BWP) is decided as follows:

· Random allocation ([11201759: 3.30.6.2.1 Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is used, if initial no MPR aware UE is present.

· MPR based allocation ([11201808: MPR Based FD resource allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZq8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is used if at least 1 MPR aware UE is present.

[PR840715]

If rdActPrbAllocOptForFr1CCA (see [12698005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qkqXM1yLEfCPFofqMEsORA)) is equal to true

[End PR840715]

If at least 1 FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as lower sub-band serving cell is included in a virtual UE, this virtual UE shall be treated as a FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as lower sub-band serving cell. If at least 1 FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell is included in a virtual UE, this virtual UE shall be treated as a FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell.

[PR840715]

else

If at least 1 FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as lower sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), is included in a virtual UE, this virtual UE shall be treated as a FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as lower sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). If at least 1 FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), is included in a virtual UE, this virtual UE shall be treated as a FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

endif

[End PR840715]

---

#### 3.30.6.2.1 Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs)

#### 3.30.6.2.1-1 Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs)

##### 3.30.6.2.1-1.0-4 Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs) Prior to executing the MPR aware PRB allocation algorithm, all UEs with 2x or 4x slot aggregation grants must be allocated PRBs in a subarea that is at either edge of the PUSCH that is part of the BWP that the UE is assigned to.  UEs with 2x or 4x slot aggregation allocations are not considered MPR aware UEs.  See 5G_UP_ALG_19289_replaced( 11205339 ) for details. A UE configured with FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA shall always be regarded as non-MPR aware UE. If at least 1 MPR aware UE is present in the set of UEs to be scheduled, L2-PS shall order the to-be-scheduled UEs in FD domain using the following steps: [PR840715] If rdActPrbAllocOptForFr1CCA (see 12698005 ) is equal to true [End PR840715]  List_1: gNB-DU shall create a list of MPR aware UEs and non-MPR aware UEs without FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA configured. If there is at least 1 MPR aware UE in one virtual UE, this virtual UE shall be treated as a MPR aware UE.  List_2: Create a list of UEs configured with FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA with the current cell as the upper sub-band serving cell by ascending order (small allocations in the top of the list).  List_3: Create a list of UEs configured with FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA with the current cell as the lower sub-band serving cell by descending order (large allocations in the top of the list). [PR840715] else  List_1: gNB-DU shall create a list of MPR aware UEs and non-MPR aware UEs without FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA configured and (FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UEs which transmission buffer in UL is not split between PCell and SCell (see 11122349 )). If there is at least 1 MPR aware UE in one virtual UE, this virtual UE shall be treated as a MPR aware UE.  List_2: Create a list of UEs configured with FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA with the current cell as the upper sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see 11122349 ), by ascending order (small allocations in the top of the list).  List_3: Create a list of UEs configured with FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA with the current cell as the lower sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see 11122349 ), by descending order (large allocations in the top of the list). endif [End PR840715]  Create the final list by adding List_2 to the top of List_1 (Note: UEs initially in List_2 remain in ascending order after adding).  Add List_3 to the bottom of the final list (Note: UEs initially in List_3 remain in descending order after adding).  gNB-DU shall allocate FD resource for the UEs in the list starting with the first UE.  For each PUSCH subarea (see 5G_UP_ALG_4879_replaced( 11196950 )) [PR840715] If rdActPrbAllocOptForFr1CCA (see 12698005 ) is equal to true [End PR840715] o gNB-DU shall swap the UEs among the UEs without FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA configured in the FD domain so that the MPR-aware UEs are in the center of the BWP, means in the UE’s inner RB allocation area (see 5G_UP_ALG_13119_replaced( 11201826 )). [PR840715] else o gNB-DU shall swap the UEs among the UEs without FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA configured and (FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UEs which transmission buffer in UL is not split between PCell and SCell (see 11122349 )) in the FD domain so that the MPR-aware UEs are in the center of the BWP, means in the UE’s inner RB allocation area (see 5G_UP_ALG_13119_replaced( 11201826 )). endif [End PR840715] Note: A UE is MPR aware if UE is power limited (has negative scaledPHR) &amp; rrmMaxNumPrbUl is at most half the number of PRBs in the BW. Note: PRBs are allocated from the UE on the top of the final list. (ID: 12699944)

#### 3.30.6.2.1-2 Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs)

##### 3.30.6.2.1-2.0-1 Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs) The start PRBs calculated as follows: For the first UE in the ordered list, calculate a random StartPRB is calculated based on 5G_UP_ALG_5041_replaced( 11201834 ) For the rest of the UEs, the temporary StartPRB is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to the previous UE in the same PUSCH subarea If the first UE in the ordered list is MPRaware and startPRb &lt; rbStartLow do as follows: If ReTx or fixed-size allocation (e.g. msg3 or msg5), steal Prbs if possible from initial Tx UEs on the list such that the condition "startPRb &gt;= rbStartLow" is satisfied. If it is possible, startPRB of the affected UEs shall be changed The allocated PRBs to initial Tx UEs  should not be less than the minimum allocatable resources. If the last UE in the ordered list is MPRaware and startPRb &gt; rbStartHigh do as follows: If ReTx or fixed-size allocation (e.g. msg3 or msg5), steal Prbs if possible from initial Tx UEs on the list such that the condition "startPRb &lt;= rbStartHigh" is satisfied. If it is possible, startPRB of the affected UEs shall be changed The allocated PRBs to initial Tx UEs  should not be less than the minimum allocatable resources. For all initial Tx MPRaware UEs in the list: If startPRb &lt; rbStartLow Reduce the allocation size, such that the condition "startPRb &gt;= rbStartLow" is satisfied startPrb is increased with the amount of Prbs reduced. If startPRb &gt; rbStartHigh: Reduce the allocation size, such that the condition "startPRb &lt;= rbStartHigh" is satisfied startPrb kept unchanged The allocated PRBs to initial Tx UEs should not be less than the minimum allocatable resources Note that rbStartLow and rbStartHigh are a UE specific value based on the allocated number of PRBs (see 5G_UP_ALG_5052_replaced( 11201911 )). (ID: 11201830)

##### 3.30.6.2.1.0-1 Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs) For UEs scheduled by a RAR (C[B,F]RA: RA-Msg3) or by a DCI Format 0_0 scrambled with a TC-RNTI (CBRA: RA-Msg3 reTx) (ID: 13442631)

**3.30.6.2.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13442632`)

Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs) [PR876217-26R3] ; To define the RA-Msg3 PUSCH allocation, the gNB computes first the location of the RA-Msg3 area in the cell bandwidth in order to be sure that the UE will transmit the PUSCH in its active bandwidth. As the UE active bandwidth is not always known by the gNB (see [11110853: Active BWP during the RA procedure](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-SvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), the U-Plane must use a common area between the initial BWP and all BWPs handling at least PRACH. In the U-Plane, there is only one PRACH configured in the cell bandwidth. Therefore, if the PRACH is starting within a BWP, then the BWP is configured with a PRACH in the gNB (for NSA call, the UE is never in the initial BWP and this initial BWP is not configured with the RACH in the UE).

The initial uplink BWP can be segmented in 3 parts separated by 2 set of 2 RBs used for the common PUCCH (ex common PUCCH config index 15). In this case even if the 3 PUSCH areas can be used for the RA-Msg3 (assuming the other BWP fully includes the initial uplink BWP), the gNB will use only the largest PUSCH area which is between the 2 sets of common PUCCH and within the intersection with all BWPs configured with a RACH in the gNB.

At the runtime when PUSCH and PRACH multiplexing is allowed, some RBs within the RA-Msg3 area may be reserved for the PRACH including if needed the 2 PUSCH guard RB.

As the TC-RNTI, scheduled by RAR UL grant or scheduled by DCI format 0_0, applies the offset between the CRB start of UE active BWP and the CRB start of the initial BWP on the decoded Frequency Domain Resource Assignment (FDRA), only if the initial uplink BWP is fully included in the UE active BWP configured with a RACH in the UE, the gNB must guarantee, when this condition is not fulfiled, that the UE active BWP (with a RACH configured) and the initial BWP are starting at the same location (because the UE active BWP is not known in the gNB before to receive the RA-Msg3 and the gNB does not know if the UE will apply the offset or not).

To determine the RA-Msg3 area, the gNB computes the RA-Msg3 boundaries as follows:

· The RB start of the RA-Msg3 area (raMsg3AreaStartRb), is the first PRB of the largest PUSCH area in the uplink BWP not overlapped by any static and dynamic dedicated PUCCH and common PUCCH resources or unavailable RBs (blanked) or by Configured Grant (CG) resources in the channel bandwidth. The C-Plane configures the first PUSCH PRB allowed for each cell BWPs and the U-Plane computes the PRB offset from the lowest boundary of the initial uplink BWP which is available in all BWPs configured with a RACH allowing to define the reference RB (virtual RB) for the RIV encoding.

For DSS cells (whatever the state of NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss), the RA-Msg3 area corresponds to the default area reserved for NR and allocated by the CRM. It corresponds to the PRACH area, which is the minimum set of RBs allocated to NR in all slots.

For non DSS cells, the RA-Msg3 area before to remove the CG resources is starting at the raMsg3AreaStatrRb defined hereafter in the cell bandwidth.

raMsg3AreaStartRb = max(rrmStartPuschPrbInitialBwp, rrmStartCrbCommonPucchLowPart - Initial UL BWP start + rrmNumPrbCommonPucch).

· The length of the RA-Msg3 area correspond to the largest contiguous RBs from the raMsg3AreaStartRb, not overlapped by the upper common PUCCH in the initial BWP and by the CG resources in the cell bandwidth, and which are available in all BWPs handling the PRACH and the initial BWP. [End PR876217-26R3]

In the RA-Msg3 area, the U-Plane starts the allocation of the RA-Msg3 from a random location ([11201840: Calculating a random startPrb for RA-Msg3](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZq-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) & [11201853: Timing of random startPrb calculation for RA-Msg3 initial Tx](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZq-_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

When the U-Plane allocates several initial transmission of RA-Msg3 or CBRA RA-Msg3 reTx, the allocations are contiguous from the first RB computed for RA-Msg3 until the end of the RA-Msg3 area and loopback to the beginning of the RA-Msg3 area. The initial RA-Msg3 location is determined when the RAR message is built and sent to to the UE several slots before the UL scheduling for the same target slot. The RA-Msg3 reTx are managed only k2 slots before the target slot and the RA-Msg3 allocations, scheduled by the RARs, are already known before the scheduling of the RA-Msg3 reTx.

---

##### 3.30.6.2.1.0-2 Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs) For UEs with initial BWP transmission (ID: 11201762)

**3.30.6.2.1.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `13459770`)

Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs) [Before PR876217-26R3] ; UE with initial BWP transmission (msg3 or **Msg5 (RRCReestablishmentComplete) or** **its reTx** when the UE active bandidth part is the initial uplink bandidth part or when the transmission is addressed to TC-RNTI - note that msg3 reTx is transmitted in the UE active bandwidth part) shall be allocated a number of PRBs as described in 5G_UP_ALG_3424_replaced([11152773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksh3mPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).****

For non-contention based handover (CFRA), location of msg3 **initial transmission** is located as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4973_replaced([11199451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))****

The location and bandwidth of the uplink initial Bandwidth part refer to 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2143_replaced([11308391](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrDvIvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2140_replaced([11308375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrDvHvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

In FDD cell shared with LTE (DSS) and when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false, L2-PS shall ensure that transmissions in initial BWP are scheduled in the guaranteed NR PRB range which includes **6 or** 7 contiguous PRBs comprising 6 PRBs starting from prachPrbOffset according 5G_UP_ALG_5180_replaced([11172453](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuMrgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).****

If long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch') **and (NRCELL.lteNrDssMode = 0 (no DSS) or NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true), the allowed PRB area where the Msg3 or its ReTx can be scheduled is defined by rrmStartPuschPrbInitialBwp and rrmNumPuschPrbInitialBwp in 5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced([11199486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOEw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**. Note, any blanked PRBs are already considered by rrmNumPuschPrbInitialBwp.

If long PUCCH is configured, then the initial transmission of Msg3 shall be allocated between:

 lower PRB = max(rrmStartPuschPrbInitialBwp, rrmStartCrbCommonPucchLowPart - Initial UL BWP start + rrmNumPrbCommonPucch) and

 higher PRB = min(rrmStartPuschPrbInitialBwp + rrmNumPuschPrbInitialBwp, rrmStartCrbCommonPucchHighPart - Initial UL BWP start)

with all parameters defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced([11199486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOEw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Both formulas above provide PRB indexes which may correspond different CRB indexes according to the BWP location considered.

If multiple UEs with Msg3(initial and ReTxn(CBRA)) **or Msg5 (initial and ReTxn) (RRCReestablishmentComplete)** are to be scheduled in the initial bandwidth part, allocate the FD resources to each UE such that:****

 Start PRB of first UE is determined by 5G_UP_ALG_5041_replaced([11201834](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZq-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (Random Start PRB) within the initial BWP.

 For Msg3 **or Msg5** Start PRB for the rest of the UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to the previous UE.****

 Retransmission UEs get the same amount of resources as their initial transmission.

 Initial transmission UEs get as many FD resources as determined in 5G_UP_ALG_3424_replaced([11152773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksh3mPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 The FD resources allocated to UEs are contiguous.[End PR876217-26R3]

[PR876217-26R3] ; Before the confirmation of the UE activate BWP by the reception of the RA-Msg3, the FD resources available are specified in the requirement: [13442631: For UEs scheduled by a RAR (C[B,F]RA: RA-Msg3) or by a DCI Format 0_0 scrambled with a TC-RNTI (CBRA: RA-Msg3 reTx)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3JCzkSYXEfGxldjOZQLezQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Upon the successful reception of the RA-Msg3 or while the new RA procedure is not detected with the same contention free preamble or while the U-Plane does not receive new indication from the C-Plane related to the target BWP after a RRC procedure, the UE active BWP is known in the U-Plane. When it is the initial BWP and even if this initial BWP is the BWP configured in the UE as a dedicated BWP, only the RRC BWP switch is possible.

For DCI Format 0_0 and 0_1, the reference is the first RB of the initial BWP configured by the C-Plane and all RBs not used by the common and dedicated long PUCCH or reserved by the PRACH or by CG PUSCH transmission(s) in dedicated BWP overlapping the initial one.

For DSS cells, the PUSCH area in the initial BWP can be impacted by the CRM resources allocation to NR.

When the common PUCCH index is set to 15 (long PUCCH), the PUSCH is split in 3 areas by two common PUCCH areas, each PUSCH area can be used for regular PUSCH allocation. For other PUCCH configuration indexes (long PUCCH), the 2 smallest PUSCH areas if present and delimited by the boundaries of the initial BWP and the common PUCCH areas may not always allow the minimum PUSCH RB allocation. 

When the common PUCCH are not used (k1 > k2 : UL scheduler always knows the common PUCCH resources before to allocate the PUSCH resources in a target slot) and not reserved for other purpose, the PUSCH allocation can allocate theses common PUCCH areas. [End PR876217-26R3].

---

##### 3.30.6.2.1.0-3 Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs) For UEs with dedicated BWP transmission (ID: 11201776)

**3.30.6.2.1.0-3.0-9**  (ID: `13459757`)

Random FD Resource allocation (Randomly ordered UEs) [Before PR876217-26R3] ; Msg3 (CFRA or CBRA(NSA cell only)) reTx are handled in the dedicated uplink bandwidth part if the active uplink bandwidth part is not the initial uplink bandwidth part. For CBRA (SA or SA+NSA cells), the UE active BWP is known when the RA-Msg3 is decoded, therefore the RA-Msg3 (Tx and reTx) is done in the initial BWP). However, from scheduling subarea perspective, to be noted that Msg3 CBRA is scheduled in the PRB area that intersect the bandwidth of the initial UL BWP i.e. it shall be in the scope of 5G_UP_ALG_5030_replaced([11201762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End PR876217-26R3]

[PR876217-26R3] ; For the UE scheduled by a RAR (C[B,F]RA: RA-Msg3) or by a DCI Format 0_0 scrambled with a TC-RNTI (CBRA: RA-Msg3 reTx), the PUSCH resources allocation is specific even if in some cases the UE is transmitting the associated PUSCH in the UE active and dedicated BWP (see [13442631: For UEs scheduled by a RAR (C[B,F]RA: RA-Msg3) or by a DCI Format 0_0 scrambled with a TC-RNTI (CBRA: RA-Msg3 reTx)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3JCzkSYXEfGxldjOZQLezQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). For the other PUSCH allocations with a DCI Format 0_1 or 0_0 scrambled with the C-RNTI using the dedicated BWP, the randomization procedure is detailed hearafter. [End PR876217-26R3]

Prior to executing the random PRB allocation algorithm, all UEs with 2x or 4x slot aggregation grants must be allocated PRBs in a subarea that is at either edge of the PUSCH that is part of the BWP that the UE is assigned to. See 5G_UP_ALG_19289_replaced([11205339](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwo7gvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. 

For all PUSCH subareas (see 5G_UP_ALG_4879_replaced([11196950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGwCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), starting from the smallest to the largest BWP, allocate FD resources to UEs in each PUSCH subarea within the considered BWP in the following order into 3 UE lists:

If rdActPrbAllocOptForFr1CCA (see [12698005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qkqXM1yLEfCPFofqMEsORA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is equal to true

1\. Create a list of FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UEs with the current cell as the upper sub-band serving cell by allocation size in an ascending order.

2\. Create a list of UEs without FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA configuration in random order.

3\. Create a list of FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UEs with the current cell as the lower sub-band serving cell by allocation size in a descending order.

else

1\. Create a list of FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UEs with the current cell as the upper sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), by allocation size in an ascending order.

2\. Create a list of UEs without FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA configuration and (FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UEs which transmission buffer in UL is not split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))) in random order.

3\. Create a list of FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UEs with the current cell as the lower sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), by allocation size in a descending order.

end if

  * Start PRB of first UE in each list in a PUSCH subarea is determined by 5G_UP_ALG_5041_replaced([11201834](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZq-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) within the considered BWP.

  * **In FDD cell shared with LTE (DSS) and when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false, a UE gets resources starting with the first free resource after the last resource dedicated to an initial BWP transmission.**

  * For each UE list, start PRB for the rest of the UEs is the resource after the last FD resource allocated to the previous UE in the same PUSCH subarea

  * Retransmission UEs get the same amount of resources as their initial transmission **if the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) or the conditions defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are not fulfilled. If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, the amount of resources needed for the retransmission follows the rules defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11970_replaced([11202476](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

  * Initial transmission UEs get as many FD resources as determined in 5G_UP_ALG_4994_replaced([11199854](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

  * The FD resources allocated to UEs are contiguous.

  * Transient phase UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_4816_replaced([11194889](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4GhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are scheduled



when NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to false

**If (NRCELL.cellTechnology == FDD) AND**

(UE’s Dedicated BWP Size == Initial BWP Size) AND

(NRBTS.actTransPhaseUlPrbNonRandom == False) then // See Note

Determine the random PRB location using the algorithm for UEs in a dedicated BWP (5G_UP_ALG_5041_replaced([11201834](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZq-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Else

StartPrb= PRB corresponding to the lowest available PRB in the dedicated BWP and the rest of the UEs are scheduled in the part above it.

Endif

**Otherwise, (When NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to true)**

Determine the random PRB location using the algorithm for UEs in a dedicated BWP

Note:

When a transient phase UE is scheduled in the uplink, DCI format 0_0 is used. If the UE’s dedicated BWP size is not equal to the initial BWP size, the RIV field in DCI 0_0 is calculated differently (3GPP 38.214 section 6.1.2.2.2). This calculation is not yet supported by the gNB and is outside of the scope of this PR. As such the equal BWP size constraint was added as a condition to randomize the transient phase UE’s PRB location. Additionally the issue addressed by the PR was not as significant in TDD. To simplify the solution it was determined that an FDD only solution was sufficient. In the future these constraints may be removed via a feature or CNI.

---

#### 3.30.6.2.2 Start PRB randomization for PUSCH

##### 3.30.6.2.2.0-1 Start PRB randomization for PUSCH Calculating a random startPrb for msg3 (ID: 11201840)

**3.30.6.2.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13420460`)

Start PRB randomization for PUSCH 13420460: Start PRB randomization for PUSCH [Before PR876217-26R3] ; For every msg3 initial transmission or retransmission, a random startPrb shall be generated relative to the PRB0 of the bandwidth used for the MSG3 transmission, as follows:

 Msg3 initial transmission (CFRA or CBRA) is transmitted into the initial uplink bandwidth part allocation area (according to 8.3 in TS 38213). Consequently, the generated startPrb (as requested with 5G_UP_ALG_5044_replaced([11201853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZq-_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be relative to the PRB0 of the initial uplink bandwidth part and within the initial uplink bandwidth part allocation area (when the initial uplink bandwidth part shall not anymore be fully contained in the UE active UL bandwidth part, this requirement shall be revisited).

 Msg3 retransmission (CFRA or CBRA) is transmitted into the UE active UL bandwidth part (according to 6.1.2.2 in TS 38214 and 7.3.1.0 in TS 38212). Though, PRB allocation area in case of CBRA (i.e. DCI addressed to TC-RNTI) is restricted to the PRB region that intersect the Initial UL BWP allocation area while PRB allocation area in case of CFRA (i.e. DCI addressed to C-RNTI) shall be allocated in the first N PRBs of the the Active BWP, where N is the RB size of the Initial BWP. Consequently, the generated startPrb shall be relative to the PRB0 of the UE active bandwidth part. A new startPrb is generated before each msg3 retransmission by directly applying the procedure specified in the requirement 5G_UP_ALG_5046_replaced([11201864](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZq_fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(FR2) or 5G_UP_ALG_5049_replaced([11201873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwaSAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(FR1).

 Note1: the generated startPrb ‘s scope is only for the current msg3 retransmission, i.e. it shall not overwrite the startPrb value generate for the initial msg3 transmission scheduled from RAR in the msg2.

 Note2: in theory, L2-PS should make sure that the RIV of the FDRA field in the DCI 0_0 computed with [image-0][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15939.001.png[/image_path][/image-0], N and Msg3 PUSCH allocation size doesn’t exceed 2^N-1, where N is the RB size of the initial BWP. However, this case is not possible with the MSG3 size allocation given by 5G_UP_ALG_3424_replaced([11152773](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksh3mPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the check could be skipped [End PR876217-26R3]

[PR876217-26R3] ; For every RA-Msg3 initial transmission or retransmission and non DSS cells, a random start RB shall be generated according to the RNTI used:

· For UEs scheduled by a RAR (C[B,F]RA: RA-Msg3 initial Tx) with TC-RNTI, the U-Plane generates a random start CRB within in the RA-Msg3 area (see [13442631: For UEs scheduled by a RAR (C[B,F]RA: RA-Msg3) or by a DCI Format 0_0 scrambled with a TC-RNTI (CBRA: RA-Msg3 reTx)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3JCzkSYXEfGxldjOZQLezQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). Then the U-Plane computes the RB offset between start CRB of the initial BWP and the random start CRB to encode the Resource Value Index (RIV).

When the initial BWP is not fully included in a BWP configured with the PRACH, the initial BWP and this BWP must have the same CRB start, because the gNB does not know yet the UE and if the UE will apply or not an offset after the FDRA decoding.

· For the RA-Msg3 retransmission(s) scheduled by a TC-RNTI (CBRA), the U-Plane applies the same rule as for the initial transmissions scheduled by the RAR (see above).

· For the RA-Msg3 retransmission(s) scheduled by a C-RNTI (CFRA ; the RA procedure is completed in the UE at the RA-Msg2 reception), the U-Plane generates a random start RB within the UE active BWP compatible with the DCI Format 0_0 FDRA size which depends on the number of RBs in the initial uplink BWP. The start RB must not overlap the dedicated and common PUCCH, the RB reserved for other purpose (RB blanked, CG resources...) in the UE active BWP. The sum of the start RB and the number of RBs allocated must not be larger than number of RBs of the initial BWP to encode the RIV.

For DSS cells, the RA-Msg3 area is restricted to the PRACH region and its start RB is not randomized and correspond to the first RB of this region. Nevertheless, for the RAR (C[B,F]RA) or a DCI Format 0_0 scrambled by the TC-RNTI (CBRA), the RIV encoding rule for the start RB corresponding to RB offset between the start CRB of the initial BWP and the first CRB of the RA-Msg3 allocation is used. For the CFRA RA-Msg3 reTx, the reference is teh UE active BWP for the start RB. [End PR876217-26R3]

---

##### 3.30.6.2.2.0-2 Start PRB randomization for PUSCH Timing of random startPrb calculation for initial Tx msg3 (ID: 11201853)

**3.30.6.2.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11201857`)

Start PRB randomization for PUSCH For msg3 initial transmission, a random startPrb shall be generated (using the randomization function in the below requirement) before any msg2/msg3 scheduling occurs.

Whenever L2PS schedules msg2 in DL, this random startPrb shall be used for the frequency domain resource assignment of msg3.

When the msg3 is scheduled in UL, a new random startPrb is generated to be used later at the next msg2/msg3 scheduling instant.

This random startPrb shall also be regenerated if the initial BWP is changed.

---

##### 3.30.6.2.2.0-3 Start PRB randomization for PUSCH Calculating a random startPrb in FR2 (ID: 11201864)

**3.30.6.2.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11201868`)

Start PRB randomization for PUSCH For FR2 Only

The start PRB for a PUSCH transmission for a UE shall be set to one of ( roundup(rrmRemainingPrb div stepSize) + 1 ) values between:

\- rrmFirstAvailablePrb: The first available non-blanked PRB &

\- rrmFirstAvailablePrb + rrmRemainingPrb: rrmRemainingPrb is the difference between the available non-blanked PRBs used for PUSCH in the active BWP used by the UE and the number of PRBs to be scheduled in the same BWP in the slot (by 1 or more UEs)

Based on the parameter rdUlPrbRandomizationEnable (see 5G_L2_1323_replaced([11183012](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27gUA_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) the value of startPrb can be calculated as follows:

a) If Randomization is disabled, startPrb shall be set to rrmFirstAvailablePrb.

b) If Randomization is enabled (not cell-specific)

If rrmRemainingPrb = 0:

startPrb= rrmFirstAvailablePrb

Else:

startprb = rrmFirstAvailablePrb + randomStep * stepSize

Where

  * stepSize = 4
  * randomStep = ( ( rrmScheduledXSfn +1 + pciSmall ) * primeLarge ) mod ( roundup(rrmRemainingPrb div stepSize) + 1 ) rrmScheduledXSfn is the extended SFN as defined in 5G_L2_233_replaced([11148538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24RZ2fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * primeLarge = 997 (A prime number larger than rrmRemainingPrb)



pciSmall = (physCellId mod 24) + 1  


c) If cell-specific randomization is enabled

o PUSCH BW is divided into X sections:

 X = 5, for BW with 80 PRBs or more

 X = 3, for BW with less than 80 PRBs

o Section used is determined by: usedSection = (physCellId + 7) mod X

o PUSCH allocation is centered in the chosen section

o If number of needed PRBs are more than number of PRBs in a section

 startprb = rrmFirstAvailablePrb, if physCellId mod 2 = 0

 startprb = rrmFirstAvailablePrb + rrmRemainingPrb – needed PRBs +1, if physCellId mod 2 = 1

---

##### 3.30.6.2.2.0-4 Start PRB randomization for PUSCH Calculating a random startPrb in FR1 (ID: 11201873)

**3.30.6.2.2.0-4.0-7**  (ID: `12700273`)

Start PRB randomization for PUSCH For FR1 Only

The start PRB for a PUSCH transmission for a UE shall be set to one of **( roundup(rrmRemainingPrb div stepSize) + 1 ) values between rrmFirstAvailablePrb & rrmFirstAvailablePrb + rrmRemainingPrb**

This startPrb randomization function shall be used generically for each BWP in each subarea (see 5G_UP_ALG_4879_replaced([11196950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGwCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) such that:

 rrmFirstAvailablePrb is the first available PRB in the PUSCH subarea where randomization should occur.

 rrmRemainingPrb is the number of PRBs not allocated to any UE in the PUSCH subarea where randomization should occur (difference between size of PUSCH subarea and PRBs allocated to all UEs in it

 If a transient phase UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_4816_replaced([11194889](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4GhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is scheduled in the slot AND the transient phase UE PRBs will not be randomized (see 5G_UP_ALG_5035_replaced([11201776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then

The values rrmFirstAvailablePrb and rrmRemainingPrb are adapted based on the size of allocation of the transient phase UE (PRBs 0 to size of allocation (currently size of initial BWP) are not in the available range).

Else // Transient phase UE’s PRB allocation will be randomized.

No change to rrmFirstAvailablePrb and rrmRemainingPrb. 

Endif

 For random FD resource allocation (see 5G_UP_ALG_5035_replaced([11201776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE has impact to startPrb, detail is shown as below.

Based on the parameter rdUlPrbRandomizationEnable (see 5G_L2_1323_replaced([11183012](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27gUA_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) the value of startPrb can be calculated as follows:

a) If Randomization is disabled

**If at least 1 MPR aware UE is present in a PUSCH subarea, startPrb shall be set to rrmFirstAvailablePrb** in the PUSCH subarea, otherwise in the PUSCH subarea, the calculation is same as Randomization enabled except randomStep which is always equal to 0.****

b) If Randomization is enabled (not cell-specific) or If cell-specific randomization is enabled and NRBTS.actFdmScheduling is set to TRUE

If n = 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

If there is an MPR aware UE in the PUSCH subarea, then

startprb = rrmFirstAvailablePrb + min(randomStep * stepSize, rrmRemaingPrb)

Else

[PR840715]

If rdActPrbAllocOptForFr1CCA (see [12698005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qkqXM1yLEfCPFofqMEsORA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is equal to true

[End PR840715]

If there is FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell in the PUSCH subarea, then

StartPrbIntraBandULCAUpperSub is set to rrmFirstAvailablePrb.

Increase rrmFirstAvailablePrb by the total allocation size of FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UEs with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell in the PUSCH subarea. 

Endif

[PR840715]

else

If there is FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), in the PUSCH subarea, then

StartPrbIntraBandULCAUpperSub is set to rrmFirstAvailablePrb.

Increase rrmFirstAvailablePrb by the total allocation size of FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UEs with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), in the PUSCH subarea. 

Endif

Endif

[End PR840715]

If there is UE with randomized PRB allocation in the PUSCH subarea, then

startprb = rrmFirstAvailablePrb + min(randomStep * stepSize, rrmRemaingPrb)

rrmFirstAvailablePrb is set to startPrb + the total allocation size of UEs with randomized PRB allocation in the PUSCH subarea;

Decrease rrmRemainingPrb by min(randomStep * stepSize, rrmRemaingPrb);

Endif

[PR840715]

If rdActPrbAllocOptForFr1CCA (see [12698005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qkqXM1yLEfCPFofqMEsORA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is equal to true

[End PR840715]

If there is FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as lower sub-band serving cell in the PUSCH subarea, then

StartPrbIntraBandULCALowerSub is set to rrmFirstAvailablePrb + rrmRemainingPrb;

Endif

[PR840715]

else

If there is FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as lower sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), in the PUSCH subarea, then

StartPrbIntraBandULCALowerSub is set to rrmFirstAvailablePrb + rrmRemainingPrb;

Endif

Endif

[End PR840715]

Endif

Where

[PR840715]

If rdActPrbAllocOptForFr1CCA (see [12698005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qkqXM1yLEfCPFofqMEsORA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is equal to true

[End PR840715]

 StartPrbIntraBandULCAUpperSub is the start PRB for FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell in the PUSCH subarea.

 StartPrbIntraBandULCALowerSub is the start PRB for FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as lower sub-band serving cell in the PUSCH subarea.

[PR840715]

else

 StartPrbIntraBandULCAUpperSub is the start PRB for FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), in the PUSCH subarea.

 StartPrbIntraBandULCALowerSub is the start PRB for FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as lower sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), in the PUSCH subarea.

Endif

[End PR840715]

If n > 1, the number of dedicated BWP per cell (5G_UP_ALG_8240_replaced([11110833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

For each PUSCH subarea j in a slot

**If PUSCH subarea j is divided by PUSCH slot aggregation UEs into smaller area, apply the following algorithm for each such smaller area.**

rrmRemainingPrb_i = free PRBs in bwp_i in PUSCH subarea j

For each bwp_i in { n..0 } in increasing size: // Dedicated BWPs are numbered small to large, i.e. 1 is the large BWP, (5G_UP_ALG_1811_replaced([11110797](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpHXP_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 0 is the initial BWP. Note the initial BWP may have same size as small or large BWP, but also another size

If there is a UE in bwp_i

rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i = lowest PRB above PUCCH of bwp_i (considering the outcome of previous iterations)

rrmRemainingPrb = min( rrmRemainingPrb_i, and rrmRemainingPrb not yet considered for PUSCH subarea j )

If a transient phase UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_4816_replaced([11194889](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4GhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is scheduled in the slot **AND the transient phase UE PRBs is randomized**(see 5G_UP_ALG_5035_replaced([11201776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **then** ****

If (NRCELL.cellTechnology == FDD) AND

(Size of UE’s Dedicated BWP == Initial BWP Size) AND

(NRBTS.actTransPhaseUlPrbNonRandom == False), then

transient phase UE PRB allocation will be randomized according to the algorithm for dedicated BWP UE.

There is no change to rrmFirstAvailablePrb and rrmRemainingPrb

Else if (NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom == False)

adapt the values rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i , rrmRemainingPrb based on the size of allocation of the transient phase UE (PRBs 0 to size of allocation (currently size of initial BWP) are not in the available range), i.e. rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i = max(rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i, PRB allocation of transient phase UE), rrmRemainingPrb = max(0, rrmRemainingPrb – PRB allocation of transient phase UE)

Else if (NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom == True)

transient phase UE PRB allocation will be randomized according to the algorithm for dedicated BWP UE.

There is no change to rrmFirstAvailablePrb and rrmRemainingPrb.

Endif 

Endif

If there is an MPR aware UE in bwp_i and the PUSCH subarea, then adjust rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i and rrmRemainingPrb_i according 5G_UP_ALG_5052_replaced([11201911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwaSB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

prbShift = min(randomStep * stepSize, rrmRemaingPrb)

startPrb_i = rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i + prbShift

Decrease rrmRemainingPrb of those BWPs not yet considered for PUSCH subarea j.

Else

[PR840715]

If rdActPrbAllocOptForFr1CCA (see [12698005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qkqXM1yLEfCPFofqMEsORA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is equal to true

[End PR840715]

If there is FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell in bwp_i and the PUSCH subarea, then

StartPrbIntraBandULCAUpperSub_i is set to rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i.

Increase rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i by the total allocation size of FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UEs with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell in bwp_i and the PUSCH subarea.

Endif

[PR840715]

else

If there is FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as upper sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), in bwp_i and the PUSCH subarea, then

StartPrbIntraBandULCAUpperSub_i is set to rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i.

Endif

Endif

[End PR840715]

If there is UE with randomized PRB allocation in bwp_i and the PUSCH subarea, then

prbShift = min(randomStep * stepSize, rrmRemaingPrb) 

startPrb_i = rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i + prbShift

Decrease rrmRemainingPrb of those BWPs not yet considered for PUSCH subarea j.

rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i is set to startPrb_i + the total allocation size of UEs with randomized PRB allocation in bwp_i and the PUSCH subarea.

Endif

[PR840715]

If rdActPrbAllocOptForFr1CCA (see [12698005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qkqXM1yLEfCPFofqMEsORA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is equal to true

[End PR840715]

If there is FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as lower sub-band serving cell in bwp_i and the PUSCH subarea, then

StartPrbIntraBandULCALowerSub_i is set to rrmFirstAvailablePrb_i + min(rrmRemainingPrb_i, rrmRemainingPrb).

Endif

[PR840715]

else

If there is FR1 intra-band contiguous UL CA UE with current cell as lower sub-band serving cell, which transmission buffer in UL is split between PCell and SCell (see [11122349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqfQNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), in bwp_i and the PUSCH subarea, then

Endif

Endif

[End PR840715]
