#### 3.30.7.0-6 Aperiodic SRS/CSI report transmission request A-CSI report multiplexing with UE data (ID: 11202276)

**3.30.7.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11202281`)

Aperiodic SRS/CSI report transmission request Whenever a UE is scheduled for UL data (initial transmission, non Msg3 or Msg5), L2-PS shall request an aperiodic CSI report on PUSCH from L1 UL if the conditions mentioned in 5G_UP_ALG_3506_replaced([11159352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7mPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are satisfied.

The CSI report shall be multiplexed with the UL data transmission at the n+K2/k2aCsi UL slot. Only one CSI report is to be multiplexed with UL data.

Delay UlData_PuschReceiveReq for A-CSI UEs scheduled with N+k2aCsi until it could be sent combined with the UlData_PuschReceiveReq generated after scheduling at time N’ with k2, such that N+k2aCsi = N’+k2

The DCI field "CSI request" shall be updated to contain the triggering state ID (see 5G_UP_ALG_3532_replaced([11159474](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktKJsfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and, in case that UE is supporting dynamic beta offset, IE "Beta offset Indicator" shall be updated with the selected beta offset set index (see 5G_UP_ALG_3561_replaced([11159600](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktKwxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If A-CSI is requested in UCI only mode, the DCI field "UL SCH" the UL DCI 0_1 (5G_UP_ALG_4937_replaced([11171437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuEIqvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be set to 0.

Mark the UE as not eligible for A-CSI. Eligibility is renewed when the UE shall receive a new CSI-RS in Active time (DRX active and not in Measurement gap), see 5G_UP_ALG_3509_replaced([11159381](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7nfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In case that UE was scheduled with PUSCH timing k2aCsi and if long PUCCH is activated UE is added to a list that, when the scheduler is completed, shall be sent to DL scheduler in order to consider it for dynamic k1 determination (in case that UE is scheduled in DL direction in the interval [N, N + k2aCsi - k1min], k1 should be computed such that Harq-Ack falls after N + k2aCsi or to be excluded from DL scheduling otherwise - see 5G_UP_ALG_2502_replaced([11116669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp6BY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_3503_replaced([11159340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) .

---

#### 3.30.7.0-7 Aperiodic SRS/CSI report transmission request SLIV for FDD PCell or SCell in FR1 UL CA overlap with TDD P-SRS (ID: 11202287)

**3.30.7.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `12656673`)

Aperiodic SRS/CSI report transmission request 12656673: Aperiodic SRS/CSI report transmission request **For FDD PCell or FDD SCell in FR1 UL CA overlap with TDD P-SRS:**

If long PUCCH is configured (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect is 'longPucch'), N=14, y is given as below:

If NRCELL.puschMappingType == 'TypeB' and NRCELL.actHighSpeedCell == false

y=2

Else

y=1

End

The supported SLIVs are:

· When PUSCH is not scheduled with aSRS and is not overlapping with TDD P-SRS: SLIV 27 (S=0, L=14)

If FDD and TDD are not in critical band combination AND (either condition 1 or 2 is fullfilled):

· When PUSCH is overlapping with TDD P-SRS in symbol #13 and y=1: SLIV 41 (S=0, L=13)

· When PUSCH is overlapping with TDD P-SRS in symbol #13 and y=2: SLIV 55 (S=0, L=12)

· When PUSCH is overlapping with TDD P-SRS in symbol #12 and (y=1 or y=2): SLIV 55 (S=0, L=12)

If FDD and TDD are in critical band combination and has impact on uplink, e.g. txSwitchWithAnotherBand (refer to 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_11305_replaced([11307687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tq-2kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

· When PUSCH is overlapping with TDD P-SRS in symbol #13 and (y=1 or y=2): SLIV 55 (S=0, L=12)

~~·~~~~When PUSCH is overlapping with TDD P-SRS in symbol #12 and (y=1 or y=2): SLIV 69 (S=0, L=11)~~

If FDD PUSCH is overlapping with more than one TDD P-SRS in a slot, FDD PUSCH shall be shorten in worst case considering above requirement. 

Condition 1: [image-0][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15995.001.png[/image_path][/image-0](refer to 5G_UP_ALG_10873_replaced\(\[11119621\]\(https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ\)\)) is less than [image-1][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15995.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]which [image-2][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_15995.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]refer to 5G_UP_ALG_10873_replaced([11119621](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for FDD PCell in case of FDD+TDD CA or for FDD SCell in case of TDD+FDD CA.

Condition 2: UE does not support parallelTxSRS-PUCCH-PUSCH.

Note:

  1. If TDD serving cell is SCell, then it shall be in SCell active status so that P-SRS is sent by UE, otherwise P-SRS is not sent and there is no need to shorten FDD PUSCH.



[CB010439-A] 2. FDD cell can be DSS cell. [End CB010439-A]

---

## 3.30.8 UL HARQ handling

### 3.30.8.1 Basic requirements and multiplicities

#### 3.30.8.1.0-1 Basic requirements and multiplicities There shall be one UL HARQ handler per configured serving subcell per UE context created in the UL scheduler. The UL HARQ handler shall support adaptive asynchronous HARQ processing. Each UL HARQ handler shall support the handling of up to 16 HARQ processes per UE context created for each subcell the UE can support until the UE context is terminated. The UL HARQ entity shall support bookkeeping of the free, allocated HARQ processes with first transmissions and the HARQ processes with pending retransmissions for all HARQ processes of all UEs and all subcells. There is one TB allocated per HARQ process. (ID: 11202316)

### 3.30.8.2 UL HARQ processing

#### 3.30.8.2.0-1 UL HARQ processing UL HARQ Initial Transmission (ID: 11202326)

**3.30.8.2.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `13439339`)

UL HARQ processing For each scheduled PUSCH initial transmission, the corresponding HARQ handling shall be carried out as below:

For a PUSCH initial transmission corresponding to a configured grant occasion, instead of current requirement, 5G_UP_ALG_14922_replaced([11202350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is executed

\- The transport block size is calculated as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_5084_replaced([11203344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

\- In case of RA Msg3 it is specified in 5G_UP_ALG_5096_replaced([11203473](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

\- The UL scheduler selects the next (in the sense of increasing HARQ process ID) available free HARQ process of the UE for the transmission of the TB (that’s recommended in order to make the check in the TTI trace easier).

  * In case that the UE has a valid configured grant (CG) configuration and its CG state machine is started and it’s not in one of the states CG-Init or CG-Error, selection of the HARQ process ID shall be performed from the range [ nrofHARQ-Processes (see 5G_UP_ALG_14592_replaced([11172878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for nrofHARQ-Processes) … MAX (it shall be same maximum value as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5071_replaced([11202310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcHP_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))].



Note that it’s expected that the value computed for nrofHARQ-Processes is sufficiently low to avoid running into HARQ stall. However, if it occurs, such case shall be logged, for further investigation.

\- In case of RA-Msg3 HARQ Process 0 is always used.

\- The UL scheduler will toggle the NDI value in the DCI of this xPUSCH transmission with the following exception:

\- Do not toggle NDI value if all of the following are true:

\- UL-SCH indicator field in DCI 0_1 is set to 0

\- non-zero CSI request field in DCI 0_1 (i.e. A-CSI request)

\- The UL Scheduler shall store for the selected HARQ process:

\- the MCS recommended by the link adaptation (rrmUlProposedMcs) and the one finally used (rrmUlUsedMcs), which can be different in case of MCS downgrade

\- the time stamp of the initial transmission (rrmInitUlTxTime);

\- the TBS (tbSize);

\- the number of PRBs;

\- On an FDD carrier shared with LTE and NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssFastAlloc=true, after receiving the EN DSS UL SUBCARRIER RESOURCE ALLOCATION REQUEST message from CRM and during the DSS UL allocation transition phase, UL scheduler shall determine the maximum number of PRBs can be used as below:

  * In case the new resource allocation is larger than the current allocation



\- The number of PRB of current resource allocation shall be used for the target UL slots that are before the end of the DSS UL allocation transition phase.

\- The number of new resource allocation shall be used for the target UL slots that are found beyond the Activation Time, i.e after the end of transition phase.

  * In case the new resource allocation has less resources than the current allocation, the number of PRB in new resource allocation shall be used for all the target UL slots starting from the beginning of the DSS UL allocation transition phase

  * If NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true and if PUSCH in NR dedicated part is larger than calculated minimum of the new and current resource allocation in the LTE shared area [CB007372-E] available for given BWP [End CB007372-E] then the maximum number of PRBs [CB007372-E] in given BWP [End CB007372-E] is limited to the number of PUSCH PRBs in NR dedicated part. 

  * If slow pooling of instantaneous throughput is enabled i.e. tddInstTputPoolingAllowed is TRUE (refer 5G_RAN_OP_3986_replaced([11693605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D33F-P9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), for every initial transmission, gNB-DU shall add the TBS(tbSize) value to rrmUsedCellThroughputPerSlot (refer 5G_UP_ALG_29086_replaced([11215102](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to keep track of the cell's throughput per slot. Before every PUSCH initial transmission, the scheduler shall also ensure that the throughput limit is not exceeded as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29088_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case that scheduling the current UE increases the throughput beyond the limit, the scheduler shall take necessary actions as described in 5G_UP_ALG_29088_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))




Notes:

  * Per 3GPP: a UE ignores all fields in DCI except the CSI request when CSI request is non zero (A-CSI request) and UL-SCH indicator field in DCI 0_1 is set to 0. If NDI were toggled in this case, then a subsequent toggle of NDI on same HARQ Process would be interpreted by UE as a retransmission instead of an initial transmission. Prevention by this exception of NDI toggle for this A-CSI grant would then allow UE to correctly interpret a subsequent NDI toggle.

---

#### 3.30.8.2.0-2 UL HARQ processing UL HARQ Initial Transmission for Configured Grant (CG) (ID: 11202350)

**3.30.8.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11202359`)

UL HARQ processing For each scheduled CG PUSCH initial transmission (activation, modification or periodic) and until the reception of a PsUser_UserStopReq sent by the C-Plane the corresponding HARQ handling shall be carried out as below:

 The transport block size is calculated as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_5084_replaced([11203344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Once computed, it’s stored together with the number of PRBs, MCS as all Link Adaptation parameters (layer, CDM group, ....) defined at the CG activation or modification, the timestamp, and used for each CG occasion until the CG is deactivated.

[CB013199-B]

  * If slow pooling of instantaneous throughput is enabled i.e. tddInstTputPoolingAllowed is TRUE (refer 5G_RAN_OP_3986_replaced([11693605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D33F-P9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), for every CG PUSCH initial transmission, gNB-DU shall add the TBS(tbSize) value to rrmUsedCellThroughputPerSlot (refer 5G_UP_ALG_29086_replaced([11215102](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to keep track of the cell's throughput per slot. Before every PUSCH initial transmission, the scheduler shall also ensure that the throughput limit is not exceeded as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29088_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case that scheduling the current UE increases the throughput beyond the limit, the scheduler shall take necessary actions as described in 5G_UP_ALG_29088_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))



[End CB013199-B]

 The UL scheduler calculates the HARQ Process ID per CG occasion in (SFN, slot) as follows:

HARQ Process ID = [floor(CURRENT_CG_slot/periodicity(in slots))] modulo nrofHARQ-Processes (see 5G_UP_ALG_14592_replaced([11172878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for nrofHARQ-Processes and 5G_UP_5893_replaced([11348488](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubWBFPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) fo periodicity), where CURRENT_CG_slot = (SFN × 10 * 2^u + slot).

Note: when the SFN cycle wraps up and SFN restarts from 0, the continuity of the HPN allocation up to (nrofHARQ-Processes - 1) is interrupted and, in result, for few CG occasions, CG could be blocked due to the fact that configuredGrantTimer is still running for the calculated HPN. Consequently, DG overwriting or additional DG to compensate the missing CG could be observed. It's the preffered solution, instead of increasing the number of HARQ processes for CG just for this short period.

---

#### 3.30.8.2.0-3 UL HARQ processing UL HARQ Retransmission and release (ID: 11202363)

**3.30.8.2.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `12715229`)

UL HARQ processing A retransmission shall be scheduled in a subcell if either CRC was negative or DTX has been received from 5G-L1-UL for the scheduled HARQ process in the subcell and reported to 5G-L2-PS. If CRC has been positive the HARQ process is freed. If a retransmission has to be scheduled for the assigned UE the UL scheduler will inform 5G-L1-UL about the scheduled retransmission and the related HARQ process id. UL scheduler will keep the NDI value in the DCI sent for the retransmission.

If UE is configured for skippable grants (5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced ([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), any PUSCH DTX indication associated with any of the following shall be ignored and not be a trigger for a HARQ retransmission:

  * Skippable UL Proactive Grant.
  * TA Grant.
  * Fake SR grant (DL RLC Status PDU grant)



[Before PR840689]

  * · UL grant after a zero BSR was received.



Note: This is for case when UL grant is sent just prior to total buffered bytes = 0 due to delay in getting buffer status from L2-LO. The grant is sent but subsequent DTX due to grant being skipped by UE is ignored.

[PR840689]

  * Non-Proactive UL grant and subsequent zero BSR
  * If a zero BSR is identified at any point after a HARQ process has been started and a DTX is detected for any of the process transmissions: assume that the entire HARQ process is being skipped and release the process.
  * This is even if a subsequent non-zero BSR is identified during the process.
  * Note: This prevents the HARQ process from completing unnecessarily. The HARQ process could be started prior zero buffer recognition due to delay in getting buffer status from L2-LO.



[End PR840689]

Any HARQ process without any expected retransmission must be released (max HARQ retransmissions or retransmission not allowed as described above).

If the maximum number of HARQ transmissions (acc. NRBTS/NRCELLGRP.maxHarqMsg3Tx for Msg3 and UE specific maxUlHarqTxNum value per 5G_UP_ALG_12741_replaced([11203324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) has been reached the retransmission scheduling of the related transport block shall be stopped and the HARQ process shall be freed. Note, 5G-L2-PS doesn’t have to inform 5G-L1-DL about releasing the HARQ process.

At the end of the transient phase, the 5G-L2-PS must flush the pending HARQ processes for either waiting for ACK/NACK or retransmission, if their initial transmission were triggered during the transient phase. About the pending HARQ processes for either waiting for ACK/NACK or retransmission with initial transmission before the transient phase, the L2-PS must flush them at the beginning of the transient if the uplink configuration is modified during this phase (ex Transform Precoding (de)activation, Uplink PT-RS modification...).

For each subcell where a new transmission shall be done based on the resource requests of the UE the following procedure shall be executed:

The UL scheduler determines the TBS see 5G_UP_ALG_5084_replaced([11203344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The UL scheduler shall select the next available free HARQ process of the UE in the subcell for the transmission of the TB. The UL scheduler will toggle the NDI value in the DCI sent for the transmission. 5G-L2-PS shall inform 5G-L1-UL about the new transmission, the TBS and the HARQ process id.

See 5G_L2_423_replaced([11152361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24n_KvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G_UP_ALG_5078_replaced([11202403](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) /5G_UP_ALG_5082_replaced([11203301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

---

#### 3.30.8.2.0-4 UL HARQ processing UL HARQ Retransmission Incremental Redundancy (ID: 11202379)

**3.30.8.2.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `12442841`)

UL HARQ processing For FR1: If byte #0 of rdHARQDisableIncrRedundancyUl (see 5G_L2_1061_replaced([11182837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF4PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not equal to 0x00,

For FR2: If byte #1 of rdHARQDisableIncrRedundancyUl (see 5G_L2_1061_replaced([11182837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF4PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not equal to 0x00, then do

If PUSCH transmission is not 2x or 4x SAg then

5G-L2-PS shall disable incremental redundancy on PUSCH.

5G-L2-PS shall use RV0 in all initial transmissions and in all HARQ retransmissions.

5G-L2-PS shall always set Redundancy version = 0 in DCI Format 0_0 (see 5G_UP_ALG_4932_replaced([11171407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuEIo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and Redundancy version = 0 in DCI Format 0_1 (see 5G_UP_ALG_4937_replaced([11171437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuEIqvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Else

Use incremental redundancy (IR) coding scheme for PUSCH initial transmissions and retransmissions (see Note 1)

EndIf

For FR1: If byte #0 of rdHARQDisableIncrRedundancyUl (see 5G_L2_1061_replaced([11182837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF4PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equal to 0x00,

For FR2: If byte #1 of rdHARQDisableIncrRedundancyUl (see 5G_L2_1061_replaced([11182837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF4PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equal to 0x00, then do

If (UL usedMcs < rdHarqDisableIncrRedundancyUlMcsThreshold - see 5G_L2_1322_replaced([11182893](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF6_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) AND (PUSCH transmission is not 2x or 4x SAg) then

UL rvIndex will be forced to 0 for all reTx i.e. UL incremental redundancy will be disabled for the current HARQ process (see Note 2)

Else 5G-L2-PS shall proceed as follows:

Use incremental redundancy (IR) coding scheme for PUSCH initial transmissions and retransmissions (see Note 1)

EndIf

Note 1: With Incremental Redundancy (IR), for each HARQ initial transmission and retransmission the encoder shall select a different set of bits from the LDPC encoding buffer to build the PUSCH transmission. Four different sets are available: RV0, RV1, RV2, RV3 (RV = Redundancy Version).

Note 2: This is workaround for an IODT issue with Xiaomi UE where high BLER is seen when IR RV sequence is used with MCS = 0.

For details regarding how to select the RV index in the DCI for SAg 2x and 4x PUSCH transmissions see 5G_UP_ALG_19345_replaced([11205186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwntaPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For all other cases the remainder of this requirement specifies how the the RV index is to be specified by the DCI.

5G-L2-PS shall indicate in each DCI the RV index the UE shall transmit in PUSCH (Redundancy Version field in DCI format 0_0 & DCI format 0_1). The RV index derives from number of HARQ transmissions according to following sequence : RV = RvSequence [((number of HARQ transmission) modulo 4)], with RvSequence corresponding to RV0, RV2,RV3,RV1 , such that initial PUSCH transmission is transmitted with Rv0, first HARQ retransmission with rv2, ..

[Before CNI-137616-B] If DTX is reported on PUSCH scheduled with RV 0 and rdRvIndex0InCaseOfDtx according to 5G_L2_3426_replaced([11182858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27fF5PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is 'true', 5G-L2-PS shall use RV 0 for the next transmission. [End CNI-137616-B]

If DTX is reported on PUSCH scheduled with **RV 0 and freshHarqTrans = True, L2-RT shall refresh L1 buffer and use RV 0 for the next transmission, otherwise L2-RT shall ignore status of DTX. Please note, DTX status is reported by L1 .**

Below tables include some examples about how to handle DTX in HARQ IR process. For HARQ CC process, the RV is always 0.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |   
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---  
|  | **RV** |  | **Fresh HARQ transmission** |  | **DTX** |  |   
Initial TX |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |   
| 1st reTx |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | No |   
| 2nd reTx |  | 2 |  | No |  | Yes |   
| 3rd reTx |  | 3 |  | No |  | No |   
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |   
|  | **RV** |  | **Fresh HARQ transmission** |  | **DTX** |  |   
Initial TX |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | No |  |   
| 1st reTx |  | 2 |  | No |  | Yes |   
| 2nd reTx |  | 3 |  | No |  | Yes |   
| 3rd reTx |  | 1 |  | No |  | No |   
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |   
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |   
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---  
|  | **RV** |  | **Fresh HARQ transmission** |  | **DTX** |  |   
Initial TX |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |   
| 1st reTx |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | Yes |   
| 2nd reTx |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | Yes |   
| 3rd reTx |  | 0 |  | Yes |  | No |   
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |   
  
For FR1 TDD and PUSCH is scheduled with periodic CSI ~~and without AN~~, If DTX is reported on CSI but non-DTX&CRC NOK are reported on PUSCH and PUSCH is scheduled with RV 0 and freshHarqTrans = True and MCS >= rdMissedPcsiRestartIncrRedundancyMcsThreshold (5G_UP_ALG_29293_replaced([11209544](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw0htPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-RT shall refresh L1 buffer and use RV 0 for the next transmission. Please note that this shall not impact Sag transmission since Sag avoids pCSI slots (cf. 5G_UP_ALG_19835_replaced([11205247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwoUdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Note, when rdMissedPcsiRestartIncrRedundancyMcsThreshold (5G_UP_ALG_29293_replaced([11209544](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw0htPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to 32, it will be skipped.

---

#### 3.30.8.2.1 HARQ Handling of PUSCH re-Transmissions

##### 3.30.8.2.1-1 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation

###### 3.30.8.2.1-1.0-4 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation At the reception of the HARQ Feedback (message UlData_PuschReceiveRespHarqU) from 5G-L1-UL for a given UE, subcell and HARQ process 5G-L2-PS shall obey the following rules:  HARQ process is freed if CRC is ok or maximum number of HARQ transmissions has been reached,  If UL HARQ retransmission is for PCell or Scell which is not in overheating status according to 5G_UP_ALG_2874_replaced( 11123313 ), L2-PS shall consider the UE as candidate to retransmit the data of that HARQ process. Retransmission can be on any subcell. Subcell is decided in 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced( 11196450 ). If HARQ feedback for Scell which is in overheating status according to 5G_UP_ALG_2871_replaced( 11123293 ), L2-PS shall stop the retransmission of the related transport block and free the HARQ process.  [Before CNI-137616-A] For FDD only and UE is not in transient phase and if SAg Mode is OFF (See 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced( 11204759 )) , if rdActSetAvgSinrToInstSinr (5G_L2_1346_replaced( 11185179 )) is set to TRUE: L2-PS shall release the HARQ process when rrmInstSinr &lt;= rdInstSinrMin (5G_UP_ALG_3078_replaced( 11130719 )). [End CNI-137616-A]  For a UE with configured grant the reception of the PUSCH HARQ feedback shall trigger the evaluation of the ‘HarqU’  event, if present, associated to the current state in the CG state machine (see 5G_UP_ALG_15099_replaced( 11172972 )). (ID: 12438214)

##### 3.30.8.2.1-2 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation

###### 3.30.8.2.1-2.0-3 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation The gNB shall support the configuration of the maximum number of uplink HARQ transmissions including initial transmission and retransmissions (maxUlHarqTxNum), as following: If NRCELL.actServiceAwareConfig is TRUE,  maxUlHarqTxNum = Max (maxUlHarqTx_Bearer[i]), where: • 'i' represents bearers (including SRB and DRB) amongst the logical channel groups with non-empty buffer in housekeeping (see 5G_UP_ALG_5114_replaced( 11203638 )) that are assumed to be transmitted by UE [Before PR843138] or all bearers if no logical channel group with non-empty buffer in housekeeping exists. [PR843138] If no logical channel group with non-empty buffer in housekeeping exists, all RLC UM bearers shall be considered, but in case no RLC UM bearers are configured too, then all bearers (including RLC AM) shall be considered [End PR843138]. • maxUlHarqTx_Bearer[i] = NRBTS/NRDRB_MAC.maxUlHarqTxDrb for DRBs with maxUlHarqTxDrb configured, NRBTS/NRCELLGRP.maxUlHarqTx otherwise Else (i.e. NRCELL.actServiceAwareConfig is FALSE)  maxUlHarqTxNum = NRBTS/NRCELLGRP.maxUlHarqTx If the UE is SAg capable and NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg is set to Enabled_VoNr, then it is possible that a 2x or a 4x SAg grant will be used. When a 2x or 4x SAg is used (5G_UP_ALG_19882_replaced( 11205071 )), the maximum number of HARQs is defined by the parameter NRCELL.maxHarqBundlesUlSlotAgg. (ID: 12737307)

##### 3.30.8.2.1.0-1 HARQ Handling of PUSCH re-Transmissions Scheduling of a Re-transmission for PUSCH (ID: 11202406)

**3.30.8.2.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11202432`)

HARQ Handling of PUSCH re-Transmissions Scheduling of a Re-transmission:

  * If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) or the conditions defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are not fulfilled, the same TBS and Rank and number of PRBs is used as for the initial transmission. If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, the same TBS, BG and Rank is used as for the initial transmission but the number of symbols and the number of PRBs may be different to the initial transmission (see 5G_UP_ALG_11970_replaced([11202476](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ;
  * Due to the dynamic assignment of UL slot format according 5G_UP_ALG_2473_replaced([11116092](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), a rule shall be observed that the UL PS shall not schedule a PUSCH HARQ retransmission until the number of OFDM symbols is higher or equals to the number of OFDM symbols used for the initial transmission if the adaptive re-transmission scheme is deactivated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to FALSE) or the conditions defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are not fulfilled. If the adaptive re-transmission scheme is activated (NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE) and the conditions defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, the allocation of the retransmission is applied as defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11970_replaced([11202476](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The number of OFDM symbols allocated by the initial transmission and the OFDM symbols available for the retransmission doesn’t determine if the UE’s transport block is sent again.
  * L2RT shall distribute the same PRB and TBS as the initial transmission for those re-transmission UEs which are included in the same virtual UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_9509_replaced([11146323](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksD9hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and which are scheduled with the initial MCS again. For a retransmission, which is scheduled with a reserved MCS, the needed amount of PRB for the adaptive retransmission is given by 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * For a retransmission corresponding to a configured grant (CG) Harq process, adaptive retransmission is not applicable.
  * Adaptive retransmission is not applicable when SAg grant has either 2x or 4x repetitions (see 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Re-transmission can be supported with different PUSCH mapping type than the initial transmission.
  * Re-transmission can be supported with different PUSCH start symbol than the initial transmission. If PUSCH start symbol is different and PUSCH number of symbols is the same, it's still considered as the same number of symbols between re-transmission and initial transmission.



[CB013199-B]

  * If slow pooling of instantaneous throughput is enabled i.e. tddInstTputPoolingAllowed is TRUE (refer 5G_RAN_OP_3986_replaced([11693605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_D33F-P9nEe-D3seUwaI0tg))), for every re-transmission, gNB-DU shall add the TBS(tbSize) value to rrmUsedCellThroughputPerSlot (refer 5G_UP_ALG_29086_replaced([11215102](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to keep track of the cell's throughput per slot. Before every PUSCH re-transmission, the scheduler shall also ensure that the throughput limit is not exceeded as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29088_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case that scheduling the current UE increases the throughput beyond the limit, the scheduler shall take necessary actions as described in 5G_UP_ALG_29088_replaced([11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))



[End CB013199-B]

---

##### 3.30.8.2.1.0-2 HARQ Handling of PUSCH re-Transmissions Scheduling of adaptive Re-transmission (ID: 11202437)

**3.30.8.2.1.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `12737441`)

HARQ Handling of PUSCH re-Transmissions 12737441: HARQ Handling of PUSCH re-Transmissions If NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is set to TRUE **and the UL MU-MIMO feature is not activated (NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is set to FALSE),** and **NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcsEnh is set to FALSE, 5G-L2-PS shall use the adaptive retransmission algorithm for allocation of resources for the retransmissions processed by non-Loner based baseband cards.****_* If the UL MU-MIMO feature is activated (NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is set to TRUE) and NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcsEnh is set to FALSE, the adaptive retransmission is deactivated disregarding of the value of NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs and the HARQ retransmissions shall be processed by the legacy HARQ method using the initial MCS for the retransmission. If NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcsEnh is set to FALSE and the cell is served by a Loner based baseband card the adaptive retransmission is deactivated disregarding of the value of NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs. If NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcsEnh is set to TRUE_ *, 5G-L2-PS shall use the adaptive retransmission algorithm for allocation of resources for the retransmissions.** If NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcsEnh is set to TRUE and the cell is served by a Loner based baseband card, 5G-L2-PS shall use the adaptive retransmission algorithm for allocation of resources for the retransmission if the initial transmission MCS is lower or equal to a threshold. The threshold is defined as:  rdCutOffMcsAdaptiveReTx64Qam (5G_UP_ALG_18194_replaced([11209085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwxeafvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if 64QAM MCS index table is used.  rdCutOffMcsAdaptiveReTx256Qam (5G_UP_ALG_18196_replaced([11209099](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwxea_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if 256QAM MCS index table is used.  rdCutOffMcsAdaptiveReTx64QamDftsOfdm (5G_UP_ALG_18198_replaced([11209114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwxebfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if 64QAM MCS index table for transform precoding is used. * rdCutOffMcsAdaptiveReTx64QamLowSE (5G_UP_ALG_19225_replaced([11209130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyFcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if 64QAM MCS LowSE index table is used. Rationale: The LDPC decoder performance of the Loner is restricted with the negative impact that most probably the adaptive retransmission scheme can’t be applied to initial transmissions with a higher MCS. For the case that the adaptive retransmission can’t be used for all initial transmissions, the cut-off MCS defines the threshold starting from that the legacy retransmission scheme shall be applied. The need of the cut-off MCS is still under evaluation. If the PUSCH transmission is NACKed or a DTX is detected for a HARQ process previously NACKed, the adaptive retransmission shall be used. If a DTX is detected for an initial UL transmission, the legacy retransmission method shall be used. Even if the adaptive retransmission algorithm is in progress, the legacy HARQ method shall be used if the R&D parameter rdEnableAdaptiveReTxBpskWa is set to True (see 5G_L2_10133_replaced([11183202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27hiKvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and an initial transmission, which is scheduled with MCS0 or MCS1, needs to be retransmitted and the transform precoding is activated (NRCELL.actUlDftSOfdm=TRUE) and if Pi/2-BPSK modulation order is active for MCS0/MCS1 (q=1). Rationale: The misinterpretation of MCS28 by a QC chip set requests the use of the initial MCS for retransmissions if the initial transmission is scheduled by Pi/2-BPSK modulation order. The following flow chart illustrates the general conditions for the provision of reserved MCS (refer to 5G_UP_IF_1897_replaced([11515718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUO_Wvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))/2260) under adaptive retransmission. **[Before PR844533]** [image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a decision-making process. The flowchart begins with a "Start" node, represented by an oval. From there, it proceeds to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "actAdaptiveReT xResMcs?". If the condition is "False", the flow goes to a rectangular node labeled "Use legacy method for reTx". If the condition is "True", the flow moves to another diamond-shaped decision node labeled "rdEnableAdapt iveReTxBpskW". From "rdEnableAdapt iveReTxBpskW", if the condition is "True", the flow goes to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "actUIDftSOfdm AND MCS0/MCS1 using pi/2-BPSK". If this condition is "False", the flow goes to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "PUSCH is DTX'ed?". If "actUIDftSOfdm AND MCS0/MCS1 using pi/2-BPSK" is "True", the flow goes to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "Valid feedback of the HARQ process received before?". From "PUSCH is DTX'ed?", if the condition is "True", the flow goes to the "Valid feedback of the HARQ process received before?" node. If "PUSCH is DTX'ed?" is "False", the flow goes to a diamond-shaped decision node labeled "CSI is DTX'ed AND and MCS >= rdMissedPcsiRestartInc". From "Valid feedback of the HARQ process received before?", if the condition is "True", the flow goes to a rectangular node labeled "Apply algorithm of the adaptive retransmission scheme". If the condition is "False", the flow goes to a rectangular node labeled "Use legacy method for reTx". From "CSI is DTX'ed AND and MCS >= rdMissedPcsiRestartInc", if the condition is "False", the flow goes to the "Apply algorithm of the adaptive retransmission scheme" node. If the condition is "True", the flow goes to a rectangular node labeled "Use legacy method for reTx". The flowchart uses standard flowchart symbols: ovals for start/end points, diamonds for decisions, and rectangles for processes or actions. Arrows indicate the flow of control between nodes, with labels "True" or "False" indicating the path taken based on the decision.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16025.002.png[/image_path][/image-0] **[PR844533]** [image-1][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a decision-making process related to retransmission schemes in a communication system. The flowchart begins with a "Start" node, represented by an oval. From the "Start" node, the flow proceeds to a diamond-shaped decision box labeled "actAdaptiveReTxResMcs?". \- If the condition is "False", the flow goes to a green rectangular box labeled "Use legacy method for reTx". \- If the condition is "True", the flow moves to another diamond-shaped decision box labeled "rdEnableAdapt iveReTxBpskW". From "rdEnableAdapt iveReTxBpskW": \- If the condition is "False", the flow goes to a green rectangular box labeled "Use legacy method for reTx". \- If the condition is "True", the flow moves to a diamond-shaped decision box labeled "actUIDftSOfdm AND MCS0/MCS1 using pi/2-BPSK". From "actUIDftSOfdm AND MCS0/MCS1 using pi/2-BPSK": \- If the condition is "False", the flow moves to a diamond-shaped decision box labeled "PUSCH is DTX'ed?". \- If the condition is "True", the flow goes to a green rectangular box labeled "Use legacy method for reTx". From "PUSCH is DTX'ed?": \- If the condition is "False", the flow goes to a green rectangular box labeled "Apply algorithm of the adaptive retransmission scheme". \- If the condition is "True", the flow moves to a diamond-shaped decision box labeled "Valid feedback of the HARQ process received before?". From "Valid feedback of the HARQ process received before?": \- If the condition is "False", the flow goes to a green rectangular box labeled "Use legacy method for reTx". \- If the condition is "True", the flow goes to a green rectangular box labeled "Apply algorithm of the adaptive retransmission scheme". The flowchart uses standard flowchart symbols: ovals for start/end points, diamonds for decision points, and rectangles for process steps. Arrows indicate the direction of the flow, and labels on the arrows ("True" or "False") specify the condition for following that path.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16025.001.png[/image_path][/image-1] Note 1: The adaptive retransmission will be used when the TBS may change for retransmission using legacy HARQ with MCS of initial transmission, even function flag is not enabled. This is to keep same TBS between initial and retransmission. (e.g. [11148312: DMRS symbols allocation in HARQ retransmission](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksJdF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)). Note 2: The RV value used for retransmission, see [11202379: UL HARQ Retransmission Incremental Redundancy](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9") **[End PR844533]** **File Name: Adaptive re-transmission flow.xlsx** **File path:**

##### 3.30.8.2.1.1 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation

###### 3.30.8.2.1.1.0-1 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Overview (ID: 11202481)

**3.30.8.2.1.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11202494`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation If NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcs is TRUE, the adaptive retransmission algorithm shall be applied for the evaluation of the resources. The algorithm shall evaluate on slot basis the number of available physical channel bits for data transmission per PRB and accumulate the resources over all available PRBs. Guard symbols and resources occupied by e.g., DMRS, PTRS, TRS, CSI-IM or SRS and for the shared spectrum with LTE (DSS) LTE-CRS and LTE-PBCH shall not be used for the data transmission which reduces the number of useable resources differently per slot.

If the PRB pooling feature is activated [CB012605-E](stream PRB based pooling, which is not used for eCPRI 7-2e based configurations)[End CB012605-E], the number of available PRBs before the first PRB assignment with adaptive retransmission shall correspond to the number of PRBs provided by the PRB pooling feature (see 5G_UP_ALG_6500_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For FR1 TDD and FR1 FDD, if dynamic UL resource allocation is enabled (NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc is set to True), the number of PUSCH PRBs in each slot can change (see 5G_UP_ALG_4976_replaced([11199501](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOExvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Therefore, the number of available PRBs before the first PRB assignment with adaptive retransmission shall be defined as min(PRBs from PRB pooling, PRBs from dynamic UL allocation).

[CB012605-E]

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the required layer PRBs for the resource allocation with adaptive retransmission shall not be greater than the max layer PRBs allocated by the BBRM in ever pooling period. The value is defined by rrmMaxNumLayerPrbPerSlot described in 5G_UP_ALG_26582_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB012605-E]

---

###### 3.30.8.2.1.1.0-2 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Definitions for the adaptive retransmission algorithm (ID: 11202499)

**3.30.8.2.1.1.0-2.0-7**  (ID: `13454532`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation The following definitions shall be used for the resource allocation algorithm in adaptive retransmission.

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $rePerPRBreTx = (12 \times nbSymbols_{reTx} - N_{RE,reTx}^{PTRS} - N_{RE,reTx}^{DMRS})$ The formula calculates the number of resource elements per Physical Resource Block (PRB) for a specific transmission ($reTx$). It subtracts the number of resource elements used for PTRS (Phase Tracking Reference Signal) and DMRS (Demodulation Reference Signal) from the total number of resource elements available in a PRB, which is assumed to be $12 \times nbSymbols_{reTx}$ (12 subcarriers per PRB multiplied by the number of symbols in the transmission).[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in white text against a black background. The formula is: `rePerPRBini = (12 × nbSymbolsini - NRE,ini^PTRS - NRE,ini^DMRS)` The text is slightly blurry, but the characters and symbols are generally discernible. The formula appears to be related to telecommunications or signal processing, likely calculating the number of resource elements per Physical Resource Block (PRB) for a given initial configuration.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

The following inequalities are defined to derive the PRBcand and PRBmin, which are the number of PRBs required to meet the target and minimum number of PRBs for retransmission.

The number of PTRS RE depends on the number of PRB and dlPtrsFreqDensityThresNrb0/1, which defines the FrequencyDensity value (2 or 4).

To simply the implementation, the initial PTRS ratio is used for retransmission and computed as follows:

[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $N_{RE,ini}^{PTRS} = \text{roundup} \left( \frac{nbRE_{ini}^{PTRS}}{PRB_{ini}} \right)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]

[image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $N_{\text{RE,reTx}}^{\text{PTRS}} = \text{roundup}\left(\frac{\text{nbSymbols}_{\text{reTx}}}{\text{nbSymbols}_{\text{ini}}} \times \frac{\text{nbRE}_{\text{ini}}^{\text{PTRS}}}{\text{PRB}_{\text{ini}}}\right)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]

with

[image-4][image_desc]The image contains text that reads "nbRE PTRS xml". The text is in a dark color against a light background. The letters are slightly blurred, but legible.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]: **The number of PTRS RE for the initial transmission (5G_UP_ALG_4669_replaced([11192649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvmZsPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

Note: This simplification introduces an error when the FrequencyDensity changes between initial transmission and retransmission.

The same number of CDM groupwith no data OR the same number of DMRS symbols from initial transmission is used for retransmission.

[image-5][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is: $N_{RE,reTx}^{DMRS} = N_{RE,ini}^{DMRS}$ The equation states that the number of DMRS REs (Resource Elements) for re-transmission is equal to the initial number of DMRS REs.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.006.png[/image_path][/image-5]

Inequality 1:

[image-6][image_desc]PRB_{cand} \times rePerPRB_{reTx} \times Q_{m,reTx} \times V_{reTx} \geq PRB_{inf} \times rePerPRB_{inf} \times Q_{m,inf} \times V_{inf} \times \frac{targetPctResourceReservedMCS}{100}[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.007.png[/image_path][/image-6]

Inequality 2:

[image-7][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $PRB_{min} \times rePerPRB_{reTx} \times Q_{m,reTx} \times V_{reTx} \geq PRB_{ini} \times rePerPRB_{ini} \times Q_{m,ini} \times V_{ini}$ Below this formula, there is a fraction: $\frac{minPctResourceReservedMCS}{100}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.008.png[/image_path][/image-7] 

Metric 1 (derived from inequality 1);

[image-8][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $PRB_{cand} = roundup \left( \frac{PRB_{ini} \times rePerPRB_{ini} \times \frac{Q_{m,ini}}{Q_{m,reTx}} \times \frac{V_{ini}}{V_{reTx}} \times \frac{targetPctResourceReservedMCS}{100}}{rePerPRB_{reTx}} \right)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.009.png[/image_path][/image-8] 

Metric 2 (derived from inequality 2);

[image-9][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $PRB_{min} = roundup \left( \frac{PRB_{ini} \times rePerPRB_{ini} \times \frac{Q_{m,ini}}{Q_{m,reTx}} \times \frac{V_{ini}}{V_{reTx}} \times \frac{minPctResourceReservedMCS}{100}}{rePerPRB_{reTx}} \right)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.010.png[/image_path][/image-9]

Also,

[image-10][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $PRB_{maxCodeRate} = roundup(\frac{TBS_{int} + CRC}{rdMaxCrReTransmissionUl \times Q_{m,reTx} \times V_{reTx}} / rePerPRB_{reTx})$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.011.png[/image_path][/image-10]

[image-11][image_desc]The image contains the text "PRB_cand" in a blurry, italicized font. The text is black and appears on a white background.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.012.png[/image_path][/image-11]: Candidate PRBs to be scheduled for the retransmission to fulfil Metric 1

[image-12][image_desc]The image contains the text "PRBmin". The text is in a sans-serif font and is colored in shades of blue and yellow, with a slight blur effect. The letters "PRB" are larger and bolder than the letters "min", which are smaller and appear as a subscript.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.013.png[/image_path][/image-12]: Minimum PRBs to be scheduled for the retransmission to fulfil Metric 2

[image-13][image_desc]The image contains the text "PRBini". The text is in a dark color against a light background. The letters are capitalized, except for the last three letters which are lowercase. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.014.png[/image_path][/image-13] : Number of PRBs used in initial transmission

[image-14][image_desc]The image contains the text "PRB MaxCodeRate". The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be in a mathematical or technical context. The letters are capitalized, except for the "e" in "Rate". The subscript "MaxCode" is smaller than the "PRB" and "Rate" parts.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.015.png[/image_path][/image-14]: Minimum number of PRBs required to satisfy the maximum allowed code rate (rdMaxCrReTransmissionUl (5G_UP_ALG_12034_replaced([11209005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kww3XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) for retransmission. The maximum allowed code rate for adaptive retransmission is defined by rdMaxCrReTransmissionUl (5G_UP_ALG_12034_replaced([11209005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kww3XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If NRCELL.actAdaptiveReTxResMcsEnh is set to TRUE, The value of rdMaxCrReTransmissionUl (5G_UP_ALG_12034_replaced([11209005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kww3XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be overwritten with 0.97 only for Loner based baseband card.

The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be in lowercase with the exception of "P", "R", and "M", which are capitalized. The text is slightly blurred and has a light background.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16036.016.png[/image_path][/image-15]: The parameter refers to NRCELL.targetPctResourceReservedMcsUl for basic reliability, and 100 (as defined in Golden SCF) for high reliability

(Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2536_replaced([11117398](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp-57vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for slots with expected A-SRS report in **FDD)****

###### 3.30.8.2.1.1.0-3 Adaptive retransmission resource allocation Resource allocation for adaptive retransmission (ID: 11202887)

**3.30.8.2.1.1.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `11203294`)

Adaptive retransmission resource allocation 11203294: Adaptive retransmission resource allocation If conditions given in 5G_UP_ALG_11945_replaced([11202437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVWfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are fulfilled, 5G-L2-PS shall find the contiguous PRBs (referenced as PRBtarget) for allocation of a retransmission in UL using the definitions in 5G_UP_ALG_11973_replaced([11202499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwd8YvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in the following steps. The algorithm shall first find the contiguous PRBs to fulfil Metric1 while keeping the maximum code rate for retransmission limitation. If the maximum code rate for retransmission allows, and the available PRBs for scheduling are not enough to fulfil Metric1, the algorithm shall find a minimum number of contiguous PRBs that fulfil Metric2. Furthermore, the algorithm shall check the available resources if the UL baseband pooling is activated. UEs, which are blocked in scheduling with its adaptive retransmission due to the reduced number of available PRB, shall be taken out of the scheduling set and the corresponding HARQ process shall be freed. Furthermore, for NR cells shared with LTE the algorithm shall check the available resources if NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actUlAdaptReTxEnhDss=TRUE. UEs, which are blocked in scheduling with its adaptive retransmission due to the number of available PRB, shall be taken out of the scheduling set and the corresponding HARQ process shall be freed. The retransmission shall be postponed, if the minimum number of contiguous PRBs to fulfil Metric2 are not available for scheduling. If the value of PRBmaxCodeRate (as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_11973_replaced([11202499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwd8YvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is calculated to be more than initial transmission PRBs and resource elements per PRB are same for initial (rePerPrbini) and retransmission (rePerPrbreTx), 5G-L2-PS shall assign the value of initial transmission PRBs to PRBmaxCodeRate. During slots w/o UCI the minimum allocation is defined by [Before CB013204-A] [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font on a dark background. The equation is: $PRB_{min} = max(PRB_{max_moderate}, PRB_{min}, minSupportedNumPrbsUI)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16043.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] [CB013204-A] PRBmin = max (PRBmaxcoderate, PRBmin, rrmMinSupportedPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31776_replaced([11199457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) [End CB013204-A] During slots a UCI needs to be carried the minimum allocation is defined by [Before CB013204-A] [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is: $PRB_{min} = max(PRB_{maxcode rate}, PRB_{min}, minSupportedNumPrbsUL, minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16043.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] [CB013204-A] PRBmin = max (PRBmaxcoderate, PRBmin, rrmMinSupportedPuschPrb(5G_UP_ALG_31776_replaced([11199457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci(5G_UP_ALG_6391_replaced([11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) [End CB013204-A] [Before CB013204-A] The R&D parameter minSupportedNumPrbsUl is specified in 5G_L2_1136_replaced([11183158](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27hiIfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the parameter minAllowedPuschPrbWithUci depends on the number of UCI bits of HARQ A/N and pCSI and is defined by the requirement 5G_UP_ALG_6391_replaced([11197037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwHXHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB013204-A] If [image-2][image_desc]PRB$_{cand}$ $\leq$ PRB$_{min}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16043.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] [image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in black text on a white background. ## 3.30.9 Transport Block Size Calculation

### 3.30.9.0-2 Transport Block Size Calculation Calculation of UL Transport Block size (ID: 11203355)

**3.30.9.0-2.0-7**  (ID: `12741638`)

Transport Block Size Calculation The UL transport block size tbSize is calculated according to 5G_UP_ALG_5085_replaced([11203349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with

 rrmUlMcs provided by the link adaptation (see 5G_UP_ALG_3071_replaced([11130097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq-_cfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or by default MCS (ullaIniMcs for basic reliability LA and ullaIniMcsHR for high reliability LA, received in PsCell_CellSetupReq)

 PRB Grant Size in UL

 scheduled MIMO layer

 number of PUSCH OFDM Symbol

For VoNR users with active 5QI-1/65/66 bearer for voice service:

If NRCELL.actUlMinTbsForVoNR is set to Enabled_VoNr and if either (the UE is not SAg capable) or (NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg is set to FALSE), then:

If the above calculated tbSize with **the final selected MCS as MCS0 and the number of PRBs granted is less than or equal to the configured value given by NRCELL.ulMinTbsForVoNR, then the gNB scheduler shall recalculate a minimum MCS to ensure a TBS that is greater than or equal to the configured UL minimum TBS given by NRCELL.ulMinTbsForVoNR as per the rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15554_replaced([11203613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The gNB scheduler shall then override the final used MCS with this selected MCS.**

For users with SAg mode = ON (5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

If a 2x or 4x SAg grant is used (5G_UP_ALG_19882_replaced([11205071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the MCS selected by SAg for 2x or 4x SAg overrides the rrmUlProposedMcs with this selected MCS.

For UE scheduled with 64qamLowSE-Index table, if the above calculated tbSize with the rrmUlProposedMcs and the number of PRBs granted is less than the R&D parameter [Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547] (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17009_replaced([11209054](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwxeZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then the gNB scheduler shall recalculate a minimum MCS to ensure a TBS that is greater than or equal to the the R&D parameter [Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547]. The gNB scheduler shall then override the rrmUlProposedMcs with this selected MCS

The value of the parameter nb_cdm_no_data is derived in two steps as follows:

  * **Step1: In 38.212, determine the right table (from 7.3.1.1.2-6 to 7.3.1.1.2-23) according to the selected UL DMRS configuration (using IE ulDmrsConfigType for _UL-DMRS-config-type and ulDmrsLen for _UL-DMRS-max-len_ and IE numOfLayers for rank)_****
  * **Step2: In the right table,** according to the value of the ‘antenna port’ field in the DCI (i.e. 5G_UP_ALG_4937_replaced([11171437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuEIqvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), derive nb_cdm_no_data from the column “Number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data”.****
  * nb_cdm_no_data shall be detemined with ulDmrsConfigType = 1, ulDmrsLen = 1,PUSCH-tp = disabled and the antenna port value according to 5G_UP_ALG_4937_replaced([11171437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuEIqvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).



The TBS computation shall take into account the number of layers (1 or 2), the number of transmitted symbols. Please refer to :

  * 5G_UP_ALG_4635_replaced([11192394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvkkgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for tables description,



\- 5G_UP_ALG_6874_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for parameters to send to 5G-L1-UL.

The TBS shall depend on whether the UL scheduler requests an aperiodic SRS transmission in the given slot as per 5G_UP_ALG_5057_replaced([11201982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwa5GvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.30.9.0-4 Transport Block Size Calculation TB Size for RA-Msg3 (ID: 11203473)

**3.30.9.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `13136341`)

Transport Block Size Calculation For any PUSCH whose first transmission was granted by RA-Msg2 (RAR) the Transport Block Size (TBS) is calculated according to ([11203349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with:

For RA-Msg3 with CP OFDM: 

  * mcs = 0
  * single layer .
  * number of PRBs granted in ([11199446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * number of CDM groups without data shall be set to 2 (nb_cdm_no_data).
  * 3 DMRS OFDM symbol (ulDmrsAddPos=2).



[CB008148-A] For RA-Msg3 with DFT-s-OFDM: 

  * mcs = 0
  * remaining characteristics are per ([11140586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_ZQIJ4PvLEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), RA-Msg3 with TP On section .



[End CB008148-A]

---

### 3.30.9.0-5 Transport Block Size Calculation Scheduling with CSS DCI 0_0 and TBS during transient phase in SA or NSA with SRB3 (ID: 11203479)

**3.30.9.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `13136363`)

Transport Block Size Calculation This requirement defines scheduling with DCI 0_0 and TBS calculation for transient phase as below cases:

· For any PUSCH that is scheduled in the transient phase (which is corresponding to the period between receiving PsUser_UserModifyReq and receiving PsUser_UserReconfCompletedReq).

· OR,**for any PUSCH that is scheduled just after a beam failure recovery which is corresponding to the period from the receiving time of Msg3 with CRNTI until the updated beam ID can be applied (5G_UP_ALG_**

For above cases, PUSCH shall be scheduled with **DCI 0_0 and the TBS shall be calculated with:**

  * MCS proposed is defined by 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and final MCS is defined by 5G_UP_ALG_5101_replaced([11203496](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKiPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * MCS used is min(final MCS, 9)**( Note: MCS 9 is highest modulation correspondence to 64QAM table which is applied to UE in transient phase)**
  * Granted within the size of the initial UL bandwidth part (see 5G_L2_899_replaced([11179052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27DoHPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , 5G_UP_ALG_4947_replaced([11198980](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwLBfvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ,max number of PRBs to be granted in case MCS0 as defined by 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), final PRBs to be granted is defined by 5G_UP_ALG_5101_replaced([11203496](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKiPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_4970_replaced([11199433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNduvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * then final PRBs used is min(final PRBs, floor(10 (rrmPhrScaled[i, j, tPHR]/10) * rrmMaxNumPuschPrb)
    * rrmPhrScaled[i,j,tPHR]: latest Power Headroom Report scaled to an allocation of rrmMaxNumPrbPusch refer to 5G_UP_ALG_9755_replaced([11130171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq-_ffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
    * [CB008148-A] If TP On is used for DCI 0_0 per ([12977641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_2nbSQbXfEfCQT7hYUxERAg)) then final PRBs must meet the rule per ([11140586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_ZQIJ4PvLEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) ) 
    * Note TP On for DCI 0_0 is only for L2-PS NewFR1 [End CB008148-A]
  * single layer
  * _dmrs-AdditionalPosition equals to 'pos2'_
  * number of CDM groups without data (nb_cdm_no_data) shall be set to 2

---

### 3.30.9.0-7 Transport Block Size Calculation Correction of the MCS for UL scheduling (ID: 11203496)

**3.30.9.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11203503`)

Transport Block Size Calculation UL MCS and PRB reduction to minimize padding bits

---

**3.30.9.0-7.0-1.0-9**  (ID: `13408556`)

Transport Block Size Calculation If A-CSI is requested (multiplexed with PUSCH data or in UCI only mode), this procedure is not executed.

With activation of the MCS/PRB reduction in UL by using the R&D parameter rdEnableMcsPrbReduction (see 5G_L2_21166_replaced([11183340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iwR_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if the legacy FD scheduler is used or by using the R&D parameter rdEnableMcsPrbReductionFdm (see 5G_L2_1179_replaced([11182832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27ee3_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for Msg5 only and by using the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actMcsPrbReductionFdmUl (for Basic Reliability) or NRCELL.dlMcsDowngradeEnhHr (for High Reliability) for transmissions after Msg5 if the FDM scheduler is used, the scheduler adapts the UL MCS and the number of UL PRBs as follows:

For slots, for which the UL transport block size (TBS) provides more transmission capacity than required by **rrmMinUlTbs, the scheduler shall downgrade the UL MCS. The MCS after downgrade shall allow the transmission of the TBS required by the buffered user data volume while minimizing the amount of padding bits.**

If NRCELL.actOptPuschPrbAlloc is set to true,

rrmMinUlTbs = MAX {NRCELL.ulMinTbs, buffered user UL data volume}

Else

rrmMinUlTbs = buffered user UL data volume

Endif

If the UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE MCS table, the MCS after downgrade shall allow the transmission of the TBS >= [Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547] ([Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547] defined in 5G_UP_ALG_17009_replaced([11209054](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwxeZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This means that, for the purpose of this procedure max([Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547] , rrmMinUlTbs) is considered as target amount of data to transmit.

If the UE is scheduled with a dynamic grant is triggered to overwrite the CG slot with additional RBs to mitigate the UCI multiplexing in the PUSCH transmission (see 5G_UP_ALG_15617_replaced([11198720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the TBS after MCS downgrade must not be lower than the TBS configured for CG grant (NRBTS/NRULCG_PROFILE.grantedPacketSize).

If “Limited UL MCS downgrade” (for Basic Reliability) is enabled [see 5G_UP_ALG_6857_replaced([11137777](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krbrYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))] the UL MCS reduction will be limited to a maximum number of MCS steps specified by NRCELL.ulMcsDowngradeMaxReduction. The MCS reduction in ILLA due to max code rate limitation shall be also counted for the maximum steps of below MCS reduction procedure. Example: NRCELL.ulMcsDowngradeMaxReduction is 4 and there's already 1 MCS reduction in ILLA due to max code rate limitation, then only maximum 3 MCS steps can be reduced in below MCS reduction procedure.

Note that this target BLER can be zero which effectively disables Ack “step up” as per “full UL MCS Downgrade” functionality. Also note than the UL downgrade function is based on the "scaled MCS" as the "non-downgraded" MCS.

For VoNR users with active 5QI-1/65/66 bearer for voice service:

If NRCELL.actUlMinTbsForVoNR is set to TRUE and if either (the UE is not SAg capable) or (NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg is set to FALSE), then:

If the above procedure results in a downgraded MCS below than required for a corresponding minimum MCS/PRB combination to ensure a TBS that is greater than or equal to the configured UL minimum TBS given by NRCELL.ulMinTbsForVoNR as per the rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15554_replaced([11203613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), then for those slots, the gNB scheduler shall limit the downgrade to a value that corresponds to the recalculated minimum MCS to achieve a corresponding tbSize given by by ulMinTbsForVoNR.

For VoNR users with active 5QI-1/65/66 bearer for voice service, if SAg Mode = ON (5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then MCS downgrade is allowed only if 1x SAg is allowed. See 5G_UP_ALG_19882_replaced([11205071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The TBS required by the buffered user UL data volume shall be determined as:

  * Amount of bits for data:



 sum of all buffered data according to the housekeeping function (see 5G_UP_ALG_5115_replaced([11203641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for UEs of request class srbTraffic dcGbrTraffic and drbTraffic;

 5G_UP_ALG_4835_replaced([11195251](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6izfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for UEs of request class ulProactSched;

 max (UL data volume according to housekeeping function, **PayloadSr, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_12751_replaced([11203952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) bytes) for UEs of request class srResp.**

  * If a 5QI-1/65/66 bearer is active and SAg mode = ON (5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then the TBS is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_19882_replaced([11205071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * Else if 5G_UP_ALG_15400_replaced([11199776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwP5-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for UEs of request class voipTraffic or deprioritizedVoip**. if NRCELL.actUlMinTbsForVoNR is set to TRUE, then the TBS is determined as per the rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15554_replaced([11203613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and used as the final TBS.**



 For UEs without a 5QI-1/65/66 bearer the amount of bits for MAC CE shall be added to the data bits

 all possible MAC CEs (see 5G_L2_2009_replaced([11215729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-6NU_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), assuming maximum MAC CE sizes). Requirement 5G_UP_ALG_5107_replaced([11203559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) describes how many bytes are considered for the MAC CEs.

See 5G_UP_ALG_5085_replaced([11203349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the TBS mapping table.

For UE works in high reliability mode the following shall be followed

If NRCELL.ulMcsDowngradeEnhHr is set to 2 (see 5G_UP_ALG_6857_replaced([11137777](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krbrYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) // No any MCS downgrade for High Reliability

disable UL MCS downgrade for HR users works in high reliability mode

else

The below defined procedure which determines the MCS downgrade value shall be followed hereafter.

In below MCS downgrade procedue, number of cdm groups without data shall be detemined as below for every MCS,

If (number of cdm groups without data determined in 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for rrmUlProposedMcs is 2)

number of cdm groups without data is 2

else

number of cdm groups without data is determined by MCS value according to 5G_UP_ALG_3098_replaced([11137832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krbravvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For VoNR users with active 5QI-1/65/66 bearer:

 If SAg mode = ON (5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then the MSC and TBS is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_19882_replaced([11205071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Else if NRCELL.actUlMinTbsForVoNR is set to TRUE and if the final selected MCS is equal to smallest MCS (MCS0) and theTBS determined with this MCS is less than or equal to the configured value NRCELL.ulMinTbsForVoNR, then FD scheduler shall reselect a minimum MCS to ensure a TBS that is greater than or equal to the configured UL minimum TBS given by NRCELL.ulMinTbsForVoNR as per the rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15554_replaced([11203613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Otherwise, below algorithm is followed hereafter.

5G-L2-PS shall try to find the smallest MCS value rrmUlMcsAfterDowngrade which fulfills all of the following conditions:

  * It is smaller than rrmUlProposedMcs (see 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * It is included in list of supported MCSes (see 5G_L2_642_replaced([11164067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25qg9fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))



With rrmMaxNumPrbUl (see 5G_UP_ALG_3090_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), it maps to an UL TB size rrmUlTbsAfterDowngrade larger than or equal to the TBS required by the buffered user UL data volume as determined above. If the UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE MCS table, it maps to a TBS = max([Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547] , rrmMinUlTbs ) .

For VoNR users with active 5QI-1/65/66 bearer:

 If SAg mode = ON (5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the MCS downgrade as discribed above is only allowed if 1x SAg is selected. See 5G_UP_ALG_19882_replaced([11205071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 Else if NRCELL.actUlMinTbsForVoNR is set to TRUE, then the UL TB size after downgrade, if less than or equal to the configured value given by NRCELL.ulMinTbsForVoNR, then gNB scheduler shall ensure a TBS that is greater than or equal to the configured UL minimum TBS given by NRCELL.ulMinTbsForVoNR as per the rules defined in 5G_UP_ALG_15554_replaced([11203613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Hereafter for such users, the gNB scheduler shall set this value to rrmUlTbsAfterDowngrade and the selected MCS as rrmUlMcsAfterDowngrade.

If such an MCS value is found 5G-L2-PS shall:

  * rrmUlUsedMcs = rrmUlMcsAfterDowngrade.
  * tbSize = rrmUlTbsAfterDowngrade.
  * cdmGroupsWithoutData = number of cdm groups without data for rrmUlMcsAfterDowngrade.



Otherwise, 5G-L2-PS shall set:

  * rrmUlUsedMcs = rrmUlProposedMcs.



If rrmUlMcsAfterDowngrade is equal to MCS0 and UL TBS after MCS reduction still provides more capacity than required, the scheduler shall reduce the number of PRBs. Thus, 5G-L2-PS shall find the smallest number of PRBs rrmNumPrbUlAfterDowngrade which fulfills all of the following conditions:

 It is smaller or equal than rrmMaxNumPrbUl

 It is not smaller than rrmMinSupportedPuschPrb (5G_UP_ALG_31776_replaced([11199457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwNdvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 If UCI or PTRS is present in UL transmission, it is not smaller than minimal PRB required by UCI (according to [11197037: Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs with UCI](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_CveOIfvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)[)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_FHPWwPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)or PTRS (according to [11197403: Minimum allowed PUSCH PRBs when UL PTRS is configured](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_FHPWwPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

 With rrmNumPrbUlAfterDowngrade it maps to an UL TB size rrmUlTbsAfterDowngrade larger than or equal to the TBS required by **the buffered user UL data volume as determined above. If the UE is scheduled with 64qamLowSE MCS table, it maps to a TBS = max([Before PR846547] rdMinTbsLowSE [PR846547] rdUlMinTbs [End PR846547] , rrmMinUlTbs )**.****

The UL scheduler shall downgrade the PRB allocation only for Rank 1 transmissions due to restrictions in L1 allocation.

If such a PRB value is found 5G-L2-PS shall set:

  * rrmMaxNumPrbUl = rrmNumPrbUlAfterDowngrade
  * tbSize = rrmUlTbsAfterDowngrade.
  * cdmGroupsWithoutData = number of cdm groups without data for rrmUlMcsAfterDowngrade.



Whenever the scheduler downgrades the MCS, an indication shall be sent to the UL link adaptation.

The UL LA does the evaluation of rrmUlUsedMcs to allow for adequate evaluation of HARQ feedback for the OLLA, see 5G_UP_ALG_3060_replaced([11129299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), whether the MCS/PRB reduction in UL is activated or not, the buffered user UL data volume shall be determined by excluding every LCG satisfying the following condition: the LCG includes only nonGBR and/or deprioritized GBR DRBs and/or deprioritized DC-GBR DRBs and every DRB of that LCG has RG weight (see 5G_UP_ALG_23167_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) equal to 0.

---

**3.30.9.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11203559`)

Transport Block Size Calculation Estimation of the Amount of Bytes for UL MAC CEs

---

**3.30.9.0-7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11203565`)

Transport Block Size Calculation In the scope of 5G_UP_ALG_5102_replaced([11203503](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the amount of bytes that are considered for the transmission of the UL MAC CEs is calculated as follows:

amount of Bytes for UL MAC CEs = maxSize(C-RNTI MAC CE) + maxSize(BSR MAC CE) + maxSize(PHR MAC CE) = 3 + 11 + 16 = 30 Bytes

where:

maxSize(C-RNTI MAC CE) = 2 Bytes + 1 Bytes (fixed-size MAC CE header) = 3 Bytes

maxSize(BSR MAC CE) = 1 Bytes (LGC bitmap) + 8 Bytes (maxNumOfLcgs) + 2 Bytes (variable-size MAC CE header) = 11 Bytes

maxSize(PHR MAC CE) = 4 Bytes (servCell Index bitmap) + 2 Bytes (other Pcell) + 4 Bytes (max num. of UL aggregated CC) * 2 Bytes + 2 Bytes (variable-size MAC CE header) = 16 Bytes

For simplification only the worst cases are considered:

  * for maxSize(BSR MAC CE) only long BSR with 8 LCGs,
  * for maxSize(PHR MAC CE) the maximum amount of UL serving cells that can be aggregated in UL. Also bytes for PHR report of the 'other Pcell' (the one on LTE) are allocated, even if right now this report is not configured to the UE.



Note: variable-size MAC CE header is 2 bytes in our cases, it becomes 3 bytes long for bigger-sized MAC CEs.

---

### 3.30.9.0-8 Transport Block Size Calculation Restriction on UE UL max data rate (ID: 11203573)

**3.30.9.0-8.0-4**  (ID: `12978058`)
