This is necessary because, currently, for CG UE in a firing states SR is masked, i.e. any positive SR should then correspond to a LCG other than the one that maps CG profile. if/when the SR mask configuration management shall be changed, the requirement must be revisited. **[End CB013961-B].**_*_ *[CB013961-A-UPD001] In case that the UE is is configured with 5QI-5 DRB, the index k of rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[] corresponds to the Logical Channel Group with the highest priority, i.e. the LCG that maps the DRB with the highest priority, other than the 5QI-5 DRB, if exists, or to the Logical Channel Group of the 5QI-5 DRB otherwise. **[End CB013961-A-UPD001]____(7_) reduce the buffered data volume to make sure that there is no second grant in case that the current allocation is smaller than PayloadSr but larger than the amount of data in the buffer before PayloadSr addition. (8_) SR is not handled with DRB priority, i..e with algorithm entry points in 5G_UP_ALG_4788_replaced([11194545](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv1qUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and/or 5G_UP_ALG_5134_replaced([11203852](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmxvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [PR833736] (9_) Procedure is executed in case that for UE in SR state SR_PENDING_WAITING_FOR_UE_RESPONSE a BSR is received for the LCG mapped to buffer k (including BSR = 0, see 5G_UP_ALG_5131_replaced([11203806](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwg_wfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for BSR handling procedure) resulting in rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[k] < rrmScheduledUlData_bytes[k] [End PR833736] [05039048] (10_) Buffer is recalculated by removing the bufferCorrSrSchedFailure that remains applied in case of Harq failure. [End 05039048] Notes: 1\. in case that the highest priority DRB changes, either because of a DRB setup or release, there is nothing that is expected to be implemented. That is, the DRB setup/release procedure shall result in SRB traffic that would place the UE in SR_RESPONSE_WITH_SR_RESP_PRIORITY state. Even if there is a pending SR, i.e. UE not scheduled and there is no new SR sent by the UE, there is still UL SRB corresponding to RRC reconfiguration completion that shall ensure UL grant with SRB priority. 2\. bufferCorrSrSchedFailure represents the quantity that is added to rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[k], in case that the buffered data minus scheduled data was less than PayloadSr. This quantity is saved in order to be subtracted in case that the scheduling with DRB priority fails and UE must finally be scheduled with higher priority SR response request class.

#### 3.30.10.6.0-3 SR handling with highest applicable DRB priority for initial transmission R&amp;D parameters (ID: 11203917)

**3.30.10.6.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11203924`)

SR handling with highest applicable DRB priority for initial transmission rdSrResponseWithDrbPriorityProhibitDuration

---

**3.30.10.6.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11203927`)

SR handling with highest applicable DRB priority for initial transmission Defines a prohibiting duration before allowing scheduling with DRB priority after scheduling of initial transmission on DL SRB

Value range: 20...100

Default value: 50

Unit: ms

Step size: 1

Used in: 5G_UP_ALG_31473_replaced([11203890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**3.30.10.6.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11203933`)

SR handling with highest applicable DRB priority for initial transmission rdSrSchedulingWithDrbPriorityThreshold

SR handling with highest applicable DRB priority for initial transmission [Before CB013961-B] Defines the number of SR occasions, as percentage from sr-TransMax, after that a SR response shall be handled with SR response request class priority.

Value range: 0...100

Default value: 40

Unit: %

Step size: 1

Used in: 5G_UP_ALG_31474_replaced([11203903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB013961-B]

### 3.30.10.7 Reception of Positive SR

#### 3.30.10.7.0-1 Reception of Positive SR Payload of SR request - PayloadSr (ID: 11203952)

**3.30.10.7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11203961`)

Reception of Positive SR The gNB shall support the configuration of the payload of SR request (PayloadSr) as following:

  * If NRCELL.actServiceAwareConfig is TRUE, then 



[Before PR834552]

  * PayloadSr = max (rdPayloadSr, payloadSizeSrDrb)



for payloadSizeSrDrb, refer to 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_8314_replaced([11312740](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrksefvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrksefvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9")).

**[CB008600-B] payloadSizeSrDrb of the DC-GBR DRB shall only be considered configured and used for PayloadSr only if the corresponding DC-GBR bearer/LCG is currently prioritised in UL scheduling (see 5G_UP_ALG_30945_replaced([11196041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). i.e. both its long-term GFBR rate (see 5G_UP_ALG_30953_replaced([11196083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the short term MDBV (see 5G_UP_ALG_30954_replaced([11196094](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) have not been exceeded. If the DC-GBR is deprioritized, PayloadSr equals to rdPayloadSr.**[End CB008600-B]****

[PR834552]

  * PayloadSr = max (rdPayloadSr, payloadSizeSrDrb)
  * payloadSizeSrDrb = NRBTS/NRDRB.payloadSizeSrDrb from the configured DRB with the smallest NRBTS/NRDRB.srPeriodicityDrb.



Conditions for the payloadSizeSrDrb calculation:

  * Only the DRBs with the configured NRBTS/NRDRB.payloadSizeSrDrb and NRBTS/NRDRB.srPeriodicityDrb shall be considered.
  * NRBTS/NRDRB.payloadSizeSrDrb from the DC-GBR DRB shall be considered only if the corresponding DC-GBR DRB is in prioritised state. If not, it shall be considered that the DC-GBR DRB does not have configured NRBTS/NRDRB.payloadSizeSrDrb value and it shall be excluded in the payloadSizeSrDrb calculation. Note, DC-GBR MDBV token bucket shall be updated first and then then the MDBV token bucket value shall be checked to get its upto date token bucket value.
  * max payloadSizeSrDrb if multiple DRBs configured with the same lowest SR periodicity, excluding VoNR bearers.



[End PR834552]

  * Else

  * PayloadSr = rdPayloadSr




For rdPayloadSr, refer to 5G_L2_1182_replaced([11184081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27oP5PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[PR834552] Note: NRBTS/NRDRB.payloadSizeSrDrb and NRBTS/NRDRB.srPeriodicityDrb of all DRBs, except DC-GBR DRB are not available in L2-PS and hence the resultant payloadSizeSrDrb of all bearers except DCGBR bearer is precomputed in Cplane and a single resultant payloadSizeSrDrb (excluding DC-GBR bearer consideration) is sent to L2-PS, in addition to the payloadSizeSrDrb and srPeriodicityDrb of DC-GBR bearer to minimize the L2L3 interface impacts. Hence, if any further changes required in the PayloadSr calculation, it might also required further L2L3 interface adaptation. Please refer to UP EFS for details[End PR834552]

---

#### 3.30.10.7.0-2 Reception of Positive SR Update of Buffer Housekeeping Function for DC-GBR Traffic (ID: 11203969)

**3.30.10.7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11203975`)

Reception of Positive SR **[CB013961-B] If NRCELL.actServiceAwareSRPrioritization is set to False, then**[End CB013961-B] gNB-DU shall update the UL buffer housekeeping variables of the UE after scheduling the SR response UL grant **[CB008600-B-UPD001] triggered due to positive SR from the UE**[End CB008600-B-UPD001] (see 5G_UP_ALG_12751_replaced([11203952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to mitigate the impact on UL scheduling latency when the SR response UL grant is not large enough to schedule the whole packet (payloadSr, see 5G_UP_ALG_12751_replaced([11203952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) potentially waiting in UE's DC-GBR LCH buffers.**_*_** *

Conditions to be satisfied:

  * The UE has configured DC-GBR bearer and the bearer has not exceeded both the long-term GFBR (see 5G_UP_ALG_30953_replaced([11196083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the short term MDBV (see 5G_UP_ALG_30954_replaced([11196094](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) target rates.
  * **[CB008600-B-UPD001]The SR response UL grant is smaller than the grant required to schedule payloadSr. Please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_12751_replaced([11203952](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for payloadSr.**[End CB008600-B-UPD001]****



**[Before CB008600-B-UPD001]**

Update of UL Buffer Housekeeping Variables (see 5G_UP_ALG_5121_replaced([11203701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

  * rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[w] =rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[w] + max(0, payLoadSr - tbsSrGrant)
  * [Before PR821165] rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes = rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes + payLoadSr [PR821165] rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes shall be changed accordingly. [End PR821165]



where

w is the index of the LCG containing DC-GBR bearer

tbsSrGrant is the payload TBS that can be sent by the UE for the scheduled PRBs in the SR response UL grant

Rationale: The expectation is that in cases, when the complete payloadSr can allocated in a single grant (SR Resp grant), the second PUSCH grant would not be triggerred for the UE as both rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes and rrmScheduledUlData_bytes would be equal to payloadSr and UE would not be eligible for second grant even with BSR housekeeping function update. However, in scenarios when the complete payloadSr TBS cannot be allocated in a single grant triggered by positive SR, by updating the buffer housekeeping variables, the UE would again be considered for the second grant with PRBs required to satisfy (payloadSr-tbsSrGrant) TBS with DC-GBR priority even before the reception of the actual BSR. This is needed for DC-GBR service where the packet delay budget is too short (in range of 5-10 ms) and by waiting till BSR for the subsequent grant would lead to a delay higher than PDB.

**[CB008600-B-UPD001]**

Update of UL Buffer Housekeeping Variables (see 5G_UP_ALG_5121_replaced([11203701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

  * For the UE, rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[w] of the DC-GBR LCG w and rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes shall be updated so that the UE can be considered immediately eligible for the UL scheduling in the next UL slot with request class = dcGbrTraffic and grant size = payLoadSr - tbsSrGrant.
  * Implementation Proposal:
  * rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[w] shall be incremented with max(0, payLoadSr - tbsSrGrant). In this case, it is assumed that rrmScheduledUlData_bytes[w] of the DC-GBR LCG w would not have been incremented with the scheduling of the first SR response UL grant and it would still have zero bytes. Hence, the remaining pending DC-GBR data pending to be scheduled for the UE would be (rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[w] - rrmScheduledUlData_bytes[w]) = payLoadSr - tbsSrGrant.
  * rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes shall be changed accordingly.



where

w is the index of the LCG containing DC-GBR bearer

tbsSrGrant is the payload TBS that can be sent by the UE for the scheduled PRBs in the SR response UL grant

**[End CB008600-B-UPD001]**

---

### 3.30.10.8 Housekeeping of the uplink congestion information for L4S

#### 3.30.10.8.0-1 Housekeeping of the uplink congestion information for L4S Overview (ID: 11203986)

**3.30.10.8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11203992`)

Housekeeping of the uplink congestion information for L4S Uplink congestion information calculation procedure:

1\. Uplink congestion information is calculated/updated each time a new BSR for a LCG that maps a L4S DRB is received, as per 5G_UP_ALG_28198_replaced([11203998](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). This is valid even for BSR value zero.

2\. When uplink PDU for a LCG that maps L4 DRB(s) the amount of scheduling on the LCG since the last BSR is updated, as per 5G_UP_ALG_28199_replaced([11204013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN3_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

3\. Uplink congestion information is reported to L2-HI-DU (and, for info, further, to gNB CU), as per 5G_UP_ALG_28203_replaced([11204030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwi04fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

#### 3.30.10.8.0-2 Housekeeping of the uplink congestion information for L4S Calculation of uplink congestion information for a LCG that maps L4S DRB at new BSR reception (ID: 11203998)

**3.30.10.8.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11204006`)

Housekeeping of the uplink congestion information for L4S When BSR for LCG j is received in the PCell or SCell(and forwarded to SCell), if LCG j contains a L4S DRB, gNB DU of the PCell shall:

Calculate/update the input for uplink congestion information calculation:

\- minBsrL4S[j] = min(minBsrL4S[j], prevBsr[j]), if the reported BSR>0, or 0 otherwise.

\- **[PR818875] if minBsrL4S[j] corresponds to the value of the last/highest index in the BSR table, then:**

minBsrL4S[j] = min(minBsrL4S[j], BSR) **[End PR818875].**

\- prevBsr[j] = BSR[j, k'], where:

BSR[j,k’] represents the upper bound value of the BSR index k' for the LCG j, and where:

\- k’ = k - 1, if the BSR index k reported by the UE is > 0 and it’s different from the last index (depending on the BSR table applicable for that BSR) **[PR818875] or it’s the last/highest index in the BSR table but it’s the first one in serie of consecutive BSR reports all advertising the highest BSR index in the BSR table**[End PR818875]****

\- k’ = k, if the BSR index k reported by the UE is 0 or it’s the last index (depending on the BSR table applicable for that BSR) **[PR818875] and it’s not the first one in serie of consecutive BSR reports all advertising the highest BSR index in the BSR table**[End PR818875]****

\- see TS 38.321 $6.1.3.1 Buffer Status Report MAC CEs for the BSR table applicable for a BSR, depending on the format

\- maxBsrIntervalL4S_ms[j] = max (maxBsrIntervalL4S_ms[j], time since previous BSR[j])

\- maxBytesScheduledL4S[j] = max (1, maxBytesScheduledL4S[j], bytesScheduledL4SLastBsrInterval[j])

o bytesScheduledL4SLastBsrInterval[j] represents total data scheduled on LCG j calculated at PDU reception since previous BSR (slot excluded) until current BSR reporting (slot included).

If this is not the first BSR for this LCG since L4S DRB addition, [PR823770] only in case that maxBytesScheduledL4S[j] > 1 or minBsrL4S[j] = 0, [End PR823770] calculate/update uplink congestion information ulCongestionInfo [j] as follows:

\- ulCongestionInfo [j] = min (1, max (0, minBsrL4S[j] / maxBytesScheduledL4S[j] – 1 – f[j])), where:

o f[j] = max(0, l4sUlDelayTarget / maxBsrIntervalL4S_ms[j] – 1) + l4sUlSystematicOffset, where:

 l4sUlDelayTarget , l4sUlSystematicOffset (which is intended to be used to compensate for potentially systematic ulCongestionInfo calculation offset that could be seen for specific DRB configuration and traffic patterns) are NIDD parameters 

In case of CA configured, for the BSR reception on AIR slot N, the procedure shall be executed at time > N+X, where X is calculated as per 5G_UP_ALG_28199_replaced([11204013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN3_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Initializations (at L4S DRB setup or when a report that contains ulCongestionInfo [j] is transmitted - see 5G_UP_ALG_28203_replaced([11204030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwi04fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

\- minBsrL4S[j] is initialized to a value larger than or equals to highest valid BSR value (see TS 38.321 $6.1.3.1 Buffer Status Report MAC CEs)

\- maxBsrIntervalL4S_ms[j] is initialized to value 1ms ~~l4sUlDelayTarget~~.

\- maxBytesScheduledL4S[j] is initialized to value 1.

\- ulCongestionInfo [j] is initialized to a meaningless value.

\- prevBsr[j] is initialized to zero only once, at L4S DRB setup (note that the consequence is that the very first ulCongestionInfo [j] is computed zero, i.e. not marking after the very first reporting interval)

---

#### 3.30.10.8.0-3 Housekeeping of the uplink congestion information for L4S Housekeeping of total uplink data amount on a LCG that maps L4S DRB (ID: 11204013)

**3.30.10.8.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11204024`)

Housekeeping of the uplink congestion information for L4S 11204024: Housekeeping of the uplink congestion information for L4S At each PDU reception that includes data on a LCG j that maps L4S DRB(s), gNB DU of PCell shall calculate/update bytesScheduledL4SLastBsrInterval[j] such that to reflect the total data amount scheduled on LCG j in both PCell and sCell(s) in uplink since the previous positive BSR for LCG j. Data received in the same slot with BSR is counted for the previous bytesScheduledL4SLastBsrInterval[j] calculation.

In case of uplink CA (configured, but not necessarily activated, to avoid correction procedure during SCell activation/deactivation), the procedure is executed only once, considering the total data received in both PCell and SCell, to keep the consistency of the calculation. That is, PCell shall wait for the indication of the SCell, before proceeding with the update. Consequently, considering the AIR time N = SFN * 10 + floor (slot/2^u), the procedure shall be executed in the PCell at time > N+X, where X shall be calculated such that to compensate for all of the following:

\- maximum delay in L1 (including fiber and processing)

\- maximum number of slots allocated for buffered UL procedure, if applicable

\- maximum inter-scheduler communication delay over the peer interface

\- the calculation is left to implementation.

Note: In case of CA with different numerologies, for a BSR that is received in a slot X in serving cell, the last data received in other service cells that is considered assembled before the BSR assembly is the data transmitted in a slot Y that overlaps on at least one the first symbol of the slot X. Examples:

[image-0][image_desc]The image displays a table illustrating three different cases of data transmission scheduling, likely in a cellular communication context. Each case shows the allocation of time slots for a primary cell (Pcell) and a secondary cell (Scell). **Case 1:** * **Pcell FDD:** Transmits "Data0" in "slot X". * **Scell TDD:** Transmits "Data1+BSR" in "slot 2X" and "Data2" in "slot 2X+1". **Case 2:** * **Pcell FDD:** Transmits "BSR+Data0" in "slot X". * **Scell TDD:** Transmits "Data1" in "slot 2X" and "Data2" in "slot 2X+1". **Case 3:** * **Pcell FDD:** Transmits "Data0" in "slot X". * **Scell TDD:** Transmits "Data1" in "slot 2X" and "BSR+Data2" in "slot 2X+1". The table uses color coding to differentiate the data types or transmission components: yellow for "Data0" and "BSR+Data0", pink for "Data1" and "Data1+BSR", and red for "Data2" and "BSR+Data2". The term "BSR" likely refers to Buffer Status Report. The "FDD" and "TDD" notations indicate the duplexing modes of the primary and secondary cells, respectively.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16153.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

In all the cases above, only Data2 received in Case 1 or Case 2 shall not be counted in current bytesScheduledL4SLastBsrInterval and it shall be treated in the next BSR period.

---

#### 3.30.10.8.0-4 Housekeeping of the uplink congestion information for L4S Reporting uplink congestion information (to L2-Hi-DU) (ID: 11204030)

**3.30.10.8.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11204036`)

Housekeeping of the uplink congestion information for L4S Every rdL4sUciReportingPeriod (see 5G_L2_22174_replaced([11188734](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28KbW_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) milliseconds, gNB DU shall report uplink congestion information ulCongestionInfo [j] as computed with 5G_UP_ALG_28198_replaced([11203998](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for each LCG j that maps L4S DRB(s) and for which ulCongestionInfo [j] has meaningful value (meaning that UE has reported BSR at least once, including BSR = 0, since the previous calculation/reporting.

Reset ulCongestionInfo [j] to a meaningless value.

---

### 3.30.10.9 Update of Buffer Housekeeping Function for Drb Priority PUG

#### 3.30.10.9.0-1 Update of Buffer Housekeeping Function for Drb Priority PUG (ID: 11204049)

**3.30.10.9.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12824611`)

Update of Buffer Housekeeping Function for Drb Priority PUG gNB-DU shall update the UL buffer housekeeping variables of the UE while trigger the DRB priority PUG scheduling( see 5G_UP_ALG_30970_replaced([11195605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv9mFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Update of UL Buffer Housekeeping Variables (see 5G_UP_ALG_5121_replaced([11203701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): [Before PR852816]

  * rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes = rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes + _NRBTS/NRDRB_PRIO_PUG.drbPriorityPugPayloadSize_
  * rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] =rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] + _NRBTS/NRDRB_PRIO_PUG.drbPriorityPugPayloadSize_



[PR852816]

  * Calculate bufferCorr = MAX(0, rrmScheduledUlData_bytes - rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes)
  * Calculate bufferCorrPUGSched = NRBTS/NRDRB_PRIO_PUG.drbPriorityPugPayloadSize+ bufferCorr
  * Update rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x]+= bufferCorrPUGSched
  * L2PS shall recalculate the rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes.



[End PR852816]

where

x is the index of the LCG ID corresponding to DRB which associated to the selected DRB priority PUG.

_DRB_PRIO_PUG.drbPriorityPugPayloadSize is the payloadSize of the selected DRB priority PUG._

[PR852816]

Note: As rrmScheduledUlData_bytes may be updated by legacy SR or other PUG, which leading rrmScheduledUlData_bytes - rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes < 0, it is better to add the bufferCorr so that DRB PUG can be sent out correctly. One shortcoming is that, after injection fake data for DRB priority PUG and L2PS receives the PUSCH feedback for legacy SR or other PUG, since rrmScheduledUlData_bytes is updated which leading more data will be scheduled out for DRB PUG. 

[End PR852816]

gNB-DU shall update the UL buffer housekeeping variables of the UE after DRB priority PUG is scheduled out in corresponding TTI before housekeeping functionality eveluation for next TTI( see 5G_UP_ALG_5121_replaced([11203701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))(Note 1), gNB-DU shall update of UL Buffer Housekeeping Variables (see 5G_UP_ALG_5121_replaced([11203701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as below:[Before PR852816]

  * rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes = max(0, rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes - _NRBTS/NRDRB_PRIO_PUG.drbPriorityPugPayloadSize + tbSize/8 )_
  * rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] =max(0, rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] - _NRBTS/NRDRB_PRIO_PUG.drbPriorityPugPayloadSize +_ tbSize/8 )**



[PR852816]

  * rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] =max(0, rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] - bufferCorrPUGSched )



L2PS shall re-calculate the rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes.

[End PR852816]

where

tbSize can be retrieved from the HARQ process associated with this UL TB.

x is the index of the LCG ID corresponding to DRB which associated to the selected DRB priority PUG.

_DRB_PRIO_PUG.drbPriorityPugPayloadSize is the payloadSize of the selected DRB priority PUG._

[PR852816]bufferCorrPUGSched shall be the corresponding value calculated while fake data injection for corresponding DRB PUG when interval timer expired. Refer to same requirement above. [End PR852816]

Note: In order to prevent UE from scheduling again in next TTI, rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes shall be updated to make pending data as zero here.

[Before PR852816]

gNB-DU shall additional update the UL buffer housekeeping variables of the UE based on Evaluation of received data amounts in UL(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_5132_replaced([11203829](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when

  * receives a PUSCH DTX for the skippable grants, or

  * HARQ Process Failure (TB has not been received correctly after UE specific maxUlHarqTxNum value per 5G_UP_ALG_12741_replaced([11203324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) HARQ transmissions), other than PUSCH DTX for skippable grants, or,

  * DRB priority PUG has been scheduled out and still not receive any feedback from UE, but DRB selected for DRB priority PUG is removed, or

  * receives a PUSCH ACK for DRB priority PUG, or,

  * rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes = max(0, rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes - _tbSize/8 + receivedDataAmount)_

  * rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] =max(0, rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] - tbSzie/8 + receivedDataAmount)



where

tbSize can be retrieved from the HARQ process associated with this UL TB.

x is the index of the LCG ID corresponding to DRB which associated to the selected DRB priority PUG.

receivedDataAmount is the received data from UE for the DRB priroity PUG, Refer to 5G_L2_372_replaced([11151301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24hRefvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in detail. For the case of PUSCH DTX for skippable grants or HARQ process failure or corresponding DRB removed after data scheduled out, this value shall be 0.

Note: Remove the fake data from rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes when receive the feedback.

[End PR852816]

[Before PR852816]

gNB-DU shall not force the UL pending bytes(rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes - rrmScheduledUlData_bytes) which added when DRB priority PUG triggered to zero, when:

  * receives sum of total BSRs is zero after a DRB priority PUG triggered(refer to [11195595](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_6rCdoPvPEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) but not send out.



Note: it is possible that gNB-DU receives BSR=0 together with or without PUSCH data from UE before sending the trigger DRB priority PUG out after it triggered due to frame structure or other reason, gNB-DU shall not discard such DRB priority PUG if DRB priority rrmDrbPriorityDuration timer is still running. CP3 shall make an alignment for this special handling for DRB priority PUG. 

[PR852816]

Note: Correct BSR=0 handling can help to correct the UL buffer housekeeping variables.

[End PR852816]

[Before PR852816][PR848118]

gNB-DU shall update the UL buffer housekeeping variables of the UE after UL grant for below when UE DRB priority PUG duration timer is running: 

  * RLC status PDU

  * SR response with srResp priority or DRB priority




then, L2PS shall update UL Buffer Housekeeping Variables (see 5G_UP_ALG_5121_replaced([11203701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) :

  * rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes = max(0, rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes + _tbSize/8)_

  * rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] =max(0, rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] +  _tbSize/8)_




where

tbSize can be retrieved from the HARQ process associated with this UL TB.

x is the index of the LCG ID corresponding to DRB which associated to the selected DRB priority PUG. 

[End PR848118][End PR852816]

gNB-DU shall update the UL buffer housekeeping variables of the UE if it has not be sent out after DRB priority PUG triggered scheduling and

  * UE state changed from Eligible to Ineligible, or

  * L2PS receives positive SR from UE, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_31306_replaced([11203946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwiN0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), or




[PR852816]

  * HARQ failure handling for SR, when UE in SR_PENDING_WAITING_FOR_UE_RESPONSE and srSchedulingWithDrbPriorityTimer is not expired and NRCELL.actServiceAwareSRPrioritization is True, refer to [11203890](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), or



[End PR852816]

  * DRB selected for DRB priority PUG is removed



Then, L2PS shall Update of UL Buffer Housekeeping Variables (see 5G_UP_ALG_5121_replaced([11203701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) :[Before PR852816]

  * rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes = max(0, rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes - _NRBTS/NRDRB_PRIO_PUG.drbPriorityPugPayloadSize_
  * rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] =max(0, rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] - _NRBTS/NRDRB_PRIO_PUG.drbPriorityPugPayloadSize)_



[PR852816]  
rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] =max(0, rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] - bufferCorrPUGSched )  
L2PS shall recalculate the rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes.   
[End PR852816]

where

x is the index of the LCG ID corresponding to DRB which associated to the selected DRB priority PUG.

[PR852816]bufferCorrPUGSched shall be the corresponding value calculated while fake data injection for corresponding DRB PUG when interval timer expired. Refer to same requirement above. [End PR852816]

[Before PR852816]

PM counter M55308C02002 shall not be increased when DRB priority PUG triggered scheduling due to fake data is used. PM counter M55308C02002 shall be increased while L2PS receives PUSCH data(no pedding) from UE for DRB priority PUG.

[End PR852816]

Note 1: According to request, DRB priority PUG shall be scheduled only once. so after DRB priroity PUG is granted, rrmBufferedDataUlTotal_bytes and corresponding rrmBufferedDataUlLcg_bytes[x] shall be updated according to tbSize.

---

## 3.30.11 PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages

### 3.30.11.0-1 PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages UCI on PUSCH (ID: 11204067)

**3.30.11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11204073`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages Computing number of available REs for UCI and Harq-ack on PUSCH

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11204094`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages The number of REs for UCI and harq-ack depends on the number of DMRS symbols and the number of PTRS REs (see 6.3.2.4.1 of 38.212, as well as 6.4.1.1.3 and 6.4.1.2.2.1 of 38.211) and is calculated as follows:

If ulDMRSMappingType is set to TypeB: 

numOfPtrsSymbols = ( numOfPuschSymbols - numOfDmrsSymbols ) if PTRS is present, otherwise it is 0.

numOfResForUciInPtrsSymbols = (12 * numOfPrb – ceil( numOfPrb / ulPtrsFrequencyDensity )) * numOfPtrsSymbols

rrmNumResForHarqAckOnPusch = rrmNumResForUciOnPusch = (12 * numOfPrb * (numOfPuschSymbols - numOfDmrsSymbols - numOfPtrsSymbols)) + numOfResForUciInPtrsSymbol

If ulDMRSMappingType is set to TypeA:

If PTRS is present,

numberOfUciResPerSymbol = 12 * numOfPrb – ceil( numOfPrb / ulPtrsFrequencyDensity )

else

numberOfUciResPerSymbol = 12 * numOfPrb

endif

rrmNumUciResBeforeFirstDmrsSymbol = dmrsTypeAPosition * numberOfUciResPerSymbol

rrmNumResForHarqAckOnPusch = ( numOfPuschSymbols - numOfDmrsSymbols - dmrsTypeAPosition) * numberOfUciResPerSymbol

rrmNumResForUciOnPusch = ( numOfPuschSymbols - numOfDmrsSymbols) * numberOfUciResPerSymbol

where

-numOfPrb, numOfPuschSymbols, numOfDmrsSymbols, ulDMRSMappingType, dmrsTypeAPosition can be found in 5G_L2_899_replaced([11179052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27DoHPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). 5G-L2-PS will not use the PUSCH symbol of FDD PUSCH (numOfPuschSymbols) which is overlapped to periodic SRS symbol in case of FR1 UL CA when [Before CB009169-BC] TDD SCell with periodic SRS configured at the overlapped slot [CB009169-BC] overlapped TDD slot is configured with periodic SRS [End CB009169-BC] according to 5G_UP_ALG_5062_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

-ulPtrsFrequencyDensity can be found in 5G_UP_ALG_5150_replaced([11204236](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwjb_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11204101`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages Computing Number of REs for UCI and Harq-ack on PUSCH, with Transform Precoding Configured

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11204120`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages The number of REs in PUSCH for UCI and harq-ack when Transform Precoding is configured for a UE shall be derived as follows:

If ulDMRSMappingType is set to TypeB: 

rrmNumResForHarqAckOnPusch = rrmNumResForUciOnPusch = ' Number of REs in Symbols without PTRS' + ' Number of REs in Symbols with PTRS'; where

\- ' Number of REs in Symbols without PTRS' = (12 * numOfPrb) * (numOfPuschSymbols - numOfDmrsSymbols - numOfPtrsSymbols)

\- ' Number of REs in Symbols with PTRS' = numOfPtrsSymbols * [(12 * numOfPrb) - (rrmNumGroups*rrmSamplesPerGroup) ]

\- numOfPtrsSymbols = numOfPuschSymbols – numOfDmrsSymbols, if UL PTRS present, else = 0

If ulDMRSMappingType is set to TypeA:

If PTRS is present,

numberOfUciResPerSymbol = 12 * numOfPrb - rrmNumGroups * rrmSamplesPerGroup

else

numberOfUciResPerSymbol = 12 * numOfPrb

endif

rrmNumUciResBeforeFirstDmrsSymbol = dmrsTypeAPosition * numberOfUciResPerSymbol

rrmNumResForHarqAckOnPusch = ( numOfPuschSymbols - numOfDmrsSymbols - dmrsTypeAPosition) * numberOfUciResPerSymbol

rrmNumResForUciOnPusch = ( numOfPuschSymbols - numOfDmrsSymbols) * numberOfUciResPerSymbol

Notes:

\- Number of REs depends on the number of DMRS symbols and the number of PTRS REs (see 38.212 6.3.2.4.2.1 and 38.211 6.3.2.4.1.1)

\- numOfPrb, numOfPuschSymbols, numOfDmrsSymbols, ulDMRSMappingType, dmrsTypeAPosition can be found in 5G_L2_899_replaced([11179052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27DoHPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). 5G-L2-PS will not use the PUSCH symbol of FDD PUSCH (numOfPuschSymbols) which is overlapped to periodic SRS symbol in case of FR1 UL CA when [Before CB009169-BC] TDD SCell with periodic SRS configured at the overlapped SCell slot [CB009169-BC] overlapped TDD slot is configured with periodic SRS [End CB009169-BC]according to 5G_UP_ALG_5062_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

\- rrmNumGroups and rrmSamplesPerGroup are derived for Transform Precoding sampleDensity; see 5G_UP_ALG_5150_replaced([11204236](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwjb_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11204128`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages Computing number of coded modulation symbols for Harq-ack on PUSCH

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11204173`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages 11204173: PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages The number of coded modulation symbols per layer for HARQ-ACK transmission is calculated (based on 3GPP 38.212 Sections 6.3.2.4.2.1 & 6.3.2.4.1.1) as follows: If the UE is scheduled in CSI only mode: numOfCodedModSym(x) = min {ceil((x + numOfCrcAck)_BetaOffsetAck / (R * Qm)), ceil(alpha * rrmNumResForHarqAckOnPusch)} where: \- R is the target code rate for the selected MCS index and MCS table according to “6.1.4.1 Modulation order and target code rate determination” in TS 38214. \- Qm is the corresponding modulation order for the selected MCS index and MCS table according to “6.1.4.1 Modulation order and target code rate determination” in TS 38214. \- See below for other members in the formula. Else: numOfCodedModSym(x) = min {ceil((x + numOfCrcAck)_ BetaOffsetAck* rrmNumResForUciOnPusch/ numCodedBits), ceil(alpha * rrmNumResForHarqAckOnPusch)} where \- x is the number of A/N bits \- numOfCrcAck = 0 if x<=11; numOfCrcAck = 6 if 1119) \- rrmNumResForUciOnPusch and rrmNumResForHarqAckOnPush are calculated as in 5G_UP_ALG_5137_replaced([11204073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwi06PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) \- For Transform Precoding configured for a UE, \- numResForUciOnPusch and rrmNumResForHarqAckOnPush are calculated per 5G_UP_ALG_5140_replaced([11204101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwi07PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) \- numResForCsiOnPusch is calculated per 5G_UP_ALG_3668_replaced([11160657](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPpRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) \- alpha is configured by higher layer and provided by IE uciOnPuschScaling in the PsUser_UserSetupReq message. \- numCodedBits = numOfCodeBlocks * codeBlockSize (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_6874_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) \- BetaOffsetAck = value taken from 38.213 Table 9.3-1 at index given by \- IE betaOffsetACKIndex1 if x <= 2 or \- IE betaOffsetACKIndex2 if 2 < x <= 11 \- IE betaOffsetACKIndex3 if x > 11\. L2-PS shall calculate two different numbers of coded modulation symbols per layer for HARQ-ACK transmission and provide them by the corresponding IEs of UlData_PuschReceiveReq message: * If at least one HARQ A/N bit or a CSI part 1 is expected on PUSCH then IE numOfUciAckSymbols shall be set to numOfCodedModSym calculated for x = max{ 2, number of expected HARQ A/N bits }, otherwise an arbitrary value within the valid range can be selected. * If one or two HARQ A/N bits are expected on PUSCH then IE numOfUciAckSymbols1bit shall be set to numOfCodedModSym calculated for x = 1, otherwise set to 0. * If UE uses Distributed PUCCH Allocation Algorithm(5G_UP_ALG_2885_replaced([11123447](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqjhoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and (NRCELL.actK1Opt = 1 or NRCELL.actK1Opt = 2) [Before PR722443], the number of ACK/NACK bits: x, should be set to the maximum HARQ ACK/NACK bits based on DPA algorithm pre-allocation result. Note, as some slots may not be scheduled, the actual HARQ ACK/NACK bits may be different from the maximum value. [PR722443] Denote n as the maximum HARQ ACK/NACK bits based on DPA algorithm pre-allocation result; If HARQ ACK/NACK bits are multiplexed in PUSCH scheduled by DCI 0_1 1st DAI of DCI 0_1 (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4466_replaced([11178897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2y0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be considered for calculating the number of ACK/NACK bits. If n < 9 The number of ACK/NACK bits: x shall be set to {[image-0][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16172.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]+ (8 - [image-1][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16172.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]) % 4} since 1st DAI of DCI 0_1 is always set to value "11"; Else The number of ACK/NACK bits: x shall be set to {[image-2][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16172.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] + (n - [image-3][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16172.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]) % 4} since 1st DAI of DCI 0_1 is calculated with all scheduling opportunity for PCell and SCell(s) based on distributed PUCCH algorithm pre-allocation result; Else The number of ACK/NACK bits: x shall be set to [image-4][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16172.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]; Where: \- [image-5][image_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16172.006.png[/image_path][/image-5]is the total number of HARQ ACK/NACK bits based on the actual scheduling result, refers to 3gpp 38.213 chapter 9.1.3 and 5G_L2_2933_replaced([11160104](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25VJzPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End PR722443] Note, the max. values of IE numOfUciAckSymbols and IE numOfUciAckSymbols1bit given by the interface should not and must not be exceeded. An error message should appear. Note also, in FDD the max. number of Harq A/N bits per UE per slot is 1 whereas in TDD the max. number of Harq A/N bits is 8 (with 4 per CC and max. 2CC).

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11204180`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages Invalidation of UCI Report

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11204189`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages If a UCI is carried via PUSCH each component of the UCI report (HARQ, CSI Part1 and CSI Part2) shall be evaluated separately and shall be invalidated if

  * the CSI Part1 is indicated as DTX (see 5G_L1_IF_200_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or the CRC of the CSI Part1 fails the CRC check
  * the CSI Part2 is indicated as DTX (see 5G_L1_IF_200_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or the CRC of the CSI Part2 fails the CRC check
  * the CRC of the HARQ report fails the CRC check.



Note: Each component of a UCI report (HARQ, CSI Part1 and CSI Part2), which is received via PUSCH, is protected separately by a CRC if the number of UCI bits is larger than 11 UCI bits.

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11204195`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages UCI only particularities

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11204202`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages 5G-L2-PS has to free the HARQ process as soon as UCI on PUSCH corresponding to a UCI only transmission is received from 5G-L1-UL.

Though, In UCI only case, 5G-L1-UL is only expected to reply with the relevant messages (i.e. there is no PUSCH indication towards 5G-L2-LO and also there is no HARQ status towards L2-PS corresponding to PUSCH transmission). Thus, only the PUSCH receive request responses that include UCI on PUSCH related IE(s) and the DL Harq-Ack feedback are expected from 5G-L1-UL

  * Consequently, in the absence of the corresponding Harq related message corresponding to the PUSCH reception, selection of the trigger for freeing the Harq process is left to implementation.
  * Additionally, 5G-L2-PS Shall ignore the received PUSCH measurements and not use them in the LA, irrespective of the reception status of the CSI.

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `11204208`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages Multiplexing HARQ-ACK information in PUSCH for UL CA UE

---

**3.30.11.0-1.0-6.0-5**  (ID: `13391586`)

PUSCH Receive Request to 5G-L1-UL and Handling of the related Response Messages For an UL CA UE configured with long PUCCH but not configured with simultaneousPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17, if a long PUCCH with HARQ-ACK information only overlaps with a PUSCH on a serving cell and in a slot, the HARQ-ACK information is multiplexed in the PUSCH.

For a UL CA UE configured with long PUCCH but not configured with simultaneousPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17, if a long PUCCH with HARQ-ACK information overlaps with multiple PUSCHs in a slot on respective serving cells, the HARQ-ACK information is multiplexed in one of the multiple PUSCHs:

If aperiodic CSI is not multiplexed in any of the multiple PUSCHs,

If PUSCH is scheduled by configured grants on PCell and PUSCH is scheduled by DCI format on SCell

The HARQ-ACK information is multiplexed in a PUSCH on SCell.

Else

The HARQ-ACK information is multiplexed in a PUSCH of the serving cell with the smallest serving cell index.

else if aperiodic CSI is multiplexed in one of the multiple PUSCHs, the HARQ-ACK information is multiplexed in the PUSCH which aperiodic CSI is multiplexed in.

For the UE configured with FDD + FR1 TDD UL CA, if a long PUCCH with HARQ-ACK information on PCell over a single slot only overlaps with the PUSCH transmission on SCell in the multiple slots, the HARQ-ACK information is multiplexed in the PUSCH on SCell in the earliest slot overlapping with the PUCCH.

For the UE configured with:  
\- FR1 TDD + FDD UL CA [CB014067-A] 2CC, or  
\- one FDD and two contiguous intra-band TDD FR1 CCs with TDD PCell UL CA 3CC: [End CB014067-A]

  * If UE is not configured with simultaneousPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17, there is no PUSCH transmission over FDD SCell in UL overlapping slots;  
[CB014067-A] For 3CC UL CA, the rules described above and the description in ([11358798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucjiC_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) determine, whether UCI in the overlapping UL slots will be transmitted:  
* on PUSCH or on PUCCH in the TDD PCell, or  
* on PUSCH in the TDD SCell [End CB014067-A]
  * If UE is configured with simultaneousPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17, [CB014067-A] For 2CC UL CA [End CB014067-A] UCI will be transmitted either on PUSCH or on PUCCH in PCell in the overlapping UL slots;   
[CB014067-B] For 3CC UL CA, the rules described above and the description in ([11159986](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktNNBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)[, 11358798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucjiC_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) determine, whether UCI in the overlapping UL slots will be transmitted:  
* on PUSCH or on PUCCH in the TDD PCell, or  
* on PUSCH in the TDD SCell [End CB014067-B]



UCI over FDD SCell PUSCH is not possible.

For more details see 3GPP 38.213 Chapter 9.

Notes:

  * PCell always has the smallest serving cell index.
  * Aperiodic CSI over PUSCH is supported on PCell only.
  * Only one PUCCH group (PUCCH over PCell only) is supported.
  * PUSCH scheduled by configured grants is supported only on PCell.

---

### 3.30.11.1 Uplink PTRS Configuration to L1

#### 3.30.11.1.0-2 Uplink PTRS Configuration to L1 Transform Precoding is not enable (ID: 11204248)

**3.30.11.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11204255`)

Uplink PTRS Configuration to L1 If uplink PTRS time density is present, the uplink PTRS configuration is related to schedule MCS and to the Time Density Table , with TimeDensity[i] corresponding to entry of index i in the uplink PTRS time density list :

If MCS is < TimeDensity[0] 

PTRS is not present

If TimeDensity[0] is not equal to TimeDensity[1] and TimeDensity[0] <=MCS< TimeDensity[1]

ulPtrsTimeDensity =4

If TimeDensity[1] is not equal to TimeDensity[2] and TimeDensity[1] <=MCS< TimeDensity[2] ulPtrsTimeDensity =2

If TimeDensity[2] is not equal maxMcsTimeDensity and TimeDensity[2] <=MCS < maxMcsTimeDensity 

ulPtrsTimeDensity =1

If uplink PTRS time density is absent, ulPtrsTimeDensity =1.

If uplink PTRS frequency density is present, the uplink PTRS configuration is related to the number of scheduled PRB (numOfScheduledPrb) and to the Frequency Density Table , with FrequencyDensity[i] corresponding to entry of index i in the uplink PTRS frequency density list :

If numOfScheduledPrb is < FrequencyDensity[0] :

PTRS is not present

If FrequencyDensity[0] is not equal FrequencyDensity[1] and FrequencyDensity[0] <= numOfScheduledPrb <FrequencyDensity[1]:

ulPtrsFrequencyDensity =2

If FrequencyDensity[1] <= numOfScheduledPrb :

ulPtrsFrequencyDensity =4

If uplink PTRS frequency density is absent, ulPtrsFrequencyDensity =2.

If PTRS is considered as not present either due to TimeDensity check or Frequency density check, 5G-L2-PS shall considered the uplink PTRS is not present for PUSCH transmission and set accordingly the IE ulPtrsFlag into the UlData_PuschReceiveReq message.

in other case 5G-L2-PS shall configure UlData_PuschReceiveReq message with the computed ulPtrsFrequencyDensity , ulPtrsTimeDensity and set the IE ulPtrsFlag to present.

[CB008662-A-UPD004] If reserved MCS(refer to 5G_UP_IF_1897_replaced([11515718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tUO_Wvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))/2260) is used for a retransmission, the MCS value shall be obtained from initial transmission to determine PTRS presence and time density.[End CB008662-A-UPD004]

---

#### 3.30.11.1.0-3 Uplink PTRS Configuration to L1 Transform Precoding is enabled (ID: 11204263)

**3.30.11.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11204269`)

Uplink PTRS Configuration to L1 When a UE is in Transform Precoder Enabled state (see 5G_UP_ALG_3270_replaced([11140467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krt_UPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UL PTRS for Transform Precoding is enabled, the uplink PTRS behavior is per 38.214 6.2.3.2 “PTR-RS reception procedure when transform precoding is enabled ”.

Behavior is the same as in 5G_UP_ALG_5152_replaced([11204248](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwjb_vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (Transform Precoding disabled), but with the following exceptions/notes:

\- Time density (LPT-RS ) is always 1. No MCS table needed. See 5G_L2_909_replaced([11179231](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27E2OvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

\- FrequencyDensity is not used

\- Sample density is present. See 5G_L2_2829_replaced([11241071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3BjRoPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

\- Both Time density and Sample density shall always be provided to L1 if UL PTRS is configured; absent otherwise

Notes:

\- CP-RT notifies L2-RT of UL PTRS for Transform Precoding parameters and activation status at cell setup/reconfiguration

\- UL PTRS Sample Density defines NumOfGroups and NumOfSamplesPerGroup per 38.214 Table 6.2.3.2-1, where: NRB0, NRB1, NRB2, NRB3, NRB4 are the sampleDensity parameters provided by C-Plane and NRB equals numOfScheduledPrb

\- IF UL PTRS for Transform Precoding is not active (per CP-RT), L2-PS notifies L1 in PUSCH receive request

---

### 3.30.11.2 Uplink BLER Configuration to L1

#### 3.30.11.2.0-1 Uplink BLER Configuration to L1 (ID: 11204283)

**3.30.11.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11204290`)

Uplink BLER Configuration to L1 L2 PS shall indicate the different BLER flags to L1 in PuschReceiveReq message depending on UE working on different mode such as BR (Basic Reliability) mode (such as target BLER 10%), HR (High Reliability) mode (such as target BLER 1%), or even for future extension to VHR mode for very high reliability service (such as 0.01%)

Loki SINR Compensation [Before CB010981-F]

The gNB-DU shall request, within every PUSCH receive request sent to a L1 Loki HW variant, that compensated SINR be provided if NRCELL/actPuschPostEqSinrCompensation = True. [End CB010981-F]

**3.30.11.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12576371`)

PUSCH CSI Part2 DTX detection combined with CSI Part1 DTX result The gNB-DU shall request, within every PUSCH receive request sent to a L1 non-ABIL HW variant, that 'AndCsi1p85' method shall be applied to PUSCH CSI Part2 DTX result if NRCELL/actPuschActCsiPart2DtxEnhancement= True.

### 3.30.11.5 Uplink CoMP Basic Configuration to L1

#### 3.30.11.5.0-1 Uplink CoMP Basic Configuration to L1 Request UL CoMP Basic to L1 (ID: 12742863)

**3.30.11.5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12742864`)

Uplink CoMP Basic Configuration to L1 For each UE selected for UL CoMP PUSCH data reception ([12740825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_G7GPo3v5EfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), the serving cell shall indicate below information in the PUSCH receive request to L1, 

  * UL CoMP PUSCH data reception request
  * the subcell ID of the associated helper cell



For each UE selected for UL CoMP SINR measurement ([12742860](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_pHagAHyeEfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), the serving cell shall indicate below information in the PUSCH receive request to L1, 

  * UL CoMP SINR Measurement request
  * the subcell ID list of the associated candidate cell(s)



Note: A UE is selected either for data reception or SINR measurement in one PUSCH receive request as per ([12742860](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_pHagAHyeEfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

---

#### 3.30.11.5.0-2 Uplink CoMP Basic Configuration to L1 Response UL CoMP Basic from L1 (ID: 12744772)

**3.30.11.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12744773`)

Uplink CoMP Basic Configuration to L1 If NRCELL.actNrUlComp='basic', for the PUSCH request sent to L1, L2 serving cell shall expect the response with the instantaneous L1 short-term average pre-equalized SINR measurement for (the every UE, the serving cell). See [12742905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_c_FJUnyrEfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for details and subsequent handling.

For the request UL CoMP PUSCH data reception to L1 in [12742863](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6OPBknyfEfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), L2 serving cell shall expect the response with the instantaneous L1 short-term average pre-equalized SINR measurement for (the every selected UE, the helper cell). See [12742905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_c_FJUnyrEfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for details and subsequent handling.

For the request UL CoMP SINR measurement to L1 in [12742863](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6OPBknyfEfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), L2 serving cell shall expect the response with the instantaneous L1 short-term average pre-equalized SINR measurement for (the every selected UE, the candidate cell(s)). See [12742905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_c_FJUnyrEfCiUemk-K-C4g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for details and subsequent handling.

## 3.30.12 Handling of eCPRI fields

### 3.30.12.0-1 Handling of eCPRI fields numCeAxcIndex Configuration (ID: 11204301)

**3.30.12.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11204314`)

Handling of eCPRI fields When actOranFDD is TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_7230_replaced([11297583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqH68_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the parameter numCeAxcIndex for an UL grant (PUSCH/PRACH/SRS/PUCCH) will depend on the subcell in which the grant will be received and will be equal to the number of antennas supported by the subcell. The supported subcells and the corresponding number of antennas is specified in 5G_UP_ALG_14_replaced([11094230](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaogfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For example, when actOranFDD is TRUE (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_7230_replaced([11297583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqH68_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if an UL grant for PUSCH is received at subcell A2, numCeAxcIndex will be set to 2. On the other hand, if it is received at subcell A4, numCeAxcIndex will be set to 4.

Note that the [Before PR796340] parameter [PR796340] flag [End PR796340] actOranFDD is only applicable for an FDD cell [PR796340] (refer 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_7230_replaced([11297583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqH68_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), SRAN_SiSo_FH.969_replaced([10949785](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3XMNd_uyEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End PR796340].

---

### 3.30.12.0-2 Handling of eCPRI fields ceAxCIndex configuration (ID: 11204321)

**3.30.12.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11204327`)

Handling of eCPRI fields For all UL channels other than PRACH, the setting of ceAxCIndex (in UlData_PuschReceiveReq, UlData_PucchReceiveReq, UlData_SrsReceiveReq) follows the selection of spatial streams in CPRI when a spatial stream is assigned to one UE.

---

### 3.30.12.0-4 Handling of eCPRI fields eCPRI Frequency Domain BF UL Region Handling in 7-2a (ID: 11204341)

**3.30.12.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `12791590`)

Handling of eCPRI fields This section is applicable when**1) FD beamforming is enabled (ulEcpriFdBeamforming = True, (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling =true and NRBTS.actEcpri72e = disabled [CB008577-A] or 2) FDD BF enabled: [End CB008577-A]**

Frequency Domain (FD) BF support in UL with Radio Units of Made ES1 chipsets has a limitation and the FD beams are organized in form of ‘beamforming regions’ with a total of max rdMaxNumUlFdBfRegion (5G_L2_1336_replaced([11182818](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27ee3PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) regions). The smallest granularity of a region is 1 PRB in frequency and 1 OFDM symbol in time domain. 

[CB008577-A]  
Note: FDD BF is not supported on the Made ES1 radio units. Below requirement is currently applied also on FDD BF because it improves L1 processing performance (merging PUCCH requests with the same beam into single eCPRI section).  
[End CB008577-A]

For eCPRI FD beams pattern handling, L2PS tracks the region for each FD allocations in UL and computes the corresponding region ID (0 to rdMaxNumUlFdBfRegion-1) per symbol to set eCpriSectionID parameter defined in the UL receive request messages. The eCpriSectionID computation at L2PS is done for [CB008577-A] not FDD BF cells: [End CB008577-A]UlData_PuschReceiveReq, UlData_PucchReceiveReq , UlData_RimReceiveReq andUlData_SrsReceiveReq. [CB008577-A] FDD BF cells: UlData_PucchReceiveReq, so only PUCCH uses region and rdMaxNumUlFdBfRegion can totally used by PUCCH. [End CB008577-A] 

In cases, when cyclic shift is used (but the time & frequency of the allocation are the same), the regionID shall be the same for both sections.

Note:

 the region assignmenment considers only non-overlapped FD resource allocation during FDM (no UL-MUMIMO).

 No strict order is applied in the region ID assignment

 if Frequency Hopping (FH) is supported and enabled, the eCpriSectionID will be an array and one eCpriSectionID value shall be computed for each Frequency Hop region.

With introduction of section extension type 12 , it is possible to allocate non-contiguous beamforming regions, ie. there can be more than 1 allocation per symbol using the same region. Two allocations are in the same region, when the same patternIds are applied to all streams of the allocations in the same way for (each of the streams has the exact same patternID).

Practically, the non-contiguous allocations of the same region can be Long PUCCH resources, that are requesting beamforming over SSB beams. For details, see 5G_UP_ALG_2214_replaced([11113079](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpaSKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

· If NRCELL.actNrRim is not equal to 0(corresponding to disabled),

when 5G-L2-PS requests the remote interference monitoring based on a selected beam pattern ID in consecutive _6 unscheduled PRB regions (see 5G_UP_ALG_9328_replaced([11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), one eCpriSectionId value shall be allocated for RI monitoring requesting_

If NRCELL.actNrRim is not equal to 0 and when 5G-L2-PS requests the RIM RS detecting in a dedicated region(see 5G_UP_ALG_13156_replaced([11161857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), one eCpriSectionId value shall be allocated for RIM RS detecting request**.**

same eCpriSectionID shall be allocated to both RIM RS and RIM RI with the same beam combination.

 If NRCELL.actUlMuMimo is enabled:

When the paired UEs which are scheduled in the same 4RX IRC MU receiver (5G_UP_ALG_5163_replaced([11204341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwkDDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), for PRB regions where only UE1 is scheduled, second best beam of UE1 might be considered (see 5G_UP_ALG_11355_replaced([11104143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5PNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). 

When FD beamforming is disabled (ulEcpriFdBeamforming == False, (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))), the eCpriSectionID for mentioned messages (UlData_PuschReceiveReq, UlData_PucchReceiveReq UlData_RimReceiveReq and UlData_SrsReceiveReq) is filled according with IDT specification: all bits are set to '1'.

When FD beamforming is enabled (ulEcpriFdBeamforming = True, (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) and NRBTS.actOranCUPlanePhase1==True, the eCpriSectionID is filled in such way, in order to achieve ORAN compliance (but the MADE ES1 workaround is not supported). ORAN requires eCPRI FCP sectionId values to be unique per slot per eAxC. If NRCELL.actLongPucchF1F3BfOnOranExtT6 is True, every Long PUCCH resource has the eCpriSectionId allocated individually.

---

### 3.30.12.0-5 Handling of eCPRI fields eCPRI Frequency Domain BF UL Region Handling in 7-2e (ID: 11204367)

**3.30.12.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11204373`)

Handling of eCPRI fields This section is applicable when FD beamforming is enabled (ulEcpriFdBeamforming = True, (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling =true and NRBTS.actEcpri72e != disabled

The number of ‘beamforming regions’ limitations shall only work on PUCCH channel in uplink side. L2PS tracks the region for each FD allocations in UL PUCCH and computes the corresponding region ID (0 to rdMaxNumUlFdBfRegion-1)

NOTE: eCpriSectionID is not used for PUSCH in 7-2e RRUs anymore. The parameter rdMaxNumUlFdBfRegion is defined in 5G_L2_1336_replaced([11182818](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27ee3PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.30.12.0-6 Handling of eCPRI fields eCPRI Long PUCCH allocations (ID: 11204379)

**3.30.12.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `11204400`)

Handling of eCPRI fields 11204400: Handling of eCPRI fields If ulEcpriFdBeamforming==True (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) AND NRCELL.pucchModeSelect==longPucch, L2PS informs the L1 of the Long PUCCH allocation in time/frequency in a following way.

L2PS shall build 2 or 4 eCPRI Resources allocation sets without any RB overlapping between each set. A set consists of one or several consecutive RBs. Each set is defined by:

  * [Before PR746425] A Resource Block (RB) equal to the Common Resource Block (as long as the carrier offset is not supported) [PR746425] Index of Common Resource Block [End PR746425] where the considered PUCCH region is starting (startPrb).
  * A number of consecutive RB(s) (numPrb). To deactivate a PUCCH region, the numPrb must be set to 0.
  * the eCpriSectionId is filled according to the requirement 5G_UP_ALG_5163_replaced([11204341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwkDDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).



To build the expected PUCCH resource allocation sets (2 or 4), the L2-PS first builds the expected non overlapped RBs used by the dedicated PUCCH and if expected in the slot by the common PUCCH. The L2-PS builds two types of PUCCH combinations at the cell setup, one with only the dedicated PUCCH and another one with both PUCCH (dedicated and common). The expected result is 1 or 2 or 3 or 4 sets of consecutive RBs per PUCCH combination. For the odd number (1 or 3) of sets (see an example below), the 5G-L2-PS split any set with more than one RB in two parts to get an even number of sets (2 or 4). When the even number of sets are defined and their characteristics (see above), the 5G-L2-PS fills first the 2 dedicated PUCCH resources allocation (lower and upper) with the 2 first sets previously defined. If 2 more sets are need, the 2 common PUCCH resources allocation (lower and upper) in the static long pucch config ecpri structure are used, otherwise, they are deactivated.

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the splitting of Resource Blocks (RBs) for PUCCH (Physical Uplink Control Channel) in a cellular network. It is divided into two main sections, each demonstrating a different scenario. The top section, labeled "Dedicated PUCCH," shows two initial sets of RBs, Set₀ and Set₁. It states that if there are two sets of consecutive RBs, no additional action is taken. However, if there is only one set of consecutive RBs with more than one RB, it is split into two smaller sets, labeled Set₀ and Set₁. The bottom section, which includes a legend differentiating "Common PUCCH" (yellow) and "Dedicated PUCCH" (blue hatched), presents a more complex scenario. It starts with three sets of consecutive RBs: Set₀, Set₁, and Set₂. The text explains that one set must be split into two parts to achieve four sets. Additionally, any set with more than one RB can be split into two smaller sets. This leads to several possible outcomes shown below the initial configuration. The possible splits are presented with "Or" connectors: * **First "Or" branch:** * The initial three sets are shown. * Below this, there are two possibilities: * Set₀ is split into Set₀ and Set₁, and Set₂ remains as is. * Set₁ is split into Set₁ and Set₂, and Set₀ and Set₂ remain as is. * **Second "Or" branch:** * This branch also starts with the initial three sets. * Below this, there are two possibilities: * Set₀ is split into Set₀ and Set₁, and Set₂ is split into Set₂ and Set₃. * Set₁ is split into Set₁ and Set₂, and Set₂ is split into Set₂ and Set₃. The diagram uses visual representations of RBs as horizontal bars, with different colors and hatching to denote common or dedicated PUCCH and the sets they belong to. The "Frequency (CRB)" label indicates that the horizontal axis represents frequency. The text within the image is: Dedicat ed PUCCH CRB Set Set1 2 sets of consecutive PBs are Defined: no addtional adion Only one set of consecutive Pes with more than 1 PB Split Set Sety Common PUCCH Dedicated PUCCH CPB Set Set1 Setz 3 sets of consecutive PBs are defined: one set must be split in 2 parts to have 4 sets. Any Set with more than 1 RB could be split in two smaller sets. Split Set Set Set Setz Or Set Set, Set Setz Or Set Set1 Setz Set Frequency (CPE) Frequency (CPB)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16205.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

When NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc='true', the configuration of PUCCH position changes dynamically at every slot (see 5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced([11199486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOEw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If, in a given slot, there is no Common PUCCH scheduled, the numPrb fields are set to zero. 

For Long PUCCH, when PUCCH Format 3 beamforming is enabled (5G_UP_ALG_2215_replaced([11113083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpaSLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), staticLongPucchConfigEcpri structure does not need to be filled. L1 shall use the patternId/eCpriSectionId from each individual PUCCH resource filled in UlData_PucchReceiveReq.

Note: The L1 behaviour is modified under CB007731 eCPRI update, which is prerequisite to PUCCH Format 3 beamforming, so no need to further modify this.

---

### 3.30.12.0-7 Handling of eCPRI fields PUCCH Boundaries When UL Fragmentation is Enabled (ID: 11204407)

**3.30.12.0-7.0-4**  (ID: `12791592`)

Handling of eCPRI fields **Handling Dedicated PUCCH**

In 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2526_replaced([11297475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqHT4PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), it will be specified whether UL fragmentation for Uplane packets is enabled or disabled over the fronthaul interface. If UL fragmentation is enabled the L2-PS will send the start and end PRBs of the scheduled dedicated PUCCH regions to L1. This can be explained as follows:

Depending on the number of dedicated BWPs configured, each allocation of PRBs for dedicated PUCCH happens for upper/lower part of the dedicated BWP, and for before and after PUCCH frequency hop.  


A PUCCH fragment is characterized by its boundaries which in turn is specified by a start and end PRB.

For any dedicated PUCCH region (upper/lower and before/after hop), during dedicated PUCCH scheduling, following areas are considered:

SR PRBs

HARQ A/N PRBs

pCSI PRBs

or, if NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 is set to true, the PUCCH areas looks as in the following:

SRBR PRBs (PRBs in the SR Basic Region)

HARQ A/N PRBs

3\. pCSI PRBs + SRCX PRBs (PRBs in the SR Capacity eXtention region)

Please note that the SRCX region is added dynamically on a base of the need of cell load (number of UE camped in the cell) and/or the number of low latency services required by UE which increase the SR resources consumption. When it is present, it is located above the last pCSI PRB utilized in the slot.

C_plane includes the SRCX PRBs in its computations of the number of the PRBs to be reserved in the pCSI region. (For example, if the pCSI PRB computation determines 14 PRB are needed for pCSI and 2 PRBs are needed for SRCX, the PRBs reserved to the pCSI+SRCX region are 16).

Each of these above mentioned areas are bounded by a start PRB and an end PRB. These boundaries will be calculated by L2-PS based on 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_9180_replaced([11310801](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrS_rfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In each of these areas, if there is a single PRB scheduled, the whole area, including the boundaries, are included in a single dedicated PUCCH fragment.

If the dedicated PUCCH consists of PRBs that belong to different areas, a single dedicated PUCCH fragment consists of the start PRB of one region, the end PRB of the other region, and all the other PRBs in between, with the start and end PRBs specifying the boundaries of the fragment. These boundaries will be sent to L1.

The location of dedicated PUCCH fragments in a slot depends on how the PUCCH region and its subareas (SR, HARQ, and pCSI or, if NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2 is set to true, SRBR, HARQ and pCSI+SRCX) **are located.**

**Handling Common PUCCH**

If UL fragmentation is enabled, the gNB-DU shall create common PUCCH fragments based on the PRBs in common PUCCH that are scheduled, and while creating the fragments the gNB-DU shall ensure that all the PRBs that are actually scheduled in common PUCCH are included in the fragment and the number of empty PRBs in a common PUCCH fragment per OFDM symbol is as less as possible.

Note: For common PUCCH resource usage, please refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4582_replaced([11187231](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvZlZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Handling FD-BF and non-BF Long PUCCH:**

The information about PUCCH boundaries will be passed to L1 for both FD-BF and non-BF long PUCCH (irrespective of NRCELL.actLongPucchPhase2 is TRUE or FALSE).

**Handling NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc = TRUE:**

When NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc==TRUE, then the pCSI boundaries become dynamic

and need to be calculated slot by slot. An example showing the calculations of the PUCCH boundaries is given below.

**Handling NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = TRUE:**

If PUCCH resource optimisation is enabled (NRCELL.actLongPucchResourceOpt = true) and NRCELL.reqMinNumPuschPrb = 0 , the same set of PRBs can be used by SR and HARQ. In that case, the HARQ region boundary will also include those PRBs which are used by SR.

-

---

The design of the fragments are done to ensure that the number of empty PRBs are always within the **[CB013151-B]** **Case 5: NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc== TRUE, NRCELL.actDynUlResAllocPhase2== TRUE and NRCELL.actPowerSavingBWP== False** In this case, we have the following representation of dedicated BWP, which is the same as in case 2. The end PRB needs to be dynamically calculated slot by slot and it may be part of the pCSI or HARQ or SRBR region depending on the allocated PUCCH resources in the slot. The full PUCCH region may be allocable to PUSCH if unused by PUCCH. The pCSI region contain the SRCX region as well while the SRBR region is always made by two PRBs only. This case is particular configuration of Case 6, where the PUCCH in case of multiple dedicated BW parts, is not one side of the BW but it is moved toward BW the center. There is no difference in the behavior of L2-PS with respect to above case 4, but it is important to mention here that the free PUCCH PRB are allocated only if adjacent to the PUSCH located on the left side of this picture. |   
Free PUCCH PRB possibly adjacent to the PUSCH in the right side of above picture stays not allocated. |   
In case no PRACH PRBs and no PUCCH PRBs are used in a slot, those PRBs will be assigned to the PUSCH region toward the cell BW center, while the second PUSCH region on the edge (right edge on above picture) stays separated and cannot be allocated to the same UE. |
