## 3.30.13 Measurements for L2 Load Balancing for Direct Spectrum Sharing (DSS)

### 3.30.13.0-1 Measurements for L2 Load Balancing for Direct Spectrum Sharing (DSS) Requirements moved to Section 3.21.4, Thomas Deiss, 09-02-2022 (ID: 11204522)

## 3.30.14 Measurement of eTh for FR1 UL CA Data Split

### 3.30.14.0-1 Measurement of eTh for FR1 UL CA Data Split Estimate Throughput for UL CA Data Split (ID: 11204535)

**3.30.14.0-1.0-8**  (ID: `13455377`)

Measurement of eTh for FR1 UL CA Data Split 5G-L2-PS shall provide the cumulated estimated average throughput _eThav of all CA eligible DRBs of FR1 UL CA UE per carrier for BSR split purpose._

For UE _k,_

MAC PS shall calculate a potential Transport Block Size called _estimatedFullBufferTbs, estimated based on unlimited data availability as_

_estFullBufferTbsPerCarrier (i, k, t) = Sum_over__ j{weightOfBearer(j,k,t) }/ sumOfWeightsOverAllEligibleBearersOnCarrier(i,t) * maxEstFullBufferTbsPerCarrier(i,k,t);**

where

_\- i =index of carrier_

_\- j=index of CA eligible DRB in LCG containing only CA eligible DRBs_

  * t=slot the calculation relates to



_\- maxEstFullBufferTbsPerCarrier(i,k,t) = TBS (max_(0, maxPRBOnCarrier[i,t]), MCS[i,k,t], Rank[i,k,t]),**

where

  * _maxPRBOnCarrier[i,t] is the maximum number PRBs available for PUSCH in cell i at slot t (refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4876_replaced([11196884](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGI9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));_

  * TBS computation is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5084_replaced([11203344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), while using proposed MCS and rank for this carrier.




Wrt. number of symbols, number of DMRS symbols, etc., the current settings of this user or this cell should be used for TBS calculation.

Wrt. SRS symbols, the decision if to take them into account or not is left to EFS - the easier choice should be taken. This will cause an error in the calculation, however _eThav does not need to be very accurate. The error caused by trying to look into future wrt. throughput, while not knowing the future load and interference situation, is much bigger._

If enhanced slice aware scheduler is deactivated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

_\- weightOfBearer(j) is the scheduling weight of the bearer,_

Else, _\- weightOfBearer(j) is the combined weight of the bearer, which is equal to the multiplication of the RG weight (see 5G_UP_ALG_23167_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the scheduling weight. While_

_\- sumOfWeightsOverAllEligibleBearersOnCarrier(i) is the sum of_ weightOfBearer of all non-GBR DRBs of all 5G UEs with non-empty buffer _in CS1 configured in the same subcells as the UE_ **

_eThav(i, k, t)=_ estFullBufferTbsPerCarrier (i, k, t)  _/ SlotDuration,_ **

where _SlotDuration is the length of the slot._

The _eTh_ av _shall be scaled according to the below formula:_ **

[image-0][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features, text, or objects.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16221.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features, text, or objects.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16221.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16221.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]

If the FR1 Ul CA UE is in power split mode specified in 5G_UP_ALG_10871_replaced([11119486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[image-3][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features, text, or objects.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16221.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]

else if scheduling is suspended on cell i in Ul overlapping slots (see 5G_UP_ALG_10921_replaced([11119951](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or cell i is FDD SCell for a UE configured with FR1 TDD + FDD or FR1 TDD + TDD + FDD (with intra-band contiguous TDD CCs with TDD PCell) UL CA and PUSCH can't be scheduled in the overlapping slot (UE power insufficient for parallel UL transmission in overlapping UL slots, see [12882346](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_cGa14bR4EfCQT7hYUxERAg); or UE is not configured with simultaneousPUCCH-PUSCH-SamePriority-r17) or cell i is FDD SCell for a UE configured with FR1 TDD + FDD UL Tx switching option 1**[CB014067-C]** or cell i is FDD SCell for a UE configured with FR1 TDD PCell + FR1 TDD SCell + FDD SCell UL Tx switching option 1 **[End CB014067-C] or cell i is FDD PCell for a UE configured with FDD + TDD FR1 UL Tx switching option 1**

eThOlpSlot(i,k, t) = 0;

else

[image-4][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features, text, or objects.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16221.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]

[image-5][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features, text, or objects.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16221.006.png[/image_path][/image-5]

else

[image-6][image_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16221.007.png[/image_path][/image-6]

Rational: In case the number of UEs in candidate set 1 of Ul scheduler is above the maximum number of UEs to be FDMed in UL, it's hard to evaluate the impact to eTh for PRB limitation introduced by small BWP, split UE TX power and etc. The reason is that the UE can get more scheduling chances according to TD PF metric defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4850_replaced([11195809](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), though it is scheduled with PRB limitation. In case Ul buffer is unbalanced between PCell and Scell, Ul transmission buffer split will be triggered (see 5G_UP_ALG_10281_replaced([11122608](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqf3TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to mitigate the issue.

Where:

  * numOfUesInCandSet1: the number of UEs in candidate set 1 of Ul scheduler;

  * rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl: the maximum number of UEs to be FDMed in UL, see 5G_UP_ALG_10654_replaced([11199273](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));




**\- eThNonOlpSlot: the estimated throughput based on the PRB constraints in non overlapping slots;**

**\- eThOlpSlot: the estimated throughput basd on the PRB constraints in overlapping slots;**

  * non overlapping slots and overlapping slots are defined in 5G_UP_ALG_10871_replaced([11119486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

  * maxPRBOnCarrier, sum_over_j{weightOfBearer(i,j,k)} and sumOfWeightsOverAllEligibleBearersOnCarrier: see above;

  * numPrbSplitPower: for PCell, equal to maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower(refer to 5G_UP_ALG_10883_replaced([11119896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))); for SCell, equal to the number of PRBs for current active BWP on Scell.

  * numPrbActiveBWP: the number of PRB for current active BWP.

  * numNonOlpSlots: the number of available non overlapping slots for PUSCH scheduling in a slot pattern;

  * numOlpSlots: the number of available overlapping slots for PUSCH scheduling in a slot pattern;

  * numOfPUSCHSlotsInSlotPattern = numNonOlpSlots + numOlpSlots ;

  * numOfPUSCHSlotsInSlotPattern: the number of available slots for PUSCH scheduling in a slot pattern;

  * available slots for PUSCH scheduling is specified in 5G_UP_ALG_2407_replaced([11115721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpw3ffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

  * rrmSlotPatternLength is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2295_replaced([11114654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpmfbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));




If NRCELL.actUlMuMimo **is configured to true,**

_eThav(i, k, t) = max{_ eThav(i, k, t), rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i, k, t]}**

Where:

rrmAvgUlUeDataRate_BytesPerSlot[i, k, t] refer to 5G_UP_ALG_4859_replaced([11195975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bSfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

**If NRBTS.actUlAmbr is configured to "SA"**

_eThav(i, k, t) = min{_ eThav(i, k, t), ulUeAmbr}**

Where:

ulUeAmbr is configured by the higher layer (5G_UP_ALG_19973_replaced([11205469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwpimPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

_eTh_ av _shall be re-calculated whenever need to be updated before the UL buffer split according 5G_UP_ALG_9591_replaced([11122888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqhFYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))._**

CA eligible DRB refers to 5G_UP_10576_replaced([11358417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uchs1_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

## 3.30.15 PUSCH Slot Aggregation (SAg)

### 3.30.15.0-1 PUSCH Slot Aggregation (SAg) SAg Overview (ID: 11204726)

**3.30.15.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13420498`)

PUSCH Slot Aggregation (SAg) 13420498: PUSCH Slot Aggregation (SAg) Informative artifact.

The objectives of PUSCH Slot Aggregation (SAg) are:

 Increase the uplink cell coverage for VoNR [CB014699-D] and Critical VoNR [End CB014699-D] UEs by using PUSCH transmission repetitions

 Decrease the PDCCH usage by using one uplink grant to instruct the UE to send multiple transmissions

For an overview of the SAg user plane functionality see 5G_UP_7633_replaced([11365877](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udWzSfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The figure below shows how a SAg On state interacts with PUSCH DFT-s-OFDM states (Transform Precoder or TP for short). The diagram is for illustration only and may not reflect implementation at gNB-DU. The key points from the interaction:

  * SAg On always has TP On
  * If UE is in SAg On state and VoNR [CB014699-D] and/or Critical VoNR [End CB014699-D] DRB is released, UE remains in TP On state until changed due to SINR.
  * UE can return to SAg On directly upon VoNR/[CB014699-D] Critical VoNR [End CB014699-D] addition or through a SINR based path to TP Off, folowed by VoNR addition and then SINR based switch to SAg On. If UE starts with VoNR and TP On (for example due to VoNR HO and CB009849-A), then it can switch to SAg On due to SINR
  * CB008148 interacts with SAg in the following ways:
  * UE can start a connection in TP On (SAg Off) state (#3 in diagram) for DCI 0_1 when that part of CB008148 is enabled and transition to SAg On state (#6) upon VoNR/[CB014699-D] Critical VoNR [End CB014699-D] addition (as for CB009649-A)



[image-0][image_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating the behavior of a system, likely related to wireless communication, with different states and transitions. The diagram is organized into several distinct regions, each representing a specific operational mode or condition. **Key Components and States:** * **Main Regions:** * "TP 0_0 On/Off & 0_1 On" (Cyan border) * "TP 0_0 On/Off & 0_1 Off" (Magenta border) * **States (represented by rounded shapes):** * **(1) Start:** The initial state of the system. * **(2) TP Off/On; Off:** A state related to TP (likely Transmission Power) being off or on, and another parameter being off. * **(3) TP Off/On; On 64QAM:** A state where TP is off or on, another parameter is on, and 64QAM is involved. * **(4) TP Off/On; Off VoNR:** A state where TP is off or on, another parameter is off, and VoNR (Voice over NR) is involved. * **(5) TP Off/On; On 64QAM VoNR:** A state where TP is off or on, another parameter is on, 64QAM is involved, and VoNR is active. * **(6) SAg On TP Off/On; On 64QAM VoNR:** A state where SAg (likely a specific feature or mode) is on, TP is off or on, 64QAM is involved, and VoNR is active. * **Transitions (represented by arrows):** * **Solid Arrows:** Indicate direct transitions between states. * **Dashed Arrows:** Indicate transitions that are conditional or represent a different type of event. * **Labels on Transitions:** These labels describe the events or conditions that trigger a transition. Examples include: * "VoNR Release" * "VoNR Add" * "DU_trig_RRC_ReConfig" (with various sub-conditions like "Good SINR, TP On", "Bad SINR, TP On", "Good SINR, TP Off", "Bad TotPwr, SAg On", "Good TotPwr, SAg Off") * "Access mode (2)", "Access mode (1)", "Access mode (3)" * "PUSCH-Config sent to UE" * "VoNR HO, SAg Off" * "SAg On to SAg On VoNR HO" * **Labels on States:** These provide a more detailed description of the state, including the status of TP, other parameters, and specific technologies like 64QAM and VoNR. **Overall Flow:** The diagram suggests a system that starts in a "Start" state and can transition to various operational states based on different triggers. These triggers often involve configuration changes (RRC ReConfig), network conditions (SINR, TotPwr), and specific feature activations (VoNR, SAg). The system can move between states within the "TP 0_0 On/Off & 0_1 On" and "TP 0_0 On/Off & 0_1 Off" regions, and also interact with access modes. The "SAg On" state appears to be a distinct operational mode that influences transitions.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-12042135.png[/image_path][/image-0] 

Notes:

  * Starting a connection with TP On for DCI 0_1 and use of TP On for DCI 0_0 are only for L2-Ps NewFR1

---

### 3.30.15.1 SAg Mode Switching

#### 3.30.15.1.0-1 SAg Mode Switching SAg Eligible UE Definition (ID: 11204746)

**3.30.15.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11204752`)

SAg Mode Switching L2 PS shall consider that a UE is eligible for PUSCH slot aggregation (SAg Eligible) when all of the following are true:

  * CP-RT indicates all of the following are true:
  * SAg feature is activated at cell setup (NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg = true, provided at user setup or reconfiguration)
  * UE supports Rel16 SAg with RepType = TypeA (provided at user setup or reconfiguration)
  * UE supports PUSCH Transform Precoder (provided at user setup or reconfiguration)
  * L2 PS determines all of the following are true:
  * [Before CB008236-A-UPD001]UE is in BR (Basic Reliability) mode (see 5G_UP_ALG_16426_replaced([11138318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) )[End CB008236-A-UPD001]
  * UE scheduling on PCell



Alternatives:

  * [CB008236-B] CP-RT indicates during a HO that UE is to start at TP Enabled::SAG On state (see 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for state definition)[End CB008236-B]



Notes:

  * Activation of SAg feature has activation of PUSCH Transform Precoder (NRCELL.actUlDftSOfdm = true) as a prerequisite
  * For SAg to be applied to a UE, it must have a VoNR DRB configured in addition to the conditions listed above. For the purpose of this requirement, the UE is "eligible" for SAg as decribed above, but must wait for SAg application until it has a VoNR DRB and the combined transform precoder/SAg on threshold criteria in 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * In a HO scenario for a UE with VoNR, if SAg was applicable at source setup and the SAg feature is enabled at the target cell, then CP will indicate to target cell L2 PS to have UE in TP Enabled::SAG On state from the start. This means Transform Precoder applied to DCI 0_1 transmissions but not to DCI 0_0 nor MSG3 which will use CP OFDM.
  * CA note: UE scheduling in SCell will never use PUSCH slot aggregation, as CP will not configure the UE in SCell with Rel 16 TDRA table (even if it configures UE with it at PCell).
  * [CB008236-A-UPD001] All SAg Eligible UEs must use the puschAllocationList-r16 table (5G_UP_ALG_19545_replaced([11204930](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwmfQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), regardless of SAg state. This is because CP will configure a UE at RRC with the puschAllocationList-r16 table at user setup (and not wait for UE to have a VoNR DRB). UEs in TP Enabled::SAG On state can select 2x and 4x repetition entries in the table, but UEs not in TP Enabled::SAG On state are restricted to 1x repetition enties [End CB008236-A-UPD001]

---

#### 3.30.15.1.0-2 SAg Mode Switching Slot Aggregation States and Transitions (ID: 11204759)

**3.30.15.1.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `13374331`)

SAg Mode Switching 13374331: SAg Mode Switching The gNB-DU shall select Slot Aggregation mode (SAg Mode) for a UE as described in algorithm below, when the UL Slot aggregation feature is enabled and the UE is SAg Eligible (.[11204746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_uUp6UfvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) ) States for a SAg Eligible UE: * TP Disabled: \+ Definition: This is the Transform Precoder Disabled state introduced by 5GC000545 in ([11140467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krt_UPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), except for the new SAg related interactions with TP Enabled state when UE is configured with VoNR . \- Transform Precoder disabled \- Slot aggregation repetitions not allowed \- CA interworking is per legacy behavior (not affected by SAg) \+ State Exit: \- To: TP Enabled::SAG On state: if the SINR Metric meets the SAG On NIDD threshold criteria and UE is configured with VoNR (see [11204811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). \- To: TP Enabled::SAG Off state: This is the same as described in ([11140672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krvNZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). \+ External State Entry: \- From: User setup due to initial connection or RRC ReEstablishment \- From: User setup due to HO if UE was not in TP Enabled::SAG On state at source cell * TP Enabled: \+ Definition: This is similar to the Transform Precoder Enabled state in ([11140467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krt_UPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , but with the addition of SAG On and SAG Off sub-states. \+ TP Enabled::SAG On sub-state: \- Definition: see ([11204797](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) \- Sub-State Exit: * To: TP Disabled state: \+ [CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] When "Forced TP On for VoNR" is FALSE:[End CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] \+ [Before CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] When NRCELL.actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl is set to FALSE, OR (NRCELL.actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl is set to TRUE AND 5QI1/65/66 DRB is not configured for the UE)[End CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] \- If the SINR metric for SAg meets the TP Off NIDD threshold criteria * To: TP Enabled::SAG Off state : \+ If VoNR DRB is released or \+ If SINR metric meets SAg OFF threshold criteria as per ([11204811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). \- External State Entry: * From: User setup due to HO if UE was in TP Enabled::SAG On state at source cell (CP-RT provides and indication during handover \+ TP Enabled::SAG Off sub-state: \- Definition: see 5G_UP_ALG_19415_replaced([11204797](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) \- State Exit: * [CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] When "Forced TP On for VoNR" (see notes below) is FALSE and "Forced Start with TP On" (see notes below) is FALSE: [End CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] * [Before CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] When NRCELL.actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl is set to FALSE, OR (NRCELL.actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl is set to TRUE AND 5QI1/65/66 DRB is not configured for the UE)[ End CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] \+ To: TP disabled state: If the rrmAvgSinrTpSwitch metric meets the TP Off NIDD threshold criteria. This is the same as described in ([11140751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krvNbvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). \+ To: TP Enabled::SAG On state : UE context modified to add 1st VoNR DRB * Else When [CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] When "Forced TP On for VoNR" (see notes below) is TRUE or "Forced Start with TP On" is TRUE: [End CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] \+ [Before CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] ( NRCELL.actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl is set to TRUE AND 5QI1 DRB is configured for the UE) [End CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] \+ To: TP Enabled::SAG On state: SINR metric meets SAg ON threshold criteria as per ([11204811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). \+ TP Enabled::SAG On sub-state: Stay in same state \- Trigger: Cell reconfiguration has NRBTS.NRCELL.actUlDftSOfdm = False \+ TP Enabled::SAG Off sub-state: Stay in same state \- Trigger: Cell reconfiguration has NRBTS.NRCELL.actUlDftSOfdm = False When SAg state is modified based on the threshold criteria as per ([11204811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), then send indication to the CP-RT for SAg ON/OFF. Applicability: * Only for SAg eligible UE (see per 5G_UP_ALG_19245_replaced([11204746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For other UEs, Transform Precoder switching described in 5G_UP_ALG_3270_replaced([11140467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krt_UPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) applies (including VoNR UEs which are **not SAg eligible).** Additional Transition Definitions: * Transitions triggered by SINR metric used for SAg require a User Reconfiguration procedure triggered by L2 PS. This includes a reconfiguration transient phase per ([11140627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krumXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), except that L2 PS requests CP to handle the corresponding SAg related actions in the user modification request: \+ Transition to TP Enabled::SAG On state : Transform Precoder enabled; Perform Slot Aggregation specific actions by CP (DRX profile update for SAg, etc.) \+ Transition to TP Disabled state: Transform Precoder disabled; Undo Slot Aggregation specific actions by CP (remove DRX profile update for SAg, etc.) Notes: [CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] * VoNR herein refers to either 5QI-1,5QI-65, 5QI-66. Configured with VoNR means UE is configured with a VoNR DRB \+ A SAg On state can only be applied if UE is configured with a VoNR DRB * "Forced TP On for VoNR": \+ True only if: NRCELL.actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl is set to TRUE and UE is configured with VoNR * "Forced Start with TP On" \+ True only if: NRCELL.actUlTransPreUsageEnh contains 'DCI01_TpOnOff_StartTpOn' and UE is configured with VoNR [End CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] * VoNR DRB Configured/Released are indications provided by CP-RT during a user modify procedure * SINR Metric and SAG SAG On threshold criteria for On vs SAG Off selection are defined by ([11204811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) * [CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] When actUlTransPreUsageEnh contains 'DCI01_TpOnOff_StartTpOn', the PUSCH Transform Precoder algorithm for Semi-static UL Waveform switching (TP On/Off switching) with DCI 0_1 starts in a TP On state (whether UE has VoNR DRB or not). This adds possibility of UE being in TP On state even for good SINR, just as actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl=TRUE with VoNR DRB does. This requires that L2-PS also monitor SAG metric for SAg Off to On switching when UE is in a TP Enabled::SAG Off sub-state. Note that actUlTransPreUsageEnh is not supported in ABIL [End CB008148-SR-B-UPD004] An illustration of the algorithm is within the state machine diagram below. Diagram Notes: * Shows interworking of SAg (CB8236) with: Always have TP On for VoNR (CB9849), Maintain TP state on HO (CNI-10897), and onLine change for actUlDftSOfdm (CB14093-C, Keep TP On to avoid extra RRC ReConfigurations when setting actUlDftSOfdm =False) \+ Note that NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg is objectLocking. Diagram applies for SAg Eligible UEs (who are configured with 3GPP Rel-16 TDRA table at UE setup). This requires actUlSlotAgg=true. After an onLine change of actUlDftSOfdm to False, no subsequent transitions to a SAg On state are allowed. \- UEs already in SAg On at time of onLine change have a path, based on subsequent improving SINR (with or without VoNR release), towards a TP Disable:: SAg Off state ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ [image-0][image_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating the L2 PS States for a PUSCH Slot Aggregation (SAg) Eligible UE. The diagram depicts various states and transitions between them, triggered by specific conditions. Here's a breakdown of the states and transitions: **States:** * **TP Enabled (On)** : This is a meta-state encompassing other states. * **SAg On + VoNR** : Represents the UE being in SAg ON state with VoNR active. * **SAg Off + VoNR** : Represents the UE being in SAg OFF state with VoNR active. * **SAg Off + No VoNR** : Represents the UE being in SAg OFF state with no VoNR active. * **TP Disabled** : This is another meta-state encompassing other states. * **TP Off + VoNR** : Represents the UE being in TP OFF state with VoNR active. * **TP Off + No VoNR** : Represents the UE being in TP OFF state with no VoNR active. **Transitions:** * **From SAg On + VoNR:** * "SINR Metric meets SAG Off OAM threshold criteria (CB8849)" leads to **SAg Off + VoNR**. * "VoNR DRB Released" leads to **SAg Off + No VoNR**. * **From SAg Off + VoNR:** * "SINR Metric meets SAG On OAM threshold criteria AND actUIDftSOfdm = True" leads to **SAg On + VoNR**. * "VoNR DRB Released" leads to **SAg Off + No VoNR**. * "Init Access/ReEstb (CB8849)" leads to **SAg Off + No VoNR**. * **From SAg Off + No VoNR:** * "rmmAvgSinrTpSwitch meets TP On OAM threshold criteria AND no VoNR" leads to **SAg On + VoNR**. * "rmmAvgSinrTpSwitch meets TP Off OAM threshold criteria AND no VoNR" leads to **TP Off + No VoNR**. * "HO (CNI-108979): Source= TP On & No VoNR" leads to **SAg Off + No VoNR**. * **From TP Off + VoNR:** * "SINR Metric meets SAG On OAM threshold criteria AND actUIDftSOfdm = True" leads to **SAg On + VoNR**. * "SINR Metric meets SAG Off OAM threshold criteria (CB8236)" leads to **SAg Off + VoNR**. * "VoNR DRB Add (CB8849)" leads to **SAg Off + VoNR**. * **From TP Off + No VoNR:** * "SINR Metric meets SAG On OAM threshold criteria AND actUIDftSOfdm = True" leads to **SAg On + VoNR**. * "SINR Metric meets SAG Off OAM threshold criteria (CB8236)" leads to **SAg Off + No VoNR**. * "VoNR DRB Add (CB8849)" leads to **SAg Off + No VoNR**. **External Triggers/Events:** * "actUIDftSOfdm changed to False" leads to **SAg On + VoNR**. * "UE Context Setup - HO from SAg On at Source (CB8236)" leads to **SAg On + VoNR**. * "HO (CB8236): Source = TP Off & SAG Off & VoNR" leads to **TP Off + VoNR**. * "HO (CNI-108979): Source = TP Any & SAG Off & No VoNR" leads to **TP Off + No VoNR**. * "HO (CNI-108979): Source = TP Off & SAG Off & No VoNR" leads to **TP Off + No VoNR**. The diagram also shows some specific conditions in parentheses, like "(CB8849)" and "(CB8236)", which likely refer to specific criteria or call back identifiers. The overall diagram visually represents the dynamic behavior of a UE's PUSCH Slot Aggregation state based on various network conditions and configurations.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-04152246.png[/image_path][/image-0] File: aDiagrams for CB14093C_v0.pptx Folder:

---

#### 3.30.15.1.0-3 SAg Mode Switching Slot Aggregation State Definitions for Transform Precoder (ID: 11204797)

**3.30.15.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13420500`)

SAg Mode Switching gNB-DU shall follow the following behavior when a SAg eligible UE is in TP enabled state (see 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) )

  * TP Enabled::SAG On sub-state:
  * Definition:
  * Transform Precoder enabled
  * Same non-SAg behavior with regards to MCS Table,QPSK vs pi/2-BPSK,SU-MIMO,UL Rank,allocated PRBs form, VRB-to-PRB mapping, Resource Allocation Type, Sample Density, LA MCS-to-SINR mapping tables is per 5G_UP_ALG_3274_replaced([11140586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krumWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * UL TBS calculation is per 5G_UP_ALG_15554_replaced([11203613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfxmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * DCI 0_1 field settings for Transform Precoder enabled, except for TDRA field, are per 5G_UP_ALG_4937_replaced([11171437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuEIqvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * Selection identified to UE in TDRA field (index) within DCI 0_1, per 5G_UP_ALG_19346_replaced([11205160](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwntZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * MPR-Aware LA behavior,per 5G_UP_ALG_3276_replaced([11140627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krumXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), is **not supported**
  * Slot aggregation allowed: UL Link Adaptation can select between n1,n2, and n4 repetitions, per 5G_UP_ALG_19339_replaced([11205042](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwmfTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * A VoNR [CB014699-D] and critical VoNR [End CB014699-D] DRB is configured
  * CA scheduling defined by 5G_UP_ALG_19252_replaced([11205370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwo7h_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). SCell activation/deactivation and Inactivity timer behavior defined by 5G_UP_ALG_19252_replaced([11205370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwo7h_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .
  * RAC GBR Load reporting per 5G_UP_ALG_19262_replaced([11201367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwWAkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_19263_replaced([11201378](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwWAkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * Interaction with L1 as defined by 5G_UP_ALG_19294_replaced([11205395](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwo7i_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_19296_replaced([11205407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwo7jfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * for UL adaptive re-transmissions see 5G_UP_ALG_5079_replaced([11202406](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * Time domain scheduling per: 5G_UP_ALG_19281_replaced([11205197](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwntavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * Frequency Domain scheduling per: 5G_UP_ALG_19288_replaced([11205331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwo7gfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * Interaction with DSS per 5G_UP_ALG_19860_replaced([11205277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwoUePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

  * TP Enabled::SAG Off sub-state:

  * Definition:
  * Transform Precoder enabled
  * Same non-SAg behavior with regards to MCS Table,QPSK vs pi/2-BPSK,SU-MIMO,UL Rank,allocated PRBs form, VRB-to-PRB mapping, Resource Allocation Type, Sample Density, LA MCS-to-SINR mapping tables . Same UL TBS calculation. Same CA interworking.
  * DCI 0_1 field settings for Transform Precoder enabled,including for TDRA field, are per 5G_UP_ALG_4937_replaced([11171437](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuEIqvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * MPR-Aware LA behavior as per 5G_UP_ALG_3276_replaced([11140627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krumXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * Slot aggregation not allowed
  * A VoNR [CB014699-D] and critical VoNR [End CB014699-D] DRB is not configured
  * UL adaptive re-transmissions are allowed

---

#### 3.30.15.1.0-4 SAg Mode Switching RF-based Criteria for Entering/Leaving TP Enabled::SAG On State (ID: 11204811)

**3.30.15.1.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11204835`)

SAg Mode Switching 11204835: SAg Mode Switching The gNB-DU shall trigger a transition to and out of TP Enabled::SAG On state (see 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) based on **_*rrmAvgSinrSagSwitch_ *as described below.****_* Preconditions: * For TDD FR1: UE must be in UL Rank_1 in addition to triggers identified below Triggers: * For entry into TP Enabled::SAG On state: If_ *rrmAvgSinrSagSwitch **is less than rrmThresholdSag**On for rdSag** OnHystThresh consecutive times.**_*_**_* For exit from TP Enabled::SAG On state: If_ _rrmAvgSinrSagSwitch is greater than rrmThresholdSag_ _Off for rdSag_ _OffHystThresh consecutive times._**_*_ Definitions: rrmAvgSinrSagSwitch = rrmAvgSinrUl + rrmOffsetSagSwitch **; where rrmOffsetSagSwitch is defined as:*** For UL Rank 1: set to 0 * For UL Rank 2: * If SINR measurement sourcefor Rank1 is SRS and PUSCH PRBs <= SRS PRBs * rrmOffsetSagSwitch = 10 * log( rrmMaxSinrPrbs / rrmMinSinrPrbs ) + rdOffsetSagSwitch; where: * rrmMaxSinrPrbs = max (next PUSCH number of PRBs, SRS PRBs) * rrmMinSinrPrbs = min (next PUSCH number of PRBs, SRS PRB) * rdOffsetSagSwitch defined by 5G_UP_ALG_21210_replaced([11209171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyFdvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) * Else: * Set to 0 rrmThresholdSag**On = NRCELL.ulTransPrecodeCtl/ulSlotAgg**OnTotPwrThresh****rrmThresholdSag** Off = NRCELL.ulTransPrecodeCtl/ulSlotAgg**OffTotPwrThresh***** rdSagOnHystThresh is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_19255_replaced([11209143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyFcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) * rdSagOffHystThresh is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_19258_replaced([11209158](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyFdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) SINR Comparison: * On every UL DCI 0_1 grant for the UE SINR Comparison Counter: * Initialize count at 0 * Keep at 0 while rrmAvgSinrUl is 0 * Increment count every time **rrmAvgSinrSagSwitch meets threshold criteria*** Reset counter to 0 when rrmAvgSinrSagSwitch fails to meet threshold criteria Requests to CP:****[CB009849-A-UPD001]****If NRCELL.actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl is set to FALSE, OR (NRCELL.actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl is set to TRUE AND**[Before CB008118-C] 5QI1[CB008118-C] 5QI-1/65/66 [End CB008118-C] DRB is not configured for the UE)****[End CB009849-A-UPD001]** For entry into TP Enabled::SAG On state: Send indication to allow CP to tell that UE will be configured with Transform Precoder enabled plus SAG additions  For exit from TP Enabled::SAG On state: Send indication to allow CP to tell that UE will be configured with Transform Precoder disabled plus removal of SAG additions****[CB009849-A-UPD001]****Else if (NRCELL.actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl is set to TRUE AND**[Before CB008118-C] 5QI1[CB008118-C] 5QI-1/65/66 [End CB008118-C] DRB is configured for the UE)** For entry into TP Enabled::SAG On state: Send indication to allow CP to tell that UE will be configured with SAG additions  For exit from TP Enabled::SAG On state: Send indication to allow CP to tell that UE will be configured with removal of SAG additions**[End CB009849-A-UPD001]**SINR measurement source: * For UE Capability for 2 UL layers, with UL Rank=1, and cell configured for DSS: * Use PUSCH as SINR measurement source (disregarding the value of NRCELL.ulSinrSourceForRank1SUMimo) * Exclude any PUSCH transmission of the proactive grant in case RIM mitigation extension was triggered (seen in[11195190: Excluding UE from UL Pre-Scheduler Candidate Set in 1st UL slot due to RIM UL mitigation extension](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_4Xz2wPvPEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Notes: – rrmAvgSinr is defined by 5G_UP_ALG_11120_replaced([11130873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krA0p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) \- Both rrmThresholdSag**On and rrmThresholdSag**Off are on-line modifiable parameters with change applying to all UEs** ** – The value of rdSagOnHystThresh should be less than the value of rdSagOffHystThresh, such that UE enters TP Enabled::SAG On state quicker than it returns to TP disabled state – The relation rrmThresholdSagOn < rrmThresholdSagOff is enforced by NIDD DoPR \- Details on indicator sent to CP as part of the "User Reconfiguration Required" procedure is up to EFS. An illustration of the algorithm is within the state machine diagram below. [image-0][image_desc]The image is a graph illustrating voltage levels over time, with annotations indicating different states of a "TP" (likely a protection or control system) and "Sag" conditions. The graph displays a blue line representing a fluctuating voltage. Two horizontal dashed lines are present: an orange one labeled "SagOff" and a green one labeled "SagOn". The time axis is divided into three main segments, indicated by vertical dashed lines and horizontal arrows with text labels. The first segment, on the left, is labeled "TP Disabled" with an orange arrow pointing left. Below this segment, a series of green upward arrows are shown, spanning a portion of this time frame and labeled "SagOn" with a bracket. This suggests that during this period, the TP was disabled, but a "SagOn" condition was active or being monitored. The second segment, in the middle, is labeled "TP Enabled:: SAG On" with a green horizontal bar spanning this entire period. The blue voltage line rises significantly during this segment, crossing both the "SagOn" and "SagOff" thresholds. The third segment, on the right, is labeled "TP Disabled" with an orange arrow pointing right. Below this segment, a series of orange upward arrows are shown, spanning a portion of this time frame and labeled "SagOff" with a bracket. This suggests that during this period, the TP was disabled, and a "SagOff" condition was active or being monitored. A dashed green line is also shown within this segment, indicating a threshold.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16235.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] File: CB008236_L2RTSFS_Diagrams_V1.pptx Folder:

SAg TDRA Tables Default puschAllocationList-r16 table

**3.30.15.2.0-1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13467389`)

SAg TDRA Tables Default puschAllocationList-r16 table:

**[CB008577-O]**

If cell has dlMimoMode as CL32x4 and aperiodic CSI report enabled for the cell ([11159329: A-CSI configuration requirements](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)  _actAperCsiCell ):_

K2min, K2mincsi of the cell shall be decided by ([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |  |   
---|---|---|---|---|---

7 | K2min | 27 | puschMappingType | n2 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0)  
8 | K2min | 41 | puschMappingType | n2 | SLIV 41: (L=13, S=0)  
9 | K2min | 55 | puschMappingType | n2 | SLIV 55: (L=12, S=0)  
10 | K2min | 27 | puschMappingType | n4 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0)  
11 | K2min+1 | 27 | puschMappingType | n4 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0)  
12 | K2min | 41 | puschMappingType | n4 | SLIV 41: (L=13, S=0)

else

**[End CB008577-O]**

|  |  |  |  |   
---|---|---|---|---|---  
pusch-symbolAllocation index | K2 | SLIV | PUSCH mapping Type | Repetitions | Comments  
0 | K2min | 27 | puschMappingType | n1 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0)  
1 | K2min+1 | 27 | puschMappingType | n1 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0)

11 | K2min+1 | 27 | puschMappingType | n4 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0)  
12 | K2min | 41 | puschMappingType | n4 | SLIV 41: (L=13, S=0)  
13 | K2min | 55 | puschMappingType | n4 | SLIV 55: (L=12, S=0)  
  
Applicability**:**

  * Table is sent only if NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg= true



Notes:

  * All of the entries in the Default puschAllocationList table (3GPP Rel 15) are duplicated at the top of the corresponding puschAllocationList-r16 table (3GPP Rel 16), with Repetions=n1, in order to minimize effort at Sherpa to handle both tables.
  * Table Availability to CP:
  * Cell Setup: Two tables
  * Default puschAllocationList-r16 table
  * Default puschAllocationList (3GPP Rel-15) table defined per **5G_UP_ALG_21785_replaced([11204947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwmfQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_21786_replaced([11204995](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwmfSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**
  * User Setup/Modify:
  * Per 5G_UP_ALG_6571_replaced([11115928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpzTt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the puschTimeDomainAllocationList is always reported to CP per dedicated BWP and may be modified to avoid DCI size conflicts per BWP. **The same behavior applies to the puschAllocationList-r16 table in this requirement (also see 5G_UP_ALG_7080_replaced([11171605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuFW1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_7112_replaced([11171658](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuF96_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_7087_replaced([11171627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuF92vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**
  * Relation to DCI 0_1 TDRA field: per 5G_UP_ALG_19346_replaced([11205160](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwntZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * Definition of Repetitions column:
  * Refers to the numberOfRepetitions-r16 field within the PUSCH-TimeDomainResourceAllocation-r16 IE sent to UE in the RRC layer
  * 5G_UP_ALG_19346_replaced([11205160](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwntZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) summarizes how the DCI 0_1 TDRA field can refer to table rows with different Repetitions
  * Dummy entries:



SAg requires cell to have NRCELL.actUlDftSOfdm = TRUE, which prevents activation of NRBTS.actTdraFallback. therefore, there is no Dummy entry addition version for the puschAllocationList-r16 table

  * K2min+1 is not used in FDD not shared with LTE.

pusch-symbolAllocation index | K2 | SLIV | PUSCH mapping Type | Repetitions | Comments

Rationale:

Refer to 5G_UP_11397_replaced([11356891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uca_JfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the usage of above mentioned SLIV in uplink Tx switching period.

Note:

  1. puschMappingType defined in NRCELL.puschMappingType.

  2. For FDD PCell, DCI Format 0_0 and 0_1 conflict detection and dedicate TDRA list modification is needed as 5G_UP_ALG_7112_replaced([11171658](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuF96_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

  3. The index(order) of above elements in the table can be different in SW implementation. It does not bring problem because gNB lookup the index according to K2 value and SLIV and set the index in the DCI for UL grant (5G_UP_ALG_4946_replaced([11198916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwLBefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

SAg TDRA Tables **[CB008577-O]**

If cell has dlMimoMode as CL32x4 and aperiodic CSI report enabled for the cell ([11159329: A-CSI configuration requirements](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)  _actAperCsiCell ):_

K2_n shall be the K2min, K2mincsi of the cell decided by ([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), additionally, K2min + 3 for the listed PUSCH symbol length below.

---|---|---|---|---

3n | K2_n | 27 | puschMappingType | SLIV 27: corresponds to (L=14, S=0) used for PUSCH  
3n+1 | K2_n | 41 | puschMappingType | SLIV 41: corresponds to (L=13, S=0) used for PUSCH  
3n+2 | K2_n | 55 | puschMappingType | SLIV 55: corresponds to (L=12, S=0) used for PUSCH  
  
else

**[End CB008577-O]**

|  |  |  |

pusch-symbolAllocation index | K2 | SLIV | PUSCH mapping Type | Comments

1 | K2min+1 | 27 | puschMappingType | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0)  
2 | K2min | 41 | puschMappingType | SLIV 41: (L=13, S=0)  
3 | K2min | 55 | puschMappingType | SLIV 55: (L=12, S=0)

5 | K2min+1 + 3 | 27 | puschMappingType | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0)  
6 | K2min + 3 | 41 | puschMappingType | SLIV 41: (L=13, S=0)  
7 | K2min + 3 | 55 | puschMappingType | SLIV 55: (L=12, S=0)

SAg TDRA Tables 3GPP Rel15 TDRA table supporting MSG2 to MSG3 timing in presence of PUSCH Slot Aggregation scheduling (see 5G_UP_ALG_4346_replaced([11177880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuyhaPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

2 | K2min + 3 | 27 | puschMappingType | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0)  
3 | K2min+1 + 3 | 27 | puschMappingType | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0)  
  
Applicability**:**

  * Table is sent at cell setup only if NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg= true

1 | 2 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n1 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999

3 | 3 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n1 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
4 | 4 | 27 | puschMappingType | n1 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0) |   
5 | 5 | 27 | puschMappingType | n1 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0) |   
6 | 6 | 27 | puschMappingType | n1 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0) |   
7 | 9 | 27 | puschMappingType | n1 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0) |   
8 | 10 | 27 | puschMappingType | n1 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0) |   
9 | 11 | 27 | puschMappingType | n1 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0) |   
10 | 11 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n1 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
11 | 12 | 27 | puschMappingType | n1 | SLIV 27: (L=14, S=0) |   
12 | 2 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n2 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
13 | 3 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n2 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
14 | 4 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n2 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
15 | 5 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n2 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
16 | 6 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n2 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
17 | 11 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n2 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
18 | 12 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n2 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
19 | 2 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n12 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
20 | 3 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n12 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
21 | 4 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n12 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
22 | 5 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n12 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
23 | 6 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n12 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
24 | 11 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n12 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
25 | 12 | 41 or 55 | puschMappingType | n12 | See SLIV selection algorithm after table. | PR723999  
  
SLIV selection algorithm:

If uplink Uplane Fragmentation (see CP_ARC_ALG_2526) = disabled then

For puschMappingType Type A: SLIV 41: (L=13, S=0) For Type B: SLIV 55: (L=12, S=0)

Else

SLIV 41: (L=13, S=0) 

EndIf

[End PR723999]

Notes:

  * The table above is built to specifically support a TMO US IODT effort under CB011118 and should be revised, if needed, once support for PUSCH Slot aggregation is added to the global product.
  * Current TDRA entries used at TMO (with equivalent of n1 repetitions) are preserved in the TDRA table
  * A UE capable of PUSCH Slot Aggregation will be configured by CP at UE setup with the table above (table provided by L2 PS to CP at cell setup). This is before UE could have a VoNR DRB and before its eligible for TP Enabled: SAG On state. This means that the table above must provide legacy entries which do not have slot aggregation repetitions.
  * Initial PUSCH repetition for Slot aggregation in CB011118 will only be sent on the 1st UL slot after special slot. This means that entries with K2=9 or 10 with repetitions > n1 are not needed
  * [Before CB011118-A-UPD001]Entries with repetitions > n1 with # of symbols < 14 are not needed[End CB011118-A-UPD001]
  * For TDD slot aggregation, Repetitions means number of consecutive slots over which the slot aggregation grant spans.CB011118 will only schedule a slot aggregation grant with repetitions >1 for bundle to start on UL slots 4 and 14 (tdLte 0ms) or slots 8 and 18 (tdLte 3ms). If 4x repetitions are desired, they will span over 12 total slots and a Repetitions=n12 is required.
  * [PR720151] When 2x or 4x SAG (n2, n12) transmissions are scheduled it is assumed that aperiodic SRS may be present in one of the repetition slots and therefore the 2x and 4x SAG transmissions must be scheduled with fewer than 14 symbols. [End PR720151]
  * [PR723999] SLIV determination when SRS is in the slot must include a check for uplink Uplane Fragmentation. When SRS is present in a slot and uplink Uplane Fragmentation is enabled then SLIV 41 must be used. [End PR723999]

---

### 3.30.15.3 SAg Link Adaptation

#### 3.30.15.3.0-1 SAg Link Adaptation Determination of 1x UL SAg (ID: 11205042)

**3.30.15.3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11205063`)

SAg Link Adaptation If:

 The UE is SAg capable

 and if NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg = TRUE

 and if a [Before CB008118-C] 5QI1 [CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-C] bearer is allocated

 and if SAg Mode is ON (See 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Then:

If (rdSagMinTbsFactor > 0) and the UE outstanding data is less than rdSagMinTbsFactor

Then 1x SAg mode is used.

Else if “Total 1x Energy” (see 5G_UP_ALG_19386_replaced([11205111](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is less than rrmAveSinrUl (See 5G_UP_ALG_11120_replaced([11130873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krA0p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Then 1x SAg mode is used

Else either 2x or 4x SAg will be used.

Note that "rdSagMinTbsFactor = 0" is used to disable the above "if" check for using 1x SAg. See 5G_UP_ALG_24427_replaced([11209183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyFePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of rdSagMinTbsFactor.

Note: There will be some exceptions that still use 1x such as UL proactive grants for Timing Advance (TA) responses and UL RLC status PDUs.

Note: For FDD + TDD FR1 UL Tx switching UE, before TDD SCell with UL Tx switching is deactivated (considering SCell deactivation delay refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2796_replaced([11121847](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqa-xPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), only 1x SAg mode shall be used.

---

#### 3.30.15.3.0-2 SAg Link Adaptation SAg Link Adaptation Overview (ID: 11205071)

**3.30.15.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11205077`)

SAg Link Adaptation When the UE is in SAg Mode = ON, the determination of a 2x or 4x SAg grant along with the TBS size, the MCS and therequired number of PRBs will be set as defined by 5G_UP_ALG_19339_replaced([11205042](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwmfTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_19341_replaced([11205080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_19386_replaced([11205111](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and 5G_UP_ALG_19387_replaced([11205133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwntYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 3.30.15.3.0-3 SAg Link Adaptation Determination of the MCS/PRB combination required for 2x and 4x SAg (ID: 11205080)

**3.30.15.3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11205104`)

SAg Link Adaptation 11205104: SAg Link Adaptation If:

 The UE is SAg capable

 and if NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg = TRUE

 and if a [Before CB008118-C] 5QI1 [CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-C] bearer is allocated

 and if SAg Mode is ON (See req 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 and if 1x SAg cannot be supported as determined from step REQ x1 Determination of 1x UL SAg

Then the MCS/PRB combination used for 2x and 4x SAg is determined as follows:

 The number of PRBs to set to be as small as possible while keeping the MCS QPSK or less and while maintaining a TBS size that is greater than or equal to “SAg VoNR minTBS” (NRCELL.minTbsUlSlotAgg). 

The number of PRBs selected in the above step cannot be less than the number of PRBs as set by rdVoipPacketAggrMinPrbs (see 5G_UP_ALG_20413_replaced([11209207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyFfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 Note that the number of PRBs used in the search for the MCS/PRB combination cannot include PRB combinations that are not valid. See 5G_UP_AG_19415 and 5G_UP_ALG_3274_replaced([11140586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krumWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 Having selected the number of PRBs in the step above, the selected MCS must be the minimum MCS which supports a TBS size of “SAg VoNR minTBS” (NRCELL.minTbsUlSlotAgg).

Below is an example MCS/PRB/TBS table to illustrate this algorithm:

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a table that shows the TBS (Transport Block Size) in bits for different MCS (Modulation and Coding Scheme) indexes and PRBs (Physical Resource Blocks). The table is titled "UL Table #1 TBS size (bits)". The first column is labeled "MCS Index" with the symbol $I_{MCS}$. The second column is labeled "Modulation Order" with the symbol $Q_m$. The remaining columns are labeled with the number of PRBs, ranging from 1 to 6. The first column lists MCS indexes from 0 to 11. The second column lists the corresponding modulation order, which is 1 for MCS indexes 0 and 1, 2 for MCS indexes 2 to 9, and 4 for MCS indexes 10 and 11. The table body contains the TBS size in bits for each combination of MCS index and number of PRBs. For example, for MCS index 0 and 1 PRB, the TBS size is 32 bits. For MCS index 7 and 3 PRBs, the TBS size is 432 bits. For MCS index 9 and 2 PRBs, the TBS size is 368 bits. For MCS index 10 and 2 PRBs, the TBS size is also 368 bits. There is a note on the left side of the table that says "QPSK is MCS 9 or less", indicating that QPSK modulation is used for MCS indexes 9 or lower. The table is presented with a header row and a first column that are shaded in gray. The title row is shaded in pink. Some cells in the table are highlighted with yellow and red circles, possibly to draw attention to specific values. Here is the table in markdown format:

MCS Index $I_{MCS}$ | Modulation Order $Q_m$ | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6  
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---  
0 | 1 | 32 | 64 | 96 | 128 | 168 | 192  
1 | 1 | 40 | 88 | 128 | 176 | 208 | 256  
2 | 2 | 48 | 104 | 160 | 208 | 256 | 320  
3 | 2 | 64 | 136 | 208 | 272 | 352 | 408  
4 | 2 | 80 | 168 | 256 | 336 | 432 | 504  
5 | 2 | 104 | 208 | 304 | 408 | 528 | 608  
6 | 2 | 120 | 240 | 368 | 504 | 608 | 736  
7 | 2 | 144 | 288 | 432 | 576 | 736 | 848  
8 | 2 | 168 | 336 | 504 | 672 | 808 | 984  
9 | 2 | 184 | 368 | 552 | 736 | 928 | 1128  
10 | 4 | 184 | 368 | 552 | 736 | 928 | 1128  
11 | 4 | 208 | 408 | 608 | 848 | 1032 | 1256  
  
For example if the target TBS is 384 bit, in the above table scan across the largest QPSK MCS (MCS 9) to find the smallest number of PRBs that meet or exceed the desired TBS (552 bits – MCS 9, 3 PRBs). Then at the 3 PRB level, pick the smallest MCS that meets or exceeds the target TBS (432 bits – MCS 7, 3 PRBs).

Note that in the above examplethe number of PRBs as set by rdVoipPacketAggrMinPrbs would need to be 2 or 3 PRBs for MCS 7, 3 PRBs to be selected. 

In the above example if rdVoipPacketAggrMinPrbs had been set to 5 PRBs rather than 2 or 3 PRBs, then a target TBS size of 384 bits would result in MCS 4, 5 PRBs. In this case the 5 PRB column at using the max QPSK MCS of MCS 9 provides for 928 bits, which is larger than needed, and the 5 PRB column would then be used to find the best MCS match which is MCS 4, 5 PRBs 432 bits.

Note that the target TBS size for 12.6 Kbps Vocoders is 384 bits. The range of the number of PRBs allowed by rdVoipPacketAggrMinPrbs is from 2 to 6 PRBs. From a UCI puncturing point of view the range of expected MCS is MCS 9 (2 PRBs) down to MCS 3 (6 PRBs) for a 384 bit TBS.

Note that the target TBS size for 23.8 Kbps Vocoders is 552 bits. Given that the range of the number of PRBs 2 to 6 PRBs. From a UCI puncturing point of view the range of expected MCS is MCS 9 (3 PRBs) down to MCS 5 (6 PRBs) for a 552 bit TBS.

Thus from a UCI puncturing point of view the overall range of MCSs to be used is expected to range from MCS 3 to MCS 9.

---

#### 3.30.15.3.0-4 SAg Link Adaptation Determination of the SINR required for 2x SAg and 1x non-SAG (ID: 11205111)

**3.30.15.3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11205126`)

SAg Link Adaptation If:

 The UE is SAg capable

 and if NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg = TRUE

 and if a [Before CB008118-C] 5QI1 [CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-C] bearer is allocated

 and if SAg Mode is ON (See requirement 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 and if 1x SAg cannot be supported as determined from requirement 5G_UP_ALG_19339_replaced([11205042](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwmfTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Then compensated total energy requirements of the 2x SAg VoNR minTBS MCS/PRB combination (see 5G_UP_ALG_19341_replaced([11205080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when used for 2x SAg is as follows:

 The generic form of the total energy required is expressed in the following form in order to improve understanding:

o Total 2x SAg Energy = (1x SINR/PRB for the selected MCS) – 2.8 dB + 10*log(selected number of PRBs) + (MPR if any) + (DeltaMPR256QAM if any) + (Unused UE PHR power if any) - (UL OLLA compensation)

 The specific requirement form for “Total 2x SAg Energy“ is more compact, but less understandable:

o Total 2x SAg Energy = (1x SINR/PRB for the selected MCS) – 2.8 dB + (MPR = 0 for SAg) + (DeltaMPR256QAM = 0 for SAg) + max{10 _log10(numSagPrbs), min {rrmPhr[i,j,tPHR] + 10_ log10(rrmNumPuschPrb[i,j, tPHR], 10*log10(rrmMaxNumPuschPrb) } } - (rrmDeltaSinr)

with

i: Index of the UE

j: Index of the carrier

t: Time (UL slot) the PUSCH transmission is received

rrmMaxNumPuschPrb: Maximum number of PRBs possible given the bandwidth

numSagPrbs: Number of PUSCH PRBs used for the 2x or 4x SAg transmission

rrmNumPuschPrb[i,j,tPHR]: Number of PUSCH PRBs contributing to the UL transmission with PHR

o The MCS/PRB combination used for this calculation is as determined in 5G_UP_ALG_19341_replaced([11205080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

o Note that the SINR/PRB for the selected MCS is taken from the 1x SINR -> MCS lookup tables and the “-2.8 dB” is used to convert that MCS SINR/PRB value from a 1x to a 2x SAg MCS SINR/PRB value. See 5G_UP_ALG_3138_replaced([11137031](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krYBCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the SINR->MCS LuT.

The specific requirement form for “Total 2x SAg Energy“ is more compact, but less understandable:

o Total 2x SAg Energy = (1x SINR/PRB for the selected MCS) – 2.8 dB + (MPR = 0 for SAg) + (DeltaMPR256QAM = 0 for SAg) + max{10 _log10(numSagPrbs), min {rrmPhr[i,j,tPHR] + 10_ log10(rrmNumPuschPrb[i,j, tPHR], 10*log10(rrmMaxNumPuschPrb) } } - (rrmDeltaSinr)

The “Total 1x Energy” which is used to determine the boundary between using 1x or falling down to either 2x or 4x SAg is Total 2x SAg Energy + 2.8 dB. I.e.:

Total 1x Energy = Total 2x SAg Energy + 2.8 dB

This is not an exact number, as there are many possible 1x MCS/PRB combinations that can be used to support the SAg minTBS which will produce slightly different “Total 1x Energy” thresholds. However several different MCS/PRB combinations were checked and the deltas seem small. 

Note that if rdVoipPacketAggr2xSkip =1 (See 5G_UP_ALG_20416_replaced([11209222](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyFfvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then none of the above calculations need to be done because 2x SAg will not be used.

---

#### 3.30.15.3.0-5 SAg Link Adaptation Determination of 2x or 4x SAg usage (ID: 11205133)

**3.30.15.3.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11205146`)

SAg Link Adaptation If:

 The UE is SAg capable

 and if NRCELL.actUlSlotAgg = TRUE

 and if a [Before CB008118-C] 5QI1 [CB008118-C] 5QI1/65/66 [End CB008118-C] bearer is allocated

 and if SAg Mode is ON (See requirement 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 and if 1x SAg cannot be supported as determined from requirement 5G_UP_ALG_19339_replaced([11205042](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwmfTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Then the “Total 2x SAg Energy” (see 5G_UP_ALG_19386_replaced([11205111](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGWvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is compared with rrmAveSinrUl (See 5G_UP_ALG_11120_replaced([11130873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krA0p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 If rdVoipPacketAggr2xSkip =0 (See 5G_UP_ALG_20416_replaced([11209222](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyFfvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rrmAveSinrUl (i.e., 1 PRB normalized total power) is greater than or equal to “Total 2x SAg minTBS Energy” then 2x SAg will be allocated using the MCS/PRB combination determined in 5G_UP_ALG_19341_replaced([11205080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 Else 4x SAg will be allocated using the MCS/PRB combination determined in 5G_UP_ALG_19341_replaced([11205080](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwnGV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.30.15.4 SAg DCI Impacts

#### 3.30.15.4.0-1 SAg DCI Impacts SAg DCI TDRA Index (ID: 11205160)

**3.30.15.4.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13420543`)

SAg DCI Impacts L2 PS shall set the Time Domain Resource assignment field in UL DCI 0_1 per 5G_UP_ALG_2424_replaced([11115802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpyFlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when the UE is in TP Enabled::SAG On state (see 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), considering:

  * UE is SAg Eligible vs not Sag Eligible (see 5G_UP_ALG_19245_replaced([11204746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * The pusch-symbolAllocation-Rel16 table (see 5G_UP_ALG_18908_replaced([11204846](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4N_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) reported to CP-RT in cell setup and the pusch-symbolAllocation index to that table based on its parameters. The index depends on:
  * K2, SLIV, and Number of slot aggregation repetitions selected by ULLA (see 5G_UP_ALG_19387_replaced([11205133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwntYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * SLIV 27 with repetitions greater than n1 are for UEs in TP enabled::SAG On state
  * SLIV 27, SLIV 41 and 55 with n1 repetitions available for all UEs.
  * SLIV 41 and 55 with repetitions greater than n1 are for UEs in TP enabled::SAG On state (5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
  * For FDD + TDD FR1 UL Tx Switching UE which is SAg eligible(see 5G_UP_ALG_19245_replaced([11204746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), FDD PCell adds specific dedicated TDRA on top of default puschAllocationList-r16 table (see 5G_UP_ALG_26270_replaced([11204898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4PPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in user modify response for SCell addition or SCell change.
  * SLIV 69, 83,[CB015279-E] 97,98,66,80,97,101 [End CB015279-E],53, 81, 55(if not present in default table) and 67 with n1 repetitions available for the FDD + TDD FR1 UL Tx switching UEs.



Note:

  * When CP-RT indicates to L2 PS that UE supports SAg, L2 PS can assume that CP will configure the UE with the pusch-symbolAllocation-Rel16 table
  * L2 PS sends the correct version of the pusch-symbolAllocation-Rel16 table to CP-RT at cell setup (even if SAg feature is disabled). The possible table variants are:
  * Table for FDD not shared with LTE (5G_UP_ALG_18908_replaced([11204846](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwl4N_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Table for FDD shared with LTE (5G_UP_ALG_19545_replaced([11204930](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwmfQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Table for TDD FR1 (5G_UP_ALG_21213_replaced([11205007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwmfSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For 4x Slot Aggregation repetitions in TDD, they span 12 total slots. The DCI TDRA field must point to an index with repetitions = n12 (not n4)
  * For FDD PCell + TDD SCell FR1 UL CA, when FDD PCell has TP enabled::SAG On state, the SLIV with repetitions greater than n1 shall be set according to the smallest symbol length of all the repetition slots, to avoid the impact of A-SRS in FDD PCell and P-SRS in TDD SCell.

---

#### 3.30.15.4.0-2 SAg DCI Impacts SAg DCI Redundancy Version (ID: 11205186)

**3.30.15.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11205192`)

SAg DCI Impacts Per 3GPP 38.214 Table 6.1.2.1-2, each succcesive SAg repetition will be transmitted with the next Redundacy Verison (RV) in the sequence 0, 2, 3, 1. The DCI that grants the SAg transmission will provide the starting RV value. As an example if the DCI specifies RV = 2 for a 4x SAg bundle, the UE will apply RVs 2, 3, 1, 0 to the four successive repetitions in the SAg bundle.

To get the most effective use of the RVs the DCI for the initial HARQ SAg bundle shall set the RV to 0. If the DCI is for a 4x bundle all 4 RV values will be used in the bundle. If HARQ retransmissions are required for a 4x bundle, the RV in the DCI for the HARQ retransmission shall also be set to 0.

For a 2x SAg bundle, the RV in the DCI for the initial bundle shall be set to 0. For HARQ retransmissions the RV in the DCI shall alternate between 3 and 0, with the first HARQ retransmission using RV 3. This ensures that across the 2x SAg bundle retransmissions the repetitions will maintain the RV sequence 0, 2, 3, 1.

---

### 3.30.15.5 SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts

#### 3.30.15.5.0-1 SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg TD scheduling priority (ID: 11205201)

**3.30.15.5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11205208`)

SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts A VoNR UE in slot aggregation (SAg) may need to transmit concurrent data, signalling messages, and other types of non-VoNR transmissions. The TD scheduling priority assigned to a UE in SAg shall follow the normal request class priorities and sub-priorities depending on the type of transmission the SAg UE needs to send. SAg UEs do not get higher priority than non-SAg UEs.

---

#### 3.30.15.5.0-2 SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg TD slots available for SAg initial transmissions (ID: 11205216)

**3.30.15.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11205226`)

SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts 11205226: SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts In FDD the start of a 2x or 4x bundle can be scheduled in any slot, i.e. the SAg bundle's initial transmission can occur in any slot.

[CB011118-A] For TDD the starting slot is based on the TD-LTE Config 2 frame structure that is being used for the IODT testing. This frame structure has two contiguous UL slots followed by 8 DL/Special slots. To support 2x and 4x SAg bundles the TD scheduler must use a K2 value in the UL grant that starts the bundle on the first of the two consecutive UL slots. Using this technique the 2x bundles will be scheduled with repetition value n2 as is used in FDD. However when scheduling a 4x bundle a repetition value of n12 must be used. See below diagram.

[image-0][image_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the structure of a TD-LTE frame and how SAg (Scheduling Assignment) bundles are allocated. The top row, labeled "TD-LTE Frame Struct," shows a sequence of colored blocks representing time slots within a frame. The blocks are labeled with letters: 'D' for Downlink, 'U' for Uplink, and 'S' for Special subframe. The sequence is: D, D, D, D, S, U, U, D, D, D, D, D, D, D, S, U, U, D, D, D, D, D, D, D, S. Above the frame structure, an arrow points to the 'S' and the subsequent 'U' slots, with the text "SAg bundles must start on first UL slot." This indicates that SAg bundles are associated with the Uplink portion of the frame, specifically starting from the first Uplink slot after a Special subframe. The second row, labeled "2x SAg bundle (n2)," shows two blue-colored blocks placed within the frame structure. These blocks are positioned to align with the second and third 'U' slots of the first SAg bundle occurrence in the TD-LTE frame. The third row, labeled "4x SAg bundle (n12)," shows four blue-colored blocks. These blocks are positioned to align with the first four 'U' slots of the second SAg bundle occurrence in the TD-LTE frame. The diagram visually represents how SAg bundles, which are groups of consecutive Uplink slots, are mapped onto the TD-LTE frame structure, emphasizing that they must begin on the first available Uplink slot following a Special subframe. The 'n2' and 'n12' likely refer to the number of time units or slots within these bundles.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16284.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

[End CB011118-A]

---

#### 3.30.15.5.0-3 SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg measurement gap avoidance (ID: 11205232)

**3.30.15.5.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11205239`)

SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts When the TD scheduler schedules a 2x or 4x SAg bundle, the PUSCH SAg repetitions shall not occur in any slots that are part of the UE’s measurement gap, i.e. 2x and 4x SAg UEs will be ineligible for scheduling in slots that result in one or more of the SAg repetitions occuring in slots where the UE is tuned away to perform neighbor cell measurements.

---

#### 3.30.15.5.0-4 SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg pCSI report avoidance (ID: 11205247)

**3.30.15.5.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11205251`)

SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts When the TD scheduler schedules a 2x or 4x SAg bundle, the PUSCH SAg repetitions shall not occur in any slots in which the UE will be sending a pCSI report on PUCCH. The pCSI reports can contain a large number of bits (> 40) that if embedded in a SAg PUSCH repetition will significantly reduce the transmission reliability of the SAg repetition.

2x and 4x SAg bundles do not have to avoid slots that contain HARQ A/N. These transmissions typically contain fewer than 4 bits and can be embedded in SAg PUSCH repetitions without impacting transmission reliability.

---

#### 3.30.15.5.0-5 SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg repetitions count toward UEs scheduled per slot limit (ID: 11205260)

**3.30.15.5.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11205270`)

SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts 11205270: SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts For slots that contain SAg PUSCH repetitions, the maximum number of additional PUSCH transmissions that can be scheduled in that slot is reduced by the number of SAg repetitions that are already scheduled in that slot. This is required to ensure that L1 PUSCH decode capacity per slot is not exceeded. Each SAg repetition in the slot consumes one L1 decode resource that cannot be used for other newly scheduled UEs. rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl specifies the L1 PUSCH decode capacity (see 5G_UP_ALG_10654_replaced([11199273](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In the example below if L2-PS is scheduling UEs for PUSCH transmissions in slot 5, L2-PS can only schedule rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl minus the number of SAg repetitions already scheduled in slot 5. If rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl is 10 then the scheduler can only schedule 10 – 3 = 7 additional UEs for PUSCH transmissions in slot 5.

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a title and a grid. The title reads "Maximum UEs scheduled per slot = 10". The grid is composed of black cells with white borders. Some cells within the grid are colored yellow, blue, and orange, forming a pattern. The yellow cells are in the top row of the colored section, the blue cells are in the middle row, and the orange cells are in the bottom row. The grid appears to be 8 columns wide and 7 rows tall, with the colored cells occupying the 4th, 5th, and 6th columns from the left.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16290.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

[CB008236-B-UPD003] Additionally, to ensure that non-static allocations can be made in a slot that has SAg and Configured Grant allocations the following equality must be met:

rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl – (SAg allocations + CG allocations) >= rdMinNonStaticSchedReservedPerSlot

Where

rdMinNonStaticSchedReservedPerSlot is defined by 5G_UP_ALG_21323_replaced([11173613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUnX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB008236-B-UPD003]

---

#### 3.30.15.5.0-6 SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg interaction with DSS (ID: 11205277)

**3.30.15.5.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11205283`)

SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts When DSS is enabled, the number of PRBs allocated to the NR cell can be dynamically increased or decreased. If the number of PRBs is increased there is no impact to L2-PS with regards to scheduling SAg allocations prior to the PRB increase. However if a PRB reduction occurs, L2-PS must know in advance which slot the reduction will occur so that L2-PS does not schedule SAg repetitions that cross the reduction boundary and use PRBs that are no longer available to NR. 

To ensure that this does not occur the DSS Central Resource Manager (CRM) sends a EN DSS UL SUBCARRIER RESOURCE ALLOCATION REQUEST with the new PRB allocation. If the PRBs are being reduced there will be a PRB reduction activation time that is at least 9ms in the future (CRM_3895). When L2-PS gets the PRB reduction resource allocation request it must ensure that it does not schedule SAg bundles that cross the reduction boundary and use PRBs that are no longer available.

---

#### 3.30.15.5.0-7 SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg interaction with Aperiodic SRS - FDD (ID: 11205290)

**3.30.15.5.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11205296`)

SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts When SAg 2x and 4x repetitions are scheduled to overlap with a slot that can contain an aperiodic SRS transmission (reserved SRS slot + k2srs, see 5G_UP_ALG_2536_replaced([11117398](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp-57vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) the SAg transmission must assume that aperiodic SRS will be transmitted in that slot and therefore the SAg transmission will contain fewer than 14 symbols. 5G_UP_ALG_5057_replaced([11201982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwa5GvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) specifies the number of PUSCH symbols (12 or 13) that are available in a slot reserved for aperiodic SRS.

[CB008236-B-UPD003]

When a SAg UE is required to transmit Aperiodic SRS (A-SRS) the SAg UE will be put in the queue of UEs that require A-SRS. When a reserved SRS slot (see 5G_UP_ALG_2536_replaced([11117398](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp-57vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is the current slot then the UE at the top of the A-SRS queue will be given a grant with the A-SRS flag set in the DCI. If the SAg UE is at the top of the queue it will be given a grant with the A-SRS flag set. The SAg UE will only transmit SRS in the first slot of the SAg PUSCH bundle (see Note). Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_5057_replaced([11201982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwa5GvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for aperiodic SRS details.

Note:

The SRS resource set sent to the UE includes an SRS slot offset that specifies how many slots after the DCI the UE will transmit SRS. For FDD the SRS slot offset is hardcoded to K2min, i.e. 2. Given that the DCI K2 value used in FDD is 2, the SRS transmission will occur in the first SAg PUSCH repetition slot. 

[End CB008236-B-UPD003]

---

#### 3.30.15.5.0-8 SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg interaction with Aperiodic SRS - TDD (ID: 11205304)

**3.30.15.5.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11205309`)

SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts In TDD, aperiodic SRS transmissions can occur in any uplink slot. When scheduling 2x and 4x SAg transmissions the scheduler does not know if an uplink slot containing the SAg repetitions will also contain aperiodic SRS transmissions. To avoid collisions, the scheduler must assume that aperiodic SRS will be present in the repetition slots and schedule the 2x and 4x SAg transmissions with fewer than 14 symbols. 5G_UP_ALG_5057_replaced([11201982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwa5GvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) specifies the number of PUSCH symbols (12 or 13) that are available in a slot reserved for aperiodic SRS. 

When a SAg UE is required to transmit Aperiodic SRS (A-SRS) the SAg UE will be put in the queue of UEs that require A-SRS. If the SAg UE is at the top of the queue it will be given a grant with the A-SRS flag set.

---

#### 3.30.15.5.0-9 SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg interaction with Periodic SRS (ID: 11205315)

**3.30.15.5.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11205320`)

SAg Time Domain Scheduling Impacts A SAg UE on an FDD PCell that has a TDD SCell(s) may be configured with periodic SRS on the TDD SCell(s). Depending on the power class of the UE, the UE may not be able to transmit SRS on the SCell and PUSCH on the PCell in the same symbol in the same slot (see 5G_UP_ALG_5062_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For SAg UEs that do not support this overlap, L2-PS must evaluate the slots that the SAg repetitions will occur to determine if the UE is transmitting SRS in the SCell(s) in any of the PUSCH repetition slots. If there is an overlap L2-PS must schedule the SAg bundle with fewer than 14 symbols. See 5G_UP_ALG_5062_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details regarding the number of PUSCH symbols that can be used when an overlap occurs.

Note: The SAg allocations on a TDD PCell have no interaction with SRS. The TDD periodic SRS transmissions are made in the special slots and do not interfere with the SAg allocations in the UL slots. It is also assumed that if the UE has an FDD SCell(s) that the UE will not be transmitting aperiodic SRS on the SCell.

---

### 3.30.15.6 SAg Frequency Domain Scheduling Impacts

#### 3.30.15.6.0-1 SAg Frequency Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg PUSCH PRB location determination - FDD (ID: 11205339)

**3.30.15.6.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13491837`)

SAg Frequency Domain Scheduling Impacts This requirement is for FDD cells only.

The objective of selecting the PRB location for 2x and 4x SAg PRB allocations is:

 Minimize PUSCH fragmentation

 Provide frequency diversity for a UE’s SAg transmissions

If DSS is disabled, the FD scheduler shall use the following algorithm to select the PRB location for 2x and 4x SAg PRB allocations:

1\. The 2x and 4x SAg UEs shall be allocated PRBs (physical location) before other UEs in that slot.

2\. For 2x and 4x SAg initial transmissions the PRBs shall be allocated at either edge of PUSCH without causing PRB collisions in any of the PUSCH repetitions (see Note 1). The PUSCH edge that is used shall alternate with each slot. 

a. 2x and 4x SAg UEs with initial transmissions that are scheduled in odd numbered slots shall be allocated PRBs at the lower edge of PUSCH. 

b. 2x and 4x SAg UEs with initial transmissions that are scheduled in even numbered slots shall be allocated PRBs at the upper edge of PUSCH.

3\. For 2x and 4x SAg retransmissions, the PRBs shall be allocated at the PUSCH edge that is opposite of the edge used for the previous transmission in that HARQ process, i.e. each successive retransmission shall be at alternating edges of PUSCH.

If DSS is enabled, the FD scheduler shall use the following algorithm to select the PRB location for 2x and 4x SAg PRB allocations:

1\. The 2x and 4x SAg UEs shall be allocated PRBs (physical location) before other UEs in that slot.

2\. The 2x and 4x SAg PRBs shall be allocated at the lower edge of the NR PUSCH without causing PRB collisions in any of the PUSCH repetitions (see Note 1).
