Note 1: When allocating PRBs for 2x and 4x SAg UEs the FD scheduler must evaluate the slots that the PUSCH repetitions will occupy to ensure that there will not be PRB collisions with PRBs that are already reserved, e.g. MSG3, PRACH, Configured Grants, UCI-only aCSI reports. If there are PRB collisions in any of the SAg PUSCH repetitions the PRB allocation (i.e. PRB location) must be changed so that there are no collisions with reserved PRBs.

[PR884900]

if NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc ='True' FD Scheduler shall allocate PRBs for a 2x and 4x SAg UE only within the static region of the PUSCH (defined by parameters longPucchStartPuschPrb and longPucchNumPuschPrb)

[PR884900 END].

---

#### 3.30.15.6.0-2 SAg Frequency Domain Scheduling Impacts SAg PUSCH PRB location determination - TDD (ID: 11205351)

**3.30.15.6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11205356`)

SAg Frequency Domain Scheduling Impacts This requirement is for TDD cells only.

The objective of selecting the PRB location for 2x and 4x SAg PRB allocations is:

 Minimize PUSCH fragmentation

 Provide frequency diversity for a UE’s SAg transmissions

The FD scheduler shall use the following algorithm to select the PRB location for 2x and 4x SAg PRB allocations:

1\. The 2x and 4x SAg UEs shall be allocated PRBs (physical location) before other UEs in that slot.

2\. For 2x and 4x SAg initial transmissions the PRBs shall be allocated from the first available PUSCH after CRB 10 (see Note1) without causing PRB collisions in any of the PUSCH repetitions (see Note 2). 

Note 1: Starting PUSCH CRB 10 is required to avoid collisions with MSG3. Unlike FDD the K2 distance between MSG2 and MSG3 is not being increased for TDD to avoid MSG3 and SAg collisions. The additional distance required for TDD is too great and will increase the setup time too much. As such measures have to be taken in the frequency domain to avoid collisions.

Note 2: When allocating PRBs for 2x and 4x SAg UEs the FD scheduler must evaluate the slots that the PUSCH repetitions will occupy to ensure that there will not be PRB collisions with PRBs that are already reserved, e.g. PRACH, Configured Grants, UCI-only aCSI reports. If there are PRB collisions in any of the SAg PUSCH repetitions the PRB allocation (i.e. PRB location) must be changed so that there are no collisions with reserved PRBs.

---

#### 3.30.15.6.0-3 SAg Frequency Domain Scheduling Impacts This requirement is for TDD cells only. The objective of selecting the PRB location for 2x and 4x SAg PRB allocations is:  Minimize PUSCH fragmentation  Provide frequency diversity for a UE’s SAg transmissions The FD scheduler shall use the following algorithm to select the PRB location for 2x and 4x SAg PRB allocations: 1. The 2x and 4x SAg UEs shall be allocated PRBs (physical location) before other UEs in that slot. 2. For 2x and 4x SAg initial transmissions the PRBs shall be allocated from the first available PUSCH after CRB 10 (see Note1) without causing PRB collisions in any of the PUSCH repetitions (see Note 2). Note 1: Starting PUSCH CRB 10 is required to avoid collisions with MSG3.  Unlike FDD the K2 distance between MSG2 and MSG3 is not being increased for TDD to avoid MSG3 and SAg collisions.  The additional distance required for TDD is too great and will increase the setup time too much.  As such measures have to be taken in the frequency domain to avoid collisions. Note 2:  When allocating PRBs for 2x and 4x SAg UEs the FD scheduler must evaluate the slots that the PUSCH repetitions will occupy to ensure that there will not be PRB collisions with PRBs that are already reserved, e.g. PRACH, Configured Grants, UCI-only aCSI reports.  If there are PRB collisions in any of the SAg PUSCH repetitions the PRB allocation (i.e. PRB location) must be changed so that there are no collisions with reserved PRBs. [PR884900] if NRCELL.actDynUlResAlloc ='True' FD Scheduler shall allocate PRBs for a 2x and 4x SAg UE only within the static region of the PUSCH (defined by parameters longPucchStartPuschPrb and longPucchNumPuschPrb) [PR884900 END]. (ID: 13491825)

### 3.30.15.7 SAg Mode Interworking With CA

#### 3.30.15.7.0-1 SAg Mode Interworking With CA CA Scheduling with Slot Aggregation (ID: 11205370)

**3.30.15.7.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12657552`)

SAg Mode Interworking With CA L2 PS shall interwork with CA as defined below,for PUSCH Slot aggregation:

[PR835544]

If (NRCELL.actForcedDftSOfdmVonrUl is set to TRUE **AND 5QI1 DRB is configured for the UE** AND (FDD + TDD FR1 R15 UL CA UE **or inter-band FDD + FDD UL CA UE** or inter-band TDD FR1 + TDD FR1 UL CA UE))**_*_** *

  * Refer to [12652679](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YQ-LkUYdEfCO0aKCUF-F5A?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YQ-LkUYdEfCO0aKCUF-F5A?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9") for details. Note: Inter band case



Else

[End PR835544]

  * UE **enters the TP enabled::SAG On state (see 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):**
  * SCell inactivity timer management:
  * See 5G_UP_ALG_18265_replaced([11120682](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqTDAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * For SCell UL Scheduling:
  * If NRCELL.sCellDeactInPoorRf = 'None':
  * If the UE is configured with FDD + TDD FR1 UL Tx switching
  * TDD SCell with UL Tx switching is deactivated.
  * Else
  * Suspend PUSCH scheduling on SCell, if not already suspended due to 5G_UP_ALG_10193_replaced([11123394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqi6lfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

  * Else If NRCELL.sCellDeactInPoorRf= ‘Deact_Scells_VoNr_SAg’ : all SCells are deactivated

  * Notes:
  * Deactivation of DL SCells also deactivates associated UL SCells (no UL Scells without DL ) and there is no need to suspend PUSCH scheduling on SCells.
  * For a buffer based reactivation scenario: no reactivation is allowed if UE is in TP enabled::SAG On state (See 5G_UP_ALG_2716_replaced([11121013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqWGQPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

  * UE **exits the TP enabled::SAG On state (see 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

  * Enable PUSCH scheduling on SCell
  * For a buffer based reactivation scenario: reactivation is allowed(see 5G_UP_ALG_2715_replaced([11120998](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqVfPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))



Notes:

  * Intent for "suspending" UL on Scell (and possibly deactivating DL CA) is to prevent PCell/SCell power split by UE and improve SAg performance. this could be accomplished by either suspending monitoring or ignoring the monitoring result.
  * L2-Hi does not need to be informed about suspension

---

### 3.30.15.8 SAg interworking with L1

#### 3.30.15.8.0-1 SAg interworking with L1 SAg L1 PUSCH receive requests (ID: 11205395)

**3.30.15.8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11205400`)

SAg interworking with L1 When L2-PS sends an uplink grant for 2x or 4x repetitions, L2-PS must send a PUSCH receive request to L1 for each PUSCH repetition. The receive request shall specify which repetition is being received so that L1 can determine which repetition is the last repetition in the bundle.

L1 will report the results of the SAg repetition decodes to L2 per the details in 5G_UP_ALG_19635_replaced([11104383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn8SgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB008236-A-UPD002] L1 will only report valid CRC and DTX results for the final repetition in the SAg bundle. Therefore L2-PS shall ignore the CRC and DTX results reported for the intermediate repetitions. [End CB008236-A-UPD002]

---

#### 3.30.15.8.0-2 SAg interworking with L1 SAg L1 UCI receive requests (ID: 11205407)

**3.30.15.8.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11205414`)

SAg interworking with L1 HARQ A/N can be embedded in one or more of the SAg bundle repetitions. If HARQ A/N is embedded in any of the individual repetitions L2-PS must send a UCI receive request to L1 to decode the HARQ A/N bits. If the HARQ A/N bits are decoded L1 shall forward it to L2-PS.

Periodic CSI and Beam Management reports will not be embedded in SAg bundle repetitions due to the number of bits that can be contained in these reports, particularly when multiple reports are bundled into one report. L2-PS shall not schedule SAg bundle repetitions in slots that contain periodic CSI and/or Beam Management reports.

---

### 3.30.15.9 SAg interworking UCI on PUSCH

#### 3.30.15.9.0-1 SAg interworking UCI on PUSCH SAg Specific Beta Offsets for HARQ A/N (ID: 11205427)

**3.30.15.9.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11205442`)

SAg interworking UCI on PUSCH If UE is in TP Enabled::SAG ON state (see 5G_UP_ALG_18906_replaced([11204759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwlRLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS shall select the working beta offset set for HARQ A/N bits as follows:

Source of Beta Offset values:

  * Slot aggregation (SAg) specific BetaOffset list for Ack bits received in cell setup : betaOffsetACK-Index1 2x/4x, betaOffsetACK-Index2 SAG, betaOffsetACK-Index3 SAG
  * Beta Offset values received in the betaOffsetConfig structure in user setup/modify message



Usage:

For ‘Dynamic’ case:

  * If (dynamicBetaOffsetInd = true) and (enableDynamicBetaOffsets = ‘enableFull’ or ‘enableLimited’, (both 5G_UP_ALG_3500_replaced([11159329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))):
  * For DCI 0_1:
  * If SAg repetitions = 1x: use Ack BetaOffsets which are not SAG-specific
  * Use the non-SAg specific (legacy) Ack values from cell Setup associated with 'DEFAULT' ; Beta offset indicator field in DCI = 3
  * If SAg repetitions = 2x or 4x: use Ack BetaOffsets which are SAG-specific
  * If enableDynamicBetaOffsets = ‘enableFull’ (5G_UP_ALG_3500_replaced([11159329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):
  * Use betaOffsetACK-Index1 2x/4x, betaOffsetACK-Index2 SAG,betaOffsetACK-Index3 SAG from SAg specific BetaOffset list; Beta offset indicator field in DCI = 2
  * Else If enableDynamicBetaOffsets = ‘enableLimited’ (5G_UP_ALG_3500_replaced([11159329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * Use betaOffsetACK-Index1 2x/4x, betaOffsetACK-Index2 SAG,betaOffsetACK-Index3 SAG from SAg specific BetaOffset list; Beta offset indicator field in DCI = 0

  * [CB013742-A] when NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to disable, [End CB013742-A]for Reconfiguration transient phase (DCI 0_0): use Ack BetaOffsets which are not SAG-specific

  * Use values received in User Setup/Modify (DCI 0_0 does not have a BetaOffset field)
  * Note: This is sequence index 0 in dynamic BetaOffset configuration at RRC layer, per 3GPP specification for DCI 0_0. It is the equivalent of BETA_OFFSET_INDEX_CONSERVATIVE (0) for A-CSI.



For ‘semiStatic’ case:

  * If dynamicBetaOffsetInd = false or enableDynamicBetaOffsets = ‘disable' (5G_UP_ALG_3500_replaced([11159329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): i.e. semiStatic case: use Ack BetaOffsets which are SAG-specific
  * Use values received in either User Modify (for reconfiguration to SAG ON) or User Setup (in HO scenario where SAG On state is maintained and target cell only supports semiStatic)



Notes:

  * Use of dynamic BetaOffset offers a better solution to get SAG-specific Ack related BetaOffsets to UE at RRC. However, population of commercial UEs which support dynamicBetaOffsetInd = true is expected to be small within time frame of PUSCH Slot Aggregation introduction. Thereofre support for semiStatic BetaOffset mode is required.
  * In a RRC ReConfiguration instance which would place UE in a TP Enabled::SAG ON state, CP may configure UE for either dynamic or semiStatic BetaOffsets
  * For semiStatic case, CP will use the values of betaOffsetACK-Index1 2x/4x, betaOffsetACK-Index2 SAG, betaOffsetACK-Index3 SAG for the betaOffsetACK components of the BetaOffset structure of the User Modify message. From L2 PS perspective, this is the same source as BETA_OFFSET_INDEX_CONSERVATIVE (0) in User Setup/Modify. For all other RRC ReConfiguration instances (including instance to get UE out of TP Enabled::SAG ON state), CP will use non-SAg specific values.
  * enableDynamicBetaOffsets (5G_UP_ALG_3500_replaced([11159329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value received in the cell setup request message.
  * The non-SAg specific (legacy) Ack values from cell Setup associated with 'DEFAULT': refers to the same Ack values within BETA_OFFSET_INDEX_ DEFAULT (3) received at cell setup for A-CSI (dynamicBetaOffsetsList[2] in the cell setup request message). CP will use these values (from non-SAg specific R&D parameters) to configure SAg UE at RRC layer with dynamic BetaOffsets index 3. See 5G_UP_ALG_3561_replaced([11159600](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktKwxfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for a reference.
  * A SAg grant with 1x, 2x, or 4x repetitions will never include a request for A-CSI. Repetitions (1x,2x,4x) in this context means number of UL slots with PUSCH transmission in a SAg bundle.

---

#### 3.30.15.9.0-2 SAg interworking UCI on PUSCH SAg Specific 1st DAI and Number of HARQ_AN Bits to Decode (ID: 11205450)

**3.30.15.9.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11205455`)

SAg interworking UCI on PUSCH [PR713960]

L2 PS shall set the 1st Downlink assignment index field (1st DAI) of a SAg grant and handle the HARQ_AN UCI within a SAg Bundle as defined below.

If there are DL HARQ_AN bits accumulated on any slot in the SAg Bundle:

  * For SAg Repetitions = 1x:
  * Set per 5G_UP_ALG_4466_replaced([11178897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2y0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (same as non-SAG Mode case)
  * For SAg Repetitions = 2x or 4x:
  * Set 1st DAI = 3
  * Number of UCI HARQ_AN bits in PUSCH decode request:
  * Equal to 0 bits if no HARQ_AN bits accumulated within the SAG Bundle or 4 bits otherwise



This applies for both of the following scenarios:

  * For one or more slots within a SAg Bundle overlaping a PUCCH with HARQ_AN resources
  * For no slots within a SAg Bundle overlaping a PUCCH with HARQ_AN resources



Notes:

  * The UL 1st DAI is applicable when Harq-codebook = dynamic and refers to T-DAI
  * 1st DAI = 3 interpreted as 0 or 4 bits
  * Differences from 5G_UP_ALG_4466_replaced([11178897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2y0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SAg 2x/4x case:
  * If PUCCH resources are from enhanced DPA 1st DAI = 3 instead of max T-DAI if T-DAI >=9
  * Per 3GPP: The 1st DAI setting in a SAG grant appies to all slots within the SAG Bundle and overrides the DL DAI for all slots
  * DPA = Distributed PUCCH Allocation algorithm
  * SAG specific BetaOffsets are used per 5G_UP_ALG_21585_replaced([11205427](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwpikvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * If OACK is less than 4 bits then only OACK are considered in decode result; where OACK represents the number of Harq-Ack bits received from DL scheduler prior to SAg grant which correspond to the number of HARQ_AN accumulating in a SAg Bundle slot.



[End PR713960]

---

## 3.30.16 UL UE-AMBR

### 3.30.16.0-1 UL UE-AMBR Overview (ID: 11205469)

**3.30.16.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11205474`)

UL UE-AMBR When UL UE AMBR (Aggregated Maximum Bit Rate) is activated (NRBTS.actUlAmbr is set to "SA"), gNB-DU shall enforce Uplink UE AMBR for non-GBR DRBs according to the signaled ulUeAmbr (Uplink UE-AMBR) value. Since for UL, gNB-DU groups the bearer into LCGs, UL UE-AMBR enforcement shall be done for all the LCGs that has non-GBR data bearers.

Note:ulUeAmbr is configured by the higher layers from F1-AP parameter "GNB-DU-UE-AMBR-UL"

---

### 3.30.16.0-3 UL UE-AMBR AMBR Per Slot (ID: 11205488)

**3.30.16.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11205495`)

UL UE-AMBR When is ulUeAmbr is configured/modified by the higher layers, gNB-DU shall do the conversion to ambr per uplink data slot as unit for UlUeAmbr is per second.

ulAmbrPerSlot = roundupInteger(UlUeAmbr/num_ul_data_slot_in_1sec)

Where

num_ul_slot_in_1sec : Number of UL data slots and it depends on Frame configuration as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_2293_replaced([11114641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpmfaPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.30.16.0-4 UL UE-AMBR Accumulated AMBR (ID: 11205502)

**3.30.16.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11205507`)

UL UE-AMBR When ulUeAmbr is configured, If avgAmbrPerUlSlot (5G_UP_ALG_19975_replaced([11205514](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) exceeds ulAmbrPerSlot (5G_UP_ALG_19982_replaced([11205488](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) then gNB-DU shall calculate accumulated AMBR between two initial transmission. This shall be used for rate capping the data rate of non-GBR data bearers.

accumulatedAmbr = rrmNumUlSlotSinceLastSchedulingWithNewTransmission * ulAmbrPerSlot

---

### 3.30.16.0-5 UL UE-AMBR Average AMBR  Calculation (ID: 11205514)

**3.30.16.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11205522`)

UL UE-AMBR When ulUeAmbr is configured by the higher layers for an UE, gNB-DU shall calculate the average AMBR using exponential filter.

avgAmbrPerUlSlot(n) = (1/rdAvgUlAmbrFilterConst) * rcvdBitsInUlSlot + avgAmbrPerUlSlot(n-rrmNumUlSlotSinceLastComputed) * (1 - 1/rdAvgUlAmbrFilterConst) pwr rrmNumUlSlotSinceLastComputed

Where

n : Current Slot on which the data is received.

rcvdBitsInUlSlot : number of bits received for non-GBR user data bearer(s) after MAC de-multiplexing in the last slot from L2Lo [CB008234-D] + number of bits received from SCell, for FR1 UL CA when Scell is activated [End CB008234-D]

rdAvgUlAmbrFilterConst : Filter constant for the exponential filter (5G_UP_ALG_19980_replaced([11209196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyFevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmNumUlSlotsSinceLastComputed : Number of UL slots since last avgAmbrPerUlSlot computed

Initial value of avgAmbrPerUlSlot is set to 0.

L2-PS shall reset Average AMBR (avgAmbrPerUlSlot) value to 0 when the higher layer configured value of UL UE-AMBR is modified to a lower value.

Rationale:When UE is not scheduled, its AMBR on time decreases. And hence exponential filter is applied to smoothen the moving average.

---

## 3.30.17 Recommended Bit Rate Indication

### 3.30.17.0-1 Recommended Bit Rate Indication Overview (ID: 12814436)

**3.30.17.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12814437`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication gNB-DU dynamically adjust the data rate of VoNR(5QI-1) or [CB006093-C] MC-PTT(5QI-65/66) [End CB006093-C] and [CB006093-B] ViNR(5QI-2) [End CB006093-B] or [CB006093-C] 5QI-67 [End CB006093-C] bearer to match the available bit rate. This adaptation is not continuous but occurs in discrete increments or decrements known as steps to prevent rapid, unstable fluctuations and ensure controlled adjustments.

The gNB-DU continuously compares the UE's current average ABR MAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) (which mapped to RBR index with the closest value below the average ABR) with the previously recommended bit rate. The instantaneous ABR is calculated for each UE in the uplink (UL), typically after receiving a Power Headroom Report (PHR) from the UE. The ABR is then averaged over a configurable period of time to smooth out short-term fluctuations.

Bit rate adaptation is triggered, only when the average ABR MAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) changes by the configured step size (rbrStepSizeUp/rbrStepSizeDown). Using a step size, ensures that these adaptations only occur when there is a meaningful change in channel conditions, providing stability to the system.

Separate, configurable step sizes are used for increasing (up-switching) and decreasing (down-switching) the bit rate.

gNB-DU provides an appropriate RBR indication to the UE (capable of RBR/RBR Query). However, it remains the responsibility of the UE to upgrade or downgrade the codec data rate accordingly.

[CB006093-C] Available Bit Rate ([12799609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_sjLOUpnAEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) calculation shall not start for a UE which is configured with UL CG Type 2. [End CB006093-C]

---

### 3.30.17.0-2 Recommended Bit Rate Indication Instantaneous Available Bit Rate (ABR) of the UE in UL (ID: 12799609)

**3.30.17.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12799610`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication The instantaneous available bit rate (ABR) of a UE (having at least one active VoNR or [CB006093-B] ViNR [End CB006093-B] bearer) is calculated each time a PHR report is received.

The ABR is derived from the maximum number of PRBs that could be allocated to the UE given the measured SINR and the reported PHR condition that can be supported by the UE with PHR >= 0 and the selected MCS [CB006093-C] and Rank [End CB006093-C] based on SINR in the ATB.

The ABR is derived from the max. number of PRBs and the selected MCS based on SINR in the ATB.

rbrABR = rbrRatio * TBSi {rbrMaxPrb, rbrMCSi, [CB006093-C] rbrUlRank [End CB006093-C]}.

where,

  * For non-power limited UE, rbrMaxPrb, rbrMCSi, rbrUlRank refer to rrmMaxNumPuschPrb and selected MCS, UL Rank proposed by Link adaptation in [11131696](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krD38vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
  * [CB006093-C] For power limited UE, 
  * rbrMaxPrb and rbrMCSi refer to rrmMaxNumPrbULATB in [11132066](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krEfCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
  * if link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency for UL is enabled for UL ([12472235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Of9dYA1aEfCuhewgqkUVKQ)), rbrMaxPrb and rbrMCSi refer to PRB_N and MCS_N defined in [12472293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kRoEYg3SEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) [End CB006093-C].
  * TBSi() looking up TBS mapping table with rbrMaxPrb and rbrMCSi, rbrUlRank in [11203344: Transport Block Size Calculation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).
  * The rbrRatio indicates a multiplier applied on the calculated ARB to adjust the proposed RBR.
  * Note: For power limited UE it refers to the case that the ATB is triggered for UE in [11132066: MPR and ATB PUSCH allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krEfCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)



The calculated rbrABR value is equal to the TBSi [bits], then transform into [kbps] and mapping to RBR index in[12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and[12803195](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0frmAJ2eEfCkxIKMlo4L6w?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) before sending it as RBR recommendation to the UE.

---

### 3.30.17.0-3 Recommended Bit Rate Indication Calculation of average ABR in UL (ID: 12799843)

**3.30.17.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12799844`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication The average available bit rate (Avg ABR MAC, UL(t)) is calculated out of NABR samples of instantaneous available bit rate(rbrABR) ([12799609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_sjLOUpnAEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) values. The frequency of the instantaneous ABR value follows the periodicity of UE PHR reports. The instantaneous ABR ([12799609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_sjLOUpnAEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is averaged over a configurable period of time (rbrMeasTime) to create a stable average ABR value, which is used for generating bit rate recommendations.

NABRis determined by the operator configurable window size (rbrMeasTime).

NABR =(rbrMeasTime*1000)/ptime, where ptime = 20 [ms].

Avg ABRMAC, UL(t) = (rbrABR(t) + (NABR -1) * Avg ABRMAC, UL(t-1)) / NABR.

where:

  * t: moment of the last UE PHR report received.
  * rbrABR(t): Last ABR sample as instant TBS value.
  * ptime: periodicity of RTP packets during active talk. Constant value of 20ms.
  * Avg ABR MAC, UL(t-1): Previous average ABR value.

---

### 3.30.17.0-4 Recommended Bit Rate Indication Recommended Bit Rate (ID: 12801140)

**3.30.17.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12815685`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Recommended Bit Rate MAC CE

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12815686`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication 12815686: Recommended Bit Rate Indication The bit rate recommendation depends on:

  * RF conditions of the UE (UL-SINR)

  * Power headroom (PHR) of the UE

  * configuration settings (NIDD)




gNB-DU to send codec adaptation indication with recommended bit rate to assist the UE to select or adapt to a codec rate for voice(5QI-1) or [CB006093-C] MC-PTT(5QI-65/66) [End CB006093-C] and [CB006093-B] video(5QI-2) [End CB006093-B] or [CB006093-C] 5QI-67 [End CB006093-C] bearer. Recommended bit rate (RBR) is applicable to increase or decrease to improve voice or Video quality and their coverage and capacity.

The gNB-DU may transmit the Recommended bit rate MAC CE to the UE for a specific logical channel and a specific direction (either uplink or downlink).

There are two cases when Recommended Bit Rate MAC Control Element is used by gNB-DU:

  * gNB indicates recommended bit rate in UL to UE by unsolicited RBR message.
  * UE asks for desired recommended bit rate in UL/DL by sending RBR query.



The UE may send RBR Query (Via RBR MAC) to the gNB-DU to indicate the recommended bit rate for a specific logical channel and a specific direction with desire bit rate. The RBR conveyed as an index mapped into Kbit/sec bit rate in Table 6.1.3.20-1, 3GPP TS 38.321.

The RBR MAC CE is identified by a MAC sub header with LCID as specified in Tables 6.2.1-1 and 6.2.1-2 for bit rate recommendation message from the gNB-DU to the UE and bit rate recommendation query message from the UE to the gNB, respectively.

[image-0][image_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the structure of a "Recommended bit rate MAC CE". The diagram is divided into two octets, Oct 1 and Oct 2, and further segmented into fields. Oct 1 contains three fields: \- LCID: This field occupies a significant portion of Oct 1. \- UL/DL: This field is positioned to the right of LCID. \- Bit Rate: This field is to the right of UL/DL. Oct 2 contains three fields: \- Bit Rate: This field occupies the left portion of Oct 2. \- X: This field is to the right of the "Bit Rate" field in Oct 2. \- R: This field is to the right of X. \- R: This field is to the right of the first R. The text "Recommended bit rate MAC CE" is displayed below the diagram. The diagram also includes horizontal tick marks above the structure, suggesting a timeline or a sequence of bits.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/img-09261354.png[/image_path][/image-0]

  * LCID: This field indicates the identity of the logical channel for which the recommended bit rate or the recommended bit rate query is applicable. The length of the field is 6 bits.
  * Uplink/Downlink (UL/DL): This field indicates whether the recommended bit rate or the recommended bit rate query applies to uplink or downlink. The length of the field is 1 bit. The UL/DL field set to 0 indicates downlink. The UL/DL field set to 1 indicates uplink.
  * Bit Rate: This field indicates an index to Table 6.1.3.20-1. The length of the field is 6 bits. For bit rate recommendation the value indicates the recommended bit rate. For bit rate recommendation query the value indicates the desired bit rate.
  * X: Bit rate multiplier. For UEs supporting recommended bit rate multiplier, when bitrate Multiplier is configured for the logical channel indicated by LCID field, X field set to 1 indicates the actual value of bit rate is the value corresponding to the index indicated by the Bit Rate field multiplied by bitrate Multiplier as specified in TS 38.331 [5].
  * R: reserved bit, set to 0

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12817115`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Scheduling the Transmission of RBR MAC CE

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12817116`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication There is no Independent Scheduling for RBR MAC CE to trigger the scheduling of the DL resources (means the gNB-DU shall not schedule a DL transmission to a UE solely to send a RBR MAC CE). The RBR MAC CE shall be appended to a DL transmission that is already scheduled for the UE for other reasons (e.g., PDSCH data).

The allocation of DL resources shall consider the size of the RBR MAC CE and MAC sub-header for RBR to determine the required TBS.

The priority of the DL transmission is not modified by the inclusion of the RBR MAC CE.

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `12824850`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Online activation of actRbr (False to True):

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12824851`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication If actRbr is enabled by modifying(online) from False to True:

  * gNB-DU shall not start ABR measurements and no longer send unsolicited RBR messages for VoNR(5QI-1 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-65/66 [End CB006093-C]) and [CB006093-B] ViNR (5QI-2/5QI-67) [End CB006093-B] bearer to the existing UE (capable of RBR).
  * gNB-DU shall start ABR measurements and sending unsolicited RBR messages for VoNR(5QI-1/65/66) and ViNR(5QI-2/67) bearer to the new UE (capable of RBR).
  * The gNB-DU shall ignore all subsequent RBR queries, it receives from the existing UE but accept RBR queries from the new UE and this behavior is independent of whether the UE's configuration, such as the bitRateQueryProhibitTimer has been re-configured.

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `12819304`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Online de-activation of actRbr (True to False):

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12819305`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication If actRbr is disabled by modifying(online) from True to False:

  * The gNB-DU shall stop ABR measurements and no longer send unsolicited RBR messages to the existing or new UE (having VoNR/ [CB006093-B] ViNR [End CB006093-B] bearer established).
  * The gNB-DU shall ignore all subsequent RBR queries it receives from the UE (both old and New UE). This behavior is independent of whether the UE's configuration, such as the bitRateQueryProhibitTimer has been de-configured.

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `12801188`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Determination of unsolicited Recommended Bit Rate (RBR)

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `12801189`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Unsolicited recommended bit rate (RBR) is a mechanism where gNB-DU proactively sends a bit rate recommendation to the UE without the UE having first sent a query. This process is a key component of RAN-assisted codec adaptation, designed to dynamically adjust the codec bit rate for voice and [CB006093-B] video [End CB006093-B] calls based on changing RF conditions.

Recommended Bit Rate (RBR) is determined based on the Available Bit Rate (ABR), which the gNB calculates for each UE. The sum of the RBR for all of the UE's DRBs must not exceed the UE's total calculated average ABR MAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Since the ABR is calculated based on UL measurements (PHR and UL-SINR), unsolicited RBR messages are sent for the UL direction only. The primary goal is to optimize the user experience by maintaining service quality and extending coverage for VoNR and [CB006093-B] ViNR [End CB006093-B]. By recommending a lower bit rate in poor RF conditions, the gNB shall prevent call drops and service degradation. Conversely, by recommending a higher bit rate in good conditions, improve voice and [CB006093-B] video [End CB006093-B] quality.

The first unsolicited RBR message is sent only after a sufficient number of PHR reports (e.g., 100 samples by default) have been collected to establish a reliable average ABR MAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). This can take several seconds after a VoNR or [CB006093-B] ViNR [End CB006093-B] bearer is established, depending on the PHR reporting frequency.

**[CB006093-C] When PDCCH/PDSCH/PUSCH GBR congestion is detected ([11201668:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) UL, [11183557:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) DL), the gNB-DU shall stop calculating ABR and stop sending any RBR message to the UE and suspension of RBR messages continues until the GBR congestion is resolved [**End CB006093-C].****

The gNB-DU sends unsolicited RBR messages to either increase (up-switch) or decrease (down-switch) the codec bit rate for a specific bearer in either the uplink (UL) or downlink (DL). The bit rate is conveyed as an index (3GPP TS 38.321, table:6.1.3.20-1) that maps to a value in kbps.

[CB006093-C] The calculation of available bit rate (ABR) and RBR indication are not applied to the UE, that is configured with a CG2 profile ([11173004](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), but the release of CG Type 2 effectively reverses the exclusion, making the bearer eligible for ABR calculation and RBR indication again. Note: The CG2 configuration is per UE, if UE have two DRB configured with CG2 profile and, in case one of DRB is released, the UE is still configured with CG2 profile. Hence, gNB-DU shall stop ABR calculation and RBR indication to the UE.

[End CB006093-C].

Note:

  * Up-Switching: When RF conditions improve, the average ABR MAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) increases. If the increase exceeds the rbrStepSizeUp threshold, the gNB-DU can recommend a higher bit rate.

  * Down-Switching: When a UE moves into poorer RF conditions, the average ABR MAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) decreases. If this decrease crosses a configurable step-size threshold (rbrStepSizeDown), the gNB sends an unsolicited RBR message recommending a lower bit rate. There are configurable lower limits for down-switching, such as rbrIndxAudioLoThreshold for voice and [CB006093-B] rbrIndxVideoLoThreshold for video [End CB006093-B], to prevent the bit rate from dropping too low.

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-6**  (ID: `12801244`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Execution of the unsolicited RBR

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12801245`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication If the variation of the average ABR MAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) (changed to the RBR index with the closest value below the average ABR ) is not exceeding the step size for up or down-switching, but the lower or upper boundary of the possible RBR index range is reached, an unsolicited RBR message shall be sent by the gNB.

The lower boundary for VoNR(5QI-1 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-65/66 [End CB006093-C]) is given by the parameter rbrIndxAudioLoThreshold, while the upper boundary for Voice shall be GFBR of the UEs VoNR bearer.

[CB006093-B] The lower boundary for ViNR(5QI-2 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-67 [End CB006093-C]) is given by the parameter rbrIndxVideoLoThreshold), while the upper boundary for Video shall be GFBR of the UEs ViNR bearer [End CB006093-B].

The sending of an unsolicited RBR message shall be triggered, if the following conditions is met:

Current RBR index for VoNR and [CB006093-B] ViNR [End CB006093-B] DRB shall be calculated as follows:

CurrentRbrIndex(VoNR) = max (rbrIndxAudioLoThreshold, min (RBRi,RBRx))

where,

RBRx= Converted value of GFBR(VoNR) to an RBR index.

RBRi= Converted value of Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) to an RBR index from a standardized RBR table in 6.1.3.20-1(3GPP TS 38.321). This value ensures the recommended bit rate does not exceed the Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

If the UE have at least one ViNR bearer, gNB-DU shall calculate remaining bit rate (in Kbps) for ViNR DRB, the reverse conversion from RBR index to a bit rate(kbps) is used by standardized RBR table in 6.1.3.20-1(3GPP TS 38.321).

Convert RBR(VoNR) in to bit rate(kbps).

[CB006093-B]

Case1(If UE have one active ViNR bearer):

Remaining bit rate(kbps) = Last calculated Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) - RBR(VoNR).

Case2(If UE have multiple active ViNR bearer):

Remaining bit rate(kbps) = (Last calculated Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) - RBR(VoNR)) / N.

where,

N= Number of ViNR bearers.

RBRj= Converted value of remaining bit rate to an RBR index from a standardized RBR table in 6.1.3.20-1(3GPP TS 38.321). This value ensures the recommended bit rate does not exceed the remaining bit rate, calculated for ViNR bearer.

CurrentRbrIndex(ViNR) = max (rbrIndxVideoLoThreshold, min (RBRj, RBRy)).

where,

RBRy = Converted value of GFBR(ViNR) to an RBR index.

[End CB006093-B]

rbrStepSize = (currentRbrIndex – lastRbrIndex).

if (rbrStepSize == Zero) //no change of RBR index value//

void;

else if (rbrStepSize < Zero) //down-switching case //

{

if (rbrStepSize) >= rbrStepSizeDown)

Trigger bit rate recommendation with reduced RBR index for the voice bearer by the configured step size (I.e., rbrStepSizeDown)

[CB006093-B]

if (rbrStepSize) >= rbrStepSizeDown)

Trigger bit rate recommendation with reduced RBR index for the video bearer by the configured step size (I.e., rbrStepSizeDown)

[End CB006093-B]  
}

else if (The waiting period before the next unsolicited RBR message for up-switching> rbrUpSwitchTimer) // up-switching case//  
{

if (rbrStepSize >= rbrStepSizeUp )

Trigger bit rate recommendation with increased RBR index for Voice or [CB006093-B] Video [End CB006093-B] bearer by the configured step size (I.e., rbrStepSizeUp)

else if (currentRbrIndex(Kbps) == GFBR)

Trigger bit rate recommendation with currentRbrIndex for Voice or [CB006093-B] Video [End CB006093-B] bearer.

}

where:

  * lastRbrIndex: RBR table index of the last RBR recommendation
  * currentRbrIndex - RBR table index of the current RBR recommendation
  * rbrIndxAudioLoThreshold: minimum value of RBR index to be used for VoNR (5QI-1 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-65/66 [End CB006093-C]), default value:2.
  * rbrIndxVideoLoThreshold: minimum value of RBR index to be used for ViNR (5QI2 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-67 [End CB006093-C]), default value:16.



If the above conditions evaluate to trigger RBR, a MAC RBR CE shall be composed of:

  * MAC CE RBR.LCID = LCID of the VoNR or ViNR bearer, MAC CE RBR.UL/DL = UL (1)
  * MAC CE RBR.BitRate = currentRbrIndex(VoNR) or currentRbrIndex(ViNR),



In addition, if rbrUpSwitchTimer> 0, the wait timer for up-switching shall be restarted.

Notes:

  * The algorithm provides an always appropriate RBR indication to the UE. However, it remains the responsibility of the UE to upgrade or downgrade the codec data rate accordingly.
  * First unsolicited UL RBR index shall be sent to the UE once enough number of subsequent PHR reports is achieved (i.e. 100 samples by default, when rbrMeasTime equals to 2s, the distance period between PHR reports may vary between 10ms to 1000ms. so, the worst case would be 100 * 1000ms = 100000ms for SID packets, what means that first Unsolicited UL RBR index will be sent after 100 seconds from the moment of VoNR/ViNR bearer established.

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-7**  (ID: `12816232`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Unsolicited RBR sharing with multi VoNR/ViNR DRBs

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `12816233`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication The RBR MAC CE is sent by the gNB-DU to the UE refers to VoNR and ViNR bearer, identified by its LCID.   
For a UE with configured VoNR bearers (5QI-1 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-65/66 [End CB006093-C]), and/or ViNR bearers (5QI-2 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-67 [End CB006093-C]), the RBR message shall be sent based on the calculated average ABR MAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

The following criteria shall be used for RBR allocation by gNB-DU between VoNR & ViNR DRBs:

  * ABR resources shall be first allocated to voice bearer (due to DRB priority) to satisfy its maximum possible bit rate (within boundaries set by the GFBR of the Voice bearer) and any remaining resources shall be allocated to ViNR DRB.
  * If the UE has more than one concurrently active ViNR bearers, the RBR MAC CE shall be sent for each ViNR bearer (w/o considering the DRB priority) with equal resource sharing.
  * [CB006093-A-UPD003] For a UE with two VoNR bearers (one active, one on-hold), RBR indications are sent for both active and on-hold VoNR bearers, when the RBR trigger condition is met. Note: This behavior aligns with the current SW implementation.



After determining the RBR for each VoNR bearer, the following formula shall be used to calculate the remaining bit rate available for ViNR bearers:

Remaining ABR for ViNR (kbps) = Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) - max (BitRate_VoNR1, BitRate_VoNR2, ... BitRate_VoNRn )

where,

BitRate_VoNRn is the bit rate in kbps corresponding to the Current Index calculated for each VoNR bearer.

[End CB006093-A-UPD003].

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-8**  (ID: `12815741`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Determination of solicited Recommended Bit Rate (RBR)

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `12815742`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication The UE initiates the process by sending a RBR query to the gNB-DU to ask for a bit rate recommendation. The gNB-DU response to this query is the solicited recommended bit rate (RBR). A solicited RBR is a UE initiated procedure, and it is a formal way for the UE to ask its serving gNB-DU, if a specific, desired bit rate can be sustained for Voice(5QI-1 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-65/66 [End CB006093-C]) and [CB006093-B] Video(5QI-2 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-67 [End CB006093-C]) [End CB006093-B] Service.

The UE send a RBR Query to the gNB-DU and this query is often triggered, when the UE needs to check, if a bit rate suggested by its peer (the other UE in a voice or [CB006093-B] video [End CB006093-B] call) can be supported by its serving gNB-DU.

The query is sent as a MAC CE and specifies the desired bit rate, the logical channel (LCID) it applies to, and the direction (uplink or downlink).  
  
The gNB-DU receives the RBR Query (via MAC CE) from the UE and then analyzes the request based on its current GBR congestion ([11201708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and Avg ABR MAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). After its analysis, the gNB determines the highest bit rate it can sustainably support for that UE and bearer. It then sends a Recommended Bit Rate MAC CE to the UE containing this recommendation. The recommended rate cannot be higher than the GFBR for that bearer.

[CB006093-C] When PDCCH/PDSCH/PUSCH GBR congestion is detected ([11183557:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) DL, [11201668:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) UL), the gNB-DU stop responding or sending any RBR message to the UE and suspension of RBR messages continues until the GBR congestion is resolved [End CB006093-C]  
  
Upon receiving the gNB-DU response, the UE is expected to adapt its codec rate accordingly and should not attempt to use a bit rate higher than what the network recommended. To prevent excessive signaling, a bitRateQueryProhibitTimer shall be configured which stops the UE from sending the same query again for a specific duration.

[CB006093-C] The calculation of available bit rate (ABR) and RBR indication are not applied to the UE, that is configured with a CG2 profile ([11173004](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), but the release of CG Type 2 effectively reverses the exclusion, making the bearer eligible for ABR calculation and RBR indication again [End CB006093-C].

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-9**  (ID: `12803195`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Execution of UE Initiated RBR Query

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `12803196`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication The RBR Query refers to an active logical channel (LCID) of the UE and specifies a direction (UL/DL). The response of the gNB-DU shall be under the control of the bitRateQueryProhibitTimer.

UE trigger a RBR query for a logical channel, direction and desired bit rate and start the bitRateQueryProhibitTimer, if bitRateQueryProhibitTimer is configured.

Current RBR index for VoNR(5QI-1 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-65/66 [End CB006093-C] and [CB006093-B] ViNR(5QI-2 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-67 [End CB006093-C]) [End CB006093-B] DRB shall be calculated as follows:

CurrentRbrIndex(VoNR) = max (rbrIndxAudioLoThreshold, min (RBRi, RBRx)).

where,

RBRi= Converted value of Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) to an RBR index from a standardized RBR table in 6.1.3.20-1(3GPP TS 38.321). This value ensures the recommended bit rate does not exceed the Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

RBRx= Converted value of GFBR(VoNR) to an RBR index.

[CB006093-A-UPD003] For a UE with two VoNR bearers (one active, one on-hold), RBR indications are sent for both active and on-hold VoNR(see [12816232](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Ss49gaf9EfCrANRYxAlR5A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB006093-A-UPD003].

If the UE have at least one ViNR bearer, gNB-DU shall calculate remaining bit rate (in Kbps) for ViNR DRB, the reverse conversion from RBR index to a bit rate(kbps) is used by standardized RBR table in 6.1.3.20-1(3GPP TS 38.321).

Convert RBR(VoNR) in to bit rate(kbps).

[CB006093-B]

Remaining bit rate(kbps) = Last calculated Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) - RBR(VoNR).

CurrentRbrIndex(ViNR) = max (rbrIndxVideoLoThreshold, min (RBRj, RBRy)).

where,

RBRj= Converted value of remaining bit rate to an RBR index from a standardized RBR table in 6.1.3.20-1(3GPP TS 38.321). This value ensures the recommended bit rate does not exceed the remaining bit rate, calculated for ViNR bearer.

RBRy= Converted value of GFBR(ViNR) to an RBR index.

[End CB006093-B]

rbrIndxAudioLoThreshold: minimum value of RBR index to be used for VoNR (5QI-1/65/66), default value:2.

rbrIndxVideoLoThreshold: minimum value of RBR index to be used for ViNR (5QI2/67), default value:16.

The response to an “early” RBR query (I.e., one immediately after VoNR or [CB006093-B] ViNR [End CB006093-B] bearer establishment) is delayed until the first Avg ABRMAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) value becomes available.

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-10**  (ID: `12816238`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Handling Uplink vs Downlink RBR Queries

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `12816239`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication For a RBR query regarding the UL direction, the gNB-DU responds with the calculated RBR value derived from the Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

For RBR Query in UL:

  * Determine the current RBRi that points to the closest RBR value below the average ABR MAC, UL (t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and trigger response to the RBR query in UL with current RBRi (via MAC CE).



For a RBR query regarding the DL direction, the response is controlled by the configuration parameter rbrDlQueryRespMode.

  * If set to 'UL_RBR', the gNB-DU reuses the calculated UL RBR value for the DL response. This assumes that the DL capacity is at least as high as the UL capacity.
  * If set to 'None', the gNB-DU responds with an RBR index of 0. This special index informs the UE that the previous recommendation is no longer valid, but no new bit rate is being recommended.



[CB006093-A-UPD003]

Note:

  * The UL and DL GFBR can be configured with different values. If the DL GFBR is configured to be lower than the UL GFBR and the UL radio conditions are good, the final RBR index sent to the UE in response of the DL query shall be min (DL/ULGFBR).



[End CB006093-A-UPD003]

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-11**  (ID: `12816159`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication Responding to Simultaneous RBR Queries

---

**3.30.17.0-4.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `12816160`)

Recommended Bit Rate Indication When the gNB-DU receives RBR queries for both voice (5QI-1 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-65/66 [End CB006093-C]) and video (5QI-1 or [CB006093-C] 5QI-67 [End CB006093-C]), it formulates its response based on the prioritized allocation of the single Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) value.

The gNB-DU first respond with an RBR index corresponding to the bit rate allocated to the voice (VoNR) bearer. This value is capped by the VoNR bearers GFBR.

The gNB responds with an RBR index corresponding to the remaining bit rate after the voice (VoNR) allocation. This is illustrated by the formula ([12803195](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0frmAJ2eEfCkxIKMlo4L6w?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for calculating the current RBR index for Video (ViNR), which subtracts the resources used by VoNR from the total Avg Avg ABR MAC, UL(t) ([12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

If a UE has more than one active video (ViNR) bearers, The RBR MAC CE shall be sent for each ViNR bearer (w/o considering the DRB priority) with equal resource sharing.

---

## 3.30.18 PLMN and Slice Awareness

### 3.30.18.1 Resource Partition Volume Control

#### 3.30.18.1.0-1 Resource Partition Volume Control Determine Low Buffer Traffic States of RGs (ID: 11205541)

**3.30.18.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11205732`)

Resource Partition Volume Control 11205732: Resource Partition Volume Control To decide the low buffer traffic states of all RGs, gNB-DU needs to execute computeLowBufferStateForRGs(). **[Before CB008250-AA]after executing computeFullBufferTempTargetShares().** **[CB008250-AA]** Precondition for determining low buffer states of RGs: full buffer temporary target shares are computed [Before PR841286] per 5G_UP_ALG_31111_replaced([11206276](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwrXxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, if** PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16121_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), **otherwise [End PR841286] per 5G_UP_ALG_26700_replaced([11207200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).****_*_ *[End CB008250-AA]** An RG j is considered to have low buffer traffic, i.e. hasLowBuffer(j) = True, if either of the following two conditions has been satisfied in the past rdMinLowBufferSlotsUL slots. Notations: * rrmRGAvgShare_j(t): Average resource share for RG _j at slot _t (see 5G_UP_ALG_23262_replaced([11207757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))_.**___[Before CB008250-AA]__* rrmRGLowBufferSlots_j: Number of consecutive slots in which RG_ j has low buffer traffic.___[CB008250-AA]__* rrmRGLowBufSlot_j(t): whether RG_ j has passed low buffer state check _for slot_ t.**__ *[End CB008250-AA]** * rdAvgShareToleranceUL: R&D parameter, the tolerance level for checking average resource share for an RG, see 5G_UP_ALG_23175_replaced([11209276](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwysh_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). * rdMinLowBufferSlotsUL: R&D parameter, the minimum number of consecutive slots for low buffer traffic state checking, see 5G_UP_ALG_23179_replaced([11209290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwysifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). * rrmRGLowBufferState_j: current low buffer traffic state of RG _j_ * rrmRGFullBufferTempTargetShare_j: temporary target share (including both high priority and low priority traffic) for RG _j, assuming each RG has full buffer low priority traffic and the actual high priority traffic, [Before PR841286]__[CB008250-AA] If_ _PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16121_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), see 5G_UP_ALG_31111_replaced([11206276](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwrXxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))__, else_ _[End CB008250-AA] [End PR841286] see 5G_UP_ALG_26700_replaced([11207200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))._**_*_**_* rrmRGInstShare_j(t): the instantaneous resource share used by RG j in slot t, see 5G_UP_ALG_23262_replaced([11207757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). * rrmRgIterm_k(t): the integral term of PI control for RG j, [Before PR841286] _ _[CB008250-AA] If_ _PLMN aware scheduler is activated for the cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16121_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), see 5G_UP_ALG_31220_replaced([11206541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwrXyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))__, else_ _[End CB008250-AA] [End PR841286] see 5G_UP_ALG_23167_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))._**_*_** When PI control is applied for RG weight computation, L2PS shall reset integral term for all RGs if at least one RG switches between low buffer state and high buffer state. The rationale is to reset integral term when there is an abrupt change in the expected low priority traffic target share rrmRGExpLowTargetShare which improves the transient behavior (having RG weight stabilize faster and with less overshoot).

---

#### 3.30.18.1.0-2 Resource Partition Volume Control Compute the Set of Non-empty Buffer RGs of a Cell or a PLMN (ID: 11205741)

**3.30.18.1.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11205835`)

Resource Partition Volume Control 11205835: Resource Partition Volume Control To compute the set of non-empty buffer RGs of a cell, gNB-DU needs to execute computeNonempBufRGs() **[CB008250-AA] with empty input parameter.** To compute the set of non-empty buffer RGs of PLMN p, gNB-DU needs to execute computeNonempBufRGs(p) with input parameter p. **[End CB008250-AA]** **[CB012238-B-UPD002]** computeNonempBufRGs() is called by SVC or enhanced SVC algorithm while computeNonempBufRGs(p) is called by PVC algorithm for a PLMN p. All variables with a subscript p are only used in computeNonempBufRGs(p) for PLMN aware scheduler. The corresponding variables without subscript p are used in computeNonempBufRGs() for legacy or enhanced slice aware scheduler. The purpose of merging computeNonempBufRGs() and computeNonempBufRGs(p) into one piece of algorithm is to avoid duplicating the same logic. **[End CB012238-B-UPD002]** An RG is considered to have empty buffer if no resource has been assigned to the RG in the past rdMinEmptyBufferSlotsUL slots. Let t be the current slot. // initialize to 0 for RG _j after RG_ j is created.**[image-0][image_desc]The image contains the following text:`rrrmRGEmpryBuf forSlots = 0`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16336.001.png[/image_path][/image-0], for every RG _j _Function Q = computeNonempBufRGs(__[CB008250-AA] p_ *[End CB008250-AA])_**_Set Q to be an empty set.__[CB008250-AA]__// empty parameter corresponds to computing the set of non-empty buffer RGs of a cell which is executed by (enhanced) SVC. Non-empty parameter p corresponds to computing the set of non-empty buffer RGs of PLMN p which is executed by PVC. The expression is: $t' \in [t - \text{randMinEmptyBufferSlotsUL} + 1, t]$.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16336.010.png[/image_path][/image-10]) _ _[End CB008250-AA]__Add_ j to Q ___[CB012238-B-UPD002]__emptyBufferRG = False Else If (p is empty) rrmExistEmptyBufferRGs = True Else rrmExistEmptyBufferRGs_p = True End If emptyBufferRG = True_ _[End CB012238-B-UPD002]__End If_ _[CB012238-B-UPD002]__If (p is empty) If (enhanced slice aware scheduler is not activated, see[11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_yoCzYPvWEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or (emptyBufferRG != hasLowBuffer(j)) rrmEmptyBufferRGsLowBufferRGsMatch = False End If Else If (emptyBufferRG != hasLowBuffer(j)) rrmEmptyBufferRGsLowBufferRGsMatch_p = False End If End If __[End CB012238-B-UPD002]__End For End Function Output: Q is the set of non-empty buffer RGs. Notations:__[Before CB008250-AA]__* rrmRGEmptyBufferSlots_j: Number of consecutive slots in which RG_ j has empty buffer. The value is not increased beyond rdMinEmptyBufferSlotsUL.___[CB008250-AA]__* rrmRGEmptyBufSlot_j(t): whether RG_ j has passed empty buffer state check _for slot_ t.**__ *[End CB008250-AA]** * rdMinEmptyBufferSlotsUL: R&D parameter, the minimum number of consecutive slots for RG empty buffer state checking, see 5G_UP_ALG_28677_replaced([11209303](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwysi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). * rrmRGInstShare_j(t): the instantaneous resource share used by RG j in slot t, see 5G_UP_ALG_23262_replaced([11207757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **[CB012238-B-UPD002]** * rrmExistEmptyBufferRGs: whether any of the RGs in the cell is in empty buffer state. * rrmExistEmptyBufferRGs_p: whether any of the RGs in PLMN p is in empty buffer state. * rrmEmptyBufferRGsLowBufferRGsMatch: whether the set of empty buffer RGs match exactly the set of low buffer RGs in the cell. * rrmEmptyBufferRGsLowBufferRGsMatch_p: whether the set of empty buffer RGs match exactly the set of low buffer RGs in PLMN p. **[End CB012238-B-UPD002]**
