**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `13415253`)

Slice Aware Scheduler **Slice/RG Weights for critical non-GBR**

---

**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `13415254`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Critical non-GBR/congestion de-prioritized critical GBR traffic (refer to [13399502](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rOoVAAsFEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is always exempted from the standard quota-based volume control. The scheduler allocates resources to critical non-GBR traffic based on a combination of its scheduling weight and a calculated maximum RG weight.

Other traffic types, such as non-critical non-GBR, de-prioritized GBR and de-prioritized DC-GBR is fully subject to the standard Slice Volume Control and the normal weight of their respective RGs.

For critical non-GBR, maximum RG weight is calculated:

(2 _rdEnhSvcPiPGainUL+1)_ rdLowBufRgWeightAmplifierUL with default R&D parameter values (refer to [11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Note: The RG weight is used for all scheduling computations. This includes the calculation of the PF metric for time-domain UE selection (refer to [11195815](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-0NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), as well as the determination of resource shares in both the prioritized (first round) and standard Weighted Round Robin (second round) allocation phases.

---

**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `11206891`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Algorithm of Enhanced SVC

---

**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-5.0-4**  (ID: `13493666`)

Slice Aware Scheduler If enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall compute an RG weight (see 5G_UP_ALG_23167_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for each configured RG in the cell with the enhanced SVC algorithm at the end of each UL slot.

The enhanced SVC algorithm consists of the following steps:

  * If QoS fairness slice aware scheduler is activated on parent gNB (NRBTS.actSliceAwareSchQosFairness = True) [CB016273-A] and intent-throughput slice aware scheduler is deactivated on parent gNB (NRBTS.actSliceAwareSchIntentTput = False)   
[End CB016273-A], computation of target shares of all RGs in the cell, according to 5G_UP_ALG_33087_replaced([11208531](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwvCJPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB016273-A] If QoS fairness slice aware scheduler is activated on parent gNB (NRBTS.actSliceAwareSchQosFairness = True) and intent-throughput slice aware scheduler is activated on parent gNB (NRBTS.actSliceAwareSchIntentTput = True), computation of target shares of all RGs in the cell, according to [13492570](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ch0buEPDEfGy8ZlFziQwLA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). [End CB016273-A] The target share of RG j is stored at rrmRGTargetShare_j.
  * computation of the low priority traffic target share for each RG (see 5G_UP_ALG_23157_replaced([11206914](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * feedback control based on the low priority traffic target share (see 5G_UP_ALG_23167_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
  * If enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and NRCELL.actNbrForNonGbrBearers=TRUE and NRCELL.nbrPuschCongHandling= TRUE, computation of dynamic target shares for non-empty buffer RGs (see 5G_UP_ALG_28672_replaced([11207717](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM8_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-6**  (ID: `11206914`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Computation of low priority traffic target share

---

**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `12639970`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 12639970: Slice Aware Scheduler This step computes the low priority traffic target share for each RG. The procedure is listed as follows. The current slot is denoted _t._ Output: the expected low priority traffic target share rrmRGExpLowTargetShare_j(t) for each RG _j_ computeFullBufferTempTargetShares() computeLowBufferStateForRGs() Make a list _S that contains all configured RGs in the cell._ Initialize the remaining quota to be equal to sum of average resource share of all RGs for slot _t, [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: R = Σ_{each RG} rrmRG AvgShare_j(t) The equation starts with the variable R, followed by an equals sign. Then, there is a summation symbol (Σ) with a subscript "each RG". The summation is over the term "rrmRG AvgShare_j(t)". The term "AvgShare_j(t)" is in parentheses, with 'j' as a subscript and 't' as a superscript.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16389.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]_.**If (R <= 0) R = 1.0 End If rrmExistLowBufferRGs = False For each RG [image-1][image_desc]The image contains the text "f ∈ S". rrmRGExpLowTargetShare_j(t): the expected low priority traffic target share for RG _j, computed at slot _t_ __ S: the set of RGs in the cell, whose low priority traffic target share hasn’t been computed.__ R: remaining quota, 0 <= _R <= 1.0_ _rrmRGTempTargetShare_j: temporary target share (including both high priority and low priority traffic) for RG_ j _rrmRGTargetShare_j: target share of RG_ j, see 5G_UP_ALG_23119_replaced([11206799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwr-2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))_hasLowBuffer(j): whether RG_ j is in the low buffer traffic state, see 5G_UP_ALG_23173_replaced([11205541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))_rrmRGAvgShare_j(t): average resource share of RG_ j at slot _t, see 5G_UP_ALG_23262_replaced([11207757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))__rrmRGMaxQuota_j: maximum quota of RG_ j _rrmRGAvgHighShare_j(t): average resource share for high priority traffic of RG_ j at slot _t, see 5G_UP_ALG_23262_replaced([11207757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))__rdRGMinLowShareUL: R &D parameter, minimum resource share given to an RG for its low priority traffic, see 5G_UP_ALG_23170_replaced([11209263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwyshfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) rrmRGDynamicTargetShare_j: _The dynamic target share computed for an RG _j in_ S according to 5G_UP_ALG_28239_replaced([11206837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))._** computeFullBufferTempTargetShares(): compute the temporary target shares of all RGs, assuming that each RG has full buffer low priority traffic and the actual high priority traffic, see 5G_UP_ALG_26700_replaced([11207200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). rrmRGFullBufferTempTargetShare_j: temporary target share (including both high priority and low priority traffic) for RG _j, assuming each RG has full buffer low priority traffic and the actual high priority traffic, see 5G_UP_ALG_26700_replaced([11207200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))._ computeLowBufferStateForRGs(): compute the low buffer traffic state for all RGs, see 5G_UP_ALG_23173_replaced([11205541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). rrmExistLowBufferRGs: if at least one RG is in low-buffer state and at least one RG is in non-low-buffer state, the value is True. If [CB012238-B-UPD002] (QoS fairness slice aware scheduler is deactivated on parent gNB, NRBTS.actSliceAwareSchQosFairness = False) and [End CB012238-B-UPD002] (dynamic target shares need to be obtained for the same set of RGs S as in the last execution of “Computation of low priority traffic target share”) and (rrmRGMinQuota_j and rrmRGMaxQuota_j of every RG j in S stay the same as in the last execution), then the computation of dynamic target shares can be skipped, for better runtime performance.

---

**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-7**  (ID: `11207200`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Compute temporary target share of RGs assuming each RG has full buffer low priority traffic

---

**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `12484556`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 12484556: Slice Aware Scheduler This procedure computes the temporary target shares of all RGs, assuming that each RG has full buffer low priority traffic and actual (the current) high priority traffic. These temporary target shares are stores in variables rrmRGFullBufferTempTargetShare_j for every RG _j, which will be used later. The procedure is listed as follows. The current slot is denoted_ t.**Function computeFullBufferTempTargetShares() Make a list _S that contains all configured RGs in the cell._Initialize the remaining quota to be equal to sum of average resource share of all RGs for slot_ t, [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: R = Σ_{each RG} rrmRG AvgShare_j(t) The equation starts with the variable R, followed by an equals sign. Then, there is a summation symbol (Σ) with a subscript "each RG". The summation is over the term "rrmRG AvgShare_j(t)". The term "AvgShare_j(t)" is in parentheses, with 'j' as a subscript and 't' as a superscript.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16391.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]_.__If (R <= 0) R = 1.0 End If For each RG [image-1][image_desc]The image contains the text "f ∈ S". The text is stylized with a gradient effect, giving it a metallic or iridescent appearance. The formula is: `rrnRGBullBuf ferTempTargetSharej = min ( ( rrnRGTargetSharej / R ) / ( Σk∈S rrnRGTargetSharek ) , rrnRGMaxQuotaj )`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16391.005.png[/image_path][/image-2] End For While (True) For each RG [image-3][image_desc]The image contains the text "f ∈ S". **3.30.18.3.0-4.0-8**  (ID: `11207350`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Feedback control based on low priority target share

---

**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-8.0-4**  (ID: `11207710`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 11207710: Slice Aware Scheduler After the low priority traffic target share is computed for each RG (see 5G_UP_ALG_23157_replaced([11206914](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU applies proportional or proportional-integral feedback control to compute the RG weight for each RG. The procedure is listed as follows. The current slot is denoted t. Output: the RG weight rrmRGWeight_j(t) for each RG j Initialization at the start of the system If rdSvcControlTypeUL = 1 For each configured RG j in the cell [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, stylized font. The equation reads "rrmRgltermj = 0". The characters are white with a slight yellow and blue tint, set against a white background. The overall image is slightly out of focus.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] End For End If The following shall be executed for every run of enhanced SVC algorithm. If rdSvcControlTypeUL = 0 For each configured RG j in the cell [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a stylized font. The formula is: rrmRGExpTargetShare,(t) = min{rrmRGExpLowTargetShare, (t)+ rrmRGAvgHighShare; (t), rrmRGMaxQuota;}[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] If [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical inequality. The text in the image is: `rrmRGAvgShare_j(t) <= rrmRGExpTargetShare_j(t)`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] [image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in an italicized font. The formula is: $rrmRGWeight_j(t) = rrmRGExpLowTargetShare_j(t) \cdot [rdEnhSvcPGainUL - (rdEnhSvcPGainUL - 1) \cdot \frac{rrmRGAvgShare_j(t)}{rrmRGExpTargetShare_j(t)}]$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] Else If [image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical inequality. The text reads: rrrnRGAvgShare,(t) ≤ rrrnRGMaxQuota,[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.005.png[/image_path][/image-4] [image-5][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a sans-serif font. The formula is: $rrmRGWeight_j(t) = rrmRGExpLowTargetShare_j(t) \cdot \frac{rrmRGMaxQuotaj - rrmRGAvgShare_j(t)}{rrmRGMaxQuotaj - rrmRGExpTargetShare_j(t)}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.006.png[/image_path][/image-5] Else [image-6][image_desc]The image contains the following text: rrr m RGW eightj(t) = 0[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.007.png[/image_path][/image-6] End If End For Else For each configured RG j in the cell If hasLowBuffer(j) [image-7][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: `rrrmRGWeight,(t) = (2 * rdEnhSvcPtP GainUL + 1) *`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.008.png[/image_path][/image-7]rdLowBufRgWeightAmplifierUL Else [image-8][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a stylized font. The formula is: rrmRGExpTargetShareⱼ(t) = min{rrmRGExpLowTargetShareⱼ(t) + rrmRGAvgHighShareⱼ(t), rrmRGMaxQuotaⱼ}[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.009.png[/image_path][/image-8] If [image-9][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical inequality. The text in the image is: $rrmRGAvgShare_j(t) \leq rrmRGMaxQuota_j$.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.010.png[/image_path][/image-9] [Before CB008250-AA] [image-10][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is: rrmRGDeltaj (t) = rrmRGExpTargetShare; (t) - rrmRGAvgShare」(t) The equation appears to be a formula defining a variable `rrmRGDeltaj` at time `t` as the difference between two other variables: `rrmRGExpTargetShare` at time `t` and `rrmRGAvgShare` at time `t`. The subscript 'j' is present on `rrmRGDeltaj` and `rrmRGExpTargetShare`, and the subscript 'j' is also present on `rrmRGAvgShare` with a closing parenthesis. The characters are slightly blurred, but the overall structure of the equation is clear.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.011.png[/image_path][/image-10] [image-11][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is: `rrmxRgjtermj = rrmxRgjtermj + rdEnhSvePtlGainUL · rrmxRGDeitaj(t)`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.012.png[/image_path][/image-11] If [image-12][image_desc]The image contains blurry text. The text appears to be "rrrmR glterm, > rdEnhSvePtPGain UI".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.013.png[/image_path][/image-12] [image-13][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, stylized font. The equation reads: "rrrmRgltermj = rdEnhSvcPtPGainUL". The text is white with a slight blue and yellow tint, set against a white background.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.014.png[/image_path][/image-13] End If If [image-14][image_desc]The image contains the following text: rrrnRg lterm, < -rdEnhSvcPVPGainUL[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.015.png[/image_path][/image-14] [image-15][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, stylized font. The equation appears to be: `rrrrRgltermj = -râEnhSvcPvPGainUL`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.016.png[/image_path][/image-15] End If [image-16][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a serif font. The formula is: rrmRGWeight(t) = max {rrmRGExpLowTargetShare_j(t) + rdEnhSvcPiPGainUL, rrmRGDelta_j(t) + rrmRgIterm_j, rrmRGExpLowTargetShare_j(t)/rdRGTinyWeightUL}[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.017.png[/image_path][/image-16] [CB008250-AA] // RG weight is updated with PI control. Execute rgWeightUpdateWithPiControl(j) [End CB008250-AA] Else [image-17][image_desc]The image contains the text "rrrrRGWeightj(t) = 0". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16396.018.png[/image_path][/image-17] End If End If End For End If If RG j has RG weight equal to 0, then no resource will be allocated to initial transmissions of nonGBR and deprioritized GBR/DC-GBR DRBs of RG j in the following UL grants until the RG weight becomes positive. However, resources can be allocated to initial transmissions and retransmissions of GBR/DC-GBR DRBs, or to retransmissions of nonGBR DRBs of RG j even if the RG weight is equal to 0. Notations: rrmRGWeight_j(t): RG weight computed for RG j at slot t rrmRGExpTargetShare_j(t): the expected target share (including both high and low priority traffic) for RG j, computed at slot t rdEnhSvcPGainUL: R&D parameter, the proportional gain of P control used in the enhanced SVC algorithm, see 5G_UP_ALG_23183_replaced([11209317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwysjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) rrmRGExpLowTargetShare_j(t): the expected low priority traffic target share for RG j, computed at slot t, see 5G_UP_ALG_23157_replaced([11206914](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) rrmRGAvgHighShare_j(t): average resource share for high priority traffic of RG j at slot t, see 5G_UP_ALG_23262_replaced([11207757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) rrmRGMaxQuota_j: maximum quota of RG j, see 5G_UP_ALG_23115_replaced([11206740](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwr-0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) rrmRGAvgShare_j(t): average resource share of RG j at slot t, see 5G_UP_ALG_23262_replaced([11207757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [Before CB008250-AA] rrmRGDelta_j(t): the difference between rrmRGExpTargetShare_j(t) and rrmRGAvgShare_j(t). [End CB008250-AA] rrmRgIterm_j: the integral term of PI control for RG j. rdEnhSvcPiPGainUL: R&D parameter, the integral gain of PI control used in the enhanced SVC algorithm, see 5G_UP_ALG_29761_replaced([11209329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwzTkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [Before CB008250-AA] rdEnhSvcPiIGainUL: R&D parameter, the integral gain of PI control used in the enhanced SVC algorithm, see 5G_UP_ALG_29656_replaced([11209341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwzTkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). rdRGTinyWeightReciUL: R&D parameter, the reciprocal of tiny RG weight for UL, to avoid cell resource waste, see 5G_UP_ALG_29661_replaced([11209353](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwzTlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008250-AA] rdSvcControlTypeUL: R&D parameter, it is used to select the type of control algorithm of enhanced SVC, see 5G_UP_ALG_29763_replaced([11209368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwzTl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). hasLowBuffer(j): returns the current low buffer traffic state of RG j, see 5G_UP_ALG_23173_replaced([11205541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).. The rationale behind setting RG weight of a low buffer RG to a static high value is to avoid PI control for the RG, because it may be impossible to attain the target share due to the low buffer traffic demand. Setting a high RG weight has the advantage to let the RG quickly attain the target share when high traffic demand comes. rdLowBufferRgWeightAmplifierUL: this R&D parameters is used in enhanced SVC algorithm when PI control is enabled to increase the chance that DRBs of a low buffer RG get scheduled, so that the RG can get its target share when enough traffic will be available, see 5G_UP_ALG_31193_replaced([11209594](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw0hvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB008250-AA] rgWeightUpdateWithPiControl(j): the function that updates RG Weight with PI Control for RG j, see 5G_UP_ALG_32119_replaced([11205864](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqws_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008250-AA]

---

**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-9**  (ID: `11207717`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Compute Dynamic Target Shares for Non-empty Buffer RGs

---

**3.30.18.3.0-4.0-9.0-3**  (ID: `12638900`)

Slice Aware Scheduler gNB-DU calls computeNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShares() to compute dynamic target shares for non-empty buffer RGs in a slot.

Initialize rrmNonempBufRGs to be an empty set at the start of the system.

Function computeNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShares()

Q = computeNonempBufRGs()

If [CB012238-B-UPD002] (QoS fairness slice aware scheduler is activated on parent gNB, NRBTS.actSliceAwareSchQosFairness = True) or [End CB012238-B-UPD002] (rrmNonempBufRGs is not equal to Q) or (rrmRGMinQuota_j or rrmRGMaxQuota_j of any RG j in Q has been changed since last slot)

rrmNonempBufRGs = Q

[CB012238-B-UPD002]

If (rrmExistEmptyBufferRGs = False)

rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShare_j = rrmRGTargetShare_j, for every RG j in Q. 

Else if (rrmEmptyBufferRGsLowBufferRGsMatch = True)

rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShare_j = rrmRGDynamicTargetShare_j, for every RG j in Q.

Set rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShare_k = 0 for every RG k not in Q. 

Else

[End CB012238-B-UPD002]

Compute dynamic target shares for the set of RGs Q according to 5G_UP_ALG_28239_replaced([11206837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and store the results in variables rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShare_k for every RG k in Q.

Set rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShare_k = 0 for every RG k not in Q. 

[CB012238-B-UPD002]

End If

[End CB012238-B-UPD002]

End If

End

Notations:

rrmNonempBufRGs: the set of non-empty buffer RGs.

rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShare_k: dynamic target share for non-empty buffer RG k.

computeNonempBufRGs: compute the set of non-empty buffer RGs, see 5G_UP_ALG_28674_replaced([11205741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqJq_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB012238-B-UPD002]

rrmRGTargetShare_j: target share of RG j, see [11206799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_3HNVEPvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

rrmExistEmptyBufferRGs: whether any of the RGs in the cell is in empty buffer state, see [11205741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_ytwokfvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

rrmRGDynamicTargetShare_j: dynamic target share of RG j, see [11206914](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

rrmEmptyBufferRGsLowBufferRGsMatch: whether the set of empty buffer RGs match exactly the set of low buffer RGs in the cell, see [11205741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_ytwokfvQEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

[End CB012238-B-UPD002]

---

#### 3.30.18.3.0-5 Slice Aware Scheduler PRB Bookkeeping (ID: 11207728)

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11207736`)

Slice Aware Scheduler High and Low Priority Traffic for Slice Aware Scheduler

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13396727`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Slice aware scheduler measures resource usage of each RG for high priority and low priority traffic, respectively.

The high priority traffic includes traffic from the set of high priority request classes, denoted by C_H = {[CB014699-D] criticalHarqRetransVoip, criticalHarqRetransGbr, criticalHarqRetransDrb [End CB014699-D], harqReTransDcGbr,**harqRetransVoip, harqRetransGbr, harqRetransDrb, [CB014699-D], criticalVoipTraffic, criticalGbrTraffic, criticalDrbTraffic, [End CB014699-D]voipTraffic, deprioritizedVoip, gbrTraffic}.**

If UE is scheduled an initial PUSCH grant with a configured DC-GBR bearer in prioritized state (see [11196041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwACUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), the DC-GBR bearer data, if any, received in the corresponding PUSCH transmission, shall be considered in high priority traffic class, namely C_H, and included in high priority traffic resource usage of the corresponding RG. 

The low priority traffic includes traffic from the set of low priority request classes, denoted by C_L = {drbTraffic,deprioritizedGbr}.

Resources used by other request classes (other than those in C_H and C_L) are not considered by slice aware scheduler. For each RG, slice aware scheduler guarantees the resources used by high priority traffic and controls the resources used by low priority traffic.

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11207757`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Resource Share Bookkeeping for a RG

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `13399176`)

Slice Aware Scheduler After the data reception from all UEs is complete in each UL slot _t, if enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and available PRBs for SAS in slot _t is larger than 0 (_rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) > 0, for the computation, see 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the average resource share shall be updated for each RG. [CB014699-D] Note: The scheduler shall allocate whatever resources are necessary to meet the demands of high priority traffic first and is not constrained by the minQuota or maxQuota configured for its RG [End CB014699-D]._**

For slot _t, the instantaneous resource share_ rrmRGInstShare_i of RG _i is the sum of the instantaneous resource share of high priority traffic and of low priority traffic:_ **

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, sans-serif font. The equation is: `rrm.RGInstShare,(t) = rrm.RGHInstShare,(t) + rrm.RGlowInstShare,(t)`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16405.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

where

 rrmRGHighInstShare_i(t): the instantaneous resource share of high priority traffic of RG _i_

 rrmRGLowInstShare_i(t): the instantaneous resource share of low priority traffic of RG

Note, if _N PRBs are allocated for CG, but_ M PRBs (_M <= _N) are used by UEs, then _M PRBs are counted in rrmRGInstShare(t) of related RGs._ ****

[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmRGHighInstShare_i(t) = \sum_{c \in C_H} \sum_{j \in J_i} rrmDrbInstShare_{j,c}(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16405.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmRGlowInstShare_i(t) = \sum_{c \in C_i} \sum_{j \in J_i} rrmDrbInstShare_{j,c}(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16405.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]

[CB016273-A]

If intent-throughput slice aware scheduler is activated on parent gNB (NRBTS.actSliceAwareSchIntentTput = True)

gNB shall compute the instantaneous throughput (transmitted bytes) of low priority traffic classes of RG i, rrmRGLowInstTput_i(t) = sum_{c in C_L} sum_{j in J_i} amountBytes_{j,c}(t).

gNB shall compute the instantaneous throughput (transmitted bytes) of traffic class drbTraffic (nonGBR iniTx) of RG i, rrmRGNgbrInstTput_i(t) = sum_{j in K_i} amountBytes_{j, drbTraffic}(t).

End If

[End CB016273-A]

where

  * J_i: the set of non-GBR (excluding 5QI-5) and GBR/DC-GBR DRBs of RG _i_
  * C_H: the set of high priority request classes, for the definition, see 5G_UP_ALG_24030_replaced([11207736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * C_L: the set of low priority request classes, for the definition, see 5G_UP_ALG_24030_replaced([11207736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
  * rrmDrbInstShare_{j,c}(t): the instantaneous resource share of DRB _j for request class_ c in slot _t (see 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))_**



[CB016273-A]

  * amountBytes_{j,c}(t): the amount of bytes allocated to the transmissions of a DRB j for traffic class c in slot t.
  * K_i: the set of non-GBR (excluding 5QI-5) DRBs of RG i.



[End CB016273-A]

The average resource share for an RG is obtained by applying an exponentially moving average over time window _τ:_

[image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation defines a variable `rrmRGAvgShare_i(t)` based on two cases: Case 1: For `t = 1` `rrmRGAvgShare_i(t) = rrmRGInstShare_i(t)` Case 2: For `t > 1` `rrmRGAvgShare_i(t) = (ε / (1 + τ)) * rrmRGInstShare_i(t) + (1 - (ε / (1 + τ))) * rrmRGAvgShare_i(t-1)` The equation uses the following symbols: \- `rrmRGAvgShare_i(t)`: Represents the average share for entity `i` at time `t`. \- `rrmRGInstShare_i(t)`: Represents the instantaneous share for entity `i` at time `t`. \- `ε`: A parameter, likely representing a discount factor or a weighting coefficient. \- `τ`: Another parameter, likely representing a discount rate or a time constant. \- `t`: Represents time. \- `i`: Represents an entity or index.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16405.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]

where,

  _τ =_ rdRGAvgShareFilterWindowUL: R&D parameter for exponential filter time window size (see 5G_UP_ALG_23264_replaced([11209379](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwzTmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

  * ε = _rdRGAvgShareFilterFactorUL: R &D parameter for exponential filter smoothing factor (see 5G_UP_ALG_23266_replaced([11209393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwzTm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))_



gNB-DU shall be able to separate the average shares of high priority and low priority traffic such that:

[image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a light gray font against a black background. The equation is: rrmRGAvgShare(t) = rrmRGAvgHighShare(t) + rrmRGAvgLowShare(t) The text is slightly blurred, making it difficult to read with absolute certainty, but the characters are discernible.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16405.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]

All variables for resource share are between 0 and 1.0, where 0 means 0% and 1.0 means 100%.

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `11207823`)

Slice Aware Scheduler NBR Resource Share Bookkeeping for a RG

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11207858`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 11207858: Slice Aware Scheduler After the data reception from all UEs is complete in each UL slot t, if enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and NRCELL.actNbrForNonGbrBearers = TRUE and the resource share bookkeeping for all RGs (5G_UP_ALG_23262_replaced([11207757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) has been done and the available PRBs for SAS in slot t is larger than 0 (rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) > 0, for the computation, see 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall compute the average NBR resource share for each RG.

\- gNB-DU shall compute the equivalent of the allocation that a NBR DRB j of UE u has obtained for initial transmission in a slot t, in terms of PRBs (converted from bytes). An NBR DRB is a DRB of NBR category, see definition at 5G_UP_ALG_4794_replaced([11194709](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv24YfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If [gNB has not received any init-tx data from NBR DRB j in slot t]

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, stylized font. The equation reads: `rrrm InstNbrPrbAllocULj(t) = 0`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16407.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] 

Else

[Before PR780629]

[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, italicized font. The equation is: $rrmlnstNbrPrbAllocUL_j(t) = rrmlnstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,drbTraf_{ic}}(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16407.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

[PR780629]

[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is: rrmInstNbrPrbAllocUL,(t) = min {rrmInstDrbPrbAllocj, drbTraffic(t), rrmNbrDesiredPrbs(LCG(j)), The equation appears to be related to resource allocation or probability calculations in a system, possibly a telecommunications or network context, given the variable names like "rrmInst", "NbrPrbAlloc", "drbTraffic", and "LCG". The subscript 'j' and the time variable 't' indicate that these values can change over time and for different entities. The 'min' function suggests that the allocation is limited by the smallest of the three specified values.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16407.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]

[image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The text in the image is: NRBTS/NRCELL/maxPrbsPerNbrUe + activeBWP(i)}[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16407.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]

[End PR780629]

where: 

  * rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,drbTraffic}(t): the equivalent number of PRBs allocated to NBR DRB j for initial transmission in a slot t. For the computation, see 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).



[PR780629]

 rrmNbrDesiredPrbs(LCG(j)): the number of PRBs allocated to the NBR LCG that contains DRB j, see 5G_UP_ALG_22159_replaced([11199843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 activeBWP(i): the number of PRBs usable for PUSCH for any DRB within UE i’s active dedicated BWP, where DRB j belongs to UE i.

[End PR780629]

  * The instantaneous share of a NBR DRB j is calculated as follow:



[image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmNbrInstShareUL_j(t) = \frac{rrmInstNbrPrbAllocUL_j(t)}{rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t)}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16407.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]

where

  * rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t): the number of PRBs available for the slice aware scheduler in slot t, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note, the total number of PRBs available for the slice aware scheduler in a cell per UL slot rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) is considered as the 100% of the resource by the slice aware scheduler. PRBs allocated for a NBR DRB shall be accounted from the 100% of the resource.
  * rrmInstNbrPrbAllocUL_j(t): the number of PRBs that NBR DRB j has obtained for initial transmission in a slot t in the above step.

  * For a slot t, the instantaneous resource share rrmRGNbrInstShareUL_i of RG i is the sum of the instantaneous resource share of each NBR DRB of RG i:




[image-5][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmNbrInstShareeUL_i(t) = \sum_{j \in \mathcal{N}_i} rrmNbrInstShareeUL_j(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16407.006.png[/image_path][/image-5]

where

  * J_i: the set of NBR DRBs of RG i.

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-4**  (ID: `11207865`)

Slice Aware Scheduler RG Load

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11207906`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 11207906: Slice Aware Scheduler [CB008250-C] If PLMN aware scheduler is activated (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16121_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or [End CB008250-C] enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and NRCELL.actNbrForNonGbrBearers = TRUE and NRCELL.nbrPuschCongHandling = TRUE, gNB-DU shall calculate resource group load as follows:

  * The instantaneous resource group load is calculated at every UL slot t:



[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is presented horizontally. The equation is: rrmRGInstLoadUL₁(t) = (rrmRGHighInstShare (t) + rrmRGNbrInstShareUL(t)) / rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShareᵢ The equation defines a variable `rrmRGInstLoadUL` at time `t` as a fraction. The numerator of the fraction is the sum of `rrmRGHighInstShare` at time `t` and `rrmRGNbrInstShareUL` at time `t`. The denominator is `rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShare` with a subscript `i`.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16410.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

where

  * i: each RG of the cell
  * rrmRGHighInstShare_i(t): the instantaneous resource share of high priority traffic of RG i, defined in 5G_UP_ALG_23262_replaced([11207757](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * rrmRGNbrInstShareUL_i(t): the instantaneous NBR resource share of RG i, defined in 5G_UP_ALG_24887_replaced([11207823](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

  * rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShare_i: the dynamic target share for non-empty buffer RG_i, [CB008250-C] if PLMN aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16121_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), defined in 5G_UP_ALG_31505_replaced([11206598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwrXyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), else, as [End CB008250-C] defined in 5G_UP_ALG_28672_replaced([11207717](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM8_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).




Exception:

If rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShare_i = 0, then rrmNonempBufRGDynamicTargetShare_i shall be set to 0.01.

  * The average resource group load can be obtained by applying an exponentially moving average over time window τ:



[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula presented in a piecewise format. The formula defines `rrmRGAvgLoadUL;(t)` based on the value of `t`. For `t = 1`, `rrmRGAvgLoadUL;(t)` is equal to `rrmRGInstLoadUL(t)`. For `t > 1`, `rrmRGAvgLoadUL;(t)` is calculated as: `ε / (1 + τ) * rrmRGInstLoadUL(t) + (1 - ε / (1 + τ)) * rrmRGAvgLoadUL;(t - 1)`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16410.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

where,

 i: each RG.

 τ = rdRGAvgShareFilterWindowUL: R&D parameter for exponential filter time window size (see 5G_UP_ALG_23264_replaced([11209379](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwzTmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 ε = rdRGAvgShareFilterFactorUL: R&D parameter for exponential filter smoothing factor (see 5G_UP_ALG_23266_replaced([11209393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwzTm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note that when a RG is newly created, t is reset to 1.

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-5**  (ID: `11207913`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Mapping DRB allocations from bytes to PRBs & calculating the instantaneous resource share of the DRBs

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `13412262`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 13412262: Slice Aware Scheduler If enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB-DU shall compute the equivalent number of PRBs allocated to DRB _j_ _for request class_ c in a slot _t rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t), which is converted to number of PRBs from bytes:_**_* If [the combination of DRB_ j and request class _c does not exist (e.g., a non-GBR DRB and the request class harqRetransGbr)] or [gNB has not received any data from DRB_ j for request class _c in slot_ t]****_[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a blurry, italicized font. The equation reads: $rrrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{jc}(t) = 0$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] Else [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t) = \frac{amountBytes_{j,c}(t)}{payloadBytes_u(t)} \cdot rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] where: * amountBytes_{j,c}(t): the amount of bytes received by gNB for the transmissions of DRB_ j for request class _c in slot_ t** _* payloadBytes_u(t): the amount of bytes of UE_ u in its transport block received by gNB in slot _t_ _* rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t): number of PRBs used by UE_ u for UL transmission in slot _t.__If UL MU-MIMO is activated (NRCELL.actUlMuMimo = True), rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t) shall be computed according to 5G_UP_ALG_26375_replaced([11208360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwubG_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The instantaneous PRBs allocated to UE _u in slot _t for high priority request classes and low priority request classes,_ rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) and _rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc_u(t) can be computed as:_**_[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) = \sum_{c \in C_H} \sum_{j \in J_u} rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] [image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc_u(t) = \sum_{c \in C_L} \sum_{j \in J_u} rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] where · · J_u: the set of non-GBR (excluding 5QI-5) and GBR DRBs of UE_ u._· C_H: the set of high priority request classes, for the definition, see 5G_UP_ALG_24030_replaced([11207736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). · C_L: the set of low priority request classes, for the definition, see 5G_UP_ALG_24030_replaced([11207736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The instantaneous PRBs allocated to UE _u that are of interest to slice aware scheduler is:_[image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in italics. The equation is: $rrmlnstUeSasPrbAlloc_u(t) = rrmlnstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) + rrmlnstUeLowPrbAlloc_u(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]. The number of PRBs available for the slice aware scheduler in slot_ t is:_[image-5][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is:`rrmAvailable PrbsForSas(t) = rrmInstSas PrbAlloc(t) + rrmInstPrbFree(t)`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.006.png[/image_path][/image-5] and [image-6][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmInstSasPrbAlloc(t) = \sum_{\text{each UE } u} rrmInstUeSasPrbAlloc_u(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.007.png[/image_path][/image-6] where * rrmInstSasPrbAlloc(t): the number of PRBs allocated in slot _t that are of interest to the slice aware scheduler. It includes the PRBs used in PUSCH transmissions for the following request classes: [CB014699-D] criticalHarqRetransVoip, criticalHarqRetransGbr, criticalHarqRetransDrb,[End CB014699-D] harqReTransDcGbr, harqRetransVoip, harqRetransGbr, harqRetransDrb, [CB014699-D] criticalVoipTraffic, criticalGbrTraffic, criticalDrbTraffic ,[End CB014699-D] dcGbrTraffic, voipTraffic, deprioritizedVoip, gbrTraffic, drbTraffic, and deprioritizedGbr._* rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t): the number of PRBs available for the slice aware scheduler in slot_ t. It shall take into account interworking with PRB pooling (5G_UP_ALG_24090_replaced([11208276](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwubEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and interworking with DSS (Dynamic Spectrum Sharing) (5G_UP_ALG_24092_replaced([11208290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwubE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))* * **Note, If _N PRBs are allocated for CG, but_ M PRBs (_M <= _N) are used by UEs, then _M PRBs are counted in rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t)._**_*__* rrmInstPrbFree(t): the number PRBs that are unoccupied in PUSCH transmission in slot_ t.* * If NRCELL.actUlCgType2 = True, for **each UE _u with CG occasion in the current slot_ t that was sent to FD scheduler and it was decided that the UE shall keep the CG unchanged (5G_UP_ALG_15617_replaced([11198720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the number of PRBs allocated for CG to the UE, rrmInstCGPrb_u(t) _(see 5G_UP_ALG_14664_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be deducted from rrmInstPrbFree(t)._**_* Note, If CG overwriting happened, then mmInstPrbFree(t) did not contain rrmInstCGPrb_u(t). So, we don’t need to do the deduction explicitly. * if gNB-DU distinguishes multiple UL BWPs in the cell (5G_UP_ALG_1811_replaced([11110797](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpHXP_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the number of PRBs that are unoccupied in PUSCH in the largest dedicated BWP is used (5G_UP_ALG_4974_replaced([11199486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwOEw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) * If UL MU-MIMO is activate (NRCELL.actUlMuMimo = True), rrmInstPrbFree(t) shall be computed according to 5G_UP_ALG_24609_replaced([11208303](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwubFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If [image-7][image_desc]The image contains the text "rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) ≤ 0". The text is slightly blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The characters are white with a subtle gradient and a faint shadow. The background is a light, textured color, possibly paper or a digital canvas.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.008.png[/image_path][/image-7], then quit the SVC algorithm execution for slot _t. **By quitting the enhanced SVC algorithm, the step 5G_UP_ALG_24437_replaced([11205843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwqwsPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be executed.**_The instantaneous share of a DRB for a request class in slot_ t is:_If [the combination of DRB_ j and request class _c does not exist (e.g., a non-GBR DRB and the request class harqRetransGbr)] or [gNB has not received any data from DRB_ j for request class _c in slot_ t]***** [image-8][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a sans-serif font and appears to be in a dark color on a white background. The equation reads: `rrmDrbInstShare_{j,c}(t) = 0`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.009.png[/image_path][/image-8] Else [image-9][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmDrbInstShare_{j,c}(t) = \frac{rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t)}{rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t)}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.010.png[/image_path][/image-9] Moreover, rrmInstUeSasPrbAlloc_u(t) can be related to rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t) with a parameter rrmInstUeSasPrbRatio_u(t). It denotes the percentage of PRBs allocated to a UE that is relevant to the slice aware scheduler. [image-10][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation presented in a series of lines. The equation defines `rrmInstUeSasPrbAlloc(t)` in terms of other variables. Here is the extracted text: rrmInstUeSasPrbAlloc(t) = rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc(t) + rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc(t) = ∑ ∑ rrmInstDrbPrbAllocjc (t) c∈CH∪CL j∈Ju ∑ ∑ amountBytesj,c(t) c∈CH∪CL j∈Ju payloadBytesu(t) ⋅ rrmInstUePrbAlloc(t) = rrmInstUeSasPrbRatio (t) ⋅ rrmInstUePrbAlloc(t)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16413.011.png[/image_path][/image-10] The above computation shall be done in each slot after the data reception from all UEs is complete. Note, the total number of PRBs available for the slice aware scheduler in a cell per UL slot rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) is considered as the 100% of the resource by the slice aware scheduler. PRBs allocated for each request class of interest to slice aware scheduler should be accounted from the 100% of the resource.

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-6**  (ID: `11208033`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Implementation Details of PRB Bookkeeping

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11208066`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 11208066: Slice Aware Scheduler For DL traffic, gNB has the full knowledge of the resources assigned to each DRB at the time of scheduling. However, this is not the case for UL traffic because a UE distributes the assigned resources to its DRBs according to the LCP procedure which is not controlled by gNB. Therefore, for UL traffic, gNB can only do PRB bookkeeping at the time of successful reception. Suppose in a UL slot t, successful reception for a DRB j and request class c happens at the gNB. If the successful reception corresponds to an initial transmission, this means that the initial transmission is successful. In this case, gNB-DU shall compute rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t) according to 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [Before CB008709-E-UPD004] [CB008709-E] if UL MU-MIMO is deactivated, and according to 5G_UP_ALG_24609_replaced([11208303](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwubFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) otherwise [End CB008709-E] [End CB008709-E-UPD004] If the successful reception corresponds to a retransmission of a UE u at the k-th try (k >= 1), this means that the initial transmission failed but last retransmission has succeeded. For simplicity in notation, we denote tk = t. In addition, the initial transmission happened at t0, and the i-th (1<=i<=k) retransmission happened at time ti. [Before CB008709-E-UPD004] [CB008709-E] If UL MU-MIMO is deactivated, [End CB008709-E] [End CB008709-E-UPD004] the formulas for computing rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc in 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be adapted as follows. The retransmission request class c has a corresponding initial transmission request class c’ (e.g. c = harqRetransDrb and c’ = drbTraffic). [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $rrmInstDrbAllocRatio_{j,c}(tk) = \frac{amountBytes_{j,c}(tk)}{payloadBytes_{u}(tk)}$ This formula defines the `rrmInstDrbAllocRatio` for parameters `j`, `c`, and `tk` as the ratio of `amountBytes` for parameters `j`, `c`, and `tk` to `payloadBytes` for parameter `u` and `tk`.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16415.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(tk) = rrmInstDrbAllocRatio_{j,c}(tk) \cdot \sum_{i=1}^{k} rrmInstUePrbAlloc_{u}(ti)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16415.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, sans-serif font. The equation is: rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc$_{j,c}$($t_k$) = rrmInstDrbAllocRatio$_{j,c}$($t_k$) $\cdot$ rrmInstUePrbAlloc$_{u}$($t_0$)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16415.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] To compute the above values, gNB-DU shall compute rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u and store the values at the scheduling time for UL slots t0, t1, … and tk. For the case that retransmission finally fails after many retransmissions, some resource usage will not be counted. This is inevitable as we do not know how many resources are used by these transmissions. [Before CB008709-E-UPD004] [CB008709-E] If UL MU-MIMO is activated, the PRB bookkeeping uses the same logic as above. Suppose that the retransmission of a UE u succeeded in slot tk at the k-th try (k >= 1). The initial transmission happened at t0, and the i-th retransmission happened at time ti. The initial transmission and retransmission of a DRB j are of request class c’ and c, respectively. The formulas for computing rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc in 5G_UP_ALG_24609_replaced([11208303](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwubFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be adapted as follows. [image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $rrmInstDrbAllocRatio_{j,c}(tk) = \frac{amountBytes_{j,c}(tk)}{\sum_{k \in J_u} \sum_{d \in C_H \cup C_L} amountBytes_{k,d}(t)}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16415.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] [image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is presented in two lines. The first line defines a term: `rrmInstDrbPrb Allocj.c (tk)` The second line provides the definition of this term: `= rrmInstDrbAllocRatioj (tk) * ∑[rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc (ti) + rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc(ti)]` where the summation is from `i=1` to `k`.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16415.005.png[/image_path][/image-4] [image-5][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is presented on a white background. The equation is: rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc$_{j,c}$($t_k$) = rrmInstDrbAllocRatio$_{j,c}$($t_k$) $\cdot$ [rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc$_{u}$($t_0$) + rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc$_{u}$($t_0$)][/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16415.006.png[/image_path][/image-5] To compute these values, gNB-DU shall compute rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u + rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc_u and store the values at the scheduling time for UL slots t0, t1, … and tk. [End CB008709-E] [End CB008709-E-UPD004] [CB008709-C-UPD003] When gNB knows that the initial transmission or retransmission of a UE is cancelled due to very bad channel condition or other reasons, it shall do PRB bookkeeping according to 5G_UP_ALG_25607_replaced([11208073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008709-C-UPD003]

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-7**  (ID: `11208073`)

Slice Aware Scheduler PRB Bookkeeping When Initial Transmission or Retransmission of UE Is Cancelled ==============================================================================

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11208080`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Introduction

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11208094`)

Slice Aware Scheduler gNB needs to do PRB bookkeeping properly at the time when initial transmission or retransmission of a UE is cancelled. Otherwise, the cell resources used by the UE during the initial transmission and retransmissions will not be counted by slice aware scheduler, which leads to smaller PM counter values and incorrect RG weight computation.

The above situation can happen, due to many reasons. We have divided the reasons into two categories: must-have and nice-to-have. The must-have causes will be addressed in the system under CB008709 while the nice-to-have causes could be addressed in the next releases if needed. 

Must-have

 Because of bad channel quality, gNB fails to decode initial transmission and HARQ retransmissions from a UE until max HARQ retransmissions have occurred. This is known as residual BLER (see 5G_UP_ALG_5074_replaced([11202363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_12741_replaced([11203324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 Flush of pending HARQ processes that may happen when UE exits the transient phase (see 5G_UP_ALG_5074_replaced([11202363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 Flush of pending HARQ processes when the gNB performs a BWP switch to the nominal BWP for a UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_21200_replaced([11111062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJzffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 A UE is in overheating status at current carrier (see 5G_UP_ALG_11438_replaced([11196580](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDsuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_5082_replaced([11203301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 During beam failure recovery when FGCR-3574 is activated (see 5G_UP_ALG_4814_replaced([11194878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4Gg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 When it is found that a UE has low SINR at the reception of HARQ feedback (see 5G_UP_ALG_5082_replaced([11203301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Nice-to-have

 CG deconfiguration cancels any pending retransmission associated with a CG HARQ process (see 5G_UP_ALG_14584_replaced([11172905](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPHxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 UE enters CG WaitForModification state (5G_UP_ALG_15622_replaced([11173051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), CG WaitForActivation state (see 5G_UP_ALG_15123_replaced([11173014](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), CG Error state (see 5G_UP_ALG_15133_replaced([11173106](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQ88fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or CG WaitForDeactivationConfirm state (5G_UP_ALG_15131_replaced([11173098](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 UE deletion during ongoing initial transmissions or retransmissions.

 Ongoing configured grant retransmission is preempted by dynamic grant initial transmission (see 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 The available cell resources do not allow to continue with retransmissions. E.g. if the PRB pooling reduces the number of PRBs after the scheduling of the initial transmission of a UE (see 5G_UP_ALG_12913_replaced([11202887](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwd8aPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_11420_replaced([11196526](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDFrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

At the scheduling time for initial transmission, gNB knows the amount of FD resources to be allocated to a VoIP DRB (deprioritized or not) and to a non-deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRB of a UE. Besides, gNB knows the total amount of FD resources to be allocated to all nonGBR and deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRBs of a UE, yet it does not know the amount to be allocated to each individual nonGBR and deprioritized GBR DRB, because this is determined by the LCP (Logical Channel Prioritization) procedure running at the UE side. Since LCP procedure is agnostic of RG weights, gNB assumes that FD resources are allocated to nonGBR and deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRBs belonging to LCGs with nonempty buffer, in proportion to their scheduling weights.

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11208099`)

Slice Aware Scheduler PRB Bookkeeping Details

* * *

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-7.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11208232`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 11208232: Slice Aware Scheduler Suppose that the initial transmission of a UE u happens at slot t0 and/or the i-th retransmission happens at slot ti (1 <= i <= k). k = 0 corresponds to the situation that the initial transmission is the last transmission of the PUSCH data. Otherwise, k-th (k >= 1) retransmission is the last transmission of the PUSCH data. After tk, gNB is aware that the initial transmission and all possible retransmissions of the UE fail. It shall compute the number of PRBs allocated to a DRB j of UE u for a request class c, rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j, c}(tk) (5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as follows. Before scheduling the initial transmission of UE u (sending the UL grant) at slot t0, gNB shall store the following information about FD resource allocation of the UE, according to 5G_UP_ALG_4988_replaced([11199729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB008600-F] 1\. FD resources allocated to the non-deprioritized DC-GBR DRB (corresponding to dcGbrTraffic request class), if UE u has a DC-GBR DRB. It is denoted by rrmDcGbrGbrPrbAlloc_u(t0). [End CB008600-F] 2\. FD resources allocated to the VoIP DRB (deprioritized or not), if UE u has a VoIP DRB. It is denoted by rrmVoipPrbAlloc_u(t0). 3\. FD resources allocated to the non-deprioritized non-VoIP GBR DRB (corresponding to gbrTraffic request class), if UE u has a non-VoIP GBR DRB. It is denoted by rrmNonVoipGbrPrbAlloc_u(t0). 4\. FD resources allocated to nonGBR (including FD resources allocated to NBR DRBs with positive token bucket) and deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRBs, if UE u has at least one nonGBR or deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRB. It is denoted by rrmNonGbrPrbAlloc_u(t0). 5\. Collect all nonGBR and deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRBs belonging to LCGs of nonempty buffer and forms them into a DRB set D. Besides, the number of FD resources (PRBs) used by UE u for UL transmission in slot ti (0 <= i <= k) are rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(ti) (see 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Note, rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(ti) may have different values for different slots ti due to adaptive retransmission. [CB008600-F] If UE u has a non-deprioritized DC-GBR DRB dc, then [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{dc,dcGbrTraff_{c}}(tk) = rrmDcGbrPrbAlloc_{u}(t0)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is: $rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{dc.harqRetransDcGbr}(tk) = rrmDcGbrPrbAlloc(t0) * \frac{\sum_{i=1}^{k} rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(ti)}{rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t0)}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] [End CB008600-F] If UE u has a non-deprioritized VoIP DRB v, then [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, light-colored font on a dark background. The equation appears to be: `rrninstDri bPbAlloc cv,uoiptra ff (tk) = rrmVoip PrbAlloc, (t0)`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] [image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a stylized font. The equation is: $rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{v,harqretransvoip}(tk) = rrmVoipPrbAlloc_u(t0) \cdot \frac{\sum_{i=1}^{k} rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(ti)}{rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t0)}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] If UE u has a deprioritized VoIP DRB v, then [image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry, stylized font. The equation appears to be: `rmm(lstDr b Pyb Alloc c_v,deprioritizedVb(tk)) = rrmV(p) Pyb Alloc_u (t0)`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.005.png[/image_path][/image-4] [image-5][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a stylized font. The equation is: $rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{v,harqretransvoip}(tk) = rrmVoipPrbAlloc_u(t0) \cdot \frac{\sum_{i=1}^{k} rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(ti)}{rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t0)}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.004.png[/image_path][/image-5] If UE u has a non-deprioritized non-VoIP GBR DRB g, then [image-6][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmu.lstDrbPrbAlloc_{g,abrPrf_{u,c}}(t_k) = rrmuNonVoipGbrPrbAlloc_{u}(t0)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.006.png[/image_path][/image-6] [image-7][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: rrminstDrbPrballocg.harqRetransabr(tk) = rrmNonVoipGbrPrballoca(t0) * (Σki=1 rrminstUePrbAllocu(ti) / rrminstUePrbAllocu(t0))[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.007.png[/image_path][/image-7] If UE u has a deprioritized non-VoIP GBR DRB g which is contained in D, then [image-8][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrnmiust.DrbPrbAlloc_{g,deprioritizedGbr}(t_k) = rrnNonGbrPrbAlloc_u(t_0) \frac{scheduleWeight_g}{\sum_{j \in D} scheduleWeight_j}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.008.png[/image_path][/image-8] [image-9][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is: `rrminstDrbPrbAllocg,harqRetransGbr(tk) = rrminstDrbPrbAllocg.deprioritizedGbr(tk) * (Σk=1 rrminstUePrballoc(ti) / rrmInstUePrballoc(t0))`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.009.png[/image_path][/image-9] For each nonGBR DRB d contained in D [image-10][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrm_{ustDrbPrbAlloc_{d,drbref_{d}}(tk)} = rrm_{NonGbrPrbAlloc_{u}(t0)} \cdot \frac{scheduleWeight_{d}}{\sum_{j \in J} scheduleWeight_{j}}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.010.png[/image_path][/image-10] [image-11][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is: rrminst DrbPrbAlloc$_{d,harqRetransDrb}$(tk) = rrminstDrbPrballoc$_{d,drbTraffic}$(tk) $\cdot$ $\frac{\sum_{i=1}^{k} \text{rrminstUePrbAlloc}_u(t_i)}{\text{rrminstUePrbAlloc}_u(t_0)}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.011.png[/image_path][/image-11] where [image-12][image_desc]The image contains the text "schedulWeightg" in a stylized font. The letters are slightly blurred and have a gradient effect, with colors ranging from light blue to pink and yellow. The text is centered horizontally and appears on a white background.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.012.png[/image_path][/image-12] of a deprioritized GBR[CB008600-F]/DC-GBR[End CB008600-F] DRB g is equal to rdDepriorGbrSchedulWeight (see 5G_UP_ALG_10990_replaced([11193445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtHYPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and [image-13][image_desc]The image contains the text "schedulWeight" with a subscript "d" at the end. The text is in a light blue and white gradient, with a slight blur.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16422.013.png[/image_path][/image-13] of a nonGBR DRB d is the scheduling weight defined by NIDD NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight.
