**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `11208240`)

Slice Aware Scheduler PRB Bookkeeping under UL MU-MIMO

* * *

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11208270`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 11208270: Slice Aware Scheduler [CB008709-E-UPD003]

The situation that initial transmission or retransmission of a UE u is cancelled is described in 5G_UP_ALG_25640_replaced([11208099](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0DfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The same notations in 5G_UP_ALG_25640_replaced([11208099](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0DfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are used.

In addition, UL MU-MIMO is activated. If UE u is not paired in the initial transmission and all retransmissions, then gNB shall use 5G_UP_ALG_25640_replaced([11208099](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0DfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to compute the number of PRBs allocated to a DRB j of UE u for a request class c, rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j, c}(tk). Otherwise, UE u is paired in at least one slot, then it has only nonGBR traffic in the initial transmission and retransmissions slots t0 to tk (see 5G_UP_ALG_9554_replaced([11146147](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksCvavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For each nonGBR DRB d contained in D

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is: $$ \text{rrmInstDrbAllocRatio}_d(tk) = \frac{\text{scheduleWeight}_d}{\sum_{j \in D} \text{scheduleWeight}_j} $$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16424.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

[Before CB008709-E-UPD004]

[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a sans-serif font. The equation is presented on two lines. The first line reads: `rrminstDrbPrbAllocg.drbTraffic(tk)` The second line, which is an equation for the first line, reads: `= rrminstDrbAllocRatio (tk) [rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc (t0) + rrminstUeLowPrbAlloc(t0)]`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16424.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrminstDrbPrbAlloc_{g,harqRetransDrb}(t_k) = rrminstDrbAllocRatio(t_k) \cdot \sum_{i=1}^{k} [rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t_i) + rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc_u(t_i)]$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16424.003.png[/image_path][/image-2][CB008709-E-UPD004]

[image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmInstDrbAlloc_{g,d}(tk) = rrmInstDrbAllocRatio_{g}(tk) \cdot rrmInstDrbAlloc_{u}(t0)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16424.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]

[image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: rrminstDrbPrballoc$_{g,harqRetransDrb}$(tk) = rrminstDrballocRatio$_{g}$(tk) $\cdot \sum_{i=1}^{k}$ rrminstUePrballoc$_{u}$(ti)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16424.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]

[End CB008709-E-UPD004]

To compute these values, gNB-DU shall compute [Before CB008709-E-UPD004] rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u + rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc_u [CB008709-E-UPD004] rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u [End CB008709-E-UPD004] according to 5G_UP_ALG_24609_replaced([11208303](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwubFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and store the values at the scheduling time for UL slots t0, t1, … and tk.

[End CB008709-E-UPD003]

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-8**  (ID: `11208276`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Interworking with PRB pooling

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11208283`)

Slice Aware Scheduler If the following condition is met:

  * For FDD cell with PRB pooling enabled (NRBTS.actSlowPrbPooling is true)
  * or TDD FR1 cell with UL PRB pooling extensions enabled (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed is true (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and NRBTS.actBbPoolExtensionTddFr1 is true (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))),



then

rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall take into account the limitation in the number of usable FD resource that the maximum number of PRBs that can be scheduled per slot for PUSCH shall not exceed the total number of usable PRBs granted to UL (availableUlPrb, defined in 5G_UP_ALG_4876_replaced([11196884](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGI9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) by the BBRM. Please note that the total number of usable PRBs granted to UL by the BBRM may change every BB pooling period (see 5G_UP_ALG_6500_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-9**  (ID: `11208290`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Interworking with DSS (Dynamic Spectrum Sharing) or DSS/PRB pooling

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `12684502`)

Slice Aware Scheduler \- For a FDD cell shared with LTE, **rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall take into account only PRBs available for NR based on allocation of DL slots and UL PRBs, (see 5G_UP_ALG_5173_replaced([11172400](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuMEd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

  * For a FDD cell shared with LTE and with PRB pooling enabled (NRBTS.actSlowPrbPooling is true), rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) (defined in 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall take into account that maximum number of PRBs available in the slots allowed for the NR are limited to the number of usable PRBs granted to UL by the BBRM. Please note that the total number of usable PRBs granted to UL by the BBRM may change every BB pooling period (see 5G_UP_ALG_6500_replaced([11212285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxAH9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).



[CB007372-M]

  * For a FDD cell shared with LTE, if NRCELL.actAsymmetricDss is set to 'true', rrmAvailablePrbsForSas(t) shall take into account also PRBs available for NR within every subframe in NR Dedicated area ([12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).



[End CB007372-M]

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-10**  (ID: `11208303`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Interworking with MU-MIMO

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11208309`)

Slice Aware Scheduler PRB bookkeeping with the help of additional WRR (deprecated after CB008709-E-UPD004)

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-10.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11208334`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 11208334: Slice Aware Scheduler [Before CB008709-E-UPD004]

If UL MU-MIMO is activated (NRCELL.actUlMuMimo = True) and UL enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the weighted round robin (WRR) algorithm (5G_UP_ALG_4999_replaced([11199901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwQhDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be called additionally after SD scheduling (5G_UP_ALG_8265_replaced([11145653](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksA6RPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) finishes and before FD scheduling (5G_UP_ALG_4959_replaced([11199240](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) begins. This invocation of WRR is only done for the purpose of PRB bookkeeping for UL slice aware scheduling. It does not affect the resource allocation in the related UL slot. However, it affects the RG weight computation and thus affects the resource allocation in later UL slots.

Before running the WRR algorithm, gNB-DU shall compute the number of PRBs that would be allocated to each UE u in the to-be-scheduled list for any high request class traffic (high request classes are those of C_H = {harqRetransVoip, harqRetransGbr, harqRetransDrb, voipTraffic, deprioritizedVoip, gbrTraffic}). It is denoted as rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) for UE u in the UL slot t (see the definition of rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc at 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

After the SD scheduling finishes, a UE in the to-be-scheduled list is either unpaired or it is paired. There can be only three possibilities:

1\. A UE u is not paired: rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) is set to FD resources reserved for traffic from high request classes (C_H).

2\. A virtual UE consists of multiple paired UEs (the set of paired UEs is denoted as U): suppose UE u from U has the largest amount of FD resources reserved for traffic from high request classes (C_H), then rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) is set to the FD resources reserved, rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_v(t) is set to 0 for other UEs v in U. If two or more UEs from U have the same largest amount of FD resources reserved, gNB-DU randomly chooses a UE u to have its rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) set to the FD resources reserved, rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_v(t) is set to 0 for other UEs v in U. See the example below.

[image-0][image_desc]The image displays a visual representation of resource allocation, likely in a telecommunications context, alongside corresponding textual data. On the left, there are three stacked rectangular bars of different colors and lengths, representing resources allocated to entities labeled 'x', 'y', and 'z'. The top bar, colored blue, is labeled "x (5 PRBs)". The middle bar, colored orange, is shorter and labeled "y (4 PRBs)". The bottom bar, colored green, is the longest and labeled "z (6 PRBs)". The lengths of the bars visually correspond to the number of PRBs (Physical Resource Blocks) indicated in their labels. To the right of these bars, there are three lines of text, each presenting a variable assignment: * `rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_x(t) = 0` * `rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_y(t) = 0` * `rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_z(t) = 6` These lines appear to be related to the resource allocation shown on the left, possibly indicating a specific state or configuration of these allocations at time 't'. The variable names suggest a "Resource Radio Management Instance User Equipment High Priority Resource Allocation" for entities x, y, and z.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16431.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

3\. A virtual UE consists of a root UE (denoted by u) and a candidate UE group (denoted by G): If the root UE has a larger number of FD resources reserved for traffic from high request classes (C_H) than the total FD resources reserved for traffic from high request classes by all UEs in G, then rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) is set to the FD resources reserved, rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_v(t) is set to 0 for each UE v in G. Otherwise, rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_v(t) is set to its FD resources reserved, for each UE v in G, rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) is set to 0. If the number of FD resources reserved for traffic from high request classes are the same for the root UE and the candidate UE group, then gNB-DU randomly chooses the root UE or every UE in the candidate UE group to have its rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc(t) counted as its FD resources reserved.

[image-1][image_desc]The image displays a visual representation of resource allocation, likely in a telecommunications context, with accompanying text. On the left side, there are three colored rectangular blocks arranged in a specific layout. The top block is green and labeled "x (6 PRBs)". Below the green block, there are two adjacent blocks. The left one is blue and labeled "y (3 PRBs)". The right one is orange and labeled "z (4 PRBs)". The blue and orange blocks together span the width of the green block. To the right of these blocks, there are two lines of text: 1\. "rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_x(t) = 0" 2\. "rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_y(t) = 3, rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_z(t) = 4," This suggests that 'x', 'y', and 'z' represent different entities or resources, and the numbers in parentheses indicate the quantity of "PRBs" (likely Physical Resource Blocks) allocated to each. The text on the right provides specific values for a variable named "rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc" at time 't' for each of these entities. The values correspond to the PRB allocations shown in the blocks: x has 0, y has 3, and z has 4. The visual representation of 'x' having 6 PRBs seems to be a separate piece of information or a different state, as the text indicates its allocation is 0.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16431.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

The inputs to the WRR algorithm are the following:

 The available FD resources is equal to the total FD resources for UL minus (sum of rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) of every UE u in the to-be-scheduled list computed in the previous step and resources reserved for traffic of {rachMsg3, contentResGrant, harqRetransRach, harqRetransSrb, AnyTrafficBlockedInPDCCH, cgModification, cgDeactivation, cgHarqRetrans, srResp, SrbTraffic, ulTaGrant}).

 The UEs joining WRR are those UEs in the to-be-scheduled list which satisfy the following two conditions --- 1) the UE does not have pending retransmissions, and 2) the UE must have initTx data for nonGBR or deprioritized GBR DRBs which belong to RGs of nonzero RG weight (see 5G_UP_ALG_23167_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). 

After the WRR algorithm has distributed FD resources to UEs, UE u gets rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc_u(t) PRBs for its low request classes (drbTraffic and deprioritizedGbr) in the UL slot t (see the definition of rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc at 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If a UE v didn’t join the WRR algorithm, then rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc_v(t) is set to 0.

rrmInstPrbFree(t) is equal to the number of unallocated PRBs after the additional WRR algorithm is completed (see definition of rrmInstPrbFree at 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

gNB-DU shall compute the equivalent of the allocation that a DRB j for a request class c has obtained in a slot t in terms of PRBs (see definition of rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc at 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The computation is performed when the UL transmission is successfully received.

If [the combination of DRB j and traffic class c does not exist] or [gNB has not received any data from DRB j for request class c in slot t]

[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t) = 0$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16431.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]

Else

[image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t) = \frac{amountBytes_{j,c}(t)}{\sum_{k \in J_u} \sum_{d \in c_H \cup c_L} amountBytes_{k,d}(t)} \cdot [rrmInstUeHighPrbAlloc_u(t) + rrmInstUeLowPrbAlloc_u(t)]$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16431.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]

where

 amountBytes_{j,c}: the amount of bytes received by gNB for the transmissions of DRB j for request class c in slot t, under MU-MIMO schedule.

 J_u: the set of non-GBR (excluding 5QI-5) and GBR DRBs of UE u.

 C_L: the low priority request class set, C_L = {drbTraffic, deprioritizedGbr}.

 C_H: the high priority request class set, C_H = {harqRetransVoip, harqRetransGbr, harqRetransDrb, voipTraffic, deprioritizedVoip, gbrTraffic}.

[CB008709-E-UPD004]

[End CB008709-E-UPD004]

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-10.0-2**  (ID: `11208341`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Introduction

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-10.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11208355`)

Slice Aware Scheduler If UL MU-MIMO is activated (NRCELL.actUlMuMimo = True) and UL enhanced slice aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12233_replaced([11278970](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpS0kvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), [CB008250-D] or PLMN aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16121_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008250-D], L2PS shall do PRB bookkeeping for UEs scheduled in a slot as follows.

The rationales for UE PRB bookkeeping are

1\. If a UE is not paired, the resources used by the UE is counted as the PRBs allocated to the UE.

2\. If retransmission (reTx) UEs and/or initial transmission (iniTx) UEs are paired in a vUE, then the resource used by a UE in the vUE is counted as a fraction of the PRBs allocated to the vUE that is proportional to the UE weight (the same UE weight computation is used by WRR). Thus, it makes sure that the resources used by each UE in the vUE sum up to a value equal to the PRBs allocated to the vUE.

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-10.0-3**  (ID: `11208360`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Compute the equivalent number of PRBs allocated to each UE in a vUE

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-10.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11208470`)

Slice Aware Scheduler 11208470: Slice Aware Scheduler After the scheduling of each slot _t, L2PS shall compute the equivalent number of PRBs allocated to each UE_ u, _rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t), contained in a vUE_ v as follows (see 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of _rrmInstUePrbAlloc). vUE_ v includes reTx UEs and iniTx UEs The UEs contains in a vUE _v_ _is denoted as_ U(v). Assume that after the scheduling, each UE _u in vUE_ v is allocated _rrmInstUeMuPrbAlloc_u(t) PRBs and vUE_ v is allocated _rrmInstVuePrbAlloc_v(t) PRBs. The following computation of_ rrmInstUePrbAlloc guarantees that [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $\sum_{u \in U(v)} rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t) = rrmInstVuePrbAlloc_v(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16437.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]**_*_** **_*_** **

If a UE _u is unpaired,_ rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t) is equal to number of PRBs allocated to UE _u for UL transmission in slot_ t.****

Otherwise, L2PS shall compute _rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t) for each UE_ u in vUE _v as follows._ **

The PRBs allocated to vUE _v are split into one or more PRB blocks denoted by_ B_v. Each PRB block must have allocation boundaries that coincide with allocation boundaries of UEs in vUE _v. Each PRB block_ b is spatially shared by a set of UEs denoted by _U^B(b) that is a subset of_ U(v). The number of PRBs in PRB block _b is denoted by_ N(b) and the total number of PRBs in all PRB blocks is equal to the number of PRBs allocated to vUE _v, i.e. [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $\sum_{b \in B_v} |N(b)| = rrmInstVuePrbAlloc_v(t)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16437.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]._**_*_** *

Note, PRBs allocated to vUE _v can be split into at most |_ U(v)| PRB blocks, where |_U(v)| is the number of UEs in_ v.****

rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u becomes the sum of PRB share computed for UE u from each PRB block as below.

For each UE _u in vUE_ v, set [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is: `rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t) = 0`[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16437.003.png[/image_path][/image-2].**

For each PRB block _b in the PRB blocks [image-3][image_desc]The image contains the letters "B" and "v" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of blue, orange, and yellow, giving them a metallic or iridescent appearance. The background is white, and the image is framed by a thick black border.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16437.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]_

For each UE _u in [image-4][image_desc]The image contains the text "UB(b)". The text is in a stylized font with a gradient effect, appearing to be in shades of orange and yellow. The "U" is large and bold, followed by a superscript "B" in a smaller font. The "B" is then followed by a lowercase "b" in parentheses.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16437.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]_

[image-5][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: rrmInstUePrbAlloc$_{u}$(t) = rrmInstUePrbAlloc$_{u}$(t) + N(b) $\cdot$ $\frac{w_{u}^{U}}{\sum_{k \in U^{B}(b)} w_{k}^{U}}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16437.006.png[/image_path][/image-5],

End For

End For

Note, _w_u^U is the UE weight of UE_ u. For both iniTx and reTx UE, the UE weight is calculated as the sum of combined weight (i.e., the product of NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight and resource group (RG) weight. **[CB008250-D] if PLMN aware scheduler is activated for a cell (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_16121_replaced([11279261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUpufvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), RG weight is computed as defined in (5G_UP_ALG_31220_replaced([11206541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwrXyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), else, as defined in **[End CB008250-D] (5G_UP_ALG_23167_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) of its non-GBR bearers that are mapped to LCGs that have non-zero buffer size. The same UE weight calculation is also applied in WRR (see 5G_UP_ALG_23866_replaced([11199933](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRIFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For the case that [image-6][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation: $\sum_{k \in U^B(b)} w_k^U = 0$.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_16437.007.png[/image_path][/image-6], which corresponds to the situation that each paired UE in PRB block _b has RG weight equal to 0, then rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t) of each UE_ u in U^B(b) _is incremented by_ N(b) divided by number of paired UEs in _U^B(b)._**_*_** ***

[Before PR871905][PR867111] For a UE that is attempted to be scheduled for SR response grant with DRB priority, i.e. UE has the SR response scheduling status SR_PENDING_WAITING_FOR_GRANT (see [11203903: SR response scheduling flow](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), RG weight of the RG to which the highest priority DRB k’ configured for the UE belongs (see [11203903: SR response scheduling flow](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwhmz_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for highest priority DRB) is calculated as MAX (1, (2* rdEnhSvcPiPGainUL + 1) * rdLowBufRgWeightAmplifierUL). See [11207350: Feedback control based on low priority target share](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for rdEnhSvcPiPGainUL rdLowBufRgWeightAmplifierUL. [End PR867111][End PR871905]

For each scheduled UE _u, L2PS computes the PRB gain of UE_ u as rrmPrbGain_u(t) = rrmInstUeMuPrbAlloc_u(t) / rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t).**

The PRB bookkeeping continues as in 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) by using the computed rrmInstUePrbAlloc_u(t) values. At the beginning of PRB bookkeeping for a slot _t, the sum of actual PRBs used by RG k, rrmActRGPrbAlloc_k(t) is initialized to 0. When L2PS has computed rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t) for a DRB_ j and a request class _c, it increments rrmActRGPrbAlloc_k(t) by rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t) * rrmPrbGain_u(t) where DRB_ j belongs to UE _u and RG_ k.***** *

Rationale: With MU-MIMO, actual PRB used for a DRB j and request class c could be different than what is counted in SAS PRB bookkeeping with rrmInstDrbPrbAlloc_{j,c}(t). Thus, the difference which is reflected in additional PRB gained per RG due to MU-MIMO will not be controlled by SAS.

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-10.0-4**  (ID: `11208477`)

Slice Aware Scheduler Compute the number of PRBs unoccupied in PUSCH

---

**3.30.18.3.0-5.0-10.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11208482`)

Slice Aware Scheduler [CB008709-E-UPD004]

If UL MU-MIMO is activated (NRCELL.actUlMuMimo = True), the number of PRBs unoccupied in PUSCH for slot t, rrmInstPrbFree(t) (see 5G_UP_ALG_23256_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition) is equal to PRBs in PUSCH minus PRBs used by every scheduled virtual UE (vUE) and every scheduled unpaired UE.

[End CB008709-E-UPD004]
